PT P
PN WO2018211945-A1; CN110662597-A; EP3626334-A1; JP2019519157-X; EP3626334-A4; US2021154622-A1; JP7073357-B2; CN110662597-B
TI Film used in apparatus for separating carbon            dioxide used for fertilizing indoor grown plants,            consists of ionic liquid affinity porous layer which            holds ionic liquid-containing liquid within gap, and            ionic liquid non-affinity porous layer.
AU TOSHIKAZU N
   MASAO I
   TAKAMASA S
   TOMOHIRO G
   NAKAMURA T
   IWAYA M
   SUZUKI T
   GOTO T
AE DAICEL CORP (DAIL-C)
   DAICEL CORP (DAIL-C)
   DAICEL CORP (DAIL-C)
   DAICEL CORP (DAIL-C)
GA 2018914825
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide separation film consists of                an ionic liquid affinity porous layer which holds                the ionic liquid-containing liquid within a gap,                and an ionic liquid non-affinity porous                layer.
   USE - Film used in apparatus for separating carbon                dioxide used for fertilizing indoor grown plants                (all claimed) e.g. strawberry, tomato, cucumber,                bean sprouts, rose, and ornamental plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide separation film is thin and                has excellent gas permeability and handleability,                and suppressed tackiness due to the addition of                stable ionic liquid whose liquid state is                maintained without increasing viscosity by                gelatinization. The carbon dioxide separation film                efficiently reduces the size of the carbon                dioxide-concentrating apparatus, and allows the                apparatus to be smoothly operated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of carbon dioxide separation                film; and
   (2) carbon dioxide-concentrating                apparatus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The inorganic                  material contains metal oxide particles having                  average particle size of 0.001-5 mu m. Preferred                  Properties: The film thickness of the liquid film                  which is formed using the ionic liquid-containing                  liquid is 0.1-2 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ionic liquid                  affinity porous layer further contains an                  inorganic material, and has average thickness of                  0.01-10 mu m. The ionic liquid-containing liquid                  contains an ionic liquid including a cation                  chosen from ammonium, imidazolium, and                  phosphonium, and an anion chosen from                  fluorine-containing anion, cyano group-containing                  anion, and anion derived from amino acid, and a                  liquid (L) which can be mutually dissolved. The                  ionic liquid affinity porous layer contains                  0.1-99 volume parts ionic liquid-containing                  liquid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The ionic liquid                  non-affinity porous layer contains a resin chosen                  from polyolefin resin, fluororesin, and cellulose                  derivative, and has contact angle of 90-150                  degrees with respect to ionic liquid-containing                  liquid. The liquid (L) is polyamine. The molar                  ratio of the ionic liquid and the liquid (L) is                  25/75-75/25.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PT P
PN JP2022139068-A
TI Carbon dioxide emission reduction propulsion            system for promoting reduction of carbon dioxide            emissions in multiple dwelling house, has computer that            calculates contribution degree index showing degree of            contribution to emission reduction.
AU UKAI K
   TAKADA K
   KAJINO S
   TANAKA T
AE AISIN CORP (AISE-C)
GA 2022C7343T
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an energy saving device that is                installed in each dwelling room of a multiple                dwelling house having the partitioned dwelling                rooms and a computer that is configured to                communicate with the energy saving device to reduce                an amount of carbon dioxide emission by use of the                energy saving device. A total reduction amount                which is a sum of the carbon dioxide emission                reduction amounts calculated for each of the                dwelling rooms is calculated based on the use state                of the energy saving device. A contribution index                representing a contribution degree is calculated                (S33) to reduce an emission of carbon dioxide for                each of the living rooms based on the total amount                of reduction and the reduction amount of carbon                dioxide emission calculated for each of the living                rooms.
   USE - System for promoting reduction of carbon                dioxide emission in multiple dwelling house.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon-dioxide emission reduction                propulsion system for promoting the reduction of                carbon dioxide emissions in a multiple dwelling                house is provided.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a contribution index calculation routine executed                by an arithmetic unit of a computer. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   S31Step for acquiring the carbon dioxide                emission reduction amount up to the present time of                the carbon dioxide emission reduction amount                calculation process
   S32Step for obtaining the total reduction                amount by the sum of the reduction amounts of the                carbon dioxide emission of each of the living                rooms
   S33Step for calculating the contribution                index
   S34Step for outputting the calculated                contribution index
   S35Step for determining whether or not the                current date and time has reached the predetermined                end date and time
DC T01 (Digital Computers)
MC T01-E02D; T01-J05A2A

PT P
PN CN114896550-A; CN114896550-B
TI Method for measuring amount of carbon dioxide            released in ebb and flow area of lake, involves            determining carbon dioxide release amount of the target            lake leveling area according to area information of            target lake leveling area and target carbon dioxide            flux.
AU JIANG D
   LIANG L
   YANG H
   DING X
   LIU Z
   DAI H
   CAO Y
   HE X
   ZHANG B
AE CHINA THREE GORGES CORP (CTGC-C)
GA 2022A7067R
AB    NOVELTY - The measurement method involves receiving the                soil layer temperature and the carbon dioxide flux                corresponding to the temperature of each soil layer                in the basic simulation unit of the target lake                leveling area, and constructing a mapping                relationship between the soil layer temperature and                the carbon dioxide flux. The basic spatial                information of the target lake and the water cycle                driving information of the target lake are                obtained. The soil temperature of the basic                simulation unit of the target lake leveling area is                determined according to the basic spatial                information of the target lake and the water cycle                driving information of the target lake. The carbon                dioxide release amount of the target lake leveling                area is determined according to the area                information of the target lake leveling area and                the target carbon dioxide flux.
   USE - Method for measuring amount of carbon dioxide                released in ebb and flow area of lake.
   ADVANTAGE - The measuring method completes the                determination of the carbon dioxide release amount                in the leveling area by combining the actual                measurement data with the relational model, and                solves the problem of difficulty in measuring the                amount of carbon dioxide released due to the                dynamic change of the leveling area and the harsh                actual geographical environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a device for measuring the amount of                carbon dioxide released in the ebb and flow area of                the lake;
   (2) a computer device comprising a memory and                a processor;
   (3) a computer readable storage medium for                storing computer instructions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of a method                for measuring amount of carbon dioxide released in                ebb and flow area of lake. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

PT P
PN US2021300765-A1; WO2021202499-A1; CA3176442-A1
TI System for producing carbon dioxide with waste            heat from industrial process, has DAC system that            comprises carbon dioxide source material thermally            coupled to heating fluid or thermal energy output of            power plant.
AU GILROYSMITH B C
   GAGNE J
   NELSON R
   MALONE C G
AE X DEV LLC (XDEV-C)
   X DEV LLC (XDEV-C)
GA 2021B2080Y
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises a direct air capture                (DAC) system electrically coupled to an electrical                power output of a natural gas power plant. The DAC                system has a carbon dioxide source material                thermally coupled to a heating fluid or thermal                energy output of the power plant to receive heat to                separate carbon dioxide from the source material.                An exhaust fluid scrubbing system separates                components of an exhaust fluid, such as carbon                dioxide. A heating system is provided with a                thermodynamic cycle comprising a refrigerant fluid                coupled to the heating fluid, and a heat-pump                system.
   USE - System for producing carbon dioxide in direct                air capture (DAC) process with waste heat from                industrial process.
   ADVANTAGE - The power plant designed to provide backup                power for an industrial process may be operated                more efficiently by powering other processes,                having a direct air capture (DAC) process during                periods that backup power is not needed. The waste                heat can be captured from both a power plant and an                industrial processes and efficiently used in a DAC                process. The integrated processes that produce                power and capture carbon dioxide using waste heat                also produce potable water. The flows of greenhouse                gases may be captured and directed to a revenue                generating process, such as carbon dioxide enhanced                oil recovery (CO 2 -EOR). A carbon dioxide stream                is produced from previously captured atmospheric                carbon dioxide with a source of heat that otherwise                would be wasted or underutilized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for providing carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an                integrated power and carbon dioxide production                system.
   Data center (402)
   Heated return fluid (403)
   Electrical power (407)
   Pump (411)
   Heat exchanger (412)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))
MC E31-N05C; E11-Q02B; J01-E02; Q75-U40; Q75-F; Q75-A02X

PT P
PN CN113426237-A
TI Carbon dioxide treatment device for production of calcium carbonate, has compressor for compressing carbon dioxide gas that is fixedly connected with drying mechanism through duct and storage assembly that used for storing carbon dioxide in fixed communication with compressor through air pipe.
AU ZHU M
   LI S
AE GUILIN JINSHAN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (GUIL-Non-standard)
GA 2021B40244
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a filtering mechanism (4) that is used for filtering carbon dioxide by washing with water. A drying mechanism (5) for drying carbon dioxide in a fixed connection with the filtering mechanism through an air guide tube (9). A compressor (6) for compressing carbon dioxide gas is fixedly connected with the drying mechanism through a duct (10). A storage assembly (7) for storing carbon dioxide in fixed communication with the compressor through an air pipe (11). An air guide tube is fixedly connected to the top side of a filter box (41). A dispersion assembly (42) is rotatably installed in the filter box.
   USE - Carbon dioxide treatment device for production of calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The device filters carbon dioxide by washing with water through a filtering mechanism and the filtering effect is good. The cleaned water is filtered through the impurity removal component, and the washed carbon dioxide is dried by the drying mechanism in a cooling manner and then sent to the storage component storage through the compressor. The effect of washing and filtering is good, the repeated use of water is realized and the phenomenon of regular replacement of the filter and the clogging of the filter are avoided by the device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the carbon dioxide treatment method for calcium carbonate production.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide treatment device for production of calcium carbonate.
   Filtering mechanism (4)
   Drying mechanism (5)
   Compressor (6)
   Storage assembly (7)
   Air guide tube (9)

AU THOMAS M
   MONKMAN G S
   HANMORE A
   FORGERON D
   GORGERON D
   FORGERON D P
AE CARBONCURE TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   THOMAS M (THOM-Individual)
   MONKMAN G S (MONK-Individual)
   HANMORE A (HANM-Individual)
   FORGERON D (FORG-Individual)
   CARBONCURE TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBONCURE TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2020A6021M
AB    NOVELTY - A wet concrete mix comprises hydraulic cement,                water, and aggregate, where a portion of aggregate                is carbonated recycled concrete aggregate (RCA),                and cement and/or water are at least partially                carbonated.
   USE - A wet concrete mix.
   ADVANTAGE - Deliberately pre-carbonating RCAs can improve                both the quality of the aggregates and the                mechanical properties of concrete produced with                such aggregates. The carbon dioxide treatment of                recycled concrete aggregates can reduce the water                absorption of the aggregates and reduce the                transport properties of concrete (as measured by                bulk electrical conductivity, chloride ion                permeability and gas permeability) made with the                aggregates as compared to untreated RCA.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) production of wet concrete mix which                involves combining hydraulic cement, water, and                aggregate, where a portion of the aggregate is RCA                that has been exposed to carbon dioxide to                carbonate the aggregate with the carbon dioxide,                and water comprises carbonated water and/or                concrete mix is exposed to carbon dioxide during                mixing;
   (2) system comprising source of carbon dioxide                operably connected to facility that comprises RCAs                and system for delivering the carbon dioxide to the                RCAs to carbonate the RCAs; and
   (3) carbonation of aggregates which involves                capturing carbon dioxide at a source of carbon                dioxide, converting the captured carbon dioxide                into a form that can be transported if it is not                already in such a form, transporting the carbon                dioxide to a facility that comprises RCAs, and                exposing the RCAs to the transported carbon dioxide                to produce carbonated RCAs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Compositions: A portion of                  aggregate that is carbonated RCA is 0.5-90% of                  the total aggregate. Carbonated RCA comprises                  coarse aggregate and/or fine aggregate. Concrete                  mix comprises carbonated hydraulic cement, where                  hydraulic cement is carbonated at a level of                  0.005-5% carbon dioxide (as carbon dioxide by                  weight cement). Water comprises carbonated wash                  water. Water comprises 1-100% carbonated wash                  water. Water comprises carbonated water. Carbon                  dioxide comprises carbon dioxide derived from                  flue gas from a power plant or a cement plant,                  preferably carbon dioxide derived from flue gas                  of a cement plant. Preferred Processes: Concrete                  mix is exposed to carbon dioxide during mixing at                  a dose of 0.01-2 wt.% by weight cement (bwc).                  Carbon dioxide is delivered as liquid carbon                  dioxide that is converted to solid and gaseous                  carbon dioxide. The facility comprising RCAs                  produces at least a portion of the RCAs from used                  concrete. Preferred Systems: Carbon dioxide at                  the source of carbon dioxide comprises gaseous                  carbon dioxide, where system further comprises a                  component to convert the gaseous carbon dioxide                  to liquid carbon dioxide. The system of claim 19                  further comprising a component to transport the                  liquid carbon dioxide to the facility for                  processing concrete. Component to transport the                  liquid carbon dioxide to the facility comprises a                  conduit suited to transport the liquid carbon                  dioxide. The system for delivering carbon dioxide                  delivers carbon dioxide to the coarse aggregates                  and/or the fine aggregates to carbonate the                  coarse aggregates and/or the fine aggregates. The                  system further comprises concrete-producing                  facility and a component to deliver carbonated                  RCAs to the concrete-producing facility to be                  used in concrete mix produced at the facility.                  Concrete-producing facility is configured to                  deliver carbon dioxide to a wet concrete mix                  produced at the facility and to deliver mix water                  to a concrete mix, where mix water comprises                  carbonated mix water. Concrete-producing facility                  comprises a component to carbonate wash water                  produced at the facility and to transport the                  carbonated wash water to a concrete mix produced                  at the facility.

PN KR2019075681-A
TI Apparatus for automatically supplying e.g. mono-ethanol-amine, to carbon dioxide removal device within submarine, has centralized administration unit for automatically controlling opening and closing of valve according to control command.
AU KYU L S
   JUNG K A
AE DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
GA 2019602760
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a centralized administration unit connected to a carbon dioxide concentration sensor (150) and receiving a sensing result of the carbon dioxide concentration sensor. A valve (171) is installed in a pipe through which an amount of a carbon dioxide absorbent passed from an absorbent supply unit (100) is controlled. An actuator (172) controls opening and closing of the valve according to a control command of the centralized administration unit. The centralized administration unit transmits the control command according to the sensing result and automatically controls opening and closing of the valve according to the control command.
   USE - Apparatus for automatically supplying a carbon dioxide absorbent i.e. mono-ethanol-amine, to a carbon dioxide removal device within a submarine.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus can be connected to an integrated platform management system (IPMS) and an atmosphere monitor unit and allow the valve to be opened to automatically supply carbon dioxide concentration to an equipment within the submarine according to standard concentration, thus increasing convenience of operators and reducing a problem generated in the submarine due to a human error. The apparatus reduces concentration of carbon dioxide generated within the submarine and enhances safety of submarine operation.

The fixed flow of Camellia seed crude oil during the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and deacidification is 1/20-1/100 of the supercritical carbon dioxide flow. The collection of the oil-like product in the separation kettle (I) is started, when the water content of the oil-like product is below 0.1 wt%.

PN WO2022185903-A1
TI Carbon dioxide adsorption battery used in            charge/discharge device, comprises positive electrode            which permeate gas, negative electrode, separator            suppresses permeation of redox compound, and            electrolyte layer containing electrolytic solution            dissolving carbon dioxide and redox compound.
AU IWASAKI H
AE KURARAY CO LTD (KURS-C)
GA 2022B5917W
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide adsorption battery (10)                comprises a positive electrode (11), a negative                electrode (12), a separator (16) arranged between                the positive electrode and the negative electrode,                and an electrolyte layer (13) arranged between the                positive electrode and the separator and between                the negative electrode and the separator. The                positive electrode is an electrode which can                permeate gas. The electrolyte layer contains an                electrolytic solution capable of dissolving carbon                dioxide and a redox compound having an N-oxy                radical group in the molecule. The separator                suppresses the permeation of the redox compound and                allows the electrolyte to permeate.
   USE - Carbon dioxide adsorption battery used in                charge/discharge device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide adsorption battery is                capable of easily separating, and adsorbing carbon                dioxide from a gas containing carbon dioxide, while                charging.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                the carbon dioxide adsorption battery during                charging.

PN CN113887015-A
TI Method for calculating pressure and temperature of carbon dioxide storage tank of combined engine, involves obtaining temperature and pressure of carbon dioxide in tank through cycle iteration, and changing pressure at any time under different mass flow conditions.
AU LENG H
   HE X
   JIANG X
AE BEIJING POWER MACHINERY RES INST (CAES-C)
GA 2022111965
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing carbon dioxide quality conservation and energy conservation equation in two states before and after the storage tank is exhausted. Assumption is made that the temperature and pressure of carbon dioxide discharged during the exhaust process remain unchanged. The temperature, pressure and remaining mass of the carbon dioxide storage tank after exhaust are solved. The temperature and pressure of carbon dioxide in the storage tank are obtained through the cycle iteration, and the pressure is changed at any time under different mass flow conditions.
   USE - Method for calculating pressure and temperature of carbon dioxide storage tank of combined engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The time-varying curve of the temperature and pressure of carbon dioxide in the storage tank can be obtained in real time under different mass flow conditions. The method is applied for the system performance design of the combined engine with limited missile space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the carbon dioxide storage tank pressure temperature calculation method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

GA 2022100834
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a combustor (100) for supplying heat by burning fuel. A pyrolysis reactor (300) pyrolyzies fuel with heat supplied from the combustor to produce hydrogen. A carbon dioxide conversion reactor (400) generates hydrogen and carbon monoxide by reacting carbon dioxide generated in the combustor with fuel. A combustion gas exhaust pipe (200) supplies heat of the exhaust gas discharged from the combustor to the pyrolysis reactor and the carbon dioxide conversion reactor, and supplies carbon dioxide contained in the exhaust gas to the carbon dioxide conversion reactor.
   USE - Hydrogen production system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system prevents emission of carbon dioxide generated in combustion process to an outer side, so that hydrogen, solid carbon and carbon monoxide are produced through a chemical reaction, and production of hydrogen and energy efficiency can be improved.

PN CN111487366-A
TI Biological detection based on gas isotope detection comprises selecting sample plants, cultivating, passing carbon dioxide, recording detection time, compressing, measuring carbon dioxide, comparing with carbon isotopes and recording.
AU HUANG Y
AE SUZHOU YIYUN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2020758092
AB    NOVELTY - Biological detection based on gas isotope detection comprises (1) selecting sample plants, (2) cultivating to testing platform, (3) passing mixed gas of carbon dioxide, oxygen and nitrogen with carbon isotope to detection platform, (4) waiting for photosynthesis, (5) recording detection time as reference time, (6) taking part of remaining air as sample air, (7) compressing, (8) detecting content of carbon dioxide in sample air, (9) measuring content of carbon dioxide with carbon isotopes, (10) adjusting environmental conditions, (11) waiting for photosynthesis until next detection time point is reached, (12) taking part of remaining air in detection platform as sample air, (13) compressing, (14) detecting content of carbon dioxide, (15) measuring the content of carbon dioxide with carbon isotopes on the basis of carbon dioxide and (16) comparing the content of carbon dioxide with carbon isotopes and recording environment corresponding to measurement results with less content.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting isotope of air in the biological environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the method for detecting isotope of air in the biological environment (Drawing includes non-English language text).

The volume ratio of sintering                suppressor with respect to the volume of the                calcium oxide and the sintering suppressor is 75%                or more.
   USE - Carbon dioxide occlusion reduction catalyst in                reaction tube of system (claimed) used for                producing methane used as fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide storage reduction catalyst                can be repeatedly used, and efficiently utilized                the heat generated during occlusion and desorption                of carbon dioxide.

PN WO2020092307-A1; KR2021079365-A; SG11202103080-A1; EP3873857-A1; US2021403358-A1; JP2022503695-W; EP3873857-A4
TI Removing ozone from streams with ultravoilet            radiation involves directing aqueous solution having            first concentration of dissolved carbon dioxide, first            concentration of dissolved ozone into vessel, removing            dissolved carbon dioxide.
AU SUNDSTROM G
   WALLIN J
   SUNDSTROM G P
AE EVOQUA WATER TECHNOLOGIES LLC (SIEI-C)
   EVOQUA WATER TECHNOLOGIES LLC (SIEI-C)
   EVOQUA WATER TECHNOLOGIES LLC (SIEI-C)
GA 202037645X
AB    NOVELTY - Removing ozone from streams with ultravoilet                radiation involves directing aqueous solution                having first concentration of dissolved carbon                dioxide and first concentration of dissolved ozone                into vessel, removing amount of the dissolved                carbon dioxide from aqueous solution to produce                first effluent having second concentration of                dissolved carbon dioxide lower than first                concentration of dissolved carbon dioxide and                irradiating the first effluent with ultraviolet                light at dosage effective to decompose                predetermined amount of the dissolved ozone in the                first effluent to produce second effluent having                second concentration of dissolved ozone lower than                the first concentration of dissolved ozone.
   USE - Method for removing ozone from streams with                ultravoilet radiation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to remove ozone from                streams with ultravoilet radiation that is cost                effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system,                which comprises channel fluidly connectable to                source of aqueous solution having first                concentration of dissolved carbon dioxide and first                concentration of dissolved ozone, dissolved carbon                dioxide removal subsystem is fluidly connected to                the channel and configured to remove amount of the                dissolved carbon dioxide from aqueous solution to                produce a first effluent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method involves                  removing the amount of dissolved carbon dioxide,                  controlling pH of the aqueous solution to a value                  effective to convert the amount of the dissolved                  carbon dioxide into carbonate and bicarbonate,                  controlling pH of the aqueous solution to be                  between about 8.3-11, measuring pH of the aqueous                  solution and pH of the first effluent, and                  responsive to the measurement, adding                  predetermined amount of acid or base to control                  pH of the aqueous solution. The method involves                  measuring first concentration of dissolved ozone                  and second concentration of dissolved ozone, and                  responsive to the measurement, controlling dosage                  of the ultraviolet irradiation and flow rate of                  aqueous solution and first effluent. Preferred                  Conditions: The first effluent is substantially                  fine of dissolved carbon dioxide. The first                  concentration of dissolved carbon dioxide is 20                  ppm. The second concentration of dissolved ozone                  is less than 2 ppb. The aqueous solution                  comprises semiconductor manufacturing process                  water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN CN109977342-A
TI Carbon dioxide discharge amount checking method, involves obtaining total fuel consumed by power generation unit, and obtaining carbon dioxide discharge amount in preset statistical period by using total amount of carbon elements.
AU WANG X
   ZHOU B
   GUO Z
   ZOU X
   ZHANG M
   LIU Y
   QIN Y
   SUN Y
   REN J
AE HUADIAN ELECTRIC POWER RES INST (HUAD-C)
GA 2019611349
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining a total fuel consumed by a power generation unit during a preset counting period. A carbon content is received from the fuel when the fuel is introduced into a furnace base. A solid product is outputted by the power generation unit. Total amount of the carbon element in the fuel is calculated. Yield of the solid product and carbon content of the solid product are calculated. The power generation unit output is obtained by calculating difference between the carbon element in total carbon dioxide and total amount of the carbon element in the solid product. Carbon dioxide discharge amount in the preset statistical period is obtained by using the total amount of carbon elements in the carbon dioxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide discharge amount checking method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables ensuring checking precision and calculation process of the carbon dioxide discharge amount at the same time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a Carbon dioxide discharge amount checking device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a carbon dioxide discharge amount checking method. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PN KR2022067591-A
TI Ion-exchanged montmorillonite catalyst for carbon dioxide capture obtained by reacting montmorillonite with aqueous acid solution e.g. nitric acid, sulfuric acid, benzoic acid or aqueous metal solution, filtering, and washing.
AU NAMSUNGCHAN
   BAEK I H
   JANG J T
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202273284B
AB    NOVELTY - Ion-exchanged montmorillonite catalyst for carbon dioxide capture.
   USE - Used as an ion-exchanged montmorillonite catalyst for carbon dioxide capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: reduces carbon dioxide emissions; is suitable for large-scale carbon dioxide capture; reduces energy requirement; improves carbon dioxide desorption rate by minimizing the volume; and reduces cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing an ion-exchanged montmorillonite catalyst comprising (a) reacting montmorillonite with aqueous acid solution or aqueous metal solution to obtain product, and (b) filtering the product and washing until the pH of the filtrate becomes 5-9; and
   (2) regenerating a solvent for capturing carbon dioxide comprising adopting use of ion-exchanged montmorillonite catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ion-exchange is the ions contained in the montmorillonite are replaced with the cations of the aqueous acid solution or the aqueous metal solution. The ion-exchange is silicon, aluminum, oxygen, iron, magnesium, sodium, calcium or potassium ions contained in the montmorillonite are replaced with cations of hydrogen or zirconium. The ion-exchanged montmorillonite catalyst has a fraction of one element 0-7% aluminum, 0-1.2% iron, and 0-1% magnesium. The ion-exchanged montmorillonite catalyst has a surface area of 50-500 m2/g. The ion-exchanged montmorillonite catalyst has a pore volume of 0.15-0.50 cm3/g. The ion-exchanged montmorillonite catalyst has average pore diameter of 1-15 nm. The ion-exchanged montmorillonite catalyst has surface acidity of greater than or equal to 2 mmol/g. The ion-exchanged montmorillonite catalyst is at least 0.3 mmol/g of total acid sites. The acid is hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, sulfuric acid, benzoic acid, oxalic acid, acetic acid, propionic acid, pyridinium ion, chloric acid, oxonium ion, iodic acid, oxylic acid, sulfurous acid, chlorous acid, phosphoric acid, arsenic acid, chloroacetic acid, hydrogen fluoride, nitrous acid, formic acid, carbonic acid, hydrogen sulfide, hypochlorous acid, hydrogen iodide, hydrogen bromide, and/or perchloric acid. The aqueous solution of an inorganic metal salt is tin(IV) chloride, titanium tetrachloride, titanium oxychloride, titanyl sulfate, zirconyl chloride, zirconium(IV) chloride, zirconium oxide sulfate, manganese(II) chloride, cobalt(II) chloride and ferric chloride. The acid aqueous solution or metal aqueous solution has a concentration of 0.5-10 mol/l. Preferred Method: In the step (a), the reaction is proceeds while stirring. The reaction of the montmorillonite and the acid aqueous solution or the metal aqueous solution is in a ratio of 1:1-1:100 (mass:volume). The reaction of step (a) is carried out at 70-110 degrees C for 1-20 hours. The filtration of step (b) is performed after cooling to room temperature.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of evaluating catalytic performance for carbon dioxide desorption that do not support the claims.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Carbon dioxide assisted energy storage            infiltration fracturing method of compact reservoir            involves pumping slick water, grinding crack wall using            proppant, introducing liquid carbon dioxide, adding            oozing agent and introducing jelly.
AU CHEN T

   LIU Y
   WANG H
AE CNPC BOHAI DRILLING ENG CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 201975246F
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide assisted energy storage                infiltration fracturing method of compact reservoir                involves pumping slick water, grinding crack wall                using proppant, introducing liquid carbon dioxide,                increasing formation energy, introducing slick                water and proppant, adding high efficiency oozing                agent to the slick water, introducing jelly and                proppant, establishing high permeability flow                guiding channel, introducing slick water or jelly,                processing, introducing liquid carbon dioxide,                increasing formation energy, introducing proppant                and slick water, completing process and then                discharging and processing for 8-20 days.
   USE - The method is useful for carbon dioxide                assisted energy storage infiltration fracturing                method of compact reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of carbon dioxide                assisted energy storage infiltration fracturing                method of compact reservoir with excellent                wettability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Component: The proppant is high                  strength ceramsite particles with particle size                  of 40-70 mesh and particle diameter of 30-50                  mesh.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves using azobisisobutyryl dimethyl ester as                  initiator and reacting at 65 degrees C for 3                  hours. Preferred Components: The high efficiency                  oozing agent comprises sodium butyl oleate and                  polyoxyethylene isooctyl phenyl ether in a mass                  ratio of 1:8. The slick water comprises 0.1 %mass                  drag reducing agent, 2 %mass clay stabilizer, 0.5                  %mass drainage additive and water. The clay                  stabilizer is organic anti-swelling agent. The                  drainage additive is cationic fluorocarbon                  surfactant. The jelly is low molecular weight                  polymer jelly formed by crosslinking low                  molecular weight thickener with 0.025 %mass                  crosslinking agent including organic zirconium                  crosslinking agent. The low molecular weight                  thickener comprises N,N-dimethylacrylamide,                  terminally carboxylated aliphatic polylactic acid                  and cetyltrimethyl quaternary ammonium salt in a                  mass ratio of 8:5:1. Preferred Conditions: The                  crosslinking agent is prepared by mixing water,                  triethanolamine, phthalic acid, ferric chloride                  and aluminum chloride in a mass ratio of                  70:5:5:3:2, heating at 65 degrees C, adding 15                  pts. wt. zirconyl nitrate, reacting for 6-7 hours                  and cooling to room temperature. Preferred                  Property: The low molecular weight thickener has                  molecular weight of less than 5 million.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The drag reducing agent                  is anionic polyacrylamide.

PT P
PN CN113683112-A
TI Use of mineralized carbon dioxide in treating saponification slag, where mineralization of carbon dioxide is carried out preparing raw material slurry by mixing saponification slag with water and stirring to form raw material slurry.
AU XI X
   ZHAN J
   FENG H
AE SICHUAN POFESO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2021E1026E
AB    NOVELTY - Use of mineralized carbon dioxide is claimed in treating saponification slag.
   USE - Mineralized carbon dioxide used in treating saponification slag and producing gypsum building material and other products.
   ADVANTAGE - The mineralized carbon dioxide is environment-friendly, and its preparation allows to realize waste utilization, or combine the saponified slag with other materials for preparing forming brick, realize solidification of saponification slag and carbon dioxide, and reduce the pollution of the environment, which greatly increases the economic value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of calcium oxide in the saponification slag is not less than 20 wt.%. The mass ratio of the soaping slag to the water is 1:(2-10), and residence time of the gas is 50 to 500 seconds. The saponification slag has a particle size of 100 mum or less. The method particularly involves crushing a brick making raw material and a stabilizer, and mixing the raw material and the stabilizer in proportion to obtain a raw powder dry material, wherein the particle size of the raw powder dry material is 20-100 mum; adding water into the dry raw powder and introducing CO2, premixing and agitating the gas by stirring to obtain slurry, where the water content in the slurry is 10-18 wt.%, and the stirring time is more than or equal to 5 minute. The slurry is homogenized to obtain raw material for making brick for more than 30 minute, then CO2 is used during homogenizing the gas. The brick making raw materials are added into a mold and pouring to obtain a molded building block. The molded building block is subjected to demolding, pre-curing and cutting the formed building block to obtain formed brick. The water generated is returned and allowed to stand for premixing, further CO2 is added into the formed brick, cured in gas atmosphere at a curing pressure of 0.1MpaG or more for 1 hour or more, and CO2 content is more than or equal to 15 vol.%, and brick making raw material comprises saponification slag. The pre-curing of the formed block is carried out at a temperature of 20-40degrees Celsius, relative humidity is 50-75%, and water-solid ratio of the formed block after pre-curing is 3-8%. The shaped bricks are fed with CO2 and gas is introduced after the curing in the gas atmosphere into the premixing stage and the homogenizing treatment process.Preferred Process: The mineralizing of carbon dioxide involves preparing raw material slurry by mixing the saponification slag with water and uniformly stirring to form raw material slurry. The obtained CO2 gas is contacted with the raw material slurry to carry out mineralization reaction, residual gas exhaust gas is discharged, and the obtained liquid phase is absorption liquid, when the pH of the absorbent is not less than 10, then the absorbent returns to continue contact with the raw material slurry, and when the pH of the absorbent is less than 10, then the absorbent is discharged and the process proceeds to step of mixing the saponification slag with water. Preferred Composition: The brick-making raw materials further comprise 30-50 pts. wt. cement, 20-40 pts. wt. quicklime, 5-8 pts. wt. gypsum, 0.1-1 pts. wt. aluminum powder and 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. stabilizer.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)
MC E11-W; E31-N05C; L02-J02C; L02-C05; L02-A02; P43-U18; P43-E05
IP B09B-003/00; B09B-005/00; C01F-011/18; C04B-011/26; C04B-028/10; C04B-040/02

PT P
PN KR2021118305-A; KR2375453-B1
TI Carbon dioxide absorbent for packaging kimchi comprises gas permeable container and mixture of calcium hydroxide, sodium carbonate and water absorbent supported in container.
AU AN D S
   LEE D S
   JUNG S Y
   SEO J Y
   LEE S J
AE UNIV DONGGUK IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UYGG-C)
   UNIV KYUNGNAM IND-ACAD COOP FOUND (UKYI-C)
GA 2021B4716S
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorbent comprises gas permeable container and a mixture of calcium hydroxide, sodium carbonate and a water absorbent supported in the container.
   USE - The carbon dioxide absorbent is useful for packaging kimchi.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorbent maintains appropriate amount of carbon dioxide in the kimchi packaging during the entire fermentation period of packaged and distributed kimchi, effectively suppresses swelling and improves the carbonated taste of kimchi.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for packed kimchi, comprising kimchi loaded in the packaging container and the carbon dioxide absorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixture is an independent solid of calcium hydroxide, sodium carbonate and water absorbent. The water absorbent is disposed close to the calcium hydroxide in the container so that the calcium hydroxide absorbs the water generated by carbon dioxide absorption. The calcium hydroxide and sodium carbonate are contained in an amount that absorbs 60-80 vol.% carbon dioxide produced by kimchi at 5-10 degrees C. The calcium hydroxide and sodium carbonate are contained in an amount of 0.02-0.16 moles per 1 kg of kimchi. The molar ratio of calcium hydroxide and sodium carbonate is 1:1-3. The water absorbent is a super absorbent polymer and is contained in an amount of 0.1-3 g per 1 kg kimchi.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of experimental method that do not support the claims.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); ; J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PT P
PN EP3552688-A1; DE102018205635-A1; EP3552688-B1
TI Carbon capturing comprises introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide into liquid containing organic superbase to form carbon dioxide superbase adduct, and implementating superbase adduct with reactant to form reaction product.
AU BRUZZANO S
   LOHMANN H
   MOELDERS N
   PETERS S
   RENNER M
   ZEIDLER-FANDRICH B
   MOLDERS N
AE FRAUNHOFER GES FOERDERUNG ANGEWANDTEN EV (FRAU-C)
   FRAUNHOFER GES FOERDERUNG ANGEWANDTEN EV (FRAU-C)
GA 2019861237
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon capturing comprises: introducing a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide into a liquid containing an organic superbase or a salt of an organic superbase to form a carbon dioxide superbase adduct in which carbon dioxide is chemically bound, implementating carbon dioxide superbase adduct with a chemical reactant to form a reaction product.
   USE - The method is useful for carbon capturing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces pressure and temperature during the reaction, and can be carried out in simple cost efficient manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed gas contains sulfur oxides, nitrogen oxides, water vapor, ammonia, methane, carbon monoxide and/or a sulfur compound, in addition to carbon dioxide or nitrogen. The mixed gas containing carbon dioxide is flue gas, methane-containing gas from the biogas treatment, ammonia-containing waste gas from ammonia synthesis, a water vapor-containing gas obtained in the production of bioethanol or a mixed gas obtained in steel production. The organic superbase is amidine and/or guanidine derivative. The amidine is 1,8-diazobicyclo (5.4.0) undec-7-ene, 1,5-diazabicyclo(4.3.0)non-5-ene and/or N-methyltetrahydropyrimidines; and/or the guanidine derivative is a 1,1,3,3-tetra-C1-4-alkyl-guanidine, 1,5,7-triazobicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene, 7-methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene and/or N-(1,3-dimethylimidazolidin-2-ylidene) cyclohexylamine. Preferred Method: The liquid into which carbon dioxide-containing mixed gas is introduced contains an alcohol in addition to the superbase, and a solvent. The solvent is a cyclic amide or a cyclic urea compound. The formation of carbon dioxide superbase adduct, and its reaction with reactants are carried out in the same reactor, or the formation of carbon dioxide superbase adduct takes place in a first reactor. Carbon dioxide superbase adduct is then removed from the first reactor and introduced into a second reactor in which the reaction takes place with the chemical reactant. The reactant is already in the liquid during the formation of carbon dioxide superbase adduct; or carbon dioxide superbase adduct is formed in the absence of the reactant and is contacted with the reactant. The reactant reacted with which carbon dioxide superbase adduct, is amine, aminobenzonitrile, alkynol, epoxide, aziridine and/or reducing agent. Carbon dioxide superbase adduct is reacted with amine to form urea derivative. Carbon dioxide superbase adduct is reacted with an amine in the presence of a reducing agent to form an N-formylamine. Carbon dioxide superbase adduct is reacted with an epoxide to form a cyclic carbonate. The epoxide is a terpene epoxide and the cyclic carbonate is a terpenic carbonate. The superbase is added such that a superbase and epoxy-containing liquid is present and carbon dioxide-containing mixed gas is introduced into the superbase and epoxy-containing liquid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E06-D07; E06-D08; E07-D04A; E07-D09D; E07-D12; E10-A17B; E11-Q02B; E31-N05C; H06-A02; H06-X01; J01-E02B

TI Bi-reforming hydrocarbon involves reacting the            hydrocarbon with steam and carbon dioxide via reforming            catalyst to obtain synthesis gas comprising hydrogen            and carbon monoxide, where the reforming catalyst            comprises treated black powder comprising            hematite.
AU WEDHAYA A A
   BAMAGAIN R
   FADHEL B A
   ALBUALI M A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202287712Q
AB    NOVELTY - Bi-reforming hydrocarbon involves reacting the                hydrocarbon with steam and carbon dioxide via                reforming catalyst to obtain synthesis gas                comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, where the                reforming catalyst comprises treated black powder                comprising hematite.
   USE - Method for bi-reforming hydrocarbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reduce carbon dioxide emission                at the facility and decrease the carbon dioxide                footprint of the facility. The reforming catalyst                can reduce catalyst effectiveness and lower                conversion of the feed into syngas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. a method of preparing a reforming catalyst                for bi-reforming methane, which involves: receiving                black powder, applying heat to the black powder to                give heat-treated black powder, and applying heat                to the heat-treated black powder in presence of air                to obtain a calcined black powder for bi-reforming                of methane, where a majority of the calcined black                powder is hematite;
   2. a reforming catalyst, which comprises:                calcined black powder for bi-reforming methane with                steam and carbon dioxide, where the calcined black                powder is black powder heat treated at a                temperature of at least 500degrees Celsius for 3                hours and calcined in presence of air at a                temperature of 775degrees Celsius for 4 hours, the                black powder from a natural gas pipeline, and the                majority of the calcined black powder is hematite;                and
   3. a bi-reformer, which comprises: a                bi-reformer vessel (302) comprising inlet to                receive methane, carbon dioxide, and steam (308), a                reforming catalyst (304) comprising calcined black                powder to convert the methane, the carbon dioxide,                and the steam into syngas, and an outlet to                discharge the syngas, where the syngas (310)                comprises hydrogen and carbon monoxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                bi-reformer for bi-reforming hydrocarbon.
   Bi-reformer vessel (302)
   Reforming catalyst (304)
   Feed (306)
   Steam (308)
   Syngas (310)

The                  reforming catalyst comprising the calcined black                  powder comprises 50 wt.% hematite.Preferred Conditions: The step of applying                  heat to the black powder involves applying the                  heat at a temperature of 500degrees Celsius to                  obtain the heat-treated black powder. The step of                  applying heat to the heat-treated black powder                  involves applying the heat to the heat-treated                  black powder in the presence of air at a                  temperature of 775degrees Celsius to obtain the                  calcined black powder.

PN CN112479864-A
TI Preparing formate by catalyzing hydrogenation of carbon dioxide by N,N-bidentate metal complex in aqueous phase, comprises e.g. reacting N,N-bidentate metal complex solution and potassium bicarbonate solution in reaction kettle.
AU BAO M
   CHEN R
   WANG W
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2021306176
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing formate by catalyzing hydrogenation of carbon dioxide by N,N-bidentate metal complex in aqueous phase comprises injecting N,N-bidentate metal complex solution into 1-2 mol/litre potassium bicarbonate solution in high pressure reaction kettle at temperature of 50-120 degrees C, then introducing 1-5 MPa mixed gas of carbon dioxide/molecular hydrogen in volume ratio of 1:1, reacting for 8-24 hours and detecting the formation of formate by using liquid chromatography, where the N,N-bidentate metal complex is 8H-3a,7a,8a-triaza-cyclopenta(a)indene derivative (I).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing formate by catalyzing hydrogenation of carbon dioxide by N, N-bidentate metal complex in aqueous phase.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple method of preparing the series of metal complex catalyst; can quickly catalyze carbon dioxide hydrogenation to generate formate at a relatively suitable temperature and pressure; donot generates non-toxic by-products; involves carrying out catalytic reaction in the aqueous solution, which avoids the pollution caused by the use of organic solvents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing formate by catalyzing hydrogenation of carbon dioxide by N, N-bidentate metal complex in aqueous phase comprises injecting N,N-bidentate metal complex solution into 1-2 mol/litre potassium bicarbonate solution in high pressure reaction kettle at a temperature of 50-120 degrees C, then introducing 1-5 MPa mixed gas of carbon dioxide/molecular hydrogen in volume ratio of 1:1, reacting for 8-24 hours and detecting the formation of formate by using liquid chromatography, where the N,N-bidentate metal complex is 8H-3a,7a,8a-triaza-cyclopenta(a)indene derivative of formula (I).
   R1, R2, R3 = CH3, CH3O, OH, CH2OHNH2, N(CH3)2 or H;
   L1 = benzene, hexamethylbenzene, p-cymene, pentamethylcyclopentadienyl or cyclopentadienyl;
   L2 = H2O or Cl;
   M = Ir, Ru or Rh;
   W = chloride anion, sulfate, trifluoromethanesulfonate or tetrafluoroborate; and
   n = 0, 1 or 2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The N,N-bidentate metal complex (I) is prepared by (i) introducing N,N-bidentate ligand and the catalyst precursor into anhydrous methanol at substance ratio of 1-2:1, stirring the reaction for 9-12 hours at 60-80 degrees C under the protection of nitrogen, cooling to room temperature after the reaction, filtering the reaction solution, collecting the filtrate and drying under vacuum to remove anhydrous methanol, recrystallizing with dichloromethane, diethyl ether or isopropanol in sequence to obtain the water-soluble metal complex catalyst (A), where the concentration of the catalyst precursor is 0.01-0.03 mol/litre. The N,N-bidentate metal complex is prepared by (ii) adding N,N-bidentate ligand and catalyst precursor (Cp.Ir(H2O)3SO4, Ru( eta -C6H6)(H2O)3SO4, Cp.Rh (H2O)3SO4) into deoxygenated water at material ratio of 1-2:1, under the protection of nitrogen, stirring the reaction for 9-12 hours at a temperature of 60-80 degrees C, cooling to room temperature, filtering the reaction solution and collecting the filtrate, drying under vacuum to remove water, recrystallizing with dichloromethane, diethyl ether or isopropanol in sequence to obtain the water-soluble metal complex catalyst (B), where the concentration of catalyst precursor is 0.01-0.03 mol/litre. Preferred Components: The concentration of the N,N-bidentate metal complex (I) is 0.1-2 mmol/litre. The catalyst precursor is (Cp.IrCl2)2, (Ru( eta -C6H6)Cl2)2, (CpasteriskRhCl2)2.

AB    NOVELTY - A biogas purification system consists of an unit for producing biogas including methane, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen sulfide in an anaerobic digester, a biogas purification unit (10) for supplying the biogas to separate processing gas including hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide and methane, and supplying the gas to a biogas purification unit (20), an unit (20) for removing hydrogen sulfide from the processed gas using an adsorbent and discharging carbon dioxide, a reforming process unit (30) for producing syngas by using methane discharged from the units (10) and (20). The unit (10) consists of an absorption tower (101) for absorbing hydrogen sulfide, and a regeneration tower (102) for regenerating an absorbent for absorbing hydrogen sulfide. The purification unit (10) consists of carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide as a liquid absorbent. The absorbent comprises water, N-methyldiethanolamine. The adsorbent comprises a mixing agent, metal oxide, an additive, and a binder.
   USE - System used for purifying biogas (claimed) for producing fuel e.g. liquid fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The ultra-clean system economically maximizes removal of hydrogen sulfide in the biogas by using an absorbent and an adsorbent, and improves the efficiency of a conversion process of biogas into fuel, using a small-scale facility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for biogas purification, which involves producing biogas in an anaerobic digester, supplying the biogas to a unit (10), separating process gas including hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide with a liquid absorbent, and discharging methane and process gas, supplying the processed gas discharged from the unit (10) to an unit (20) to remove hydrogen sulfide with an adsorbent, and supplying methane discharged from the unit (10) and carbon dioxide discharged from the unit (20) to a reforming process unit to produce syngas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the biogas purification system.
   Biogas purification units (10,20)
   Reforming process department (30)
   Absorption tower (101)
   Regeneration tower (102)
   Rebuild (103)
   Adsorption tower (201)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The system further consists of Fisher-Tropsch (FT) reaction processing unit into which the syngas generated by the reforming processing unit is injected.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide is chosen from zinc oxide, iron oxide, cobalt oxide, copper oxide, nickel oxide, zirconium oxide, molybdenum oxide, vanadium oxide, tungsten oxide, titanium oxide, chromium oxide, silver oxide, manganese oxide, aluminum oxide, sodium oxide, lithium oxide, ruthenium oxide, rhodium oxide, potassium oxide, palladium oxide, and lithium cobalate. The additive is chosen from activated carbon, boehmite, zeolite, clay, alumina and silica.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The biogas generated by the units (10) and (20) comprises 10-70 wt.% methane, 1-30 wt.% carbon dioxide, and 100-30000 ppm hydrogen sulfide. The absorbent comprises 5-10 wt.% N-methyldiethanolamine, and 90-95 wt.% water. The weight ratio of the water to N-methyldiethanolamine is 8:1. Preferred Components: The mixture comprises 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, tetraethylenepentamine, diethylenetriamine, bis (3-aminopropyl)amine, 1,4 -diaminobutane, dibutylamine, 2-amino-1-butanol, 5-amino-1-pentanol, N-propylethylenediamine, 1-dimethylamino-2-propanol, 1-propanamine, N-propyl-propanamine, 1-butanamine, 2-methyl-1-propanamine, 2-butanamine, 1,2-dimethyl propylamine, 3-methyl-2-butanamine, hexylamine, tri-n-octylamine, allylamine, diallylamine, 3-methoxy propylamine, 3-ethoxyl propylamine, 3-propoxy propylamine, 3-isopropoxy propylamine, 3-butoxyl propylamine, 3-decyloxy propylamine, 3-lauryloxy propylamine, dimethylaminoethylamine, ethylaminoethylamine, 1,2-diaminopropane, 1,3-diamino propane, N,N-diethyl-1,3-propanediamine, dibutylaminopropylamine, bis-(3-aminopropyl)ether, 1,2-diaminobutane, and/or 1,4-diaminobutane. The binder comprises N-methyldiethanolamine. The adsorbent is in the form of a continuous cylinder. Preferred Process: The method further involves injecting the synthesis gas generated by the reforming process unit to the (Fisher-Tropsch) processing unit to produce fuel and compounds.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The binder is chosen from polyvinyl alcohol bentonite, methyl cellulose, saccharides, and cellulose-based polymer compounds.

TI Carbonated water dispenser used in e.g. home, has control unit which is configured to control water outlet so as to control overflow whole quantity of carbonated water generated in generation tank.
AU GODA R
   FUKUNAGA T
   ARATA Y
   NISHIKAWA K
   LEONA G
   TAKAYUKI F
   HIROKI A
   KOJI N
   GOHDA L
   NISHIKAWAKOJI
AE AIR WATER INC (AIAR-C)
   AIR WATER INC (AIAR-C)
GA 202083623G
AB    NOVELTY - The dispenser (10) has a drinking water supply unit (32) which supplies the drinking water (15) to the carbonated water generation tank (31). The carbon-dioxide-gas supply unit (33) supplies the carbon dioxide gas to the generation tank supplied with the drinking water. A water outlet is provided for discharging the carbonated water generated in the generation tank. A control unit controls drinking water supply unit, so as to supply an amount of drinking water according to the usage amount required by the user to generation tank, and controls carbon-dioxide-gas supply unit so as to supply carbon dioxide gas of quantity to generation tank according to supply amount of drinking water supplied from drinking water supply unit, amount of dissolution of carbon dioxide gas dissolved in drinking water and/or pressure inside generation tank. The control unit controls water outlet so as to control overflow whole quantity of carbonated water generated in generation tank.
   USE - Carbonated water dispenser used in home, restaurant, hospital and office.
   ADVANTAGE - The control unit controls the water outlet so as to control overflow whole quantity of carbonated water generated in the generation tank, so as to always supply clean carbonated water by suppressing the growth of bacteria as the carbonated water is not generated and stored in advance, so that the provision of the carbonated water dispenser excellent in hygiene can be possible. The carbonated water dispenser supplies a carbon dioxide gas according to the pressure inside a generation tank and generates carbonated water in timely manner, so as to easily adapted also to a regulation of high pressure gas safety law, while suppressing variations in the amount of dissolved carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of the whole structure of the carbonated water dispenser.
   Carbonated water dispenser (10)
   Drinking water (15)
   Carbonated water generation tank (31)
   Drinking water supply unit (32)
   Carbon di-oxide gas supply unit (33)

TI Use of poly(etherketoneketone) to reduce carbon            dioxide and hydrogen sulfide permeability of a part            e.g. tube, sheath or inner liner intended to come into            contact with a petroleum effluent.
AU BUSSI P
   BRULE B
   PAUL C
   BUCCI P
   BRUHL B
   POL C
AE ARKEMA FRANCE (AQOR-C)
   ARKEMA FRANCE (AQOR-C)
   ARKEMA FRANCE (AQOR-C)
   ARKEMA FRANCE (AQOR-C)
GA 201946799S
AB    NOVELTY - Use of poly(etherketoneketone) (PEKK) to                reduce carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide                permeability of a part intended to come into                contact with a petroleum effluent, is                claimed.
   USE - The PEKK is useful to reduce carbon dioxide                and hydrogen sulfide permeability of a part                intended to come into contact with a petroleum                effluent, where the part is a tube, a sheath or an                inner liner used for transport of a petroleum                effluent (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The PEKK: provides the tube with improved                tensile strength; provides a better protection of                the metallic elements of the corrosion induced by                the migration of gases; and reduces the thickness                of the sheaths, thus reducing the weight of the                pipe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) conduit for transport of a petroleum                effluent, comprising a layer intended to be in                contact with the petroleum effluent, where the                layer intended to be in contact with the petroleum                effluent comprises PEKK and has carbon dioxide                permeability at 130 degrees C of less than 10x 10-9                of carbon dioxide for a thickness of 1 cm and a                surface of 1 cm2/second and per bar of carbon                dioxide pressure, and the amount of carbon dioxide                is measured by gas chromatography; and
   (2) manufacturing the conduit, comprising                either: preparing a tube comprising PEKK of                appropriate size, and inserting the tube into a                metal tube to form a liner; (a) providing a metal                carcass, (b) extruding around the metal carcass at                least one layer of a composition comprising PEKK by                extrusion crosshead, (c) placing a vault around the                obtained structure; (d) laying at least one layer                of tensile armor, and (e) extruding an outer sheath                of polymer around the obtained assembly; or (a1)                extruding at least one layer of a composition                comprising PEKK by cross-head extrusion, and the                steps (c)-(e) as per se.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The PEKK: has a degree                  of crystallinity of greater than 5%; and has T:1                  ratio of 35-100%, preferably 60-80%. The carbon                  dioxide permeability at 130 degrees C is less                  than 10x 10-9 cm3 of carbon dioxide for a                  thickness of 1 cm and a surface of 1 cm2/second                  and per bar of carbon dioxide pressure, where the                  amount of carbon dioxide is measured by gas                  chromatography. The layer intended to be in                  contact with the petroleum effluent is: a sealing                  sheath surrounding a metal carcass; free of                  reinforcing fibers; and surrounded by at least                  one mechanical reinforcing layer. The mechanical                  reinforcing layer is made of metal, or composite                  material. The composite material comprises PEKK                  and carbon fibers. The conduit further comprises:                  a metal-rigid frame; and a flexible metal                  reinforcement.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109078500-A; CN109078500-B
TI Method for preparing fluoropolymer plate film, involves mixing fluoropolymer, porogen and dispersing agent, adding premix into fluoropolymer film, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide into closed container and depressurizing.
AU LIU F
   LIN H
   FAN D
   FAN Y
   ZHANG L
AE NINGBO LVSEFANGZHOU ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2019016654
AB    NOVELTY - A fluoropolymer plate film preparing method involves mixing a fluoropolymer, porogen and dispersing agent to obtain a premix. The premix is added into fluoropolymer film and clamped between the mold, the clamp with a film of mold is placed in sealed container at 180-360 degrees C. The supercritical carbon dioxide is introduced into the closed container to a pressure of 15-40 MPa and maintained at the pressure for a predetermined time T to uniformly distribute the supercritical carbon dioxide in the fluoropolymer film and depressurized the sealed container to foam supercritical carbon dioxide in the fluoropolymer film to obtain a fluoropolymer primary film, where the fluoropolymer primary film comprises multiples of first openings. The fluoropolymer primary film is immersed in an etching solution and the pore-forming agent in the fluorine-containing polymer film is etched to form multiples of second openings to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing fluoropolymer plate film.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A fluoropolymer plate film preparing method involves mixing a fluoropolymer, porogen and dispersing agent to obtain a premix. The premix is added into fluoropolymer film and clamped between the mold, the clamp with a film of mold is placed in sealed container at 180-360 degrees C. The supercritical carbon dioxide is introduced into the closed container to a pressure of 15-40 MPa and maintained at the pressure for a predetermined time T to uniformly distribute the supercritical carbon dioxide in the fluoropolymer film and depressurized the sealed container to foam supercritical carbon dioxide in the fluoropolymer film to obtain a fluoropolymer primary film, where the pressure of the sealed container is greater than or equal to 8 MPa/s and fluoropolymer primary film comprises multiples of first openings. The fluoropolymer primary film is immersed in an etching solution and the pore-forming agent in the fluorine-containing polymer film is etched to form multiples of second openings to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porogen comprises inorganic carbonate and/or water soluble polymer, where the inorganic carbonate is calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate and cesium carbonate and the water-soluble polymer is polyoxyethylene, polyvinyl alcohol and polyvinylpyrrolidone. The etching solution is acidic solution or water, where the acidic solution is sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, phosphoric acid, nitric acid, acetic acid and citric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersing agent is stearic acid amide, stearate, oleic acid amide, erucyl amide and ethylene bisstearamide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fluoropolymer is polyvinylidene fluoride and/or polyperfluoroethylene propylene.

The lifting protective cover is also connected with a carbon dioxide monitoring instrument. The temperature and humidity monitoring instrument, the carbon dioxide monitoring instrument and an external control device electrically connected by a bolt.
   USE - The intelligent detection and early warning device is useful for compost fermentation.
