PN CN111617752-A
TI Regeneration method of waste activated carbon used            for water treatment, by sending dried waste activated            carbon into industrial microwave oven by transmission            belt, adjusting microwave heating frequency, and            regeneration carbonizing.
AU LIU K
   WEI D
   ZHENG L
   LU B
   LI W
   WEI C
AE JIANGSU SHUANGYOU ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202090298C
AB    NOVELTY - Regeneration method of waste activated carbon                involves stirring and washing waste activated                carbon by clean water, ensuring basically invisible                other impurities in cleaned waste activated carbon,                settling, filtering and naturally drying, putting                dried wet waste activated carbon into blast drying                box, setting temperature interval, sending dried                waste activated carbon into industrial microwave                oven by transmission belt, adjusting microwave                heating frequency, adjusting heating time of                material, performing waste activated carbon                regeneration carbonization treatment, putting into                reaction kettle, adding activated liquid comprising                clean water, dilute sulfuric acid, dilute                hydrochloric acid and zinc chloride into reaction                kettle, sealing reaction kettle, adding carbon                dioxide gas through pipeline, starting regenerated                activated carbon activation process, filtering                activated carbon, washing using clean water,                drying, and packaging.
   USE - The method is used for regenerating waste                activated carbon used for water treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces loss of material in                regeneration process, greatly improves activation                effect by adjusting ratio of regeneration liquid                and partial pressure of carbon dioxide, ensures                uniform heating of material and qualified rate of                final product, and improves regeneration rate and                service life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Regeneration method of waste activated carbon                involves stirring and washing waste activated                carbon by clean water, ensuring basically invisible                other impurities in cleaned waste activated carbon,                settling, filtering and naturally drying to water                content of 40-50%, putting dried wet waste                activated carbon into blast drying box, setting                temperature interval as 60-80 degrees C, sending                dried waste activated carbon into industrial                microwave oven by transmission belt, adjusting                microwave heating frequency to 50-200 MHz,                adjusting heating time of material for 60-300                minutes, performing waste activated carbon                regeneration carbonization treatment, putting into                reaction kettle, adding activated liquid comprising                clean water, dilute sulfuric acid, dilute                hydrochloric acid and zinc chloride into reaction                kettle, sealing reaction kettle, adding carbon                dioxide gas through pipeline, starting regenerated                activated carbon activation process, filtering                activated carbon, washing using clean water 2-3                times to neutral, drying to 80-100 degrees C, and                packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Activation is done at                  180-270 degrees C for 5-10 hours at kettle total                  pressure of 0.8-2 MPa and carbon dioxide partial                  pressure of 0.4-1.2 MPa. Preferred Components:                  The liquid-solid (volume mass) ratio of activated                  liquid to activated carbon to be activated in                  kettle is 3-6:1. The concentration of sulfuric                  acid in activated liquid is 0.01-0.05 mol/l. The                  concentration of hydrochloric acid is 0.01-0.05                  mol/l. The concentration of zinc chloride is                  0.1-0.5 g/l.

PN CN111303945-A; CN212560126-U
TI Low-temperature methanol washing process device has desorption tower which includes cooler, gas-liquid separation tank and liquid phase reflux and top of methanol-water separation tower is connected to desorption tower.
AU YANG S
   CHEN J
   QIAN Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 202058067B
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide methanol-rich flash evaporator whose gas-phase outlet is connected to the synthesizer circulation compressor. A portion of the liquid phase is connected to the top of the decarburization tower, and the other portion is connected to the hydrogen sulfide concentration tower. The hydrogen sulfide concentration tower kettle is connected to the desorption tower through heat exchanger. The top of desorption tower is provided with a reflux flow path, which includes a cooler, a gas-liquid separation tank and liquid phase reflux. The gas phase is further heat-exchanged and flashed, and the liquid phase is connected to the top of the hydrogen sulfide concentration tower. A portion of the tower kettle of desorption tower is connected to the decarburization tower through a heat exchange, and a portion is connected to the methanol-water separation tower. The top of the methanol-water separation tower is connected to the desorption tower.
   USE - Low-temperature methanol washing process device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device with low energy consumption and high carbon capture rate is provided. The method uses the coupling of the cooling capacity of liquefied natural gas and the carbon dioxide liquefaction process of low-temperature methanol washing, thereby improving the purity of captured carbon dioxide, reducing the methanol consumption of the original low-temperature methanol washing process, reducing the cooling capacity of the low-temperature methanol washing process and compressing power consumption. The method achieves higher resource utilization, reduces carbon emissions, and reduces the use of lean methanol by 15-45%, reduces overall energy consumption by 10-50%, and increases the carbon capture rate by 20-50 %. The minor equipment changes reduce the cost of factory adjustment processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process method for low-temperature methanol washing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a device illustrating a low-temperature methanol washing process. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

A power is provided from the power supply to the wire element that heats, which in turn heats portion of the change portion. The change is caused from the first state where the spring is in the coiled, compressed state, to the second state, where the spring can uncoil into the sprung state. The action of the sprung spring on an inflation apparatus (1) leads to inflation of the lifejacket. The inflation apparatus is used with a carbon dioxide (CO2) gas canister (10).

The inflation can usually be filling with a gas lighter than water so that the person wearing the lifejacket is maintained on the water surface, awaiting rescue. The inflation device is far more sophisticated which can only occur when really needed, and on extended contact with the water.

PN CN110028403-A; CN110028403-B
TI Preparing succinic acid compound comprises e.g. adding substrate, photocatalyst and alkali into reaction vessel, adding reducing agent and solvent under carbon dioxide atmosphere, and irradiating reaction solution using visible light.
AU YU D
   LI J
   JU T
   ZHOU Y
   FU Q
   YE J
   LIAO L
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201965133W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing succinic acid compound comprises adding substrate, photocatalyst and alkali into reaction vessel at a molar ratio of 1:0.005-0.5:1-20, then adding reducing agent and solvent under carbon dioxide atmosphere, stirring the reaction at room temperature for 2-48 hours, then irradiating the reaction solution using visible light having wavelength of 400-550 nm during the reaction, quenching the reaction using 2N dilute hydrochloric acid, separating and purifying the reaction mixture to obtain succinic acid compound, where molar ratio between reducing agent and substrate is 1-20:1, substrate is 1,1-diarylethene compound (I), monoaryl substituted olefin compound (II), acrylates (III) or 4-(butyl-2,3-dien-2-yl)-1,1'-biphenyl (IV).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing succinic acid compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has mild condition; solves the problem of high toxicity and vigorous reaction conditions; and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing succinic acid compound comprises adding substrate, photocatalyst and alkali into reaction vessel at a molar ratio of 1:0.005-0.5:1-20, then adding reducing agent and solvent under carbon dioxide atmosphere stirring the reaction at room temperature for 2-48 hours, then irradiating the reaction solution using visible light having wavelength of 400-550 nm during the reaction, quenching the reaction using 2N dilute hydrochloric acid, separating and purifying the reaction mixture to obtain succinic acid compound, where molar ratio between reducing agent and substrate is 1-20:1, substrate is 1,1-diarylethene compound of formula (I), monoaryl substituted olefin compound of formula (II), acrylates of formula (III) or 4-(butyl-2,3-dien-2-yl)-1,1'-biphenyl of formula (IV).
   A = not defined;
   R1 = H, alkyl or halo group;
   R2 = H, aryl, substituted aryl, alkyl, substituted alkyl, hydrocarbyloxy, mercapto, hydroxy, ester or halo group;
   R3 = H or alkyl group;
   R4 = H or alkyl group; and
   R5 and R6 = hydrocarbon group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalyst is 2,4,5,6-tetra(9H-carbazol-9-yl)isophthalonitrile, (4,4'-di-tert-butyl-2,2'-bipyridine)bis((2-pyridinyl)phenyl)iridium(III) hexafluorophosphate, (4,4'-di-tert-butyl-2,2'-bipyridine)bis((2-pyridinyl)phenyl)iridium(III) hexafluorophosphate, fac-tris(2-phenylpyridine)iridium(III), or tris(2,22-bipyrazine)ruthenium bis(hexafluorophosphate). The alkali is caesium carbonate, lithium tert-butoxide, sodium tert-butoxide, potassium tert-butoxide, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate and/or caesium fluoride. The reducing agent is tetramethylethylenediamine, helium hydride, N,N-diisopropylethylamine or triethylamine. The photocatalyst is 2,4,5,6-tetra(9H-carbazol-9-yl)isophthalonitrile; alkali is caesium carbonate; molar ratio between substrate, photocatalyst and alkali is 1:0.02:4.5. The reducing agent is N,N-diisopropylethylamine, where molar ratio between reducing agent and substrate is 1:1. The solvent is dimethylformamide, dimethylacetamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, acetonitrile and/or methanol. Preferred Method: The method comprises irradiating the reactant using visible light having wavelength of 450 nm during the reaction, and stirring the reaction for 24 hours.

PT P
PN CN110003006-A
TI Preparation of 7-position side chain of oxycephalosporin involves mixing 4-(4-methoxybenzyloxy)phenylacetic acid-4-methoxybenzyl ester and organic solvent, adding potassium t-butoxide, reacting, and introducing carbon dioxide.
AU WANG Z
AE SHANXI QIANXIU PHARM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201963225B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of 7-position side chain of oxycephalosporin comprises uniformly mixing 4-(4-methoxybenzyloxy)phenylacetic acid-4-methoxybenzyl ester and organic solvent in reaction vessel to obtain reaction liquid; adding potassium t-butoxide to reaction liquid; reacting for 2 hours; introducing carbon dioxide into reaction liquid; adding saturated sodium chloride solution; stirring; adjusting pH of reaction liquid to 1.0-1.5 with concentrated hydrochloric acid; standing; extracting layer of organic solvent phase; adding lower aqueous phase with organic solvent; extracting again layer of organic solvent phase; combining organic solvent phases; distilling resulting organic solvent phase under vacuum; adding with methanol while stirring; controlling temperature at 10-15 degrees C; filtering out crystals; washing crystals with methanol; and drying to obtain 7-position side chain of oxycephalosporin, i.e. 4-(4-methoxybenzyloxy)benzenemalonate-4-methoxybenzyl monoester.
   USE - The method is for preparation of 7-position side chain of oxycephalosporin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple steps, cheap and easy to obtain raw materials, low preparation cost, short reaction route, high molar yield, easy for industrialization, and produces product with of less than or equal to 99% based on high-performance liquid chromatography.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of preparing 7-position side chain of oxycephalosporin comprises adding 4-(4-methoxybenzyloxy)phenylacetic acid-4-methoxybenzyl ester and organic solvent to reaction vessel; uniformly stirring to completely dissolved to obtain reaction liquid; adding potassium t-butoxide to reaction liquid; reacting for 2 hours; introducing carbon dioxide into reaction liquid; adding saturated sodium chloride solution; stirring; adjusting pH of reaction liquid to 1.0-1.5 with concentrated hydrochloric acid; standing; extracting layer of organic solvent phase; adding lower aqueous phase with organic solvent; extracting again layer of organic solvent phase; combining organic solvent phases; distilling resulting organic solvent phase under vacuum; adding with methanol while stirring; controlling temperature at 10-15 degrees C; filtering out crystals; washing crystals with methanol; and drying to obtain 7-position side chain of oxycephalosporin, i.e. 4-(4-methoxybenzyloxy)benzenemalonate-4-methoxybenzyl monoester.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction is performed at - 35 to - 45 degrees C. Preferred Component: The organic solvent is dimethylformamide, dimethylacetamide, or tetrahydrofuran. The mol ratio of 4-(4-methoxybenzyloxy)phenylacetic acid-4-methoxybenzyl ester, potassium t-butoxide, and carbon dioxide is 1:(1.2-10):(2-15), preferably 1:2:5.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)
MC B10-C03
IP C07C-067/313; C07C-069/736

PT P
PN WO2019104383-A1; AU2018377854-A1; CA3083377-A1; KR2020106157-A; CN111655044-A; EP3716786-A1; SG11202004923-A1; IN202017026818-A; VN73697-A; US2021002155-A1; EP3716786-A4; US11390543-B2
TI Inactivating microorganism in aqueous solution for            sterilizing aqueous solution by passing bubbles of gas            through aqueous solution where gas comprises carbon            dioxide and has predetermined temperature when gas            contacts the aqueous solution.
AU PASHLEY R M
   SANCHIS A G
   NINHAM B
AE NEWSOUTH INNOVATIONS PTY LTD (NEWS-C)
   NEWSOUTH INNOVATIONS PTY LTD (NEWS-C)
   NEWSOUTH INNOVATIONS PTY LTD (NEWS-C)
   NEWSOUTH INNOVATIONS PTY LTD (NEWS-C)
   NEWSOUTH INNOVATIONS PTY LTD (NEWS-C)
GA 201950435R
AB    NOVELTY - A microorganism in aqueous solution is                inactivated by passing bubbles of gas through                aqueous solution where gas comprises greater than                or equal to 10 vol.% carbon dioxide (CO2) and has a                temperature of greater than or equal to 18 degrees                C when gas first contacts the aqueous                solution.
   USE - Inactivation of microorganism in aqueous                solution for sterilizing aqueous solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses a combustion gas which has                been treated as a waste product. Trace gases, such                as carbon monoxide, hydrogen gas (H2), sulfur                dioxide (SO2) and nitrogen oxides may have biocidal                actions contributing to the inactivation of                microorganisms in the aqueous solution. The                presence of water vapor in combustion gasses                reduces the bubble column evaporative cooling                effect, allowing the combustion gas to heat the                aqueous solution to a greater extent than another                gas having a lower amount of water vapor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) system (100) for inactivating                microorganisms present in aqueous solution,                comprising gas supply which supplies gas comprising                greater than or equal to 10 vol.% carbon dioxide                (CO2) in which the gas has temperature of greater                than or equal to 18 degrees C; and gas-delivery                apparatus which receives the supply of gas and                delivers gas into the aqueous solution in the form                of bubbles; and
   (2) apparatus for inactivating microorganisms                present in aqueous solution, comprising                gas-permeable material having a flow surface at one                side that enables the flow of aqueous solution                across the flow surface, and chamber which is                arranged at an opposite side of gas-permeable                material to supply a gas that inactivates the                microorganisms present in aqueous solution such                that the gas is able to pass through the                gas-permeable material and into aqueous solution                flowing across the flow surface as gas                bubbles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system/apparatus.
   System (100)
   Gas source (101)
   1st tap (102)
   Air flow meter (103)
   Gas heater (104)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microorganism is                  algae, protozoa, fungi, spore, virus or bacteria,                  preferably virus or bacteria. Preferred Process:                  The bubbles are passed through aqueous solution                  while the aqueous solution is exposed to                  atmospheric pressure. The bubbles are formed by                  passing the gas through a porous material in                  contact with the aqueous solution. Preferred                  Parameters: The bubbles have a diameter of 0.1-7                  mm. The gas has temperature of greater than or                  equal to 100 degrees C, preferably 18-100 degrees                  C. The aqueous solution has a bulk temperature of                  18-80 degrees C, preferably 18-55 degrees                  C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas comprises                  50-100 vol.% CO2 or 10-50 vol.% CO2. The gas                  bubbles occupy 10-60 vol.% of the total volume of                  combination of aqueous solution and bubbles as                  the bubbles pass through the aqueous solution.                  Preferred Device: The gas-delivery apparatus is a                  bubble column evaporator. The gas supply                  comprises a heater to heat a gas to form the gas                  having a temperature of greater than or equal to                  18 degrees C. The gas supply further comprises a                  gas source that provides gas that comprises                  greater than or equal to 10 vol.% CO2. The flow                  surface is in the form of a channel. The flow                  surface is configured such that gas bubbles pass                  through the aqueous solution in a direction                  transverse to the direction of flow of the                  aqueous solution as it flows across the flow                  surface. The gas-permeable material is selected                  such that gas bubbles passing into the aqueous                  solution have a diameter of 0.1-7 mm when the                  aqueous solution is under 0.9-1.5 bar                  pressure.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); Q78 (Heat exchange in general (F28))
MC D04-A01J; D04-A02; E12-B02; E31-N05C; Q78-A04; Q78-U17

PT P
PN CN109628204-A
TI Preparing extracted aroma type tea seed oil            involves adding tea seed meal to nutrient water,            filtering, obtaining the filtrate and the tea seed meal            after soaking, and standby, and then preparing tea seed            meal enzymatic hydrolysate.
AU ZHENG Z
   KONG X
   YUAN M
   MA Q
   ZHENG Y
AE JINHUA ACAD AGRIC SCI (JINH-Non-standard)
   ZHEJIANG JIANGKANG AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019385767
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing extracted aroma type tea seed oil                involves adding tea seed meal to nutrient water for                1.5 hours, filtering, obtaining the filtrate and                the tea seed meal after soaking, and standby. The                tea seed meal enzymatic hydrolysate is prepared by                taking the tea seed meal soaked in the nutrient                water, coarsely pulverizing by using a tissue                crusher, and then grinding and crushing by using                colloid. After passing through a 90 mesh sieve,                adding the filtrate, stirring uniformly, adding                alkaline protease, adjusting the pH to 9.0, the                temperature is 55 degrees C, enzymatic hydrolysis                for 4 hours. The enzyme is cooled at room                temperature and centrifuged at 4900 r/min for 24                minutes.
   USE - Method for preparing extracted aroma type tea                seed oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare extracted aroma                type tea seed oil with high quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The supernatant is obtained and 15% of the                weight of glucose is added to the tea seed meal                hydrolysate, and the mixture is uniformly stirred,                and then the glycerin is added dropwise, and the                autoclave is placed at a pH of 8. The reaction                speed is 490r/min, the temperature is 155 degrees C                for 4 hours, and the cooling treatment is obtained.                The tea seed oil is placed in the extraction                kettle, and injecting the carbon dioxide fluid into                the kettle body by using a high pressure pump,                dissolving the odorous substance in the tea seed                oil in the fluid, and then converting the carbon                dioxide fluid into a gaseous state by                decompression, extracting the separated tea seed                oil and carbon dioxide gas state; and putting the                extracted tea seed oil into the centrifuge,                removing water and impurities by centrifugation to                obtain extracted tea seed oil, adding the obtained                raw liquid to the tea seed oil to obtain                concentrated tea seed oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The mass-to-volume                  ratio of the tea seed meal to the nutrient water                  is 1:10. The volume ratio of the glycerin to the                  enzymatic hydrolysate is 1:1100. The amount of                  the raw liquid is 0.1-0.2% of the tea seed oil.                  Preferred Components: The nutrient water is                  composed 1 pts. wt. Aureobasidium polysaccharide,                  4 pts. wt. sweet-scented Osmanthus, 5 pts. wt.                  mint and 1000 pts. wt. water.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-A02C; D05-C19; D05-H13; D10-A01; D10-A02
IP C11B-001/02; C11B-001/04; C11B-003/00; C11B-003/02

PT P
PN CN109369405-A
TI Processing plant peroxidase-based acidic ionic liquid aromatic ring nitration method comprises e.g. mixing polyethylene glycol, pyridine and toluene heating, dropping thionyl chloride, continuously heating, reacting and cooling.
AU WANG Z
   XU M
   WU R
   LI D
   CHEN J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY SHANGYU INST CO (UYZT-C)
GA 2019214700
AB    NOVELTY - Processing plant peroxidase-based acidic ionic liquid aromatic ring nitration method comprises e.g. (i) mixing polyethylene glycol, pyridine and toluene heating, dropping thionyl chloride, continuously heating and reacting, cooling, (ii) heating sodium alcoholate and imidazole reaction, adding obtained chlorinated modified polyethylene glycol, preserving temperature and reacting, taking the upper liquid, adding 1,3-propane sulfonic acid lactone, subjecting to cooling reaction, adding acetyl nitrate and nitric acid, heating, reacting, washing, filtering to obtain acidic ionic liquid, (iii) adding the solution of the plant peroxidase and carbon nanotubes to acidic ionic liquid, and mixing, and (iv) adding aromatic ring solution to reaction vessel, introducing carbon dioxide, heating, pressurizing, uniformly mixing, heating ionic liquid containing plant peroxidase and reacting.
   USE - The method is useful for processing plant peroxidase-based acidic ionic liquid aromatic ring nitration method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing plant peroxidase-based acidic ionic liquid aromatic ring nitration method comprises (i) mixing polyethylene glycol, pyridine and toluene heating, dropping thionyl chloride, continuously heating and reacting, cooling to room temperature, stopping the reaction by separating and extractive distillation to obtain chlorinated modified polyethylene glycol, (ii) heating sodium alcoholate and the imidazole reaction, adding the obtained chlorinated modified polyethylene glycol, preserving temperature and reacting, taking the upper liquid, adding 1,3-propane sulfonic acid lactone, subjecting to cooling reaction, adding acetyl nitrate and nitric acid, continuously heating and reacting, washing and filtering to obtain acidic ionic liquid, (iii) adding the solution of the plant peroxidase and the carbon nanotubes to the acidic ionic liquid prepared in the step (ii), and uniformly mixing to obtain an acidic ionic liquid containing the plant peroxidase, and (iv) adding the aromatic ring solution to the reaction vessel, introducing carbon dioxide, heating and pressurizing to make the carbon dioxide to a subcritical state, uniformly mixing, heating the ionic liquid containing the plant peroxidase prepared in the step (iii), and reacting to obtain aromatic ring nitration derivative.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the ratio of polyethylene glycol, pyridine, toluene and thionyl chloride is 1 g:1-1.2 ml: 3.5-3.6 ml: 0.9-1.1 ml. In the step (ii), the molar ratio of sodium ethoxide to imidazole is 1:1. In the step (iii), the plant peroxidase is horseradish peroxidase or cytochrome C peroxidase. In the step (iii), the plant peroxidase-containing acidic ionic liquid has a plant peroxidase content of 15-20 mg/ml, and the carbon nanotube content is 0.1-0.5 wt%. In the step (iv), the aromatic ring is benzene, chlorobenzene, pyridine or diaminopyridine. In the step (iv), the volume ratio of the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid to the ionic liquid is 3-5:1-2. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the heating reaction is carried out at 85-90 degrees C for 10-15 hours. In the step (ii), the heating reaction process comprises firstly, the temperature is raised to 70-75 degrees C at 5-10 degrees C/minutes, the chlorinated modified polyethylene glycol is added, and the reaction is incubated at 70-75 degrees C for 6-8 hours, the upper liquid was added to the 1,3-propane sulfonate lactone, the temperature was lowered to 50-55 degrees C, acetyl nitrate and nitric acid were added, and the reaction was further heated at 50-55 degrees C for 2-4 hours. In the step (iv), the heating and pressurizing process comprises firstly, the carbon dioxide is subcritical state at a temperature of 30-31 degrees C and a pressure of 6.9-7.1 MPa, and the ionic liquid containing the plant peroxidase is introduced. Continue to raise the temperature to 45-50 degrees C, pressurize to 10-13 MPa, and reacting for 1-3 hours.

TI Three-dimensional urchin/porous composite structure lithium ion battery copper/copper oxide/tin dioxide/carbon anode comprises three-dimensional nano multi-aperture copper, copper oxide film, tin dioxide and carbon dioxide.
AU LIU W
   CHENG P
   CHEN X
   YAN J
   LI N
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201914504M
AB    NOVELTY - Three-dimensional urchin/porous composite structure lithium ion battery copper/copper oxide/tin dioxide/carbon anode comprises three-dimensional nano multi-aperture copper, copper oxide film, tin dioxide and carbon dioxide. The oxide film is continuous film formed by three-dimensional nanoporous copper surface partial oxidation. The copper oxide film encloses three-dimensional nano multi-aperture copper. The tin dioxide is covered by carbon dioxide coated on copper membrane-encapsulated three-dimensional nano copper. The carbon-coated tin dioxide layer has sea urchin structure.
   USE - Three-dimensional urchin/porous composite structure lithium ion battery copper/copper oxide/tin dioxide/carbon anode.
   ADVANTAGE - The anode can simplify production process of negative electrode of lithium ion battery, avoid active component falling off during the charging and discharging process of lithium ion battery, and improve cycle performance and rate performance of negative electrode of lithium ion battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the anode comprising polishing copper-aluminum alloy sheet, washing with deionized water, performing dealloying treatment to remove aluminum in copper-aluminum alloy to control dealloying temperature to 50-90 degrees C to complete dealloying treatment, washing with deionized water and ethanol, and vacuum drying to obtain three-dimensional nanoporous copper, where atomic percentage of copper and aluminum in copper-aluminum alloy sheet is 15-50:100-(15-50); soaking three-dimensional nanoporous copper in sodium hydroxide-terephthalic acid-stannous sulfate mixed aqueous solution, stirring at 50-90 degrees C for 1-12 hours, stirring at room temperature for 1-12 hours, and growing tin-based metal organic framework in situ on surface of three-dimensional nanoporous copper during stirring reaction to obtain precursor; and calcining precursor at 300-600 degrees C for 1-8 hours in mixed atmosphere of nitrogen and air during roasting process, and partially oxidizing surface of three-dimensional nanoporous copper to form continuous copper oxide film.

TI Producing adipic acid derivatives, comprises contacting alkene compounds with carbon dioxide in a polar aprotic solvent, in the presence of nickel(ll) salt catalyst, reducing means e.g. metal and photochemical reducing means, and ligand.
AU MARTIN ROMO R F
   TORTAJADA NAVARRO A
AE FUNDACIO INST CATALA DINVESTIGACIO QUIMI (CATA-Non-standard)
   ICREA INST CATALANA RECERCA I ESTUDIS AV (ICRE-Non-standard)
GA 201887804P
AB    NOVELTY - Producing adipic acid derivatives (I), comprises contacting alkene (II) with carbon dioxide in a polar aprotic solvent, in the presence of (a) nickel(ll) salt catalyst, (b) reducing means comprising a metal, an electrochemical reducing means and a photochemical reducing means, and (c) a ligand comprising (2,2')bipyridinyl derivatives (L).
   USE - The process is useful for producing adipic acid derivatives.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides adipic acid derivatives with high yield and improved selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing adipic acid derivatives of formula (I) and their salts, stereoisomers or mixtures, comprises contacting alkene derivatives of formula (II) with carbon dioxide in a polar aprotic solvent, in the presence of (a) nickel(ll) salt catalyst, (b) reducing means comprising a metal, an electrochemical reducing means and a photochemical reducing means, and (c) a ligand comprising (2,2')bipyridinyl derivatives of formula (L), the process further comprises transforming adipic acid derivatives of formula (Ia) in (Ib).
   n = 0 or 1; and
   R1-R6 = H, (1-12C)alkyl or a ring system comprising 1-4 optionally saturated, or aromatic rings, where the rings are isolated, optionally fused, having 3-8 C, CH, CH2, N, NH, O or S, and are optionally substituted by at least one halo, (1-6C)alkyl, (1-6C)haloalkyl, (1-6C)alkylcarbonyl, (1-6C)alkyloxy, (1-6C)alkyloxycarbonyl, (1-6C)alkylcarbonyloxy, formyl, CN or nitro; either
   X1, Y1 = H, halo or OH; or
   CX1Y1 = optionally saturated ring comprising 3-6 members comprising -C-, -CH- or -CH2-, where the ring is optionally substituted by at least one halo, (1-6C)alkyl, (1-6C)haloalkyl, (1-6C)alkylcarbonyl, (1-6C)alkyloxy, (1-6C)alkyloxycarbonyl, (1-6C)alkylcarbonyloxy, formyl, CN or nitro;
   R7, R7a, R9, R9a = H, (1-12C)alkyl or (1-12C)haloalkyl; either
   R10, R10a = H, (1-12C)alkyl, (1-12C)haloalkyl or (1-12C)alkyloxy; or
   CR10R10a = phenyl; and

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Preferred Process: The step of transforming comprises submitting (Ia) to a catalytic hydrogenation reaction. The contacting step is further carried out in the presence of a halide salt which is soluble in the aprotic polar solvent, where the halide salt is a bromide salt i.e. tetrabutylammonium bromide. The contacting is carried out a pressure of carbon dioxide at 0.8-1 in a polar aprotic solvent including N-methylpyrollidinone, N,N-dimethylformamide and N,N-dimethylacetamide, at a temperature comprised of 40-70 degrees C, preferably 50 degrees C. Preferred Components: The step of reducing means is metal, preferably manganese and an electrochemical reducing means or metal, and a photochemical reducing means including a photosensitizer, a light source, and electron donor compound. The nickel (II) salt is nickel (II) chloride, nickel (II) bromide and their solvates with 1,2-dimethoxyethane, preferably tetrabromonickelate salt or tretrabutylammonium tetrabromonickelate. The ligand (L) is 4,7-diphenyl-(1,10)phenanthrolin-2-yl. The molar ratio of the nickel (II) salt and the reducing means is comprised of 1:1-1:2. The molar ratio of the nickel (II) salt and ligand (L) is 1:1.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - Preparation of 10 Compounds (I) are disclosed e.g. (E)-2-phenyl-hex-3-enedioic acid of formula (Ia), (Z)-2-phenyl-hex-3-enedioic acid, (E)-2-(4-isopropyl-phenyl)-hex-3-enedioic acid, (Z)-2-(4-isopropyl-phenyl)-hex-3-enedioic acid and (E)-2-(4-methoxy-phenyl)-hex-3-enedioic acid.
   EXAMPLE - Oven-dried Schlenk tube containing a stirring bar was charged with 22 mg manganese, 6-methyl-2,2'-bipyridine and nickel (II) chloride. The tube was connected to a vacuum line where it was evacuated and backfilled under a carbon dioxide flow at least three times. The 1,3-diene and the solvent were added into the tube under carbon dioxide flow. Once added, the Schlenk tube was closed under carbon dioxide (1 atm) and stirred for 40 hours. The reaction mixture was then quenched with 2 M hydrochloric acid to hydrolyze the resultant carboxylate and extracted 3 times with ethyl acetate. The combined organic phases were washed with brine and dried over magnesium sulfate and concentrated under reduce pressure. Fluorene was added as a standard and an aliquot was taken, the solvent was removed and residue was dissolved to give (E)-2-phenyl-hex-3-enedioic acid (yield 48%).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of plus minus - alpha -lipoic acid, acetyl L-carnitine or acetyl L-carnitine hydrochloride, and N-acetyl-L-cysteine is 0.065-0.098:0.054-0.078:0.752-0.858. The basic culture solution comprises inorganic salt buffer, an amino acid, EDTA, sodium pyruvate, alanyl glutamine, sulfonic acid, glucose, D-sorbitol, human serum albumin and bactericides in a ratio of 98.303-120.12:1.2-3.8:0.009-0.012:9.459-11.561:24.741-30.239:0.432-0.528:0.9-1.1:0.207-0.254. The inorganic salt buffer solution comprises sodium chloride, potassium chloride, magnesium sulfate, calcium chloride, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, potassium dihydrogen phosphate and sodium hydrogencarbonate in a molar ratio of 89.937-109.923:5-6.083:0.9-1.1:1.62-1.98:0.135-0.165:0.135-0.165:0.576-0.704. The condition of the equilibration in the incubator includes equilibration in carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C and 6% for 5-10 hours, and pH is 7.20-7.40.

AB    NOVELTY - The on-line dry ice cleaning system has a dry ice sending module (10), a cleaning module (20) and a recovery module (30) connected in sequence which are controlled by a controller (40). The dry ice sending module includes a dry ice sending station (11). A dry ice sending pipe (12) is connected to the output end of the dry ice sending station. A first transfer pump (121) is provided in the dry ice sending pipe. The cleaning module includes a cleaning target structure (21), a cleaning circulation pipe (22), and an input end of the cleaning target structure is connected to the dry ice sending pipe. The recovery module includes a retrievable material station (31), and an input end of the retrievable material station is connected to a cleaning output pipe (23). The top of the dry ice sending station, the top of the cleaning target structure, and the top of the retrievable material station are all connected with a carbon dioxide recovery pipe (50).
   USE - On-line dry ice cleaning system for liquid materials such as storage tanks and pipelines.
   ADVANTAGE - The on-line dry ice cleaning system for liquid materials can realize the function of on-line cleaning, the cleaning operation is simple and convenient, the cleaning efficiency is high, the cleaning effect is good, and the cleaned material can be collected and used at the same time.

AB    NOVELTY - Inducing spinal cord motor nerve precursor                cell to differentiate into spinal motor neuron                involves digesting spinal cord motor nerve                precursor cell into a single cell and inoculating                into coated culture dishes for adherent culture.                The culture liquid is spinal cord motor nerve                precursor cell culture medium, and culture box is                maintained at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide                saturated humidity.
   USE - Method for inducing spinal cord motor nerve                precursor cell to differentiate into spinal motor                neuron.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for inducing spinal cord motor                nerve precursor cell to differentiate into spinal                motor neuron is efficient, quick, stable and safe,                can obtain more than 70% spinal motor neuron can be                obtained on the third day of differentiation.

The culture liquid is spinal cord motor nerve                precursor cell culture medium, and culture box is                maintained at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide                saturated humidity. The spinal cord motor precursor                cells obtained are cultured in basic culture                medium, and different time and space-specific                signal pathways are added at different times to                regulate the differentiation of small molecules and                growth factors. They are then cultured in a                humidified incubator maintained at 37 degrees C                with 5% carbon dioxide for 3-30 days, and c liquid                is changed once in every two days to obtain the                product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                spinal motor neuron-related signaling pathway that                control small molecules and growth factor, which                involves activating hedgehog signaling pathway                (SHH) activator, retinoic acid signaling pathway                activator, gamma -secretase signaling pathway                inhibitors, histone acetyltransferase inhibitors,                and the acting time are differentiated into D0-1,                D0-3, D0-4, D0-5, D0-6, preferably D0-3, most                preferably D0-1.

The spinal cord motor neural precursor                  culture medium is added with a rho kinase (ROCK)                  signaling pathway inhibitor, preferably the ROCK                  signaling pathway inhibitor Y27632, and the final                  use concentration is 10 mu M. Preferred                  Composition: The SHH signaling pathway activator                  and end use concentrations of one kind or the                  combination of multiple kinds of 1-500ng/mL SHH,                  0.1-20 mu M smoothened agonist (SAG) and 0.1-20                  mu M purmorphamine. The combination of the                  retinoic acid signaling pathway activator and end                  use concentration comprises 10-10000nM,                  preferably 10-1000nM, more preferably 10-100nM                  retinoic acid, and 0.1-10 mu M gamma -secretase                  signaling pathway inhibitors and end use                  concentration of MRK 560, and combination of one                  or multiple of 1-50 mu M, preferably 0.1-20 mu M,                  more preferably 1-100 mu M DAPT, 1-50 mu M BMS                  299897, 50 mu M compound W 1-begacestat. PN CN107859226-A
TI Molding gypsum hollow mold box comprises e.g. mixing clay and foaming agent under carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain foamed clay, mixing gypsum powder with water, modulating into gypsum slurry and mixing gypsum slurry with foamed clay.
AU WANG S
AE GUIZHOU JINSANLI TECHNOLOGY BUILDING MAT (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018263850
AB    NOVELTY - Molding gypsum hollow mold box comprises e.g. (i) mixing clay as auxiliary material and foaming agent under carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain foamed clay, (ii) mixing gypsum powder with water, modulating into gypsum slurry, mixing gypsum slurry with foamed clay and adding specific amount of water and stirring, (iii) casting gypsum mold for first time, laying first layer of fiber in form of mesh on first molded body and (iv) casting gypsum mold for second time, covering first layer of fiber and laying second fiber layer on second molded body in mesh form.
   USE - The method is useful for molding gypsum hollow mold box.
   ADVANTAGE - The mold box has high strength, strong water absorbing capacity and excellent performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Molding gypsum hollow mold box comprises (i) uniformly mixing clay as auxiliary material and foaming agent in the ratio of 3:1 under carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain foamed clay, (ii) mixing gypsum powder with water in a mass ratio of 2:1, modulating into gypsum slurry, mixing the gypsum slurry with the foamed clay in a ratio of 1:1, and adding specific amount of water according to the requirement and stirring, (iii) casting gypsum mold for first time, where pouring volume is one-third of the preset volume, laying the first layer of fiber in the form of a mesh on the first molded body, (iv) casting gypsum mold for second time, covering first layer of fiber, where pouring volume is two-thirds of the preset volume, laying second fiber layer on the second molded body in a mesh form, where the laying direction is perpendicular to the direction of the first layer of fiber strands, (v) casting a third cast on a gypsum mold, and covering the second layer of fiber, (vi) coloring, (vii) fully drying in a natural environment for 48-36 hours, and (viii) performing late pretreatment comprising burr removing, repairing, and trimming to obtain the gypsum hollow mold box.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The foaming agent is inorganic foaming agent and/or organic foaming agent. The inorganic foaming agent is mixture of sodium bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide, ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium hydrogen carbonate, the organic foaming agent is mixture of amine, butane, pentane, and/or hexane. The mixing of the gypsum slurry and the foamed clay is obtained by stirring under normal pressure conditions. The three castings are mixing with gypsum slurry and foamed clay, and then casting at room temperature and pressure. The drying of the gypsum mold to a constant weight at a temperature of 77 degrees C.

PN JP2018008913-A
TI Method for monitoring condition of catalyst during manufacture of methane, involves monitoring condition of catalyst in methane formation reaction by increasing amount of hydrogen supplied in reactor at predetermined intervals.
AU YOSHIDA H
   UMEMIYA S
AE FUJITSU LTD (FUIT-C)
GA 2018053528
AB    NOVELTY - Method for monitoring condition of catalyst involves monitoring the condition of catalyst in a methane formation reaction carried out by reacting carbon dioxide and hydrogen continuously in the presence of catalyst in a reactor by increasing the amount of hydrogen supplied in the reactor in a definite period of time at predetermined intervals, measuring the change in the reaction efficiency of the methane formation reaction with respect to increase in the supply amount of hydrogen, and monitoring the condition of catalyst based on the change of the reaction efficiency.
   USE - Method for monitoring condition of catalyst during manufacture of methane used as fuel for system (all claimed) for generating electric power.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables monitoring of condition of catalyst with suppressed decline in the reaction efficiency of methane formation reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for monitoring condition of catalyst involves monitoring the condition of catalyst in a methane formation reaction carried out by reacting carbon dioxide and hydrogen continuously in the presence of catalyst in a reactor, and producing methane by setting and increasing the amount of hydrogen supplied in the reactor in a definite period of time at predetermined intervals, measuring the change in the reaction efficiency of the methane formation reaction with respect to increase in the supply amount of hydrogen, and monitoring the condition of catalyst based on the change of the reaction efficiency. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) monitoring apparatus, which consists of a hydrogen supply amount regulator and a measuring device for measuring the change in the reaction efficiency of the methane formation reaction;
   (2) monitoring program, which is capable of performing the monitoring process using a computer; and
   (3) electric power generation system, which consists of a gas-separation apparatus for separating carbon dioxide from the waste gas discharged from the electric power generating apparatus, a methane production apparatus, the hydrogen supply amount regulator, and the measuring device. The electric power generating apparatus is capable of generating electric power by burning methane as a fuel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Component: The electric power generation system further consists of a gas discharge apparatus for discharging the gas within a circulation path between the electric power generating apparatus, the gas separation apparatus, and the methane production apparatus to the outside of the circulation path.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Method for monitoring condition of catalyst further involves increasing the amount of hydrogen supplied in the reactor based on the fluctuation range of reaction efficiency in the normal state of methane formation reaction, and setting the predetermined interval based on the length of reactor and the gas flow rate in the reactor.

TI Tubular reactor for mineralization reaction of carbon dioxide, comprises gas filter unit including external gas filter and tubular reaction portion in which a flow path is formed, where tubular reaction portion is fixed inside reaction unit.
AU YOUN M
   JUNG S
   BAK K T
   LEE W H
   KIM E Y
   KANG S P
   KIMHAKJU
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202022884L
AB    NOVELTY - Tubular reactor comprises a gas filter unit including an external gas filter (131) to diffuse and a tubular reaction portion (110) in which a flow path is formed in the portion. Tubular reaction portion is fixed inside the reaction unit and is formed in the central axis in the longitudinal direction of the reaction unit, facing the inner peripheral surface of the reaction unit. Fluids contain liquid medium, calcium compounds, and carbon dioxide-containing gasses are introduced from one end to the other. A plate is formed, and a multiple of stirring units having a shape in which the plate is twisted based on the central axis direction are combined with each other. A fixed stirring unit is located adjacent to one end of the reaction unit. The gas containing carbon dioxide is separated into a liquid medium in a micro-bubble state.
   USE - Tubular reactor is used for mineralization reaction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The tubular reactor is economical and cost-effective and has high reaction efficiency. The tubular reactor minimize the volume of the reaction device and improves the space efficiency. The tubular reactor have an effect of converting carbon dioxide into a carbonate mineral form at a high conversion rate even at normal temperature and pressure and can lower the cost of carbon dioxide reduction and convert carbon dioxide into a carbonate mineral form with high purity to be stably and permanently stored.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a device for mineralization of carbon dioxide, which comprises tubular reactor, reactor, separator for separating and discharging calcium carbonate particles from the fluid and a fluid containing calcium carbonate particles and alkali metal chloride as reactants is introduced from the reactor, where the fluid flowing from one end of the reaction unit further includes an alkali metal hydroxide, and the calcium compound; and
   (2) a method for immobilizing carbon dioxide, which involves using a tubular reactor for the mineralization reaction of carbon dioxide, liquid medium, a fluid containing a calcium compound and a carbon dioxide-containing gas, passes through one end of the reaction unit, flowing into the reaction unit and reacting to discharge the fluid containing the reactant through the other end of the reaction unit, and separating the calcium carbonate particles from the fluid containing the reactant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of tubular reactor.
   Reactor (100)
   Reaction portion (110)
   Fixed stirring unit (121)
   Second stirring unit (122)
   External gas filter (131)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The volumetric flow rate ratio of the liquid medium and carbon dioxide is 1:4-20. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide and calcium compounds is 1:1-7. The fluid introduced further comprises an alkali metal hydroxide, and the calcium compound is a calcium salt. The concentration of the calcium compound in the liquid medium is 0.2-2 Molar, and concentration of alkali metal hydroxide in the liquid medium is 0.3-3 Molar. The carbon dioxide has multiple of calcium carbonate particles of 95% or more. Preferred Process: The method for immobilizing carbon dioxide introducing a liquid medium, a liquid containing calcium chloride, alkali metal hydroxide and carbon dioxide-containing gas having calcium carbonate particles as reactants into the reaction unit through one end of the reaction unit and reacted through the other end of the reaction unit and discharging the fluid containing alkali metal chloride, where a fluid containing a reactant from the reaction unit flows into the separation unit, and calcium carbonate in the separation unit, and separating the particles and a fluid containing alkali metal chloride is introduced into the electrolysis unit from the separation unit, and the electricity, in the decomposition unit, the alkali metal chloride is converted to alkali metal hydroxide, and the fluid containing the alkali metal hydroxide converted in the electrolysis unit flows into one end of the reaction unit.

PT P
PN US2020017820-A1; US11015167-B2
TI Culturing algae comprises culturing alkaliphilic algae in open pond medium having specific pH to allow increased atmospheric carbon dioxide fixation into the medium and incorporating e.g. inorganic carbon buffer and calcium in the medium.
AU PENDYALA B
   VADLAMANI A
   VIAMAJALA S
   VARANASI S
   HANIFZADEH M
AE UNIV TOLEDO (TOOD-C)
   UNIV TOLEDO (TOOD-C)
GA 202005939L
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing (k1) algae comprises culturing alkaliphilic algae in an open pond medium (M1) having a pH above 9.5, sufficient to allow increased fixation of atmospheric carbon dioxide into the open pond medium, incorporating an inorganic carbon buffer into the open pond medium and incorporating less than 7 mg/ml calcium and/or magnesium into the open pond medium, where the open pond medium is free of any concentrated supply of carbon dioxide and no concentrated source of carbon dioxide is used to supply carbon into the open pond medium.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing algae, where the algae comprise eukaryotic microalgae or prokaryotic cyanobacteria (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methods: do not require concentrated carbon dioxide inputs, thus reducing production costs up to 25%; produce cultures at high pH, which allows rapid absorption of atmospheric carbon dioxide and permits high growth rates; alleviate the need for an open pond to be in proximity to a flue gas source; produce lower amounts of nitrogen in the algae, which is advantageous for biofuel production from the harvested biomass; utilize tailored media conditions which eliminate most of the obstacles encountered with mesophilic algae cultivation in open-ponds i.e. need to situate open ponds close to a carbon dioxide emission source, costs associated with carbon dioxide concentration and the energy and infrastructure costs associated with the supply of carbon dioxide for commodity-scale biomass production; utilize the aqueous medium which has high inorganic carbon and other nutrients which can be recycled indefinitely without compromising the algal growth; and produce alkaliphilic microalgae under high salinity environment which promotes the production of phycocyanin, a high value pigment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) culturing (k2) algae comprising culturing alkaliphilic algae in an open pond medium having a pH above 9.5, sufficient to allow increased fixation of atmospheric carbon dioxide into the open pond medium, incorporating into the open pond medium the inorganic carbon buffer and regulating 5-27 mg/l nitrogen input in the open pond medium, so as to modulate the biochemical composition of the algae; and
   (2) an open pond system comprising a medium (M2) having a pH above 9.5 and exposed to solar radiation, the inorganic carbon buffer in the medium and alkaliphilic algae in the medium, where the open pond system is free from any unnatural or concentrated carbon dioxide supply.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The less than 1.5 mg/ml calcium or less than 0.5 mg/ml magnesium is incorporated into the open pond medium (M1). The 7 mM to 1 M sodium bicarbonate/sodium carbonate mixture or a potassium bicarbonate/potassium mixture is incorporated into the open pond medium. The method (k1) further comprises: incorporating glucose or other sugars or carboxylic acids into the open pond medium; circulating the algae within the open pond medium; harvesting biomass from the cultured algae and recovering remnant media; recycling the remnant media in a second open pond medium; and converting the harvested biomass to at least one fuels. The step of converting comprises hydrothermal liquefaction to produce biocrude having N content of less than 4%. The method (k1) further comprises: regulating 5-27 mg/l nitrogen input in the open pond medium, so as to modulate the biochemical composition of the microalgae; and improving phycocyanin production by increasing at least one of biomass concentration, nitrogen concentration, and salinity in the open pond medium. Preferred Components: The pH of the open pond medium is at least 9.9
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic carbon buffer comprises either a sodium bicarbonate/sodium carbonate mixture or a potassium bicarbonate/potassium carbonate mixture. The medium (M2) further comprises: less than 7 mg/ml calcium and/or magnesium; and at least one nutrients comprising sodium nitrate, magnesium sulfate, calcium chloride, sodium chloride, ferric ammonium citrate, boric acid, manganese(II) chloride, zinc chloride, copper(II) chloride, sodium molybdate, cobalt(II) chloride, nickel(II) chloride, vanadium(V) oxide or potassium bromide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. PN IN201721016491-A; IN356047-B
TI Desulfurization system for recovery of pure constituents from flue gas, is integrated into which at least portion of sulfur oxide-containing gas stream's heat is recovered and each component of flue gas is regenerated separately.
AU GHOSH P K
   GARG P
   THOOL V R
   PADH B
   REDDY B R
   RAMACHANDRAN S K
   KEZHAKKETHIL S R
AE INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (CHTE-Non-standard)
   RUBAMIN LTD (RUBA-Non-standard)
   RUBAMIN PVT LTD (RUBA-Non-standard)
GA 2019378548
AB    NOVELTY - Desulfurization system comprises several heat exchanger unit (2,5), an absorber unit (8) for absorption of sulfur trioxide producing oleum, alkali scrubbing units (10, 13) absorption of sulfur dioxide, absorption unit (15) for absorption of carbon dioxide, storage units (11,16) and conduits (1,4,6,7,9,12,14,17). The system is integrated into which at least portion of the sulfur oxide-containing gas streams heat is recovered and utilized for effective absorption of carbon dioxide and each component of flue gas is regenerated separately.
   USE - Desulfurization system used for recovery of pure constituents e.g. carbon dioxide, oleum and sodium sulfide (claimed) from flue gas stream generated in industries such as roasting metal catalyst and fuel combustion.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is economical and effectively allows separation and regeneration of pure sulfur dioxide and sulfur trioxide along with carbon dioxide from waste flue gas stream, with minimum energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for process using the desulfurization system, which involves (i) feeding a flue gas through conduit to heat exchanger which cools down the flue gas temperature, (ii) feeding cooled gas to absorption unit where sulfur trioxide is selectively absorbed producing oleum which is regenerated to sulfuric acid in unit (18), (iii) feeding sulfur trioxide gas stream obtained via a conduit to an alkali scrubbing unit (10) in which sulfur dioxide is selectively absorbed producing sodium sulfite and stored into storage unit, (iv) optionally feeding sulfur dioxide free gas stream from the above step via a conduit to an alkali scrubbing unit (13), (v) feeding sulfur trioxide and sulfur dioxide free gas stream produced via a conduit to an absorption unit in which carbon dioxide is selectively absorbed in hot amine solution received from a storage unit and collecting pure carbon dioxide via a conduit. An integrated heat flow exists between hot flue gas and stream of carbon dioxide laden amine solution, and a heat exchanger unit exists between carbon dioxide-free hot amine solution and carbon dioxide-laden cold amine solution. The process utilizes an internal thermal energy for cooling and heating different stream for effective and selective regeneration of each component of flue gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of flue gas treatment system.
   Conduits (1,4,6,7,9,12,14,17)
   Heat exchanger unit (2,5)
   Carbon dioxide stream (3)
   Absorbing unit (8,15)
   Scrubbing unit (10,13)
   Storage unit (11,16)
   Carbon dioxide-laden amine solution (17)
   Sulfuric acid unit (18)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The system further consists of regeneration unit. Preferred Process: Oleum is produced in the absorption unit by absorption of sulfur trioxide gas in 92-98 w/w% sulfuric acid. The oleum is regenerated by dilution with sulfuric acid and recirculated in the absorption unit through conduits. Sodium sulfate is produced in alkali scrubbing unit by contacting with 10 v/v% alkali solution which is chosen from sodium carbonate and sodium hydroxide. The carbon dioxide is produced in a absorption unit (15) comprising 30-60 w/w% amine solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine is chosen from monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol and methyldiethanolamine in combination with piperazine.

PN JP2018075240-A
TI Producing carbon dioxide micro/nano bubble water            to perform bactericidal and bacteriostatic action of            microorganisms, by sending water and carbon dioxide            gas, forming gas/liquid mixed fluid, pumping and            causing cavitation.
AU OSHITA S
   MATSUDA S
   KATO J
AE UNIV TOKYO (UYTY-C)
   BAY CRUISE YG (BAYC-Non-standard)
GA 201838674B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing carbon dioxide micro/nano                bubble water involves (a) sending water from the                liquid container and the carbon dioxide gas from                the carbon dioxide gas generating apparatus to the                two fluid nozzle and forming gas/liquid mixed                fluid, (b) pumping the gas-liquid mixed fluid to a                pump tube, (c) causing cavitation to boil the                gas-liquid mixed fluid, (d) returning the pressure                to atmospheric pressure to make the dissolved gas                into micro/nano bubbles and (e) releasing the                micro/nano bubbles to the liquid container.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbon                dioxide micro/nano bubble water to be generated in                water opened to the atmosphere by pressurization of                several atmospheres to perform bactericidal and                bacteriostatic action of microorganisms and control                diseases caused by insects and pests. The CO2                micro/nano bubble water is used for controlling                pests in cultivation of agricultural crops. The CO2                micro/nano bubble water is used in the cleaning and                sterilizing process of cut vegetables, cut                vegetables manufacturing equipment, agricultural                products, foods, agricultural products and food                processing machinery. The CO2 micro/nano bubble                water is used for hand washing and sterilization in                public facilities such as hospitals and nursing                homes (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces CO2 micro/nano bubble                water having 106-107 per ml of carbon dioxide (CO2)                nano/micro bubbles. The CO2 micro/nano bubble water                is used instead of the pesticide used for                controlling pests in cultivation of agricultural                crops and replaces high concentration sodium                hypochlorite solution, hence it reduces                environmental pollution and health damage by                workers and improves working environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing carbon dioxide micro/nano                bubble water to be generated in water opened to the                atmosphere by pressurization of several atmospheres                to perform bactericidal and bacteriostatic action                of microorganisms and control diseases caused by                insects and pests involves (a) sending water from                the lower portion of the liquid container and the                carbon dioxide gas from the carbon dioxide gas                generating apparatus to the two fluid nozzle and                forming gas/liquid mixed fluid in which water and                carbon dioxide gas are mixed by the two fluid                nozzle, (b) pumping the gas-liquid mixed fluid to a                pump tube by a pressurized liquid pump, (c) causing                cavitation in the pumping tube to boil the                gas-liquid mixed fluid, (d) returning the pressure                to atmospheric pressure with a pressure reducing                valve to make the dissolved gas into micro/nano                bubbles and (e) releasing the micro/nano bubbles to                the liquid container and storing.
TF ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Insecticide; Pesticide. No                biological data given. Test details are described                but no results given. Test details are described                but no results for the compounds of the claims are                given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The CO2 micro/nano bubble water is applied by                spraying on the soil with liquid or directly on the                agricultural crop (claimed). No dosage details                given.

PN CN108315523-A; CN108315523-B
TI Producing direct reduced iron by carbon            dioxide-methane autothermal reforming involves            obtaining carbon dioxide circulating gas and reducing            gas at decarbonization purifier outlet respectively,            warming carbon dioxide circulating gas.
AU CHEN Q
   SUN Y
   TANG Z
   GU Y
   WANG D
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
   UNIV CHINESE ACAD SCI (UCAS-C)
GA 2018600687
AB    NOVELTY - Producing direct reduced iron by carbon                dioxide-methane autothermal reforming involves                obtaining a carbon dioxide circulating gas and a                reducing gas at a decarbonization purifier outlet,                respectively, warming the carbon dioxide                circulating gas through heat exchange, mixing the                desulfurized methane-rich feed gas with the carbon                dioxide recycle gas after heat exchange to form a                mixed gas, where the mixed gas exchanges heat with                the crude syngas from the reformer, heating the                mixed gas after the heat exchange and reacting with                the preheated oxygen into the reformer to form the                crude syngas, cooling after heat exchange with the                mixed gas, exchanging the cooled synthesis gas with                the reducing gas, cooling the crude syngas after                heat exchange and entering into the decarbonization                purifier, heating the reducing gas after heat                exchange, passing through the combustion furnace,                raising the temperature.
   USE - Method for producing direct reduced iron by                carbon dioxide-methane autothermal reforming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Entering the reducing gas into the shaft                furnace to reduce the iron reaction directly,                completing the direct reduced iron reaction,                exiting the generated top gas from the shaft                furnace and exchanging heat with the carbon dioxide                recycle gas from the decarbonization purifier                outlet, cooling the top gas after the heat exchange                and subjecting to the washing and cooling                treatment, entering the top gas into the                decarbonization purifier. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a system for producing direct reduced                iron by carbon dioxide-methane autothermal                reforming, which comprises a first heat exchanger,                a second heat exchanger, a third heat exchanger, a                reformer, and a combustion Furnace, shaft furnace,                decarbonization purifier and top gas cleaner, where                a mixture inlet of the first heat exchanger is                connected to a desulfurization device of the                methane-rich feed gas and a carbon dioxide recycle                gas outlet of the third heat exchanger, a crude                syngas inlet of the first heat exchanger is                connected to an outlet of the reformer, a mixed gas                outlet of the first heat exchanger is connected to                a mixed gas inlet of the reformer, a crude syngas                outlet of the first heat exchanger is connected to                a raw syngas inlet of the second heat exchanger, a                reducing gas inlet of the second heat exchanger is                connected to a reducing gas outlet of the                decarburization purifier, a crude syngas outlet of                the second heat exchanger is connected to a crude                syngas inlet of the decarbonizing purifier, a                reducing gas outlet of the second heat exchanger is                connected to a reducing gas inlet of the combustion                furnace; a reducing gas outlet of the burning                furnace is connected to a reducing gas inlet of the                shaft furnace, a top gas outlet of the shaft                furnace Connected to the top gas inlet of the third                heat exchanger, a carbon dioxide recycle gas inlet                of the third heat exchanger is connected to a                carbon dioxide recycle gas outlet of the                decarburization purifier, a top gas outlet of the                third heat exchanger is connected to the top gas                cleaner inlet, the top gas scrubber outlet is                connected to the top gas inlet of the                decarbonization purifier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The methane-rich feed                  gas is enriched in methane, including a                  combination of one or more of natural gas, oil                  field gas, coke oven gas, and refinery gas. The                  methane-enriched feedstock gas is enriched in                  methane and carbon dioxide, including a                  combination of one or more of coalbed methane,                  shale gas, methanol synthesis vent gas, and                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis gas. The methane-rich                  feed gas subjected to the desulfurization                  treatment has a sulfur content of less than 5                  parts per million (ppm). The outlet temperature                  in the reformer is between 700-1250 degrees C.                  The molar ratio of the oxygen to the methane in                  the mixed gas is between 0.4-0.6, and the molar                  ratio of carbon dioxide to methane in the mixed                  gas is between 0.3-1. The molar ratio of hydrogen                  to carbon monoxide in the crude syngas from the                  reformer is between 1.1-1.5. The molar content of                  methane is between 0-2%. The mixed gas exchanges                  heat with the crude syngas from the converter,                  and the mixed gas after heat exchange is heated                  to 400-700 degrees C. The crude syngas is cooled                  to 450-730 degrees C after heat exchange with the                  mixed gas. The cooled synthesis gas after the                  cooling is subjected to heat exchange with the                  reducing gas, and the crude synthesis gas after                  the heat exchange is further cooled to 200                  degrees C or less. The reducing gas after heat                  exchange with the crude syngas is heated to                  280-420 degrees C, the temperature is further                  increased to 800 degrees C or more through the                  combustion furnace, the reducing gas enters the                  shaft furnace to carry out a direct reduced iron                  reaction. The fuel gas of the combustion furnace                  includes the top gas obtained after the washing                  and cooling treatment. Preferred Process: The                  direct reduced iron by carbon dioxide-methane                  autothermal reforming is produced by recovering                  heat by the top gas from the shaft furnace first                  through a waste heat boiler, producing a steam                  by-product, where the temperature of the top gas                  is reduced to 300-400 degrees C, exchanging heat                  with the carbon dioxide circulating gas from the                  outlet of the decarbonization purifier, cooling                  the top gas after heat exchange, subjecting to                  washing and cooling treatment, where the                  temperature is lowered to 35-40 degrees C,                  entering the top gas into the decarburization                  purifier.

MC M24-A03; M24-A05B; M24-D02; M24-D04

PN CN111732559-A
TI Production of chlorohydrin epoxide for dehydrochlorination of calcium carbonate, involves reacting water and calcium carbonate, reacting mixture of hypochlorous acid and calcium chloride, and adding obtained carbonate to separation unit.
AU WU J
AE WU J (WUJJ-Individual)
GA 202099290N
AB    NOVELTY - Production of chlorohydrin epoxide involves (i) adding chlorine gas, water and calcium carbonate to a hypochlorous acid reaction unit, in which water and chlorine react to form hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride and reacting calcium carbonate and hydrogen chloride to form calcium chloride, water and carbon dioxide, (ii) adding mixture of hypochlorous acid and calcium chloride and raw materials of chlorohydrin into the chlorohydrin reaction unit and reacting to obtain chlorohydrin intermediate product, (iii) reacting the intermediate product with sodium hydroxide to obtain epoxide, (iv) adding the obtained material into the epoxide separation unit to separate the epoxide, (v) adding the obtained calcium hydroxide-containing saponification liquid and carbon dioxide from the hypochlorous acid reaction unit into the carbon dioxide recovery unit and reacting, and (vi) adding obtained calcium carbonate and sodium chloride mixture to the calcium carbonate separation unit.
   USE - Production of chlorohydrin epoxide used for dehydrochlorination of circulating calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and environmentally-friendly production of chlorohydrin epoxide with reduced generation of waste water and waste residue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of chlorohydrin epoxide in an electrolytic alkali production device comprising a hypochlorous acid reaction unit, a chlorohydrin reaction unit, a saponification reaction unit, an epoxide separation unit, a carbon dioxide recovery unit and calcium carbonate separation unit, involves (i) adding chlorine gas, water and calcium carbonate to the hypochlorous acid reaction unit, in which water and chlorine react to form hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride and reacting calcium carbonate and hydrogen chloride to form calcium chloride, water and carbon dioxide, (ii) adding mixture of hypochlorous acid and calcium chloride from the hypochlorous acid reaction unit and raw materials of chlorohydrin into the chlorohydrin reaction unit and reacting the raw material of chlorohydrin and hypochlorous acid to obtain chlorohydrin intermediate product, (iii) adding chlorohydrin intermediate product and sodium hydroxide to the saponification reaction unit, reacting the intermediate product with sodium hydroxide to obtain epoxide and reacting calcium chloride with sodium hydroxide to form sodium chloride and calcium hydroxide, (iv) adding the obtained material into the epoxide separation unit to separate the epoxide, (v) adding the obtained calcium hydroxide-containing saponification liquid and carbon dioxide from the hypochlorous acid reaction unit into the carbon dioxide recovery unit and reacting carbon dioxide with calcium hydroxide to form calcium carbonate, and (vi) adding calcium carbonate and sodium chloride mixture to the calcium carbonate separation unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The raw material of the chlorohydrin is ethylene, propylene, vinyl chloride, or propylene chloride. The epoxides are ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epichloroethylene, or epichlorohydrin.
