PT P
PN IN201811047387-A
TI Modified heterogeneous hydrogen exfoliated graphene-copper/titanium dioxide nanocatalyst used for conversion of carbon dioxide into methanol.
AU CHAKRABORTTY S
   RUJ B
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 202083938W
AB    NOVELTY - Modified heterogeneous hydrogen exfoliated graphene (mHEG)-copper/titanium dioxide nanocatalyst having particle size of 40-60 mesh, is claimed, where ratio of mHEG, copper and titanium dioxide is 1:3:2.
   USE - The mHEG-copper/titanium dioxide nanocatalyst is useful for conversion of carbon dioxide into methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanocatalyst: provides easy implementation for modified hydrogen exfoliation graphene from synthesized graphene oxide with enhanced surface area; provides high yield methanol production from carbon dioxide; minimizes the difficulties arise for low yield production of methanol from carbon dioxide through the developed novel heterogeneous catalyst; minimizes the rapid rise of carbon dioxide in the environment through the photocatalytic conversion/reduction of carbon dioxide into renewable energy source e.g. methanol under solar/UV light excitation; is technically and economically proficient; does not require sophisticate instrument; provides high accuracy of optimum value relative to manual optimization process; allows less capital investment; provides economical/commercial value and develops nanocomposite material produce methanol with a high productivity; provides an optimized method of methanol production for scale-up; provides economical, environmentally safe and easiest method for the production of methanol; and provides a zero cost feed and waste to wealth concept.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) synthesizing (p1) mHEG-copper/titanium dioxide nanocatalyst, comprising (i) providing dried graphene oxide by chemical oxidation method, (ii) reducing the dried graphene oxide as provided in step (i) by hydrogen in a gas purging system in argon atmosphere at a temperature of 160-220 degrees C at a fixed hydrogen flow rate for period of 1-4 minutes to obtain mHEG, and (iii) mixing mHEG as obtained in step (ii) with titanium dioxide and copper salt in a solvent in a ratio of 1:3:2 by sol-gel method to obtain mHEG-copper/titanium dioxide nanocatalyst; and
   (2) conversion (p2) of carbon dioxide into methanol using mHEG-copper/titanium dioxide nanocatalyst, comprising (a) purging carbon dioxide in photo catalytic reactor containing the nanocatalyst in water to get a reaction mixture, (b) stirring the reaction mixture as obtained in step (a) for 15-20 minutes at temperature of 50-80 degrees C at a stirring speed of 150-300 rpm to obtain a reaction mixture, and (c) illuminating the reaction mixture as obtained in step (b) with UV light for 2-4 hours to obtain methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent used is ethanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper salt used comprises copper sulfate or copper hydroxide. Preferred Process: The gas purging system consists of a three neck round bottom flask, oil bath, ultrapure hydrogen gas cylinder and temperature-controlled heater. The optimized conditions for getting maximum yield of methanol are; catalyst dosage of 10 g/l (mHEG-copper/titanium dioxide), carbon dioxide flow rate of 3 l/m, process temperature of 50 degrees C, reaction pH of 3, and UV (250W) exposure time of 3 hours. The maximum methanol productivity is 0.142 g/l.hour and yield is 0.042 g/g catalyst. The process parameters were optimized using central composite design software.
   EXAMPLE - 1.25 g Modified heterogeneous hydrogen exfoliated graphene was added with 2 g photocatalytic grade titanium dioxide and continuously sonicated in the presence of 30 ml absolute ethanol. For the modification of the catalyst, 15 ml of 0.01 M glucose, 3 g copper sulfate were added into the prepared catalyst mixture followed by pH 3.2 with 50% nitric acid and thoroughly sonicated continuously for additional 2 hours. The gel type solution was kept around 24 hours at 30-35 degrees C and dehydrated at 85 degrees C for 12 hours to form powder which was then placed into a crucible and kept in a muffle furnace at 500 degrees C for 2 hours and made it a powder like sample with a 40-60 mesh particle size which further used for the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide.

PN CN113234209-A
TI Initiating ring-opening dispersion polymerization of lactide with luteolin in supercritical carbon dioxide for biomedical polymer material, by taking lactide, luteolin, and triblock polymer polycaprolactone-polydimethylsiloxane-polycaprolactone, and performing ring-opening dispersion polymerization.
AU WANG J
   CHEN R
   ZHAN S
AE UNIV DALIAN (UYDV-C)
GA 2021948876
AB    NOVELTY - Method for initiating ring-opening dispersion polymerization of lactide with luteolin in supercritical carbon dioxide, involves taking lactide as a raw material, luteolin as an initiator, stannous octoate as a catalyst, triblock polymer polycaprolactone-polydimethylsiloxane-polycaprolactone (PCL-PDMS-PCL) as a stabilizer, and performing ring-opening dispersion polymerization method to synthesize polylactide.
   USE - Method useful for initiating ring-opening dispersion polymerization of lactide with luteolin in supercritical carbon dioxide for biomedical polymer material application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses luteolin as the initiator to synthesize biomedical material polylactide in supercritical carbon dioxide, obtains the polymer product as a granular powder, has product yield up to 93% and number average molecular weight of the polymer of 13799 with molecular weight distribution of 1.98, and is simple and efficient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method involves (i) adding lactide, luteolin, stabilizer, and catalyst to a reactor, installing reactor, purging the reactor with carbon dioxide for 5 minutes, stop the purging, adjusting the stirrer speed to 400 rpm, start heating, and turning on the plunger pump to pressurize the system and react when the temperature reaches a certain temperature, and (ii) stop heating and stop stirring after the temperature of the system cool to room temperature after the reaction, at the same time slowly deflating to normal pressure, collecting the crude product in a beaker, slowly dripping dichloromethane until the product is completely dissolved, precipitating the white powder in frozen methanol, and drying the obtained polymer in a vacuum-drying oven to a constant weight to obtain the product polylactide. Preferred Composition: The added amount of luteolin is based on the amount of lactide, where the added amount of luteolin is 1 mol added for every 100 mol lactide. The amount of stabilizer added is based on the amount of lactide, where the added amount of stabilizer is 1 g for every 20 g lactide. The amount added of catalyst is based on the amount of lactide, where the amount added of catalyst is 0.4 g for every 20 g lactide. In step (ii), the amount of frozen methanol is 10 times the volume of dichloromethane. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the plunger pump is turned on to pressurize the system to 16-20 MPa, preferably 16 MPa and the reaction time is 24 hours when the temperature reaches 110-130 degrees C, preferably 130 degrees C. The temperature of the vacuum drying oven is 40 degrees C.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

PN CN112481033-A
TI Extracting Cedrela sinensis essential oil involves performing pretreatment, supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, sterilization and concentration to obtain essential oil.
AU LIU X
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG X
   LIU Y
   REN L
   GAO L
AE UNIV FUYANG NORMAL (UFYN-C)
GA 202130130G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting Cedrela sinensis essential oil involves performing pretreatment, supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, sterilization and concentration to obtain essential oil. The pretreatment process involves shredding C.sinensis buds as raw materials, wrapping with a filter, and soaking in petroleum ether for 10-60 minutes. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process involves passing the pretreated C.sinensis buds through the extraction kettle, then passing them into the separator (S1) and the separator (S2) in sequence, where the conditions in the extraction kettle are extraction pressure of 20-30 MPa, extraction temperature of 38-45 degrees C and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-40 l/hour, conditions in separator (S1) are pressure 7-8 MPa and temperature 33-36 degrees C and conditions in separator (S2) are pressure of 5.5-6.5 MPa and temperature of 28-31 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting C.sinensis essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method involves mixing the C.sinensis buds with 30-50 wt.% water, pulping, then performing UV sterilization and performing low-temperature vacuum concentration at 48-50 degrees C and 10-12 KPa for 2-4 hours to 20-40 vol.% extract. The conditions in the extraction kettle are extraction pressure of 26 MPa, extraction temperature of 40 degrees C and carbon dioxide flow rate of 35 l/hour, conditions in separator (S1) are pressure 7.5 MPa and temperature 34 degrees C and conditions in separator (S2) are pressure of 6 MPa and temperature of 29 degrees C. The method involves subjecting the extraction residue to ethanol extraction and recovery process by soaking and extracting with 1-2 times 50-80 vol.% ethanol solution, then concentrating to 10-20 vol.% original volume, and mixing with the concentrated essential oil.

PT P
PN WO2018138686-A1; EP3551606-A1; CN110325505-A; US2019352250-A1
TI Producing disubstituted oxalate compounds (I) i.e. dimethyl oxalate, comprises contacting cesium salt with at least one alcohol and carbon dioxide under reaction conditions to produce a composition comprising (I).
AU AL-JABER A
   KOROBKOV I
   AL-BAHILY K
   VIDJAYACOUMAR B
   ALBAHILY K
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201860403H
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a disubstituted oxalate compounds (I), comprises contacting a cesium salt with at least one alcohol and carbon dioxide under reaction conditions to produce a composition comprising (I).
   USE - The process is useful for producing disubstituted oxalate compounds i.e. dimethyl oxalate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process is one-pot synthesis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing a disubstituted oxalate compounds of formula (R1-O-C(O)-C(O)-O-R2) (I), comprises contacting a cesium salt with at least one alcohol and carbon dioxide under reaction conditions to produce a composition comprising (I).
   R1, R2 = alkyl or aromatic group (both optionally substituted), preferably methyl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) the composition for producing (I), comprising the cesium salt, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and the alcohol; and
   (2) the disubstituted oxalate compounds produced by the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process: the reaction conditions comprise a temperature of 125-200 degrees C, preferably 150 degrees C; the reactions conditions comprise a pressure of 2-5 (preferably 3-4) MPa; the cesium salt is cesium oxalate which is obtained by contacting carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, under reaction conditions to form a composition comprising the cesium oxalate; the reaction conditions for obtaining the cesium oxalate comprise a reaction temperature of 200-400 degrees C, preferably 300-325 degrees C; the reaction conditions for obtaining the cesium oxalate comprise providing carbon dioxide at a pressure of 2-3 (preferably 2.5) MPa, and providing carbon monoxide at a pressure of 1-3 (preferably 2) MPa, or hydrogen gas at a pressure of 0.05-0.5 (preferably 0.1) MPa; the carbon dioxide is contacted with the cesium carbonate at a reaction temperature of 200-400 degrees C, preferably 300-325 degrees C for at least 1 hour to obtain a cesium carbonate/carbon dioxide reaction mixture, and contacting the cesium carbonate/carbon dioxide reaction mixture with the hydrogen gas at the reaction temperature; the formation of sodium formate is inhibited; and the reaction conditions for obtaining the cesium oxalate comprise providing carbon monoxide at a pressure of 2-6 (preferably 2) MPa, and providing oxygen at a pressure of 0.05-5 (preferably 0.1) MPa. The process: further comprises isolating the cesium oxalate salt from the product stream before converting it to the (I); is a single reactor synthesis so that cesium oxalate is generated in situ and then contacted with the alcohol and additional carbon dioxide to produce (I); isolating (I) from the product stream; and reacting (I) under conditions sufficient to form oxalic acid or ethylene glycol. Preferred Components: The alcohol is methanol.
   EXAMPLE - 500 mg Caesium carbonate was added to a 100 ml Parr reactor in a glove box. 35 bar Carbon dioxide and 1 bar hydrogen gases were then charged and the mixture was stirred for 1-2 hour at 325 degrees C and cooled to room temperature by applying cool air to the reactor. The reactor cooled to 25 degrees C and depressurized. The reaction mixture contained cesium oxalate, cesium formate, and cesium bicarbonate. 5 ml Methanol was then added to the reactor, and the reactor was pressurized with 35 bar carbon dioxide. The obtained mixture was heated to 150 degrees C, stirred overnight and then depressurized. The remaining solvent (methanol) was removed by evaporation under vacuum. The product composition was analyzed and identified as being a mixture of dimethyl oxalate, cesium formate and cesium bicarbonate. The overall yield of dimethyl oxalate was 89% and the yield of cesium formate as a byproduct was 4-5%.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)
MC E10-C02D1; E10-E02E1; E10-E02E2; E10-E04L; E10-G02A1; E10-G02B2; E11-F06; E31-N05B1; E31-N05C
IP C07C-051/41; C07C-055/07; C07C-067/08; C07C-069/36

PN CN108096195-A; CN108096195-B
TI Preparation of azilsartan medoxomil solid dispersion by supercritical anti-solvent method by i.e. reacting azilsartan medoxomil and water-soluble carrier in organic solvent with carbon dioxide gas, releasing and collecting solid dispersion.
AU WANG Z
   SONG Y
   XU W
   CHEN Z
   MIAO H
   WANG W
AE UNIV CHINA PHARM (UYCP-C)
GA 201844458G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of azilsartan medoxomil solid dispersion by supercritical anti-solvent method comprises dissolving azilsartan medoxomil and water-soluble carrier in organic solvent to obtain azilsartan-carrier mixed solution; introducing carbon dioxide gas into crystallization kettle and adjusting temperature and pressure; continuously feeding to carbon dioxide gas, maintaining temperature and pressure and adding azilsartan-carrier mixed solution into crystallization kettle; continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas, maintaining temperature and pressure for given period, releasing, reducing crystallizing kettle pressure to atmospheric pressure, opening crystallizing kettle and collecting azilsartan medoxomil solid dispersion. The water-soluble carrier is polyvinylpyrrolidone K30 (PVP K30).
   USE - Supercritical anti-solvent method for preparing azilsartan medoxomil solid dispersion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves dissolution rate and stability of azilsartan medoxomil solid dispersion.it has environmental friendly
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is mixed solvent of acetone, dichloromethane and dimethyl sulfoxide, in volume ratio of 1:1:0.01. Preferred Method: The crystallization kettle is set at 35-55 degrees C under 9-17 MPa, preferably at 40 degrees C under 11 mPa. The crystal volume flow of mixed solution to crystallization kettle is 0.9-3.0 mL/minute, preferably 0.9 mL/minute. The flow speed of carbon dioxide fed to kettle is 1-4 L/minute, preferably 2.5 mL/minute. The temperature and pressure are maintained for 0.5-2 hours, preferably 1.5 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of azilsartan and PVP-K30 is 1: 1-1:5, preferably 1:3. The mass concentration of azilsartan mixed solution is 3.5-9.5 g/L, preferably 5.0 g/L.

PN CN109266668-A
TI Trapping promoter and enhancer, by culturing breast cancer 4T1 cells in cell culture medium, preparing ligation reaction system, transforming ligation product, performing alkaline transfection and detecting alkaline phosphatase activity.
AU MA S
   LI Q
   LU Y
AE LI Q (LIQQ-Individual)
   MA S (MASS-Individual)
   LU Y (LUYY-Individual)
GA 201911879M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for trapping promoter and enhancer involves culturing the breast cancer 4T1 cells in a cell culture medium containing 10% serum at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide, extracting genomic DNA and total RNA when the cells grown to a logarithmic growth phase, picking all the monoclonals, culturing the cells in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, discarding the cell culture medium in each well before transfection, adding the mixture of the plasmid and liposome obtained in the step (l) to each well, shaking the mixture on a shaker at 37 degrees C for 10 minutes, incubating in 5% carbon dioxide for 5 hours, aspirating the cell culture medium, replacing with 500 mu l fresh culture medium containing no antibiotics containing 10% serum, culturing at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, performing alkaline transfection for 36 hours and detecting the alkaline phosphatase activity and setting mSEAP positive control plasmid and the negative control liposome lipofecton 2000.
   USE - The method is useful for trapping promoter and enhancer for discovering genes and super-promoters.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for various transfection and transformed cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for trapping promoter and enhancer involves (a) culturing the breast cancer 4T1 cells in a cell culture medium containing 10% serum at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide, extracting genomic DNA and total RNA when the cells grown to a logarithmic growth phase, where the cell culture medium containing 10% serum comprises 89 %mass Roswell park memorial institute (RPMI)-1640 culture solution, 10 %mass fetal calf serum and 1 %mass penicillin-streptomycin solution, (b) digesting the metastatic mouse breast cancer 4T1 cell genomic DNA and expression vector Pro-free-trap using restriction endonucleases Bacillus amyloliquefaciens H1 (BamHI) and Haemophilus influenzae III (Hind III), reacting at 37 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain mouse breast cancer 4T1 cells genomic DNA fragment and restriction vector of expression vector Pro-DNA-trap linearize vector DNA, where the digestion system comprises 2 mu l 10xuffer BamH1, 4 mu l mouse breast cancer 4T1 cell genomic DNA, 0.8 mu l restriction endonuclease BamHI (10 U/ mu l), 1.6 mu l restriction endonuclease HindIII and double distilled water to 20 mu l, (c) preparing ligation reaction system for linearized vector DNA and all randomly digested genomic DNA fragments, reacting at 22 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain a recombinant plasmid, where the ligation reaction system comprises 1 mu l 10xT4 DNA ligasebuffer, 5 mu l genomic DNA fragment, 3 mu l linearized vector and 1 mu l T4 DNA ligase, (d) transforming ligation product into Escherichia coli competent GT115 cells and culturing in a low-salt solid Luria-Bertani (LB) medium at 37 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain monoclonal, (e) picking all the monoclonals, incubating with low-salt liquid LB containing bleomycin at 37 degrees C and shaking in shaker at 180 revolutions per minute for 12 hours, (f) extracting the plasmid in the bacterial solution and performing double enzyme digestion with restriction endonucleases BamH I and Hind III and storing the plasmid at -20 degrees C, (g) using pEGFP-N1 plasmid as template and SEAP-PF and SEAP-PR as upstream and downstream primers, performing PCR amplification of cytomegalovirus (CMV) promoter, where the PCR reaction system comprises 2.5 mu l 10xTaq easybuffer, 2 mu l dNTPs (2.5 mM), 0.5 mu l Taqpolymerase (5 U/ mu l), 1 mu l upstream primer (10 mu M), 1 mu l downstream primer (10 mu M), 1 mu l template (10 mu g/ml) and double distilled water to 25 mu l, where the PCR reaction is performed by pre-denaturing at 94 degrees C for 3 minutes, denaturing at 94 degrees C for 30 seconds, annealing at 60 degrees C for 30 seconds, extending at 72 degrees C for 1 minute for 40 cycles and extending at 72 degrees C for 10 minutes, (h) digesting the target fragment of the CMV promoter obtained by the step (g) with restriction endonucleases BamH I and HindIII and reacting at 37 degrees C for 4 hours, where the enzyme digest comprises 2 mu l 10xbuffer BamH1, 5 mu l DNA fragment obtained in the step (g), 0.8 mu l restriction endonuclease BamH I (10 U/ mu l), 1.6 mu l restriction endonuclease HindIII (10 U/ mu l) and double distilled water to 20 mu l, (i) reacting the linearized Pro-free-trapc vector obtained in the step (b) and the ligation reaction system of the target fragment obtained in the step (h) at 22 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain recombinant plasmid Pro-CMV-trap, where the ligation reaction system comprises 14 mu l fragment obtained in the step (h), 3 mu l linearized Pro-free-trap vector obtained in the step (b), 2 mu l 10xT4 DNA ligase buffer and 1 mu l T4 DNA ligase, (j) transforming the ligation product into E.coli GT115 competent cells and plating on the low salt LB solid medium at 37 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain monoclonal, (k) picking a single clone, incubating with low-salt liquid LB containing bleomycin at 37 degrees C for 18 hours in a shaker at 180 revolutions per minute, sequencing and identifying the positive clones and extracting the plasmid, (l) diluting the liposome with the cell culture solution for 25 times, allowing to stand at room temperature for 5 minutes, separately adding the plasmid obtained in the step (k) to the diluted liposome and uniformly mixing and leaving at room temperature for 20 minutes and (m) inoculating mouse breast cancer 4T1 cells in logarithmic growth phase in 24-well cell culture plates, uniformly dispersing 5x 105 cells in each well, adding 500 mu l cell culture medium without antibiotics containing 10% serum at 37 degrees C, culturing the cells in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, discarding the cell culture medium in each well before transfection, adding the mixture of the plasmid and liposome obtained in the step (l) to each well, shaking the mixture on a shaker at 37 degrees C for 10 minutes, incubating in 5% carbon dioxide for 5 hours, aspirating the cell culture medium, replacing with 500 mu l fresh culture medium containing no antibiotics containing 10% serum, culturing at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, performing alkaline transfection for 36 hours and detecting the alkaline phosphatase activity and setting mSEAP positive control plasmid and the negative control liposome lipofecton 2000.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (c), the genomic DNA fragment is obtained by performing random restriction enzyme digestion of the genomic DNA with restriction endonucleases BamH I and Hind III at 37 degrees C for 4 hours. The step (m) involves transfecting the liposome with the random genomic fragment for 36 hours, collecting the cell supernatant in each well, centrifuging at 10000 revolutions per minute for 5 minutes, placing the supernatant after centrifugation in 65 degrees C water bath for 5-10 minutes, inhibiting the endogenous alkaline phosphatase activity of the cells, adding 50 mu l 1xdilution buffer, 100 mu l 1xassay buffer, 20 mu l L-high arginine and 20 mu l water, heating in water bath at 37 degrees C for 10 minutes, adding 20 mu l SEAP substrate (PNPP) to the wells, measuring the alkaline phosphatase activity under OD450 conditions in the dark at 37 degrees C for 10 minutes in the dark and using the index as a promoter or enhancer activity assay.

TI Predicting high-content carbon dioxide condensate            gas reservoir oil ring volume used e.g. in device, by            inputting main control parameter of high carbon dioxide            condensation gas reservoir to be predicted to target            volume prediction model.
AU RUI Z
   LIU X
   ZUO M
   TANG J
   QIN Q
   YANG R
   XING J
   ZHANG Y
   JIANG D
   CHEN H
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 2022A4730V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for predicting high-content carbon                dioxide condensate gas reservoir oil ring volume                involves establishing oil ring volume prediction                database, determining parameter correlation degree                between each influence parameter and corresponding                oil ring volume, determining influence parameter of                parameter association degree greater than preset                parameter association degree as main control                parameter, inputting parameter value corresponding                to main control parameter to volume prediction                model of oil ring to obtain prediction volume                output by volume prediction model, determining                target kernel function of volume prediction model                according to predicted volume to obtain target                volume prediction model, and inputting main control                parameter of high carbon dioxide condensation gas                reservoir to be predicted to target volume                prediction model to obtain predicted value of oil                ring volume output by target volume prediction                model.
   USE - The method is used for predicting high-content                carbon dioxide condensate gas reservoir oil ring                volume in device and processor (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prediction accuracy can be improved by the                technical solution, the prediction process is                simple and fast, and the prediction time is greatly                reduced. The main control parameter input volume                prediction model affecting the high CO2 condensate                gas reservoir oil ring volume to train the volume                model, and by determining the target kernel                function to obtain the corresponding target volume                model to predict the pre-measured high CO 2                condensation gas reservoir, which can improve the                prediction accuracy of the oil ring volumes. The                prediction time can greatly reduce the prediction                of the volume error value between the actual volume                of the main control parameters and the predicted                volume. The volume prediction process can be                simplified and the time required for the prediction                can be reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for predicting high-content carbon                dioxide condensate gas reservoir oil ring volume                involves establishing oil ring volume prediction                database, determining parameter correlation degree                between each influence parameter and corresponding                oil ring volume, determining influence parameter of                parameter association degree greater than preset                parameter association degree as main control                parameter, inputting parameter value corresponding                to main control parameter to volume prediction                model of oil ring to obtain prediction volume                output by volume prediction model, determining                target kernel function of volume prediction model                according to predicted volume to obtain target                volume prediction model, and inputting main control                parameter of high carbon dioxide condensation gas                reservoir to be predicted to target volume                prediction model to obtain predicted value of oil                ring volume output by target volume prediction                model. The oil ring volume prediction database                comprises influence parameters affecting high                carbon dioxide condensation gas reservoir oil ring                volume.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for predicting high-content carbon dioxide                condensate gas reservoir oil ring volume.

TI Apparatus for culturing Sphagnum, comprises culture vessel for Sphagnum and culture medium arranged in vessel that contains airspace above medium, and inlet pipe is connected to source of carbon dioxide, and tube is permeable to carbon dioxide and is configured to supply carbon dioxide into airspace.
AU WRIGHT N
AE MICROPROPAGATION SERVICES EM LTD (MICR-Non-standard)
GA 202273888L
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus 100 comprises a culture vessel 102 for Sphagnum 106 and a culture medium 104 arranged in the culture vessel. The culture vessel comprises an enclosed airspace 108 above the culture medium, and Sphagnum is arranged in the culture medium. An inlet pipe 112 is connected to a source of carbon dioxide 110, and a tube is permeable to carbon dioxide. The pipe is connected to inlet pipe, where tube is arranged at least partially in the enclosed airspace. The pipe is configured to supply carbon dioxide into enclosed airspace by diffusion through a wall of the pipe.
   USE - Apparatus for culturing Sphagnum (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus reduces level of contamination, does not interfere with Sphagnum in the culture vessel, clusters culture vessel around a light source, and provides sufficient light intensity for suitable growth.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) an apparatus for culturing Sphagnum comprises culture vessel for Sphagnum, a liquid culture medium arranged in the culture vessel, a Sphagnum is arranged in the liquid culture medium, and a light source is configured to supply light to the Sphagnum, where light source is arranged at one side of culture vessel to create a temperature differential in culture vessel to stir the liquid culture medium;
   (2) a system for culturing Sphagnum comprises a first culture vessel for Sphagnum, and a first liquid culture medium arranged in the first culture vessel, where first culture vessel comprises a first enclosed airspace above the first liquid culture medium, and Sphagnum arranged in the first liquid culture medium, a second culture vessel for Sphagnum, and a second liquid culture medium arranged in the second culture vessel, where second culture vessel comprises a second enclosed airspace above the second liquid culture medium, and Sphagnum arranged in the second liquid culture medium, and a light source configured to supply light to first culture vessel and second culture vessel, where light source is arranged between first side of first culture vessel and first side of second culture vessel, where first side of first culture vessel and first side of the second culture vessel are arranged adjacent to each other, and light source is arranged to extend across a height of first liquid culture medium and second liquid culture medium; and
   (3) a method for culturing Sphagnum by providing a culture vessel for Sphagnum; providing a culture medium arranged in culture vessel, providing Sphagnum arranged in the culture medium; providing an inlet pipe connected to a source of carbon dioxide; and providing a tube permeable to carbon dioxide, where tube is connected to the inlet pipe, and tube is arranged at least partially in the enclosed airspace; and supplying carbon dioxide into the enclosed airspace by diffusion through a wall of the tube.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of apparatus.

PN RD689004-A
TI Extreme ultraviolet lithography apparatus comprises carbon dioxide laser, which has gold catalyst to enhance laser power, where gold catalyst combines carbon monoxide and oxygen into carbon dioxide and unit for splitting oxygen molecules.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 2021A43611
AB    NOVELTY - The extreme ultraviolet lithography apparatus comprises a carbon dioxide laser, which has a gold catalyst to enhance the laser power, where the gold catalyst combines carbon monoxide and oxygen into carbon dioxide. The electrical discharge by the radio frequency generators create oxygen radicals in the gas volume by converting carbon dioxide molecules into carbon monoxide and oxygen. A unit for splitting oxygen molecules is located in a power amplifier of the carbon dioxide laser.
   USE - Extreme ultraviolet lithography apparatus.

TI Production of carbon monoxide and dihydrogen            involves contacting gas containing carbon dioxide with            aqueous adsorption solution, and subjecting bicarbonate            loaded stream to electrochemical conversion.
AU FRADETTE L
   FRADETTE S
   FRADETT L
   FRADETT S
AE CO2 SOLUTIONS INC (SAIM-C)
   SAIPEM SPA (SAIM-C)
   SAIPEM SPA (SAIM-C)
   BAIPEM SPA (BAIP-Non-standard)
   SAIPEM SPA (SAIM-C)
GA 2020065690
AB    NOVELTY - Production of carbon monoxide and dihydrogen                from gas containing carbon dioxide (14) involves                contacting gas containing carbon dioxide with                aqueous adsorption solution (16) to produce a                bicarbonate loaded stream and carbon dioxide                depleted gas (18), and subjecting the bicarbonate                loaded stream to an electrochemical                conversion.
   USE - Production of carbon monoxide and                dihydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                producing gaseous stream containing carbon dioxide                and hydrogen.
   Absorption unit (10)
   Bicarbonate conversion unit (12)
   Gas containing carbon dioxide (14)
   Aqueous absorption solution (16)
   Depleted gas (18)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The aqueous adsorption                  solution comprises sodium carbonate having                  concentration of 0.5-2mol/l, potassium carbonate                  having concentration of 1-6 mol/l and potassium                  bicarbonate. The carbon dioxide loading in the                  absorption solution, before contacting                  gas-contacting gas containing carbon dioxide is                  0.5-0.75 mol C/mol potassium ion. The carbon                  dioxide loading in the bicarbonate loaded stream                  is 0.75-1 mol C/mol potassium ion. The                  electrochemical conversion comprises bicarbonate                  ions of the bicarbonate loaded stream into gas                  stream containing carbon dioxide and dihydrogen                  in an electrolytic cell provided with alkaline                  electrolyte solution and generating bicarbonate                  depleted stream. The alkaline electrolyte                  solution comprises aqueous solution of potassium                  hydroxide and sodium hydroxide having                  concentration of 0.5-10 mol/l.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The aqueous adsorption                  solution comprises promoter and/or catalyst, and                  absorption compound containing sterically                  hindered amines, sterically hindered                  alkanolamines, tertiary amines, and/or tertiary                  alkanolamines, tertiary amino acids and                  carbonates, preferably                  2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol,                  2-amino-2-hydroxymethyl-1,3-propenediol,                  N-methyldiethanolamine, dimethylmonoethanolamine,                  diethylmonoethanolamine, triisopropanolamine,                  triethanolamine, N-methyl N-secondary                  butylglycine, diethylglycine, dimethylglycine,                  potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate, and/or                  cesium carbonate. The promoter and/or catalyst                  comprises piperazine, diethanolamine,                  diisopropanolamine, methylaminopropylamine,                  3-aminopropanol, 2,2-dimethyl-1,3-propanediamine,                  diglycolamine, 2-amino-2-ethylpropanol,                  1-amino-2-propanol, 2-methyl-methanolamine,                  piperidine, arsenite, hypochlorite, sulfite,                  glycine, sarcosine, alanine N-secondary butyl                  glycine, pipecolinic acid and/or carbonic                  anhydrase or its analog, preferably glycine,                  sarcosine, alanine N-secondary butyl glycine,                  pipecolinic acid and carbonic anhydrase or its                  analog. The carbonic anhydrase or its analog is                  separated from bicarbonate loaded stream.                  Preferred Composition: The aqueous absorption                  solution comprises less than or equal to 1 wt.%                  carbonic anhydrase or analogue having                  concentration of up to 10 g/l, preferably                  0.15-0.3 g/l. Preferred Property: The aqueous                  absorption solution has pH of 8.5-10.5.

   USE - Method for preparing chitosan by using supercritical carbon dioxide reaction process (claimed).

Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature is 20-60 degrees C, the reaction pressure is 10-50 MPa, and reaction time is 30 minutes to 3 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - A method for preparing Torreya grandistotal                flavonoids by supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction method comprises (S1) taking                Torreyagrandisexocarp after extracting volatile oil                as raw material, and carrying out supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction to obtain the extract                solution, where the carbon dioxide flow in the                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process is                1-2 L/hour, the extraction is carried out at a                temperature of 45-55&#8451; and at extraction                pressure of 24-32 MPa for 1.5-2.5 hours, and (S2)                steaming the obtained extracting solution and                dissolving in methanol to obtain Torreya                grandistotal flavonoids.
   USE - The method is used for preparing Torreya                grandis total flavonoids by supercritical                extraction method. The Torreya grandis total                flavonoid is used for inhibiting germination of                weed seeds, where the weed comprises crabgrass,                bluegrass and wild oat (all claimed).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (S1), the                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process                  is carried out using an entrainer, where the                  entrainer comprises ethanol and methanol in a                  volume ratio of (1-3):(1-3), and the dosage ratio                  of the entrainer and Torreya grandisexocarp is                  (5-15) mL:1 g. In step (S2), the rotary                  evaporation is rotary evaporation to obtain a                  sticky paste, the concentration of the methanol                  is 50-70 wt.%, and the dosage of the methanol is                  20-30 mL. In step (S2), the rotary evaporation                  temperature is 45-55&#8451;.
   ACTIVITY - Herbicide.

AU HE X

   GAO Y
   ZHANG X
   FAN M
AE GEORGIA TECH RES CORP (GTEK-C)
   UNIV WYOMING (UYWO-C)
GA 2021E3463A
AB    NOVELTY - Ionic liquid (IL) catalyst comprises a                catalytic amine configured to capture carbon                dioxide (CO2), an ionic liquid where the ionic                liquid reacts with the catalytic amine to produce                an ionic liquid catalyst and the ionic liquid                catalyst is configured to capture carbon dioxide                (CO2).
   USE - Ionic liquid (IL) catalyst for catalytic                carbon dioxide(CO2) capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The ionic liquid catalyst reduces the overall                CO2 capture energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a process for synthesizing an ionic liquid                (IL) catalyst for capturing CO2 which                involves:
   a. mixing a basic amine with an acid, where                the acid is added to the basic amine under a                nitrogen atmosphere to form a cation                solution;
   b. evaporating the cation solution to obtain a                cation;
   c. reacting the cation with an anion in water                to form an IL solution;
   d.washing a bottom phase of the IL solution to                remove excess salt; and
   e. evaporating the IL solution to form the IL                catalyst;
   (2) a method for catalyzing both CO2 sorption                and desorption of chemisorption-based technologies,                which involves:
   i. providing an ionic liquid catalyst;
   ii. providing CO2 through a gas inlet;                and
   iii. absorbing and desorbing CO2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid                  catalyst is a catalyst for any chemisorption                  based CO2 captures. The ionic liquid catalyst is                  selected from the group of formula (EMmim+)(NTF )                  based catalysts. The catalyst comprises                  carboxylic acid (COOH). The catalytic amine is                  monoethanolamine. The IL catalyst comprises                  ethanoic acid based formula (CH3COOCH2mim)(NTF2),                  denoted as (EMmim) (NTF2). The process further                  comprises hydrolyzing an ester group of the IL                  catalyst to form carboxylic acid groups. The IL                  catalyst comprises Brensted acid sites. The                  cation comprises (EMmim)(Br+) and the anion                  comprises lithium based formula Li(NTF-). The                  basic amine comprises methylimidazole and the                  acid comprises methyl- bromoacetate. The IL                  catalyst is ultrasonic treated. the IL catalyst                  is thermally stable at temperatures 300degrees                  Celsius. The CO2 reacts with monoethanolamine                  (MEA) to form monoethanolamine based formula                  (MEA+COO-) intermediate and where the reaction is                  the rate limiting step. The method further                  comprises treating MEA with an ionic liquid (IL)                  for providing an IL catalyst. The absorbing and                  desorbing CO2 without the presence of water is a                  solid based CO2 capture, or liquid based CO2                  capture.

GA 2021E99220
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide analyzer, comprising a measuring chamber for measuring concentration of carbon dioxide; the measuring chamber is provided with a chamber; the measuring chamber side wall is provided with an air inlet pipe; the end part of the air inlet pipe located in the chamber is provided with a filter device; the chamber inner side wall fixedly connected with the light source device; a filter lens and a detector; the light source emitting light reaches the detector through the filter lens; the inner side wall of the chamber is connected with a cleaning layer through the cleaning device; the cleaning device is electrically connected with the control system of the peripheral device; The utility model controls the control system, so that the current direction in the electrified coil is changed, namely the forward direction and the reverse direction; the current direction in the electric coil generates change to drive the metal ring to reciprocate up and down, so as to drive the cleaning layer to wiping the mirror surface of the filter lens; keeping the accuracy of the continuous work of the carbon dioxide analyzer.

PT P
PN US2021316255-A1; WO2021211440-A1
TI Separation article used in separation of gaseous component from gaseous mixture comprising methane and carbon dioxide, comprises surfaces, and layer comprising amorphous crosslinked fluorinated copolymer adjacent to at least one surfaces.
AU WHITE W R
   JUNK C P
AE CHROMIS FIBEROPTICS INC (CHRO-Non-standard)
   CHROMIS FIBEROPTICS INC (CHRO-Non-standard)
GA 2021B7037S
AB    NOVELTY - A separation article comprises a surface (a) and a surface (b), and a layer comprising an amorphous crosslinked fluorinated copolymer adjacent to at least one of the surface (a) or the surface (b).
   USE - Separation article e.g. film, membrane, tube or fibers is used in separation of gaseous component which is carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and/or helium from gaseous mixture comprising methane and carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mechanically-robust separation article has superior selectivity and reliability performance, high thermal stability, and excellent chemical resistance. The crosslinking improves mechanical property of the fluoropolymer, thereby permitting use of polymer types which is excessively brittle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: separation (P1) of gaseous component (gc1) which is carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and/or helium from gaseous mixture comprising methane and carbon dioxide, which involves passing the gaseous mixture across the separation article; and amorphous crosslinked fluorinated copolymer, which is produced by (i) copolymerizing 1-99.5 mol% fluorinated ring monomers which is 5-membered ring and 0.2-40 mol% crosslinker in the presence of an initiator by solution or aqueous emulsion polymerization to produce a copolymer (c1), and (ii) crosslinking the copolymer (c1).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The fluorinated ring monomer is a perfluorinated ring monomer or an olefinic compound. The fluorinated ring monomer comprises 5-6-membered ring. The fluorinated ring monomer comprises compounds of formulae (A1)-(A4), preferably compound of formula (A1a) or (A1b). The crosslinker comprises a perfluorinated olefinic compound comprising at least one crosslinkable group, preferably chosen from 7 compounds e.g. compounds of formulae (B1)-(B5). The crosslinkable group comprises a photoactive group or a thermally active group. The crosslinkable group comprises an alkyl ester group, a cyano group, or a fluorinated vinyl ether group. The content of the fluorinated ring monomer is 80-99 mol%, and crosslinker is 0.2-20 mol%. The fluorinated ring monomer is of formula (C1), preferably chosen from compounds of formulae (C1a)-(C1c). The initiator comprises a hydrocarbon peroxide, a fluorocarbon peroxide, a hydrocarbon peroxydicarbonate and/or an inorganic fluorocarbon initiator. The permeate stream has a concentration of component (gc1) that is greater than a concentration of the component (gc1) in the retentate stream.R1-R8 = F, CF3, CF2CF3, CF2H, CF2CF2H, CFHCF3, or CFHCF2H; R9 = F, CF3, or CF2CF3; m,n = 1-3; and x = 1 or 2.(i) R6 and R7 can be contained within 5-6-membered ring.Preferred Process: The fluorinated ring monomer is produced by performing cyclopolymerization of a linear monomer comprising an alkenyl group on one end of the monomer and a vinyl ether on the other end of the monomer. The method (P1) involves passing the gaseous mixture across the separation article having a feed side and a permeate side, and a selective layer comprising the amorphous crosslinked fluorinated copolymer that is selectively permeable to the component (gc1), providing a driving force sufficient to provide for permeation of at least one portion of the gaseous mixture from the feed side to the permeate side of the separation article, resulting in a gaseous permeate stream comprising the component (gc1) on the permeate side of the separation article and a gaseous retentate stream on the feed side of the separation article, withdrawing the permeate stream from the permeate side of the separation article, and withdrawing the retentate stream from the feed side of the separation article. More than about 50% or more of the component (gc1) in the gaseous mixture permeates through the separation article.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The separation article comprises a multilayer structured article, and at least one layer of the structure comprises the copolymer. The separation article comprises a layer of the amorphous crosslinked fluorinated copolymer with a thickness of less than or equal to 1 microm.Preferred Process: The process (ii) is performed by exposing to ultraviolet radiation at a wavelength of less than 300 nm, preferably 250-300 nm from 0.5-48 hours, or by heating at 300-350degrees Celsius for 0.5-60 minutes. The fluorinated ring monomer and the crosslinker are copolymerized with a fluorinated vinyl monomer comprising a fluorinated vinyl compound or a fluorinated vinyl ether compound. The amorphous crosslinked fluorinated copolymer comprises the copolymer (c1) and crosslinker unit. The copolymer (c1) comprises 1-99.5 mol%, preferably 80-99 mol% fluorinated ring unit of formula (I) or (II), and 1-20 mol% perfluorinated co-monomeric unit of formula (III), (IV) or (V), that is not the fluorinated ring unit or crosslinker unit.Preferred Properties: The copolymer (c1) has a glass transition temperature of 0-300degrees Celsius, a number average molecular weight of 10-2000 kDa, and a weight average molecular weight of 10000-3000000 g/mol.

AU WANG C
   ZHONG Y
   YANG F
   TAN J
   WANG M
   HUANG M
AE SHENZHEN SHENSHUI BAOAN WATER CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A52920
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for adding carbon dioxide to adjust pH value in water factory water purifying process, comprising a gas dissolving tank; the top end of the gas dissolving tank is connected with a softening water injecting device through a pipeline; the middle part of the gas dissolving tank is connected with a carbon dioxide injecting device through a pipeline; the bottom end of the gas dissolving tank is connected with a feeding pump through a pipeline and a liquid outlet valve set on the pipeline; the pipeline connected with the outlet end of the feeding pump is further provided with a carbonic acid solution injection valve; the carbonic acid solution injection valve injects the mixed carbonic acid solution into the raw water pipe of the water purifying plant. The utility model prepares the carbon dioxide gas into supersaturated carbonic acid solution by the gas dissolving tank, the carbon dioxide utilization rate is high, then directly injecting the carbonic acid solution into the to-be-processed water by the precise metering device, accurately adjusting and stabilizing the pH value in the water.

AU HUNWICK R J
AE HUNWICK R J (HUNW-Individual)
   HUNWICK R J (HUNW-Individual)
   HUNWICK R J (HUNW-Individual)
   HUNWICK R J (HUNW-Individual)

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves enabling atmospheric air                to pass into an upper end of an elongate hollow                tower. PT P
PN WO2021029979-A1; US2022280911-A1
TI Increasing hydrothermal stability of adsorbent            comprising a zeolite, which is used to remove carbon            dioxide from feed stream, comprises coating zeolite            with silylation agent to get silylated zeolite, and            performing swing adsorption process.
AU WANG Y
   LI Q
   CUNDY S M
   DECKMAN H W
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202117353U
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing (P1) hydrothermal stability of an                adsorbent comprising a zeolite in a swing                adsorption process, comprises coating the zeolite                with a silylation agent to result in a silylated                zeolite, and performing the swing adsorption                process, where the swing adsorption process                comprises contacting the silylated zeolite with                feed stream comprising water. The zeolite is a                small pore cationic zeolite.
   USE - The process is useful for increasing                hydrothermal stability of an adsorbent comprising a                zeolite in a swing adsorption process which is                useful for removing carbon dioxide from a feed                stream (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: improves hydrothermal stability                of the adsorbent, thus increasing the lifetime of                an adsorbent, hence economical; and slows down the                degradation of the adsorbent while maintaining                properties e.g. equilibrium adsorptive properties                with respect to small molecules.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for:                increasing (P2) hydrothermal stability of an                adsorbent comprising a zeolite in a swing                adsorption process for removal of carbon dioxide                from a feed stream, comprising coating the zeolite                with a silylation agent to result in a silylated                zeolite; and performing the swing adsorption                process for removal of carbon dioxide from the feed                stream, where the feed stream comprises carbon                dioxide and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The swing adsorption                  process is pressure swing adsorption (PSA)                  process, temperature swing adsorption (TSA)                  process, pressure temperature swing adsorption                  (PTSA) process, vacuum swing adsorption (VSA)                  process, vacuum temperature swing adsorption                  (VTSA) process, partial pressure purge                  displacement (PPSA) process, partial pressure                  temperature swing adsorption (PPTSA) process                  and/or displacement desorption swing adsorption                  (DDSA) process. The swing adsorption process                  preferably is a rapid cycle swing adsorption                  process, where the swing adsorption process odf                  (P1) removes water from the feed stream and                  results in a product stream with less than 10 ppm                  of water. The feed stream further comprises an                  acid gas, where the acid gas is hydrogen sulfide                  or carbon dioxide. In the process (P2), the feed                  stream comprises less than 1000 ppm water and the                  adsorption process results in a product stream                  with less than 100 ppm of carbon dioxide.                  Preferred Components: The small pore zeolite has:                  an 8 membered ring structure; and a maximum                  effective pore size of less than 5 Angstrom . The                  small pore zeolite comprises a structure type                  ABW, AEI, AFX, ANA, ATT, BCT, BIK, BRE, CAS, CDO,                  CHA, DDR, EAB, EDI, EEI, EPI, ERI, ESV, GIS, GOO,                  IHW, ITE, JBW, KFI, LEV, LTA, LTJ, LTN, MER, MON,                  MTF, MWF, NSI, PAU, PHI, RHO, RTH, SAS, SFW, THO,                  TSC, UFI, YUG, ETL, IFY, ITW, RTE and/or RWR. The                  zeolite is coated either before formulating the                  adsorbent or after formulating the adsorbent. The                  silylation agent is of the formula (SiR1R2R3R4)                  (I).At least one of R1, R2, R3, and R4 = a                  hydrolysable moiety, andthe remaining radicals R1-R4 = alkyl,                  alkenyl, alkynyl, heteroalkyl, cycloalkyl,                  heteroaryl, alkylcycloalkyl,                  hetero(alkylcycloalkyl), heterocycloalkyl, aryl,                  arylalkyl or hetero(arylalkyl).The silylation agent preferably is                  hexamethyldisilazane or trimethylsilyl                  chloride.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109270843-A; CN109270843-B
TI Waterway fuzzy proportional integral derivative (PID) control method for trans-critical carbon dioxide system, involves substituting final output into PID controller and controlling opening of water flow regulating valve.
AU CAO F
   YE Z
   WANG Y
   LI M
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2019117814
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining the variables and domains. The variables determined are fuzzified. The domain of input and output variables is defined as seven fuzzy subsets. The linear membership function for subset is established. The fuzzy inference rule table is established. The input variables are calculated according to set outlet water temperature. The membership degree of input variable on each fuzzy subset is calculated according to membership function. The deblurring operation is performed by weighted average method. The calculation results are corrected, according to the operating conditions of the trans-critical carbon dioxide system. The real-time ambient temperature, gas cooler inlet water temperature and gas cooler outlet water temperature are collected. The final output is substituted into proportional integral derivative (PID) controller, and the opening of water flow regulating valve in trans-critical carbon dioxide system unit is controlled by PID controller.
   USE - Waterway fuzzy PID control method for trans-critical carbon dioxide system.
   ADVANTAGE - The targeted control strategy ensures stable and safe control under extreme operating conditions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a waterway fuzzy PID control method for trans-critical carbon dioxide system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

TI Visual ultra-critical carbon dioxide fracturing            physical simulation testing method, involves removing            pore pressure and three-way confining pressure in rock            sample, cutting rock sample to observe hydraulic crack            in side of rock sample.
AU ZHANG G
   ZHENG X
   WANG Y
   LIN C
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 2018A0416B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves fixing a cured rock sample                in a confining pressure chamber of a visual                two-dimensional hydraulic fracture simulation                experimental device. Three-way confining pressure                and pore pressure are applied to the cured rock                sample. A high-speed camera is opened on a top end                of the visual two-dimensional hydraulic crack                simulation experimental device. Supercritical                carbon dioxide fracturing fluid is injected into a                center hole of the rock sample. Injection pressure                and rock sample test surface image information are                continuously recorded in testing process. Injection                pressure, pore pressure and three-way confining                pressure in the rock sample are sequentially                removed. The rock sample is cut to observe a                hydraulic crack in an inner side of the rock                sample.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables avoiding critical carbon                dioxide fracturing manual initiation in                full-process image, and reducing rock sample                surface stress, strain and pore pressure.

PN CN108728152-A; CN108728152-B
TI Method for preparing synthetic oil, involves reacting methane with water vapor under steam reforming condition, reacting methane with carbon dioxide and re-integrating portion of water vapor into gas.
AU JIN C
   WU Y
   ZHANG R
   HOU C
   SUN X
   YAN Z
   XIA G
   LI M
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
GA 201887231T
AB    NOVELTY - A synthetic oil preparing method involves reacting methane with water vapor under steam reforming condition to obtain steam reforming synthesis gas, reacting methane with carbon dioxide under dry reforming conditions to obtain a dry reforming synthesis gas. A portion of the water vapor is re-integrated into a gas and integrated a partial steam reforming Tropsch synthesis catalyst and Fischer synthesis gas to prepare a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed. The Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed is contacted with a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst at a reaction temperature for producing a alpha -olefin to obtain a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis product stream. The alpha -olefin, methane and carbon dioxide are separated from the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis product stream, sent the separated methane to steam reforming synthesis gas and dry reforming synthesis gas forming steps, and sent the separated carbon dioxide to dry reforming synthesis gas forming step.
   USE - Method for preparing synthetic oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing the synthetic oil with effectively reducing the system energy consumption and greenhouse carbon dioxide gas emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a synthetic oil prepared by the method
   (2) a synthetic oil production system.

AB    NOVELTY - Extracting smoke oil involves extracting                liquid mixture of liquid carbon dioxide and smoke                oil, separately collecting the smoke oil and the                liquid carbon dioxide filtered by the mixture,                separating and purifying the collected smoke oil,                purifying subsequent smoky oil, collecting liquid                carbon dioxide and returning to process tank.
   USE - Method for extracting smoke oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to extract smoke oil which                does not contaminate system. The product is highly                pure and has good efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for a smoke oil extraction device, which comprises                process tank, a conveying pipe (1), a conveying                pump (2), a filtering device (3), a collecting                device (4), a separating and purifying device (5),                and the process tank is connected to the pump                through a conveying pipe The other end of the pump                is connected to the filtering device through a                conveying pipe, the filtering device is connected                with the collecting device, and the collecting                device is respectively connected with the process                tank and the separating and purifying device                through the conveying pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a smoke                oil extraction device.
   Conveying pipe (1)
   Conveying pump (2)
   Filtering device (3)
   Collecting device (4)
   Separating and purifying device (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The mixture of the                  liquid carbon dioxide and the smoke oil in the                  extraction process tank enters the transfer pump                  through the transfer pipeline, and the transfer                  pump delivers the mixed liquid to the filter                  device. The mixed liquid conveyed by the transfer                  pump separates the viscous smoke oil containing                  impurities from the pure liquid carbon dioxide                  through an adsorption net provided in the filter                  device. The smoke oil remains on the adsorption                  net. The viscous smoke oil on the adsorption                  network is mixed with the dilute solvent, and the                  mixed liquid smoke oil and liquid carbon dioxide                  are separately collected. The collected pure                  liquid carbon dioxide is returned to the process                  tank through a conveying pipe, and the side wall                  of the process tank is washed during the reflow                  process and mixed in the process tank. The liquid                  is stirred. The liquid smoke oil contains                  impurity after dilution is separated and purified                  to obtain a higher purity smoke oil for                  collection. Preferred Composition: The filtering                  device is configured to separate the mixed smoke                  liquid containing the impurities from the pure                  liquid carbon dioxide by the mixed liquid                  conveyed by the transfer pump through the                  adsorption net. The smoke oil remains on the                  adsorption net. The viscous smoke oil on the                  adsorption net is mixed with the dilute solvent,                  and the mixed liquid smoke oil and liquid carbon                  dioxide are respectively transported to the                  collection device. The process tank is uniformly                  provided with return ports, and the pure liquid                  carbon dioxide in the collecting device enters                  the respective return ports of the process tank                  through the conveying pipe. The side wall of the                  process tank is flushed during the reflow process                  and the mixture in the process tank is agitated.                  The separation and purification device is                  configured to separate and purify the diluted                  liquid oil containing impurities conveyed by the                  collection device to obtain a higher purity smoke                  oil. The pipeline is collected.

AB    NOVELTY - Temperature-sensitive dynamic release carbon                dioxide heat-extinguishing material comprises an                ionic liquid capable of absorbing carbon dioxide,                an alcohol amine, a surfactant and water, where the                ratio of the ionic liquid to the total absorption                liquid is 5-15%, the ratio of the alcohol amine                absorbent to the total absorbed liquid is 5-15%,                the amount of the surfactant is 0.5% and the amount                of water is 70-89.5%.
   USE - Used as temperature-sensitive dynamic release                carbon dioxide heat-extinguishing material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                method of temperature-sensitive dynamic release                carbon dioxide heat-extinguishing material,                comprising (i) configuring a carbon dioxide                absorbing liquid at a normal temperature, adding                pyridine type ionic liquid (or other type ionic                liquid), N-methyldiethanolamine (MDEA) or other                alcohol amines, water and a surfactant into a                mixing tank and mixing uniformly to obtain an                absorption solution, (ii) introducing a high                concentration of carbon dioxide gas into the                absorption liquid and allowing the absorption                liquid to absorb carbon dioxide to reach a                saturated state, (iii) transporting the saturated                carbon dioxide absorbing liquid to the well or                transporting to the well through the grouting                pipeline and (iv) using the pump, injecting the                absorption liquid into the gob area and the area                requiring heat prevention according to the                accompanying injection (sprinkling) or with the                yellow mud slurry through the pre-buried grouting                pipe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol amines are                  N-N-methyldiethanolamine (MDEA), and may also be                  other alcohol amines and the ratio of the alcohol                  amine absorbent to the total absorbed liquid is                  5-15%. The surfactant is sodium                  dodecylbenzenesulfonate or sodium lauryl sulfate                  and the amount of the absorbent is 0.5%.                  Preferred Method: The ionic liquid capable of                  absorbing carbon dioxide is mainly a pyridine                  type ionic liquid, and may be another type of                  ionic liquid and the ratio of ionic liquid to                  total absorbed liquid is 5-15%. The material is                  capable of absorbing a more amount of carbon                  dioxide at a normal temperature and having an                  absorption capacity of 0.15-0.20 mol/mol. The                  material releases carbon dioxide at an initial                  temperature of 75-80 degrees C, the rate of                  carbon dioxide release increases gradually as the                  temperature increases and the rate of carbon                  dioxide release reaches a maximum when the                  temperature reaches to 100-120 degrees C. The                  material can absorb carbon dioxide from the mine                  for repeated use after the carbon dioxide is                  released from the material.

PN WO2021226410-A1; US2021348300-A1; US2021348301-A1; US11371162-B2; US2022290325-A1; US2022316094-A1
TI Generating synthetic diamond used for transforming            atmospheric carbon into diamonds by conveying mixture            comprising carbon dioxide and impurities through a            pressurized unit at temperatures to promote            liquefaction and mixing another mixture.
AU SHEARMAN R
   WOJNO D
   IPPOLITO A
AE IMPOSSIBLE DIAMOND INC (IMPO-Non-standard)
   IMPOSSIBLE DIAMOND INC (IMPO-Non-standard)
GA 2021C5861B
AB    NOVELTY - A synthetic diamond is generated by ingesting                a first mixture extracted from a first air sample,                where the first mixture comprises carbon dioxide                and first concentration of impurities comprising                nitrogen; conveying the first mixture through a                pressurized unit at temperatures to promote                liquefaction of first mixture; in a methanation                reactor, mixing the second mixture with a stream of                hydrogen to generate first hydrocarbon mixture                comprising hydrocarbons and a third concentration                of impurities comprising nitrogen, carbon dioxide,                and hydrogen; conveying the first hydrocarbon                mixture through a separation unit which removes                impurities from hydrocarbon mixture to generate a                second hydrocarbon mixture comprising hydrocarbons                and fourth concentration of impurities less than                the third concentration of impurities; and                depositing the second hydrocarbon mixture in a                diamond reactor containing a set of diamond seeds                to generate a first set of diamonds via chemical                vapor deposition.
   USE - Generation of synthetic diamond used for                transforming atmospheric carbon into                diamonds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces carbon dioxide in the                atmosphere and provides an alternative to natural                diamonds sourced via dangerous and                environment-unfriendly practices. Since the                diamonds are generated from carbon dioxide                extracted from air captured in a particular                location, at a particular date and time, and/ or                near a particular individual or group of people,                these diamonds can be uniquely associated with                particular locations, times, dates, and people and                individual stories, thus, achieve greater relevance                and importance to owners and wearers (e.g., than                mined diamonds).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A synthetic diamond is generated by ingesting                a first mixture extracted from a first air sample,                where the first mixture comprises carbon dioxide                and first concentration of impurities comprising                nitrogen; conveying the first mixture through a                pressurized unit at temperatures within a first                temperature range to promote liquefaction of first                mixture to generate first exhaust stream comprising                impurities comprising nitrogen and second mixture                comprising carbon dioxide and second concentration                of impurities less than the first concentration of                impurities; in a methanation reactor, mixing the                second mixture with a stream of hydrogen to                generate first hydrocarbon mixture comprising                hydrocarbons and a third concentration of                impurities comprising nitrogen, carbon dioxide, and                hydrogen; conveying the first hydrocarbon mixture                through a separation unit which removes impurities                from hydrocarbon mixture to generate a second                hydrocarbon mixture comprising hydrocarbons and                fourth concentration of impurities less than the                third concentration of impurities; and depositing                the second hydrocarbon mixture in a diamond reactor                containing a set of diamond seeds to generate a                first set of diamonds via chemical vapor                deposition. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                generating synthetic diamonds which involves                ingesting a first air sample collected during an                air capture period to extract a first mixture                comprising carbon dioxide and a first concentration                of impurities comprising nitrogen; conveying the                first mixture through a first liquefaction unit                configured to remove impurities from the first                mixture to generate first exhaust stream comprising                impurities comprising nitrogen, and second mixture                comprising carbon dioxide and a second                concentration of impurities less than the first                concentration of impurities; in a methanation                reactor, mixing the second mixture with a stream of                hydrogen to generate a first hydrocarbon mixture                comprising hydrocarbons and a third concentration                of impurities comprising nitrogen, carbon dioxide,                and hydrogen; conveying the first hydrocarbon                mixture through a second liquefaction unit                configured to remove impurities from the second                hydrocarbon mixture to generate second exhaust                stream comprising impurities, and second                hydrocarbon mixture comprising hydrocarbons and a                fourth concentration of impurities less than the                third concentration of impurities; and depositing                the second hydrocarbon mixture in a diamond reactor                containing a set of diamond seeds to generate a                first set of diamonds via chemical vapor                deposition.

TI Separating a mixture of a high-grade ketone, water and carbon dioxide comprises e.g. introducing a high-grade ketone, water and carbon dioxide raw material to be separated from a raw material inlet of the dividing wall column.
AU LI D
   XIA M
   JIA L
   HOU B
   XIAO Y
   LU H
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 201936535G
AB    NOVELTY - Separating a mixture of a high-grade ketone, water and carbon dioxide comprises e.g. introducing a high-grade ketone, water and carbon dioxide raw material to be separated from a raw material inlet of the dividing wall column, under the action of providing heat by a wall separating left side reboiler, performing gas-liquid separation, gas phase gets out from a gas phase outlet of the dividing wall column, and entering a condenser, obtaining condensing liquid and enters a gas-liquid-liquid separator, performing heat insulation at constant pressure process, separating to obtain carbon dioxide-rich gas phase, ketone-rich phase and water-rich phase, ketone-rich phase flows back to the dividing wall column left half side, water-rich phase flows back to the dividing wall column right half side, and extracting to obtain the carbon dioxide-rich gas for releasing, and obtaining waste water with trace amount of ketone content.
   USE - The method is useful for separating a mixture of a high-grade ketone, water and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has good separation effect, has installation space; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating a mixture of a high-grade ketone, water and carbon dioxide comprises introducing a high-grade ketone, water and carbon dioxide raw material to be separated from a raw material inlet of the dividing wall column, under the action of providing heat by a wall separating left side reboiler, performing gas-liquid separation, gas phase gets out from a gas phase outlet of the dividing wall column, and entering a condenser, obtaining condensing liquid and enters a gas-liquid-liquid separator, performing heat insulation at constant pressure process for 10-30 minutes of residence time of sufficient gas-liquid-liquid balance, separating to obtain carbon dioxide-rich gas phase, ketone-rich phase and water-rich phase, ketone-rich phase flows back to the dividing wall column left half side, water-rich phase flows back to the dividing wall column right half side, and extracting to obtain the carbon dioxide-rich gas for releasing, obtaining waste water with trace amount of ketone content at the dividing wall column right half side and obtaining the high grade ketone product at the dividing wall column left half side. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for dividing wall column, comprising a shell (6), a lower end enclosure (7), an upper end enclosure (8), the clapboard (13) is located between the shells is along the axial direction of the shell, the shell is divided into a dividing wall column left half side (9) and dividing wall column right-side, clapboard is lower end enclosure, clapboard is upper end enclosure or between the upper end enclosure with a part of the space, the dividing wall column left half side with the ketone-rich phase inlet, the middle raw material inlet to be separated, the lower part of the rich-phase partition left side inlet, the lower end enclosure of the left semi-side of the dividing wall column bottom has advanced one outlet on the dividing wall column right half side with the water-rich phase inlet, the lower part is provided with the water-rich phase partition left side inlet, the lower end enclosure of right half side and the dividing wall column bottom with aqueous phase outlet and the upper end enclosure is provided with a gas phase outlet.
