PT P
PN US2019071338-A1; US10604432-B2
TI Treating livestock waste containing ammonia            nitrogen to recover dissolved ammonium bicarbonate            involves e.g. treating the waste to form concentrated            gas, treating the gas to form dissolved ammonium            carbonate solution and crystallizing.
AU BASSANI D
   ORENTLICHER M
   SIMON M M
   PAGANO S
AE BION ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGIES INC (BION-Non-standard)
GA 2019217796
AB    NOVELTY - Treating (M1) livestock waste containing                ammonia nitrogen involves treating the waste to                create a gas without the use of chemicals,                converting substantially all ammonia in the waste                to gaseous form in the gas, the gas also containing                carbon dioxide and water vapor, treating the gas by                partial condensation to concentrate the ammonia and                carbon dioxide followed by absorption of the                concentrate gases and removing significant amount                of water vapor in liquid condensate form from the                gas, causing formation of a concentrated gas                containing higher concentrations of ammonia gas and                carbon dioxide gas than before condensation,                treating the concentrated gas to form dissolved                ammonium carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate liquid                solution, crystallizing the dissolved ammonium                carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate condensate                liquid solution at a temperature of less than 35                degrees C to form solid ammonium bicarbonate and                ammonium carbonate.
   USE - The process is useful for removing the ammonia                nitrogen within a liquid wastewater and capturing                the nitrogen in the form of crystallized ammonium                bicarbonate from livestock waste which is useful                for preparing organic N-fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: removes ammonium bicarbonate                derived from wastewaters, sludges and solids                containing ammonia and carbon dioxide, without the                use of chemical additives; utilizes two-stage                reverse osmosis unit dioxide gas, thus eliminating                the need to concentrate the ammonium bicarbonate                solution after condensation using filters, hence                reducing cost, and maintenance expense of a                filtering unit process, potentially providing a                more efficient and cost effective overall                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating (M1) livestock waste containing                ammonia nitrogen comprises treating the waste at                least 80 degrees C to create a gas and, without the                use of chemicals, converting substantially all                ammonia in the waste to gaseous form in the gas,                the gas also containing carbon dioxide and water                vapor, treating the gas by partial condensation to                concentrate the ammonia and carbon dioxide followed                by absorption of the concentrate gases, or total                condensation of the gas and/or followed by                dewatering using reverse osmosis, without the use                of chemical(s) and remove from the gas a                significant amount of water vapor in liquid                condensate form, causing formation of a                concentrated gas containing higher concentrations                of ammonia gas and carbon dioxide gas than before                condensation, treating the concentrated gas using                absorption to form a dissolved ammonium carbonate                and ammonium bicarbonate liquid solution at the                saturation concentrations for dissolved ammonium                carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate, crystallizing                the dissolved ammonium carbonate and ammonium                bicarbonate condensate liquid solution at a                temperature of less than 35 degrees C to form solid                ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium carbonate. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for treating                (M2) livestock waste containing ammonia nitrogen                comprising treating the waste using anaerobic                digestion creating a biogas and a digestate,                treating the biogas to separate and concentrate                carbon dioxide in the biogas from other gases in                the biogas forming a concentrated carbon dioxide                biogas, treating the digestate at least 80 degrees                C to create the gas and, removing a significant                amount of water vapor and organics in liquid                condensate form, causing formation of a condenser                effluent concentrated gas containing higher                concentrations of ammonia gas and carbon dioxide                gas than before condensation, treating the                concentrated carbon dioxide biogas and the                condenser effluent concentrated gas using                absorption operating at a 20-50 degrees C to form a                dissolved ammonium carbonate and ammonium                bicarbonate liquid solution at the saturation                concentrations for dissolved ammonium carbonate and                ammonium bicarbonate at the crystallizer                temperature, crystallizing the dissolved ammonium                carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate condensate                liquid solution at less than 35 degrees C to form                solid ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium                carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The treating livestock                  step comprises: stripping the waste of ammonia                  using biogas from an anaerobic digester used to                  treat the waste prior to stripping; drying the                  waste using a dryer and removing ammonia with the                  water vapor in gaseous form; separation of the                  ammonia and other gases using membrane modules,                  where substantially all the gaseous ammonia in                  the gas is converted into ammonium carbonate and                  ammonium bicarbonate using the carbon dioxide in                  the gas without the addition of carbon dioxide                  not present in the digestate or with the addition                  of carbon dioxide from another source. The                  condensation is a two stage condensation process                  comprising sequential condensation at two                  temperatures or residence times. The process (M2)                  further comprises recycling a portion of the                  saturated dissolved ammonium carbonate and                  ammonium bicarbonate liquid solution from the                  crystallization to the absorber to assist with                  the absorption. The solid crystals of ammonium                  bicarbonate grown in the crystallizer are                  produced creating a solid ammonium-salt.                  Preferred Components: The waste is digested dairy                  manure. The ammonium-salt is dried, pelletized or                  granulated.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

TI Carbon dioxide supply device for supplying carbon dioxide to plant planted in cultivation room of greenhouses, has air mixing unit that is provided with combustion gas from exhaust port, so as to generate air-fuel mixture with air for mixing.
AU YAMAJI H
AE NORITZ CORP (NOTS-C)
GA 202279212Y
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has a combustion gas generating unit (100) which is provided to output combustion gas containing carbon dioxide from an exhaust port (105). An air mixing unit (200) is arranged in a housing and arranged in lower portion of combustion gas generation unit. A supply duct (310) is provided for derived carbon dioxide outside a carbon dioxide gas supply device from the air mixing portion. A housing opening unit is provided in housing for storing combustion gas generator unit. An opening unit for generating ventilation passage is provided between combustion gas and air mixing units. The air mixing portions are provided with combustion gas from the exhaust port.
   USE - Carbon dioxide gas supply apparatus for supplying carbon dioxide gas for plants planted in cultivation room of greenhouses, plastic greenhouses and other plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The size of the carbon dioxide supply device is reduced by arranging the air mixing unit at the lower portion of the combustion gas generation unit. The temperature rise inside the device is suppressed by passing air through the inside of combustion gas generating unit.

PN KR2021121730-A; KR2354125-B1
TI Crystalline silica-based scavenger for treatment of carbon dioxide containing radioactive carbon comprises silica structure, and boron oxide ions and alkaline earth metal ions impregnated in silica structure.
AU KIMHYUNGJOO
   KIMSEONGJUN
   YANGHEECHUL
   HEE-CHUL E
   LEE K Y
   SEO B K
AE KOREA ATOMIC ENERGY RES INST (KAER-C)
GA 2021B5897W
AB    NOVELTY - Crystalline silica-based scavenger comprises silica structure, and boron oxide ions and alkaline earth metal ions impregnated in the silica structure, where oxygen ions in the alkaline earth metal ions and boron oxide ions form an ionic bond.
   USE - The crystalline silica-based scavenger is useful for treatment of carbon dioxide containing radioactive carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The crystalline silica-based scavenger can easily dissociate ionic bonds, has high efficiency (fast reaction rate and high collection amount) under mild reaction conditions, is suitable for immobilization and solidification and can create great demand for capture of radiocarbon in carbon waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing crystalline silica-based trapping agent, comprising (a) mixing silica, boron oxide and alkaline earth metal oxide, melting and cooling to prepare an amorphous silica-based scavenger, and (b) raising the temperature of the prepared amorphous silica-based trapping agent above the crystalline temperature, maintaining for a certain time, and then cooling; and
   (2) radiocarbon-containing carbon dioxide treatment system, comprising a carbon dioxide gas cylinder for supplying carbon dioxide containing radioactive carbon. A carbon dioxide capture reactor for converting the supplied carbon dioxide into liquefaction and carbonate. A carbon dioxide detector for detecting the generation of the non-captured carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide trapping reactor is a crystalline silica-based trapping agent dispersed in water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline earth metal ion comprises strontium (Sr), calcium (Ca), barium (Ba), radium (Ra), magnesium (Mg), and/or beryllium (Be). The crystalline silica-based trapping agent is in a gel state dispersed in water. In the step (a) molar ratio of silica, boron oxide and alkaline earth metal oxide is 30:15:55-40:25:35. The carbon dioxide trapping reactor comprises 1-10 g silica-based trapping agent with respect to 100 ml of the water. Preferred Method: In the step (a) melting is performed at 900-1400 degrees C. In the step (b) crystallinity temperature is 700-800 degrees C. In the step (b), temperature increase is performed at a rate of 1-10 degrees C/min. In the step (b) holding is for 30 minutes to 10 hours. The carbon dioxide capture reactor maintains a temperature of 20-40 degrees C and a pressure condition of 0.5-1.5 atm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystalline structure contains boron-based compound of formula (I).M = alkaline earth metal.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of quantitative and qualitative analysis of crystalline silica-based scavenger that do not support the claims.

AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 201907409K
AB    NOVELTY - The renewable energy calculation method involves absorbing the carbon dioxide into the absorbent (1). The balance load of the absorbent absorbing the carbon dioxide is measured. A weight fraction of the unreacted absorbent and weight fraction of the carbon dioxide about the absorbent total weight is produced. The renewable energy of the absorbent absorbing the carbon dioxide is produced using the phase change calorie of the absorbent. The phase change calorie of the standard absorbent is measured by the differential scanning calorimetry. The measurement of the weight reduction by the thermogravimetric analysis is performed about the standard absorbent with the measurement of the phase change calorie by the differential scanning calorimetry.
   USE - Renewable energy calculation method for the carbon dioxide liquid absorbent for processing the combustion fossil power.
   ADVANTAGE - The measurement of the weight reduction by the thermogravimetric analysis is performed about the standard absorbent with the measurement of the phase change calorie, and hence ensures accurately calculating the renewable energy value using the absorption equilibrium experiment and thermal analysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a renewable energy indexing system for the carbon dioxide liquid absorbent; and
   (2) a performance evaluation method for the carbon dioxide liquid absorbent.

PT P
PN EP3741863-A1
TI Producing organic acid and/or alcohol comprises e.g. providing basic, aqueous medium containing captured carbon dioxide in form of hydrogencarbonate-anions and/or carbonate-anions, and feeding basic, aqueous medium comprising anode.
AU HAAS T
AE EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 2020B65617
AB    NOVELTY - Producing organic acid and/or alcohol comprises e.g. (a) providing a basic, aqueous medium containing captured carbon dioxide in the form of hydrogencarbonate-anions and/or carbonate-anions, (b) feeding the basic, aqueous medium containing the captured carbon dioxide into a bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit comprising an anode, a cathode, an ion-selective anion-exchange membrane, and two water-dissociating bipolar membranes, capable of providing for an acidic compartment between the water-dissociating bipolar membrane on the anode side and the ion-selective anion-exchange membrane and for a basic compartment between the water-dissociating bipolar membrane on the cathode side and the ion-selective anion-exchange membrane, and (c) regenerating the captured carbon dioxide to regenerated carbon dioxide by transporting the captured carbon dioxide in the form of hydrogencarbonate-anions and/or carbonate-anions across the anion-exchange membrane from the basic compartment.
   USE - The method is useful for: producing organic acid and/or alcohol; and performing the process (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the carbon dioxide concentration in the air; and provides a use for the carbon dioxide which is stored.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing organic acid and/or alcohol comprises (a) providing a basic, aqueous medium containing captured carbon dioxide in the form of hydrogencarbonate-anions and/or carbonate-anions, (b) feeding the basic, aqueous medium containing the captured carbon dioxide into a bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit comprising an anode, a cathode, an ion-selective anion-exchange membrane, and two water-dissociating bipolar membranes, capable of providing for an acidic compartment between the water-dissociating bipolar membrane on the anode side and the ion-selective anion-exchange membrane and for a basic compartment between the water-dissociating bipolar membrane on the cathode side and the ion-selective anion-exchange membrane, (c) regenerating the captured carbon dioxide to regenerated carbon dioxide by transporting the captured carbon dioxide in the form of hydrogencarbonate-anions and/or carbonate-anions across the anion-exchange membrane from the basic compartment into the acidic compartment in acidic, aqueous solution, (d) contacting the regenerated carbon dioxide and hydrogen with at least one acetogenic cell in an aqueous production medium under suitable conditions to produce at least one organic acid and/or alcohol from the regenerated carbon dioxide, and optionally (e) recovering the organic acid and/or alcohol. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for apparatus for performing a process comprising a bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit comprising an anode, a cathode, an ion-selective anion-exchange membrane, a means of receiving captured carbon dioxide, and two water-dissociating bipolar membranes, capable of providing for an acidic compartment between the water-dissociating bipolar membrane on the anode side and the ion-selective anion-exchange membrane and for a basic compartment between the water-dissociating bipolar membrane on the cathode side and the ion-selective anion-exchange membrane, a fermenter comprising at least one acetogenic cell, an aqueous production medium, a means of receiving a hydrogen stream, and a means of receiving the recovered carbon dioxide, where the acetogenic cell is capable of converting the hydrogen and the regenerated carbon dioxide to at least one organic acid and/or alcohol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises providing a mixture of gases comprising carbon dioxide and contacting the mixture of gases with a basic, aqueous medium containing at least one base, thus capturing the carbon dioxide, which is contained in the basic, aqueous medium in the form of hydrogencarbonate- and/or carbonate-anions. The basic, aqueous medium in step (a) contains a base comprising hydroxides or carbonates, preferably of alkaline and earth alkaline hydroxides and carbonates, preferably comprising sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide or calcium hydroxide. In the step (a) the basic, aqueous medium is sprayed through a nozzle into a stream of the mixture of gases comprising carbon dioxide. In the step (b) the bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit comprises an alternating stack of ion-selective anion-exchange membranes and water-dissociating bipolar membranes of an amount 3-20 membrane. In the step (c), amperage of 5-20 A, preferably 5-10 A is applied to the bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit. In the step (d) the contacting takes place in a fermenter comprising the at least one acetogenic cell, the aqueous production medium, a means of receiving a hydrogen stream, and a means of receiving the recovered carbon dioxide. In the step (d) the pH of the aqueous production medium is 5-6.5. The organic acid and/or alcohol produced in step (d) are acetic acid and/or ethanol. The organic acid and/or alcohol produced in step (d) are acetic acid and/or ethanol, preferably with the acetogenic cell being Clostridium autoethanogenum, comprising a further step (f) contacting the acetic acid and/or ethanol with a second organism capable of converting acetic acid and/or ethanol to at least one fatty acid. Preferred Components: The mixture of gases comprises carbon dioxide at a concentration of 350-450 ppm, preferably the mixture of gases is air. In the step (d) the acetogenic cell comprises Clostridium aceticum (DSM 1496), Clostridium autoethanogenum (DSM 10061, DSM 19630 and DSM 23693), Clostridium carboxidivorans (DSM 15243), Clostridium coskatii (ATCC no. PTA-10522), Clostridium drakei (ATCC BA-623), Clostridium formicoaceticum (DSM 92), Clostridium glycolicum (DSM 1288), Clostridium ljungdahlii (DSM 13528), Clostridium ljungdahlii C-01 (ATCC 55988), Clostridium ljungdahlii ERI-2 (ATCC 55380), Clostridium ljungdahlii O-52 (ATCC 55989), Clostridium mayombei (DSM 6539), Clostridium methoxybenzovorans (DSM 12182), Clostridium ragsdalei (DSM 15248), Clostridium scatologenes (DSM 757), or Clostridium species ATCC 29797. The second organism is Clostridium kluyveri and preferably the at least one fatty acid is hexanoic acid. The apparatus further comprises: a means of contacting carbon dioxide in a mixture of gases with a basic, aqueous medium to capture the carbon dioxide; and a means of recovering the organic acid and/or alcohol.

AB    NOVELTY - A zero-carbon-emission ethanol reforming hydrogen production fuel cell system comprises an oxygen-enriched carbon dioxide gas flow generating device to generate oxygen-enriched carbon dioxide gas flow, a reforming hydrogen production device including a heat supplier for supplying heat to the reforming hydrogen production chamber and the oxygen-rich carbon dioxide gas flow generating device and a reforming hydrogen production chamber to prepare hydrogen-rich gas, an ethanol aqueous solution outlet of the ethanol aqueous solution storage device is communicated with the ethanol vapor inlet of the reforming hydrogen production chamber through a pipeline, a oxygen-rich gas outlet is in communication with the cathode inlet of the hydrogen fuel cell through a pipeline, and a discharge port of the hydrogen fuel cell is in communication with the carbon dioxide liquefaction recovery device through a valve (i) and carbon dioxide intake port.
   USE - Zero-carbon-emission ethanol reforming hydrogen production fuel cell system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A zero-carbon-emission ethanol reforming hydrogen production fuel cell system comprises an oxygen-enriched carbon dioxide gas flow generating device to generate oxygen-enriched carbon dioxide gas flow, and includes a fixed bed (A), a fixed bed (B), an air inlet, a carbon dioxide inlet, an oxygen-deficient air outlet, and an oxygen enriched air outlet, where the fixed bed (A) and the fixed bed (B) have an oxygen carrier and are used for oxygen desorption or oxygen adsorption, the air inlet is connected to the input ends of the fixed bed (A) and (B) through a valve (a) and a valve (b), the input end of the fixed bed (A) is connected to the carbon dioxide inlet through a valve (c), a carbon dioxide air inlet is connected to the input end of the fixed bed (B) through a valve (d), the anoxic air outlet is connected to the output end of the fixed bed (A) through a valve (e), the output end of the fixed bed (B) is connected to the oxygen-deficient air outlet and the oxygen-rich gas outlet through a valve (f) and a valve (g), the output end of the fixed bed (A) and the oxygen-rich gas outlet are connected through a valve (h), a reforming hydrogen production device including a heat supplier for supplying heat to the reforming hydrogen production chamber and the oxygen-rich CO2 gas flow generating device and a reforming hydrogen production chamber to prepare hydrogen-rich gas, an ethanol aqueous solution outlet of the ethanol aqueous solution storage device is communicated with the ethanol vapor inlet of the reforming hydrogen production chamber through a pipeline, and the ethanol aqueous solution in this pipeline is heated to ethanol vapor through the heat exchange device, the outlet of the gas product prepared by the reforming hydrogen production chamber is communicated with the anode feed port of the hydrogen fuel cell through a pipeline, and the hydrogen-rich gas in the pipeline is cooled by the heat exchange device, a oxygen-rich gas outlet is in communication with the cathode inlet of the hydrogen fuel cell through a pipeline, and a discharge port of the hydrogen fuel cell is in communication with the carbon dioxide liquefaction recovery device through a valve (i) and carbon dioxide intake port.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas stream flowing out through the oxygen-rich gas outlet is the oxygen-rich carbon dioxide gas stream with an oxygen concentration above 20%. The hydrogen fuel cell is a proton exchange membrane hydrogen fuel cell, a hydrogen phosphate fuel cell, a molten carbonate hydrogen fuel cell or a solid oxide hydrogen fuel cell.

PN US2020255313-A1; US11254581-B2
TI Treating livestock waste containing ammonia            nitrogen, comprises e.g. treating livestock waste to            create gas and, without use of chemicals, and            converting all ammonia in waste to gaseous form in the            gas.
AU BASSANI D
   ORENTLICHER M
   SIMON M M
   PAGANO S
AE BION ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGIES INC (BION-Non-standard)
GA 202077654J
AB    NOVELTY - Treating (P1) livestock waste containing                ammonia nitrogen, comprises e.g.: treating                livestock waste at a temperature of at least 80                degrees C to create a gas and, without the use of                chemicals, converting all ammonia in the waste to                gaseous form in the gas, where the gas also                containing carbon dioxide and water vapor; treating                the gas containing gaseous ammonia, carbon dioxide,                and water vapor, using partial condensation, or                repeated partial condensation, to concentrate the                ammonia and carbon dioxide followed by absorption                or adsorption of the concentrated gases, or total                condensation of the gas followed by dewatering                using reverse osmosis; and all without the use of                chemical(s) and remove from the gas a significant                amount of water vapor in liquid condensate form,                causing formation of a concentrated gas containing                higher concentrations of ammonia gas and carbon                dioxide gas than before condensation.
   USE - The method is useful for treating livestock                waste containing ammonia nitrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces capital cost, energy costs,                operating costs, and maintenance costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating (P1) livestock waste containing                ammonia nitrogen, comprises: treating livestock                waste at a temperature of at least 80 degrees C to                create a gas and, without the use of chemicals,                converting all ammonia in the waste to gaseous form                in the gas, where the gas also containing carbon                dioxide and water vapor; treating the gas                containing gaseous ammonia, carbon dioxide, and                water vapor, using partial condensation, or                repeated partial condensation, to concentrate the                ammonia and carbon dioxide followed by absorption                or adsorption of the concentrated gases, or total                condensation of the gas followed by dewatering                using reverse osmosis; all without the use of                chemical(s) and remove from the gas a significant                amount of water vapor in liquid condensate form,                causing formation of a concentrated gas containing                higher concentrations of ammonia gas and carbon                dioxide gas than before condensation; treating the                concentrated gas using absorption or adsorption to                form concentrated ammonium carbonate and ammonium                bicarbonate; and crystallizing the concentrated                ammonium carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate at a                temperature of less than about 35 degrees C to form                solid ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium carbonate.                An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for treating                (P2) livestock waste containing ammonia nitrogen,                comprising (i) treating livestock waste using                anaerobic digestion creating a biogas and a                digestate, (ii) treating the biogas to separate and                concentrate carbon dioxide in the biogas from other                gases in the biogas forming a concentrated carbon                dioxide biogas, (iii) treating the digestate at a                temperature of at least 80 degrees C to create a                gas and, without the use of chemicals, converting                all ammonia in the waste to gaseous form in the                gas, where the gas also contains carbon dioxide and                water vapor, (iv) treating the gas from digestion                containing gaseous ammonia, carbon dioxide, and                water vapor, using partial condensation, or                repeated partial condensation, operating at a                temperature of at least 80 degrees C, without the                use of chemicals, to remove from the gas a                significant amount of water vapor and organics in                liquid condensate form, causing formation of a                condenser effluent concentrated gas containing                higher concentrations of ammonia gas and carbon                dioxide gas than before condensation, (v) treating                the concentrated carbon dioxide biogas and the                condenser effluent concentrated gas using                absorption or adsorption operating at a temperature                of 20-50 degrees C to form concentrated ammonium                carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate, and (vi)                crystallizing the concentrated ammonium carbonate                and ammonium bicarbonate at a temperature of less                than about 35 degrees C to form solid ammonium                bicarbonate and ammonium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the processes (P1) and                  (P2), the treating livestock waste at a                  temperature of at least 80 degrees C comprises:                  stripping the waste of ammonia; stripping the                  waste using biogas from an anaerobic digester                  used to treat the waste before stripping; drying                  the waste using a dryer and removing ammonia with                  the water vapor in gaseous form; and separation                  of the ammonia and other gases using membrane                  modules. In the process (P1); the condensation is                  a two-stage condensation process comprising                  sequential condensation at two temperatures or                  residence times, where the total ammonia and                  carbon dioxide concentration in the concentrated                  gas is at least 2 times greater than in the gas                  before treatment with condensation; and the solid                  crystals of ammonium bicarbonate grown in the                  crystallizer are produced creating a solid                  ammonium-salt. The process (P2) further comprises                  recycling a portion of the concentrated ammonium                  carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate from the                  crystallization to assist with the absorption or                  adsorption. Preferred Components: The gaseous                  ammonia in the gas is converted into ammonium                  carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate using: the                  carbon dioxide in the gas without the adding                  carbon dioxide which is not present in the                  digestate; or the carbon dioxide in the gas with                  the addition of carbon dioxide from another                  source. The waste is digested livestock manure.                  The concentrated ammonium carbonate and ammonium                  bicarbonate after absorption or adsorption                  comprises at least 5 times the concentration of                  dissolved ammonium bicarbonate in the digestate.                  The ammonium-salt is dried, pelletized or                  granulated.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   USE - The catalyst is useful for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has higher carbon dioxide conversion and methanol selectivity, has carbon dioxide conversion rate of not greater than 14%, and methanol selectivity of not greater than 70%, and has good stability. The method improves activity of the catalyst, improves carbon dioxide conversion rate and selectivity of methanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   using method of catalyst for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol, comprising (a) adding catalyst into fixed-bed reactor, passing into hydrogen and nitrogen reducing gas mixture, and carrying out reduction reaction, and (b) completing the reduction, and introducing the raw material mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen to carry out the reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize methanol.
   (1) preparing catalyst for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol, comprising (1) dispersing UiO-67 in copper salt solution, and adding carbonate solution to obtain the first precipitate, and (2) aging first precipitate, filtering, drying and calcining to obtain target product; and
   (2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the ratio of the adding amount of UiO-67, copper salt solution and carbonate solution is (1-1.5) g: (8.7-19.2) ml: 50 ml. In the step (1), the preparation of UiO67 is as follows: dispersing zirconium tetrachloride and biphenyl dicarboxylic acid in the mixed solution of N,N-dimethylformamide and formic acid, and reacting to obtain UiO-67, where the ratio of the adding amounts of the zirconium tetrachloride, biphenyl dicarboxylic acid, N,N-dimethylformamide and formic acid is: (0.8-1.2) mol: 1 mol: (200-250) ml: (30-40) ml, the reaction temperature is 80-120degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is 10-72 hours.Preferred Components: In the step (1), the copper salt solution is an inorganic salt solution of copper with concentration of 0.2-0.5 mol/l, the solute of the copper salt solution is copper nitrate, copper sulfate or copper chloride. In the step (1), the carbonate solution is an aqueous sodium carbonate solution with concentration of 0.5-1 mol/l. In the step (2), the aging time is 2-4 hours. In the step (2), the drying process is specifically as follows: thermally insulating at a constant temperature of 45degrees Celsius for 50 minutes, heating to 75degrees Celsius for 60 minutes, heating to 80-110degrees Celsius for second time, and thermally insulating at constant temperature for 12 hours. In the step (2), the roasting process is specifically as follows: heating from normal temperature to 120degrees Celsius for first time, thermally insulating for 60 minutes, heating to 240degrees Celsius for second time, thermally insulating for 120 minutes, and heating to 350-500degrees Celsius for third time, and roasting for 2-4 hours.

TI Preparation device useful for producing high-quality liquid carbon dioxide comprises a drying system, a de-weighting system, a cleaning system and a light-removing system, where the drying system includes a wheel dehumidifier.
AU WU J
   XU M
   SHEN J
AE HANGZHOU KUAIKAI HI TECH CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 201891926K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation device comprises a drying system, a de-weighting system, a cleaning system and a light-removing system, where the drying system includes a wheel dehumidifier, a compressor, a heater, a smoke cooler and a dryer, the rotary dehumidifier includes a dehumidification zone and a regeneration zone, the smoke cooler, the heater and the regeneration zone are connected via a regeneration gas pipeline. The dehumidification zone is connected with the compressor via a feed gas conduit and the compressor is connected with the heater via the feed gas conduit. The heater is connected to the smoke cooler via the raw material gas pipeline. The smoke cooler is connected with the top of the dryer via the feed gas conduit. The de-weighting system includes a de-weighting tower and a de-weighting circulating pump, and the de-weighting tower is provided with a liquid carbon dioxide spraying device. The bottom of the de-weighting tower is connected to the bottom of the dryer.
   USE - The device is useful for producing high-quality liquid carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, low operating energy consumption and convenient operation, ensures the stable production of liquid carbon dioxide with a content exceeding 99.995%, reduces the production of solid waste and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation device comprises a drying system, a de-weighting system, a cleaning system and a light-removing system, where the drying system includes a wheel dehumidifier, a compressor, a heater, a smoke cooler and a dryer, the rotary dehumidifier includes a dehumidification zone and a regeneration zone, the smoke cooler, the heater and the regeneration zone are connected via a regeneration gas pipeline. The dehumidification zone is connected with the compressor via a feed gas conduit and the compressor is connected with the heater via the feed gas conduit. The heater is connected to the smoke cooler via the raw material gas pipeline. The smoke cooler is connected with the top of the dryer via the feed gas conduit. The de-weighting system includes a de-weighting tower and a de-weighting circulating pump, and the de-weighting tower is provided with a liquid carbon dioxide spraying device. The bottom of the de-weighting tower is connected to the bottom of the dryer through the raw material gas pipeline. The bottom of the de-weighting tower is connected with the de-weighting circulation pump. The de-weighting circulating pump is connected to the de-weighting tower via a carbon dioxide discharge pipe. An outlet of the de-circulating circulation pump is connected to the smoke cooler via the carbon dioxide discharge pipe. A carbon dioxide discharge valve is disposed on the carbon dioxide discharge pipe disposed between the de-circulating circulation pump and the smoke cooler. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing high quality liquid carbon dioxide comprising (i) passing the dried dehydrated gas carbon dioxide into the dehumidification zone of the rotary dehumidifier and is initially dehydrated, and then enters the compressor through the raw material gas pipeline for compression and then enters the heater through the raw material gas pipeline, where the cooled gas carbon dioxide enters the smoke cooler through the raw material gas pipeline and is further cooled and then enters the dryer for drying again, where the dew point temperature of the carbon dioxide after drying and cooling is -20 to -50 degrees C, and the gas carbon dioxide cooled and dried is passed through the raw material gas pipeline to the next process; (ii) discharging the washed and dehydrated dried gas carbon dioxide by two stages in the de-weighting tower and the gas CO2 after removing the heavy component impurities with higher boiling point from the top of the de-weighting tower, passing through the raw material gas pipeline to the next process, where the heavy component impurities are dissolved in the liquid carbon dioxide, and are enriched together with the liquid carbon dioxide in the tower of the de-weighting tower and discharged to the de-circulating circulation pump via the liquid pipeline, where the liquid carbon dioxide is circulated to the middle of the de-weighting tower to be the first-stage spray absorbing liquid by the de-weighting circulating pump, and the liquid carbon dioxide is continuously discharged into the smoke cooler through the sewage carbon discharge valve to ensure that the de-weighting tower kettle is also stable, where the continuous discharge ensures that the heavy component content in the tower kettle is in the range of 0.1-5%, and the liquid carbon dioxide containing heavy component impurities is discharged through the liquid pipeline, the liquid carbon dioxide is vaporized in the smoke cooler, and the vaporized carbon dioxide is recovered into the smoke cooler by the carbon dioxide discharge pipe, where the vaporized carbon dioxide in the smoke cooler is discharged to the heater through the regeneration gas pipeline, and the high temperature gas carbon dioxide generated in the compressor can be used as a dry gas source in the rotary dehumidifier; (iii) discharging the low-temperature deep decontamination carbon dioxide gas from the top of the de-weighting tower enters from the top of the deep adsorber and is discharged through the bottom of the deep adsorber, then after entering from the top of the deep dryer, it is finally discharged from the bottom of the deep dryer to the next process; (iv) discharging the liquefied rectification after the carbon dioxide gas from the deep dryer, which is liquefied by the liquefier from the middle of the de-lighting tower into the de-lighting tower, where the light components are circulated to the smoke cooler through the carbon dioxide discharge pipe and the light-removing condenser, and the liquid carbon dioxide is settled to the bottom of the de-lighting tower, where the collection of the finished liquid carbon dioxide is carried out via the control of the product flow control valve, when the liquid carbon dioxide exceeds the set value, the liquid carbon dioxide exceeding the set liquid level is sent to the de-weighting tower as a supplementary washing liquid under the action of the carbon dioxide scrubber pump.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the preparation device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The decontamination system includes a depth adsorber and a deep dryer, where the top of the depth adsorber is connected with the top of the de-weighting tower via the feed gas conduit. The bottom of the depth adsorber is connected with the top of the deep dryer via the feed gas conduit. The top of the deep dryer is connected with the liquefier via the feed gas line. At least two of the depth adsorbers are disposed and the depth adsorbers are connected end to end in series. The light removal system comprises a liquefier, a de-lighting tower, and a light-removing condenser. An upper portion of the de-lighting tower is connected to the de-light condenser and the de-light condenser is connected to the smoke cooler via the carbon dioxide discharge pipe. A pressure control valve is disposed on the carbon dioxide discharge pipe between the delight condenser and the smoke cooler. The liquefier is connected with a central portion of the de-lighting tower, and a bottom of the de-lighting tower is connected to a carbon dioxide scrubbing pump via a liquid conduit. The carbon dioxide scrubber pump is connected with the de-weighting column via the liquid conduit, and the de-lighting tower is in fluid communication with a product flow control valve via a liquid conduit. Preferred Method: In step (i) the rotary dehumidifier operates in a continuous mode, where the working pressure of the dehumidification zone is 1-10 kPa, working temperature of the dehumidification zone is 20-40 degrees C, the working pressure of the regeneration zone is 1-10 kpa and the working temperature of the regeneration zone is 110-150 degrees C, the working pressure of the heater is 1-10 kPa and the operating temperature of the heater is 20-40 degrees C. In step (ii) the operation pressure of the de-weighting column is 2.2-4 MPa and the operating temperature is -15 to -5 degrees C. In step (iii) the operating pressure of the depth adsorber is 2.2-4 MPa, the operating temperature is -15 to -5 degrees C, the operating pressure of the deep dryer is 2.2-4 MPa and the operating temperature is -15 to -5 degrees C.

   LEI T
   XUE Z
AE SHAANXI DAHE PURIFICATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2021A16593
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing hardness of wastewater of carbon                dioxide-containing waste gas involves: introducing                the wastewater to a mixed wastewater adjustment                tank, and adjusting the pH with an alkali;                introducing the pH adjusted wastewater into a                carbon dioxide mixer through a pressurized pump,                mixing with carbon dioxide-containing waste gas,                then introducing into a pressurized mixing                microreactor for reaction, flowing the wastewater                from microreactor into a filter for filtering, and                introducing the filter residue into a slurry tank;                introducing the effluent from the filter into a                buffer tank, then introducing into a DH membrane                filter, filtering to obtain a clear liquid,                subjecting to reverse osmosis process, and                introducing the filter residue into slurry tank;                filtering by introducing the slurry obtained in the                slurry tank into a plate and frame filter press for                press filtering, introducing press filtrate to                buffer tank, and introducing slurry after press                filtration; and cleaning.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing hardness of                wastewater of carbon dioxide-containing waste                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple in operation, and reduces                acid consumption and the introduction of sodium                ions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reducing hardness of wastewater of carbon                dioxide-containing waste gas involves: introducing                the wastewater to a mixed wastewater adjustment                tank, and adjusting the pH with an alkali;                introducing the pH adjusted wastewater into a                carbon dioxide mixer through a pressurized pump,                mixing with carbon dioxide-containing waste gas,                then introducing into a pressurized mixing                microreactor for reaction, flowing the wastewater                from microreactor into a filter for filtering, and                introducing the filter residue into a slurry tank;                introducing the effluent from the filter into a                buffer tank, then introducing into a DH membrane                filter, filtering to obtain a clear liquid,                subjecting to reverse osmosis process, and                introducing the filter residue into slurry tank;                filtering by introducing the slurry obtained in the                slurry tank into a plate and frame filter press for                press filtering, introducing press filtrate to                buffer tank, and introducing slurry after press                filtration; and cleaning by taking external                pickling tank to connect with microreactor and DH                membrane filter to form a closed pickling loop,                flowing the acid in the pickling tank through                microreactor, filter, buffer tank and DH membrane                filter, and then returning to pickling tank.

PN CN112048472-A
TI Inducing and culturing immune cytokine-induced killer cells involves extracting mononuclear cells, adjusting density, inoculating into culture medium, culturing, sampling and counting cells, resuspending, culturing and harvesting cells.
AU LIU L
   MU H
   GUO Q
AE ZHONGKANG REGENERATIVE MEDICINE TECHNOLOGY HAINAN CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7731Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for inducing and culturing immune cytokine-induced killer (CIK) cells involves (a) extracting mononuclear cells, and (b) culturing CIK cells by using culture medium, adjusting the density of peripheral blood mononuclear cells to 3-5x 106 cells/ml, inoculating into culture flasks, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator for 2-3 hours, adding the culture medium to the culture flask, culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, sampling and counting the cells, resuspending in culture medium, inoculating in a culture flask at a density of 1-2x 106/ml, adding 1000 U/ml interferon lambda (IFN- lambda ), placing in carbon dioxide incubator, culturing for 24 hours, then adding medium, 1000 international unit (IU)/ml interleukin (IL)-2 and 20 ng/ml IL-15 to the culture flask, maintaining in a carbon dioxide incubator for 2 days, every 2-3 days, adding the medium at a volume ratio of 1:1, placing in the carbon dioxide incubator for maintenance, and after 14-16 days, harvesting cells.
   USE - The method is useful for inducing and culturing immune CIK cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method induces and cultures immune CIK cells with good stability in proliferation number and large proliferation number.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for inducing and culturing immune cytokine-induced killer (CIK) cells involves (a) extracting mononuclear cells, and (b) culturing CIK cells by using culture medium, adjusting the density of peripheral blood mononuclear cells to 3-5x 106 cells/ml, inoculating into culture flasks, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator for 2-3 hours, adding the culture medium to the culture flask, culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, sampling and counting the cells, resuspending in culture medium, inoculating in a culture flask at a density of 1-2x 106/ml, adding 1000 U/ml interferon lambda (IFN- lambda ), placing in carbon dioxide incubator, culturing for 24 hours, then adding medium, 1000 international unit (IU)/ml interleukin (IL)-2 and 20 ng/ml IL-15 to the culture flask, maintaining in a carbon dioxide incubator for 2 days, every 2-3 days, adding the medium at a volume ratio of 1:1, placing in the carbon dioxide incubator for maintenance, and after 14-16 days, harvesting cells, where the culture medium contains 2000 U/ml serum, 0.3 mg/ml IL-1, 1000 U/ml gamma -interferon and 15 mu mol/ml proliferation-inducing peptide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves every 2-3 days supplementing 150-200 ml medium, 70-80 mu l IL-2 and 70-80 mu l IL-15. The culturing step in the carbon dioxide incubator is performed at 37 degrees C with carbon dioxide concentration of 5% and humidity of 95%. In step (b), the supplemental medium is 25-30 ml. The step (a) involves collecting 25-35 ml peripheral blood of the patient, transferring to a centrifuge tube, adding physiological saline to the centrifuge tube, mixing the peripheral blood with the physiological saline evenly, transferring the uniformly mixed peripheral blood and physiological saline into a centrifuge tube of 5-20 ml human lymphocyte separation liquid, performing centrifugal separation to divide the cell liquid into layers at 1800-2200 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 13-18 minutes, after the layered cell liquid is sucked off the upper plasma, transferring the liquid above the red blood cell layer to a centrifuge tube, adding physiological saline, mixing, centrifuging the obtained mononuclear cells at 1300-1400 rpm for 4-6 minutes, discarding the supernatant and repeating for centrifugation step for 2-3 times to obtain peripheral blood mononuclear cells. PN US2022146482-A1
TI Method for measuring carbon dioxide concentration inside room of building, involves predicting occupancy of room by applying information in unified knowledge base to set of knowledge functions, and comparing predicted concentration to predetermined concentration.
AU FADEL A M
   FUERST J
AE NEC LAB EURO GMBH (NIDE-C)
GA 202266343S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves extracting data from building sub-systems and integrating the data into a unified knowledge base (15). A room layout is determined. The occupancy of a room is predicted by applying the information in the unified knowledge based on a set of knowledge functions. The carbon dioxide concentration inside the room is determined by a carbon dioxide prediction model generated using the knowledge base, the determined room layout, and the predicted occupancy of the room. The predicted concentration is compared to a predetermined concentration for adjusting ventilation in the room, where the knowledge functions are formed into an ensemble using a subset of labeled data. The ensemble is used to label unlabeled data.
   USE - Method for measuring a carbon dioxide concentration inside a room of a building. Uses include but are not limited to residential homes, and non-residential buildings such as stores, office buildings, and plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables measuring room-level carbon dioxide levels without requiring dedicated carbon dioxide sensors using machine learning in a cost-effective manner. The creation of per-room occupancy prediction models is a flexible way that adapts automatically to the available data in the building through a dynamic and optimized ensemble of knowledge functions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system for measuring a CO concentration inside a room; (2) a tangible, non-transitory computer-readable medium comprises instructions for measuring a carbon dioxide concentration inside a room of a building
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block representation of a method for measuring carbon dioxide concentration inside a room of a building.

   Smart devices (13)

   Knowledge base (15)

PN CN114033343-A; CN114033343-B
TI Pore size simulation method for carbon dioxide oil            displacement and embedding in high water-containing oil            reservoir, involves simulating geological reservoir and            oil-water two-phase discharge of carbon dioxide            injection.
AU TAO W
   WANG Z
   WEI H
   ZHU X
   CHEN L
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202234585J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing a microscopic                structure numerical model with channel                characteristics. The perturbation is applied to                enhance the heterogeneity of the porous media                structure A porous lattice point in a porous medium                structure is identified. A random number is                generated with size between 0-1 in each lattice                points. The pore lattices point is occupied by                water phase. A point water phase density is set. An                oil-water two-phase distribution is formed under                the determined water content. A geological                reservoir and an oil water two-water discharge of                carbon dioxide injection once water-driven                petroleum exploitation are simulated. An inlet and                outlet and a boundary condition are set. The                injection rate is constant and the gas injection                volume is continuously increased. The newly                injected gas phase pushes the original oil-water                two-phase in the system to discharge the geological                reservoir to achieve the dual goals of oil                displacement and carbon dioxide geological                storage.
   USE - Pore size simulation method for carbon dioxide                oil displacement and embedding in high                water-containing oil reservoir used in fields of                oil and gas exploitation technology and carbon                dioxide sequestration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a high aqueous reservoir                of carbon dioxide oil displacement and embedded                pore size simulation method, effectively, directly                displaying the complex geological structure in                three-phase memory state, and by changing the                parameter exploring fluid property, structure                property and other factors of the influence of the                embedding and oil displacement effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustarting                the pore size simulation method for carbon dioxide                oil displacement and embedding in high                water-containing oil reservoir. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

PN US2021221755-A1; US11220470-B2
TI Recovering methane and carbon dioxide from biogas source for beneficial use or sequestration involves e.g. extracting gas stream having biogas from biogas-generating facility and compressing, cooling and separating stream from liquid water.
AU WHITMORE N
AE WM INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY HOLDINGS LLC (WMIN-Non-standard)
   WM INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY HOLDINGS LLC (WMIN-Non-standard)
GA 2021830620
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering methane and carbon dioxide from a biogas source for beneficial use or sequestration, comprises: (a) extracting at least one gas stream comprising a biogas from a biogas-generating facility; (b) compressing, cooling, and separating the gas stream from liquid water; (c) removing a majority of trace contaminants from the gas stream; (d) cooling the gas stream in a liquefaction unit to liquefy at least some of the contained carbon dioxide; (e) separating remaining gaseous carbon dioxide from the gas stream and recycling to a point upstream from the liquefaction unit such that the carbon dioxide is substantially recovered as a liquid product; (f) generating a biomethane product for beneficial use; and (g) monitoring material and energy inputs and outputs from a biogas processing facility, where at least some of steps (b)-(f) occur in the biogas processing facility.
   USE - The process is useful for recovering methane and carbon dioxide from biogas source for beneficial use or sequestration.
   ADVANTAGE - The process : is advantageous to allow most difficult to adsorb contaminants, low boiling halogenated hydrocarbons, to pass through physical adsorbents and instead remove them downstream in carbon dioxide liquefaction process; is advantages of ensuring purified gas stream is produced as well as advantage that feed gas warms liquid carbon dioxide as two streams become in contact, which allows for immediate flashing of bottoms stream for methane recovery and recycle; is not only results in fuel gas savings, but improved carbon intensity rating for biomethane fuel and improved generation of credits; and is accomplishing this feat while simultaneously and synergistically generating purified biomethane product and improving overall methane recovery efficiency of process results in significant improvement beyond current practices.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process, the additional steps of: (a1) removing oxygen from the gas stream to produce a deoxygenated gas stream; (b1) drying the gas stream to reduce the water vapor content; (c1) utilizing the liquified carbon dioxide as an absorbent to purify the gas stream; (d1) separating nitrogen from the gas stream; (e1) sequestering or supplying the carbon dioxide product for sequestration or other utilization; or (f1) utilizing or supplying the biomethane product stream as a compressed or liquefied fuel for vehicles, as process heat fuel, as feedstock for fuel or chemical synthesis, or as feedstock for hydrogen generation. In the process, the step of including the further step of utilizing the separated nitrogen stream to regenerate contaminant-laden adsorbent or absorbent. The including step further comprises step of transmitting the contaminant-laden nitrogen stream to a thermal oxidizer or flare for thermal destruction of the contaminants. In the process, including the additional step of monitoring the reservoir for verification of extended or permanent storage of carbon dioxide or monitoring the conversion of carbon dioxide to carbonate minerals for verification of sequestration. The air intrusion into the biogas is controlled to achieve minimum oxygen to hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen sulfide ratio of 0.5. The overall methane recovery efficiency is greater than 98%. In the process, including the additional step of monitoring the reservoir for verification of extended or permanent storage of carbon dioxide or monitoring the conversion of carbon dioxide to carbonate minerals for verification of sequestration. The air intrusion into the biogas is controlled to achieve minimum oxygen to hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen sulfide ratio of 0.5.Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide is sequestered through injection into an underground geologic reservoir for permanent storage or reacted with another material to form carbonate minerals. The gross heating value of the biomethane product stream is greater than 1000 Btu/SCF. The carbon intensity of the biomethane product stream is less than 25 g carbon dioxide e/MJ. The halogenated hydrocarbon level in the biomethane product stream is less than 0.1 ppmv. The siloxane level in the biomethane product stream is less than 0.01 mg silicon/m3. The credits are generated due to the reduction in greenhouse gases. The overall methane recovery efficiency is greater than 98%.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Method for producing ready-mix concrete, involves            adding first portion of separated carbon dioxide to            process water, and adding second portion of separated            carbon dioxide to ready-mix concrete produced at            facility.
AU PIENADO R E
   GRASLEY Z C
   PEINADO R E
AE MEEDL68 LP (MEED-Non-standard)
   MEEDL68 LP (MEED-Non-standard)
   MEEDL68 LP (MEED-Non-standard)
   MEEDL68 LP (MEED-Non-standard)
GA 2019855175
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves positioning a facility                (10) on a portion of water, and the facility that                is configured to produce and deliver ready-mix                concrete. An emission that includes carbon dioxide                is generated during operation of the facility. The                ready-mix concrete is produced at the facility. The                process water is generated by using the feed water                during production of the ready-mix concrete, and                the process water including calcium and silica                ions. The carbon dioxide is separated from the                generated emission. The separated C02 is liquefied                by compressing the carbon dioxide. The first                portion of the separated carbon dioxide is added to                the process water. The second portion of the                separated carbon dioxide is added to the ready-mix                concrete produced at the facility. The emission                includes a first emission generated by the first                emission source while moving the facility and a                second emission generated by the second emission                source while producing the ready-mix                concrete.
   USE - Method for producing ready-mix concrete for                multitude of construction projects such as bridges,                tunnels, support walls, erosion barriers, trenches,                commercial and residential structure, roads and                underwater foundations.
   ADVANTAGE - The system requires less energy to deliver                ready-mix concrete. The inner most layer presents                less resistance to flow of the ready- mix concrete                while providing greater wear resistance. The carbon                dioxide is bound in a liquid or solid media that                prevents the C02 from entering the atmosphere for a                long period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for producing and delivery of ready-mix                concrete.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                facility of the system for producing and delivery                of ready-mix concrete.
   Facility (10)
   Vessel (12)
   Decks (16)
   Power generation system (18)
   Pumps (34)

PN CN109826709-A; CN209875312-U
TI Low temperature environment power generation system, has water separator whose outlet is connected to inlet of compressor unit that is connected with inlet of condenser, where outlet of condenser is connected to carbon dioxide pump.
AU ZHENG K
   HUANG Z
AE SHANGHAI POWER EQUIP RES INST (SNPT-C)
GA 201950000C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide pump (1) whose outlet is divided into two paths. A first path is fixed to a carbon dioxide collecting device (15). A second path is fixed to a high pressure side inlet of a low temperature regenerator (2). A high pressure side outlet of the low temperature regenerator is connected with a low temperature waste heat retriever (3) that is connected with a high pressure side inlet of a high temperature regenerator (4). A water inlet of a water separator (11) is connected with an outlet of a cooler (10). A water outlet of the water separator is connected to an inlet of a water collecting device (12). A carbon dioxide outlet of the water separator is connected to an inlet of a compressor unit (13) that is connected with an inlet of a condenser (14). An outlet of the condenser is connected to an inlet of the carbon dioxide pump.
   USE - Low temperature environment power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has high energy utilization efficiency, power generation efficiency, working parameter and small key device development investment, and avoids discharge of pollutants, filters carbon dioxide in a complete manner and recycles condensed water in an easier manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a low temperature environment power generating method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a low temperature environment power generation system.
   Carbon dioxide pump (1)
   Low temperature regenerator (2)
   Low temperature waste heat retriever (3)
   High temperature regenerator (4)
   Cooler (10)
   Water separator (11)
   Water collecting device (12)
   Compressor unit (13)
   Condenser (14)
   Carbon dioxide collecting device (15)

TI Process for poly-generating carbon dioxide for            enhanced oil recovery in internal combustion            turbomachine of ship, involves injecting portion of            carbon dioxide-containing product into well to recover            hydrocarbon from subterranean deposit.
AU WILLIAMS M
   DE JAGER M
   GIL H
AE ACCEL ENERGY CANADA LTD (ACCE-Non-standard)
   WILLIAMS M (WILL-Individual)
   DE JAGER M (DJAG-Individual)
   GIL H (GILH-Individual)
GA 201929121P
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves forming aqueous slurry of                carbonaceous particles. Aqueous slurry carbonaceous                particles in an internal combustion engine are                combusted to form a carbon dioxide-containing                product i.e. sulfur oxide, and generate electrical                energy. A portion of the carbon dioxide-containing                product is injected into a well to assist in                recovering a hydrocarbon from a subterranean                deposit, where the aqueous slurry of carbonaceous                particles comprises an emulsifier and surfactant                and the carbonaceous particles comprise petroleum                coke, coal, a biomass, a liquid petroleum residues,                bitumen, lube oil and fuel oil.
   USE - Process for poly-generating carbon dioxide for                tertiary enhanced oil recovery in an internal                combustion engine e.g. reciprocating internal                combustion engine and internal combustion                turbomachine (all claimed), of a lorry, ship or a                rail haulage vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enables providing distributed                power to oilfields within an electrical                distribution and transmission grid for displacing                power transmission of faraway generation and                earning distribution/transmission credits under                current Alberta infrastructure pricing. The process                enables facilitating waste heat recovery using an                organic or inorganic Rankine cycle so as to improve                process efficiencies. The process enables providing                a combination of carbon dioxide enhanced oil                recovery and storage of carbon dioxide in oil                reservoirs of Alberta to increase tertiary crude                oil production and provide a critical, near and                long- term solution to reduce greenhouse gas                emissions from power generation and other                industrial facilities.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                system for poly-generating carbon dioxide for                tertiary enhanced oil recovery.
   Solid fuel material handling process                (100)
   Main crusher system (110)
   Petroleum coke feed material (120)
   Hydrocarbon product (140)
   Feed tanks (150)

PN CN108786452-A
TI Factory exhaust gas discharge system, comprises nitrogen oxide reduction device, oxidation device, carbon dioxide capture device and adsorption device, which are sequentially connected.
AU ZHANG R
AE ZHANG R (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2018928959
AB    NOVELTY - A factory exhaust gas discharge system comprises a nitrogen oxide reduction device, an oxidation device, a carbon dioxide capture device and an adsorption device, which are sequentially connected, where the carbon dioxide capture device is connected to a carbon dioxide conversion device, and the carbon dioxide conversion device is connected to a hydrogen production device.
   USE - Factory exhaust gas discharge system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen oxide reduction device is a heating wire, and a surface of the heating wire is coated with a catalyst which catalyzes the formation of carbon dioxide and nitrogen by nitric oxide and carbon monoxide. The catalyst is 20-30 pts. wt. palladium carbon, 5-10 pts. wt. calcium oxide, 12-18 pts. wt. silicon carbide, 2-3 pts. wt. carbon on silver and 3-9 pts. wt. cadmium hydroxide. The heating device is disposed between the nitrogen oxide reduction device and the oxidizing device and activated carbon is disposed in the heating device. The hydrogen production device is an electrolysis water device and it is comprises a hydrogen pipe, an oxygen pipe and an inlet pipe connected to the electrolysis water device and the hydrogen pipe is connected with the carbon dioxide conversion device. The adsorption device has an adsorption solution for adsorbing exhaust gas and the adsorption solution is 20-31 parts diethylhydroxylamine, 42-55 parts ethanol, 15-20 parts lanthanum nitrate, 5-20 parts activated carbon and 10-15 parts ammonium carbonate.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the proppant comprising mixing organic acid, sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate and ethanol binder at weight ratio of 1:3:3:2 and at 50-60 degrees C to obtain carbon dioxide autogenous agent; mixing fatty amine, sodium silicate and acetic acid at weight ratio of 2:1:1 to obtain curing agent; mixing expanded graphite, silicon carbide and sodium methylsiliconate at weight ratio of 2:2:1 to obtain additive; heating inner core to 80-100 degrees C, adding additive, mixing, adding carbon dioxide autogenous agent, mixing, adding curing agent, mixing, cooling, crushing and sieving through 30-50 mesh screen to obtain middle layer coated sand; and heating to 80-100 degrees C, adding additive, mixing, adding moisture-proof adhesive, mixing, adding curing agent, mixing, cooling, crushing and sieving.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide autogenous agent comprises 4.2-4.8 wt.% organic acid, 12.3-13.8 wt.% sodium carbonate, 12.6-13.8 wt.% sodium bicarbonate and/or 8.4-9.6 wt.% ethanol binder. The curing agent comprises 1-1.5 wt.% fatty amine, 0.5-0.76 wt.% sodium silicate and 0.5-0.74 wt.% sodium acetate. The additive comprises 0.4-0.8 wt.% expanded graphite, 0.4-0.8 wt.% silicon carbide and 0.2-0.4 wt.% sodium methylsiliconate. The inner core is 40-70 mesh quartz sand or ceramsite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The moisture-proof adhesive is oil-soluble phenolic resin.

   HE W

AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 2019294765
AB    NOVELTY - A cobalt-potassium-dimethylamine composite                hydrate (I) is new.
   USE - New cobalt-potassium-dimethylamine composite                hydrate is used as metal organic framework material                for capturing carbon dioxide and 2C hydrocarbon                gases, or separating carbon dioxide/methane gas,                carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas and methane/2C                hydrocarbon gas (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A cobalt-potassium-dimethylamine composite                hydrate of formula: (Co3K2(TZIA)3).(DMA)1.(H2O)3                (I) is new.
   TZIA3- = 5-tetrazolyl-isophthalic acid                trivalent anion.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of compound (I), which                involves adding 5-(1H-tetrazol-5-yl)isophthalate, a                potassium source and a cobalt source in a                polytetrafluoroethylene reactor comprising a mixed                solution of N,N-dimethylformamide and an organic                solvent, sealing and placing the reactor in an oven                for constant-temperature solvothermal reaction,                obtaining blue crystals, washing the blue crystals                with N,N-dimethylformamide and drying; and
   (2) usage method of compound (I), which                involves subjecting the metal organic framework                material to solvent exchange to capture carbon                dioxide and 2C hydrocarbon gases after high vacuum                degassing at different temperatures, or to separate                carbon dioxide/methane, carbon dioxide/nitrogen and                methane/2C hydrocarbon gases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The potassium source                  is potassium fluoride dihydrate. The cobalt                  source is cobalt chloride hexahydrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The compound (I)                  belongs to hexagonal system, and has a space                  group of P63/m, unit cell parameters a and b of                  19.670(5) Angstrom , c of 10.205(5) Angstrom ,                  alpha and gamma of 90 degrees , and beta of 120                  degrees , and a one-dimensional channel size of                  15x 15 Angstrom in a c-axis direction. Preferred                  Composition: The mol ratio of                  5-(1H-tetrazol-5-yl)isophthalate, potassium                  source and cobalt source is 2:(1-10):2. The                  organic solvent is acetonitrile. The volume ratio                  of acetonitrile and N,N-dimethylformamide is                  1:(1-4). The ratio of total amount of                  acetonitrile and N,N-dimethylformamide to                  5-(1H-tetrazol-5-yl)isophthalate is 9.0 ml:0.1                  mmol.

PN CN114687825-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide cycle power            generation unit end temperature control strategy, has            pre-cooler hot side outlet carbon dioxide temperature            control loop for adjusting temperature of carbon            dioxide inlet of compressor.
AU YAO M
   LI H
   WU S
   ZHANG T
   ZHANG L
   GU Z
   QIAO Y
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2022902671
AB    NOVELTY - The strategy involves determining a pre-cooler                hot side outlet temperature set value according to                a supercritical carbon dioxide cycle power                generation unit design specification. A temperature                sensor of a precooled hot-side outlet is utilized                to measure the actual temperature of the pre-cooled                hotside outlet in real time. The temperature sensor                is utilized to calculate the difference between the                temperature of a circulating water supply and the                circulating water. The circulation water supply                temperature is adjusted according to the control                signal of a cooling tower fan. The cooling tower                fan motor frequency obtained by the controller is                utilized as the control signal of the cooling tower                fan, and the circulating water supply temperature                is adjusted according to the control signal of the                cooling tower fan.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide cycle power                generation unit end temperature control strategy                for use in thermal control power plant, nuclear                power and solar thermal power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The control strategy can reduce the                environment temperature change and the disturbance                of the lifting load to the circulating cold end                temperature, improve the control precision of the                carbon dioxide compressor, effectively ensure the                safe compressor efficient operation, and greatly                improve the generating efficiency of the machine                set.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block flow diagram                illustrating the supercritical carbon dioxide cycle                power generation unit end temperature control                strategy. (Drawing includes non-English language                text)
