TI Preparing catalyst useful for direct hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to aromatic hydrocarbon comprises e.g. mixing diammonium phosphate, transition metal salt and metal salt auxiliary agent with water and evoparating mixed solution.
AU DUAN X
   HUANG Y
   YE L
   YUAN Y
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 201938731J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst comprises (a) mixing diammonium phosphate, transition metal salt and metal salt auxiliary agent with water to obtain a mixed solution; (b) evoparating mixed solution to dryness, drying product obtained by evaporation and calcining to obtain a catalyst precursor; (c) subjecting the catalyst precursor to hydrogen reduction to obtain a catalyst for directly hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce aromatic hydrocarbons, where transition metal salt is ammonium molybdate and/or ammonium tungstate and metal salt auxiliary agent is copper nitrate, nickel nitrate, cobalt nitrate and iron nitrate.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for direct hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to aromatic hydrocarbon (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves mechanical strength and thermal stability; and has easily available raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for use of catalyst for direct hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to aromatic hydrocarbon; and # use method of catalyst for direct hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to aromatic hydrocarbon comprising placing the catalyst in a reactor with a mixed gas of carbon dioxide / dihydrogen/dinitrogen for hydrogenation reaction to obtain a reaction product, where pressure of the hydrogenation reaction is 2.0 to 3.0 MPa, the temperature is 250-350 degrees C, and the time is 2-24 hours, the space velocity of the mixed gas is 1000-10000 ml / (houyr-gcat), and the molar ratio mixture gas dihydrogen and carbon dioxide is 2-3.5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar concentration of diammonium phosphate in the mixed solution is 0.2-2.2 mol/l and molar ratio of thediammonium phosphate, the transition metal salt and metal salt auxiliary agent is (0.5-1.2): 1: (0.1-1.2). The composition of the catalyst is transition metal phosphide and a promoter phosphide, where the transition metal phosphide is molybdenum phosphide and / or tungstne phosphide and auxiliary phosphide is tricopper phosphide, dinickel phosphide , cobalt phosphide and Iron phosphide. The mass ratio of the transition metal phosphide and promoter phosphide is 1-20:1. Preferred Method: In the step (b) evaporation temperature is 80-100 degrees C, drying temperature is 80-120 degrees C, and the drying time is 8-14 hours, calcination temperature is 450-550 degrees C and the calcination time is 2-4 hours. In the step (c) flow rate of hydrogen is 30-100 ml/minute. In the step (c) hydrogen reduction temperature is 550-650 degrees C and the hydrogen reduction time is 1-3 hours.

PN CN110075565-A
TI Method for extracting Houttuynia cordata preparation, involves taking Houttuynia cordata, washing with water and drying, followed by subjecting Houttuynia cordata to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, and purifying.
AU XU Y
AE FUJIAN ZHONGXI AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201969538D
AB    NOVELTY - A Houttuynia cordata preparation extracting method involves taking a sufficient amount of Houttuynia cordata, washing with water, and drying, subjecting the dried product to pulverization treatment, spraying water, subjecting the Houttuynia cordata to a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, adding a polar solvent to the carbon dioxide fluid to obtain a preliminary extraction, entering second extraction stage, introducing carbon dioxide, introducing a carrier into the carbon dioxide, controlling the extraction temperature for controlling second extraction phase, adding a carbon dioxide fluid with a modifier, adding a carrier, extracting, controlling the extraction temperature, removing impurities in the Houttuynia cordata, passing the carbon dioxide fluid added with the modifier, adding the houttuyfolium solvent, extracting, separating the extract from the Houttuynia cordata, and purifying.
   USE - Method for extracting Houttuynia cordata preparation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables extracting Houttuynia cordata preparation with improved extraction efficiency, high purity, high efficiency and is safe for extraction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A Houttuynia cordata preparation extracting method involves taking a sufficient amount of Houttuynia cordata, washing with water, and drying, adopting a drying device of an industrial dryer, with a drying time of 2-3 hours, and a drying temperature of 80-90 degrees C, subjecting the dried Houttuynia to pulverization treatment using a pulverizing device of a pulverizer having a filtering function, after smashing and screening, placing the Houttuynia cordata in a temperature of 20-30 degrees C for 3-4 hours, spraying water of the Houttuynia 20-30% having the temperature of the clean water is 40-50 degrees C, subjecting the Houttuynia cordata to a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, adding a polar solvent to the carbon dioxide fluid to obtain a preliminary extraction, after the carbon dioxide is introduced for 2 hours, entering second extraction stage, introducing carbon dioxide continuously for extraction, introducing a carrier into the carbon dioxide, controlling the extraction temperature at 80-100 degrees C, a pressure of 10-15 MPa, and a time for controlling second extraction phase is 2-4 hours, adding a carbon dioxide fluid with a modifier, adding a carrier of 30-40 mg/m3 of a carbon dioxide fluid, extracting, controlling the extraction temperature to be 90-110 degrees C, and the pressure of 25-30 MPa, the time of 1.5-2 hours, removing impurities in the Houttuynia cordata, passing the carbon dioxide fluid added with the modifier, adding the houttuyfolium solvent to the carbon dioxide fluid, extracting at the extraction temperature of 100-120 degrees C, the pressure of 25-30 MPa, a time of 4-5 hours, separating the extract from the Houttuynia cordata, and purifying, where the polar solvent comprises methanol, isopropanol and stearic acid, the extraction temperature is 70-75 degrees C, and the extraction pressure is 25-30 MPa, and the modifier is acetone.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The houttuyfolium solvent is ethanol and acetonitrile. The obtained product comprises houttuyfolium, lauric aldehyde and 2-undecyl ketone.

PN CN112281087-A; CN112281087-B
TI Preparing high-load and high-heat-resistant woven fiber reinforced aluminum-based composite material, comprises e.g. placing fiber preform in packaging mold, then introducing liquid carbon dioxide into packaging mold, and immersing.
AU WANG Z
   XU Z
   CAI C
   YU H
   XIONG B
   YANG W
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 202113160R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-load and high-heat-resistant woven fiber reinforced aluminum-based composite material, comprises preparing fiber preform, and placing the fiber preform in a packaging mold, making liquid carbon dioxide under pressure and temperature, then introducing liquid carbon dioxide into packaging mold, and making the liquid carbon dioxide completely immerse the fiber preform for 20-24 hours, adjusting the pressure and temperature thus the liquid carbon dioxide is vaporized and released, and placing the packaging mold in a vacuum pressure infiltration device, and then soaking molten liquid metal into the packaging mold by air pressure to infiltrate the fiber preform to obtain composite material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-load and high-heat-resistant woven fiber reinforced aluminum-based composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has characteristic that the volume is increased when the liquid carbon dioxide phase is changed into gas, thus the fiber preform is expanded; avoids residual stress problem caused by different heat expansion coefficients; and improves load of the composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The molten liquid metal used for soaking is ZL301 aluminum alloy. The fiber preform is polyacrylonitrile-based carbon fiber M40 woven.

PT P
PN CN110773198-A; CN110773198-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction            catalyst by adding nickel nitrate and polymethyl            methacrylate nanospheres to graphene oxide dispersion,            spray drying, ball milling precursor material and            nano-sulfur powder, and calcining.
AU ZHANG Y
   WANG J
AE UNIV ZHAOQING EAST CHINA NORMAL PHOTOELE (UYZH-Non-standard)
GA 202015777K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing carbon dioxide                electrochemical reduction catalyst, involves (a)                using Hummers' method to prepare a graphene oxide                dispersion, adding nickel nitrate and polymethyl                methacrylate nanospheres having a particle diameter                of 30-100 nm to the dispersion, ultrasonically                processing for 30-60 minutes to obtain a uniformly                mixed solution, spray drying at 120-160 degrees C,                and collecting the product to obtain a precursor                material, and (b) ball milling the precursor                material and nano-sulfur powder in a ball mill                tank, adding the mixture to a tube furnace under                nitrogen protection, and calcining to obtain a                nickel sulfide composite sulfur-doped graphene                catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method adjusts the catalyst preparation                conditions, increases the specific surface area of                contact between carbon dioxide and catalyst,                suppresses the hydrogen evolution reaction                accompanying the reduction of carbon dioxide,                improves the utilization and conversion of carbon                dioxide and improves Faraday efficiency. The                prepared catalyst has high specific surface area                with a special morphology and structure and more                catalytic active sites.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The ball mill rotates                  at a speed of 500-800 rpm for 3-5 hours. The                  calcination temperature in the tube furnace is                  100-200 degrees C, and the time is 8-24 hours.                  Preferred Composition: The concentration of the                  graphene dispersion is 1-5 mg/ml. The                  concentration of nickel nitrate in the graphene                  oxide dispersion is 0.01-0.2 g/ml. The                  concentration of polymethyl methacrylate                  nanospheres in the graphene dispersion is                  0.05-0.4 g/ml. The mass ratio of the precursor                  material to the nano-sulfur powder is                  1:(2-5).

PT P
PN KR1982720-B1
TI Foaming agent used in resin composition, contains carbon dioxide transporter, silicon dioxide, inorganic compound comprising at least one of azide compound, ammonium nitrite, and sodium borohydride and encapsulated acid transporter.
AU KIM G U
   SHIM J C
AE RESINTECH CO LTD (RESI-Non-standard)
GA 2019492443
AB    NOVELTY - A resin foaming agent comprises a carbon dioxide transporter, silicon dioxide, an inorganic compound, an encapsulated acid transporter, and a crosslinking agent. The inorganic compound comprises at least one of an azide compound, ammonium nitrite, and sodium borohydride. The crosslinking agent comprises peroxide and epoxide.
   USE - Foaming agent is used in resin composition (claimed) for forming injection molded product.
   ADVANTAGE - The foaming agent has high resin solubility. The molded product obtained using the foaming agent has excellent flame retardance and surface properties. Since the foaming agent generates carbon dioxide during foaming, the molded product is harmless to human body and environmentally-friendly. Since the reaction between the carbon dioxide transporter and the acid transporter in the foaming agent occurs at 70-420 degrees C, carbon dioxide gas is generated and released from the resin and injected to a mold of an injection molding machine, thus simplifying the production of molded product. By discharging the carbon dioxide into the resin during foaming, the homogeneous and uniform pores are formed in the resin, thus reducing the weight of the molded product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The foaming agent comprises 0.5-40 wt.% acid transporter, 2-99 wt.% carbon dioxide transporter and 1-10 wt.% crosslinking agent, further 5-10 wt.% cell controlling agent comprising at least one of silicon, carbon and alkali metal alkylsiliconate, a flame retardant comprising at least one of phosphorus-containing polyester, ammonium polyphosphate, phosphonate, phosphinate, polyphosphate, pyrophosphate, phosphate, melamine derivative and nitrogen base, and at least one of starch, tricalcium phosphate, calcium carbonate, calcium sulfate, silane, and fat. The carbon dioxide transporter comprises talc, and at least one of an alkali metal, an alkaline earth metal, and aluminum, preferably one or more of ammonium carbamate, ammonium hydrogen carbonate, ammonium carbonate, iron hydrogen carbonate, iron carbonate, aluminum hydrogen carbonate, aluminum carbonate, calcium hydrogen carbonate, calcium carbonate, magnesium hydrogen carbonate, magnesium carbonate, sodium hydrogen carbonate and sodium carbonate. The carbon dioxide transporter and acid transporter are released at 70-420 degrees C. The acid transporter comprises at least one of citric acid, monoisopropyl citrate, and monosodium citrate.

PT P
PN WO2018189097-A1; CN110494410-A; US2021130259-A1; US11117848-B2
TI Oxidative coupling of methane (OCM) comprises e.g. contacting oxygen and methane with OCM catalyst in a reactor to give reactor effluent comprising ethylene, ethane, methane, carbon dioxide and water and cooling the effluent.
AU MITKIDIS G
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 201881188F
AB    NOVELTY - Oxidative coupling of methane (OCM) comprises: (a) contacting oxygen and methane with an OCM catalyst in a reactor (2), resulting in a reactor effluent comprising ethylene, ethane, methane, carbon dioxide and water; (b) cooling the reactor effluent to obtain a liquid stream comprising water and a gas stream comprising ethylene, ethane, methane and carbon dioxide; (c) removing carbon dioxide from at least a part of the gas stream comprising ethylene, ethane, methane and carbon dioxide resulting in a gas stream comprising ethylene, ethane and methane; and (d) passing at least a part of the gas stream comprising ethylene, ethane and methane as obtained in step (c) through a membrane (9), preferably a membrane comprising silver(I) ions or copper(I) ions, to obtain a stream comprising ethane and a stream comprising ethylene.
   USE - The process is useful for oxidative coupling of methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides a high yield of relative pure ethylene at a reduced energy demand.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of oxidative coupling of methane.
   Reactor (2)
   Separator (5)
   Carbon dioxide removal unit (8)
   Membrane (9)
   Stream comprising ethylene (11)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In step (d), a stream comprising ethane and methane and a stream comprising ethylene and ethane are obtained, where the step (d) is followed by: (e) feeding the stream comprising ethane and methane as obtained in step (d) to a distillation column to obtain a stream comprising ethane and a stream comprising methane; (f) optionally feeding at least a part of the stream comprising methane as obtained in step (e) to step (a); (g) passing at least a part of the stream comprising ethylene and ethane as obtained in step (d) through a second membrane, preferably a membrane comprising silver(I) ions or copper(I) ions, to obtain a stream comprising ethane and ethylene and a stream comprising ethylene; and (h) optionally feeding at least a part of the stream comprising ethane and ethylene as obtained in step (g) to the membrane (the first membrane) used in step (d), or optionally feeding at least a part of the stream comprising ethane and ethylene as obtained in step (g) to step (a). The stream comprising ethane as obtained in step (d) further comprises methane, where the step (d) is followed by: (eA) feeding the stream comprising ethane and methane as obtained in step (d) to a pressure swing adsorption (PSA) unit to obtain a stream comprising ethane and a stream comprising methane; and (fA) optionally feeding at least a part of the stream comprising methane as obtained in step (eA) to step (a). In step (d), a stream comprising ethane and methane and a stream comprising ethylene and ethane are obtained, where the step (d) is further followed by: (gA) passing at least a part of the stream comprising ethylene and ethane as obtained in step (d) through a second membrane, preferably a membrane comprising silver(I) ions or copper(I) ions, to obtain a stream comprising ethane and ethylene and a stream comprising ethylene; and (hA) optionally feeding at least a part of the stream comprising ethane and ethylene as obtained in step (gA) to the membrane (the first membrane) used in step (d), or optionally feeding at least a part of the stream comprising ethane and ethylene as obtained in step (gA) to step (a). In step (d), a stream comprising ethane and methane and a stream comprising ethylene and ethane are obtained, where the step (d) is further followed by: (gB) feeding the stream comprising ethylene and ethane as obtained in step (d) to a distillation column to obtain a stream comprising ethane and a stream comprising ethylene; and (hB) optionally feeding at least a part of the stream comprising ethane as obtained in step (gB) to step (a). At least a part of the gas stream comprising ethylene, ethane and methane as obtained in step (c) is subjected to distillation, resulting in a stream comprising methane and a stream comprising ethylene, ethane and methane which latter stream is fed to step (d), where the step (d) is followed by: steps (eA) and (fA) as per se; and (gC) optionally feeding at least a part of the stream comprising methane as obtained in the distillation to step (a). At least a part of the gas stream comprising ethylene, ethane and methane as obtained in step (c) is fed to a first PSA unit, resulting in a stream comprising methane and a stream comprising ethylene, ethane and methane which latter stream is fed to step (d), where the step (d) is followed by: (eA1) feeding the stream comprising ethane and methane as obtained in step (d) to a second PSA unit to obtain a stream comprising ethane and a stream comprising methane; step (fA) as per se; and (gC1) optionally feeding at least a part of the stream comprising methane as obtained in the first PSA step to step (a). At least a part of the gas stream comprising ethylene, ethane and methane as obtained in step (c) is fed to a first PSA unit, resulting in a stream comprising methane and ethane and a stream comprising ethylene and ethane which latter stream is fed to step (d) to obtain a stream comprising ethane and ethylene and a stream comprising ethylene, where the stream comprising ethane and ethylene as obtained in step (d) is recycled to the first PSA unit, the stream comprising methane and ethane as obtained in the first PSA step is fed to a second PSA unit to obtain a stream comprising ethane and a stream comprising methane, and at least a part of the stream comprising methane as obtained in the second PSA step is optionally fed to step (a).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108317648-A
TI Data machine room carbon dioxide heat pipe air conditioner system, has heat pipe condenser fixed at outer side of data room, where heat pipe air conditioner system utilizes carbon dioxide as cooling medium.
AU TONG Z
   LIU X
   LI Z
AE UNIV QINGDAO TECHNOLOGY (UQDT-C)
GA 2018600218
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a data machine room provided with multiple machine cabinets. A heat pipe condenser and a heat pipe evaporator are fixed on a side of the machine cabinet. The heat pipe evaporator and a cooling unit are installed on the side of the machine cabinet. The heat pipe evaporator is connected with a heat pipe condenser. The gas pipe is connected to a top end of the heat pipe condenser by the heat pipe evaporator. The liquid pipe is connected to a bottom part of the heat pipe condenser by the heat pipe evaporator. The heat pipe condenser is fixed at an outer side of the data room, where a heat pipe air conditioner system utilizes carbon dioxide as a cooling medium.
   USE - Data machine room carbon dioxide heat pipe air conditioner system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can satisfy carbon dioxide heat pipe requirements under a high-pressure running working condition.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a data machine room carbon dioxide heat pipe air conditioner system.

PT P
PN CN108295905-A
TI Preparation of surfactant-enhanced carbon dioxide            reforming catalyst for performing carbon            dioxide/methane reforming reaction, involves reacting            surfactant, nickel and distilled water, adding            molecular sieve, evaporating, drying, calcining.
AU GUO F
   ZHANG P
   ZHANG C
   XU J
AE UNIV CHONGQING TECHNOLOGY (UYQG-C)
GA 201859144Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing surfactant-enhanced                carbon dioxide reforming catalyst, involves (a)                dissolving the surfactant tetrapropylammonium                hydroxide and nickel in quantitative distilled                water in a ratio of 1:100-1:5, stirring until                completely dissolved to obtain surfactant solution,                (b) adding nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate to                surfactant solution, stirring until completely                dissolved to obtain mixed solution, (c) adding                MCM-41 molecular sieve to mixed solution, and                immersing to obtain mixed slurry, (d) placing the                mixed slurry in water bath, and evaporating to                dryness in a water bath to obtain dry powder, (e)                placing the dry powder in oven and drying to obtain                crude catalyst, (f) placing the crude catalyst in                muffle furnace and calcining to obtain final                product. The total mass of carrier and active                component nickel is 100%. The quality of active                component nickel in the catalyst is 10 %mass.
   USE - Method for preparing surfactant-enhanced                carbon dioxide reforming catalyst for performing                carbon dioxide/methane reforming reaction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of                surfactant-enhanced carbon dioxide reforming                catalyst with high temperature activity and low                temperature activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  surfactant and nickel is 1:50. The carrier is                  MCM-41 molecular sieve.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The surfactant is                  tetrapropylammonium hydroxide.

GA 2020B6417E

The method stabilizes the carbonate ion intermediate produced by the reaction of alkoxide with carbon dioxide by utilizing the coordination to the metal ion, and produces an efficient catalytic cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An ionic ligand comprises cyclohexa-2,4-dien-1-ol-containing compound of formula (I).
   Ar = aryl;and
   G = H or methoxy.

The metal halide is metal iodide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic ligand is prepared from tetraarylphosphonium salt. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide fixation reaction is a reaction of epoxide and carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide fixation method further involves attacking the epoxide using halide ion of the metal halide by nucleophilic attack, generating an alkoxide as an intermediate, reacting alkoxide with the carbon dioxide, producing a cyclic carbonate and regenerating the Lewis acid/halide ion composite catalyst.
   Preferred Definitions:
   Ar = 4-methoxyphenyl or phenyl.

PT P
PN CN110396563-A; CN110396563-B
TI Recycling steel residue, involves grinding steel residue, performing reduction treatment and carburizing treatment, performing ball milling on carburized steel residue in carbon dioxide-containing atmosphere and performing conversion.
AU CHEN D
   GUO H
   LI P
   CHEN S
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 201992713X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recycling steel residue, involves (a) grinding steel residue, performing reduction treatment on ground steel residue using reducing gas at 600-800 degrees C, where the reducing gas comprises hydrogen and selective carbon dioxide, (b) performing carburizing treatment on reduced steel residue using carburizing gas at 600-700 degrees C, where the carburizing gas comprises carbon monoxide and selective carbon dioxide and hydrogen, (c) performing ball milling on carburized steel residue in carbon dioxide-containing atmosphere, and performing magnetic separation to obtain concentrate and tailings, and (d) introducing carbon dioxide-containing gas to the tailings, and performing conversion treatment to obtain tailings.
   USE - Method for recycling steel residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the high added value of electric furnace, eliminates the free calcium oxide contained in steel residue and improves the comprehensive utilization degree of steel residue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide in reducing gas is 80-100:0-20. The volume ratio of hydrogen gas, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide in carburizing gas is 0-30:60-100:0-20. Preferred Conditions: The method involves performing reduction treatment in 0.2-1.5 MPa for less than 60 minutes, and performing carburizing treatment in 0.2-1.5 MPa for 60-240 minutes. The step (d) involves performing conversion treatment for 6-24 hours. Preferred Properties: The magnetic field strength of magnetic separation is 60-200 mT. The particle size of polished steel residue is 60% or more and less than 325 mesh. The fineness of ball milled carburization product is 100% and less than 325 mesh.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)
MC M24-A07B; M24-B01A; M24-D06

TI Promoting capture of low concentration carbon            dioxide using riboflavin by placing cation exchange            membrane in electrolytic cell to divide electrolytic            cell into anode region and cathode region, and            releasing high purity carbon dioxide.

   XIE H
   LIU T
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2018872179
AB    NOVELTY - The method for promoting capture of low                concentration carbon dioxide using riboflavin                involves placing cation exchange membrane in an                electrolytic cell to divide the electrolytic cell                into an anode region and a cathode region, adding                flavin mononucleotide solution and potassium                hydrogen carbonate solution as electrolyte in the                cathode region, adding reduced flavin                mononucleotide solution and potassium hydrogen                carbonate solution as electrolyte in the anode                region, and applying direct current power source                between the anode electrode and the cathode                electrode under the action of current, receiving                two protons from the potassium hydrogen carbonate                solution to form reduced flavin mononucleotide, and                releasing high purity carbon dioxide.

TI Manufacture of methionine used as feed additive            for animal, involves hydrolyzing            5(2(methylthio)ethyl)imidazolidine2,4dione in presence            of alkali compound, precipitating methionine by            introducing carbon dioxide, and separating.
AU OMOTO N
   SATOH Y
   MORIKAWA M
   KOIZUMI Y
   YAMASHIRO N
   KATAGAMI R
AE SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
GA 201885657L
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of methionine involves hydrolyzing                5(2(methylthio)ethyl)imidazolidine2,4dione in                presence of an alkali compound, obtaining a                reaction liquid containing an alkali salt of                methionine, precipitating methionine by introducing                carbon dioxide into the reaction liquid, obtaining                slurry including methionine, adding the slurry to a                pressure filter, subjecting to solid-liquid                separation (s1), obtaining solid methionine and                mother liquor, and collecting carbon dioxide from                the mother liquor.
   USE - Manufacture of methionine used as feed                additive for animal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables environmentally-friendly                and economical manufacture of methionine by                preventing release of carbon dioxide mother                liquor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacturing equipment of methionine, which                comprises a reaction equipment for obtaining the                reaction liquid, a crystallization equipment for                obtaining the slurry, a pressure filtration                equipment for obtaining solid methionine and mother                liquid, and a carbon dioxide collection                equipment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The press filter is a                  continuous press filter. The downstream of the                  pressure filtration equipment is equipped with                  the solid-liquid-separation equipment. The                  pressure filtration equipment is continuous                  pressure filtration installation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The solid-liquid                  separation process (s1) further involves reducing                  liquid content of solid methionine by drying or                  using filter press. The manufacturing method                  involves mixing solid methionine and a re-slurry                  liquid, obtaining slurry, and subjecting to                  solid-liquid separation (s2). Preferred                  Composition: The liquid content of solid                  methionine obtained by process (s2) is less than                  the liquid content of solid methionine obtained                  by process (s1).

PN CN114109304-A
TI Temporary plugging agent-assisted carbon dioxide            huff and puff oil recovery method, comprises e.g.            injecting temporary plugging agent into slug, replacing            with water, allowing to stand, injecting carbon dioxide            slug, and injecting water, and stewing.
AU YANG Y
   LI C
   QIN X
AE UNIV SICHUAN SCI & ENGINEERING (USLG-C)
GA 202238259A
AB    NOVELTY - Temporary plugging agent-assisted carbon                dioxide huff and puff oil recovery method,                comprises (i) injecting the temporary plugging                agent into slug, replacing it with water, and                allowing to stand for setting, (ii) injecting                carbon dioxide slug, and injecting water to                replace, (iii) stewing the well, and (iv) opening                the well to produce.
   USE - The method is useful for temporary plugging                agent-assisted carbon dioxide huff and puff oil                recovery.
   ADVANTAGE - The temporary plugging agent-assisted carbon                dioxide huff and puff oil: can effectively block                the dominant seepage channels in the reservoir;                prevents carbon dioxide from forming gas channeling                along the dominant seepage channel and thus                expanding the sweep volume of carbon dioxide; and                can be degraded under the conditions of the                reservoir and thus avoiding permanent damage to the                reservoir; greatly improves the plugging strength,                and facilitating the flow of crude oil after huff                and puff. The method has characteristics of simple                process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temporary plugging                  agent is prepared by adding deionized water and                  acrylic acid into a round bottom flask, adjusting                  the pH of the solution with sodium hydroxide,                  then adding acrylamide, sodium allyl sulfonate                  and dipropylene glycol diacrylate, fully                  stirring, adding deionized water to make a                  solution, and passing nitrogen to remove oxygen,                  and adding ammonium persulfate-sodium bisulfite                  initiator and stirring to dissolve completely.                  The pH value of the solution adjusted with sodium                  hydroxide is 7-7.5. The mass concentration of the                  solution prepared by adding deionized water is                  25-30%. In step (i), the amount of the temporary                  plugging agent slug is 10-50 t, and the                  displacement is 3-5 t/h, 20-30 t clean water                  should be injected to replace it after the                  temporary plugging agent is injected into the                  slug, and then wait for 5h to congeal. In step                  (i), the amount of the carbon dioxide slug is                  200-500 t, the displacement is 4-6 t/h, after the                  carbon dioxide slug is injected, 20t-30t of clean                  water needs to be injected to replace, and the                  well is opened for production after 20-30 days of                  soaking.Preferred Components: In step (i), the slug                  of the temporary plugging agent is composed of                  deionized water, acrylic acid, sodium hydroxide,                  acrylamide, sodium allyl sulfonate, dipropylene                  glycol diacrylate, and ammonium persulfate-sodium                  bisulfite initiator. The temporary plugging agent                  slug is of formula (I). The molar ratio of                  ammonium persulfate to sodium bisulfite is 1:1.                  The mass percentage of the acrylic acid is 7-12%;                  the addition amount of the sodium hydroxide is                  55.5% of the added amount of acrylic acid, and                  the pH is adjusted to 7-7.5; the addition amount                  of the acrylamide is 15-20%; the addition amount                  of the sodium allyl sulfonate is 0.1-0.8%; the                  addition amount of dipropylene glycol diacrylate                  is 0.1-0.4% of the total added amount of acrylic                  acid and acrylamide; the total addition amount of                  the ammonium persulfate and sodium bisulfite is                  0.1-0.5% of the total added amount of acrylic                  acid, acrylamide, sodium allyl sulfonate and                  dipropylene glycol diacrylate, and the rest is                  deionized water, and the mass is in grams.x, y, z, m, a, b, and c = degrees of                  polymerization.

reservoirs are considered.

GA 201861863F
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing carbon dioxide to ethanol on copper                indium alloy modified CuInS2 thin film electrode                involves modifying copper indium metal alloy on                surface of CuInS2 film by potentiostatic deposition                method under condition of illumination. The CuInS2                film electrode modified with copper indium alloy is                used as photocathode, and carbon dioxide is reduced                to ethanol by photoelectrocatalytic reaction in                aqueous solution.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide to ethanol                on copper indium alloy modified CuInS2 thin film                electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves                  mixing copper salt, indium salt, and                  triethanolamine to form an electrodeposition                  solution when the potentiostatic method deposits                  copper-indium alloy on the surface of CuInS2                  film, where copper salt is one or more selected                  from a group consist of copper chloride, copper                  sulfate and copper nitrate, and indium salt is                  one or more selected from a group consist of                  indium chloride, indium sulfate, and indium                  nitrate, and when the potentiostatic method                  deposits copper-indium alloy on the surface of                  CuInS2 film the concentration of copper salt is                  3-12mM, concentration of indium salt is 3-6mM,                  concentration of triethanolamine is 0.1-0.4 M,                  and molar ratio of copper salt to indium salt is                  1:1-2:1, and when the potentiostatic method                  deposits a copper-indium alloy on the surface of                  CuInS2 film then the pH of the deposition                  solution is 3.6-6.0, and when the potentiostatic                  method deposits a copper-indium alloy on the                  surface of the CuInS2 film then the deposition                  potential is -0.6 to -1.5 V with respect to the                  saturated calomel electrode, and when the                  potentiostatic method deposits copper-indium                  alloy on the surface of CuInS2 film the                  electrodeposition time is 2-60 seconds, where                  during the photoelectrocatalytic reaction the                  reduction potential is -0.5 to -1.0 V with                  respect to the saturated calomel electrode.

The top of a magnetic fluid generating nozzle (5) is provided with a first-stage exhaust port (52). The side of the magnetic fluid generation nozzle away from the nozzle is connected to a second-level exhaust port and a liquid outlet (54). The liquid outlet is connected with a self-heating separating container (6) through the liquid outlet pipeline (55), and the top of the self-heating separating container communicates with the circulation loop of the compressor set through the carbon dioxide discharging pipeline (62). The self-heating separating container is communicated with the liquid inlet (51) of the magnetic fluid generating spraying pipe through a liquid discharging pipeline (63).
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide cycle power generation system for use in marine floating nuclear power station. Can also be used in high-speed rotating machinery e.g.compressor and turbine rotating machinery.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide cycle power generation system solves the problem that the carbon dioxide leakage causes the waste of the working substance loading based on carbon dioxide and the threat to the operator in the cabin caused by the leakage of carbon dioxide in the high-speed rotating machine using carbon dioxide working substance. The carbon dioxide dissolving liquid in the self-heating separating container enters the magnetic fluid generating nozzle again through the liquid discharging pipeline, and is mixed with carbon dioxide mixed gas entering the magnetic flow generating spraying pipe, realizing the recycling of the gas in the circulating loop of the compressor set. The working substance waste of gas caused by carbon dioxide is avoided.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the supercritical carbon dioxide cycle power generation system for use in marine floating nuclear power station.

PT P
PN CN114656352-A
TI Preparation of phenylpropionic acid derivative            using semiconductor photocatalysis activated carbon            dioxide by taking mesoporous boron nitride carbon as            photocatalyst, activating carbon dioxide under room            temperature and visible light illumination condition            and adding styrene organic molecule.
AU WU S
   WU Z
   SUN L
   YUAN T
   ZHENG M
   WANG X
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202286900U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of phenylpropionic acid                derivative using semiconductor photocatalysis                activated carbon dioxide involves taking mesoporous                boron nitride carbon as photocatalyst, activating                carbon dioxide under room temperature and visible                light illumination condition and adding styrene                organic molecule to generate the phenylpropionic                acid derivative.
   USE - As preparation method of phenylpropionic acid                derivative using semiconductor photocatalysis                activated carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes immobilization of carbon                dioxide to prepare phenylpropionic acid derivatives                under the conditions of room temperature, normal                pressure and visible light illumination, utilizes                the mesoporous boron nitride carbon material for                fixation and conversion of photocatalytic carbon                dioxide, adopts simple reaction operation, mild                conditions, and low cost, meets the actual                production needs, avoids metal residues, and has                great application potential, higher                regioselectivity and stronger economic                applicability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves adding                  mesoporous boron nitride carbon photocatalyst,                  alkali and styrene-based raw materials to the                  solvent, filling with carbon dioxide gas,                  stirring and reacting for 20-40 hours at room                  temperature under the condition of visible light                  illumination, after the reaction is completed,                  the reaction liquid is acidified with                  hydrochloric acid, filtering the photocatalyst                  and extracting the reaction liquid by using                  dichloromethane or ethyl acetate, combining the                  organic phase, drying, filtering, decompressing                  and distilling the solvent to obtain a crude                  product which is purified by column                  chromatography to obtain the phenylpropionic acid                  derivative. The preparation of mesoporous boron                  nitride carbon involves (i) preparing urea, boric                  acid, citric acid and silica solution in the mass                  ratio of (1-10):(1-5):(1-5):(1- 5), mixing                  evenly, stirring and drying, (ii) The mixed                  powders were calcined at 1000-1200&#8451; for                  1-6 hours in an ammonia atmosphere, and the                  silicon sphere template was removed with ammonium                  hydrogen fluoride to obtain mesoporous boron                  nitride carbon photocatalysts.Preferred Components: The styrene-based raw                  materials is one of styrene, indene, 1,2-styrene,                  1,1-styrene, and                  N-(3-phenoxybenzyl)-4-vinylaniline. The used                  alkali is potassium carbonate. The solvent is N,                  N-dimethyl formamide.Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the                  used photocatalyst and the styrene-based raw                  material is 0.3:1.Preferred Condition: The carbon dioxide                  pressure is normal pressure.Preferred Properties:The                  boron-nitrogen-carbon is a mesoporous rich                  graphite and has a specific surface area of                  140-600 m2/g, a pore diameter of 5-20 nm, and an                  absorption band edge of 400-600 nm.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)

PN CN113564624-A
TI Recycling method of lead material for carbon dioxide reduction of formate by splitting waste lead-acid battery to obtain cathode sheet, processing, and loading on gas diffusion type carbon paper to obtain lead-containing electrode.
AU FANG W
   XIA B
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2021C9194D
AB    NOVELTY - A recycling method of lead material for carbon dioxide reduction of formate comprises splitting the waste lead-acid battery to obtain cathode sheet, processing to obtain lead-containing plate material, loading on gas diffusion type carbon paper to obtain lead-containing electrode, taking lead containing electrode as working electrode, continuously introducing carbon dioxide in the sealed three-electrode system, and performing carbon dioxide electrocatalysis reaction to carry out in-situ conversion of lead to lead carbonate to be used as active substance to catalyze and convert carbon dioxide into formate.
   USE - The method is for recycling lead material for carbon dioxide reduction of formate.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is capable of efficiently catalyzing carbon dioxide into formate, and has excellent stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The electrocatalysis reaction is done at -0.7 to -1.1 V for 10 minutes
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reference electrode in three-electrode system is silver silver chloride electrode or mercury mercury oxide electrode, auxiliary electrode is platinum sheet or foamed nickel, and electrolyte is potassium hydroxide solution or sodium hydroxide solution.

PT P
PN JP2021084864-A
TI Manufacture of methanol used for e.g. coal-fired power generation, involves e.g. allowing carbon dioxide absorbent containing amine and non-aqueous solvent to absorb carbon dioxide, and adding e.g. olefin compound to amine solution.
AU HARA Y
AE TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
GA 202159908R
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of methanol involves (1) allowing a carbon dioxide absorbent containing an amine and a non-aqueous solvent to absorb carbon dioxide, (2) adding an olefin compound and/or an acid catalyst to an amine solution containing absorbed carbon dioxide and heating to 80 degrees C or more to produce a carbamic acid ester, and (3) heating the carbamic acid ester to 70 degrees C or more in the presence of hydrogen and palladium catalyst and regenerating the amine.
   USE - Manufacture of methanol used for coal-fired power generation, fuel conversion from coal to biomass, and collection of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of methanol with low energy, and can reduce the energy for separating, recovering, and purifying carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidic catalyst is solid acid such as organic acid, mineral acid, metal halide, aluminum chloride, zinc chloride, and boron fluoride, a sulfuric acid, phosphate, methanesulfonic acid, trifluoromethanesulfonic acid, p-toluenesulfonic acid, a trifluoroacetic acid, an ion exchange resin, zeolite, and a silica alumina. The palladium catalyst comprises palladium which is supported on activated carbon or a metal oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine is a primary amine such as ethanolamine, alkylamine, and alkylenediamine. The non-aqueous solvent is at least one chosen from olefin compound and ether compound. The olefin is at least one chosen from ethylene, propylene, isobutene, cyclohexene, and styrene.
   EXAMPLE - 4 g n-Butylamine and 20 g cyclohexene were placed in a pressure-resistant container, and a mixed gas of 40% carbon dioxide and 60% nitrogen were supplied at room temperature and atmospheric pressure to absorb carbon dioxide. The temperature was raised to 60 degrees C and maintained at 60 degrees C for 2 hours. Then, 1.2 g aluminum chloride was added, the pressure-resistant container was sealed, and the temperature was raised until the internal temperature reached 119 degrees C. The mixture was cooled to room temperature, 0.4 g palladium/carbon ethylenediamine composite was added, and the mixture was heated to 78 degrees C while pressurizing to 0.427 MPaG in the presence of hydrogen. After heating for 4 hours, the mixture was cooled, and the mixture inside the pressure-resistant container was analyzed to obtain cyclohexanol and methanol.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); X15 (Non-Fossil Fuel Power Generating Systems)

PT P
PN CN110292905-A
TI Preparation of spherical calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent for adsorbing carbon dioxide, involves dissolving calcium oxide formed by calcining in inert carrier precursor, reacting and subjecting to extrusion spheronization.
AU TONG X
   LIU W
   XU M
   YANG Y
   CAO J
   LI Q
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2019856424
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of spherical calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent involves dissolving the calcium oxide formed by calcining calcium-based material in a solution of an inert carrier precursor at 850-950 degrees C for 30-90 minutes, carrying out hydration reaction to obtain a mixed suspension, stirring the mixed suspension obtained under heating, evaporating the water in the mixed suspension to obtain soft material mixture of an inert carrier precursor and calcium hydroxide, and subjecting the obtained soft material mixture to extrusion spheronization.
   USE - Preparation of spherical calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent for adsorbing carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process prepares adsorption pellets with good adsorption performance and mechanical properties which meet material performance and strength requirements of the calcium circulation system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of spherical calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent for adsorbing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further involves pre-calcining, and converting the calcium-based compound into calcium oxide to obtain precalcined calcium-based adsorbent. Preferred Composition: The mass fraction of calcium oxide in the calcined adsorbent is more than 50%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The calcium-based material is one or more of natural limestone, dolomite, calcium nitrate, calcium carbonate, calcium formate, calcium acetate, calcium propionate, calcium gluconate, calcium lactate, calcium tartrate and calcium malate. The inert carrier precursor is one or more of aluminum nitrate, magnesium acetate, magnesium nitrate, and manganese acetate. Preferred Conditions: The rotation speed of the extruder is 20-50 rpm, and spheronizer is 1000-1400 rpm. Preferred Property: The diameter of spherical calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent is 0.9-3.35 mm.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN JP7048125-B1; JP2022105973-A
TI Carbon dioxide fixation device used in active equipment of combustion facility continuously discharging large amount of combustion gas in relatively large scale, performs fixation of carbon dioxide by reaction of two stages on reaction tube.
AU SORIMACHI K
AE SORIMACHI K (SORI-Individual)
GA 2022581796
AB    NOVELTY - The fixation device (1A) has a fixed reaction liquid supply unit (7A, 7B) which supplies a fixed reaction liquid containing a carbon dioxide fixing agent. A tubular reaction tube (10A) is provided for introducing a gas containing carbon dioxide. The fixed material of carbon dioxide is made recoverable by a fixed material recovery unit on the downstream side. Sodium hydroxide is used as the carbon dioxide fixing agent. One of the chloride of the group II element and the chloride of the bivalent metal element is used. Nozzles (20, 21) continuously arranged from the base end side to the tip side in the reaction tube. The upper-side prescribed range supplies sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, and the lower-side predetermined range supplies one of the aqueous solution of the group two element and the chloride of the bivalent metal element. The fixation of carbon dioxide is performed by the reaction of two stages on the upper side and the lower side of the reaction tube.
   USE - Carbon dioxide fixation device used in active equipment of combustion facility continuously discharging large amount of combustion gas in relatively large scale such as thermal power plant and incinerator.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide fixing device can continuously and efficiently immobilize carbon dioxide from a large amount of gas containing carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The fixation device has a sensor which is provided for measuring the carbonate ion concentration is arranged at a predetermined position of the flow path where the liquid flows down in the reaction tube. A dilution unit is provided for lowering the carbonic acid ion concentration in the liquid flowing down inside the reaction tube by jetting water on the basis of the measured data by the sensor is arranged in a prescribed position inside the reaction tube.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the carbon dioxide fixation device.
   Carbon dioxide fixation device (1A)
   Filters (6)
   Fixed reaction liquid supply unit (7A, 7B)
   Tubular reaction tube (10A)
   Nozzles (20, 21)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The one of the chloride of the Group two element and the chloride of the divalent metal element is calcium chloride.

TI Carbon dioxide recovery or discharge device that            condenses the carbon dioxide in the outside air taken            in and releases a gas containing carbon dioxide at a            high concentration to an external facility comprises an            adsorbing unit.

AE NIPPON SEIKO KK (NSEI-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide recovery or discharge device                that condenses the carbon dioxide in the outside                air taken in and releases a gas containing carbon                dioxide at a high concentration to an external                facility comprises an adsorbing unit in which an                adsorbing unit (1a) including a breathable                container containing zeolite (10) and a heater                provided around the breathable container is stacked                in multiple stages, and taking in the outside air                and the main portion containing the adsorption                portion (1). A blower unit for sending to the                suction portion, a storage container (4) connected                to the main portion, and a first on-off valve (20)                attached to the main portion. A second on-off valve                (21) attached to a pipe connecting the main portion                and the storage container. A third on-off valve                (22) attached to a pipe connecting the storage                container and the external facility, and when the                first on-off valve is opened and the second on-off                valve and the third on-off valve are closed.
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery or discharge                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide recovery or discharge                device is a simple structure, and the carbon                dioxide in the taken-in external air is raised                efficiently, and the gas which contains a carbon                dioxide in high concentration is supplied to an                external installation, and has low maintenance                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The outside air is sent to the adsorbing                portion by the air blowing unit for a predetermined                time, and the zeolite in the outside air is                oxidized, and after stopping the blowing of the                blowing unit and closing the first on-off valve and                then heating the zeolite by the heater to release                the carbon dioxide from the zeolite, then opened                the second on-off valve to send the gas in the main                portion to the storage, and recovery of carbon                dioxide characterized in that the third on-off                valve is opened to release the gas in the storage                container to the external facility.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of                carbon dioxide recovery or discharge device.

TI Low-energy consumption anhydrous carbon dioxide            phase change absorbent for e.g. recovery of carbon            dioxide from flue gas, comprises diamine compound            having primary amine and tertiary amine, and is free of            solvent, water and ionic liquid.
AU ZHANG Q

   LI Y
   ZHANG J
   CAI Y

   JIN X
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
   UNIV TIANGONG (UYTI-Non-standard)
   UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 201895709G
AB    NOVELTY - A low-energy consumption anhydrous carbon                dioxide phase change absorbent comprises a single                diamine compound (I) having both primary amine and                a tertiary amine, and is free of other organic                solvent, water and ionic liquid. The tertiary amine                nitrogen atom is linked to two alkyl branches. The                diamine compound is preferably                N,N-dimethylaminoethylamine,                N,N-diethylethylenediamine,                N,N'-diisopropylethylenediamine or                N,N'-dibutylethylenediamine.
   USE - Low-energy consumption anhydrous carbon                dioxide phase change absorbent for recovering                carbon dioxide from chemical reaction tail gas,                combustion flue gas and natural mixed gas, and                removing carbon dioxide from city gas and natural                gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbent is economical, can be                effectively regenerated, and has low thermal                decomposition temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A low-energy consumption anhydrous carbon                dioxide phase change absorbent comprises a single                diamine compound having both primary amine and a                tertiary amine, and is free of other organic                solvent, water and ionic liquid. The diamine                compound is of formula (I), in which tertiary amine                nitrogen atom is linked to two alkyl branches. The                diamine compound is preferably                N,N-dimethylaminoethylamine,                N,N-diethylethylenediamine,                N,N'-diisopropylethylenediamine or                N,N'-dibutylethylenediamine.
   R1,R2 = CH3;
   R3 = -CH2-CH2-;
   or R1,R2 = CH3-CH2-;
   R3 = -CH2-CH2-;
   or R1,R2 = CH3-CH(CH3)-;
   R3 = -CH2-CH2-;
   or R1,R2 = CH3-CH2-CH2-CH2-;and
   R3 = -CH2-CH2-.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of                regenerating low-energy consumption anhydrous                carbon dioxide phase change absorbent, which                involves absorbing carbon dioxide with 2-4 g phase                change absorbent in a reactor, placing the reactor                in an oil bath, controlling the temperature to                90-120 degrees C, introducing nitrogen at rate of                25-45 ml/minute, heating for 40-80 minutes,                calculating carbon dioxide release amount and                regeneration efficiency, placing reactor in water                bath at 20-50 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide                at rate of 20-40 ml/minute, performing phase change                reaction for 40-60 minutes, sealing, calculating                carbon dioxide absorption amount, repeating the                regeneration and absorption 4 times to calculate                the regeneration efficiency of the diamine and the                absorption capacity of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The absorbent absorbs                  carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 20-40 ml/minute,                  and reaches absorption saturation at 8-15                  minutes. The carbon dioxide loading amount of the                  absorbent is 0.4-0.499 mol carbon dioxide/mol                  amine. The absorbent undergoes liquid-solid phase                  transformation after absorbing carbon dioxide,                  and solid phase white carbamate crystals are                  directly formed from the liquid phase. The                  decomposition temperature is 45-60 degrees C, at                  which carbon dioxide is regenerated. The                  absorbent is a waterless single absorbent, and                  excess liquid is not generated after absorbing                  carbon dioxide. The absorption load measured at                  50 degrees C is more than the absorption load                  measured at 30 degrees C by 0.01-0.02 mol carbon                  dioxide/mol of amine.

PN CN110923059-A
TI Extraction of walnut oil using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid involves drying and crushing walnut kernels, adding to extraction kettle, injecting carbon dioxide gas, to dissolve walnut oil component in supercritical fluid and collecting.
AU LIU M
   ZHAN H
   XIANG N
   LONG Y
   YANG Z
AE GUIZHOU AEROSPACE WUJIANG ELECTRO-MECHAN (CAES-C)
GA 202026207C
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of walnut oil using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid involves drying and crushing walnut kernels, adding walnut powder to material barrel of the extraction kettle, injecting carbon dioxide gas into the extraction kettle, keeping temperature and pressure inside the extraction kettle in contact with natural materials in the supercritical state of the carbon dioxide gas for dissolving walnut oil component of the walnut powder in supercritical fluid and collecting the walnut oil from outlet of separation kettle.
   USE - Extraction of walnut oil using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, environmentally-friendly and economical, and uses carbon dioxide as extractant to produce walnut oil with high yield and less consumption of solvent and reduces energy consumption. The walnut oil has high purity, no odor, no solvent residue and good color and is environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The extraction is carried out at 35 degrees C under pressure of 40 MPa for 1.5 hours. The separation is carried out at 35 degrees C under pressure of 14 MPa. Preferred Process: The drying process involves washing walnuts, drying in oven at 50-60 degrees C until the moisture content of walnut to be 10-15%, placing in pulverizer to crush into walnuts into powder having particle size of more than 60 mesh.

PT P
PN CN108359123-A; CN108359123-B
TI Preparing thermoplastic polymer expanded bead comprises e.g. adding thermoplastic polymer bead to spouted bed, introducing carbon dioxide to replace the air, then heating the spouted bed and injecting carbon dioxide continuously.
AU ZHAO L
   XU Z
   LIU T
   YAO S
   ZONG Y
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2018628908
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing thermoplastic polymer expanded bead comprises adding thermoplastic polymer bead to spouted bed, introducing carbon dioxide to replace the air, then heating the spouted bed to foaming temperature, and continuously injecting carbon dioxide to make the carbon dioxide in a supercritical state, then performing a carbon dioxide cycle to maintaining the thermoplastic polymer beads in the bed in a spouted state, depressurizing the spouted bed and discharging the supercritical carbon dioxide in bed to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing thermoplastic polymer expanded bead.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, low energy consumption and low production cost; shortens the foaming time and improves the uniformity of obtained foamed bead size.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for thermoplastic polymer expanded bead is obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The thermoplastic polymer beads are polyethylene beads, polypropylene beads or thermoplastic polyurethane beads. The thermoplastic polymer beads have an average diameter of 2-4 mm. The loaded volume of the thermoplastic polymer beads does not exceed 50% of volume of the bed layer of spouted bed. Preferred Method: The heating is programmed temperature-controlled heating and the heating rate is 1-10 degrees C/minute. The foaming temperature is (Tm-30 degrees C) to (Tm-10 degrees C). The injection pressure of the injection is 10-15 MPa (preferably 12-15 MPa). The spouting speed in the spouted state is 0.20-0.50 m/second. The maintaining time of the spouting state is 20-40 minutes, preferably 25-30 minutes.Tm = melting point of polymer beads.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.)

TI Method for removing carbon dioxide concentration in i.e. low-oxygen body-building room, involves fixing sensor in airtight space, and adsorbing carbon dioxide in gas through carbon dioxide adsorbent using reproducible adsorption removing device.
AU UNANNOUNCED I
AE TIANJIN CNRO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202240051T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves connecting an air inlet and an air outlet of a reproducible adsorption removing device in an airtight space (S101). An air suction valve and an exhaust valve are installed (S102) on the air outlet of the reproducible adsorption removing device. The air outlet of the reproducible adsorption removing device is switched (S103) to an airtight space or atmospheric environment by an exhaust valve. A sensor is fixed (S104) in the airtight space for monitoring carbon dioxide concentration and sending a monitoring result to a main control module. A switching state of an air suction valve and the exhaust valve and starting and stopping of an air pump are controlled (S105) by the main control module according to the received monitoring result to remove carbon dioxide in the airtight space. The carbon dioxide in gas is adsorbed by the reproducible adsorption removing device for obtaining carbon dioxide adsorbent by air-blowing.
   USE - Method for removing carbon dioxide concentration in environment i.e. low-oxygen body-building room.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables conveniently embedding in a low oxygen system by a carbon dioxide removing system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for removing carbon dioxide concentration in environment.

   Step for installing air suction valve and exhaust valve on air outlet of reproducible adsorption removing device (S102)
   Step for switching air outlet of the reproducible adsorption removing device to airtight space (S103)
   Step for fixing sensor in airtight space for monitoring carbon dioxide concentration and sending monitoring result to main control module (S104)
   Step for controlling switching state of air suction valve and exhaust valve and starting and stopping of air pump by main control module (S105)

PN CN114493270-A
TI Method for testing ventilation times of room in residential building based on carbon dioxide release source, involves calculating room ventilation times according to assumed room penetration air amount if difference between estimated and detected values meets preset condition.
AU REN J
   LIU J
   GUO X
   WANG H
   WAN L
AE UNIV SHANDONG JIANZHU (SDSJ-C)
   CHINA CERTIFICATION CHINA SECURITIES BEI (CHCE-Non-standard)
GA 2022712010
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining indoor staff information based on carbon dioxide emission rate. A current indoor carbon dioxide concentration calculation value is calculated based on the emission rate, an assumed room permeation air amount, a room volume and a current detected outdoor carbon dioxide atmosphere concentration value. An estimated value of carbon dioxide concentrations is determined based on a calculated value and a calculated error. A room ventilation times is calculated according to the assumed room penetration air amount if a difference between the estimated value and the current detected carbon dioxide atmospheric concentration value meets a preset condition. A measurement value is removed at one time.
   USE - Method for testing ventilation times of a room in a residential building based on carbon dioxide release source.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simplifying the workload of measuring air exchange times in the room, reducing the measuring cost, and is convenient for detecting the times of air exchange in the rooms. The method ensures the estimated value and the measured value maximum measuring error condition, iteratively solving indoor ventilation quantity, so as to obtain the room ventilation time, thus reducing the release amount of carbon dioxide in the measuring process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a system for testing ventilation times of a room in a residential building based on carbon dioxide release source;
   (2) a device for testing ventilation times of a room in a residential building based on carbon dioxide release source; and
   (3) a computer storage medium for storing a set of instructions Method for testing ventilation times of a room in a residential building based on carbon dioxide release source.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for testing ventilation times of a room in a residential building based on carbon dioxide release source (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Low-carbon discharge composite cycle power generation system has supercritical carbon dioxide circulation sub-system which is closed cycle, and waste heat circularly cooling water sub- system which is open cycle.

   XU Q
   GAO S

   HAO N

AE SHANGHAI POWER EQUIP RES INST (SNPT-C)
GA 2021D5333W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a composite cycle power generation system that is provided with a hydrogen gas turbine cycle sub-system (1) that is connected with a supercritical carbon dioxide cycle sub system. The supercritical CO2 cycle sub-system (2) is provided with an open cycle and a closed cycle. A hydrogen air turbine cycle -system and a hydrogen air cooling water cycle-system are connected with each other in sequence.
   USE - Low-carbon discharge composite cycle power generation system for use in power generation industry i.e. gas turbine industry, and water supply industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite cycle power generation system combines the hydrogen gas turbine cycle system with the closed supercritical carbon dioxide cycle system, thus improving the energy utilization efficiency, and meeting the requirement of carbon reduction. The top cycle combustion hydrogen fuel greatly reduces the generation of carbon dioxide generating cycle system using pure hydrogen fuel to reach zero carbon dioxide generation, thus realizing zero carbon emission of the circulating system. The waste heat solves the problem of the comprehensive utilization of the energy cascade. At the same time, the system realizes the high power generation efficiency and low pollutant discharge of the system, optimizes the system application mode, and has active social effect.

PN AU2018100586-A4
TI Inhibiting growth of microorganisms in raw milk and perishable organic liquids e.g. fruit juices, by adding carbon dioxide to raw milk, maintaining at specific carbon dioxide concentration, releasing carbon dioxide and processing raw milk.
AU HAGEMEYER R
AE AUSSIE FOOD TO YOU PTY LTD (AUSS-Non-standard)
GA 201860242Y
AB    NOVELTY - Process for inhibiting the growth of microorganisms in raw milk involves (a) adding carbon dioxide to the raw milk to achieve a carbon dioxide concentration of at least 200 parts per million (ppm), (b) placing the carbon dioxide-enhanced raw milk in a vessel and maintaining carbon dioxide concentration of at least 200 ppm without subjecting the raw milk to undesirable biological changes, (c) releasing the carbon dioxide from the raw milk, (d) processing the raw milk and (e) packaging the processed milk for distribution.
   USE - The process is used to inhibit the growth of microorganisms in raw milk and other perishable organic liquids comprising liquids used in food, feed, pharmaceutical or industrial fields, preferably dairy products, vegetable juices, fruit juices, plant extracts, fungal extracts and flavoring agents.
   ADVANTAGE - The process improves the stability of raw milk, extends shelf life of raw milk, facilitates bulk transportation of perishable organic liquids for long distance and cools the liquids to great extent, is economical and offers more time to handle or manufacture the product for human consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for transporting the raw milk involving (i) filling a bulk pressure vessel at a first location with the raw milk mixed with carbon dioxide at a first location to achieve carbon dioxide concentration of 200-2400 ppm, (ii) maintaining the raw milk mixed with carbon dioxide in a pressure vessel of at least 68 kPa head pressure, during transportation by a truck, a rail and/or a ship to a second location, (iii) removing the carbon dioxide from the raw milk by a process involving at least one of agitation, vacuum and/or negative pressure, where the raw milk product does not sustain undesirable biological changes after the process and (iv) processing the raw milk and packaging the processed product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (d) involves one or more processes chosen from pasteurization, homogenization, condensing, culturing and evaporation. The carbon dioxide mixed with the raw milk is maintained at a pressure and temperature to result in a pH of not greater than 6. The undesirable biological changes include one or more of standard plate count of the raw milk increasing by more than 1 log 10 colony forming unit (cfu)/ml, preferably 5 log 10 cfu/ml, the count of thermoduric bacteria, psychotropic bacteria, Escherichia coli and coliform increasing by more than 1 log 10 cfu/ml, and protein precipitation from the raw milk. The carbon dioxide enhanced raw milk is in the vessel is for at least 4 days, preferably at least 3 days. The carbon dioxide is added to the raw milk using a dry ice or a sparge. The carbon dioxide enhanced raw milk is removed from the vessel by compressed air. Preferred Device: The vessel is an inter modal tank suitable for transportation by at least one of truck, rail and cargo ship.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
