TI Preparing glycerol carbonate by catalyzing            glycerol and carbon dioxide using organic amine halogen            salt, comprising catalyzing carbon dioxide and glycerol            using organic amine halide salt ionic liquid as            catalyst.
AU WU K
   WANG B
   LUO C
   LU H
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 202206662U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing glycerol carbonate by catalyzing                glycerol and carbon dioxide using organic amine                halogen salt, comprising catalyzing carbon dioxide                and glycerol using organic amine halide salt ionic                liquid as catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing glycerol                carbonate by catalyzing glycerol and carbon dioxide                using organic amine halogen salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and easy to operate, and                has low energy consumption and mild reaction                process; utilizes catalyst which has excellent                catalytic activity for two-step reaction; and                realizes the efficient fixation of carbon dioxide                to the high value-added chemical glycerol carbonate                under normal pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic amine is                  amine compound of formula (I), and formamidine                  compound of formula (II). The alkylene oxide is                  ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin,                  epibromopropane, butylene oxide, hexamethylene                  oxide, glycidol, styrene oxide, glycidyl ether,                  and/or allyl glyceryl ether. The organic amine                  contains at laest one cyclic structures. The                  molar ratio between alkylene oxide and glycerin                  is 1-10:1, the addition amount of catalyst is                  0.1-10 mol.% of glycerin, and the partial                  pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.1-5 MPa.Preferred Method: The method comprises                  taking catalyst and then carrying out catalytic                  reaction of carbon dioxide, glycerol and alkylene                  oxide under the action of catalyst to obtain                  glycerol carbonate. The reaction temperature is                  70-130degrees Celsius and the reaction time is                  0.5-12 hours. The organic amine halide salt is                  obtained by reacting hydrohalic acid with an                  organic amine in a molar ratio of 0.25-1:1. The                  method comprises reacting hydrohalic acid with an                  organic amine in a molar ratio of 0.25-1:1, and                  subjecting the product to vacuum distillation to                  obtain organic amine halide salt.R1-R4 = 1-8C straight or branched chain                  alkyl group, H, or structure containing hydroxy,                  primary amine, secondary amine, tertiary amine                  and/or imine group.R1 and R2 and/or R2 and R3 and/or R3 and R4                  = optionally bonded together with each other to                  form cyclic ring.

PN US2019345374-A1; US11060014-B2
TI Preparing stable capsule dispersion involves providing co-polymerized capsules containing dense liquid with density greater than that of supercritical carbon dioxide, and functionalizing co-polymerized capsules.
AU AL-OTAIBI F
   KOKAL S
   SCHMIDT H
   CHANG Y
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201995393U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing stable capsule dispersion involves providing co-polymerized capsules containing dense liquid with density greater than that of supercritical carbon dioxide, functionalizing co-polymerized capsules by adding carbon dioxide-philic compound to outer sides of capsule walls to create functionalized co-polymerized capsules, preparing stable capsule dispersion by adding functionalized co-polymerized capsules to continuous phase of supercritical carbon dioxide such that stable dispersion of functionalized co-polymerized capsules in supercritical carbon dioxide is achieved.
   USE - Method for preparing stable capsule dispersion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare stable capsule dispersion that has improves mobility of carbon dioxide and reduces cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The dense liquid is dense aqueous liquid. The dense liquid is water. The dense liquid is heavy liquid filler selected from toluene, crude oil, ester, silicone oil, alcohols, acetone, and combinations. The capsule wall of each functionalized capsule is composed of co-polymer that is polymerization product of first co-monomer and second co-monomer. The first monomer is selected from the group consisting of triethylene tetramine, hexamethylene tetramine, ethylene diamine, hexamethylene diamine, diethylene triamine, and combinations. The second monomer is selected from polymeric diphenylmethane diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyante, hexamethylene diisocyanate, 1,4 phenylene diisocyanate, toluene 2,4 diisocyanate, and combinations. The carbon dioxide-philic compound comprises fluorinated carbon dioxide-soluble surfactant or oxygenated hydrocarbon carbon dioxide-philic molecules. The capsule wall of each functionalized capsule is composed of a hydrogel selected from the group consisting of gelatin, chitosan, starch, alginate, polyvinyl alcohol, polyethylene oxide, polyvinyl pyrrolidone, polyisopropyl acrylamide, and combinations. The carbon dioxide-philic compound comprises poly (1,1 dihydroperfluoroctyl acrylate). Preferred Conditions: The dense liquid has density of 0.5 g/cc. The functionalized capsules have size in range of from about 10-1,000 nanometers. Preferred Process: The method involves subjecting capsule wall of each functionalized capsule defining inner area and has outer side functionalized with carbon dioxide-philic compound after formation of capsules, carbon dioxide-philic compound comprises multiple functional groups attached to outer side by functionalization to stabilize each functionalized co-polymerized capsule in continuous phase of supercritical carbon dioxide. The multiple functional groups extending outwardly from outer side where attached and into continuous phase of supercritical carbon dioxide, such that density of capsule dispersion is greater than density of supercritical carbon dioxide at same conditions and such that viscosity of the capsule dispersion is greater than the viscosity of supercritical carbon dioxide at same conditions. The method involves providing capsules comprises providing first liquid, first liquid having density greater than density of supercritical carbon dioxide, adding first monomer to first liquid to create first monomer-liquid composition, providing second liquid, second liquid operable to form liquid phase when combined with first liquid, adding second monomer to second liquid to create second monomer-liquid composition, adding first monomer-liquid composition to second monomer-liquid composition, agitating the first monomer-liquid composition and second monomer-liquid composition to create dispersion of first monomer-liquid composition in the second monomer-liquid composition and allowing first and second monomers to diffuse and to co-polymerize at liquid-liquid interface at a pre-determined time such that co-polymerized capsules are formed encapsulating first liquid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The heat                exchanger is located to receive heat from a heat                source (110), heat the pressurized carbon dioxide                and obtain the heated and expanded carbon dioxide.                An energy conversion device is located along the                conduit loop to remove energy from the heated and                expanded carbon dioxide. A cooling coil is located                along the conduit loop to remove heat from carbon                dioxide exiting the energy conversion device before                the carbon dioxide reenters the pump.
   USE - Heat and power cogeneration system for                small-scale applications of a refrigeration system                (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to                automotive, solar, portable power supplies,                industrial, domestic and residential applications                and vehicular and mobile living space applications                i.e. boats, recreational vehicles, temporary                dwellings and trailers.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                refrigeration system.

TI Solid sorbent composition for reversibly capturing            carbon dioxide from a carbon-dioxide-containing gaseous            stream comprises calcium oxide, calcium aluminate, and            mixed metal oxide characterized by a perovskite            crystalline structure.
AU WEISSMAN J
   LOEBICK C
AE PRECISION COMBUSTION INC (PREC-Non-standard)
   PRECISION COMBUSTION INC (PREC-Non-standard)
GA 202003700T
AB    NOVELTY - A solid sorbent composition comprises calcium                oxide, calcium aluminate, and mixed metal oxide                characterized by a perovskite crystalline                structure.
   USE - A solid sorbent composition for reversibly                capturing carbon dioxide from a                carbon-dioxide-containing gaseous stream (claimed).                Can be used in capturing carbon dioxide from any                gaseous stream containing carbon dioxide, such as                emissions streams produced in combustion processes                or streams derived from closed environments                including airplanes, spaceships, and                submarines.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is capable of capturing carbon                dioxide with improved sorbent capacity, with long                lifetime, and with little, if any, degradation over                numerous carbonation-decarbonation cycles, as                compared with known carbon dioxide sorbents. The                composition advantageously tolerates certain flue                gas contaminants that otherwise cause problems for                prior art amine sorbents and can be thermally                integrated to produce steam, lowering the parasitic                demand to a penalty of less than or equal to 5%                energy efficiency. Furthermore, the calcium oxide                looping process offers a potential cost reduction                of about 50% versus prior art calcium oxide looping                technologies and about 70% versus prior art amine                technologies.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for synthesis                of solid sorbent composition capable of reversibly                capturing carbon dioxide from a                carbon-dioxide-containing gaseous stream which                involves (a) preparing slurry comprising liquid                diluent, calcium oxide or its precursor, calcium                aluminate or its precursor, and mixed metal oxide                having a perovskite crystalline structure or its                precurser; (b) milling slurry; (c) drying slurry to                remove liquid diluent; and (d) calcining resulting                dried material under calcination conditions                sufficient to produce solid sorbent capable of                reversibly capturing carbon dioxide, where solid                sorbent comprises calcium oxide, calcium aluminate,                and mixed metal oxide having the perovskite                crystalline structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Composition                  comprises 30-90 wt.% calcium oxide (dried basis),                  based on total weight of composition. Composition                  comprises 5-50 wt.% calcium aluminate and 2-20                  wt.% mixed metal oxide characterized by a                  perovskite crystalline structure, based on total                  weight of composition. Calcium aluminate is                  selected from crystalline structures of molecular                  formula Ca9(Al2O6)3 , CaAl4O7, CaAl2O4, and/or                  Ca12Al14O32Y1, preferably a mixture of                  Ca9(Al2O6)3 and Ca12Al14O33. Perovskite                  crystalline structure is represented by formula                  ATX3 (I). Perovskite crystalline structure is                  represented by formula A'T'X3 (II). Mixed metal                  oxide characterized by a perovskite structure is                  selected from lanthanum aluminate, barium                  titanate, and/or strontium titanate. Precursor to                  calcium oxide is calcium hydroxide, calcium                  carbonate, and/or calcium nitrate. Precursor to                  calcium aluminate is a mixture comprising calcium                  oxide (anhydrous or hydrated) and alumina, or                  hydrotalcite of formula CaAl2(CO3)2(OH)4.3H2O, or                  hydrocalumite of formulae Ca4Al2(OH)12(Cl,                  OH)2.4H2O or Ca4Al2(OH)12(CO3).4H2O, or any                  mixture of the materials. Precursor to mixed                  metal oxide having the perovskite structure is                  selected from corresponding mixed metal nitrates,                  sulfates, halides, and/or hydroxides.Y1=O2-, N2-, (OH-)2, (F-)2, (Cl-)2,                  (H2O)4(Cl-)2, (H2O)4(F-)2, or (e-)2;e-=free electron;A=divalent cation of Group IIA;T=tetravalent cation of Group IVA;X=divalent oxide;A'=trivalent cations of Group IIIA and                  lanthanide rare earths; andT'=trivalent cation of Group TIM.Preferred Condition: Calcination is                  conducted under air or oxygen at 800-1400 degrees                  C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Slurry contains                  10-50% solids and comprises a liquid diluent                  selected from water and/or 1-4C alcohols.
   Preferred Definitions:
   A=Ba and/or Sr;
   T=Ti;
   A'=La, Y, Sc, Gd, and/or Yb; and
   T'=Al.

TI Treating/cleaning gas of sulfur dioxide, carbon            dioxide and oxygen, comprises e.g. bringing sulfur            dioxide and carbon dioxide depleted gas of ammonia in            contact with sulfuric acid solution to form solution of            ammonium sulfate and clean gas.
AU STRICKROTH A
   ALAIN S
AE CPPE CARBON PROCESS & PLANT ENG SA (CPPE-Non-standard)
   CPPE CARBON PROCESS & PLANT ENG SA (CPPE-Non-standard)
   CPPE CARBON PROCESS & PLANT ENG SA (CPPE-Non-standard)
   CPPE CARBON PROCESS & PLANT ENG SA (CPPE-Non-standard)
GA 201936467E
AB    NOVELTY - Treating/cleaning a gas containing sulfur                dioxide, carbon dioxide and oxygen, comprises: (a)                bringing the gas in contact with an activated                carbon catalyst, converting sulfur dioxide to                sulfur trioxide/sulfuric acid on the activated                carbon catalyst, washing the sulfur                trioxide/sulfuric acid from the activated carbon                catalyst to obtain a sulfuric acid solution and a                sulfur dioxide depleted gas; (b) bringing the                sulfur dioxide depleted gas in contact with an                aqueous ammonia solution, where carbon dioxide is                converted to obtain sulfur dioxide and carbon                dioxide depleted gas containing ammonia; and (c)                bringing the sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide                depleted gas containing ammonia in contact with the                sulfuric acid solution obtained in step (a) to form                a solution containing ammonium sulfate and a                treated, clean gas.
   USE - The method is useful for treating/cleaning a                gas containing sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide and                oxygen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enhances the performance of the                removal of sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide from                gas (preferably natural gas, syngas, flue gas                generated i.e. by combustion of carbon and                hydrogen-containing fuels e.g. coal, oil and                natural gas, by chemical processes e.g.                metallurgical processes); utilizes the sulfur                dioxide and carbon dioxide concentration fluctuated                repeatedly between 500-700 (preferably 0-10) ppm                for sulfur dioxide and around 6 (preferably 9)                vol.% for carbon dioxide, with a sulfur dioxide                removal efficiency superior to 98%, preferably 99%                and a carbon dioxide conversion efficiency of 51%,                preferably 50%; and eliminates the ammonia slip                i.e. the quantity of unreacted ammonia emitted from                equipment to filter impurities from a gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activated carbon                  catalyst comprises a mixture of an activated                  carbon catalyst and a filler material. The                  mixture comprises no other solid ingredients than                  an activated carbon catalyst or a filler                  material. The filler comprises fillers made of                  ceramic material, made of metal and/or fillers                  made of plastic material. The filler material is                  a shape comprising saddle shaped, ring shaped,                  ball shaped, torus shaped, prism shaped or                  irregular shaped. The mixture of the activated                  carbon catalyst and the filler material is in a                  fixed bed. The activated carbon catalyst or the                  mixture of the activated carbon catalyst and the                  filler material is washed in step (a) with water                  or an aqueous solution in a quantity of 5                  l/hour/m3 of activated carbon catalyst and 100                  l/hour/m3 of activated carbon catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The activated carbon                  catalyst/the mixture of the activated carbon                  catalyst and the filler material is washed by                  intermittent spraying with water or an aqueous                  solution in counter flow to the gas. The gas is                  brought into contact with the mixture has a                  temperature of 10-150 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The gas containing sulfur dioxide,                  carbon dioxide and oxygen comprises a natural                  gas, syngas, flue gas generated by combustion of                  carbon and hydrogen-containing fuels e.g. coal,                  oil and natural gas, by chemical processes and/or                  by metallurgical processes. The sulfur dioxide                  comprises 50-5000 ppm of the gas. The carbon                  dioxide comprises 2-20 vol.% of the gas. The                  oxygen comprises 5-20 vol.% of the gas. The                  sulfuric acid solution comprises 5-50 vol.%. The                  aqueous ammonia solution in step (b) comprises                  ammonia and/or urea.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN KR2019000529-A; KR2413443-B1
TI Substrate processing apparatus for processing            substrate used in semiconductor manufacturing field,            has supply unit that is provided to supply            supercritical carbon dioxide having temperature            different to processing chamber.
AU JUNG Y S
   SUN J Y
AE KCTECH CO LTD (KCTE-C)
GA 2019032908
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a processing chamber (100)                in which a substrate (1,1-1) to which a chemical                liquid is applied is introduced so that a substrate                processing process is performed using a                supercritical carbon dioxide. A supply unit                (200,200-1) is provided to supply the supercritical                carbon dioxide having a temperature different to                the processing chamber. The supply unit is provided                with multiple supply units. A first supply unit is                provided to supply the supercritical carbon dioxide                to the first preset temperature. A second supply                unit is provided to supply the supercritical carbon                dioxide to the second preset temperature higher                than the first preset temperature.
   USE - Substrate processing apparatus for processing                substrate used in semiconductor manufacturing                field.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the supercritical carbon dioxide is                supplied at a low temperature at an initial stage                in which the supercritical carbon dioxide is                supplied into the processing chamber, the                evaporation of the chemical solution applied to the                substrate is prevented. The supercritical carbon                dioxide at a high temperature is supplied, after                the pressure in the processing chamber reaches the                process pressure, and the temperature of the                supercritical carbon dioxide is varied according to                the process step, thus the substrate processing                performance is improved. Since the temperature of                the supercritical carbon dioxide supplied into the                processing chamber is varied, and the fluctuation                pressure is formed inside the processing chamber,                the dissolution reaction between the supercritical                carbon dioxide and the chemical solution is                performed smoothly, and the substrate processing                efficiency is maximized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                substrate processing method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the                substrate processing apparatus for processing                substrate.
   Substrate (1,1-1)
   Processing chamber (100)
   Exhaust line (104)
   Housing (110)
   Supply unit (200,220-1)
DC U11 (Semiconductor Materials and Processes)

PT P
PN WO2018166795-A1; EP3401817-A1; CN110603601-A
TI Sleep monitoring system for detecting asthma symptoms, has processor to identify asthma symptom exhibited by subject if monitored change of carbon dioxide concentration in portion of confined space exceeds benchmark for monitoring period.
AU SU J
   KELLY D P
   RONDA C R
   SU J S
AE KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
GA 201874022Y
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has carbon dioxide (Co2) sensor (21) and a processor (31) that is communicatively coupled to the carbon dioxide sensor. The processor is adapted to monitor a change in a carbon dioxide concentration in a portion of the confined space in close vicinity to the subject is asleep from sensor data produced by the carbon dioxide sensor over a monitoring period. The change in the carbon dioxide concentration caused by exhalation of carbon dioxide by the subject into portion of the confined space is monitored before the exhaled carbon dioxide diffuses into the total volume of the confined space. The monitored change of the carbon dioxide concentration in portion of the confined space is compared against a benchmark for subject. An asthma symptom exhibited by subject is identified if the monitored change of the carbon dioxide concentration in portion of the confined space exceeds the benchmark for subject for monitoring period.
   USE - Sleep monitoring system for detecting asthma symptoms.
   ADVANTAGE - The data collection during calibration is repeated a number of times, in order to improve the accuracy of the extracted thresholds. The asthma medication is automatically released in the proximity of the subject is monitored upon detection of asthma symptoms, thus improving the management of the condition and reducing the risk of serious complications developing from the onset of these symptoms.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method of detecting asthma symptoms; and
   (2) a computer program product for detecting asthma symptoms.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the sleep monitoring system.
   Sleep monitoring system (10)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (21)
   Communication link (25)
   Processor (31)
   Data storage device (33)

   ADVANTAGE - Technical solution reduces power consumption, eliminates the risk of formation of gas hydrates, intensifies extraction of hard-to-recover reserves of high-viscosity oil.

TI Pressurized oxygen-rich supercritical carbon dioxide fluidized bed driven organic Rankine cycle power generation system, has waste heat boiler driven organic Rankine cycle unit connected with carbon dioxide collecting compression unit.
AU SHAO Y
   ZHONG W
   GENG C
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)

AB    NOVELTY - The system has an air-separating oxygen-generating unit orderly connected with a pressurized oxygen-rich fluidized bed driven supercritical carbon dioxide generating unit, a waste heat boiler driven organic Rankine cycle unit and a carbon dioxide collecting compression unit. AB    NOVELTY - Separating carbon dioxide from 2-5C alkanes,                comprises: introducing a first stream comprising                2-5C alkanes and carbon dioxide into a first                separation zone (230), where the first separation                zone comprises a hydrocarbon solvent; separating                the first stream into a recycle stream and a second                stream in the first separation zone, where the                recycle stream comprises carbon dioxide and at                least one of carbon monoxide, hydrogen gas, and                methane, and the second stream comprises 2-5C                alkanes; introducing the second stream into a                second separation zone; and separating the second                stream into a third stream and a fourth stream,                where the third stream comprises 2C alkanes and the                fourth stream comprises 3-5C alkanes.
   USE - The method is useful for separating carbon                dioxide from 2-5C alkanes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                system (200) for separating carbon dioxide from                2-5C alkanes, comprising a first separation zone                comprising a hydrocarbon solvent and that is                configured to separate a first stream comprising                2-5C alkanes and carbon dioxide into a recycle                stream and a second stream, where the recycle                stream comprises carbon dioxide and at least one of                carbon monoxide, hydrogen gas and methane and the                second stream comprises 2-5C alkanes, and a second                separation zone that is fluidly connected to the                first separation zone and that is configured to                separate the second stream into a third stream and                a fourth stream, where the third stream comprises                2C alkanes and the fourth stream comprises 3-5C                alkanes.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of a first                system for separating carbon dioxide in the                production of alkanes.
   Reaction zone (110)
   Feed stream (111)
   System (200)
   Carbon dioxide separator (210)
   First separation zone (230)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The second separation                  zone is fluidly connected to the first separation                  zone so that a portion of the fourth stream is                  used as the hydrocarbon solvent in the first                  separation zone. The system further comprises: a                  third separation zone comprising an amine solvent                  that is fluidly connected to one of the first                  separation zone or the second separation zone and                  that is configured to remove a purge amount of                  carbon dioxide; a reaction zone that is fluidly                  connected to the first separation zone so that                  the first stream is a feed stream introduced from                  the reaction zone to the first separation zone;                  and a splitter that is fluidly connected to the                  first separation zone so that a portion of the                  recycle stream is purged at the splitter.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: introducing at least a portion of the                  fourth stream into the first separation zone as                  the hydrocarbon solvent; introducing the recycle                  stream into a reaction zone, where a portion of                  the recycle stream is purged before the recycle                  stream is introduced into the reaction zone, a                  ratio of the hydrocarbon solvent to 2-5C alkanes                  in the first separation zone is 1:1-5:1, the                  first stream is a feed stream (111) that is                  introduced from a reaction zone into the first                  separation zone, and the third stream further                  comprises carbon dioxide, and the method further                  comprises introducing the third stream to a                  carbon dioxide separator (210) where a purge                  amount of carbon dioxide separated from the third                  stream; removing a purge amount of carbon dioxide                  from the first stream before it is introduced                  into the first separation zone, where the step of                  removing the purge amount of carbon dioxide                  comprises introducing a stream comprising carbon                  dioxide into a carbon dioxide separator that                  comprises an amine solvent, and isolating the                  purge amount of carbon dioxide in the amine                  solvent; and introducing the amine solvent and                  the purge amount of carbon dioxide into a carbon                  dioxide stripper that strips the purge amount                  carbon dioxide from the amine solvent, to form a                  stripped amine solvent, and introducing the                  stripped amine solvent into the carbon dioxide                  separator.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Fixation of carbon dioxide for recovery, involves            forming alkaline aqueous solution containing metal            element which can be combined with carbonate ions,            separating metal element, forming carbonate mineral,            lowering pH of aqueous solution, and adding new raw            material to aqueous solution.
AU NORIAKI W
   JIAJIE W
   NORIYOSHI T
   WATANABE N
   WANG J J
   TSUCHIYA N
AE UNIV TOHOKU (TOHO-C)
GA 2022859777
AB    NOVELTY - Fixation of carbon dioxide involves forming an                alkaline aqueous solution containing a metal                element which can be combined with carbonate ions                to form a carbonate mineral and a chelating agent,                reacting the metal element with the chelating agent                in the aqueous solution to separate the metal                element from the raw material as metal ions,                reacting a carbonate ion generated from a compound                with the metal ion to form a carbonate mineral by                adding a compound capable of generating carbonate                ion in the aqueous solution to the aqueous                solution, injecting carbon dioxide gas into the                aqueous solution to lower the pH to or near the pH                value of the aqueous solution, adding a new raw                material of the same type as the raw material to                the aqueous solution and repeating the processes of                separation to pH adjustment for several                times.
   USE - Fixation of carbon dioxide for recovery of                carbon dioxide emitted from                environmentally-friendly industrial equipment (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of reducing material                cost and equipment cost, and effectively recovers                carbon dioxide from industries having high                environmental load due to carbon dioxide                emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) recovery of carbon dioxide, which involves                performing carbon dioxide fixation method;                and
   (2) a carbon dioxide fixation device, which                has an aqueous solution forming unit provided to                form the alkaline aqueous solution, a mineral                forming portion, a pH-lowering portion, and a raw                material addition portion provided so as to add the                new raw material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material                  contains at least one of calcium, magnesium,                  iron, copper, and manganese as the metal element                  and comprises one or more of silicate minerals,                  steel slag, and waste. The compound capable of                  generating carbonate ion is at least one of                  sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, lithium                  carbonate, and carbon dioxide. Preferred Process:                  The solid component is recovered from the aqueous                  solution after separating the metal element from                  the raw material. The aqueous solution is formed                  by adding the raw material and chelating agent to                  water. Preferred Conditions: In the separation                  process, the metal element and chelating agent                  are reacted at 5-80 degrees C. The carbonate ion                  and metal ion are reacted at 70-170 degrees                  C.

PT P
PN CN113933908-A
TI Method for identifying carbon dioxide gas layer for oil and gas exploration, involves constructing intersection map of density and thermal neutron cross-section based on density and thermal neutron cross-section measurement values.
AU ZHANG J
   LI Y
   ZHANG P
   LI Q
   GUAN L
   ZHANG C
   WANG X
   ZHANG W
AE CHINA OILFIELD SERVICES LTD (CNOO-C)
GA 202217333V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (S110) the density measurement value using the density logging process. The thermal neutron cross-section measurement value is obtained (S120). The intersection map of the density and the thermal neutron cross-section is constructed (S130), according to the density measurement value and the thermal neutron cross-section measurement value. The carbon dioxide gas layer is identified (S140), according to the density and the thermal neutron cross-section intersection map.
   USE - Identification method of carbon dioxide gas layer used for oil and gas exploration.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the thermal neutron cross-section and the density of hydrocarbon gas such as carbon dioxide and methane are large different, the carbon dioxide gas layer is accurately identified, according to the density and thermal neutron cross-sections intersection. Thus, the identification precision of the gas layer is improved. The identification efficiency is increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a carbon dioxide gas layer identification device; (2) a computing device for identifying carbon dioxide gas layer; and (3) a computer storage medium storing program for identifying carbon dioxide gas layer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the carbon dioxide gas layer identification method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Step for obtaining density measuring value obtained by density logging method (S110)
   Step for obtaining thermal neutron cross-section measuring value (S120)
   Step for constructing intersection map of density and thermal neutron cross-section (S130)
   Step for identifying carbon dioxide gas layer (S140)

PN IN202141059985-A
TI Adsorption performance of pore expanded metal-organic frameworks structures for carbon dioxide capture efficiency.
AU AKSHINTHALA P
   MADDULA S P
   YARASI S
   MALLADI S
   SAHA A
   KUMAR P
   GAYATRI N S
   MATTAPARTHI S
AE MLR TECHNOLOGY INST (MLRT-Non-standard)
GA 202220514E
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorption performance of pore expanded metal-organic frameworks (MOF) structures for carbon dioxide capture efficiency. Carbon dioxide sorption ability of nickel-MOF-74 analogs by using quantum-chemical calculations and grand canonical monte carlo simulations. Nickel-sphingosine 1-phosphate (SPP), nickel-benzyl butyl phthalate (BPP) and nickel-tetraphenylporphyrin (TPP) systems have exhibited excellent carbon dioxide uptake capacities, 8.2, 11.5 and 11.8 mmol/g respectively at 1 bar pressure along with the remarkable selectivity for carbon dioxide over methane and nitrogen. The computed isosteric heat of adsorption is found to be 47, 44 and 41 kJ/mol for nickel-SPP, nickel-BPP and nickel-TPP respectively.
   USE - The method is useful for adsorbing pore expanded MOF structures for carbon dioxide capture efficiency.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes nickel-SPP, nickel-BPP and nickel-TPP systems that have exhibited excellent carbon dioxide uptake capacities, 8.2, 11.5 and 11.8 mmol/g respectively at 1 bar pressure along with the remarkable selectivity for carbon dioxide over methane and nitrogen, and computed isosteric heat of adsorption of 47, 44 and 41 kJ/mol, respectively; utilizes sixth coordination of nickel with water molecule that enhanced the carbon dioxide sorption capacity by 3 mmol/g due to cooperative electrostatic interactions; provides pore expanded MOFs with CUS sites that have exhibited over 80% selectivity for carbon dioxide in presence of methane and nitrogen; provides adsorbed pore expanded MOF which reduces the energy needed for gas regeneration process; and estimates the moisture tolerance of designed MOFs and its regenerability, stability and cost-effective features while operation itself in a safe and reliable manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The framework can be substantially purged of adsorbed carbon dioxide by exposing it to a removal flow pressure at 1 bar. The carbon dioxide adsorption-desorption isotherms of hydrated MOFs are well fitted with Langmuir-II adsorption isotherm.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN IN201611036660-A; IN385534-B
TI Sequestrating carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon by micro algae involves adding modified algal growth media to fibrous matrix photobioreactor, injecting carbon dioxide with traces of hydrocarbon and transferring pure culture to photobioreactor.
AU VETRIVEL A
   LAL C R
   REGINALD E
   CHUGH P
   KASHYAP R K
   SINHA R
   ANGUSELVI V
   RAM L C
   EBHINMASTO R
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
   COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 2018347830
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydrocarbon are sequestrated by micro algae by preparing modified algal growth media from coal mine water, collecting carbon dioxide with traces of hydrocarbon from natural gas processing unit as nutrient carbon source, adding modified algal growth media to fibrous matrix photobioreactor, injecting carbon dioxide with traces of hydrocarbon, transferring pure culture of algal strain of Oscillatoria ATCC 29135 to fibrous matrix photobioreactor, and allowing to incubate at pH 6-8 and 25-32 degrees C for 25-45 days to sequestrate CO2 and hydrocarbon.
   USE - Sequestration of carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydrocarbon by micro algae.
   ADVANTAGE - Method is environment friendly, helps natural gas processing industries for reducing carbon dioxide emission in atmosphere, and obtains valuable products from algal biomass that can be used to generate revenues.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydrocarbon are sequestrated by micro algae by preparing modified algal growth media from coal mine water, collecting carbon dioxide with traces of hydrocarbon from natural gas processing unit as nutrient carbon source, adding modified algal growth media to fibrous matrix photo bioreactor followed by injecting carbon dioxide with traces of hydrocarbon, transferring pure culture of algal strain of Oscillatoria ATCC 29135 to fibrous matrix photobioreactor and allowing to incubate at pH 6-8 and 25-32 degrees C for 25-45 days to sequestrate CO2 and hydrocarbon, optionally harvesting algae from algal culture and extracting value added products namely protein rich feed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: Modified algal growth media containing coal mine water has pH 6.5-7.5, 1.5-2.7 ppm iron (Fe), 3-8 ppm potassium (K), 0.005-0.08 ppm copper (Cu), 0.07-0.08 ppm molybdenum (Mo), 0.1-0.15 ppm cobalt (Co) and 0.1-0.12 ppm nickel (Ni). Modified algal growth media further contains additional supplements chosen from carbon source, potassium nitrate sodium hydrogen phosphate, Tween 20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20), indole-3-acetic acid (IAA), vitamin B12, vitamin B1, and magnesium(II). Preferred Parameters: Efficient carbon dioxide (CO2) capture process is done at light of wavelength red (700 nm), yellow (580 nm) and sunlight. Modified algal growth media is sterilized for 15-17 minutes at 121-123 degrees C and 90-110 kPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydrocarbon captured by algae contain 90-99.21% carbon dioxide, 0.56-0.65% methane, 0.1-0.14% ethane, 0.02-0.04% propane, 0.10-0.15% nitrogen and 60-80 ppm hydrogen sulfide.

PN US2022310281-A1
TI Preparing a radionuclide metal that is a fission            product of the fissioning of uranium involves            fissioning a molten fuel salt containing uranium, and            generating an irradiated fuel salt mixture containing            molten uranium salt and fission products, and fission            products include radionuclide metal.
AU DUNCKLEY C P
   WALTER J C
   CZERWINSKI K
AE TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
GA 2022C4688N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a radionuclide metal that is a                fission product of the fissioning of uranium                involves fissioning a molten fuel salt containing                uranium, and generating an irradiated fuel salt                mixture containing molten uranium salt and fission                products. The fission products include radionuclide                metal, and contacted at least some irradiated fuel                salt mixture with supercritical carbon dioxide                containing a ligand that forms a metal complex with                the radionuclide metal and does not form a metal                complex with the uranium in the fuel salt, and                formed a combined fuel salt and supercritical                carbon dioxide mixture containing an amount of                radionuclide metal complexes. The radionuclide                metal complexes are separated from the combined                fuel salt and supercritical carbon dioxide mixture,                and decomposed the radionuclide metal complexes to                obtain the radionuclide metal.
   USE - Method for preparing a radionuclide metal that                is a fission product of the fissioning of                uranium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows the same starting material                to undergo multiple irradiations and separation                operations without extensive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   1. a method of manufacturing luthenium (177Lu)                radioisotope, which involves preparing an enriched                dry ytterbium (176Yb) oxide source material within                a pressurized containment vessel, irradiating the                176Yb oxide containing source with neutrons at                suitable neutron flux density to achieve reaction                of 176Yb to 177Yb through the reaction                176Yb(n,&#947;)177Yb from which 177Yb decay to                177Lu by the reaction, injecting the pressurized                containment vessel containing the irradiated 176Yb                oxide source with supercritical carbon dioxide                containing a ligand that selectively forms a metal                complex with the lutetium but does not form a metal                complex with the ytterbium, thereby forming a                supercritical carbon dioxide mixture containing an                amount of lutetium metal complexes, removing the                supercritical carbon dioxide mixture containing an                amount of lutetium metal complexes, and separating                at least some radionuclide lutetium complexes from                the supercritical carbon dioxide mixture to provide                a 177Lu rich separation fraction; and
   2. a method of manufacturing copper (67Cu)                radioisotope, which involves packing an enriched                zinc (70Zn) oxide source material in an appropriate                a cavity within an irradiation target box,                irradiating the 70Zn source, yielding 67Cu through                the reaction 70Zn(p,&#945;)67Cu with estimated                cross-sections of 67Cu formation of 15-27 megabyte,                contacting the 70Zn oxide target with supercritical                carbon dioxide containing a ligand that selectively                forms a metal complex with the copper but does not                form a metal complex with the zinc oxide, thereby                forming a supercritical carbon dioxide mixture                containing an amount of radionuclide metal                complexes, separating radionuclide metal complexes                from the combined radionuclide and supercritical                carbon dioxide mixture, and separating the                radionuclide from the combined radionuclide and                supercritical carbon dioxide mixture, and purifying                the 67Cu radionuclide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The radionuclide metal                  is actinium (227Ac), bismuth (213Bi), cesium                  (131Cs), 133Cs, carbon (11C), chromium (51Cr),                  cobalt (57Co), 60Co, copper (64Cu), 67Cu,                  dysprosium (165Dy), erbium (169Er), fluorine                  (18F), gallium (67Ga), 68Ga, germanium (68Ge),                  gold (198Au), holmium (166Ho), indium (111In),                  iridium (192Ir), iron (59Fe), or lead (212Pb).                  The molten fuel salt contains a chloride salt of                  uranium.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC K08 (Nucleonics; X-ray techniques - including conversion of chemical elements, nuclear explosives and plasma techniques other than electron beam or plasma welding methods and apparatus and X-ray films (G01T, G21G, H, J, K, H05G, H).)
MC K08-B; K09-E

PN CN110806435-A
TI Method for evaluation of AC impedance spectroscopy            for influence of carbon dioxide-water-rock interaction            on pore-throat structure of compact rocks, involves            drilling core sample, preparing solution followed by            re-measuring spectrum.
AU YAN W
   LI W
   DENG J
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 202016381J
AB    NOVELTY - An AC impedance spectroscopy evaluation method                involves drilling a core sample from a                full-diameter core and dry it after oil absorption,                and preparing a solution to simulate formation                water based on the water sample analysis of the                produced water in the oil field. The core sample is                put into the prepared formation aqueous solution                and saturate under vacuum. The two ends of the core                sample saturated are attached to electrode pads and                connected to a lead, and lead is connected to                electrochemical workstation. The disturbance                voltage is set to test the AC impedance spectrum of                the rock sample. The core sample measured is put                into the core holder, followed by connecting carbon                dioxide-formation water displacing device,                experiment temperature and confining pressure,                injecting carbon dioxide into the rock core and                stratum water, re-measuring the AC impedance                spectrum, drawing the Nyquist diagram of the carbon                dioxide water-rock interaction, calculating the                size of the disconnected pores.
   USE - Method for evaluation of AC impedance                spectroscopy for the influence of carbon                dioxide-water-rock interaction on the pore-throat                structure of compact rocks.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables evaluation of AC impedance                spectroscopy in fast and accurate manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An AC impedance spectroscopy evaluation method                involves drilling a core sample from a                full-diameter core and dry it after oil absorption,                and preparing a solution to simulate formation                water based on the water sample analysis of the                produced water in the oil field. The core sample is                put into the prepared formation aqueous solution                and saturate under vacuum. The two ends of the core                sample saturated are closely attached to the                electrode pads and connected to a lead, and the                lead is connected to an electrochemical                workstation. The open potential of the system is                tested. The disturbance voltage is set to test the                AC impedance spectrum of the rock sample. The core                sample measured is put into the core holder,                followed by connecting carbon dioxide-formation                water displacing device, experiment temperature and                confining pressure, injecting carbon dioxide into                the rock core and stratum water, re-measuring the                AC impedance spectrum of the core sample, drawing                the Nyquist diagram of the carbon dioxide                water-rock interaction before and after, fitting                the equivalent circuit, calculating the size of the                disconnected pores and connected pore resistance,                and comparing the changes of the disconnected pores                and connected pore resistance before and after the                carbon dioxide -water-rock interaction if the core                pore resistance becomes larger, indicates that                carbon dioxide-water-rock interaction reduces core                permeability and blocks internal pore throats, core                pore resistance decreases, indicating that carbon                dioxide-water-rock interactions increase core                permeability and increase pore throat connectivity                within cores.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); S01 (Electrical Instruments); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC D04-A01; S01-D05B; S03-E03C; S03-E13F; S03-E14E7

PN CN108938825-A
TI Extracting tea polyphenols from black tea leaves            by crushing black tea leaves, homogenizing, treating,            adding polysorbate to carbon dioxide fluid, extracting,            separating polyphenol, purifying, concentrating and            spray-drying product.
AU WU S
AE GUANGXI CLOONEY TEA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A0738E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting tea polyphenols from                black tea leaves involves (a) drying and crushing                black tea leaves, adding distilled water,                homogenizing, heating, adding                1-allyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide, stirring and                cooling the slurry, (b) ultrasonically treating                slurry at 25-30 KHz for 5-8 minutes, (c) extracting                treated slurry using supercritical carbon dioxide,                adding sodium lauryl sulfate, ethyl acetate and                polysorbate to carbon dioxide fluid, extracting to                obtain crude tea polyphenol, separating crude tea                polyphenol from slurry and extracting remaining                slurry is again extracted by supercritical carbon                dioxide, acetone and sodium lauryl sulfate to                carbon dioxide fluid, extracting, separating crude                tea polyphenol, mixing both the polyphenols, adding                hydrochloric acid to adjust pH and centrifuging or                filtering material and (d) placing tea soup on LX-8                macroporous adsorption resin column, eluting                polyphenol, concentrating under reduced pressure                and spray-drying product.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting tea                polyphenols from black tea leaves.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the extraction rate and                purity of tea polyphenols.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting tea polyphenols from                black tea leaves involves (a) drying and crushing                black tea leaves, adding distilled water, uniformly                homogenizing in a vacuum high shear homogenizer,                heating, controlling the temperature of slurry,                adding 1-allyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide,                stirring at 200-300 revolutions per minute and                cooling the slurry, (b) ultrasonically treating                slurry at 25-30 KHz for 5-8 minutes, (c) extracting                treated slurry using supercritical carbon dioxide,                adding sodium lauryl sulfate, ethyl acetate and                polysorbate to carbon dioxide fluid, where the                total amount of sodium lauryl sulfate, ethyl                acetate and polysorbate is 15-24% and the weight                ratio of sodium lauryl sulfate, ethyl acetate and                polysorbate is (1.2-2.5):(0.6-2):(1.5-3),                extracting at 52-58 degrees C and 26-30 MPa at the                carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-25 l/hour for                1.5-3.5 hours to obtain crude tea polyphenol,                separating crude tea polyphenol from slurry at                40-50 degrees C and 3-6 MPa and extracting                remaining slurry is again extracted by                supercritical carbon dioxide, acetone and sodium                lauryl sulfate to carbon dioxide fluid, extracting                at extraction pressure to 50-56 degrees C and 22-27                MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-25 L/hours                for 1-2 hours, separating crude tea polyphenol at                40-50 degrees C and 3-6 MPa, mixing both the                polyphenols, adding 6-16% hydrochloric acid                (18-28%) to adjust the pH to 3-5.2 and centrifuging                or filtering the material and (d) placing tea soup                on LX-8 macroporous adsorption resin column,                eluting polyphenol, concentrating under reduced                pressure and spray-drying product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  drying and crushing black tea leaves, passing                  through 60-80 mesh sieve, adding distilled water                  in a ratio of 1:10-15, uniformly homogenizing in                  a vacuum high shear homogenizer, heating in a                  water bath at 58-70 degrees C, controlling the                  temperature of slurry, adding                  1-allyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide in a ratio of                  1:0.2-0.4, stirring at 200-300 revolutions per                  minute for 22-30 minutes and cooling for 0.7-1.2                  hours. In step (d), the tea soup is placed on                  LX-8 macroporous adsorption resin column, and the                  LX-8 macroporous adsorption resin column has an                  aspect ratio of 1:5-15, the sample loading is                  37-56 times of column volume, and the loading                  flow rate is 0.5-2 BV/hour, the impurity is                  eluted using 1.2-7.5% ethanol aqueous solution,                  and the amount is 20-26 times column volume, and                  the flow rate is 1-4 BV/hour, further eluted with                  an 56-73% ethanol aqueous solution in an amount                  of 12-18 times column volume and flow rate of 1-4                  BV/hour, eluate of aqueous ethanol solution                  having a volume fraction of 56-73% is collected,                  concentrated under reduced pressure, and product                  is spray-dried. Preferred Composition: The total                  amount of acetone and sodium lauryl sulfate is                  12-18% of carbon dioxide fluid. The weight ratio                  of acetone and sodium lauryl sulfate is                  (1-2):(0.4-0.9).
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI Direct contact cooler for ammonia-based carbon dioxide abatement system, comprises flue gas stream path, first treatment section, and ammonia-rich wash water inlet.
AU STALLMANN O
   BIRGER S
AE NUOVO PIGNONE TECNOLOGIE SRL (NUOV-C)
GA 202239723T
AB    NOVELTY - The cooler has a flue gas stream path (19) extending from a flue gas inlet (3.4) to a flue gas outlet (3.5). A first treatment section (3.2) and a second treatment section (3.3) are arranged along the flue gas stream path, where the first treatment section is arranged upstream of the second treatment section with respect to a flue gas stream along the flue gas stream path. An ammonia-rich wash water inlet (3.6) is placed between the first treatment section and the second treatment section. An ammonia-lean wash water outlet (3.8) is placed upstream of the first treatment section.
   USE - Direct contact cooler for ammonia-based carbon dioxide abatement system (claimed) for removing carbon dioxide from flue gas used for power generation or production of materials such as cement, steel and glass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces carbon dioxide emissions from flue gas or other sources of carbon dioxide. The ammonia-based carbon dioxide abatement system is capable of removing carbon dioxide from the flue-gas stream with high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an ammonia-based carbon dioxide abatement system; and
   (2) a method for removing carbon dioxide from flue gas using ammonia-based carbon dioxide abatement process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the direct contact cooler of the system.
   First treatment section (3.2)
   Second treatment section (3.3)
   Flue gas inlet (3.4)
   Flue gas outlet (3.5)
   Ammonia-rich wash water inlet (3.6)
   Ammonia-lean wash water outlet (3.8)
   Flue gas stream path (19)

PT P
PN IN201921013332-A
TI Capturing carbon dioxide using hybrid clay by adding hybrid clay to calcium chloride solution for preparing cation exchange hybrid clay, filtering, washing, drying, heating, preparing power form, passing carbon dioxide gas and separating.
AU SHUKLA A D
   SHAH M V
   SHAH K B
AE SHUKLA A D (SHUK-Individual)
   SHAH M V (SHAH-Individual)
   SHAH K B (SHAH-Individual)
GA 2020A1607U
AB    NOVELTY - Method of capturing carbon dioxide using hybrid clay involves (i) adding the hybrid clay to a calcium chloride solution, (ii) allowing to react overnight the hybrid clay with the calcium chloride solution for preparing a cation exchange hybrid clay, (iii) filtering the cation exchange hybrid clay, (iv) washing the cation exchange hybrid clay with a water to remove any adhering calcium, (v) drying the cation exchange hybrid clay at atmospheric temperature for 2 hours, (vi) heating the cation exchange hybrid clay obtained in step (v) at 120 degrees C for 6 hours, (vii) preparing a power form of the cation exchange hybrid clay, (viii) filling the powder of the cation exchange hybrid clay in to a cylindrical shape adsorption apparatus, (ix) passing a carbon dioxide gas at a specific pressure, at specific rate for the duration thru the cylindrical shape adsorption apparatus and (x) separating the hybrid clay containing carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for capturing carbon dioxide using hybrid clay.
   ADVANTAGE - The method overcomes does not utilize modified clay or modified materials for carbon dioxide capturing, and expensive materials; does not adopt lengthy processing steps for preparing the materials; does not utilize complex system or apparatus for carbon dioxide capturing; ensures high capturing capacity; and does not utilize toxic materials. The method adopts hybrid clay with improved capacity and efficiency of capturing carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the hybrid clay modified with calcium chloride used for capturing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves adopting specific pressure of 0.5 kg/cm2, specific rate of 7 l/minutes and duration of 5 hours. Preferred Component: The hybrid clay is bentonite clay (montmorillonite mineral). The cation exchange hybrid clay is calcium exchange hybrid clay. Preferred Composition: The calcium chloride solution has various concentration, such as 1M, 2M, 3M and 4M. Preferred Property: The hybrid clay has 80-120 meq/100 gm cation exchange capacity.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E11-Q02B; E11-W; E31-N05C; E31-P02D; E34-D02; J01-E03; J01-F; J01-H01

PN WO2020204346-A1; KR2020117107-A; KR2237029-B1; EP3950116-A1; US2022072501-A1
TI Carbon dioxide photoconversion reactor used for converting carbon dioxide to recyclable energy, has liquid discharge unit through which generated liquefied fluid is discharged, and elongated structure is arranged in predetermined direction.
AU KANG Y
   JUNG H
   KIM M
   KANG Y T
   JUNGHANSOL
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 202098061V
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide photoconversion reactor (10) has main portion (100) provided with sealed inner space, and photocatalytic material provided in inner space to generate reaction gas and liquefied fluid from carbon dioxide using light conversion reaction. A gas injection unit (200) provides passage through which carbon dioxide moves. A steam injection unit (300) provides passage through which water vapor moves. A gas discharge unit (400) through which the generated reaction gas is discharged. A liquid discharge unit (500) through which generated liquefied fluid is discharged. At least one elongated structure (600) arranged across inner space in predetermined direction. The main portion has hexahedral shape by separating transparent plates from each other. The transparent plate to which light is incident from light source among transparent plates. The main portion has through plate (700) arranged at position spaced apart from bottom surface by predetermined distance to pass liquefied fluid.
   USE - The reactor is used for converting carbon dioxide to recyclable energy using carbon dioxide, light and water vapor.
   ADVANTAGE - The reactor changes the flow of carbon dioxide and water vapor to help diffusion in closed internal space, increases the light conversion efficiency by increasing contact area between carbon dioxide and water vapor and coated photocatalytic material, and prevents the reflection and refraction of light incident from light source.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of the reactor.
   Carbon dioxide photoconversion reactor (10)
   Main portion (100)
   Gas injection unit (200)
   Steam injection unit (300)
   Gas discharge unit (400)
   Liquid discharge unit (500)
   Elongated structure (600)
   Through plate (700)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalytic material comprises material chosen from titanium dioxide, zinc oxide, zirconium oxide, cadmium sulfide, vanadium oxide, tungsten trioxide and/or strontium titanate.

TI Carbonizing reactor useful for separating carbon comprises carbon dioxide inlet pipe, a stirring device including stirring shaft and a stirring paddle and a decomposition reactor subject.
AU ZHAO L
   CHEN A
   SUN Y
   WANG X
   LIU H
   XIAO Y
   LONG Y
AE CHN ENERGY INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (SHGR-C)
   NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
GA 201990283N
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonizing reactor comprises carbon dioxide inlet pipe, a stirring device and a decomposition reactor subject. The stirring device comprises a stirring shaft and a stirring paddle. The carbon dioxide inlet pipe and the reactor main body for providing carbon dioxide source is connected to each other. The stirring shaft passes through the carbon dioxide inlet pipe extends into the reactor main body. The stirring paddle drives the stirring through the stirring shaft to obtain carbon reaction in the carbon partial reactor react under stirring conditions and a blade is disposed on the stirring shaft for scraping the cemented adherent solids generated in the discharge section of the carbon dioxide intake pipe during the carbon reaction.
   USE - The carbonizing reactor is useful for separating carbon (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonizing reactor has good structure and design, easy implement, adjustable frequency, good clearing effect, stable carbon reaction, less impurity and high quality and no hysteresis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) separating carbon using carbonizing carbon reactor comprising, transporting carbon dioxide source through the carbon dioxide intake conduit to the carbon reactor, and tperforming carbon dioxide reaction in contact with the sodium silicate source, turning stirring device on to carry out the carbon reaction under stirring conditions, driving the scraper on the stirring shaft is driven by the stirring shaft, where blade scrapes off the cemented adherent solids produced in the discharge section of the carbon dioxide intake duct; and
   (2) treating fly ash comprising (a) extracting sodium silicate from fly ash and (b) using sodium silicate source and performing the carbon reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the carbonizing reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The projection area of the blade disposed at the same axial height of the agitating shaft at a horizontal plane perpendicular to the agitating shaft is less than 50% of the cross-sectional area of the carbon dioxide intake duct of the same axial height, preferably 10-20%. The discharge section is a distance section of the discharge port of the carbon dioxide intake pipe is 10-30% of the total length of the carbon dioxide intake pipe. The concentration of the carbon dioxide gas in the carbon dioxide source is 30% by volume or more, concentration of sodium silicate in the sodium silicate source is 8-15 wt.% and conditions for the carbon reaction include pH 7-12.

TI Low pressure coal carbon dioxide partial inerting protection method, involves injecting carbon dioxide into inner side area of bunker, and turning off nozzles on branch spraying device by controller after completing injection process.
AU YIN X
   QIAO Z
   ZHAO G
   YAO H
   YAN H
   CHU G
   ZHAO Y
   SONG Z
   WEN G

   ZHANG J
AE DATANG ENVIRONMENT IND GROUP CO LTD (CDAT-C)
   JIANGSU NANJING THERMOELECTRIC ENG DESIG (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201855116G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves opening a partition valve corresponding to an inerting branch by a controller (4). A signal is detected by the controller according to a temperature detector or a carbon dioxide concentration detector. Amount of carbon dioxide injected into an interior of a bunker is calculated (5). Pressure and flow rate of the pressure flow adjusting device and a current limiting device are adjusted. Multiple nozzles of an inerting branch spraying device are turned on by the controller. The carbon dioxide is injected into an inner side area of the bunker to achieve partial inerting protection of the bunker. The nozzles on the inerting branch spraying device are turned off (6) by the controller after completing injection process.
   USE - Low pressure coal carbon dioxide partial inerting protection method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing low-pressure carbon dioxide as an operating medium to locally protect a flammable point in inner side of the bunker to achieve optimal effect and save low-pressure carbon dioxide storage and utilization.

   Step for calculating amount of carbon dioxide injected into interior of bunker (5)

GA 202041507A
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst composition for directly producing                low-carbon olefins by hydrogenation of carbon                dioxide, is claimed. The catalyst uses                dual-function catalyst composition of indium(III)                oxide-chromium(III) oxide and molecular                sieve.
   USE - The catalyst composition is useful for                directly producing low-carbon olefins by                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing low-carbon olefins by hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide comprising contacting and reacting                carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas mixture with                catalyst composition to obtain low-carbon                hydrocarbon-containing product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The indium(III)                  oxide-chromium(III) oxide and molecular sieve                  exist in a form independent of each other, e.g. a                  separate package or a mechanical mixture of each                  other. The weight percentage of indium(III)                  oxide-chromium(III) oxide, the weight percentage                  of indium(III) oxide is 20-70 wt.%, preferably                  40-50 wt.%, the weight percentage of                  chromium(III) oxide is 70-20 wt.%, preferably                  30-50 wt.%, and the weight percentage of the                  auxiliary component is 0-10 wt.%, preferably 0-2                  wt.% alkali metal/alkaline earth metal oxide                  component and 8-10 wt.% other oxide components.                  Preferred Components: The alkali metal/alkaline                  earth metal is lithium, sodium, potassium,                  rubidium, cesium, beryllium, magnesium, calcium,                  and/or strontium, preferably an alkali metal,                  preferably potassium, sodium, and/or magnesium.                  The other oxide components are manganese oxide,                  cerium(IV) oxide, aluminum oxide, and/or                  gallium(III) oxide. The molecular screening is                  AlPO-18, AlPO-17, AlPO-34, AlPO-14, AlPO-11,                  AlPO-5, SAPO-18, SAPO-17, SAPO-11 and/or SAPO-5.                  The molecular sieve is phosphorus aluminum                  molecular sieve (i.e., AlPO molecular sieve)                  and/or silicophosphorus aluminum molecular sieve                  (i.e., SAPO molecular sieve), preferably                  phosphorus aluminum molecular sieve. The                  molecular sieve is a carbon-containing molecular                  sieve obtained by treatment. The weight ratio of                  indium(III) oxide-chromium(III) oxide to                  molecular sieve is 1:8-8:1, preferably 1:5-5:1,                  more preferably 1:3-3:1. The process conditions                  of carbon dioxide hydrogenation: reaction                  temperature 340-460 degrees C (preferably                  reaction temperature 380-420 degrees C), reaction                  pressure 0.5-7 Mpa, carbon dioxide:hydrogen                  (molar ratio) is 1:0.4-3, GHSV 1000-8000                  hours-1.

GA 202205488U
AB    NOVELTY - A copper catalyst for producing ethanol,                includes metallic copper as main portion, and                utilizes high surface energy stepped sites as                active sites, where the longitudinal depth and                number of the high surface energy stepped sites are                controllably adjusted, and the longitudinal depth                is 0.1-20 nm.
   USE - Copper catalyst for producing ethanol by                carbon dioxide electroreduction (claimed), used in                electrochemistry applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The crystal planes are reorganized into high                surface energy Cu(110), Cu(210), and Cu(310)                crystal planes, thereby showing the atomic                arrangement characteristic of high surface energy.                The copper catalyst with periodic stepped surface                has lower coupling barrier and better ethanol                reaction path, thereby realizing electrocatalytic                carbon dioxide reduction to prepare ethanol fuel                with high selectivity. The method enables                performing electro-catalysis carbon dioxide                reduction to prepare ethanol fuel, and is                economical and easy to produce in large                scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   1. preparation method of copper catalyst for                producing ethanol by carbon dioxide                electroreduction, which involves (S1) pickling                polished copper to remove surface impurities and                oxide layers, (S2) using plasma enhanced vapor                chemical deposition method to grow vertical                graphene of different sizes on the copper surface                after pickling, and controlling the copper surface                to generate different vertical depths and different                numbers of high surface energy stepped sites                according to the thermal expansion energy                difference between the two, and (S3) adding copper                growing vertical graphene to electrolyte for                electrolysis, peeling off the graphene attached to                the surface, rinsing with deionized water and                drying with nitrogen; and
   2. application of copper catalysts for carbon                dioxide electroreduction to ethanol, which involves                using copper containing controllable high surface                energy stepped active sites as catalyst and working                electrode, in electrolyte, where the copper                catalyst catalyzes the reduction of carbon dioxide                to ethanol, by (i) adding copper catalyst                containing controllable high surface energy                step-like active sites to certain concentration of                cathode electrolyte, (ii) sealing the electrolytic                cell, passing carbon dioxide to cathode                electrolytic cell to pre-saturate the electrolyte                and activate the working electrode, and (iii)                activating, under condition that carbon dioxide is                continuously fed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metallic copper                  portion is one of copper foil or foamed copper.                  Preferred Composition: In step (3), the solution                  is potassium bicarbonate solution, where the                  concentration is 0.1-1 mol/L, the electrolysis                  time is 50-600 seconds, and the electrolysis                  voltage is -2--0.5 V. In step (a), the cathode                  electrolyte is one of potassium bicarbonate                  solution or potassium solution, and the                  concentration is 0.1-1 mol/L.
