GA 2021A5047G
AB    NOVELTY - Dielectric packing material is having a hollow                cylindrical structure and multiple grooves on a                surface.
   USE - Dielectric packing material for converting                carbon dioxide to valuable materials by scattering                non-thermal plasma.
   ADVANTAGE - The dielectric packing material effectively                scatter non-thermal plasma through the edges and                thereby to improve carbon dioxide (CO2) conversion                and energy efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a catalyst composite for converting carbon                dioxide to valuable materials by scattering                non-thermal plasma, is having a structure in which                a surface of the dielectric packing material is                coated with a catalyst;
   (2) a method of producing a dielectric packing                material for converting carbon dioxide to valuable                materials by scattering non-thermal plasma, which                involves performing a reduction reaction by                sintering a dielectric packing material at a                temperature of 1,000-1,200degrees Celsius and an                oxygen partial pressure (PO2) of 10-8-10-6                atmospheric pressure;
   (3) a method of preparing a catalyst composite                for converting carbon dioxide to valuable materials                by scattering non-thermal plasma, which involves                coating a surface of the dielectric packing                material with a catalyst;
   (4) a device of preparing valuable materials                from carbon dioxide using a dielectric barrier                discharge, which includes a reactor body, an                internal electrode and an external electrode                provided in the reactor, an inlet tube for                supplying reactants into the reactor, a power                supply for supplying current to the internal                electrode and the external electrode to generate                plasma. and a ground portion of the current                connected to the external electrode, where the                reactor body is packed with the dielectric packing                material or a catalyst composite comprising said                dielectric packing material in which a surface of                the dielectric packing material is coated with a                catalyst; and
   (5) a method of preparing valuable materials                from carbon dioxide, which involves packing a                reactor body with the dielectric packing material                or a catalyst composite comprising said dielectric                packing material in which a surface of the                dielectric packing material is coated with a                catalyst, and supplying a carbon-dioxide-containing                gas mixture, then heating supplied gas mixture, and                preparing valuable materials by applying a high                voltage to an internal electrode and an external                electrode provided in the reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dielectric packing                  material is at least one selected from the group                  consisting of aluminum oxide (Al2O3), zirconium                  oxide (ZrO2), calcium oxide (CaO), magnesium                  oxide (MgO), zeolite, quartz sand and glass                  beads. The catalyst is selected from the group                  consisting of copper (Cu), platinum (Pt),                  palladium (Pd), gold (Au), indium oxide (In2O3),                  zinc oxide (ZnO), barium titanium oxide (BaTiO3)                  and/or titanium oxide (TiO2). Preferred                  Conditions: The dielectric packing material has a                  particle size of 180-300 micrometer. The coating                  is dip coating or plasma spray coating
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN JP2020130009-A
TI Biological transportation system for use in vehicles such as ships and trucks, has carbon dioxide supply unit that supplies carbon dioxide generated by electric power generation unit to organism to give anesthetic effect to organism.
AU NAKAJIMA T
   HATAE T
AE TOKYO GAS CO LTD (TOLG-C)
GA 2020836687
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a storage unit (10) for storing fuel gas, electric power generation unit for generating electric power while generating carbon dioxide from the fuel gas, and a cooling unit for cooling living things by using the generated electric power. A carbon dioxide supply unit supplies the carbon dioxide generated by the electric power generation unit to an organism to give an anesthetic effect to the organism. The electric power generation unit reforms the fuel gas to generate hydrogen. A fuel cell (20) uses the hydrogen and oxygen to generate the carbon dioxide and the electric power.
   USE - Biological transportation system for use in vehicles (claimed) such as ships and trucks for transporting living creatures such as live fish.
   ADVANTAGE - The biological transportation system can supply carbon dioxide without mounting a cylinder.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The fuel cell is a solid oxide fuel cell. The electric power generation unit uses an internal combustion engine that burns the fuel gas to generate the carbon dioxide and operates a generator to generate electric power. The fuel cell or the internal combustion engine is a power source of a moving body that transports biological organisms. The cooling unit has a heat pump having an expansion valve that decompresses and vaporizes the refrigerant, and supplies the cold heat to a stage before the expansion valve. The electric power generated by the electric power generation unit is used as power for moving the biological transportation system. An electrolyzing unit electrolyzes water with electric power generated by the electric power generating unit. The oxygen generated by the electrolyzing unit is supplied to a living organism. The hydrogen generated by the electrolyzing unit is supplied to the electric power generating unit. The control unit controls the electric power generation unit in accordance with the amount of carbon dioxide required to maintain the anesthetic action of the living thing. The living thing is a live fish.

   Storage unit (10)
   Fuel cell (20)
   Heat pump (30)
   Cooler box (40)

TI Apparatus used to remove carbon dioxide comprises syngas generation unit to convert carbon source to syngas and reactor to convert syngas to hydrocarbons, and acid gas removal unit with first and second acid gas absorbers and stripper.
AU AHMED L C

   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201845180M
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus (100) comprises: a syngas generation unit (102) for converting a carbon source to syngas and a reactor for converting syngas to hydrocarbons; and an acid gas removal unit (104) comprising a first acid gas absorber (106), a second acid gas absorber (108), and an acid gas stripper. The syngas generation unit is in upstream fluid communication with the first acid gas absorber and the reactor is in downstream fluid communication with the first acid gas absorber and in upstream fluid communication with the second acid gas absorber, where the first acid gas absorber and the second gas absorber are both in upstream fluid communication with the acid gas stripper.
   USE - The apparatus is useful for removing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus does not comprise a compressor that is downstream from the syngas generation unit and upstream from the reactor, and utilizes only one compressor is used upstream from the acid gas stripper, hence provides very lower capital cost, decrease in volumetric flow of gases to the reactor, decrease in compressor duty, and decrease in steam consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for removing carbon dioxide, comprising (a) converting a carbon source to a first product comprising syngas and carbon dioxide, (b) removing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the first product in a first acid gas absorber present in an acid gas removal unit, thus producing a second product comprising syngas, (c) converting the second product comprising syngas to a third product comprising a hydrocarbon product and carbon dioxide, (d) removing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the third product in a second acid gas absorber present in the acid gas removal unit, (e) stripping the removed carbon dioxide from the first product in an acid gas stripper 1 and (f) stripping the removed carbon dioxide from the third product in the same acid gas stripper 1.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the apparatus.
   Apparatus (100)
   Syngas generation unit (102)
   Acid gas removal unit (104)
   First acid gas absorber (106)
   Second acid gas absorber (108)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The acid gas stripper has a stripped carbon dioxide effluent that is in fluid communication with the syngas generation unit. The apparatus further comprises: a compressor that is downstream from the reactor and upstream from the second acid gas absorber; a condenser that is downstream from the compressor and upstream from the second acid gas absorber; a compressor that is downstream from the acid gas stripper; and a cryogenic separation unit that is downstream from the second acid gas absorber. The first acid gas absorber is a first carbon dioxide absorber. The second acid gas absorber is a second carbon dioxide absorber. The reactor comprises a cobalt/manganese catalyst and/or cobalt/molybdenum catalyst. The apparatus comprises a single acid gas removal unit. Preferred Method: The step (c) further comprises contacting the second product comprising syngas with a cobalt/manganese catalyst, thus converting the second product comprising syngas to the third product comprising hydrocarbon product and carbon dioxide. The method further comprises: recycling the stripped carbon dioxide back to the syngas generation unit; compressing the stripped carbon dioxide before recycling the stripped carbon dioxide back to the syngas generation unit; compressing the third product in a compressor prior to removing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the third product; removing condensate from the third product after compressing the third product; and after step (d), purifying the third product in a cryogenic separation process. The step (e) comprises stripping the removed carbon dioxide in the first product by separating the removed carbon dioxide from a solvent and further comprises the step of recycling the solvent to the first acid gas absorber or the second acid gas absorber, and/or the step (f) comprises stripping the removed carbon dioxide in the third product by separating the removed carbon dioxide from a solvent and further comprises the step of recycling the solvent to the first acid gas absorber or the second acid gas absorber. At least 80% of the carbon dioxide in the first product is removed in the first acid gas absorber and/or at least 80% of the carbon dioxide in the third product is removed in the second acid gas absorber.

PN EP3919728-A1; EP3919728-A4; US2021381391-A1; JP2021191951-A; US11499445-B2
TI Carbon dioxide turbine power generation system,            has carbon dioxide medium shutoff valve that closes to            shut off supply of medium from regenerative heat            exchanger to combustor when load rejection is to be            performed.
AU ONO T
   YAMAMOTO T
   NISHIMURA K
   OKUYAMA T
   TASHIMA T
   IWAI S
   NISHIMURA H
   TAJIMA T
AE TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
GA 2021D9762J
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide (CO2) turbine                (30) which is driven by a supercritical medium                supplied from a combustor (81). A power generator                (40) is caused to generate power by the driving of                the CO2 turbine. A CO2 medium shutoff valve is                installed in a medium flow path between a                regenerative heat exchanger (82) and the combustor,                in which the valve closes to shut off the supply of                the medium from the regenerative exchanger to the                combiner when load rejection is to be performed. A                moisture separator (85) separates water from the                medium discharged from a cooler (83).
   USE - Carbon dioxide turbine power generation system                for generating power with supercritical carbon                dioxide working medium introduced to single carbon                dioxide turbine from combustor. Can also be used in                electric power system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can be easily prevented from                reaching an over-rotation speed condition in which                a rotary shaft of the carbon dioxide turbine                rotates at an overspeed condition exceeding a rated                rotation speed, so that damage to the turbine rotor                and rotor blades can be prevented.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the block diagram of the                overall configuration of a carbon dioxide turbine                power generation system.
   Carbon dioxide turbine (30)
   Power generator (40)
   Combustor (81)
   Regenerative heat exchanger (82)
   Cooler (83)
   Moisture separator (85)

PT P
PN WO2021187352-A1; JP2022508309-X
TI Polyether compound used in ionic composition for            forming molded product such as component of equipment            for e.g. carbon dioxide separation and carbon dioxide            storage, comprises monovalent group-containing            repeating unit and amine-containing repeating unit as            at least portion of repeating unit.
AU HAYANO S
AE ZEON CORP (JAPG-C)
GA 2021B5922U
AB    NOVELTY - A polyether compound comprises a monovalent                group-containing repeating unit (I) and an                amine-containing repeating unit (II) as at least                portion of the repeating unit (I).
   USE - Polyether compound used in ionic composition                for forming molded product (all claimed) such as                component of equipment for carbon dioxide                separation, carbon dioxide storage, and carbon                dioxide transport and absorption of carbon dioxide                from natural gas, biogas and carbon                dioxide/nitrogen containing gas and carbon                dioxide/methyl containing gas. Can also be used in                electrolyte composition for electrochemical devices                such as secondary batteries, fuel cells,                dye-sensitized solar cells, and actuators.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyether compound has excellent carbon                dioxide absorption property. The ionic composition                has low temperature characteristic.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polyether compound comprises a monovalent                group-containing repeating unit of formula (I) and                an amine-containing repeating unit of formula (II)                as at least portion of the repeating unit                (I).
   A = monovalent group;
   R1 = 3C or more alkyl;
   R2-R4 = H or 1-8C alkyl, in which R2 and R3                optionally bonded to each other;and
   X = anion containing nitrogen, carbon or                oxygen.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for ionic                composition, which comprises the polyether compound                and an ionic liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic composition                  comprises 50-3000 parts mass ionic liquid with                  respect to 100 parts mass of the polyether                  compound.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyether compound                  comprises 50-100 mol% repeating unit (II) with                  respect to the entire repeating unit (I).                  Preferred Property: The number average molecular                  weight (Mn) of the polyether compound is                  500-100000.
   EXAMPLE - 3.22 g Tetranormal butylammonium bromide and                50 ml toluene were added to glass reactor and                cooled to 0 degrees C. 1.256 g Triethylaluminum                dissolved in 10 ml normal hexane was added and                reacted for 15 minutes to obtain catalyst                composition. 10.0 g Epichlorohydrin was added to                the obtained catalyst composition, and                polymerization reaction was carried out at 0                degrees C. After the start of the polymerization                reaction, the viscosity of the solution gradually                increased. After the reaction for 12 hours, a small                amount of water was poured into the polymerization                reaction solution to stop the reaction. The                obtained polymerization reaction solution was                washed with 0.1 N aqueous hydrochloric acid                solution to decalcify the catalyst residue, washed                with ion-exchanged water, and then the organic                phase was dried under reduced pressure at 50                degrees C for 12 hours. The yield of the colorless                and transparent oily substance thus obtained was                9.9 g. The number average molecular weight (Mn) of                the obtained substance by gel permeation                chromatography was 1050, and the molecular weight                distribution was 1.35. The obtained oily substance                was polyepichlorohydrin and had bromomethyl group                at the polymerization initiation terminal and                hydroxyl group at the polymerization termination                terminal. 5.0 g Polyepichlorohydrin and 13.4 g                1-normal butylimidazole, and 10.0 g acetonitrile                were added to glass reactor with stirrer replaced                with argon and heated to 80 degrees C. After                reacting at 80 degrees C for 48 hours, the reaction                was stopped by cooling to room temperature. The                obtained reaction product was washed with an equal                mass mixed solution of toluene/methanol/water, the                organic phase containing 1-normal butylimidazole                and toluene was removed, and the aqueous phase was                dried under reduced pressure at 50 degrees C for 12                hours to obtain 11.7 g 1-normal butylimidazolium                structure-containing polyether compound as light                red solid. The molded article was prepared by using                the ionic composition containing the obtained                component and had excellent carbon dioxide                absorption property.
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A85 (Electrical applications.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)
MC A10-E21; A12-W11; L03-E04; L03-E05B1
IP C08G-065/325; C08K-005/3445; C08L-071/03

TI Gas turbine plant has carbon dioxide recovery device that includes absorption tower, and heat exchanger that is formed from material with greater corrosion resistance than material from which waste heat recovery boiler is formed.
AU ATSUSHI T
   TETSUYA T

GA 202110827X
AB    NOVELTY - The gas turbine plant (100) has a waste heat recovery boiler (2) that generates steam from the heat of exhaust gas and guides the exhaust gas to the exhaust line. A carbon dioxide recovery device (3) recovers carbon dioxide included in the exhaust gas. A heat exchanger (4) is arranged between the waste heat recovery boiler and the carbon dioxide recovery device and that cools the exhaust gas to a predetermined temperature. A circulation line (L2) branches from a position between the carbon dioxide recovery device and the heat exchanger and is connected to an entrance of the gas turbine. The carbon dioxide recovery device has an absorption tower that absorbs carbon dioxide included in the exhaust gas by bringing the exhaust gas at the predetermined temperature and an absorbing liquid into contact with one another. The heat exchanger is formed from a material with greater corrosion resistance than a material from which the waste heat recovery boiler is formed.

PN US11384280-B1
TI Composition of matter used for enhanced oil recovery applications, comprises dispersion of capsules in critical or supercritical carbon dioxide, containing an aqueous solution encapsulated by zeolite-templated carbon particles.
AU WANG Y
   AL-QASIM A S
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2022893425
AB    NOVELTY - A composition of matter comprises a dispersion of capsules in critical or supercritical carbon dioxide, containing an aqueous solution encapsulated by zeolite-templated carbon particles.
   USE - The composition of matter comprising dispersion of capsules in critical or supercritical carbon dioxide, is used in enhanced oil recovery applications.
   ADVANTAGE - In the composition of matter comprising the dispersion of capsules, the supercritical carbon dioxide miscible with hydrocarbons, contacts hydrocarbon fluid in reservoir, to enable displacing of fluid from the rock surfaces, so that the fluid is pushed toward the production well. Carbon dioxide getting dissolved in the hydrocarbon fluid, reduces the viscosity of the hydrocarbon fluid, making it to swell. Hence, the ability to recover hydrocarbons is enhanced and the production efficency is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. preparation of aqueous capsules dispersion solution. The method involves:
   1) providing a medium of critical or supercritical carbon dioxide; 2) passing the aqueous solution into the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide medium; and 3) passing a zeolite-templated carbon particle into the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide medium.
   2. method for treating a hydrocarbon-bearing formation, which involves passing a dispersion of the aqueous solution capsules comprising an aqueous solution encapsulated by zeolite-templated carbon particles, in a medium of critical or supercritical carbon dioxide, into the hydrocarbon-bearing formation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The aqueous solution diameter of the capsules contained in the dispersion composition, is 10nm to 100 mum. The hydrophobic zeolite-templated carbon particles have particle size 10-200 nm.The particle size of the zeolite-templated carbon particles, is 10-200 nm. The bulk density of the dispersion, is 0.9 -1.1 g/ml.Preferred Composition: The dispersion comprises 60-70 vol.% aqueous solution and up to 5.0 wt.% zeolite-templated carbon particles. The bulk density of the dispersion, is 0.9-1.1 g/ml.Preferred Process: The preparation of aqueous solution capsules dispersion, involves passing the aqueous solution into the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide medium, through a pump provided to enable supply of fluids at a temperature and pressure greater than temperature and pressure of the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide medium, respectively.The aqueous solution and the zeolite templated carbon particle are introduced into the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide medium simultaneously, or the aqueous solution is introduced into the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide medium before adding the zeolite templated particle into the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide medium. The zeolite templated particles are preferably added to the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide medium, before adding the aqueous solution into the critical or supercritical carbon dioxide medium.

PN WO2021231591-A1; US2021354078-A1
TI Membrane process for separating carbon dioxide from flue gas, involves removing treated gas from feed side of membrane, removing permeate from permeate side, and cooling permeate.
AU BAKER R
   BAKER R W
AE MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
   MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
GA 2021D0711S
AB    NOVELTY - Membrane process (201) involves passing a fluid stream comprising the flue gas (204) across a membrane (205) permeable to carbon dioxide and water (202), removing treated gas having less carbon dioxide than the flue gas, from a feed side of the membrane, removing permeate comprising carbon dioxide and water, from a permeate side of the membrane at a sub atmospheric pressure of 0.1-0.4 bar, and cooling the permeate to remove at least some of the water from the permeate and form a smaller volume of water-depleted, permeating carbon dioxide enriched, using a vacuum pump to increase the gas pressure to at least about atmospheric pressure.
   USE - Membrane process for separating carbon dioxide from flue gas generated by coal power plant, natural gas power plant, natural gas boiler, cement plant, steel plant and oil refinery (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process is capable of separating carbon dioxide from flue gas in a simple and cost-effective manner, and enhances the carbon dioxide separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for separating carbon dioxide from flue gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a simple block diagram of the unit operations utilized in the process.
   Membrane process (201)
   Water (202)
   Treated flue gas (204)
   Membranes (205)
   Membrane permeate (207)
   Cooled permeate gas (210)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The fluid stream passed across the membrane comprises at least 70% its saturation concentration of water. The membrane process removes at least 50%, preferably 50-80% carbon dioxide of the flue gas. The water vapor content of the membrane feed gas is 70-100% water saturation concentration of the gas. Preferred Properties: The membrane has a water/carbon dioxide selectivity of greater than 2, measured at the operating conditions of the process. The membrane has a carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity of greater than 10, measured at the operating conditions of the process. The water-depleted permeate has a carbon dioxide concentration of greater than 35%.

PT P
PN CN107961757-A; CN107961757-B
TI Composite calcium-based carbon dioxide absorbent useful for hydrogen production in fossil fuel industry, cyclic carbonation/calcining separation application and capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas, comprises calcium oxide and mayenite.
AU LI Y
   CHI Z
   YAN X
   MA X
   ZHAO J
   LU C
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 2018345107
AB    NOVELTY - A composite calcium-based carbon dioxide absorbent comprises calcium oxide and mayenite. The composite calcium-based carbon dioxide absorbent has a hollow porous tubular structure.
   USE - Composite calcium-based carbon dioxide absorbent is useful for hydrogen production in fossil fuel industry, cyclic carbonation/calcining separation application and capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite calcium-based carbon dioxide absorbent has high activity and cycle stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the method for preparing the composite calcium-based carbon dioxide absorbent, which involves mixing limestone with distilled water/deionized water at room temperature, stirring into a suspension, adding the suspension to a hydroxyl group-containing solution, filtering the mixed liquid to obtain a clear liquid, drying to obtain a solid material, weighing the solid material, stirring with distilled water/deionized water at room temperature to completely dissolve the solid material to obtain an uniform solution, adding aluminum salt to the uniform solution, stirring at room temperature to completely dissolve aluminum salt, soaking dried template material in aluminum salt solution, drying the template material, pyrolyzing the dried samples under high purity nitrogen, calcining the pyrolyzed sample at 700 degrees C under an air atmosphere, taking out the calcined sample and sieving to obtain composite calcium-based carbon dioxide absorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of mayenite is less than 30 %mass. Preferred Components: The template material comprises absorbent cotton. The aluminum salt is a water-soluble aluminum salt, preferably, aluminum nitrate, aluminum acetate and aluminum trichloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydroxyl group-containing solution is carboxylic acid, preferably formic acid, acetic acid or propionic acid. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of limestone and distilled water/deionized water is 0.5-5:8. The volume ratio of the hydroxyl group-containing solution and distilled water/deionized water is 1:2-5. The mass ratio of aluminum salt to solid material is 3-20:1. The mass ratio of template material and distilled water/deionized water is 1:20-30. Preferred Conditions: The soaking step is carried out at room temperature for 30-55 hours, preferably 48 hours. The drying step is carried out at 80-100 degrees C, preferably 80 degrees C for 10-14 hours, preferably 12 hours. The pyrolysis step is carried out at 500-70 degrees C, preferably 600 degrees C for 3-6 hours, preferably 4 hours. The calcination step is carried out at 600-800 degrees C, preferably 700 degrees C for 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Method of separating carbon dioxide and hydrogen            chloride in sucralose tail gas involves subjecting tail            gas to dewatering, removing trichloroethane and            N,N-dimethylformamide, and performing rectification            separation.
AU XU S
AE SHANDONG KANGBAO BIOCHEMICAL TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A13610
AB    NOVELTY - Method of separating carbon dioxide and                hydrogen chloride in sucralose tail gas involves                (1) feeding initial mixed tail gas to the liquid                ring pump for dewatering treatment, using                concentrated sulfuric acid as medium in the liquid                ring pump, removing water, obtaining mixed tail gas                (I), feeding into demister tank, removing                concentrated sulfuric acid mist, obtaining mixed                tail gas (II), feeding into buffer tank, (2)                removing trichloroethane and N, N-dimethylformamide                in mixed tail gas by feeding into compressor (I),                pressurizing, feeding into solvent column,                obtaining mixed tail gas (III), (3) obtaining pure                carbon dioxide and hydrogen chloride mixture by                transporting mixed tail gas (III) from top of                solvent column to the shell side of heat exchanger                (I), liquefying carbon dioxide and hydrogen                chloride in mixed tail gas (III), feeding into                solvent tower reflux tank, (4) carrying out                hydrogen chloride rectification separation, and (5)                carrying out carbon dioxide rectification                separation.
   USE - Method of separating carbon dioxide and                hydrogen chloride in sucralose tail gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of separating carbon dioxide and                hydrogen chloride in sucralose tail gas involves                (1) feeding initial mixed tail gas into the liquid                ring pump for dewatering treatment, using                concentrated sulfuric acid as medium in the liquid                ring pump, removing water and pressurizing the                initial mixed tail gas by liquid ring pump,                obtaining mixed tail gas (I), feeding mixed tail                gas (I) into the demister tank, removing                concentrated sulfuric acid mist, obtaining mixed                tail gas (II), transferring mixed tail gas (II)                into the buffer tank, (2) removing trichloroethane                and N,N-dimethylformamide in tail gas by feeding                mixed tail gas (II) to compressor (I) from buffer                tank, pressurizing, feeding mixed tail gas (II)                into the solvent column containing liquefied                trichloroethane and N, N-dimethylformamide in mixed                tail gas (II) at the bottom of the solvent column,                obtaining mixed tail gas (III), collecting mixed                tail gas (III) from the top of the solvent column,                (3) obtaining a pure carbon dioxide and hydrogen                chloride mixture by feeding mixed tail gas (III)                from the top of the solvent column into the shell                side of heat exchanger (I), feeding mixed tail gas                (III) into compressor (II), pressurizing, feeding                mixed tail gas (III) into tube side of heat                exchanger (I), cooling and feeding mixed tail gas                (III) into heat exchanger (II), cooling, liquefying                both carbon dioxide and hydrogen chloride in mixed                tail gas (III), feeding liquid carbon dioxide and                hydrogen chloride into solvent tower reflux tank,                collecting output from the bottom of the solvent                tower reflux tank comprising the solvent tower                reflux tank output (A) and the solvent tower reflux                tank output (B), (4) carrying out hydrogen chloride                rectification separation by feeding solvent column                reflux tank discharge (B) into the rectification                tower for rectification separation at 3.0 MPa,                providing bottom of the distillation column with a                reboiler, transporting the hydrogen chloride liquid                into cooler, filling with hydrogen chloride after                cooling down, adjusting temperature at the bottom                of the distillation column to 8-15 degrees C and                temperature at the top of the distillation column                to -6 degrees C and temperature of the material in                the reboiler to 10-20 degrees C, (5) carrying out                carbon dioxide rectification separation by                extracting mixed gas containing carbon dioxide gas                and a small amount of air from the top of the                rectification tower, conveying mixed gas into the                shell side of the heat exchanger (III), feeding                mixed gas into compressor (III), pressurizing,                conveying mixed gas to the tube side of heat                exchanger (III), cooling, feeding the mixed gas                into the cooler (I), passing liquefied carbon                dioxide to the rectification tower reflux tank,                collecting output from the bottom of the                rectification tower reflux tank comprising the                rectification tower reflux tank output (A) and the                rectification tower reflux tank output (B), feeding                distillation column reflux tank discharge (B) into                cooler (II), filling with carbon dioxide after                cooling down, adjusting compressor (III) pressure                of the mixed gas to 6.0 MPa, controlling the                temperature of the mixed gas at lower than 45                degrees C, reducing cooler (I) temperature of the                mixed gas to 0-10 degrees C and liquefying carbon                dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                device for separating carbon dioxide and hydrogen                chloride in sucralose tail gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The initial mixed                  tail gas component in step (1) comprises 51.19                  wt.% carbon dioxide, 43.61 wt.% hydrogen                  chloride, 1.85 wt.% trichloroethane, 1.21 wt.%                  N,N-dimethylformamide, 2.08 wt.% air, and 0.06                  wt.% water.

PT P
PN US9901876-B1
TI Removing and reclaiming various compounds of e.g. water from cleaning liquid, comprises directing cleaning liquid solution from wet scrubber to first liquid-solid centrifuge separator (L-SCS) and directing cleaning fluid to second L-SCS.
AU KUKU L O
   MATHEW M
   WOUDENBERG M P
AE MILLENIUM SYNTHFUELS CORP (MILL-Non-standard)
   KUKU L O (KUKU-Individual)
   MATHEW M (MATH-Individual)
   WOUDENBERG M P (WOUD-Individual)
GA 2018152681
AB    NOVELTY - Removing and reclaiming various compounds of calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide, water, mercuric salts, calcium oxide, and calcium sulfate from cleaning liquid formed during clean-up of exhaust gas in exhaust gas flow-path from fossil fuel fired power plant, comprises: e.g. (a) directing cleaning liquid solution from wet scrubber to first liquid-solid centrifuge separator by e.g. (a1) selectively directing lighter liquid within liquid-solid separator in form of carbon dioxide and water, through one of heater at 100 degrees C and bubbler tank containing noble gas, to separate carbon dioxide from water.
   USE - The process is useful for removing and reclaiming various compounds of calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide, water, mercuric salts, calcium oxide, and calcium sulfate from cleaning liquid formed during clean-up of exhaust gas in exhaust gas flow-path from fossil fuel fired power plant (claimed), where the reclaimed products are used commercially and/or reused in an exhaust gas clean-up system.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is safe, simple, excellent and cost effective; and regenerates some of the products back into the process for the gas clean-up system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing and reclaiming various compounds of calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide, water, mercuric salts, calcium oxide, and calcium sulfate from a cleaning liquid formed during clean-up of exhaust gas in an exhaust gas flow-path from a fossil fuel fired power plant, comprises: (a) directing the cleaning liquid solution from the wet scrubber to a first liquid-solid centrifuge separator by (a1) selectively directing the lighter liquid within the liquid-solid separator in the form of carbon dioxide and water, through one of a heater at 100 degrees C and a bubbler tank containing a noble gas, to separate the carbon dioxide from the water, (a2) directing the carbon dioxide to a carbon dioxide compressor and on to a carbon dioxide storage tank for commercial uses, (a3) directing the separated water back to the wet scrubber for reuse, and (a4) selectively directing the heavier salts containing calcium sulfate, calcium carbonate, and mercuric salts from the liquid-solid separator, through a resolver valve to a first stirred tank; and (b) directing the cleaning fluid from the reaction chamber to a second liquid-solid centrifuge by (b1) selectively directing the lighter liquid from the second liquid-solid centrifuge separator, in the form of water and soluble carbonic acid, through one of another passage in the heater at 100 degrees C, and another compartment of the bubbler tank, to chemically act on the water and carbonic acid producing carbon dioxide and water, (b2) directing the carbon dioxide to the carbon dioxide compressor for storage in the commercial carbon dioxide storage tank, (b3) directing the separated water back to the wet scrubber for reuse, (b4) selectively directing the heavier salts in the form of calcium salts and mercuric salts from the second liquid-solid centrifuge separator through the resolver valve to the first stirred tank, (b5) selecting the one of the heavier salts from the first and second liquid-solid centrifuge separators to pass through the resolver vale, (b6) adding one of dibromomethane and water to the various heavier salts in the stirred tank to cause the calcium sulfate to separate from the calcium carbonate, the lighter calcium sulfate floats on the top and is removed and stored in a commercial calcium sulfate tank, and (b7) selectively directing the remaining heavier salts in the form of calcium carbonate and trace mercuric salts to one of a commercial calcium carbonate storage tank, the wet scrubber for reuse, a second heater maintained at 850 degrees C to separate the calcium carbonate from the mercuric salts, and a second stirred tank to remove the mercuric salts. The exhaust gas clean-up system includes a catalytic converter operative to remove nitrogen oxides, a wet scrubber containing a solution of calcium carbonate and water in it and operative to remove sulfur and carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas in the form of a liquid solution of different salts and carbon dioxide as the exhaust gas is exposed to the solution of calcium carbonate and water, and a reaction chamber operative in a fluid solution of calcium hydroxide and water to remove a fluid solution of various salts of calcium salts and mercuric salts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the step of: selectively directing the lighter liquid from the first liquid-solid centrifuge separator, the lighter liquid is directed to the heater at 100 degrees C and subsequently to an air cooler to condense the water from the carbon dioxide; selectively directing the lighter liquid from the first liquid-solid separator, the lighter liquid is directed to the bubbler tank containing a noble gas, where the noble gas is argon; selectively directing the remaining heavier salts to one of a commercial calcium carbonate storage tank, the wet scrubber, and a second stirred tank, the heavier salts are selectively directed to the commercial calcium carbonate storage tank and the wet scrubber; and selectively directing remaining heavier salts to one of a commercial calcium carbonate storage tank, the wet scrubber and a second stirred tank, the heavier salts is directed through the second stirred tank. In the step of adding one of dibromomethane and water, dibromomethane at 30 degrees C is added to the first stirred tank. The tetrabromoethane is added to the heavier salts in the second stirred tank to separate the lighter calcium carbonate from the heavier mercuric salts, the lighter calcium carbonate is selectively passed to the wet scrubber, the commercial calcium carbonate storage tank, and a second heater at 850 degrees C, the heavier mercuric salts are stored in a commercial mercury tank. The calcium carbonate is directed through the 850 degrees C heater chemically reducing the calcium carbonate to carbon dioxide and calcium oxide, the carbon dioxide is passed back to the wet scrubber for removal and the calcium oxide is directed to the reaction chamber to mix with the water forming more calcium hydroxide. The process in combination with a cyclone separator and an activated carbon filter disposed in the exhaust gas flow-path downstream of the fossil fuel fired power plant and operative to remove fly ash, mercury, and mercuric salts. The glycerol is added to the reaction chamber and the cleaning liquid solution within the second liquid-solid centrifuge separator is a mixture of glycerol, water and solubilized calcium hydroxide, the lighter liquid solution from the second liquid-solid separator is directed to one of the another passage in the 100 degrees C heater and the another compartment of the bubbler tank in the form of water, glycerol, and water soluble carbonic acid, the glycerol contained in the fluid solution is separated in it and returned to the reaction chamber for reuse in the process. The heavier salts from the second liquid-solid separator are in the form of calcium carbonate and mercuric salts with glycerol attached. The heavier salts, in the form of, calcium carbonate and mercuric salts with glycerol attached, are directed to the first stirred tank, the heavier salts at the bottom of the first stirred tank are selectively washed with water to remove additional glycerol and the additional glycerol solution is returned to the reaction chamber prior to proceeding with the separation step within the first stirred tank. The first pre-mix tank contains the cleaning solution for the wet scrubber and a second pre-mix tank contains the cleaning solution for the reaction chamber, and glycerol is added to the cleaning solution in the second pre-mix tank to increase the solubility of the calcium hydroxide in water. In the step of selectively directing remaining heavier salts in the form of calcium carbonate and trace mercuric salts to one of a commercial calcium carbonate storage tank, the wet scrubber for reuse, the heater being maintained at 850 degrees C, and a second stirred tank, the heavier salts are directed through the second heater, where the calcium carbonate is broken down into carbon dioxide and calcium oxide, the carbon dioxide is directed back to the wet scrubber for removal and the calcium oxide along with the mercuric salts are directed through the second heater to a shaker and sifter. The process where within the shaker and sifter, the more coarse and heavier mercuric salts are sifted away from the fine calcium oxide and passed to a commercial mercury tank and the powdery calcium oxide is directed back to the reaction chamber where it mixes with water to form additional calcium hydroxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. PN JP2022024943-A
TI Coal-fired power generation system used for artificial photosynthesis, is operated by collecting exhaust gas in tank with device that emits carbon dioxide, when large amount of carbon dioxide is produced, blowing to furnace, burning magnesium, decomposing carbon dioxide, and reducing magnesium oxide.
AU SAKAGAMI I
AE SAKAGAMI I (SAKA-Individual)
GA 202250878L
AB    NOVELTY - Coal-fired power generation system is operated by collecting exhaust gas in a tank with a device that emits carbon dioxide, when a large amount of carbon dioxide is produced, blowing to a magnesium furnace, burning magnesium, and decomposing carbon dioxide and becomes burning residue of carbon and magnesium oxide, using the combustion heat energy of this magnesium, generating power by the principle of thermal power generation, and when the debris collects to some extent, dripping to the chute, blowing carbon dioxide blower between the throttle bottom and the intermediate magnesium, dripping the mixed powder of carbon and magnesium oxide from the chute, blowing by a powerful blower during the fall, and has a different specific gravity, so that the powder is separated by utilizing the difference in the falling position, collecting and reusing as carbon and magnesium in a conveyor, and collecting and reducing magnesium oxide by irradiating a pulsed laser with sunlight in a sturdy tank.
   USE - The coal-fired power generation system is used for artificial photosynthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy cycle system uses carbon dioxide which affects global warming as an industrial waste gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Coal-fired power generation system is operated by collecting exhaust gas in a tank with a device that emits carbon dioxide, since carbon dioxide is heavy, tends to accumulate at the bottom due to downward replacement, and a certain flow rate from the bottom of the tank blows air to multiple other tanks to create a tank with a high carbon dioxide concentration, when a large amount of carbon dioxide is produced, blowing to a magnesium furnace, burning magnesium, and decomposing carbon dioxide and becomes burning residue of carbon and magnesium oxide, using the combustion heat energy of this magnesium, generating power by the principle of thermal power generation, and the magnesium furnace has magnesium spread in the middle, squeezing the bottom of the furnace so that burning debris collects on the bottom of the heat, narrow the outlet, and when the debris collects to some extent, dripping to the chute, blowing carbon dioxide blower between the throttle bottom and the intermediate magnesium, dripping the mixed powder of carbon and magnesium oxide from the chute, blowing by a powerful blower during the fall, and has a different specific gravity, so that the powder is separated by utilizing the difference in the falling position, collecting and reusing as carbon and magnesium in a conveyor or other conveyor, and collecting and reducing magnesium oxide by irradiating a pulsed laser with sunlight in a sturdy tank, where this reduction method is based on the magnesium energy cycle theory by Tokyo institute of technology yabe group, spontaneous emission such as nitrogen emission such as blast time lag, and nitrogen is lighter than carbon dioxide, so that can be released from the upper part of the tank, in this way, plants absorb carbon dioxide, produce organic matter, and emit oxygen by photosynthesis, and in this system, using the magnesium energy cycle theory, carbon dioxide is absorbed, electric energy is obtained by thermal power generation, and oxygen is released.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); P41 (Crushing: centrifuging, separating solids (B02, B03, B04).)

PN WO2020049319-A1; WO2020049319-A9
TI Preparation of polycarbonate ether polyol used for producing higher polymer, involves mixing organometallic catalyst, double metal cyanide catalyst and optionally solvent with epoxide to form mixture, and adding starter compound and epoxide.
AU KEMBER M
   LEELAND J
   HOLLIS E
   RILEY K
AE ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
GA 202020690H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polycarbonate ether polyol involves (a) (i) mixing an organometallic catalyst (I), double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst and optionally carbon dioxide and/or solvent with epoxide and optionally starter compound and/or carbon dioxide to form mixture (m1), or (ii) mixing DMC catalyst and optionally starter compound, carbon dioxide and/or solvent with epoxide and optionally carbon dioxide and/or solvent to form mixture (m1), or (iii) mixing epoxide, the catalyst (I), starter compound and carbon dioxide and optionally solvent to form mixture (m1), or (iv) mixing the catalyst (I), DMC catalyst and optionally starter compound, epoxide, carbon dioxide and/or solvent to form mixture (m1), and (b) adding one or more of starter compound, epoxide, carbon dioxide, the catalyst (I), DMC catalyst and/or solvent to the mixture (m1) to form mixture (m2) comprising starter compound, epoxide, carbon dioxide, the catalyst (I), DMC catalyst and optionally solvent.
   USE - Preparation of polycarbonate ether polyol used for producing higher polymer (all claimed) e.g. polyurethane. Uses include but are not limited insulation panels, high-performance adhesives, high-resilience foam seating, seals and gaskets, wheels and tires, and synthetic fibers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of polycarbonate ether polyol with narrow molecular weight distribution, improved ratio of ether to carbonate linkages and thermal degradation properties, and/or low polydispersity index.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of polycarbonate ether polyol involves (a) (i) mixing an organometallic catalyst of formula (I), double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst and optionally carbon dioxide and/or solvent with epoxide and optionally starter compound and/or carbon dioxide to form mixture (m1), or (ii) mixing DMC catalyst and optionally starter compound, carbon dioxide and/or solvent with epoxide and optionally carbon dioxide and/or solvent to form mixture (m1), or (iii) mixing epoxide, the catalyst (I), starter compound and carbon dioxide and optionally solvent to form mixture (m1), or (iv) mixing the catalyst (I), DMC catalyst and optionally starter compound, epoxide, carbon dioxide and/or solvent to form mixture (m1), and (b) adding one or more of starter compound, epoxide, carbon dioxide, the catalyst (I), DMC catalyst and/or solvent to the mixture (m1) to form mixture (m2) comprising starter compound, epoxide, carbon dioxide, the catalyst (I), DMC catalyst and optionally solvent.
   M = metal cation of formula: M-(L)v';
   group a = multidentate ligand, preferably tetradentate ligand or two bidentate ligands;
   (E)m = one or more activating groups attached to the ligand(s);
   dotted line = linker group covalently bonded to the ligand;
   E = activating functional group;
   m = 1-4;
   L = coordinating ligand, preferably neutral ligand, or anionic ligand which is capable of ring-opening an epoxide;
   v = 0-4;and
   v' = valency of M.
   (i) The catalyst (I) has an overall neutral charge. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of high molecular weight polyether carbonate, which involves performing steps (a) and (b).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The usage amount of epoxide is 1-95 wt.%, preferably 5-20 wt.% with respect to total content of epoxide mixed in step (a). The usage amount of starter compound is 1-95 wt.%, preferably 5-20 wt.% with respect to total content of starter compound mixed in step (a). The usage amount of catalyst (I) is 1-95 wt.%, preferably 5-20 wt.% with respect to total content of catalyst (I) mixed in step (a). The usage amount of DMC catalyst is 1-75 wt.%, preferably 5-20 wt.% with respect to total content of DMC catalyst mixed in step (a). The usage amount of carbon dioxide is 1-75 wt.%, preferably 5-20 wt.% with respect to total content of carbon dioxide mixed in step (a). The usage amount of solvent is 1-75 wt.%, preferably 5-20 wt.% with respect to total content of solvent mixed in step (a). The weight ratio of catalyst (I) with respect to DMC catalyst is 300:1-1:100, preferably 5:1-1:5. The starter compound is a substituted aromatic compound of formula: Z-(Rz)a, preferably at least one chosen from 1,2-ethanediol, 1,2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,2-butanediol, 1,3-butanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol, 1,10-decanediol, 1,4-cyclohexanediol, 1,2-diphenol, 1,3-diphenol, 1,4-diphenol, neopentyl glycol, catechol, cyclohexenediol, 1,4-cyclohexanedimethanol, dipropylene glycol, diethylene glycol, tripropylene glycol, triethylene glycol, tetraethylene glycol, polypropylene glycols having a molecular weight of 1500 g/mol such as polypropylene glycol (PPG) 425, PPG 725, and PPG 1000, triols such as glycerol, benzenetriol, 1,2,4-butanetriol, 1,2,6-hexanetriol, tris(methyl alcohol)propane, tris(methyl alcohol)ethane, tris(methyl alcohol)nitropropane, trimethylolpropane, polypropylene oxide triol, polyester triol, calix(4)arene, 2,2-bis(methyl alcohol)-1,3-propanediol, erythritol, pentaerythritol, sorbitol, oxalic acid, malonic acid, succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid, pimelic acid, suberic acid, azelaic acid, sebacic acid, undecanedioic acid, dodecanedioic acid, lactic acid, glycolic acid, 3-hydroxypropanoic acid, 4-hydroxybutanoic acid, 5-hydroxypentanoic acid, ethanolamine, diethanolamine, methyldiethanolamine, and phenyldiethanolamine. The catalyst (I) is used in combination with a co-catalyst, preferably tetraalkylammonium salts such as tetrabutylammonium salt, tetraalkylphosphinium salts such as tetrabutylphosphonium salt, bis(triarylphosphine)iminium salts such as bis(triphenylphosphine)iminium salt, or a nitrogen-containing nucleophile such as N-methylimidazole, dimethylaminopyridine such as 4-methylaminopyridine, 1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene, 7-methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene or 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene. The DMC catalyst comprises t least two metal centers and cyanide ligands, and further comprises at least one of one or more complexing agents, water, a metal salt and/or an acid. The DMC catalyst is prepared by treating a solution of a metal salt of formula: M'(X')p with a solution of a metal cyanide salt in the presence of at least one of complexing agent, water, and/or an acid. The complexing agent is at least one chosen from (poly)ether, polyether carbonate, polycarbonate, poly(tetramethylene ether diol), a ketone, an ester, an amide, an alcohol, and urea, preferably at least one chosen from propylene glycol, polypropylene glycol, methoxy ethylene glycol, dimethoxyethane, tert-butyl alcohol, ethylene glycol monomethyl ether, diglyme, triglyme, methanol, ethanol, isopropyl alcohol, n-butyl alcohol, isobutyl alcohol and sec-butyl alcohol, 3-buten-1-ol, 2-methyl-3-buten-2-ol, 2-methyl-3-butyn-2-ol and 3-methyl-1-pentyn-3-ol.Z = group comprising 2 or more -Rz groups;Rz = -OH, -NHR', -SH, -C(O)OH, -P(O)(OR')(OH), -PR'(O)(OH)2 or -PR'(O)OH;R' = H, or optionally substituted alkyl, heteroalkyl, aryl, heteroaryl, cycloalkyl or heterocycloalkyl;a = 2 or more;M' = Zn(II), Ru(II), Ru(III), Fe(II), Ni(II), Mn(II), Co(II), Sn(II), Pb(II), Fe(III), Mo(IV), Mo(VI), Al(III), Sr(II), W(IV), W(VI), Cu(II), or Cr(III);X' = anion, preferably halide, oxide, hydroxide, sulfate, carbonate, cyano, oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate or nitrate;andp = 1 or more.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The mixture (m1) is maintained at a temperature of 50-150 degrees C, preferably 50-90 degrees C for 5 minutes or more, preferably 16 hours or more prior to step (II). The step (i) involves mixing the catalyst (I), DMC catalyst and optionally carbon dioxide to form mixture (m3), maintaining the mixture (m3) at 0-250 degrees C, preferably 80-130 degrees C and subsequently adding epoxide and optionally starter compound and/or carbon dioxide to form mixture (m1). The step (b) involves mixing DMC catalyst epoxide, and optionally starter compound, carbon dioxide and/or solvent to form a pre-activated mixture, maintaining the pre-activated mixture at 50-110 degrees C, preferably 60-90 degrees C and adding the pre-activated mixture to mixture (m1) to form mixture (m2). The method further involves (c) adding epoxide to mixture (m2), starter compound, carbon dioxide and/or solvent to form mixture (m4) such that the molar ratio or weight ratio of epoxide, starter compound, carbon dioxide and/or solvent to catalyst (I) in mixture (m4) is 75% or more. The step (b) involves adding one or more starter compound (s1), epoxide, carbon dioxide, the catalyst (I), DMC catalyst and/or solvent to mixture (m1), and adding a starter compound (s2) and optionally epoxide, carbon dioxide, catalyst (I), DMC catalyst and/or solvent to form mixture (m2) comprising starter compound (s1), starter compound (s2), epoxide, carbon dioxide, catalyst of formula (I), DMC catalyst and optionally solvent. The DMC catalyst is dry-mixed with the other components. The catalyst (I) is dry-mixed with the other components, or mixed as a solution comprising the catalyst (I) and one or more of the starter compound, epoxide and/or solvent. The carbon dioxide is provided continuously. The method is carried out at a pressure of 1-60 bar carbon dioxide, preferably 1-5 bar. The temperature of the reaction increases during the course of the method. Preferred Composition: The mixture (m1) comprises less than 1 wt.%, preferably 0 wt.% water. The starter compound in step (a) is a starter compound (s1). The starter compound (s2) is polypropylene glycol having a molecular weight of 200-1000 Da, preferably 400 Da. Each starter compound comprises two or more hydroxyl groups, optionally eight or more hydroxyl groups. Preferred Properties: The starter compound (s1) has a molecular weight of 200 Da or more and the starter compound (s2) has a molecular weight of at most 200 Da.
   Preferred Definitions:
   group a = group chosen from 12 groups e.g. groups of formulae (i)-(v);and
   M = Cr.

PT P
PN CN109281720-A; CN209179828-U
TI Energy conversion and carbon sequestration system based on steam turbine based on magnesium comprises e.g. subsystem for utilizing renewable energy to electrolyze magnesium, magnesium, water reaction subsystem and carbon fixation subsystem.
AU ZHENG K
   HUANG Z
AE SHANGHAI POWER EQUIP RES INST (SNPT-C)
   SHANGHAI POWER EQUIP RES INST (SNPT-C)
GA 201915843F
AB    NOVELTY - Energy conversion and carbon sequestration system based on steam turbine based on magnesium comprises e.g. a subsystem for utilizing renewable energy to electrolyze magnesium, magnesium and water reaction subsystem, carbon fixation subsystem and steam turbine generator set. The subsystem for utilizing renewable energy power electrolytic magnesium includes a renewable energy power plant and an electrolytic magnesium plant. The redundant power transmission system of the renewable energy power plant is connected to the electrolytic magnesium plant. The electrolytic magnesium plant is connected to the magnesium supply device through the transportation link. The magnesium and water reaction subsystem includes a hydrogen feed water pump. The hydrogen feed water pump outlet is connected to the cooler water side inlet. The water side outlet of the cooler is connected to the water inlet of the reactor. The reactor gas product outlet is connected to the cooler gas product side inlet.
   USE - Used as energy conversion and carbon sequestration system based on steam turbine based on magnesium.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: consumes large amount of renewable energy; can produce high quality hydrogen and fix carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Energy conversion and carbon sequestration system based on steam turbine based on magnesium comprises a subsystem for utilizing renewable energy to electrolyze magnesium, magnesium and water reaction subsystem, carbon fixation subsystem and steam turbine generator set. The subsystem for utilizing renewable energy power electrolytic magnesium includes a renewable energy power plant and an electrolytic magnesium plant. The redundant power transmission system of the renewable energy power plant is connected to the electrolytic magnesium plant. The electrolytic magnesium plant is connected to the magnesium supply device through the transportation link. The magnesium and water reaction subsystem includes a hydrogen feed water pump. The hydrogen feed water pump outlet is connected to the cooler water side inlet. The water side outlet of the cooler is connected to the water inlet of the reactor. The reactor gas product outlet is connected to the cooler gas product side inlet. The cooler gas product side outlet is connected to the water separator. The water separator gas outlet is connected to the hydrogen collecting device. A magnesium supply device is connected to the magnesium inlet of the reactor. The carbon sequestration subsystem includes a magnesium oxide carbon fixation device and a carbon dioxide supply device. The solid product outlet of the reactor is connected to the magnesium oxide carbon fixation device solid inlet. A carbon dioxide supply device is connected to the gas inlet of the magnesium oxide carbon fixation device. The steam turbine generator set includes a feed water pump (1). The feed pump exit is divided into two ways one is connected to the inlet of the boiler (2) and another way to connecting the magnesium oxide carbon fixation device to the water side inlet. The magnesium oxide carbon fixation device water side outlet is connected with reactor water side inlet. The water side outlet of the reactor is connected to the inlet of the boiler. The outlet of the boiler is connected to the inlet of the steam turbine (3). The outlet of the steam turbine is connected to the inlet of the condenser (5). The outlet of the condenser is connected to the inlet of the feed pump. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method of magnesium based steam turbine energy transfer and carbon sequestration, comprising (i) using steam turbine-based energy conversion remote transmission and carbon sequestration system, and magnesium as carrier, supplying redundant power of the renewable energy power plant into the electrolytic magnesium plant, transporting the magnesium produced by the electrolytic magnesium plant into the magnesium supply device at the place of use through the transportation link, (ii) adding magnesium supply device inputs magnesium into the reactor, and using the hydrogen production feed pump, and supplying water into the reactor, magnesium reacting with water in the reactor to form magnesium oxide and hydrogen, cooling hydrogen and water mixture from reactor through cooler, using water separator and separating the water in the hydrogen and water mixture, conveying the hydrogen into the hydrogen collection device, releasing the heat by the reactor and transferring into the working fluid of the steam turbine generator set, (iii) using carbon dioxide supply device inputs carbon dioxide into the magnesium oxide carbon fixation device, where the reactor outputs a solid product to the magnesium oxide carbon fixation device, carrying out carbonation reaction, reacting magnesium oxide and carbon dioxide to obtain magnesium carbonate and occuring in the magnesia carbon fixation device, transferring the heat of reaction into the working fluid of the steam turbine unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the energy conversion and carbon sequestration system based on steam turbine based on magnesium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the raw material of the electrolytic magnesium is magnesium chloride. In the step (ii), the reaction temperature of magnesium and water in the reactor is 650-1000 degrees C and the pressure is 20-40 MPa.

TI METHOD FOR STORAGE OF DRIED-JERKED AND SMOKED FISH IN MEDIUM OF CARBON DIOXIDE.
AU NEVEROV E N
   UKHIN A M
   KOROTKII I A
   KOROTKIKH P S
AE UNIV KEMEROVO (UYKE-Non-standard)
GA 2020828743
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to cooling equipment, to fish cooling and storage installations. Disclosed is a method for storing dried-jerked and smoked fish in a carbon dioxide medium, which includes storing fish in a refrigerated display rack, note here that designed container with holes accommodating pressed snow-like carbon dioxide is installed into cargo volume of commercially available refrigeration counters.
   USE - Refrigerating equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention ensures storage of fish so that at supply of heat carbon dioxide begins to sublimate, thus creating gaseous medium in cargo volume of show-window, as a result value of shrinkage with given method of fish storage and realization is reduced.

PN CN110845304-A
TI Co-producing methanol from melamine tail gas            comprises performing ammonia-carbon separation on the            melamine tail gas to obtain carbon dioxide and liquid            ammonia and reacting carbon dioxide with hydrogen.
AU TANG Y
   QIN Y
   SONG G
   GONG Y
   KONG D
AE BEIJING EDGEIN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202019957H
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing methanol from melamine tail gas                comprises (a) performing ammonia-carbon separation                on the melamine tail gas to obtain carbon dioxide                and liquid ammonia; and (b) reacting carbon dioxide                with hydrogen.
   USE - The method is useful for co-producing methanol                from melamine tail gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical, convenient,                and easy to implement; and has low energy                consumption and makes full use of melamine tail                gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                device useful for co-production of melamine tail                gas and producing methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pressure of carbon                  dioxide obtained after ammonia-carbon separation                  is 1.5-2.5 MPa. The temperature of carbon dioxide                  obtained after ammonia-carbon separation is 60-80                  degrees C. The ratio of the pressure of the                  carbon dioxide obtained after the separation of                  ammonia and carbon to the pressure during the                  reaction is 2-4. The molar ratio of the hydrogen                  to the carbon dioxide is 2-5: 1. The temperature                  of the reaction is 190-270 degrees C. The                  pressure of the reaction is 3-10 MPa. The                  volumetric space velocity of the reaction is                  3000-10000/hour. The reaction is performed after                  preheating a mixture of carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen, where the preheating temperature is                  80-130 degrees C. The method comprises condensing                  the product of the reaction to obtain the                  methanol, using the liquid ammonia to condense                  the product of the reaction, where the                  temperature of the liquid ammonia is 60-80                  degrees C, and the pressure is 1.5-2.5 MPa;                  gasifying the liquid ammonia into ammonia gas,                  after condensing the product obtained by the                  reaction, and mixing the ammonia gas with the                  melamine tail gas to perform the ammonia-carbon                  separation. The product obtained by the reaction                  is subjected to the condensation, by gas-liquid                  separation and rectification to obtain the                  methanol, mixing the gas obtained after the                  gas-liquid separation with carbon dioxide                  obtained after ammonia carbon separation, and                  preheating.

TI Beer-taste beverage comprises extract of plant raw            material containing coriander seeds with carbon            dioxide.
AU INUI T
   YONEZAWA D
   YONEZAWA O
AE SUNTORY HOLDINGS LTD (SUNR-C)
   SUNTORY LTD (SUNR-C)
   SUNTORY HOLDINGS LTD (SUNR-C)
   SUNTORY HOLDINGS LTD (SUNR-C)
   SUNTORY HOLDINGS LTD (SUNR-C)
GA 2019A37734
AB    NOVELTY - Beer-taste beverage comprises an extract of a                plant raw material containing coriander seeds with                carbon dioxide.
   USE - As beer-taste beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage has high flavor of coriander                seeds, and low sugar content, where the extract has                a high content of terpene hydrocarbons.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for manufacturing the beer-taste beverage,                involving (i) extracting the plant extract from the                plant raw material containing coriander seeds using                carbon dioxide as an extraction solvent, (ii)                separating the plant extract extracted in step (ii)                from carbon dioxide, and (iii) adding the extract                obtained in step (ii) during the production process                of the beer-taste beverage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The extract has a                  ratio of limonene content to linalool content of                  1.5% or more, preferably 1% or more, and a ratio                  of the gamma -terpinene content to the linalool                  content of 1.5% or more, preferably 0.5% or more.                  The beverage comprises 1 ppb or less valencene or                  1 ppb or less nootkatone. Preferred Conditions:                  The step (ii) is carried out at 40 degrees C or                  less, preferably 20 degrees C or less. In step                  (i), the carbon dioxide density is 400-1000 kg/m3                  and the carbon dioxide has a pressure of 6-50                  MPa.

PN CN108314751-A; CN108314751-B
TI Preparing diacetone acrylamide and sodium acrylate copolymer comprises e.g. carrying out precipitation polymerization of diacetone acrylamide and sodium acrylate monomer in carbon dioxide as solvent and persulfate salt compound as initiator.
AU LAN P
   LV J
AE UNIV JIAXING (JIXA-C)
   UNIV JIAXING (JIXA-C)
GA 201859637G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing diacetone acrylamide and sodium acrylate copolymer comprises taking carbon dioxide as solvent, persulfate salt compound as initiator, critical temperature and pressure of carbon dioxide, and carrying out precipitation polymerization of diacetone acrylamide and sodium acrylate monomer to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing diacetone acrylamide and sodium acrylate copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses supercritical carbon dioxide as a medium, it avoids environmental pollution caused by use of toxic, harmful, volatile and flammable organic solvents; and saves energy and resources.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is potassium persulfate, sodium persulfate or ammonium persulfate. The amount of diacetone acrylamide is 0-70% of amount of two monomers, and the amount of acrylate is 30-100% of amount of two monomers. The total monomer of the diacetone acrylamide and sodium acrylate is 2-6 mol/l. The concentration of initiator is 0.08-0.3 mol/l. Preferred Method: The method comprises adding diacetone acrylamide, acrylate monomer and initiator into a reaction kettle, sealing, then adding 0.05-0.5 mol/l concentration of initiator into carbon dioxide, discharging air in the reaction kettle, adding 1-8 mol/l concentration of the total monomer of the diacetone acrylamide and sodium acrylate, then closing the inlet valve of the reaction kettle, heating, heating, continuously increases pressure in the reaction kettle, when the reaction temperature reaches to 32-40 degrees C, controlling pressure in the reaction kettle to 8-45 MPa, stirring by stirrer, and reacting for 3-20 hours. The pressure in the reaction kettle is 20-30 MPa. The reaction temperature is 33-38 degrees C. The reaction time is 4-10 hours. The method comprises passing cooling water to cool the reaction kettle to room temperature, opening the gas outlet valve of the reaction kettle, and then reducing the pressure to normal pressure to obtain final product.

AU YANG X
   WEI Z
   ZHANG S
   ZHAO L
   CHEN J
AE SHANGHAI CHAOLIN POWER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202214207Y
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a supercritical carbon dioxide power generation closed circulation system capable of preventing heater from quenching, wherein the supercritical carbon dioxide power generation closed circulation system further comprises a heater bypass pipeline capable of closing and opening; when the heater bypass pipeline is closed, the carbon dioxide working medium outlet at the cold end of the heat exchange unit is sent to the heater; when the heater bypass pipeline is opened, the carbon dioxide working substance sent to the heater is redirected to the working medium inlet of the energy conversion unit by the heater bypass pipeline. when the system operates normally, the heater bypass pipeline is in the closed state. When the system emergently occurs, when the heater stops heating, the heater bypass pipeline is quickly opened, at this time, the carbon dioxide working substance bypasses the heater, directly enters the energy conversion unit through the heater bypass pipeline, so that the cold carbon dioxide working medium will not enter the heater, causing the quenching accident of the heater.

The carrier of manganese complex is carbon nanotube-modified carbon paper or carbon paper which is subjected to inert gas firing treatment.
