PT P
PN CN108251593-A; CN108251593-B
TI Improvement of denitrification by dynamically adjusting bottom blowing carbon dioxide flowrate of converter steelmaking involves providing the bottom blowing nitrogen in molten pool agitation and controlling bottom blowing flowrate.
AU ZHU R
   HU S
   DONG K
   LIU R
   LV M
   WU W
   HAN B
   WANG Y
   WANG X
   ZHU C
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 201855323D
AB    NOVELTY - Improvement of denitrification by dynamically adjusting bottom blowing carbon dioxide flowrate of converter steelmaking comprises providing the bottom blowing nitrogen in molten pool agitation since reaction of carbon dioxide, silicon and manganese is exothermic reaction, controlling bottom blowing flowrate to 0.01-0.1 Nm3/t/minute, blowing carbon dioxide at the bottom, using carbon dioxide and carbon to absorb heat, which provides bottom blowing nozzle for blowing at large flowrate in the later stage of smelting, controlling the bottom blowing flow to 0.01-0.1 Nm3/t/minute, establishing functional relationship based on the decreasing ratio of the real-time molten steel decarburization rate, gradually increasing flow rate f of the bottom blowing carbon dioxide and increasing the bottom blowing carbon dioxide flow in the late stage of converter smelting from the initial f0 where maximum flow rate fmax is reached at the end of smelting.
   USE - Method for improving denitrification by dynamically adjusting bottom blowing carbon dioxide flowrate of converter steelmaking.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can stabilize the nitrogen content of converter end point within 15 ppm; and does not substantially affect the life of the furnace bottom.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises making up for the gradual decrease of bubble generation in the furnace due to the gradual decrease of decarburization rate in the later stage of smelting, enhancing the mixing of molten pool, improving the kinetic conditions of denitrification, controlling the nitrogen content of end steel within 15 ppm, gradually increasing the bottom blowing where flowrate of carbon dioxide can reduce the erosion of bottom of the converter by continuous high-flow bottom blowing carbon dioxide, and cooperating with the mushroom head formed in middle of smelting where method does not affect the life of the furnace bottom of the converter. The minimum value of bottom blowing carbon dioxide flow at the beginning of converter smelting is 0.01-0.1 Nm3/t/minute. The maximum flowrate of bottom blowing carbon dioxide at the end of smelting is 0.02-0.2 Nm3/t/minute. The furnace gas flow in early stage of smelting is gradually increased from 0 to stable value M. The flowrate of furnace gas in the middle of smelting is basically maintained at stable value M, and the fluctuation range is plus minus 10%. The furnace gas flowrate in the later stage of smelting is gradually decreased from stable value M to the endpoint of blowing. The steady value M is furnace gas flowrate when the decarburization rate is substantially constant, and is 5-10 Nm3/t/minute. The decreasing ratio of decarburization rate of real-time molten steel is obtained using formula Z=(v0-v)/v0 where Z is proportion of decarburization rate of molten steel, v0 is decarburization rate of molten steel at the beginning of the converter smelting and v is the real-time molten steel decarburization rate. The flow rate f of bottom-blown carbon dioxide is obtained by using formula f=f0+Z(fmax-f0) where f0 is the bottom blowing carbon dioxide flow at the beginning of the converter smelting. Preferred Composition: The nitrogen content in the bottom-blown carbon dioxide gas is not higher than 1%.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)
MC M24-B02C; M24-C06; M24-C09; M24-D02; M24-E
IP C21C-005/35

PN CN113813948-A
TI Preparing cobalt-indium oxide/carbon composite            photocatalyst useful for photocatalytic reduction of            carbon dioxide to generate carbon monoxide and            hydrogen, comprises e.g. adding methanol solution of            cobalt nitrate dropwise, collecting precipitate, and            calcining powder in nitrogen atmosphere.
AU LI Z
   ZHAO S
   LIANG Q
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 202206962B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cobalt-indium oxide/carbon composite                photocatalyst comprises (1) taking indium nitrate                hydrate, stirring and dissolving it in                dimethylformamide at room temperature, adding                terephthalic acid, stirring to fully dissolve,                stirring in oil bath, and centrifuging and washing                with absolute ethanol to collect white precipitate                and vacuum-dried to obtain product indium-Materiaux                de Linstitut Lavoisier (MIL)-68 after reaction is                completed and cooled to room temperature, (2) fully                dispersing indium-MIL-68 in methanol solution,                adding methanol solution of cobalt nitrate dropwise                under stirring conditions, continuously stirring                after the dropwise addition, adding the methanol                solution of 2-methylimidazole dropwise, stirring,                collecting precipitate, and washing and drying to                obtain zeolitic imidazolate frameworks                (ZIFs)-67-indium-MIL-68 powder, and (3) calcining                the ZIF-67-indium-MIL-68 powder in nitrogen                atmosphere to obtain cobalt-indium oxide/carbon                composite photocatalyst.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide to generate carbon                monoxide and hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is relatively simple and                economical; and has easy to control preparation                conditions. The photocatalyst has stable chemical                properties, no secondary pollution, readily                available raw materials and low preparation cost,                high catalytic efficiency and certain research and                application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cobalt-indium oxide/carbon composite                photocatalyst comprises (1) taking indium nitrate                hydrate, stirring and dissolving it in                dimethylformamide (DMF) at room temperature, adding                terephthalic acid, stirring for 5-10 minutes to                fully dissolve, stirring in oil bath at                110-120degrees Celsius for 120-150 minutes, and                centrifuging and washing with absolute ethanol to                collect the white precipitate and vacuum-dried to                obtain the product indium-Materiaux de Linstitut                Lavoisier (MIL)-68 after the reaction is completed                and cooled to room temperature, (2) fully                dispersing the indium-MIL-68 obtained in the step                (1) in methanol solution, adding the methanol                solution of cobalt nitrate dropwise under stirring                conditions, continuously stirring for 30-60 minutes                after the dropwise addition, adding the methanol                solution of 2-methylimidazole dropwise, stirring at                20-25degrees Celsius for 24-28 hours, collecting                the precipitate, and washing and drying to obtain                zeolitic imidazolate frameworks                (ZIFs)-67-indium-MIL-68 powder, and (3) calcining                the ZIF-67-indium-MIL-68 powder prepared in the                step (2) in nitrogen atmosphere to obtain the                cobalt-indium oxide/carbon composite                photocatalyst.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) cobalt-indium oxide/carbon composite                photocatalyst prepared by the preparation method;                and
   (2) use method of the photocatalyst,                comprising dispersing the cobalt-indium                oxide/carbon composite photocatalyst and ruthenium                terpyridine chloride hexahydrate in acetonitrile                with volume ratio of 4:1:1 by ultrasonication in                the mixed solution of water and triethanolamine,                pouring the mixed solution into the reactor for                continuous stirring, introducing pure carbon                dioxide gas to evacuate the gas in the reactor, and                applying light to complete the photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide to generate carbon                monoxide and hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  mass ratio of indium nitrate hydrate and                  terephthalic acid is 1:1. In the step (2), the                  mass ratio of indium-MIL-68 and ZIF-67 is                  1-2:1-2. The molar ratio of cobalt nitrate                  hexahydrate to 2-methylimidazole is 1:4.Preferred Method: In the step (1), the                  drying temperature is 60degrees Celsius. In the                  step (2), methanol is used for washing, and the                  drying condition is vacuum drying at 60degrees                  Celsius. In the step (3), the calcination method                  is specifically comprises spreading out the                  ZIF-67-indium-MIL-68 powder prepared in the step                  (2) in ceramic crucible with tin foil,                  introducing nitrogen, calcining at 120-150degrees                  Celsius to remove organic ligands in                  metal-organic framework (MOF), heating to                  500-600degrees Celsius at 5degrees Celsius/minute                  for 2 hours after calcination is completed and                  cooled to room temperature, closing the nitrogen                  gas, and collecting the black powder to obtain                  the cobalt-indium oxide/carbon composite                  photocatalyst. The calcination time for removing                  organic ligands in MOF is 2 hours. The                  temperature is raised to 550degrees Celsius at                  rate of 5degrees Celsius/minute.

AB    (CN210496452-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims LiOH absorbing carbon dioxide device, comprising a closed frame containing the LiOH absorbent, the closed frame is a cylinder with two open ends, the cylinder is provided with a plurality of non-woven fabric bag packaged with the LiOH absorbent; the non-woven cloth bag comprising a bag body, an upper cover and a lower cover, the upper cover and the lower cover and the bag body through the locking edge, the upper cover is provided with a handle, the side wall of the bag body is provided with a slot and is provided with a zipper. The utility model improves LiOH absorbent bulk density increase carbon dioxide absorption rate at the same time, medicine storage and filtering together, at the same time the electroless condition suitable for current; improve the forced ventilation efficiency, improve agent utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:

   USE - Used as absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the bulk density of the lithium hydroxide lithium hydroxide absorbent while increasing the carbon dioxide absorption rate; integrates medicine storage and filtration, which is suitable under the condition of electric and non-electric condition; improves the efficiency of forced ventilation; and increases the utilization rate of medicine.

AU SHINZO I
AE EUREKA ENG INC (EURE-Non-standard)
   EUREKA ENG INC (EURE-Non-standard)
GA 201993576F
AB    NOVELTY - A biomass-derived carbon dioxide (CO2)-free                electric power and hydrogen joint production system                comprises electric power production apparatus which                produces electric power using direct gasifying gas                which carries out thermal decomposition                gasification of biomass, and hydrogen production                apparatus which produces hydrogen from                hydrogen-rich gasifying gas which obtained by                steam-reforming of biomass-derived carbonized                material. Electric power production apparatus has                biomass supply apparatus which supplies biomass,                direct heat-type gasifier for thermal decomposition                gasification of biomass to directly produce                gasifying gas, and cogeneration apparatus in which                one side portion of direct gasifying gas is                supplied from direct heat-type gasifier, and                electric power and heat are produced by one side                portion of direct gasifying gas. Hydrogen                production apparatus has biomass-derived carbonized                material supply apparatus and indirect heating type                gasifier.
   USE - A biomass-derived carbon dioxide (CO2)-free                electric power and hydrogen joint production                system.
   ADVANTAGE - While electric power is efficiently producible                with gasifying gas directly, hydrogen is generable                utilizing effectively the unreacted char containing                gasification residue of the thermal decomposition                gasification reaction. Funding cost of the biomass                for hydrogen production (carbon source) is                unnecessary.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A biomass-derived carbon dioxide (CO2)-free                electric power and hydrogen joint production system                comprises electric power production apparatus which                produces electric power using direct gasifying gas                which carries out thermal decomposition                gasification of biomass, and hydrogen production                apparatus which produces hydrogen from                hydrogen-rich gasifying gas which obtained by                steam-reforming of biomass-derived carbonized                material. Electric power production apparatus has                biomass supply apparatus which supplies biomass,                direct heat-type gasifier for thermal decomposition                gasification of biomass to directly produce                gasifying gas, and cogeneration apparatus in which                one side portion of direct gasifying gas is                supplied from direct heat-type gasifier, and                electric power and heat are produced by one side                portion of direct gasifying gas. Hydrogen                production apparatus has biomass-derived carbonized                material supply apparatus which supplies                biomass-derived carbonized material that is the                unreacted char containing gasification residue of                thermal decomposition gasification reaction                directly taken out from heat-type gasifier, and                unreacted char containing gasification residue and                water vapor are supplied, and the other portion of                direct gasifying gas directly produced by the                heat-type gasifier is supplied as gas for heating,                and indirect heating type gasifier in which                unreacted char containing gasification residue and                water vapor are indirectly heated, steam-reforming                of unreacted char containing gasification residue                is carried out with water vapor, and hydrogen rich                gasifying gas is produced. Water is supplied from                water supply apparatus, hydrogen rich gasifying gas                is supplied from indirect heating type gasifier,                and heat-exchange is carried out between hydrogen                rich gasifying gas and water. A gas cooler supplies                water vapor to indirect heating type gasifier while                cooling the hydrogen rich gasifying gas. A hydrogen                separator, where hydrogen rich gasifying gas cooled                from gas cooler is supplied, separates hydrogen gas                from hydrogen-rich gasifying gas.

PN CN108862370-A
TI Preparing nano zinc oxide comprises e.g. mixing and grinding ground zinc-containing raw ore with first leaching agent, adding calcium oxide and calcium hydroxide, filtering obtained slurry in press crystallization step and vacuum extracting.
AU YUE H
   LI S
   LONG Z
   JIANG T
   LIU Q
AE CHONGQING DONGQUN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201895528G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano zinc oxide comprises e.g. mixing and grinding ground zinc-containing raw ore with first leaching agent, filtering to obtain first leachate and purifying, adding calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide into first leachate, filtering to obtain first solid and first filtrate, adding calcium hydroxide and/or calcium oxide into first filtrate to obtain second solid and second filtrate, taking second solid for calcination, adding second leaching agent into obtained calcined product in first calcination step to obtain second leaching solution, adding active agent into second leachate and introducing pressurized carbon dioxide to obtain slurry containing crystals, filtering obtained slurry in press crystallization step to obtain third solid and third filtrate, vacuum extracting third filtrate, decomposing portion of ammonium carbonate in third filtrate into ammonia and carbon dioxide, rinsing third solid with water, drying third solid and calcining.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano zinc oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and easy to operate; does not require steaming, reduces energy consumption; avoids high temperature and high pressure safety hazard and equipment corrosion caused by ammonia evaporation, solves process recovery rate and quality grade problem; and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano zinc oxide comprises (i) mixing and grinding ground zinc-containing raw ore with first leaching agent, filtering to obtain first leachate, where first extracting agent is mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium bicarbonate or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium carbonate, or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia, ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium carbonate and optionally purifying obtained first leachate, (ii) adding calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide into first leachate, stirring, filtering to obtain first solid and first filtrate, (iii) adding calcium hydroxide and/or calcium oxide into first filtrate, stirring and filtering to obtain second solid and second filtrate, (iv) taking second solid for calcination, where calcination temperature is 150-1050 degrees C, preferably 250-350 degrees C, (v) adding second leaching agent into obtained calcined product in first calcination step, stirring and filtering to obtain second leaching solution, where second leaching agent is mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium bicarbonate or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium carbonate, or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia, ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium carbonate, (vi) adding active agent into second leachate and introducing pressurized carbon dioxide to obtain slurry containing crystals, (vii) filtering obtained slurry in press crystallization step in sealed environment to obtain third solid and third filtrate, vacuum extracting third filtrate to normal pressure, decomposing portion of ammonium carbonate in third filtrate into ammonia and carbon dioxide, rinsing the third solid with water, and (viii) drying third solid and calcining at temperature of 450-900 degrees C to obtain final product having average particle diameter of 10-100 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass concentration of total ammonia in first leaching agent is 5-15%; molar concentration of efficient carbonate in first leaching agent is C first extracting agent carbonate is (n raw ore total zinc - n ore zinc carbonate) x a/V first extractant, where C first leaching agent carbonate is molar concentration of efficient carbonate in first leaching agent, n total zinc in ore is amount of zinc element in zinc-containing raw ore; n ore carbonate zinc is amount of zinc carbonate in zinc ore, V first leaching agent is volume of first leaching agent; a is 100-600%, preferably 150-250%; mass concentration of total ammonia in second extracting agent is 6-12%; and mass concentration of efficient carbonate in second extracting agent is 8-15%. The zinc ammine ion concentration (by mass of zinc element) in first leachate is greater than or equal to 10 g/l, preferably 10-50 g/l. In step (vi), crystallization pressure is 0.3-0.6 MPa. The active agent is sodium hexametaphosphate and/or sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate. Preferred Method: The method comprises amount of calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide added in pretreatment step is n(pretreatment) is equal to (n first leachate carbonate -n zinc ammine ion) x b, where n pretreatment is addition amount of calcium hydroxide and/or calcium oxide in the pretreatment step, n first leachate carbonate is amount of efficient carbonate in first leachate, n zinc ammine ion is amount of zinc ammine ion substance in first leachate and value of b is 90% less than or equal to b less than or equal to 110%. In step (iii), adding calcium hydroxide and/or calcium oxide into first filtrate until the precipitate no longer increases. In step (vii), carrying out reaction of decomposing part of ammonium carbonate in third filtrate into ammonia and carbon dioxide at temperature of 70-90 degrees C. In step (iii), passing carbon dioxide into obtained second filtrate, taking second filtrate into which carbon dioxide is passed as the first leaching agent and cycle for first leaching of zinc-containing ore. In step (vii), depressurizing third filtrate to normal pressure, collecting carbon dioxide released by decomposition of ammonium carbonate, recycling for pressure crystallization, collecting third filtrate the pressure reduction to normal pressure and recycling for secondary leaching.

TI Preparing aromatic plant throat lozenge, by mixing aromatic plant extract, honeysuckle extract, Chrysanthemum extract and crystal sugar, heating, filtering mixture, cooling mixture and stirring mixture.
AU FAN L
AE WEIHAI VOCATIONAL COLLEGE (WEIH-Non-standard)
GA 201836742M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing aromatic plant throat lozenge, involves preparing aromatic plant extract containing essential oil, washing the honeysuckle, drying the honeysuckle, crushing the honeysuckle, mixing honeysuckle pollen with 95% ethanol to prepare an extract, placing the extract in kettle, transferring supercritical carbon dioxide fluid into extraction kettle, dissolving the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid with the extract and obtaining honeysuckle extract, washing the Chrysanthemum, vacuum-drying the Chrysanthemum, extracting Chrysanthemum powder with water, filtering the extract, centrifuging the filtrate and concentrating the mixture to obtain Chrysanthemum extract, mixing 4-5 pts. wt. aromatic plant extract, 6-8 pts. wt. honeysuckle extract, 3-5 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum extract, and 65-75 pts. wt. crystal sugar, heating the mixture at 220-250 degrees C, filtering the mixture, cooling the mixture to 110-130 degrees C and stirring the mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aromatic plant throat lozenge (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The lozenge is nontoxic.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing aromatic plant throat lozenge, involves (a) crushing loquat leaves, folium eucalypti and peppermint into powder, where the mass ratio of the loquat leaves, folium eucalypti and peppermint is 1:0.8-1.6:4-8, adding 5 times purified water to the aromatic plant powder, placing the powder in microwave extraction device, controlling microwave extraction power at 350-500 W, extracting the mixture for 15-25 minutes, transferring the mixture to distillation pot at 55-69 degrees C and 2-3 KPa for 1-1.5 hours, increasing temperature to 150-160 degrees C, where the inlet temperature of the tube is 150 degrees C, and the outlet temperature is 70 degrees C, separating the mixture by an oil-water separator to obtain aromatic plant extract containing essential oil, (b) washing the honeysuckle, drying the honeysuckle, crushing the honeysuckle into 20 meshes sieve, mixing honeysuckle pollen with 95% ethanol to prepare an extract, placing the extract in kettle, transferring supercritical carbon dioxide fluid into extraction kettle, where the pressure of the extraction kettle is 24-28 MPa, the temperature is 35-55 degrees C and the extraction time is 2-4 hours, dissolving the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid with the extract and obtaining honeysuckle extract, (c) washing the Chrysanthemum, vacuum-drying the Chrysanthemum at 40-60 degrees C for 12-24 hours, extracting Chrysanthemum powder with water at 80-95 degrees C for 60-90 minutes, filtering the extract, centrifuging the filtrate at 10000-12100 g for 15-20 minutes and concentrating the mixture to obtain Chrysanthemum extract, and (d) mixing 4-5 pts. wt. aromatic plant extract, 6-8 pts. wt. honeysuckle extract, 3-5 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum extract, and 65-75 pts. wt. crystal sugar, heating the mixture at 220-250 degrees C, filtering the mixture, cooling the mixture to 110-130 degrees C and stirring the mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the aromatic plant extract, honeysuckle extract, Chrysanthemum extract and the mint powder is 1:1.2:6. The microwave extraction power is 450 W, and the extraction time is 20 minutes. The distillation temperature is 62 degrees C and the distillation time is 1 hour. The mass ratio of the honeysuckle powder and ethanol is 1:4-6. The pressure in the extraction kettle is 26 MPa, the temperature is 45 degrees C and the extraction time is 3 hours. The pressure of the separation kettle is set to 7 MPa and the temperature is 46 degrees C.

PT P
PN KR2018002558-A
TI Internal state control system of cooling container, includes control unit to control start-up of vent regardless of inside and outside temperature difference of cooling container and to control cooling device and heater of vent.
AU CHO J
   CHOI H
   CHOI S
   JEON M
   JUNG J
   KIM C
   KIM J
   KIM T
   LEE B
   LEE J
   LEE K
   MOON Y
   PARK B
AE UNIV DONG-A RES FOUND IND-ACAD COOP (UYDS-C)
GA 2018063600
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) includes a analysis section (200) to analyze and deliver the internal state information collected from a gathering unit (100). A control unit (300) controls a cooling container according to analysis result. A vent (400) ventilates the inner portion of container under the control of control unit. The control unit includes a ventilation control unit to control start-up of vent regardless of inside and outside temperature difference of the container, and controls the cooling device and heater of the vent to selectively operate, when user's range is out of set range.
   USE - Internal state control system of cooling container used for transporting product such as agricultural product and dairy product.
   ADVANTAGE - The internal state of the cooling container is controlled by sensing the internal state of the cooling container, so that the internal state of the cooling container is maintained in the optimal environment. The freshness and quality of the contents is improved by controlling the internal environment according to the temperature most sensitive to the contents stored in the container. The indoor humidity and the concentration of the carbon dioxide and ethylene gas are interlocked with each other based on the internal temperature of the container, and the indoor air is ventilated according to the detection result so as to prevent the quality of the contents from deteriorating. The temperature difference of the cooling container outside is compared and the mode ventilating the internal air of cooling container or the mode maintaining the temperature is differently controlled according to the temperature differential. Therefore the internal state of the cooling container is efficiently controlled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The cooling container internal state information gathering unit collects the cooling container internal state information by sensing the temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide (CO2) concentration and ethylene gas concentration of the moving cooling container. The gathering unit includes a temperature and humidity sensor to measure the inner temperature and humidity of the container and senses the information. A carbon dioxide (CO2) concentration sensor measures the carbon dioxide concentration when the temperature of the container is 0 degree Celsius or greater. An ethylene gas concentration sensor measures the ethylene gas concentration when the temperature of the container is 0 degree Celsius or greater. The analysis section includes a status information receiving unit to receive the cooling container internal state information from the gathering unit. A status information analysis section receives and analyzes the internal state information and delivers the analyzed result to the control unit. The control unit obtains the external temperature information of the container to compare the difference between the external and inner temperature of the container.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the cooling container internal state control system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Cooling container internal state control system (10)
   Cooling container internal state information gathering unit (100)
   Cooling container internal state information analysis section (200)
   Cooling container control unit (300)
   Cooling container vent (400)

AE UNIV LANZHOU (ULAN-C)
GA 2022D48960
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide gas extraction device (10)                comprises a water container (100), which is                provided with an injection hole (101) that can be                opened or closed. One end of the preparation                pipeline (200) is connected to the water body                container, and the other end of the preparation                pipeline is connected to the vacuum pump (110). The                first pair of ports (210) is used for connecting                with the dry ice cold trap (120), and the second                pair of ports (220) is used for connecting with the                ice fingers (130). The first valve (310) is                arranged between the water body container and the                first paired port. The second valve (320) is                arranged between the first paired port and the                second paired port.
   USE - Carbon dioxide gas extraction device.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide in the atmosphere in the                preparation pipeline can be pumped out by a vacuum                pump, and then combined with a dry ice cold trap,                an ice finger and a vacuum pump to separate the gas                produced by the reaction, and the impurity gas is                pumped away, and finally carbon dioxide with higher                purity is obtained.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon                dioxide gas extraction method.

   ADVANTAGE - The sorbitan polyether carboxylate containing                chemical composition: solves the problem that the                minimum miscibility pressure of carbon dioxide and                crude oil in most domestic reservoirs is high, and                the carbon dioxide miscibility flooding cannot be                realized, resulting in low oil displacement                efficiency; helps carbon dioxide miscibility                flooding reservoirs to realize miscible oil                displacement and improve the oil displacement                recovery rate; and solves the problem of chemical                agent injection in low-permeability and                ultra-low-permeability reservoirs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sorbitan polyether carboxylate containing                chemical composition comprises sorbitan polyether                carboxylate and additives. The sorbitan polyether                carboxylate is of formula (I). The additive is                substituted ethylene glycol compound of formula                (II).
   R', R'', R''' = (CH2)eH;
   e = 0-4; x1-x4, y1-y4, z1-z4, a, b, c =                0-50;
   x1+x2+x3+x4+y1+y2+y3+y4+z1+z2+z3+z4, a+b+c =                more than 0;
   M1-M4 = H or -(C=O)-R1;
   R2 = 1-50C hydrocarbyl (optionally                substituted);
   R3-R5 = (CH2)jH;

   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:

   (2) reducing minimum miscibility pressure of                carbon dioxide flooding, comprising mixing the                chemical composition with liquid or supercritical                carbon dioxide, and injecting it into the formation                together.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: (I) is prepared by (i)                  heating sorbitol and dehydrating agent A to                  dehydrate and etherifying to obtain sorbitan,                  (ii) reacting sorbitan and epoxy compound in the                  presence of catalyst B to obtain sorbitan                  polyether compound, (iii) the sorbitan polyether                  compound obtained in step (ii) is subjected to                  condensation reaction with substituted carboxylic                  acid compound (R1C(=O)OH) in the presence of                  catalyst C to obtain final product. In step (i),                  the reaction temperature is 140-180degrees                  Celsius; the dehydrating agent A is phosphorus                  pentoxide, p-toluenesulfonic acid and/or                  phosphoric acid, and the amount of the                  dehydrating agent is preferably 0.5-2 wt.% of the                  mass of sorbitol; and the epoxy compound is                  ethylene oxide, propylene oxide and/or butylene                  oxide. In step (ii), the emperature of reaction                  is 100-140degrees Celsius; the catalyst B is                  sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium                  hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide, and the                  amount of catalyst B is 0.5-5 wt.% of sorbitan                  quality. In step (iii), the reaction temperature                  is 180-220degrees Celsius; the catalyst C is                  sodium bicarbonate and/or potassium bicarbonate,                  and the amount of the catalyst C is 0.5-3 wt.% of                  the mass of the sorbitan polyether compound. In                  the reducing minimum miscibility pressure of                  carbon dioxide flooding, the amount of the                  chemical composition is 0.1-5 wt.% of the mass of                  carbon dioxide under the injection pressure,                  preferably 0.5-3 wt.%; the reservoir temperature                  is 40-180degrees Celsius, and the injection                  temperature is 0-100degrees Celsius.Preferred Components: The molar ratio of (I)                  to (II) is 1:0.005-50, preferably 1:0.1-10.

The matrix is a                  polymer consisting of phthalocyanine, polyimide,                  polysulfone and/or polyethylene glycol.

PN CN113492507-A
TI Processing carbon dioxide copolymer film by            blending carbon dioxide copolymer raw material with            toughening agent, end-capping agent and filler,            stepwise heating dried mixture in coating extruder,            passing via cooling roller, pressure roller and            traction roller and extruding.
AU YANG Q
   GAO T
   DANG G
   FENG C
AE JILIN JINYUAN NORTH TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LT (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 2021C0366L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for smart processing of carbon dioxide                copolymer film, involves (a) blending carbon                dioxide copolymer raw material with a toughening                agent, an end-capping agent and a filler, uniformly                mixing and stirring by stirrer, and uniformly                stirring for 8 minutes at 55-67degreesC, (b) adding                the stirred mixture to an oven and drying the                mixture at 55-65degreesC for 4-6 hours, (c) passing                the dried mixture to a coating extruder,                sequentially passing through feeding section,                plasticizing section, melting section and forming                section, and heating mixed material in first three                sections step-by-step heating manner, cooling                fourth section and forming through combined action                of cooling roller, pressure roller and traction                roller, and extruding carbon dioxide copolymer film                from die opening.
   USE - Method for smart processing of carbon dioxide                copolymer film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method accurately adjusts the temperature,                humidity and pressure of carbon dioxide copolymers                of different molecular weight to form carbon                dioxide copolymer film of excellent quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The addition amount                  of toughening agent, end-capping agent and filler                  in step (a) is 2-4 wt.%, 0.01-0.85 wt.% and                  0.1-0.5 wt.%, respectively based on weight of                  carbon dioxide copolymer raw material. The                  moisture content of dried mixture is less than or                  equal to 0.05%, and the air volume is more than                  0.056 m2/min/n.Preferred Conditions: The environmental                  temperature of coating extruder is 16-28degreesC,                  and the humidity is 45 plus minus 5%. The coating                  extruder adopts a programmable logic                  controller-programmable control system, and has a                  barrel that is equipped with a water circulation                  control system to ensure the accuracy of                  temperature control of raw material in barrel,                  and the mold is equipped with temperature                  automatic adjustment system and detection                  equipment, according to actual thickness of film                  exiting the die. The heating temperature of each                  zone and gap of die opening are adjusted to                  ensure the uniformity of the film thickness. The                  coating extruder stretches and cools the material                  into film under the action of traction,                  extrusion, and cooling in the forming section.                  The pressure of pressure roller during extrusion                  is 5-6 Pa, the pressure of traction roller is 4-5                  Pa, the gap between die opening is 0.05-5 mum,                  the water temperature in cooling roll is                  30-50degreesC, and the distance from die opening                  to contact line of cooling roll and pressure roll                  is 50-100 mm. The thickness of carbon dioxide                  copolymer film is 0.02 plus minus 0.04                  microns.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The dough manufacturing step is made by mixing 10- 300 pts. wt. organic mixture relative to 100 pts. wt. of the solid mixture. The dry firing is performed by drying molded body manufactured through the molding step at a temperature of 80-150 degrees C and then firing at a temperature of 400-900 degrees C. PN CN110376091-A; CN110376091-B
TI Expanded tobacco shred carbon dioxide adsorption amount measuring method, involves obtaining intersection point coordinate based on intersection loss value, and obtaining weight-loss value to obtain carbon dioxide expanded tobacco shred.
AU CHEN D
   WANG D
   HONG W
   LI H
   ZENG Q
   LUO D
   QUE W
   CHANG M
   WANG R
   JIANG H
   LI J
   HUANG X
   QIU Y
AE CHINA TOBACCO FUJIAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 2019901861
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining thermogravimetic curve of an expanded tobacco shred, where the thermogravimetic curve is curve of a weight value of expanded tobacco shred change over time. Loss value of the expanded tobacco shred change is obtained. Inflection points are obtained along coordinate direction of the curve. An inflection line section is considered as a straight line. Straight line value is obtained. Intersection point coordinate is obtained based on an intersection loss value. Final weight-loss value is obtained to obtain carbon dioxide expanded tobacco shred.
   USE - Expanded tobacco shred carbon dioxide adsorption amount measuring method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables accurately measuring expanded cut tobacco in carbon dioxide adsorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) an Expanded tobacco shred carbon dioxide adsorption amount measuring device
   (2) a computer-readable storage medium has a set of instructions for measuring expanded tobacco shred carbon dioxide adsorption amount.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating an expanded tobacco shred carbon dioxide adsorption amount measuring method. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

TI Cultivation installation for growing plant, in which supply of carbon dioxide is stopped, when drainage volume detected by drainage volume sensor does not reach setting amount.
AU NAKADA J
   TADA M
AE ISEKI AGRIC MACH MFG CO LTD (ISEN-C)
GA 2019261863
AB    NOVELTY - The installation has a cultivation apparatus (2) that is provided for performing supply and drainage liquid of a nutrient solution within a cultivation room, and growing a plant. A carbon-dioxide supply apparatus (23) is used to supply the carbon dioxide. A transpiration detection unit (51) is used to detect the state of a transpiration of a plant. A drainage liquid amount sensor (26a) is used to detect the amount of drainage liquid of the cultivation apparatus. The transpiration detecting unit is set to detect a state in which transpiration of the plant is not active, while the carbon dioxide supply device supplies carbon dioxide. The supply of carbon dioxide is stopped, when the drainage volume which the drainage volume sensor detects does not reach setting amount.
   USE - Cultivation installation for growing plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The supply of carbon dioxide can be stopped appropriately.

PT P
PN WO2020049586-A1; IN201821021206-A
TI Preparing standardized carbon dioxide extract of Azadirachta indica leaves, by powdering, and subjecting powder to extraction, separating extractives, subjecting, and collecting ethyl alcohol with extract, and combining extracts.
AU SOMAN G S
AE NISARGA BIOTECH PRIVATE LTD (NISA-Non-standard)
GA 2020211564
AB    NOVELTY - Process for preparing standardized carbon dioxide extract of Azadirachta indica leaves involves (a) powdering the clean and matured dried A.indica leaves having moisture content less than 12% to obtain powder with fine particles having size of 0.42 mm or less, (b) subjecting the powder of step (a) to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, (c) separating the carbon dioxide extractives at a pressure varying 40-65 Bar at a temperature lower than the extraction temperature to obtain extract A, (d) subjecting the remaining residual powder after separating extract A to further extraction using mixture of carbon dioxide and ethyl alcohol, (e) collecting the ethyl alcohol with extract from separator, followed by vacuum distillation of ethanol to obtain extract B, and (f) combining extract A and B to obtain standardized carbon dioxide extract of A.indica leaves.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing standardized carbon dioxide extract of A.indica leaves, which is used for treating cancer, preferably oral or colon cancer.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enables robust leaf extract without compromising on heat sensitive phytoconstituents and exhibits excellent antitumor activity against oral and colon cancers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for preparing standardized carbon dioxide extract of Azadirachta indica leaves involves (a) powdering the clean and matured dried A.indica leaves having moisture content less than 12% to obtain powder with fine particles having size of 0.42 mm or less, (b) subjecting the powder of step (a) to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at a pressure varying 80 Bar (80 kg/cm2) and 350 Bar (350 kg/cm2) at a temperature of 31-45 degrees C at a flow rate of 10-40 kg carbon dioxide per kg of raw material, (c) separating the carbon dioxide extractives at a pressure varying 40-65 Bar at a temperature lower than the extraction temperature to obtain extract A, (d) subjecting the remaining residual powder after separating extract A to further extraction using mixture of carbon dioxide and ethyl alcohol at the pressure of 80-350 Bar and at a temperature of 31-45 degrees C, (e) collecting the ethyl alcohol with extract from separator by reducing the solvent pressure of 40-65 Bar at a temperature lower than the extraction temperature, followed by vacuum distillation of ethanol to obtain extract B, and (f) combining extract A and B to obtain standardized carbon dioxide extract of A.indica leaves. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: (1) a standardized carbon dioxide extract of A.indica comprising 3 mg/g nimbolide, 130 mu g/g nimbin and 200 mu g salinin; and (2) a herbal composition comprising 50-300 mg standardized carbon dioxide extract of A.indica along with one or more pharmaceutical carriers/excipients.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The extract A is optionally subjected to high velocity micro-jet or nozzle to get a particle size of 10-100 nm. The separation temperature in step (c) and collection temperature in step (e) is maintained at 10-30 degrees C. The vacuum distillation of ethanol is carried at temperature 45 degrees C or less. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide comprises 3 to 10% ethyl alcohol. The standardized extract obtained in step (f) comprises 3 mg/g nimbolide, 130 mu g/g nimbin and 200 mu g/g salinin. The extract further comprises various other active phytoconstituents such as desacetylnimbin, azadiradione, azadirone, nimbolin, and nimbinene in minor amounts. The extract comprises 3 mg/g nimbolide, 130 mu g/g nimbin and 200 mu g/g salinin along with other active phytoconstituents, desacetylnimbin, azadiradione, azadirone, nimbolin, and nimbinene in minor amounts. The excipients/carriers are chosen from distilled water, saline, aqueous glucose solution, alcohol (e.g. ethanol), surfactants, propylene glycol, Tween-80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80) and polyethylene glycol, and oily carriers such as various animal and vegetable oils, white soft paraffin, paraffin, wax, glucose, fructose, sucrose, maltose, yellow dextrin, malt dextrin, white dextrin, aerosol, microcrystalline cellulose, calcium stearate, magnesium stearate, sorbitol, stevioside, corn syrup, lactose, citric acid, tartaric acid, malic acid, succinic acid, lactic acid, L-ascorbic acid, dl- alpha -tocopherol, glycerin, propylene glycol, glycerin fatty ester, polyglycerine fatty ester, sucrose fatty ester, sorbitan fatty ester, propylene glycol fatty ester, Acacia, carrageenan, casein, gelatin, pectin, agar, vitamin B group, nicotinamide, calcium pantothenate, amino acids, aerated or fumed silica, calcium salts, pigments, flavors and preservatives.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The composition in the form of solid or liquid is administered by oral route (claimed) at a dose of 50-300 mg twice or thrice a day.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

PT P
PN CN107675069-A; CN107675069-B
TI Smelting of high-manganese steel involves blowing            carbon dioxide and oxygen mixed gas during            decarburization of high-manganese steel.
AU LIU J
   LIU H
   SHEN Y
   DING H
   XU H
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 201814491U
AB    NOVELTY - Smelting of high-manganese steel involves                blowing mixed gas of carbon dioxide and oxygen at                0.5-3 Nm3/minute.ton, during decarburization of the                high-manganese steel. The content of carbon dioxide                in the mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and                oxygen is 50% or less.
   USE - Smelting of high-manganese steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient smelting of                high-manganese steel. By using carbon dioxide                instead of oxygen, the volatilization of manganese                in the steel due to the excessively high local                heating caused by the reaction of oxygen and carbon                in the steel is suppressed. Carbon dioxide has                weaker oxidizing property as compared with oxygen                gas, and can reduce the oxidation loss of manganese                oxide, thus reducing the production cost of                high-manganese steel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The high-manganese                  molten steel generated during the smelting has                  carbon content of 3-5.5% and manganese content of                  2% or more.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); M27 (Ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).)
MC M24-C06; M27-A01; M27-A01C; M27-A01J; M27-A04

PT P
PN CN114433059-A
TI Catalyst useful for catalyzing carbon dioxide            hydrogenation to synthesize light olefin complex,            prepared by mixing active components zinc            oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide with            silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve for carrying out            catalytic carbon dioxide hydrogenation.
AU WANG J
   LIU S
   TIAN D
   MEN Y
AE UNIV SHANGHAI ENG & TECHNOLOGY (USES-C)
GA 202266332P
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst, prepared by mixing active components                zinc oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide with                silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve (SAPO) for                carrying out catalytic carbon dioxide                hydrogenation, where the silicoaluminophosphate                molecular sieve SAPO comprises molecular sieves                SAPO-20, SAPO-34 and /or SAPO-44, preferably                SAPO-34, the silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve                SAPO is molecular sieve with silicon                dioxide/aluminum oxide molar ratio of 0.05-0.65,                preferably 0.05-0.45, the mass ratio of component                zinc oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide to                silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve SAPO is 1-5,                preferably 2-3, the zinc oxide/lithium-zirconium                dioxide is obtained by loading zinc oxide on oxide                lithium-zirconium dioxide prepared by                co-precipitation method, where lithium exists in                state of lithium oxide), the molar ratio of                lithium: zirconium in lithium-zirconium dioxide is                5:95-40:60, preferably 5 :95-30:70, is                claimed.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalyzing carbon                dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize light olefin                complex (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: is economical; and has                dual-function, high the carbon dioxide conversion                rate about 28.56%, high yield of light olefins                about 8.77%, and low selectivity of carbon monoxide                and methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst, prepared by mixing active components                zinc oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide with                silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve (SAPO) for                carrying out catalytic carbon dioxide                hydrogenation, where the silicoaluminophosphate                molecular sieve SAPO comprises molecular sieves                SAPO-20, SAPO-34 and /or SAPO-44, preferably                SAPO-34, the silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve                SAPO is molecular sieve with silicon                dioxide/aluminum oxide molar ratio of 0.05-0.65,                preferably 0.05-0.45, the mass ratio of component                zinc oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide to                silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve SAPO is 1-5,                preferably 2-3, the zinc oxide/lithium-zirconium                dioxide is obtained by loading zinc oxide on oxide                lithium-zirconium dioxide prepared by                co-precipitation method, where lithium exists in                state of lithium oxide), the molar ratio of                lithium: zirconium in lithium-zirconium dioxide is                5:95-40:60, preferably 5 :95-30:70, and mass ratio                of zinc oxide to final product zinc                oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide is 2-16%,                preferably 8-13, is claimed.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                of catalyst for catalyzing carbon dioxide                hydrogenation to synthesize light olefin complex,                comprising taking the mixed gas containing hydrogen                and carbon dioxide as raw materials in a volume                ratio of hydrogen: carbon dioxide =1-4, preferably                2-3, which has a catalytic reaction temperature of                320&#8451;-420&#8451;, preferably                360-400&#176;C, the reaction pressure of 1-5                MPa, preferably 2-4 MPa, and gas phase space                velocity of 1500-12000 ml/g/hour, preferably                1500-9000 ml/g/hour, contacting and carrying out                the reaction with the catalyst under the conditions                of 3000-9000 ml/g/hour to generate light olefin                complex (olefins of 2 carbon-4 carbon).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mixing process of the                  components zinc oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide                  and silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve SAPO                  is carried out by mechanically mixing the                  components zinc oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide                  and silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve SAPO                  by ball milling, where the particle size of the                  mixed material is &#8804; 120 meshes, and                  granulating to obtain desired catalyst. The                  catalyst is prepared by (i) preparing carbon                  dioxide hydrogenation active component zinc                  oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide comprises (A)                  dissolving lithium and zirconium precursors in                  ethanol and aqueous solution with a volume ratio                  of 1:4-4:1, preferably 2:1-4:1, where the                  precursor of lithium comprises lithium nitrate,                  lithium carbonate, and/or lithium sulfide, the                  precursor of zirconium comprises zirconium                  oxychloride, zirconium nitrate, and/or zirconium                  hydroxide, and the lithium: zirconium molar ratio                  is 5:95-40:60, dropping aqueous ammonia to the                  above solution as a precipitant until pH value                  8-11, preferably 9-10 at room temperature, where                  the mass concentration of ammonia is 25-28%,                  stirring the resulting solution room temperature                  for 1-5 hours, preferably 1-2 hours, washing with                  deionized water, drying at 60-110&#176;C,                  preferably 60-80&#176;C for 12-24 hours, and                  calcining at 350-500&#8451;, preferably                  350-400&#8451; for 2-6 hours, preferably 3-4                  hours to obtain carrier lithium-zirconium                  dioxide, where the Li exists in the form of                  lithium oxide, and (B) impregnating zinc nitrate                  on the lithium-zirconium dioxide carrier in an                  equal volume according to the mass ratio of zinc                  oxide accounting for the final product zinc                  oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide 2-16%, preferably                  5-15%, drying at 80-120&#8451;, preferably                  80-90&#8451; for 12-24 hours, calcining at                  350-500&#176;C, preferably 350-400&#176;C                  for 3-6 hours, preferably 3-4 hours to obtain                  zinc oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide, (ii)                  choosing SAPO-20, SAPO-34, and SAPO-44,                  preferably SAPO-34, are selected as active                  components for methanol conversion, where the                  molecular sieve SAPO is a molecular sieve with a                  silicon dioxide/aluminum oxide molar ratio of                  0.05-0.65, preferably 0.05-0.45, and (iii)                  preparing hydrogenation and methanol conversion                  bifunctional catalyst by respectively taking                  carbon dioxide hydrogenation active component                  zinc oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide obtained in                  the step (i) and methanol conversion active                  component SAPO-34 molecular sieve obtained in the                  step (ii), mechanically mixing the components                  zinc oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide and                  silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve SAPO by                  ball milling according to zinc                  oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide and molecular                  sieve SAPO mass ratio 1-5, preferably 1-3, where                  the particle size of the mixed material is                  &#8804; 120 meshes, and granulating to obtain                  desired catalyst. The granulation is carried out                  by tableting the uniformly mixed particle size,                  which is respectively &#8804; 120 meshes zinc                  oxide/lithium-zirconium dioxide and molecular                  sieve SAPO powder particles under the pressure of                  10-40MPa, preferably 30-40 MPa), crushing and                  sieving the particle size of 35-45 mesh,                  preferably 38-42 meshes to 55-65 meshes,                  preferably 58-62 meshes, and passing through a                  35-45 meshes sieve, but not the particles that                  cannot pass through a 55-65 meshes sieve to                  obtain desired catalyst. Preferred Components: In                  addition to the raw material gas containing                  hydrogen and carbon dioxide, the mixed gas does                  not contain or may contain nitrogen and/or argon                  inert atmosphere gases. The content of the inert                  atmosphere gas does not exceed 30%, preferably                  &#8804; 10% of the total volume of the mixed                  gas.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN JP2019181451-A; JP7004881-B2
TI Carbon dioxide recovery system for use in home, has take-out processing unit that is configured for taking out gaseous carbon dioxide from sodium carbonate and sodium hydrogen carbonate by chemical reaction.
AU MURAKI K
AE MURAKI K (MURA-Individual)
   CARBON RECOVERING RES AGENCY (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 201988416D
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an electrolysis processing unit (1) for electrolyzing a sodium chloride aqueous solution to generate a sodium hydroxide aqueous solution. An absorption treatment unit (2) dissolves carbon dioxide in the air into the aqueous sodium hydroxide solution to produce an aqueous solution containing sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate. The supply hydrochloric acid is generated when the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution is generated in the electrolysis processing section. A take-out processing unit (39,40) is configured for taking out gaseous carbon dioxide from the sodium carbonate and the sodium hydrogen carbonate by a chemical reaction.
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery system for use in home, office, or educational site (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide recovery system can extract and recover carbon dioxide with a simple configuration without using special energy such as thermal power.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The electrolysis processing unit is a reaction vessel divided up and down through a semipermeable membrane (16) or a cation exchange membrane. A cathode is placed in a lower space divided into upper and lower portions. An anode is placed in an upper space divided into the upper and lower sides. An openable and closable drainage path is connected to the lower side of the reaction vessel. The sodium hydroxide aqueous solution is generated in the lower space by energizing the anode and the cathode in a state where each of the lower space and the upper space is filled with a sodium chloride aqueous solution. The generated aqueous sodium hydroxide solution is configured to be discharged through the drainage path. The hydrochloric acid present in the upper space is opened by opening the semipermeable membrane or the cation exchange membrane in a state where all of the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution in the lower space is discharged. The absorption processing unit includes a container (11) for storing the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution. An air supply unit is configured for supplying the air to the aqueous sodium hydroxide solution stored in the container. The air supply unit with an air supply device that takes in and discharges the surrounding air. A pipe unit (35) is placed at one end connected to an air discharge portion of the air supply device, and has an end immersed in the stored sodium hydroxide aqueous solution. The other end of the pipe unit has multiple cuts from the tip end side. Multiple strip-shaped portions are arranged in a circumferential direction. A cut portion penetrating inside and outside is provided on a side surface on the other end side of the pipe unit. The description of sodium in the carbon dioxide recovery system is replaced with potassium. The absorption processing unit includes a self-propelled household robot.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the carbon dioxide recovery system.
   Electrolysis processing unit (1)
   Absorption treatment unit (2)
   Container (11)
   Semipermeable membrane (16)
   Pipe unit (35)
   Take-out processing units (39,40)
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)
MC E11-N; E11-Q02B; E31-B03D; E31-N05C; E33-B; J03-B02; J03-B04

PT P
PN WO2022015882-A1
TI Active carbon dioxide capture unit for capturing carbon dioxide from input gas, comprises inlet, non-aqueous region, aqueous region and anion exchange membrane.
AU SINGH M R
   PRAJAPATI A
   SARTAPE R
AE UNIV ILLINOIS FOUND (UNII-C)
GA 202213798M
AB    NOVELTY - An active carbon dioxide (CO2) capture unit (10) comprises an inlet (12) through which an input gas is introduced into the unit; a non-aqueous region (14) comprising a non-aqueous CO2 binding organic liquid containing hydroxyl (OH-)-arranged to be in contact with the input gas to chemisorb CO2 from the gas and convert the chemisorbed CO2 into hydrocarbon carbonate (HCO3-) ion by reacting with OH-; an aqueous region (16) arranged downstream of the non-aqueous region, where HCO3- reacts with H2O and decomposes CO2 and CO32-; an anion exchange membrane (18) between the non-aqueous and aqueous regions to facilitate HCO3- diffusion and migration from the non-aqueous region to the aqueous region; and a captured CO2 outlet (24) downstream of the aqueous region.
   USE - An active carbon dioxide (CO2) capture unit for system for active carbon dioxide capture and carbon dioxide reduction to a product gas (claimed). Can be used for capturing and converting carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide from sources like power plants and chemical industries and from air generally.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is fully integrated systems that combine carbon dioxide capture and conversion into a single process; is sustainable and energy-efficient; and reduces anthropogenic carbon footprint. The anion-exchange membrane is capable of capturing and converting oxygen from sources like power plants and chemical industries and even from the air generally, which can advantageously reduce the anthropogenic carbon dioxide footprint.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) system for active CO2 capture and CO2 reduction to a product gas, comprising the active CO2 capture unit, reduction unit arranged downstream of the active CO2 capture unit such that the reduction unit receives captured CO2 from the CO2 capture outlet, the reduction unit comprising reduction unit inlet for receiving the captured CO2 from the CO2 capture outlet, the reduction unit comprising a catalyst for reduction of the captured CO2 arranged such that the captured CO2 is flowed through the reduction unit into contact with the catalyst, where upon contact with the catalyst, the captured CO2 is reduced to a product gas comprising one or more of C2H4, C2H5OH, CH3COOH, CH3OH, CH4, C3H6, CO, and H2, catalyst for oxidation of H2O arranged downstream of the reduction unit and in fluid communication with the reduction unit to generate protons for CO2 reduction and O2 as a byproduct, where the catalyst for oxidation is an anode, separator arranged between the catalyst for reduction and the catalyst for oxidation, and energy source; and
   (2) capturing CO2 using the active CO2 capture unit which involves flowing the input gas into the inlet and into the non-aqueous region for chemisorbing CO2 and conversion of the CO2 to HCO3- by OH- present in the non-aqueous region, flowing the HCO3- across the anionic exchange membrane and into the aqueous region, where the flow of HCO3- is driven at least partially by a gradient of moisture across the anion exchange membrane, where upon flow in the HCO3- into the aqueous region, the HCO3- interacts with H2O present in the aqueous region and decomposes to CO2 and CO32-, and flowing the CO2 from the aqueous region to the captured CO2 outlet.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a carbon dioxide capture unit.
   Active carbon dioxide capture unit (10)
   Inlet (12)
   Non-aqueous region (14)
   Aqueous region (16)
   Anion exchange membrane (18)
   Captured carbon dioxide outlet (24)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active CO2 capture unit further comprises cathode arranged upstream of the non-aqueous region and anode arranged downstream of the aqueous region, where aqueous region comprises an aqueous electrolyte such that an electric field is adapted to be generated within the capture unit. Anode is a planar and/or porous anode. Cathode is a porous cathode. Anode is a porous anode. Input gas is an anthropogenic CO2 source. Anthropogenic CO2 source is air or flue gas. Catalyst for reduction is a copper mesh. Catalyst comprises metal nanocrystals. Metal nanocrystals comprise copper (Cu) nanocrystals. Cu nanocrystals are Cu nanocubes having dominate (100) facets. Metal nanocrystals are grown on a mesh substrate. Catalyst for oxidation comprises one or more oxides of nickel (Ni), iron-nickel (Fe-Ni), platinum (Pt)-coated Ti, Ir, and/or Ru. Preferred Properties: Copper mesh has a mesh size of 40-120 mesh. Active CO2 capture unit and reduction unit is spaced a distance of 5-20 mm. Preferred Device: Energy source comprises a photocell and/or electrochemical cell. The system further comprises an outlet for the O2 byproduct arranged spatially separated from an outlet for the product gas. Preferred Processes: 33. The method of claim 32, further comprising flowing H20 into the cathode for decomposition of the H20 into H2 and OH-, and flowing the OH- into the non-aqueous region. The method further comprises applying an electric field across the anion exchange membrane to increase a rate of transfer of HCO3- across the anion exchange membrane. Preferred Condition: Input gas has a temperature of 20-120 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Non-aqueous CO2 binding organic liquid comprises an ionic liquid. Ionic liquid is imidazolium based or phosphonium based. CO2 binding organic liquid comprises one or more ionic liquids selected from choline hydroxide, tetrabutylphosphonium methanesulfonate, and 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate. Non-aqueous CO2 binding organic liquid comprises a non-aqueous polar organic solvent. Non-aqueous polar organic solvent comprises one or more alcohols, organic amidine bases, and guanidine bases. Organic solvent comprises one or more of ethylene glycol, methanol, and ethanol. Non-aqueous region comprises an alkali metal hydroxide dissolved in the non-aqueous CO2 binding organic liquid. Aqueous region comprises one or both of an aqueous electrolyte and water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Anion exchange membrane comprises a hollow-fiber structure. Anionic exchange membrane comprises quaternary amines or phosphonium attached to a polymer backbone resin with hydroxide, carbonate, and/or bicarbonate moieties. Polymer backbone is polystyrene.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN US2021079315-A1; US11021675-B2
TI Producing refined oil product, by extracting biomass using high-pressure solvent stream, mixing oil extract dissolved in solvent with medium-pressure solvent stream, and delivering diluted oil extract to medium-pressure separator column.
AU CHORDIA L
   RAMESH S
AE THAR PROCESS INC (THAR-Non-standard)
   CHORDIA L (CHOR-Individual)
   RAMESH S (RAME-Individual)
GA 2021280004
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing a refined oil product from a biomass, involves (a) extracting a biomass using a high-pressure solvent stream to provide a first oil extract dissolved in solvent, (b) mixing the first oil extract dissolved in solvent with a medium-pressure solvent stream to provide a diluted oil extract, and (c) delivering the diluted oil extract to a medium-pressure separator column to separate contaminants from the diluted oil extract and provide a dilute refined oil.
   USE - The process is useful for producing a refined oil product from a biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enhances separation efficiency, increases yield of refined oils, reduces or eliminates need for alcohol-based solvents in post processing, eliminates time and labor of a conventional winterization process, and is safe and suitable for large commercial scale system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) A process for producing a refined oil product from a biomass, involves (a) extracting a biomass using a carbon dioxide stream to provide a first oil extract dissolved in carbon dioxide at 225-450 K with pressure of 37-750 bar, (b) mixing the first oil extract dissolved in carbon dioxide with a medium pressure carbon dioxide stream to provide a diluted oil extract, where the medium pressure carbon dioxide stream is provided at a temperature of 225-380 K with a pressure of 35-225 bar, and (c) delivering the diluted oil extract to a medium pressure separator column to separate contaminants from the diluted oil extract and provide a dilute refined oil, where the medium pressure separator column is maintained at a separation temperature of 225-380 K and a separation pressure of 35-225 bar, and the process uses only carbon dioxide as solvent and does not include any other solvents; and
   (2) A process for producing a refined oil product from a Cannabis plant material, involves (i) extracting the Cannabis plant material using a high-pressure carbon dioxide stream to provide a first oil extract dissolved in carbon dioxide, where the extraction is at an extraction temperature of 225-450 K with an extraction pressure of 37-750 bar, (ii) mixing the first oil extract dissolved in carbon dioxide with a medium-pressure carbon dioxide stream to provide a diluted oil extract, where the medium-pressure carbon dioxide stream is provided at a temperature of 225-380 K and a pressure of 35-225 bar in a volume of at least 10 (volume/volume)% to a total volume of the first oil extract dissolved in CO2, (iii) delivering the diluted oil extract to a medium-pressure separator column to separate contaminants from the diluted oil extract and provide a dilute refined oil, and (iv) delivering the dilute refined oil to a low-pressure separator column to evaporate the carbon dioxide and provide a refined oil product, where the refined oil product is enriched for cannabidiols, and substantially free of waxes, lipids, oleoresins, and fatty acids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process further involves delivering the dilute refined oil to a low-pressure separator column to evaporate the solvent and provide a refined oil product. The process removes at least 95% of the contaminants from the diluted oil extract, where the contaminants comprise waxes, lipids, oleoresins and/or fatty acids. The extracting the biomass is at an extraction pressure is 0.5-10 times the critical pressure of the solvent. The extraction temperature is 0.75-1.5 times the critical temperature of the solvent. The medium-pressure solvent stream is provided at a pressure of 0.5-3 times the critical pressure of the solvent, and a temperature of 0.75-1.25 times the critical temperature of the solvent. The medium-pressure solvent stream is provided at subcritical temperature and about critical pressure. The diluted oil extract is maintained on the medium-pressure separator column at a pressure of 0.5-3 times the critical pressure of the solvent and a temperature of 0.75-1.25 times the critical temperature of the solvent. The dilute refined oil is maintained on the low-pressure separator column at a pressure of 0.4-2 times the critical pressure of the solvent and a temperature of 0.9-1.25 times the critical temperature of the solvent. The low-pressure separator column is maintained at subcritical pressure and subcritical temperature to evaporate the solvent. The process further involves delivering the dilute refined oil to a low-pressure separator column to evaporate carbon dioxide and provide a refined oil product, where the low-pressure separator column is maintained at a temperature 279-380K and a pressure of 30-150 bar.Preferred Components: The solvent is carbon dioxide. The biomass comprises a botanical plant material. The botanical plant material comprises Cannabis. The refined oil product is enriched for cannabidiols. The refined oil product is substantially free of waxes, lipids, oleoresins, fatty acids, and totally free of hydrocarbon and alcohol solvents.Preferred Composition: The medium-pressure solvent stream is provided in a volume of at least 5 (volume/volume)% to a total volume of the first oil extract dissolved in the solvent.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

General compositions.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
