PN US2019185887-A1; US10619173-B2
TI Producing a fuel or fuel intermediate containing carbon derived from non-fossil organic material comprises e.g. converting biogenic carbon dioxide sourced from a production process and hydrogen from stream enriched in hydrogen and arranging.
AU FOODY P J
   FOODY B
AE IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
GA 201951678K
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a fuel or fuel intermediate containing carbon derived from non-fossil organic material comprises: (i) providing biogenic carbon dioxide that is sourced from a production process comprising at least one of a step of fermentation and a thermal process; (ii) providing a stream enriched in hydrogen that is sourced from a hydrogen production process, the hydrogen production process including a step of removing fossil carbon dioxide from a stream including hydrogen to provide the steam enriched in hydrogen; (iii) converting the biogenic carbon dioxide and hydrogen from the stream enriched in hydrogen to a second product, the second product comprising at least one of a biogenic carbon-based fuel and a biogenic carbon-based fuel intermediate; and (iv) carrying out or arranging.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a fuel or fuel intermediate containing carbon derived from non-fossil organic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods increase product yield and produce more fuel or fuel intermediate at lower cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing a fuel or fuel intermediate containing carbon derived from non-fossil organic material comprises: (i) providing biogenic carbon dioxide that is sourced from a production process comprising at least one of a step of fermentation and a thermal process, where the production process produces a first product derived from the non-fossil organic material, the first product is (a) an energy product and/or (b) a biogenic carbon-based product selected from a chemical product, a fuel and a fuel intermediate, and the biogenic carbon dioxide is generated during the production process; (ii) providing a stream enriched in hydrogen that is sourced from a hydrogen production process, the hydrogen production process including a step of removing fossil carbon dioxide from a stream including hydrogen to provide the steam enriched in hydrogen; (iii) converting the biogenic carbon dioxide and hydrogen from the stream enriched in hydrogen to a second product, the second product comprising at least one of a biogenic carbon-based fuel and a biogenic carbon-based fuel intermediate where the converting comprises subjecting at least a portion of the biogenic carbon dioxide and hydrogen to a reverse water gas shift reaction to produce carbon monoxide and water, and where the carbon monoxide so produced, biogenic carbon dioxide, and hydrogen are fed to a biologic or chemical conversion process to produce the second product; and (iv) carrying out or arranging for at least one parties to carry out at least one step that contributes to a reduction in the life cycle greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions of the second product. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing (II) ethanol containing carbon derived from non-fossil organic material comprising (i) fermenting sugar from the non-fossil organic material to produce a fuel, where biogenic carbon dioxide is produced during the fermenting, (ii) collecting the biogenic carbon dioxide produced during the fermenting to produce collected biogenic carbon dioxide, (iii) providing hydrogen, (iv) combining at least a portion of the collected biogenic carbon dioxide and the hydrogen to produce a combined stream and (v) converting biogenic carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the combined stream to the ethanol, where the converting (v) comprises subjecting at least a portion of the biogenic carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the combined stream to a reverse water gas shift reaction to produce carbon monoxide and water, and where the carbon monoxide so produced, biogenic carbon dioxide, and hydrogen are introduced to a biologic conversion process to produce the ethanol; and
   (2) producing (III) a fuel or fuel intermediate containing carbon derived from non-fossil organic material comprising providing biogenic carbon dioxide that is sourced from a production process comprising a step of fermentation, where the biogenic carbon dioxide is generated during the step of fermentation, providing hydrogen and converting the biogenic carbon dioxide and hydrogen to a second product, the second product comprising biogenic carbon where the converting comprises subjecting at least a portion of the biogenic carbon dioxide and hydrogen to a reverse water gas shift reaction to produce carbon monoxide and water, and where the carbon monoxide so produced, biogenic carbon dioxide, and hydrogen are fed to a biologic or chemical conversion process to produce the second product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The one step is: introducing at least a portion of fossil carbon dioxide recovered from at least one streams comprising fossil carbon dioxide generated during the hydrogen production process into an apparatus for transporting carbon dioxide, withdrawing carbon dioxide from the apparatus and introducing the withdrawn carbon dioxide underground, and using at least a portion of the first product to displace the use or production of a corresponding fossil-based product, where the first product is selected from the chemical product and the energy product. The process (II) comprises generating or causing the generation of a fuel credit, generating or causing the generation of a biofuel credit for each of the first and the second products, where each of the first and second products is ethanol. The life cycle GHG emissions are at least 50% lower than a gasoline baseline as measured by EPA methodology. The conversion process of step (iii) is a biological conversion of the carbon monoxide, biogenic carbon dioxide and the hydrogen to ethanol by a bacterium from the genus Clostridium. The process (II) further comprises: (i) dividing the combined stream comprising the collected carbon dioxide and the hydrogen into a first stream and a second stream, each stream comprising biogenic carbon dioxide and hydrogen; (ii) subjecting the first stream comprising the biogenic carbon dioxide and the hydrogen to the reverse water gas shift reaction to produce a stream comprising the carbon monoxide and water; and (iii) combining at least the carbon monoxide so produced in the reverse water gas shift reaction with the second stream to produce a second combined stream comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, where the second combined stream is fed to the biologic conversion process that produces the ethanol. Preferred Components: The biogenic carbon dioxide is sourced from a production process comprising a step of fermentation. Each of the first and the second products comprises an alcohol where the alcohol is methanol or ethanol. The first product comprises ethanol. The second product comprises one of ethanol and acetic acid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PN US2020246746-A1; US10940433-B2
TI System used to capture carbon dioxide, comprises carbonation reactor comprising mixture of reaction materials, first port for receiving reaction materials, second port and grinding mechanism, and calcination reactor comprising first port.
AU MENDOZA E Y M
   DROZD V
   DURYGIN A
   SAXENA S K
AE UNIV FLORIDA (UYFL-C)
   MENDOZA E Y M (MEND-Individual)
   DROZD V (DROZ-Individual)
   DURYGIN A (DURY-Individual)
   SAXENA S K (SAXE-Individual)
GA 202074966Q
AB    NOVELTY - System (S1) comprises (a) a carbonation reactor comprising a mixture of reaction materials, a first port for receiving the reaction materials, a second port for receiving a stream of gaseous carbon dioxide, and a grinding mechanism inside the carbonation reactor, the reaction materials reacting with carbon dioxide to form carbonates, where the first port is located at the upper end of the carbonation reactor and the second port is located at the lower end of the carbonation reactor, and (b) a calcination reactor comprising a first port for receiving carbonates produced in the carbonation reactor, where the carbonates are decomposed in the calcination reactor at room temperature conditions to reproduce the reaction materials, and a second port for releasing and recycling the reproduced reaction materials back to the carbonation reactor.
   USE - The system is useful for capturing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a system (S2) comprising a carbonation reactor comprising (1) a mixture of reaction materials comprising water and at least one of (i) magnetite and iron or (ii) magnetite and carbon, (2) a first port for receiving the reaction materials, where the first port is located at the upper end of the carbonation reactor, (3) a second port for receiving a stream of gaseous carbon dioxide, where the second port is located at the lower end of the carbonation reactor, and (4) a grinding mechanism inside the carbonation reactor, where the reaction materials react with carbon dioxide to form siderite, and a calcination reactor comprising a first port for receiving siderite produced in the carbonation reactor, the siderite being decomposed in the calcination reactor at room temperature conditions to reproduce magnetite and iron, or magnetite and carbon, and a second port for releasing and recycling magnetite and iron, or magnetite and carbon back to the carbonation reactor; and
   (2) a system (S3) comprising a reactor comprising a mixture of reaction materials, a first port for receiving the reaction materials, where the first port is located at the upper end of the carbonation reactor, a second port for receiving a stream of gaseous carbon dioxide, where the second port is located at the lower end of the carbonation reactor, a grinding mechanism inside the reactor, where the reaction materials react with carbon dioxide to form carbonates, a sonication unit on the wall of the reactor, where the sonication unit comprises at least one ultrasonic transducer, and a third port for obtaining carbonates.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reaction materials comprise: magnetite, hematite, wustite and/or goethite; and (i) magnetite and iron or (ii) magnetite and carbon. The reaction materials further comprise a reducing agent including metallic iron or carbon.

PN US2019291063-A1; WO2019183528-A1; CA3094748-A1; AU2019239331-A1; CN112165991-A; EP3768434-A1; EP3768434-A4; US11298664-B2
TI Nozzle used in carbonation system for producing carbonated beverages, comprises metal tip defining conduit comprising 1st portion defined by 1st interior surface of metal tip, and 2nd portion defined by 2nd interior surface of metal tip.
AU GAO Q
AE BEDFORD SYSTEMS LLC (BEDF-Non-standard)
   BEDFORD SYSTEMS LLC (BEDF-Non-standard)
   BEDFORD SYSTEMS LLC (BEDF-Non-standard)
   BEDFORD SYSTEMS LLC (BEDF-Non-standard)
GA 201982126P
AB    NOVELTY - Nozzle (100) comprises a metal tip (102) having a 1st end (104) and a 2nd end (106), the metal tip defining a conduit (108) extending from the 1st end towards the 2nd end. The conduit comprises a 1st portion (110) defined by at least one 1st interior surface of the metal tip, the 1st interior surface extending from the 1st end, the 1st portion exhibiting a substantially constant diameter of 0.35-0.41 mm and a length of 0.17-0.23 mm; and a 2nd portion (112) defined by at least one 2nd interior surface of the metal tip, the 2nd interior surface extending from the 1st interior surface. The 2nd portion exhibits a diameter that increases with distance from the 1st portion.
   USE - The nozzzle is useful in carbonation system for producing carbonated beverages; and for carbonating a liquid (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nozzle can improve the efficiency of the carbonation system because the metal tip is configured to emit carbon dioxide bubbles exhibiting a relatively small average diameter. The relatively small average diameter of the carbon dioxide bubbles emitted from the metal tip increases the total surface area of the carbon dioxide bubbles. Icreasing the total surface area of the carbon dioxide bubbles increases the rate at which the carbon dioxide bubbles are dissolved in water and, in turn, increases the efficiency of the carbonation system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a carbonation system configured to produce carbonated beverages, comprising a coupler configured to be coupled to a gas source and configured to receive a gas from the gas source; a water reservoir configured to hold water, the water reservoir comprising an inlet (116) and an outlet; a carbonation head fluidly coupled to the coupler; and the nozzle disposed in the water reservoir and coupled to the carbonation head, the nozzle configured to dispense the gas received by the coupler into the water reservoir, and
   (2) carbonating a liquid, which involves flowing carbon dioxide through a conduit of a metal tip of the nozzle; dispensing the carbon dioxide from the 1st portion of the metal tip as bubbles into water; and dissolving the carbon dioxide in the water, thus forming carbonated water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic cross-sectional view of metal tip of nozzle.
   Nozzle (100)
   Metal tip (102)
   1st end (104)
   2nd end (106)
   Conduit (108)
   1st portion (110)
   2nd portion (112)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The method further involves, before flowing carbon dioxide through the conduit of the metal tip of the nozzle, flowing the carbon dioxide through an elongated channel (134) of a plastic stem of the nozzle, the plastic stem coupled to the metal tip. It also involves releasing the carbonated water toward a dispenser of a beverage machine. The operation of flowing occurs within the beverage machine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The metal tip comprises stainless steel.

PT P
PN CN110065943-A; CN110065943-B
TI Coal gasification separation carbon dioxide device has overflow tanks that are provided for outputting oxygen carrier reducing materials and are connected with air reactor for oxidizing reducing materials to regenerate oxygen carriers.
AU CHEN S
   ZHANG X
   XIANG W
   SUN C
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 201968134L
AB    NOVELTY - A coal gasification separation carbon dioxide device has a moving bed reactor that is connected to the upper end through a gasification reactor transition section. The lower end of the mixed gasification reactor is provided with a feeder for introducing materials. The overflow tank (D) and overflow tank (C) are provided for introducing oxygen carrier (A) and oxygen carrier (B), and are connected to the sides of the mixed gasification reactor and the moving bed reactor. The overflow tank (B) and overflow tank (A) are provided for outputting oxygen carrier reducing material (A) and oxygen carrier reducing material (B). The overflow tank (B) and overflow tank (A) are connected with air reactor for oxidizing oxygen carrier reducing material (A) and oxygen carrier reducing material (B) to regenerate oxygen carrier (A) and oxygen carrier (B).
   USE - Coal gasification separation carbon dioxide device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device only needs to use the condensation method to separate the carbon dioxide and the water vapor. The consumption of large amount of energy consumption required is prevented. The oxygen carrier reduction materials are recycled in device to realize the cycle regeneration. The device has low operating costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for separating carbon dioxide, which involves (1) adding coal to mixed gasification reactor through feeder, introducing water vapor, and performing gasifying reaction with coal to form synthesis gas having main component of hydrogen and carbon monoxide, (2) reacting synthesis gas hydrogen and carbon monoxide with oxygen carrier (A) from overflow tank (D) to obtain oxygen carrier reduction material (A), passing gaseous products such as water, carbon dioxide and unreacted carbon monoxide and hydrogen to the moving bed reactor through the gasification reactor transition section, (3) passing oxygen carrier reducing material to the air reactor through overflow tank (B), introducing into the air, and reacting oxygen carrier reducing material (A) with oxygen to form the oxygen carrier (A), (4) reacting carbon monoxide and hydrogen passed to moving bed reactor with oxygen carrier (B) from overflow tank (C), passing generated oxygen carrier reducing material (B) to the air reaction through overflow tank (A), passing air, reacting oxygen carrier reducing material (B) with oxygen to form oxygen carrier (B), and (5) discharging generated gaseous products such as carbon dioxide and water through the outlet of the moving bed reactor, and separating water by condensation to obtain pure carbon dioxide.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC E11-Q01B; E31-N05C; H09-C
IP C01B-032/50; C10J-003/56

PT P
PN JP2019000090-A
TI Managing fish and shellfish in anesthetic state to prevent sudden death of fish and shellfish, by adjusting carbon dioxide and oxygen concentration in water tank containing anaesthetized fish and shellfish to predetermined range.
AU SEKIYAMA M
   OMORI M
   WATANABE S
   YOSHIDA Y
AE NIKKEN LEASE KOGYO KK (NIKK-Non-standard)
GA 2019044469
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) of managing fish and shellfish in an anesthetic state, involves adjusting carbon dioxide concentration and oxygen concentration in the water tank containing the anaesthetized fish and shellfish to a predetermined range for promoting the reaction of ammonia to ammonium ion in the water tank.
   USE - The methods are useful for managing fish and shellfish in an anesthetized state to prevent the sudden death of the fish and shellfish in the anesthesia state and transporting the fish and shellfish (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces toxic components in water tank, promotes the reaction of ammonia to ammonium ion with low toxicity, suppresses the generation of ammonia caused by the decomposition of protein, exhibits easy maintenance of carbon dioxide concentration, and contributes to long-term maintenance, long distance transportation in anesthesia state, and improvement of transportation efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an anesthesia maintenance device for fish and shellfish, comprising (a) a main body portion for inserting anesthetized fish and shellfish, (b) an oxygen supply unit for maintaining the oxygen concentration in the main body portion at 60% or more, and (c) an aeration unit for performing aeration to maintain the concentration of carbon dioxide in the main body portion at 40 ppm or less;
   (2) an anesthesia maintenance system for fish and shellfish, comprising (a) an anesthesia tank for transferring fish and shellfish to an anesthetic state, and (b) a maintenance tank for transporting fish and shellfish while maintaining the anesthetic state of the anesthetized fish and shellfish in the anesthesia tank;
   (3) a method (M2) for maintaining the anesthesia state of fish and shellfish, involving (a) anesthetizing the fish and shellfish in a water tank having carbon dioxide concentration of 65-85 ppm and oxygen concentration of 60% or more, and (b) maintaining the fish and shellfish that have transitioned to the anesthesia state in step (a) in water tank having carbon dioxide concentration of 40 ppm or less and oxygen concentration of 60% or more; and
   (4) a method (M3) for transporting the fish and shellfish while maintaining in the anesthetic state, involving (a) performing the step (a) of the method (M2), (b) maintaining the carbon dioxide concentration at 40 ppm or less and the oxygen concentration at 60-100%, and (c) transporting the fish and shellfish while maintaining in the anesthesia state and lowering the concentration of carbon dioxide in the water tank at the transport destination to awaken the fish and shellfish.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In method (M1), the carbon dioxide concentration is adjusted by performing aeration in the water tank. In method (M2), the water in the anesthesia tank is diverted for part or all of the water in the maintenance tank. Preferred Apparatus: The maintenance device further comprises (d) a protein skimmer for removing impurities in the main body portion, and (e) a storage box for inserting the fish and shellfish into the main body portion in a state where the fish and shellfish is stored tightly. One of the aeration unit and the oxygen supply unit serves as the other. The oxygen supply unit maintains the oxygen concentration in the main body portion at 60-100%. The mode of the particle diameter of the bubbles supplied from the oxygen supply unit or the aeration unit is 100 mu m or more. Preferred System: The carbon dioxide concentration in the maintenance tank is lower than the carbon dioxide concentration in the anesthesia tank. The maintenance tank maintains the carbon dioxide concentration at 30 ppm or less and the oxygen concentration at 80% or more.
DC B06 (Inorganics - including fluorides for toothpastes etc.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); P14 (Animal management and care (A01K, L, M).)
MC B05-C01; B05-C04; B05-C08; B11-C06; B14-C07; B14-S12; C05-C01; C05-C04; C05-C08; C11-C06; C14-C07; C14-S12; P14-A01A; P14-A99; P14-E01C; P14-E01F
IP A01K-063/02; A61K-033/00; A61P-023/00

PT P
PN CN108126054-A
TI Extracting total flavonoids from Guangxi golden camellia, involves drying and crushing Guangxi golden camellia leaves, adding potassium sulfate powder, ultra-fine grinding, sieving, extracting, collecting and purifying product.
AU CHEN J
AE GUANGXI TIANCHANG INVESTMENT CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201846947H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting total flavonoids from Guangxi golden camellia, involves (a) drying leaves of Guangxi golden camellia, crushing into powder, adding potassium sulfate powder to the ground powder, ultra-fine grinding and sieving, (b) taking 80-130 g ultra-fine ground powder obtained in step (a), adding into the extraction vessel of the supercritical carbon dioxide extractor of 1 l, adding ethanol as an entrainer, soaking for 1-2 hours, setting the temperature of the extraction vessel to 40-50 degrees C, (c) opening the carbon dioxide gas tank valve, setting flow rate of carbon dioxide to 15-30 l/minute, starting extraction when the extraction kettle reaches the required temperature and pressure, and extracting for 2-3 hours and (d) sequentially introducing the extracted carbon dioxide fluid into the separation vessel 1 and the separation vessel 2, and collecting and purifying product.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting total flavonoids from Guangxi golden camellia.
   ADVANTAGE - The method extracts total flavonoids in a simple manner with good color and without solvent residues.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting total flavonoids from Guangxi golden camellia, involves (a) drying leaves of Guangxi golden camellia, crushing into powder, adding potassium sulfate powder to ground powder in a weight ratio of (1-5):100, ultra-fine grinding and sieving through a sieve of 10-20 mesh size to obtain ultra-fine ground powder, (b) taking 80-130 g ultra-fine ground powder obtained in step (a), adding into the extraction vessel of the supercritical carbon dioxide extractor of 1 l, adding 200-500 ml ethanol as an entrainer, soaking for 1-2 hours, setting the temperature of the extraction vessel to 40-50 degrees C, where the temperature of the separation kettle 1 is 15-30 degrees C and the temperature of the separation kettle 2 is 40-50 degrees C, (c) opening the carbon dioxide gas tank valve, setting the flow rate of carbon dioxide to 15-30 l/minute, where the pressure of the extraction kettle is 15-35 MPa, the pressure of the separation kettle 1 is 10-20 MPa and the pressure of the separation kettle 2 is 8-10 MPa, starting the extraction when the extraction kettle reaches the required temperature and pressure and extracting for 2-3 hours and (d) sequentially introducing the extracted carbon dioxide fluid into the separation vessel 1 and the separation vessel 2, collecting and purifying product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the weight ratio of potassium sulfate powder and golden camellia powder is 3:100. In step (b), the concentration of ultra-fine ground powder is 103 g. The temperature of the extraction vessel in step (b) is 50 degrees C, the temperature of the separation vessel 1 is 20 degrees C and the temperature of the separation vessel 2 is 50 degrees C. In step (b), 70-95%, preferably 85% ethanol (300 ml) is added as an entrainer. In step (c), the pressure of the extraction kettle is 25 MPa, the pressure of the separation reactor 1 is 12 MPa and the pressure of the separation vessel 2 is 8 MPa. The flow rate of carbon dioxide in the step (c) is 20 l/minute and the extraction time is 2 hours.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)
MC B04-A08G2; B04-A10; B04-A98; B11-B03C; B11-C05
IP A61K-127/00; A61K-036/82

PT P
PN CN114687824-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide circulating system            for adjusting temperature of fluorine salt high            temperature stack, has high temperature heater,            reheater and low temperature heater that are arranged            in high temperature reactor.
AU YAN J
   CHEN W
   WANG J
   ZHONG D
   ZHAO X
   ZHAO Q
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2022954477
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a high temperature reheat and                high and low temperature heat exchangers connected                in series. The system further comprises pre-cooler                (8), a first-level main compressor, an inter-stage                cooler (9), a second-level master compressor, a                low-temperature regenerator (7), a medium                temperature regenerator, a medium-high-pressure                turbine (5), a high-pressure high pressure turbine,                and a high pressure low pressure turbine. An                opening of a shunt valve (16) is adjusted to change                a carbon dioxide flow ratio to enter a cold side of                the high temperature heater and a cold surface of                the low temperature heater. The carbon dioxide                flows through the high pressure turbines and the                re-heater (1-3).
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide circulating                system for adjusting temperature of fluorine salt                high temperature stack used in energy conversion                system design field. Can also be used in                photo-thermal, nuclear reactor, fossil fuel and                various heat source.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses fluorine salt cooling high                temperature stack of pool type structure as heat                source, using space characteristics type structure,                and arranges multi-stage heater and re-heater in                the stack, so that the height is matched with the                temperature range of the high temperature heat                source to realize full utilization of the molten                salt heat, thus improving the circulation                efficiency. The system realizes intelligent control                of the temperature by changing the carbon dioxide                side flow, which greatly reduces the pile side                control difficulty. The hot end configuration of                the system is high temperature reheating and high                and low temperature heat exchanger connected in                series, and the cold end configuration is the                re-compression stage cooling, thus fully using the                heat of the fluorine salts under different                temperature gradient by reheating.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                operation method of supercritical carbon dioxide                circulating system for controlling temperature of                fluorine salt high temperature stack.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                supercritical carbon dioxide circulating                system.
   Re-heater (1-3)
   High temperature regenerator (5)
   Low temperature regenerator (7)
   Pre-cooler (8)
   inter-stage cooler (9)
   Shunt valve (16)
DC K05 (Nuclear reactors and simulators - including reactor processes, components and accessories, but excluding power plant (G21B, C).); Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P)); Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M)); X14 (Nuclear Power Generation)
MC K05-B07E1; K05-B03; Q51-C; Q52-A; X14-B03

PT P
PN CN114558574-A
TI Method for preparing iron-prussian-like blue            precursor for use in the preparation of a            photocatalytic carbon-dioxide hydrogenation catalyst,            involves mixing the solution A containing potassium            ferricyanide in water and solution B containing            transition metal salt in water and stirring in an oil            bath.
AU ZHU S
   YAN T
AE UNIV QUFU NORMAL (UNQN-C)
GA 2022771877
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing MFe-PBA                (iron-prussian-like blue) precursor involves                dissolving potassium ferricyanide in water to                obtain solution A; dissolving the transition metal                salt in water to obtain solution B; the transition                metal salt is soluble salts of manganese, copper,                nickel, cobalt or zinc; preferably halogen salts,                nitrates or sulfates; mixing the solution A and                solution B, and stirring in an oil bath at 40-80                &#176;C for 4-8 hours until the reaction is                complete; MFe-PBA precursor is obtained by                centrifugation and drying of the precipitate.
   USE - MFe-PBA (iron-prussian-like blue) precursor                for use in the preparation of a photocatalytic                carbon-dioxide hydrogenation catalyst, which is                used in photocatalytic carbon dioxide hydrogenation                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The synthesis process of the precursor is                simple and easy to control, and the post-treatment                process is simple. The photocatalyst preparation                method has high activity and high selectivity                production, and simultaneously has good stability                and great industrialization potential.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   a MFe-PBA (iron-prussian-like blue)                precursor;
   a method for preparing photocatalyst for                photocatalytic carbon dioxide hydrogenation, which                involves spreading MFe-PBA precursor on a magnetic                boat, placing in a tube furnace, and calcining in                air at 350-700 &#176;C for 2-6 hours, and the                heating rate is controlled at 2-6                &#176;C/minutes, to obtain a bimetallic mixed                oxide; placing bimetallic mixed oxide in a tube                furnace, and the volume ratio of hydrogen/argon is                10 wt.% at a gas flow rate of 20-60 ml/minutes, and                heating to 200-800 &#176;C at a heating rate of                2-6 &#176;C/minutes for reduction treatment for                1-4 hours;
   a photocatalyst; and
   a method for photocatalytic carbon dioxide                hydrogenation, which involves introducing carbon                dioxide and hydrogen, and the hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide is catalyzed by xenon lamp                illumination, the low temperature is 150-250                &#176;C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  solution A to solution B is 1:2.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E11-P; E11-D02; E32-B; E10-E04E1; H04-F06; H04-E08; H04-F02E; H04-F05; J04-E11; J04-E04C; J04-E04B; N06-C10

PN WO2022074356-A1; GB2600238-A
TI Producing hydrogen by generating synthesis gas in synthesis gas generation unit, subjecting synthesis gas to water-gas shift stages in water-gas shift unit, cooling and separating condensed water, and passing resulting de-watered hydrogen-enriched gas to carbon dioxide separation unit.
AU CUI Y
   GARCIA M
   GLEN P E
   MACLEOD N
   MISTRY N
   NICHOLSON M T
   ROLOFF-STANDRING S
   SMITH T
   STANNESS H E
   STANWAY M A
   UN DIN K
   DIN K U
AE JOHNSON MATTHEY PLC (JOHO-C)
GA 202251692D
AB    NOVELTY - Process for the production of hydrogen, comprises: (a) generating a synthesis gas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and steam in a synthesis gas generation unit; (b) increasing the hydrogen content of the synthesis gas and decreasing the carbon monoxide content by subjecting it to one or more water-gas shift stages in a water-gas shift unit to provide a hydrogen-enriched gas, (c) cooling the hydrogen-enriched gas and separating condensed water from it, and (d) passing the resulting de-watered hydrogen-enriched gas to a carbon dioxide separation unit to provide a carbon dioxide gas stream and a hydrogen gas stream.
   USE - The process is used for the production of hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The process improves the lifetime of the copper catalyst at the more demanding conditions, resulting in an enhanced efficiency hydrogen process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for the production of hydrogen, comprises: (a) generating a synthesis gas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and steam in a synthesis gas generation unit; (b) increasing the hydrogen content of the synthesis gas and decreasing the carbon monoxide content by subjecting it to one or more water-gas shift stages in a water-gas shift unit to provide a hydrogen-enriched gas, (c) cooling the hydrogen-enriched gas and separating condensed water from it, (d) passing the resulting de-watered hydrogen- enriched gas to a carbon dioxide separation unit to provide a carbon dioxide gas stream and a hydrogen gas stream. The synthesis gas from step (a) is fed without adjustment of the carbon monoxide content to a water gas shift reactor, operated adiabatically or with cooling, at an inlet temperature of 200-280 degrees C and an exit temperature below 360 degrees C, and containing a catalyst comprising 30-70 wt.% copper, expressed as copper(II) oxide, combined with zinc oxide, alumina and silica, where the catalyst has a silica content, expressed as silicon dioxide, of 0.1-5 wt.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The synthesis gas generation unit comprises an autothermal reformer, fed with a reformed synthesis gas obtained from an upstream adiabatic pre-reformer, a fired steam reformer or a gas-heated reformer. The water gas shift unit comprises a stage of medium temperature shift, or isothermal shift, preferably a stage of isothermal shift, and optionally a downstream low-temperature shift stage. The purification unit is a pressure-swing adsorption unit or a temperature swing adsorption unit, preferably a pressure-swing adsorption unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further comprises passing the hydrogen gas stream to a purification unit to provide a purified hydrogen gas. The synthesis gas generation comprises one or more steps comprising adiabatic pre-reforming, catalytic steam reforming in a fired- or gas-heated reformer, autothermal reforming, and catalytic partial oxidation, applied to a gaseous or vaporized hydrocarbon such as natural gas, naphtha or a refinery off-gas. The synthesis gas generation comprises non-catalytic partial oxidation or gasification of a carbonaceous feedstock such as coal, biomass or municipal waste, optionally followed by one or more stages of catalytic steam reforming or autothermal reforming. The carbon dioxide removal stage is performed using a physical wash system or a reactive wash system, such as a reactive wash system, preferably an amine wash system. One or more of the carbon dioxide removal unit streams are heated in heat exchange with the hydrogen-enriched gas stream. Preferred Components: The hydrogen content of the synthesis gas fed to the water gas shift reactor, on a wet gas basis, is 30-50 vol.% and the carbon monoxide content of the synthesis gas fed to the water gas shift reactor, on a wet gas basis, is 6-20 vol.%. The catalyst has a copper content, expressed as copper(II) oxide, of 45-65 wt.%. The catalyst has a zinc content, expressed as zinc oxide, of 20-50 wt.%, preferably 20-40 wt.%. The catalyst has an aluminum content, expressed as aluminum oxide, of 5-40 wt.%, preferably 8-25 wt.%. The catalyst has one or more promoter metal oxides, comprising oxides of magnesium, cobalt, manganese, vanadium, titanium, zirconium or rare earths, present in an amount of 0.1-5 wt.%. The catalyst has a silica content, expressed as silicon dioxide, of 0.1-2 wt.%, preferably 0.2-1 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - The example section describes the preparation of formulation containing copper(II) oxide/zinc oxide/aluminum oxide/magnesium oxide/silicon dioxide that does not support the claims.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN113698793-A
TI Coating surface of heavy calcium carbonate with nanometer calcium carbonate comprises e.g. crushing natural carbonate minerals to obtain heavy calcium carbonate, introducing gaseous medium containing carbon dioxide into mixture, and filtering mixture material and composite calcium carbonate slurry.
AU YANG C
   WANG Y
   WANG Z
   WU Q
AE ZHEJIANG QINGDA POWDER MATERIAL CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021E2496U
AB    NOVELTY - Coating surface of heavy calcium carbonate with nanometer calcium carbonate comprises crushing natural carbonate minerals to obtain heavy calcium carbonate, taking part of heavy calcium carbonate and mixing with calcium hydroxide slurry with preset mass fraction concentration, introducing gaseous medium containing carbon dioxide into mixture, preparing mixed slurry containing nano-grade light calcium carbonate and heavy calcium carbonate, making preset volume of mixed slurry with remaining heavy calcium carbonate, introducing gaseous medium containing carbon dioxide into mixed material again, so that calcium hydroxide in mixed material and carbon dioxide in gaseous medium react to form nanometer calcium hydroxide, sequentially filtering remaining mixture material and composite calcium carbonate slurry, and drying and crushing to obtain composite calcium carbonate coated with nanometer calcium carbonate on surface.
   USE - The method is useful for coating surface of heavy calcium carbonate with nanometer calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has excellent surface coating effect of heavy calcium carbonate, reliable preparation and convenient operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Coating surface of heavy calcium carbonate with nanometer calcium carbonate comprises crushing natural carbonate minerals to obtain heavy calcium carbonate, taking part of heavy calcium carbonate and mixing with calcium hydroxide slurry with preset mass fraction concentration, introducing gaseous medium containing carbon dioxide into mixture, preparing mixed slurry containing nano-grade light calcium carbonate and heavy calcium carbonate, making preset volume of mixed slurry with remaining heavy calcium carbonate, introducing gaseous medium containing carbon dioxide into mixed material again, so that calcium hydroxide in mixed material and carbon dioxide in gaseous medium react to form nanometer calcium hydroxide, where nano-grade light calcium carbonate and heavy calcium carbonate in mixed slurry are surface-compounded to form composite calcium carbonate slurry, sequentially filtering remaining mixture material and composite calcium carbonate slurry, and drying and crushing to obtain composite calcium carbonate coated with nanometer calcium carbonate on surface.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for coating the surface of heavy calcium carbonate with nanometer calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (1) physically crushing the natural carbonate minerals, crushing and grading the crushed product and sieving to obtain coarse material of heavy calcium carbonate with particle size of less than 100 microns, mixing the prepared heavy calcium carbonate coarse material with water, adding to the grinding equipment for grinding treatment, and drying the ground product to obtain heavy calcium carbonate with particle size of 0.1-10 microns and powdery form, (2) preparing calcium hydroxide slurry with mass fraction concentration of 19-21%, adding 100 pts. wt. prepared calcium hydroxide slurry into the first processing vessel, continuously stirring the calcium hydroxide slurry at stirring speed of 350-550 revolutions/minute, adding 20-30 pts. wt. heavy calcium carbonate to the calcium hydroxide slurry at the same time, introducing air containing 25-40% carbon dioxide into the lower liquid level of the mixed slurry after stirring for 3-8 minutes, carrying out the continuous bubbling treatment for 30-40 minutes, carbonizing the calcium hydroxide in the mixed slurry to generate nano-level light calcium carbonate, mixing the nano-level light calcium carbonate and heavy calcium carbonate to form mixed slurry, compounding part of the nano-level light calcium carbonate on the surface of the heavy calcium carbonate to form surface-coated nano-calcium carbonate, maintaining the stirring process when the air is introduced, and stopping the stirring when the bubbling process is completed, (3) taking the center of the lower liquid level of the mixed slurry as the material removal point to extract 75-85% of the volume of the mixed slurry, transferring it to the second processing container, adding 30-50 pts. wt. heavy calcium carbonate, continuously stirring the mixture at stirring speed of 550-650 revolutions/minute for 20-40 minutes, again introducing the air containing 25-40% carbon dioxide to the lower liquid level of the mixture, so that the mixture is also subjected to bubbling during the stirring process when stirring for 3 to 5 minutes, at the same time, reacting the calcium hydroxide in the mixture with carbon dioxide in the air to form nanometer calcium hydroxide, and surface-compositing the nanometer light calcium carbonate and heavy calcium carbonate to form composite calcium carbonate slurry, and (4) discharging the remaining mixed slurry and the composite calcium carbonate slurry prepared in the step (3), and sequentially filtering, drying, and pulverizing to obtain composite calcium carbonate coated with nanometer calcium carbonate. In the step (3), polyacrylate sodium salt is also added as dispersant when heavy calcium carbonate is added, where the added part of the dispersant is 0.01-0.03 times of the mixed slurry. The temperature of the second processing vessel is dynamically maintained at 20-50degrees Celsius. In the step (2), the temperature of the first processing vessel is dynamically maintained at 30-50degrees Celsius. In the step (2) and step (3), the air containing carbon dioxide is passed into the mixed slurry or mixed material in stirring state at homeopathic angle.Preferred Components: In the step (1), the natural carbonate mineral is mixture of calcite, marble and/or limestone. The pH of the mixed slurry is controlled to be 7.5-8 after the step (2) is completed. The pH of the composite calcium carbonate slurry is controlled to be 7 after the processing of the step (3) is completed. In the step (2), the air containing 25-40% carbon dioxide is passed into the lower liquid level of the mixed slurry at inlet flow rate of 2.5-3 m3/hours. The nanometer light calcium carbonate suspended in the mixed slurry contains at least nanometer light calcium carbonate with particle size of 40-600 nanometers. In the step (3), the air containing 25-40% carbon dioxide is introduced into the lower liquid level of the mixed material at flow rate of 1.5-2 m3/hours. The nanometer light calcium carbonate suspended in the mixed slurry contains at least nanometer light calcium carbonate with particle size of 40-600 nanometers. In the step (4), it is placed under negative pressure drying treatment at 95-105degrees Celsius for 6-7 hours when the material obtained by filtration is dried.
DC A82 (Coatings, impregnations, polishes - excluding textile finishing.); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); G01 (Inorganic pigments and non-fibrous fillers (C09C).)

PT P
PN KR2020122158-A
TI Gas treatment system of offshore structure for receiving and processing gas from gas well, has methanol supply unit that injects methanol generated by methanol generation unit into production line extending from gas well.
AU OH H J
   KIM Y H
   NAM K
   PARK S M
   KIM S
AE KOREA SHIPBUILDING & OFFSHORE ENG CO LTD (HHIH-C)
GA 2020A64968
AB    NOVELTY - The gas treatment system has a carbon dioxide removal unit (30) that removes carbon dioxide from a gas produced in a gas well. A moisture removal unit (10) removes moisture from the gas produced in the gas well. A liquefaction unit (20) liquefies the gas from which the moisture and carbon dioxide are removed for gas-liquid separation. A methanol generator (40) generates methanol using the carbon dioxide removed from the gas, moisture, and evaporation gas separated from the liquefied gas. A methanol supply unit injects methanol generated by the methanol generation unit into a production line extending from the gas well.
   USE - Gas treatment system of offshore structure (claimed) for receiving and processing gas from gas well.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy use efficiency is improved by efficiently cooling the regeneration gas for regeneration of the adsorption unit in the process of adsorbing moisture from natural gas. The production availability of the liquefaction process can be greatly increased by supplementing the boil-off gas to the refrigerant, and the sizing of the liquefaction configuration can be reduced. The generation of boil-off gas in the storage tank is reduced, thus stabilizing the pressure of the storage tank and minimizing the boil-off gas treatment capacity. Since the carbon dioxide is changed into methanol and stored, instead of separating the carbon dioxide and discharging into the atmosphere during gas production,the hydrate generation is suppressed, an environmental pollution is prevented, the equipment is made compact, and the operating costs are reduced. The size and operating costs of the liquefier are reduced by reducing the load of the refrigerant in the liquefier. The need for periodically receiving the methanol from other ships is eliminated, thus the operating costs and the hydration plugging risk are reduced. The generation of methane hydrate in the submarine production line can be suppressed, since methanol is generated by using the waste heat of the generator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of the gas treatment system.
   Moisture removal unit (10)
   Liquefaction unit (20)
   Carbon dioxide removal unit (30)
   Methanol generation unit (40)
   Methanol tank (51)

MC H01-B01; H01-C11; H01-D05; H01-D06C; Q24-B02A; Q24-P08; Q69-B02; Q69-C; Q69-D

PN EP3620224-A1; US2020078723-A1; CN110882606-A; US10874977-B2; EP3620224-B1
TI Method for removal of acidic gas components from            synthesis gas, involves arranging first and second feed            lines such that carbon dioxide desorbed from first            loaded detergent is absorbed by second loaded detergent            by pressure release.
AU CORBET S
   GUAN M
   GUBRINSKI A
   RAPPOLD D
   SCHMIDT S
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE ETUD & EXPL PROCEDES GEORGES (AIRL-C)
GA 202020381R
AB    NOVELTY - The method (100) involves supplying a first                detergent loaded with carbon dioxide (CO2) to an                expansion tank from a first absorption device                through a first supply line for pressure release. A                second detergent loaded with CO2 is fed to the                expansion tank from a second absorption device                through a second supply line for pressure relief                such that the carbon dioxide concentration in the                first loaded detergent is higher than the carbon                dioxide concentration in the second loaded                detergent. The first loaded detergent and the                second loaded detergent are partial mixed in the                flash tank. The partially regenerated detergent is                obtained by mixing and depressurization. The first                feed line and the second feed line are arranged                such that the carbon dioxide desorbed from the                first loaded detergent is partially absorbed by the                second loaded detergent by the pressure                release.
   USE - Method for removal of acidic gas components                from synthesis gas by absorption of acidic gas                components in physically acting detergent e.g.                methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide content in the recycle gas                can be reduced. The yield of valuable gases such as                carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the recycle gas can                be increased. The liquid under high pressure such                as loaded detergent is reduced in pressure in the                expansion tank. The load on the compressor is                reduced such that the smaller compressor can be                used and less electrical power is required for                compressor. The smaller absorption device is                required and the smaller amount of detergent to be                used in the absorption device is required.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) an apparatus for generating recycle gas                from detergent loaded with carbon dioxide;                and
   (2) a plant for removal of acidic gas                components from synthesis gas by absorption of                acidic gas components in physically acting                detergent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram                illustrating the method for removal of acidic gas                components from synthesis gas.
   Method for removal of acidic gas components                from synthesis gas (100)
   Indirect heat exchangers (E01,E02)
   Pressure relief device (H01)
   Hot regeneration device (H02)
   Recycle gas compressor (K01)
   Absorption devices (T01,T02)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - The physically active detergent is selected                  from one element from the group comprises                  methanol, 1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP), aqueous                  solution of 1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP), a                  mixture of dimethyl ethers of Polyethylene                  glycol, and propylene carbonate                  (4-methyl-1,3-dioxolan-2-one).

TI Preparing carbon nanotube loaded cobalt particle            composite catalyst comprises pyrolyzing melamine to            synthesize graphite-phase carbon nitride under inert            atmosphere, synthesizing precursor cobalt-metal-organic            framework-graphitic carbon nitride by hydro-thermal            method, and carbonizing.
AU GONG L
   LU W
   ZHU H
   DING Y
   LI Y
   ZHAO Z
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2022A2371N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon nanotube loaded cobalt                particle composite catalyst comprises (i)                pyrolyzing melamine to synthesize graphite-phase                carbon nitride (g-C3N4) under inert atmosphere;                (ii) synthesizing precursor cobalt-metal-organic                framework-graphitic carbon nitride (Co-MOF-g-C3N4)                by hydro-thermal method; and (iii) obtaining                cobalt-carbon nanotube (Co-CNTs) catalyst by the                carbonization of the inert atmosphere.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for carbon dioxide                hydromethanation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation process is simple, and is                suitable for large-scale production. The carbon                nanotube-supported cobalt particle catalyst is                prepared by the two-step method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   carbon nanotube loaded cobalt particle                composite catalyst prepared by the above-mentioned                method; and
   use of the carbon nanotube loaded cobalt                particle composite catalyst, comprising carrying                out reaction evaluation in a fixed bed reactor                under the reaction conditions at temperature of                200-500&#8451;, space velocity of                1000-5000/hour, pressure of 1-10 MPa, and carbon                dioxide:hydrogen is 0.1-5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), during                  synthesis of graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4) by                  pyrolysis of melamine, the inert atmosphere is                  argon, helium and/or nitrogen, the airspeed is                  500-3000/hour, preferably 1000-3000/hour, the                  pyrolytic carbonization temperature is                  600-1000&#176;C, preferably                  700-900&#176;C, the time is 0.1-10 hours,                  preferably 1-8 hours, the heating rate from room                  temperature to the pre-pyrolysis temperature does                  not exceed 5&#176;C/minute, preferably in the                  range of 1-5&#176;C/minute. The precursor                  Co-MOF-g-C3N4 is synthesized by hydrothermal                  method by (i) dissolving g-C3N4, MOF ligand and                  cobalt salt into a mixed solution composed of                  dimethylformamide (DMF), methanol and ethanol,                  where the volume ratio of DMF and methanol is                  1:5-5:1, preferably 2:5-5:2, the volume ratio of                  DMF and ethanol is 1:5-5:1, preferably 2:5-5-2,                  the Co-MOF includes CoMOF-71, Co-MOF-74 and                  Co-MOF-78, the ligands are terephthalic acid (the                  corresponding Co-MOF is Co-MOF71), 2,5-dihydroxy                  p-(phthalic acid) (the corresponding Co-MOF is                  Co-MOF74), 2,7-tetrahydropyrrolidinedicarboxylic                  acid (the corresponding Co-MOF is Co-MOF78), the                  ratio of g-C3N4,ligand and cobalt salt is 0.5-5                  g:0.1-10 g:0.1-5 g, preferably 1-4g:0.5-10g:0.1-4                  g, the ratio of g-C3N4 and mixed solution is 1-10                  g:50-300 ml, preferably 2-8 g:100-300 ml; (ii)                  stirring at room temperature for 0.5-2 hours,                  then placing the mixed solution in the reactor to                  carry out a hydrothermal reaction, washing and                  drying the obtained solids, i.e., Co-MOF-X, where                  the cobalt salt is cobalt nitrate, cobalt                  acetate, cobalt sulfate, cobalt hydrochloride                  and/or cobalt citrate, the hydrothermal reaction                  temperature is 80-200&#176;C, preferably                  120-150&#176;C, and the hydrothermal reaction                  time is 1-48 hours, subjecting the solid to                  hydrothermal treatment, washing 3-5 times with                  methanol, and then drying under vacuum at                  50-120&#176;C. The Co-CNTs catalyst is                  obtained by carbonizing Co-MOF-X under an inert                  atmosphere of 400-1100&#176;C, preferably                  500-1000&#176;C for 0.1-10 hours, preferably                  1-5 hours, the airspeed is 500-3000/hour,                  preferably 1000-3000/hour, and the inert                  atmosphere is argon gas, helium gas or nitrogen                  gas.

PN CN114794271-A
TI Performing industrial production method for cold            soluble black tea powder, involves crushing tea,            feeding crushed tea to counter-current circulation            extracting device, extracting to obtain tea juice,            cooling tea juice, adding precipitator, and            precipitating tea juice.
AU WAN F
   WANG H
   ZHANG C
   TAN Z
   ZHANG Z
AE BEIJING POWDERY HEALTH IND CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   POWDERY HUBEI HEALTH IND CO LTD (POWD-Non-standard)
GA 2022A1596W
AB    NOVELTY - Performing industrial production method                involves crushing tea, feeding crushed tea to                counter-current circulation extracting device,                extracting to obtain tea juice, cooling tea juice,                adding precipitator, and precipitating tea juice.                The precipitator is calcium hydroxide, magnesium                hydroxide or mixture of two. The pH value of tea                juice after adding precipitate agent is controlled                at 9-11. The tea juice is centrifuged to remove                precipitate, carbon dioxide is filled to adjust pH                value of feed liquid. The pH of tea juice after                returning is controlled to 4-5. The tea juice is                placed into vacuum tank, and vacuumized to remove                redundant carbon dioxide gas. The tea juice is                filtered by ceramic membrane. The salt is removed                by using electrodialysis membrane. The reverse                osmosis membrane concentration is carried out and                spray-dried to obtain tea powder.
   USE - Method for performing industrial production                method used for cold soluble black tea                powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to have easy operation and                clear and transparent appearance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The precipitate agent is                  dissolved into 5-10 wt.% emulsion. The tea juice                  is added. The temperature of tea juice is                  controlled at 5-25&#8451; after cooling. The                  pH value in sealed pressure tank is adjusted. The                  carbon dioxide gas pressure is controlled at 2-4                  times of atmospheric pressure, after finishing                  charging, pressure is kept for 10-30 hours,                  redundant calcium and magnesium ion are                  completely settled. The reverse osmosis membrane                  is concentrated until concentration of tea juice                  is 30% and concentrating is stopped.Preferred Conditions: The settling time is                  30-60 minutes. The centrifuge is disc or                  three-foot centrifuge. The aperture of ceramic                  film is 100-200 nm. The electrodialysis membrane                  removes salt ions. The total ash content of final                  tea powder is ensured to 5%, and salt is stopped.                  The vacuum degree for vacuumizing and removing                  carbon dioxide is -30-(-)50 Pa, and degassing                  time is 5-20 minutes. The temperature of                  circulating counter-current extraction is                  controlled at 60-90&#8451;, feeding amount is                  controlled at 200-300 kg per hour, water adding                  amount is controlled to 3-5 t/hour. The                  extracting time is controlled at 30-50 minutes,                  and needed water is used as deionized water. The                  granularity of crushed tea is controlled at 30-60                  meshes.Preferred Components: The black tea raw                  material comprises Yunnan black tea and keemun                  black tea.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-D02
IP A23F-003/18; A23F-003/28

   DENG J
AE SHENZHEN JINHAOHUI IND DEV CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202296955D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing active nano calcium powder                comprising adding a crystal form control agent into                the calcium hydroxide solution, uniformly stirring,                and performing ultrasonic dispersion treatment to                obtain a calcium hydroxide suspension, introducing                a carbon dioxide gas into the suspension of calcium                hydroxide until the pH value of the suspension of                calcium hydroxide is 6.5-7.0, and adding a                dispersant to obtain a mixed solution, heating the                mixed solution to 80-90&#8451;, introducing                carbon dioxide gas into the mixed solution,                preserving heat and aging, and washing to obtain a                calcium carbonate slurry, filtering and drying the                calcium carbonate slurry to obtain a calcium                carbonate cake, and applying a surface treatment                agent to the calcium carbonate cake, and crushing                and sieving to obtain the active nano calcium                powder.
   USE - The method can well reduce the agglomeration                phenomenon generated by active nano-calcium powder                when preparing, and reach better mechanical                property of the material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of                  the calcium hydroxide solution is 20-35 kmol/l,                  and a mass ratio of the crystalline control agent                  to the calcium hydroxide solution is 1: (2-8). The dispersant comprises at                  least one of PEG 400, PEG 600, sodium                  polycarboxylate, and sodium polyacrylate. The                  mass ratio of the surface treatment agent to the                  calcium carbonate cake is (20-50): 1. The step of applying a surface treatment agent to                  the calcium carbonate cake, crushing and sieving                  to obtain the active nano calcium powder, where                  the crushing process parameter is: crushing for                  10 -15 minutes at 2000-2500 rotations/minutes by                  using an ultrafine pulverizer.

   USE - The fertilizer is useful in agriculture for                promoting absorption and utilization of carbon                dioxide by crops, increasing power for                photosynthesis, improving resistance of crops to                pests and diseases, and enhancing ability to resist                cold, drought, and dry and hot wind.
   ADVANTAGE - The fertilizer: is water-soluble, and plant                nutrient liquid; has nutrient elements required by                crops, which achieves stable and high yield. The                method is simple and easy, and suitable for                industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparing fertilizer, comprising (a)                stirring and mixing water-soluble silicon element                liquid and chelated micro element composite liquid,                heating and keeping warm, sealing, allowing to                stand and cooling to obtain mixed liquid, (b)                adding amino acid into mixed solution a and                stirring to obtain mixed solution b, and (c) adding                water into mixed solution b and uniformly stirring                to obtain fertilizer.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The fertilizer                  comprises 8-12 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt.                  water-soluble silicon element solution, 2-4 pts.                  wt., preferably 3 pts. wt. chelated micro element                  complex solution, 8-12 pts. wt., preferably 10                  pts. wt. amino acid and 75-79 pts. wt.,                  preferably 77 pts. wt. water. The preparation of                  water-soluble silicon element liquid comprises                  (i) pulverizing silica and removing impurities,                  and adding edible alkali to obtains mixture,                  where the weight ratio of silica and edible                  alkali is 7:3, and (ii) heating mixture at                  1500-1800degrees Celsius, cooling, removing                  impurities and adding pure water to obtain                  water-soluble silicon element liquid. The content                  of silicon in water-soluble silicon element                  liquid is 10-12 wt.%. The chelated micro element                  complex solution includes 2-4 wt.% iron, 2-4 wt.%                  zinc, 2-4 wt.% copper, 2-4 wt.% manganese, 2-4                  wt.% calcium and 2-4 wt.% magnesium                  elements.Preferred Method: In the method, the                  rotating speed of stirring is 60-80                  revolutions/minutes, the heating temperature is                  85-95degrees Celsius, the heat preservation time                  is 2-3 hours, and the sealing and allowing to                  stand time is 4-6 hours. In the use method, the                  dilution with water is 600 times.

TI Recovery method of sodium dichloroisocyanurate            production wastewater involves dechlorinating mother            liquor of sodium dichloroisocyanurate to recover            cyanuric acid and chlorine, passing calcium chloride            and hydrochloric acid solution into ammonia gas,            introducing ammonia and carbon dioxide.

   DONG Z
   WANG J
AE HEBEI LIUHE CHEM CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202265955G
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery method of sodium dichloroisocyanurate                production wastewater involves: i. dechlorinating                mother liquor of sodium dichloroisocyanurate to                recover cyanuric acid and chlorine; ii. passing                calcium chloride and hydrochloric acid solution                obtained after the step (1) treatment into ammonia                gas to when the pH value in the solution is greater                than 5, then introducing ammonia and carbon dioxide                were simultaneously into the solution, and when the                pH value is greater than 8, adding the solution to                the solution, filtering to obtain calcium                carbonate; iii. evaporating ammonium chloride                solution obtained after the treatment in step (2)                and concentrating.
   USE - Recovery method of sodium dichloroisocyanurate                production wastewater
   ADVANTAGE - Recovery method of sodium dichloroisocyanurate                production wastewateris suitable for the combined                production of cyanuric acid and sodium                dichloroisocyanurate, ammonia gas comes from the                pyrolysis of urea in the production process of                cyanuric acid, sulfuric acid is used for absorbing                ammonia gas before replacement, and cyanuric acid                and calcium carbonate are recovered, so that the                production cost is greatly reduced, the cyclic                utilization of calcium carbonate is realized, and                the purpose of carbon neutralization is                achieved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Sodium                  dichloroisocyanurate mother liquor contains                  0.8-1.2 wt.% dichloroisocyanuric acid in mass                  fraction and 8-13 wt.% calcium chloride in mass                  fraction. Preferred Method: Dechlorination                  treatment is to adding hydrochloric acid and                  reacting with the dichloroisocyanuric acid in the                  mother liquor to produce cyanuric acid and                  chlorine, and the chlorine is recovered through                  the pipeline, when the pH of the solution is                  0.2-0.5, stop adding hydrochloric acid, adding                  hydrogen peroxide until the content of available                  chlorine in the solution is 0, and filtering the                  solution to obtain cyanuric acid, ammonia gas                  comes from the ammonia gas produced by the urea                  pyrolysis method to produce cyanuric acid.

TI Preparing Bismuth oxycarbonate nanosheet            containing oxygen vacancies comprises uniformly mixing            ethanol, water, cetyltrimethylammonium salt and            hexamethylenetetramine, adding carbonate powder and            bismuth salt powder, carrying out hydrothermal            reaction, separating solid, and drying.
AU XIE Y
   ZHAO Y
   ZU X
   SUN Y
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 202265400K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bismuth oxycarbonate nanosheet                containing oxygen vacancies comprises (a) uniformly                mixing ethanol, water, cetyltrimethylammonium salt                and hexamethylenetetramine, and adding carbonate                powder and bismuth salt powder, uniformly mixing to                obtain a mixed solution, (b) taking above-mentioned                mixed solution, carrying out hydrothermal reaction                under high pressure condition, separating solid,                and drying to obtain bismuth oxycarbonate nanosheet                without oxygen vacancy, and (c) irradiating                above-mentioned bismuth oxycarbonate nanosheets                without oxygen vacancies with ultraviolet light in                a vacuum environment to obtain bismuth oxycarbonate                nanosheets containing oxygen vacancies.
   USE - The nanosheet is useful as visible light                catalyzing carbon dioxide reduction catalyst                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is easy to operate; has high                efficiency and high stability; and catalytically                reduces in visible light.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1 oxygen vacancy-containing bismuth                oxycarbonate nanosheet with a thickness of 0.5-3                unit cell layers; and
   2 producing carbon monoxide by catalyzing                carbon dioxide reduction with visible light,                comprising using oxygen vacancy-containing bismuth                oxycarbonate nanosheets or oxygen                vacancy-containing bismuth oxycarbonate nanosheets                as catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of                  the ethanol and water is (20-50):(1-5). The mass                  ratio of the cetyltrimethylammonium salt,                  hexamethylenetetramine, carbonate powder and                  bismuth salt powder is                  (200-500):(200-500):(400-800):(800-1200). The                  cetyl trimethyl ammonium salt includes cetyl                  trimethyl ammonium bromide, cetyl trimethyl                  ammonium chloride. The carbonate includes sodium                  carbonate and/or potassium carbonate. The bismuth                  salt includes bismuth chloride and/or bismuth                  nitrate.Preferred Method: The step (a) specifically                  comprises fully mixing ethanol and water, then                  adding cetyltrimethylammonium salt, fully mixing,                  then adding hexamethylenetetramine, fully mixing,                  adding carbonate powder and bismuth salt powder                  and uniformly mixing to obtain a mixed solution.                  The temperature of the hydrothermal reaction is                  120-160degreesC., and the reaction time is 10-14                  hours. In the step (c), the vacuum environment is                  0.1-0.001 atm, and irradiation time of the                  ultraviolet light is 0.1-1 hours.

PN CN113912903-A; CN113912903-B
TI Preparing large-size transparent silica aerogel            block comprises stirring and mixing 3-aminopropyl            triethoxysilane, methyl orthosilicate and absolute            ethanol, and then adding deionized water, and            continuously mixing, aging, and drying with            supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU LIU Z
   LI G
   XIN Z
   ZHANG Z
   WANG M
AE CHINA BUILDING MATERIALS ACAD (CHNB-C)
GA 2022158795
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing large-size transparent silica                aerogel block comprises (i) stirring and mixing                3-aminopropyl triethoxysilane, methyl orthosilicate                and absolute ethanol, and then adding deionized                water, and continuously mixing, (ii) adding the                hydrolysis catalyst to the mixed solution, stirring                and mixing for 20-40 minutes to make it hydrolyzed,                then adding into a constant temperature water bath                for 22-26 hours in a water bath, (iii) adding                polycondensation catalyst, stirring for 28-32                minutes, allowing to stand for 28-32 minutes, then                adding dry ice-acetone bath to make it gel,                reacting with organic monomers with isocyano groups                to obtain polyurea cross-linked silica wet gel,                (iv) forming the wet gel, adding ethanol,                reinforcing material, mixed solution of fiber woven                sheet and silicon source precursor, aging for 1-2                days, and drying with supercritical carbon dioxide                to obtain silica aerogel blocks,
   USE - The method is useful for preparing large-size                transparent silica aerogel block.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces product having excellent                bulk, light transmittance, heat insulation and                mechanical properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silicon source                  precursor includes ethyl orthosilicate, sodium                  silicate and/or polypolysiloxane. The reinforcing                  material includes mixture of ceramic fibers,                  glass fibers and/or mullite fibers. The raw                  materials of the fiber woven sheet are                  polyurethane and polyethylene oxide. The                  polyurethane-polyethylene oxide blended                  nanofibers are prepared by wet spinning of                  polyurethane and polyethylene oxide,                  polyurethane-polyethylene oxide blended                  nanofibers are woven into sheets through                  electrospinning to prepare fiber woven sheets.                  The hydrolysis catalyst includes hydrochloric                  acid, and the amount thereof is 8-12 ml. The                  polycondensation catalyst includes ammonia water,                  and the amount thereof is 1-5 ml. The molar ratio                  of 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane, methyl                  orthosilicate and absolute ethanol is                  0.8-1.2:0.8-1.2:13-15. The molar ratio of the                  methyl orthosilicate and deionized water is                  0.8-1.2:7-9.
