PT P
PN WO2021152054-A1
TI Electrochemical formation of formate involves feeding anolyte comprising polyol to anode compartment, feeding catholyte having carbon dioxide to cathode compartment and applying voltage difference between cathode and anode.
AU VAN DEN BOSCH B
   FIGUEIREDO M C C
   SCHOUTEN K J P
   BRANDS M
   RAWLS B J
   PHILIPS M F
AE AVANTIUM KNOWLEDGE CENT BV (AVAN-Non-standard)
GA 202190290P
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical formation of formate, where the process is performed in an electrochemical cell comprising a cathode compartment containing a cathode, an anode compartment containing a nickel-based anode and an ion exchange membrane separating the anode compartment from the cathode compartment, involves: (a) feeding an anolyte comprising at least one polyol to the anode compartment; (b) feeding a catholyte comprising carbon dioxide (CO2) to the cathode compartment; and (c) applying a voltage difference between the cathode and the anode such that at the cathode CO2 is reduced to formate and at the anode the at least one polyol is oxidized to formate.
   USE - The process is useful for electrochemical formation of formate (claimed), where the formate is useful for preparing fuels and industrial chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The process can operate at high Faraday efficiencies at high current densities, thus enabling for the first time an economically viable process for the large-scale production of formate. Since a high concentration of relatively pure formate is formed at both the cathode and at the anode, the electricity costs and capital expenses for formate production are strongly reduced compared to unpaired processes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cathode is a gas diffusion electrode, and the catholyte is a combination of gaseous CO2 led through the gas diffusion electrode and a liquid catholyte comprising a base fed to the cathode compartment. The cathode contains indium. The anode contains nickel sulfide or nickel-molybdenum-nitride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the at least one polyol in the anolyte is 0.01-1 M. The excess polyol substrate feed at the anode is 5-200, preferably 20-120, more preferably 50-90, where the excess polyol substrate feed is defined as the ratio between the molar polyol substrate feed at the anode and the maximum rate of polyol substrate conversion at the anode. Preferred Components: The polyol is one or more of glycerol, sorbitol, erythritol, ethylene glycol and glucose. The anolyte comprises a mixture of two or more polyols. It is an industrial waste stream selected from biomass hydrolysis or biomass hydrogenation processes. It comprises sorbitol, erythritol, glucose and glycerol. The ion exchange membrane is a bipolar membrane. The product stream from the cathode and the product stream from the anode are combined into a single product stream comprising a formate solution.

   (1) a method for producing the alkane                dehydrogenation catalyst involving colliding                high-energy particles with the raw material                graphene;
   (2) a method for producing hydrogen involving                extracting hydrogen from an alkane using the alkane                dehydrogenation catalyst; and
   (3) a method for producing ammonia involving                using hydrogen obtained in above-mentioned method                to reduce nitrogen oxides to obtain ammonia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The graphene used has                  2-200 of the structures per 100 nm2 of the atomic                  film of the graphene. Preferred Method: The                  method involves using graphene as raw material                  graphene obtained by the detonation method. The                  hydrogen producing step further involves                  occluding hydrogen extracted from alkane at an                  atomic defect site of graphene.

PT P
PN CN112210084-A
TI Preparing metal-organic framework used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves adding ligand and triethylamine to N,N-dimethylacetamide, ultrasonically processing, adding copper salt solution, reacting, and filtering.
AU HONG Y
AE HONG Y (HONG-Individual)
GA 2021082399
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal-organic framework involves: (S1) adding 100 ml propionic acid to a three-necked flask, heating at 50 degrees C for refluxing, adding 4-formyl-1H-pyrazole to the flask, stirring for 20 minutes, dripping pyrrole, stirring for 30 minutes, refluxing the solution for 10 hours under dark conditions, where the molar ratio of 4-formyl-1H-pyrazole to pyrrole is 1:1, washing the obtained product with acetone, and drying to obtain a ligand; and (S2) adding the ligand and triethylamine to N,N-dimethylacetamide, ultrasonically processing to fully dissolve to obtain a solution (I), adding a copper salt to a container with deionized water and fully dissolving to prepare a solution (II), slowly adding the solution (I) to the solution (II), mixing, transferring to a high-pressure reaction kettle, placing in an oven, reacting at 80-100 degrees C for 2 days, cooling to room temperature at 0.2 degrees C/minute, filtering, washing with acetone, and drying at 40 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing metal-organic framework used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of ligand and the copper salt is 1:(0.5-1.1). The mass-to-volume ratio of the ligand and triethylamine is (0.4-0.6) g:1 ml. The volume ratio of N,N-dimethylacetamide and deionized water is 10:(1-2). The copper salt is copper(II) acetate monohydrate. The ligand is 2,7,12,17-tetrakis(1H-pyrazol-4-yl)-21,22,23,24-tetraazapentacyclo(16.2.1.1.1.1)tetracosa-1,3,5,7,9,11(23),12,14,16,18(21),19-undecaene.

PN CN112156221-A; CN112156221-B
TI Method for preparing pyrogen-free biocompatible medical glue material, involves carrying ionic liquid to process base material to obtain required medical glue material, when base material is not in contact with ionic liquid.
AU WANG W
   TIAN X
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG Y
   WANG F
   PENG X
   PENG T
   LI X
AE BEIJING FUALIE SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV CO L (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   WEINASAIDE BEIJING MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (WEIN-Non-standard)
GA 202104026F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing the cyanoacrylate alkyl ester compound with polylactic acid to obtain a medical adhesive matrix for use. The aerogel particles are added to the medical glue matrix, and stirred and mixed thoroughly to a gel state to obtain a matrix material for use. The base material is put in a closed container filled with ionic liquid, the air is removed and the container is filled with carbon dioxide. The temperature and pressure in the closed container is controlled to make the carbon dioxide gas in a supercritical state. The ionic liquid is carried to process the base material to obtain the required medical glue material, when the base material is not in contact with the ionic liquid. The medical glue material is sterilized to get the product. The alkyl cyanoacrylate compound includes a mixture of n-butylcyanoacrylate and n-octyl cyanoacrylate. The ionic liquid includes choline chloride ionic liquid or choline bromide ionic liquid.
   USE - Method for preparing pyrogen-free biocompatible medical glue material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The long-term bonding strength is greatly improved and is more suitable for clinical application.

PT P
PN CN112023934-A
TI Preparing copper indium bimetallic single atom            catalyst by dissolving soluble zinc salt in mixed            solvent of absolute ethanol and N,N-dimethylformamide,            dissolving 2-methylimidazole in absolute ethanol,            aging, crystallizing and mixing.
AU YUAN J
   LIU F
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2020C43305
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing copper indium bimetallic                single atom catalyst by (i) dissolving soluble zinc                salt, copper salt and indium salt in a mixed                solvent of absolute ethanol and                N,N-dimethylformamide to obtain solution (A), and                dissolving 2-methylimidazole in absolute ethanol to                obtain solution (B), mixing solution (A) and                solution (B) at normal temperature to obtain a                precursor solution, aging and crystallizing to                obtain precursor powder copper/indium-doped                zeolitic imidazolate framework (ZIF-8), (ii) mixing                the precursor powder copper/indium-doped ZIF-8 with                the nitrogen source material, uniformly grinding to                obtain a bimetallic monoatomic catalyst through                high temperature pyrolysis under the protection of                inert gas, and (iii) using the prepared catalyst as                the cathode material, electrocatalyzing carbon                dioxide, and efficiently reducing to methanol in                the aqueous phase.
   USE - Method for preparing copper indium bimetallic                single atom catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method eliminates tedious processing steps                and has the characteristics of simple and easy                operation, and realizes the high-efficiency                electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to                methanol in the aqueous phase.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the                  soluble indium salt comprises indium                  acetylacetonate, the soluble zinc salt comprises                  zinc nitrate, zinc chloride, and/or zinc acetate,                  and the soluble copper salt comprises copper                  chloride, copper acetate and/or copper nitrate.                  In step (ii), the nitrogen source material                  comprises dicyandiamide, melamine and/or                  graphitic carbon nitride. In step (ii), the inert                  gas comprises nitrogen, argon and/or helium.                  Preferred Composition: In step (i), the molar                  ratio of the soluble zinc salt to the indium salt                  is 15:1-30:1. In step (i), the molar ratio of the                  soluble indium salt to copper salt is 1:1-1.3:1.                  In step (i), the molar ratio of the soluble zinc                  salt to 2-methylimidazole is 1:4-1:8. In step                  (ii), the mass ratio of the precursor powder to                  nitrogen source substance is 1:1.5-1:4. Preferred                  Conditions: In step (ii), the calcination is                  carried out at 950-1100 degrees C at a heating                  rate of 2-5 degrees C/minute for 1-2 hours.
DC J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN111499498-A
TI Extracting high-purity hydroxytyrosol from fermentation broth by adding dopamine and polyallylamine to reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, filtering fermentation broth, freeze-drying, dissolving in ester solvent, filtering and drying.
AU GUO H
   ZHANG H
   DAI J
   GAO S
   HU G
   LU W
AE JIANGSU HUILI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202077221Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting high-purity hydroxytyrosol from fermentation broth, involves (a) adding dopamine and polyallylamine to the fermentation broth and introducing carbon dioxide to adjust the pH to acidity to obtain an acidic fermentation broth, (b) filtering the acidic fermentation broth to remove bacteria, and concentrating by ultrafiltration to further remove impurities such as protein, (c) filtering to remove small organic molecules such as pigments, (d) adjusting the pH to 2-4 with acetic acid, blowing nitrogen, carrying out thin film evaporation and concentration, and controlling the temperature at 35-40 degrees C to obtain a concentrated solution, (e) freeze-drying the concentrated liquid into solids, and (f) dissolving the solids in an ester solvent at room temperature, filtering out the clear liquid, drying the clear liquid at 40 degrees C to form solids to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is used for extracting high-purity hydroxytyrosol from fermentation broth.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively purifies hydroxytyrosol in the fermentation broth and effectively ensures that the product is not oxidized by air during the extraction process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises adding 40-60 %mass, preferably 40-45 %mass dopamine, and 1-10 %mass, preferably 5-10 %mass polyallylamine. The step (b) comprises performing concentration by using a 600-1000 kD ultrafiltration membrane. The step (c) comprises filtering with activated carbon and/or diatomaceous earth. Preferred Components: The ester solvent in step (f) is ethyl acetate, methyl acetate, butyl acetate, ethyl butyrate, and/or ethyl propionate.

PN FR3091993-A1; WO2020156981-A1; FR3091993-B1; CN113474000-A; EP3917554-A1; US2022096361-A1
TI Obtaining extract of patchouli leaves by            harvesting upper part of aerial parts of patchouli,            drying and crushing it, extracting supercritical carbon            dioxide in presence of co-solvent, recovering crude            extract and filtering mixture.
AU IMBERT I
   COQUET C
   GONDRAN C
   LABARRADE F
   BORSOTTO J
   GARNIER S
   DUROURE L
   KOKE C
AE ISP INVESTMENTS LLC (ISPI-C)
   JAFER LTD (JAFE-Non-standard)
   ISP INVESTMENTS LLC (ISPI-C)
   JAFER ENTERPRISES R & D SL (JAFE-Non-standard)
   JAFER LTD (JAFE-Non-standard)
   JAFER ENTERPRISES R & D SL (JAFE-Non-standard)
   ISP INVESTMENTS LLC (ISPI-C)
   JAFER ENTERPRISES R & D SL (JAFE-Non-standard)
   JAFER ENTERPRISES R & D SLU (JAFE-Non-standard)
   ISP INVESTMENTS LLC (ISPI-C)
GA 2020718480
AB    NOVELTY - Method for obtaining an extract of patchouli                (Pogostemon cablin) leaves involves (a) harvesting                upper part of aerial parts of patchouli, drying and                crushing or freezing the mixture, (b) extracting                supercritical carbon dioxide in presence of a                co-solvent i.e. a hydro-alcoholic solution of                ethanol at a concentration of 50-96 volume/volume%,                and (c) recovering the crude patchouli extract                which is decolorized in presence of activated                carbon and filtering the mixture, before                evaporation of co-solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining an extract                of patchouli leaves P.cablin, which is used in                cosmetic composition for caring skin (integuments),                improving skin appearance, combating signs of skin                aging, improving hydration of skin and                strengthening barrier function (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                cosmetic composition, which comprises 2.5-4.5% and                preferably 3-4% phenolic compounds, and 15-48%,                preferably 20.5-42.5%, more preferably 26-32% lipid                compounds, and solubilized patchouli extract                obtained by the method and a medium as an active                agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Component: The hydro-alcoholic                  ethanol solution is at a concentration of 60-80                  volume/volume%, preferably 70 volume/volume%. The                  mass ratio between the supercritical carbon                  dioxide and co-solvent is 15 volume/volume% and                  the mass ratio between the supercritical carbon                  dioxide and dried patchouli leaves is 10-70                  volume/volume%, preferably 30-50 volume/volume%.                  The leaf extract further comprises 50-80%,                  preferably 55-75%, more preferably 60-70%                  volatile compounds. The solubilized patchouli                  extract is at a concentration of 0.1-10%,                  preferably 0.5-5%. Preferred Method: The method                  involves performing extraction at 35-70 degrees                  C, preferably 40-65 degrees C, more preferably                  50-60 degrees C under a pressure within extractor                  of 90-1000 bar, preferably 160-800 bar, more                  preferably 230-700 bar. The method further                  involves (d) dissolving crude extract obtained in                  step (c) in a solvent of saturated or unsaturated                  fatty alcohol type comprising 8-30C to obtain a                  concentration of crude extract 2-6 wt.% of total                  weight of the solubilized extract.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological. Test details are described but                no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Endocannabinoid receptors CB2 inhibitor;                Endocannabinoid receptors TRPV1 suppressor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)

   PETRICK S (PETR-Individual)
GA 202071329G
AB    NOVELTY - Thermal process involves consisting of organic carbon, such as plastic, wood, paper, frying fats and oils, as well as leftover. Heated portion wise within a short time 0.5 seconds to 5.0 seconds. The target temperature is in between 570 angstrom centigrade and 900 angstrom centigrade. The heating takes place in a chamber made of metal or ceramic and contains an unreactive atmosphere. Atmosphere is made from argon, helium or other noble gasses or from carbon dioxide (CO2) or preferably from nitrogen (N2) consist. The carbon is removed from the hot zone and collected in a suitable vessel with the help of light gas current. The used gas does not reacts with the H2 when it flows out. Noble gasses such as argon, helium or even carbon dioxide (CO2) or preferably nitrogen (N2) suitable.

PN IN202041028146-A; IN358457-B
TI Producing glycerol carbonate by direct carbonation reaction involves reacting glycerol and carbon dioxide in the presence of metal-based Schiff base complex catalysts, deep eutectic solvents reaction media, and the water adsorbent.
AU PALANIVELU K
   KRISHNAVENI V
AE UNIV CHENNAI ANNA (UYCH-Non-standard)
GA 202082339J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing glycerol carbonate by direct carbonation reaction involves reacting glycerol and carbon dioxide (CO2) at the reaction temperature of 30-90 degrees C and 7 bar CO2 for 24 hours in the presence of metal-based Schiff base complex catalysts, deep eutectic solvents (DESs) reaction media, and the water adsorbent for forming glycerol carbonate product. The glycerol and CO2 are mixed in 1:1 -mole ratio, glycerol and DESs are mixed in 3:1 mole ratio and the dosage of metal-based Schiff base complex catalysts is 1 wt.% to the initial mass of glycerol. The water adsorbent i.e., molecular sieve 3 angstorm is in the amount of 20% (w/v).
   USE - Method for producing glycerol carbonate by direct carbonation reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produced glycerol carbonate by direct carbonation reaction, that utilize the industrial combustion waste gas CO2 for the production of high value-add glycerol carbonate product, and utilize biodiesel manufacturing industrial waste that is glycerol for the production of value-add glycerol carbonate product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The water adsorbent is molecular sieve is 3 angstrom. The water adsorbent is pre-activated at 300 degrees C for 24 hours. The reline-DES solvent consists of choline chloride and urea in 1:2 mole ratio. Preferred Components: Th metal is selected from titanium, iron, aluminum, magnesium, and calcium metals. The metal-based Schiff base complex catalyst is represented by formula (I).. Preferred Process: The method of producing metal-based Schiff base complex catalyst by one-pot synthesis for producing glycerol carbonate involves reacting Schiff base solution and titanium isopropoxide in 1:1 mole ratio at 80 degrees C for 24 hours in the presence of potassium carbonate (K2CO3) catalyst (40 wt.% to the initial mass of L-arginine) and the water-methanol mixed solvent in 1:2 (v/v) ratio for forming the titanium metal-based Schiff base complex. The Schiff base solution is prepared by mixing L-arginine and salicylaldehyde in 1:2 mole ratio.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110950761-A
TI Synthesizing seven-position side chain of oxycephalosporin comprises e.g. adding toluene and acetone, p-hydroxyphenylacetic acid to reactor, adding strong base catalyst to reaction solution, passing carbon dioxide and concentrating.
AU LV L
   DAI J
   QIU Q
   SONG D
   MU H
AE SHANDONG JINCHENGKERUI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020282650
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing seven-position side chain of oxycephalosporin comprises (1) adding a mixture of toluene and acetone, p-hydroxyphenylacetic acid, acid binding agent, phase transfer catalyst and benzyl chloride to the reactor to complete the reaction, (2) adding strong base catalyst to the reaction solution, adjusting the pH value of the solution to not greater than 13, and passing carbon dioxide at -50 to 55 degrees C, concentrating and crystallizing to obtain the product, where the volume ratio of toluene to acetone in the mixed solution is 1:4-1:0.25, the ratio of the mass of p-glycolic acid to the volume of the mixed solution is 0.6:1-0.7:1 and the mass ratio of p-glycolic acid to benzyl chloride of methoxybenzoic acid is 0.6:1-0.8:1.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing seven-position side chain of oxycephalosporin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple operation; is economical; has improved production capacity, and improved yield, reaching more than 90%, and high purity of the product, reaching more than 99%; and obtains surprising technical effects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid binding agent comprises sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, and/or potassium bicarbonate. The phase transfer catalyst comprises polyether catalyst, cyclic crown ether catalyst, quaternary ammonium salt catalyst, tertiary amine catalyst and/or quaternary ammonium base catalyst, tetrabutylammonium bromide, polyethylene glycol and cetyltrimethylammonium bromide. The strong base catalysis is organometallic lithium compound, Grignard reagent, and/or alkyl copper lithium. The organometallic lithium compound comprises butyl lithium, lithium diisopropylamide, and/or benzyl lithium. Preferred Method: The temperature of the reaction is 60-80 degrees C. The method further comprises the step of stirring during the reaction, and the stirring speed is 50-500 revolutions/minute and nitrogen protection conditions under the reaction conditions. The reaction is completely specific as the residual p-hydroxyphenylacetic acid in the reaction solution is not less than 3 g/l.

PN US2020047114-A1; US11478744-B2
TI Membrane used in chamber for separating e.g.            hydrogen, comprises polymer substrate comprises pore            channels having preset average diameter and average            length, and metal-organic framework comprising metal            ion and organic ligand.
AU AL-MAYTHALONY B
   USMAN M
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
   KING ABDULAZIZ CITY SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KIAB-C)
GA 202012710E
AB    NOVELTY - A membrane comprises a polymer substrate                having pore channels and 0.1-50 wt.% metal-organic                framework comprising a metal ion and an organic                ligand which is coordinated to the metal ion, with                respect to a total weight of the membrane. The pore                channels have an average diameter of 0.1-2 mu m,                and an average length of 2-100 mu m. The                metal-organic framework is arranged on a wall                surface of the pore channels and an outer surface                of the polymer substrate.
   USE - Membrane e.g. metal organic framework membrane                used in chamber e.g. cylindrical membrane module                for separating hydrogen and carbon dioxide from gas                mixture of hydrogen and carbon dioxide, oxygen,                nitrogen, methane, ethylene, ethane, propylene, and                propane (all claimed). Can also be used for                separating liquid mixtures by pervaporation, water                treatment, air purifiers, chemical filters, oil and                gas refineries, fermenters, and bioreactors.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane has excellent flexibility and                effectively separates target gas from gas                mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of membrane; and
   (2) recovery of gas from gas mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The metal-organic                  framework has an average thickness of 100-2000                  nm. Preferred Components: The metal ion is an ion                  of at least one metal chosen from transition                  metal, a post-transition metal, and an alkaline                  earth metal, preferably copper, zinc, iron,                  nickel, cobalt, manganese, chromium, cadmium,                  magnesium, calcium and zirconium. The metal                  organic framework is HKUST-1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The wall surface of                  the pore channels and the outer surface of the                  polymer substrate are modified with carboxylate                  groups. The organic ligand has at least two                  carboxylate groups, preferably                  benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer substrate                  comprises at least one polyester chosen from                  poly(ethylene terephthalate), poly(trimethylene                  terephthalate), poly(butylene terephthalate),                  poly(ethylene naphthalate), and                  poly(cyclohexylenedimethylene terephthalate),                  preferably poly(ethylene terephthalate). The                  membrane is manufactured by ion-irradiating                  polymer substrate with heavy ion have a fluence                  of 103-1010 heavy ions/cm2 and an average kinetic                  energy of 5-25 MeV per nucleon, etching                  sensitized polymer with etchant solution                  comprising 0.5-5 M sodium hydroxide, and                  immersing polymer substrate in solution (A)                  comprising 0.01-100 mM metal ion, and solution                  (B) comprising 0.01-100 mM organic ligand.                  Preferred Property: The membrane has BET surface                  area of 100-500 m2/g.

PT P
PN WO2019229030-A1; CA3098517-A1; EP3801878-A1; US2021205783-A1
TI Preparation of hybrid inorganic-organic material            used for capturing carbon dioxide, involves dissolving            and/or suspending and/or dispersing inorganic and            organic material in ionic liquid, and coprecipitating            inorganic and organic material.
AU OCONNOR P
AE CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
   CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
   CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
GA 2019A2317L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of hybrid inorganic-organic                material involves dissolving and/or suspending                and/or dispersing an inorganic and organic material                in an ionic liquid solvent to form an ionic                solution/suspension/dispersion, and coprecipitating                the inorganic and organic materials together.
   USE - Preparation of hybrid inorganic-organic                material such as active carbon-metal oxide hybrid                material, and active carbon-metal carbonate-metal                oxide hybrid material used as sorbent granulate for                capturing carbon dioxide, and manufacture of                component in catalyst sorbent, building and                construction material, packaging material, paper                material, coating and polymer in electrical                application (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of hybrid                inorganic-organic material with excellent flame                retardance, tensile property, adhesiveness, and                porosity. The inorganic material can improved the                strength of carbonized hybrid material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for hybrid                inorganic-organic material, which comprises                micro-dispersed domain of organic and/or inorganic                material having average diameter of less than 1000                nm, preferably 300 nm or more, and nano-dispersed                domain of organic and/or inorganic material having                average diameter of 10-300, preferably                100-200.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid                  solvent is molten salt hydrate, salt having                  melting point of less than 200 degrees C,                  hydrates of inorganic salt, zinc halide, calcium                  chloride and/or lithium chloride, preferably                  hydrates of zinc chloride, optionally with minor                  amount of other organic component and/or                  inorganic salt hydrate, in which salt hydrate                  comprises 55-85 wt.% salt, and preferably zinc                  chloride hydroxide. The hybrid inorganic-organic                  material is carbon-alumina hybrid material, and                  fiber of organic material with dispersed domain                  of inorganic material, preferably alumina. The                  hybrid inorganic-organic material is carbonized                  and is active carbon-metal oxide hybrid material.                  Preferred Composition: The inorganic material                  comprises at least 80 wt.% non-carbon material,                  metal oxide chosen from aluminum oxide, silicon                  dioxide, zinc oxide, titanium oxide, iron oxide,                  and iron (III) oxide, preferably gamma -alumina                  or boehmite, in which metal oxide is optionally                  peptized before or during                  dissolution/suspension/dispersion in the ionic                  liquid, preferably by peptizing mixture of the                  dispersed metal oxide and organic component by                  adding water and/or base to increase the pH. The                  organic material comprises at least 80 wt.%                  carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen, preferably                  bio-origin, cellulose, hemi-cellulose, lignin                  and/or sugar. The hybrid inorganic-organic                  material comprises 5-95 wt.%, 20-80 wt.%                  preferably organic material. Preferred Process:                  The method further involves (a) dissolving,                  suspending, and/or dispersing inorganic or                  organic material in the ionic liquid solvent to                  form solution/suspension/dispersion (A), (b)                  dissolving, suspending, and/or dispersing                  inorganic or organic material in the obtained                  solution (A), creating                  solution/suspension/dispersion (B) comprising                  mixture of inorganic and organic material, (c)                  optionally adding additional ionic liquid (IL)                  solvent after step a and before step b or during                  addition of the inorganic or organic material                  into solution/suspension/dispersion (A), (d)                  optionally adding additional inorganic and/or                  organic materials and additional ionic liquid                  (IL) solvent to solution/suspension/dispersion                  (B), and (e) optionally adding acid to reduce the                  pH in dissolving, suspending and/or dispersing                  step (a) and/or (b). The precipitation in the                  ionic solution/suspension/dispersion is obtained                  by lowering the temperature, adding water,                  mechanical action, drying, and/or adding an                  anti-solvent. The precipitation is obtained by                  adding to the ionic solution anti-solvent chosen                  from ketone, ether, alkanol, alkanenitrile, and                  1-10C, preferably 4-6C anti-solvent. The                  anti-solvent is added to the ionic solution and                  the ionic solution comprising the precipitate                  material is (I) spun to shape the precipitate                  material in the form of fibers or (II)                  granulated, for example in fluid bed granulation,                  to shape the precipitate material in the form of                  particles. The method further involves drying,                  filtering, washing, sintering, calcining,                  shaping, and carbonization. The carbonization                  step in which organic material in the precipitate                  material is carbonized to form carbon homogeneous                  hybrid carbon-inorganic material. The precipitate                  material is carbonized to form active                  carbon-metal oxide hybrid material, and active                  carbon-metal carbonate-metal oxide hybrid                  material.

PN CN110452558-A; CN110452558-B
TI Preparing nano-calcium carbonate useful for hard            polyvinyl chloride, comprises e.g. adjusting specific            gravity of the refined lime milk, performing            carbonation reaction of mixed gas containing carbon            dioxide, modifying and post processing.
AU LAN Q
   ZHU Y
   LIU L
   LIAO J
   FENG J
   LI R
AE GUANGXI HESHAN HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI HESHAN HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019980054
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-calcium carbonate, comprises                adjusting specific gravity of the refined lime milk                to 1.060-1.070, then introducing into a bubbling                carbonation reaction kettle, adjusting the                temperature of the lime milk to 25-30 degrees C,                carrying out carbonation reaction of the mixed gas                containing carbon dioxide by adjusting pH to 7 to                obtain nano-calcium carbonate suspension, then                heating the nano-calcium carbonate suspension to                75-90 degrees C, adding surfactant composed of                carboxylated polyethylene wax, epoxy sunflower oil                and xylitol, stirring for 30-60 minutes, then                surface modifying, filtering the surface modified                nano-calcium carbonate suspension and dehydrating,                drying and crushing to obtain final product.
   USE - The nano-calcium carbonate is useful for hard                polyvinyl chloride (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-calcium carbonate: has low moisture                absorption, excellent processing performance,                mechanical performance and anti-yellowing                performance; satisfies toughening requirement of                filling hard polyvinyl chloride to improve                yellowing of hard polyvinyl chloride, turns red and                other color phase problems.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of                  surfactant is 2-3% by mass of the calcium                  carbonate dry substance. The surfactant is                  composed of 30-40 wt.% carboxylated polyethylene                  wax, 55-65 wt.% epoxy sunflower oil and 5 wt.%                  xylitol. Preferred Method: The carbonation                  reaction is carried out by introducing mixed gas                  having carbon dioxide having concentration of                  33%. The drying is carried out in an oven at                  100-110 degrees C.

   LIAO J
AE GUANGXI LIUZHOU IRON & STEEL GROUP CO (GUAN-Non-standard)

GA 2019795043
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves reflecting a laser line and an angle laser vibration mirror on a fixing position. A laser vibration mirror is adjusted by a reflecting point as an origin. AB    NOVELTY - Waste-free yeast culturing involves heating cereal grains to 90 degrees C and feeding into a mash vessel, introducing a liquefaction enzyme, then cooling to 60 degrees C and after demeration cooling up to 32 degrees C , adding known components, and then directing to the culture tank. After filling the fermentation tank, oxygen gas is delivered in the amount ensuring maintenance of carbon dioxide concentration in the gas leaving the ladle in a concentration of not less than 80%, while the tub constantly maintains the pH, and the excess wort flows gravitationally to the vat, continuously dispensed raw wort from the buffer tank, and the quantity of wort is controlled automatically, depending on the flow of wort from the vat. The part of the wort flowing to the ladle is directed to the tank with the mixer, where it is acidified at pH 2.1-2.5 and preferably after 30 minutes flows into the ladle, which process is carried out continuously cycle by cycle.

PN CN109609731-A; CN109609731-B
TI Performing high chromium grinding forging isothermal quenching heat treatment involves grinding high chromium forging ball while introducing nitrogen, and forging high chromium forging ball into graphite powder.
AU CHEN Q
AE NINGGUO HUAFENG WEAR RESISTING MATERIAL (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2019357838
AB    NOVELTY - Performing high chromium grinding forging isothermal quenching heat treatment involves grinding high chromium forging ball at 900-920 degrees C for 30-45 minutes while introducing nitrogen, and forging high chromium forging ball into the graphite powder. The graphite powder is uniformly adhered to the treated high chromium forging ball surface, and is placed at 700-850 degrees C for 2-3 hours. The mixed gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide is introduced while heating process. The mixed gas comprises nitrogen and carbon dioxide in volume ratio of 65-80:20-35. The high chromium slab is forged into a mixed salt of potassium permanganate and sodium nitrite at 290-300 degrees C for 5-15 minutes, and naturally cooled to room temperature, and then sprayed with water to rinse off the residual phosphoric acid on the surface.
   USE - Method for performing high chromium grinding forging isothermal quenching heat treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves the toughness and strength of the high chromium grinding forging by the multi-stage austempering, and improves the service life of the high chromium grinding and forging.

GA 2019439773
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing progenitor osteogenic cells involves (a) taking a tissue sample from periosteum of a flat bone having a size of 5x 5 mm, (b) subjecting the biopsy sample to mechanical and enzymatic treatment after 1-2 hours, incubating the sample in type II collagenase solution, and centrifuging at 800 revolutions per minute (rpm) and at 18-20 degrees C for 5 minutes, (c) resuspending the precipitate in growth medium by adding fetal calf serum and antibiotic agent/antimycotic agent in a standard concentration, (d) transferring the cell suspension to a 25 cm2 culture plastic bottle, and culturing cells in a carbon dioxide incubator at 5% carbon dioxide for 10-15 days until a subconfluent monolayer is formed, and (e) culturing cells in a microcarriers in a ratio of 2:1, where the microcarriers are pre-treated with a solution of type I collagen and then treated with 1% fibrinogen solution for 1 hour.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polyimide compound comprises 50 mol% or more, preferably 70 mol% or more structural units comprising the pentafluorosulfanyl group and 10 mol% or more, preferably 50 mol% or more structural units comprising active hydrogen atoms with respect to all structural units constituting the polyimide compound, and a pentafluorosulfanyl group in a diamine component. The polyimide compound comprises a pyrrolidine-2,5-dione-based structural unit of formula (I), or a pyrrolidine-2,5-dione-based structural unit of formula (IIa) and a pyrrolidine-2,5-dione-based structural unit of formula (IIb). The active hydrogen atom is a hydrogen atom contained in -OH, -COOH, -NHCO-, or -SO2NH-. The structural unit (I) is a preferably structural unit of formula (III) or (IV). The gas separation membrane is a gas-separation composite film (10), in which the gas-separation layer is provided on a gas-permeable support layer. The support layer comprises a porous layer (2) and a non-woven fabric layer. The gas separation membrane comprises the non-woven fabric layer, the porous layer and the gas-separation layer, in this order.Z1-Z3 = tetravalent group;X1 = divalent group comprising pentafluorosulfanyl group and active hydrogen atom;X2 = divalent group comprising pentafluorosulfanyl group and not comprising active hydrogen atom;X3 = divalent group comprising active hydrogen atom and not comprising pentafluorosulfanyl group;L2 = single bond or divalent group;R1,R2 = substituent;q = 0-3;andr = 0-5.

TI Coal-fired power plant desulfurization wastewater concentration softening treatment method comprises e.g. placing raw water of desulfurization wastewater of coal fired power plant into raw water concentration tower.
AU ZHAO G
AE GUANGDONG YUEDIAN JINGHAI ELECTRICITY GE (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019024709
AB    NOVELTY - Coal-fired power plant desulfurization wastewater concentration softening treatment comprises (i) placing raw water of desulfurization wastewater of coal-fired power plant into raw water concentration tower, heating, and concentrating in raw water concentration tower to obtain first-stage concentrated water, (ii) placing first-stage concentrated water into reaction tower, reacting with calcium hydroxide, precipitating and separating to obtain secondary concentrated water, (iii) placing secondary concentrated water into carbon dioxide reaction tower, introducing carbon dioxide gas, reacting, adjusting pH, mixing secondary reaction tower with sodium carbonate, reacting, and precipitating in secondary sedimentation tank, and (iv) placing secondary concentrated water into crystallizer concentration tower for concentrating wet crystallizer produced tertiary-stage concentrated water, conveying to desulfurization absorption tower, and concentrated softening desulfurized wastewater.
   USE - The method is useful for coal-fired power plant desulfurization wastewater concentration softening treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can recycle the sodium chloride crystal salt, and can be reused after processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for coal-fired power plant desulfurization wastewater concentration softening treatment device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the coal-fired power plant desulfurization wastewater concentration softening treatment device.

TI Method for measuring hydrocarbon component minimum phase pressure influence, involves measuring carbon dioxide minimum miscibility pressure of various oil samples by hanging drop method and determining gas and well flow composition.
AU WANG X
   LV C
   LUN Z
   WANG R
   ZHAO S
   ZHOU X
   ZHAO C
   XU G
   ZHOU G
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP PETROLEUM (SNPC-C)
GA 2018A1350L
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrocarbon component minimum phase pressure influence measuring method involves measuring the carbon dioxide minimum miscibility pressure of various oil samples by hanging drop method, after measuring, respectively determining the gas and well flow composition in the hanging drop chamber of the corresponding oil sample. The resulting measurement results are utilized and calculated the content of carbon components in each oil sample under the formation conditions of multiple oil samples. The relationship between the measured content of the same carbon component in multiple oil samples and the minimum miscible pressure of multiple oil samples is analyzed to obtain the effects of different carbon components on the mixed phase of the oil sample and estimated the influence of hydrocarbon component on the minimum miscible pressure of oil and gas according to the analysis result.
   USE - Method for measuring hydrocarbon component minimum phase pressure influence.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables measuring the hydrocarbon component minimum phase pressure influence with effectively improving oil displacement efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a hydrocarbon component minimum phase pressure influence measured by the method.

PN CN108728139-A
TI Processing nitrogen-free coal pyrolysis comprises using four feed gases as fuel fluids for coal pyrolysis, one is oxygen and one is low-pressure steam that has been purified and recovered after coal pyrolysis.
AU QU Y
   ZHOU H
   ZHANG X
   CHEN B
   YAO Q
AE CHINA SINOGY REFINERY CHEM ENG CO LTD (CHSI-Non-standard)
GA 2018883024
AB    NOVELTY - Processing nitrogen-free coal pyrolysis comprises using four feed gases as fuel fluids for coal pyrolysis, one is oxygen, one is low-pressure steam that has been purified and recovered after coal pyrolysis, one is carbon dioxide purified and recovered after coal pyrolysis and one is purified gas that is purified and returned to the furnace after coal pyrolysis.
   USE - The method is useful for processing nitrogen-free coal pyrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the ineffective gas in the system and achieves the effect of high rate and is energy saving.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for coal pyrolysis nitrogen-free process system comprising a combustion unit, a coal pyrolysis unit, a tar recovery unit and a purification unit, where the combustion unit is provided with a gas distribution system, and the gas distribution system has the following four gas distribution pipes an oxygen pipeline, a low pressure steam pipeline connected to the purification unit and a carbon dioxide pipeline connected to the purification unit and a purified gas pipeline connected to the purification unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume of oxygen in the fuel fluid is 15-30% by volume. The pressure of the fuel fluid is 0.05-0.1 MPa (G) and in terms of volume content the oxygen in the fuel fluid accounts for 15-30%, low pressure steam accounts for 10-25%, carbon dioxide accounts for 10-25% and purified gas accounts for 40-60%. The volume ratio of low pressure steam to carbon dioxide is preferably 1:1-9. The purified gas comprises 12-25 wt.% carbon monoxide , 30-70 wt.% hydrogen and 15-30 wt.% methane by volume. Preferred Method: The oxygen in the fuel fluid is room temperature and the pressure is 0.05-0.1 MPa (G). The low pressure steam temperature is 100-120 degrees C, the pressure is 0.05-0.1 MPa (G), the carbon dioxide is room temperature, the pressure is 0.05-0.1 MPa (G) and the purified gas is room temperature and the pressure is 0.05-0.1 MPa (G). The method comprises after the fuel fluid in the combustion unit is burned the generated high temperature combustion gas enters the coal pyrolysis unit for pyrolysis of low rank coal and after the low rank coal is pyrolyzed in the coal pyrolysis unit, where the solid portion is quenched by the quenching unit to be sent to the blue carbon product and the gas portion is recovered by the tar recovery unit to recover the coal tar and then purified by the purification unit, the self-purifying unit outputs four materials i.e. one is purified low-pressure steam, one is purified carbon dioxide, one is recycled gas and one is dried gas product, among them the low-pressure steam, carbon dioxide and purified gas are mixed as a raw material gas with a single stream of oxygen as a fuel fluid to enter the combustion unit and the dried gas product is sent as a product. The combustion temperature in the combustion unit is 600-1000 degrees C. The pyrolysis temperature of the coal in the coal pyrolysis unit is 500-800 degrees C; the temperature of the gas part taken from the gas outlet of the coal pyrolysis unit 2 is 80-100 degrees C, the gas composition is in terms of volume ratio is 6-25 wt.% carbon dioxide, 8-14 wt.% carbon monoxide, 20-60 wt.% hydrogen, 10-20 wt.% methane, 1-30 wt.% CmHn, and a very small amount of other gases.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The combustion unit is a burner. The coal pyrolysis unit is an internal thermal pyrolysis furnace, and the coal pyrolysis unit is further configured with a quenching unit. The tar recovery unit includes a gas-liquid separator, a cooling tower, an electric tar catcher, and a cyclone trap connected in sequence, and an ammonia recovery unit is connected after the cyclone trap. The ammonia recovery unit is connected to the gas-liquid separator through a circulation pipeline. The purification unit includes a gas desulfurization device, a conversion device, and a decarburization device.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

TI Highly antibacterial controlled degradable magnesium-based composite bone implant comprises e.g. magnesium alloy implant matrix in which in situ synthesized antibacterial nano copper particles are dispersed.
AU XIA M
   LIU A
   LIN Y
   LI N
   DING H
   CHEN Z
   YUAN T
   YE W
AE HUAIYIN TECHNOLOGY INST (HUIA-C)
GA 2018707337
AB    NOVELTY - Highly antibacterial controlled degradable magnesium-based composite bone implant comprises magnesium alloy implant matrix in which in situ synthesized antibacterial nano copper particles are dispersed, and magnesium silicate-magnesium oxide-nano-reinforced phase carrying controlled degradation function.
   USE - Used as highly antibacterial controlled degradable magnesium-based composite bone implant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for forming highly antibacterial controlled degradable magnesium-based composite bone implant, comprising (i) acquiring a three-dimensional model of a bone implant, (ii) weighing spherical magnesium alloy powder, nano-copper oxide powder and nano-silica powder, ball-milling and mixing under the environment of high-purity argon gas and high-purity carbon dioxide mixed gas to obtain composite forming powder, (iii) forming composite forming powder in situ by a laser selective melting process under a mixed atmosphere of high purity argon and high purity carbon dioxide to form nano copper particles and magnesium silicate-magnesium oxide-nano-reinforced phase dispersed in the magnesium matrix composite bone implant, and (iv) subjecting the bone implant obtained in the step (iii) to vacuum stress relief annealing treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nano-copper particles and the magnesium silicate-magnesium oxide-nano-reinforced phase are formed by in-situ reaction of a spherical magnesium alloy, nano-copper oxide and nano-silica, where the spherical magnesium alloy, the nano copper oxide and the nano silica have a mass ratio of 500:10:1-500:1:10. The spherical magnesium alloy is a medical magnesium-aluminum-zinc alloy, a magnesium-zinc-manganese alloy, a magnesium-calcium alloy or a magnesium-neodymium-zinc-ziconium alloy. The mass ratio of the spherical magnesium alloy powder, the nano copper oxide powder and the nano silica powder in the step (ii) is 500:10:1 to 500:1:10. In the step (ii): the spherical magnesium alloy powder has a particle diameter of 10-60 mu m, a sphericity of not greater than 90%, and a purity of not greater than 99.7; the size of the nano-sized copper oxide is 10-50 nm, and the size of the nano-silica is 30-100 nm. Preferred Method: The ball milling mixing in the step (ii) is performed by a low temperature vacuum protection ball milling process, and the process conditions are: the process is cooled by liquid nitrogen, the ball milling is carried out at 0-25 degrees C for 5-8 minutes for each forward rotation under vacuum of less than 0.1 Pa and rotation speed of 50-150 revolutions/minute, stopped immediately after 5 minutes, then the ball milling is 5-8 minutes at milling time of 2-4 hours. The laser selective melting process conditions in the step (iii) are: the laser beam energy density is 60-210 J/m3, the laser beam spot size is 10-70 mu m, the powder bed thickness is 20-50 mu m, and the oxygen content is less than 10 ppm. The vacuum stress relief annealing treatment in the step (iv) is carried out at 50-150 degrees C for 2-8 hours under vacuum degree of less than 0.1 Pa.

Perilla leaves, 5-15 pts. wt., preferably 8-12 pts. wt. sweet Osmanthus flower, 8-15 pts. wt., preferably 9-13 pts. wt. lobster sauce, 3-7 pts. wt., preferably 4-6 pts. wt. tsaoko cardamom, 3-6 pts. wt., preferably 4-5 pts. wt. cortex acanthopanacis, 3-7 pts. wt., preferably 4-6 pts. wt. peach kernel, 2-5 pts. wt., preferably 3-4 pts. wt. evening primrose and 1-3 pts. wt., preferably 1.5-2.5 pts. wt. sea buckthorn. Preferred Method: The process of rosemary extraction comprises adding 15% ethanol to rosemary according to material to liquid ratio of 1:4-6, ultrasonically processing 10-15 minutes under the power of 540 W, carrying out microwave radiation for 5-8 minutes, repeating radiation for 4-6 times, cooling and filtering, combining the filtrates, evaporating the ethanol, adjusting the pH to 2-2.5 with hydrochloric acid to obtain extract A, extracting with ethyl acetate for 3 times, where the volume ratio of extract A to ethyl acetate is 5-7:3-4, and concentrating. The preparation of traditional Chinese medicinal extract comprises cleaning, crushing and mixing the all raw materials using water, heating and decocting for 2-5 hours, and concentrating.
   ACTIVITY - Gastrointestinal-Gen; Antilipemic; Antianginal; Cardiant; Vasotropic.

PN CN108334975-A; CN108334975-B
TI Method for dynamically predicting oxygen content in anhydrous environment, involves establishing dynamic prediction model of oxygen content to achieve dynamic prediction of oxygen content in anhydrous storage environment.
AU ZHANG X
   ZHANG Y
   GAO Q
   XIAO X
   LIU Y
   FU Z
   LIU X
AE UNIV CHINA AGRICULTURAL (UCAG-C)
   UNIV CHINA AGRICULTURAL (UCAG-C)
GA 201860616U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating correlation coefficient between time sequence of carbon dioxide, temperature and humidity. The time sequence of oxygen in anhydrous storage environment of group of samples is calculated. Association degree between the carbon dioxide, the temperature, the humidity and the oxygen of two adjacent time nodes are obtained based on the correlation coefficient. A dynamic prediction model of oxygen content is established to achieve dynamic prediction of the oxygen content in the anhydrous storage environment according to the time sequence of the oxygen and the maximum correlation coefficient.
   USE - Method for dynamically predicting oxygen content in anhydrous environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables accurately obtaining the oxygen content in the anhydrous storage environment, avoiding accidental error caused by the single oxygen obtaining process so as to improve economic loss caused by lack of oxygen during storage in effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for dynamically predicting oxygen content in anhydrous environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view illustrating a method for dynamically predicting oxygen content in anhydrous environment. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PT P
PN CN108300562-A
TI Preparation of peony seed oil involves crushing peony seed, low-temperature drying, supercritical carbon dioxide extracting, heating, adding sodium hydroxide and alumina, precipitating, active carclazyte decolorizing and vacuum distilling.
AU CHEN Z
AE ANHUI XINYUANKANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201859040U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of peony seed oil comprises shelling and removing impurity peony seed, soaking in 15-20 degrees C water for 8-10 hours, taking out, draining water, storing at low temperature and crushing to obtain 130-150 meshes moutan seed powder, low-temperature drying moutan seed powder to water content of 8-10%, soaking anhydrous ethanol for 4-5 hours and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain peony seed oil, heating peony seed oil to 50-60 degrees C, adding 9-11 wt.% sodium hydroxide solution for 15-20 minutes under 60-70 revolutions/minute, heating up to 70-80 degrees C, adding alumina, stirring for 20-30 minutes at 18-25 revolutions/minute, standing for 8-10 hours, separating, removing water layer and precipitating to obtain deacidified oil; and active carclazyte decolorizing of deacidified oil to obtain decolorized oil and deodorizing by vacuum distilling.
   USE - Method for preparing peony seed oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces high yield peony seed oil containing high unsaturated fatty acid content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dosage of sodium hydroxide solution is 65-68 times by weight of peony seed oil. The dosage of aluminum oxide is 1-2 times by weight of peony seed oil.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The powder is stored at -40-- 60 degrees C environment for 3-5 hours or stored in liquid nitrogen for 4-6 minutes. The low-temperature drying is carried out at 40-50 degrees C. The carbon dioxide extraction is carried out at 35-45 degrees C under for 23-35 MPa for 50-90 minutes, using cyclohexane as entrainer. The carbon dioxide flow rate is set at 15-18 L/hour. The flow rate of entrainer cyclohexane is 6-8% by flow rate of carbon dioxide. The active carclazyte decolorizing treatment involves adding deacidified oil with adding carclazyte 4-5% by weight of deacidified oil, stirring at 50-70 revolutions/minute, decolorizing at 95-110 degrees C for 40-60 minutes, cooling to 40-50 degrees C and filtering to obtain decolorized oil. The vacuum distillation is carried out at 150-170 degrees C under 0.04-0.06 MPa for 4-5 hours, stopping heat, cooling oil to 45 degrees C or less and removing vacuum to obtain peony seed oil. Preferred Components: The amount of anhydrous ethanol is 0.8-1.2 times by weight of peony seed.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D10-A01; D10-A02
IP C11B-001/04; C11B-001/10; C11B-003/02; C11B-003/06; C11B-003/10; C11B-003/12

AE UNIV SHANTOU (USHT-C)

GA 201817158B
AB    NOVELTY - A surface hydrophobic metal organic framework                material comprises a crystal outer surface                connected with functional hydrophobic group through                chemical modification. The metal organic framework                is zeolitic imidazolate framework-90                (ZIF-90).
   USE - Surface hydrophobic metal organic framework                material is useful for capturing carbon dioxide and                gas adsorption and separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The surface hydrophobic metal organic                framework material has high hydrothermal stability                and specific surface area and excellent hydrophobic                performance, and is simple and economical to                prepare.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) the method for preparing the surface                hydrophobic metal organic framework material, which                involves preparing metal organic framework material                ZIF-90, washing the solid product of metal organic                framework material ZIF-90 with ethanol, placing in                a flask, adding an amino straight-chain alkane                solution, heating for 4 hours, collecting a solid                product, washing the solid product with ethanol and                carrying out vacuum-drying activation; and
   (2) the method for enhancing hydrothermal                stability of metal organic framework material and                hydrophobicity of outer surface layer, which                involves connecting functional hydrophobic group on                surface layer of metal organic framework material                through chemical modification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The functional                  hydrophobic group is amino straight-chain alkane                  having a chain length of 6-18. The molar ratio of                  the amino straight-chain alkane to ZIF-90 is                  1:4-100. Preferred Method: The step of preparing                  metal organic framework material ZIF-90 involves                  adding organic ligand 2-imidazole formaldehyde                  and zinc nitrate hexahydrate in dimethylformamide                  to a mole ratio of 1:4, heating under stirring                  condition for 0.5 hour, adding water, and                  collecting the solid product, where the ratio of                  water to dimethylformamide is 1:1. Preferred                  Conditions: The heating step is carried out at                  60-80 degrees C. The vacuum drying activation is                  carried out at 50-200 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The solvent in the amino                  straight-chain alkanes solution is methanol,                  ethanol or dimethylformamide.

PN CN113926440-A
TI Preparing bimetallic composite absorbent useful for capturing carbon dioxide from high temperature flue gas, comprises e.g. dissolving strontium nitrate and cerium nitrate in deionized water, stirring to fully dissolve, adding citric acid and ethylene glycol, drying, foaming, calcining and cooling.
AU NIU M
   ZHANG S
   SONG G
   GU H
AE NANJING INST TECHNOLOGY (NANJ-C)
GA 202217790X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bimetallic composite absorbent comprises (S1) dissolving strontium nitrate and cerium nitrate in deionized water, stirring to fully dissolve, and obtaining aqueous solution, (S2) adding citric acid and ethylene glycol into the aqueous solution, where the total concentration ratio of strontium nitrate and cerium nitrate in the ethylene glycol, citric acid and aqueous solution is 4:2.5:1, mixing uniformly, stirring, and evaporating to form gel, (S3) drying and foaming the gel, where the drying temperature of the gel is 180degrees Celsius, and (S4) calcining at 500degrees Celsius for 1 hours, drying the foamed gel, calcining at 900degrees Celsius for 3 hours in an air atmosphere, grinding, and cooling to obtain bimetallic composite agent.
   USE - The bimetallic composite absorbent is useful for capturing carbon dioxide from high temperature flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbent reduces reaction temperature, effectively inhibits sintering, maintains utilization rate close to 100%, and has good application prospects in conversion and utilization of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) bimetallic composite absorbent, comprising strontium, cerium and oxygen elements i.e., Sr2CeO4; and
   (2) using method of bimetal composite absorbent to absorb high temperature flue gas containing carbon dioxide, comprising (i) passing the high-temperature flue gas containing carbon dioxide through the reactor containing the bimetallic composite absorbent, where the absorbent reacts with carbon dioxide to absorb carbon, and the reaction temperature is 800-925degrees Celsius, and (ii) completing the carbon absorption reaction, replacing the reactor with an inert gas, and performing decarburization reaction, where the reaction temperature is 850-1000degrees Celsius.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (S1), the molar ratio of strontium nitrate and cerium nitrate is not less than 2:1.

TI Palladium based metal material for preparing a            catalyst, which is used to catalyze the hydrogenation            of carbon dioxide to formic acid reaction, is a            palladium based monometallic material or a palladium            based bimetallic material.
AU DENG W
   REN G
   SUN L
   DI S
   YU T
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 202163993W
AB    NOVELTY - Palladium (Pd) based metal material is a Pd                based monometallic material or a Pd based                bimetallic material. The Pd based metal material is                a Pd based bimetal material, the second metal is                any one of iron (Fe), cobalt (Co), nickel (Ni),                copper (Cu), and zinc (Zn). The active metal Pd and                the second metal are all supported on a carrier in                a sub-nanometer or mono-atom dispersion state, and                including 0.001-20 wt.% Pd, and the content of the                second metal is 0-40 wt.%.
   USE - Palladium based metal material used for                preparing a catalyst, which is used to catalyze the                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to formic acid                reaction (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The palladium based metal material has the                advantages of high active metal dispersion,                adjustable structure and properties on the atomic                scale, and can realize the process of hydrogenation                of carbon dioxide at room temperature and pressure                to produce formic acid in terms of catalytic                effect, and is expected to solve the problem of low                efficiency of the heterogeneous catalyst in the                catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide into formic                acid under mild conditions, providing a successful                example for the capture and conversion of carbon                dioxide at room temperature and pressure, and the                preparation method is simple and low in production                cost, and has good practical application                value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a method for preparing Pd based metal                materials, which involves immersing a carbon                material or a nitrogen-doped carbon material in a                Pd containing precursor solution, stirring under an                inert atmosphere, filtering, washing, and vacuum                drying at a high temperature; and
   a method for synthesizing formic acid by                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, which involves                adding the Pd based metal material and/or the                catalyst into the reaction system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier is a                  porous carbon material or a nitrogen-doped porous                  carbon material. The precursor of Pd is an acid                  or salt containing Pd, including any one of                  palladium chloride, potassium chloropalladate,                  palladium nitrate, palladium acetate, palladium                  trifluoroacetate, palladium acetylacetonate. The                  second metal precursor is any one or more of                  chlorides, nitrates, sulfates and corresponding                  metal organic complexes containing Fe, Co, Ni,                  Cu, and Zn. The inert atmosphere is nitrogen.                  Preferred Conditions: The stirring treatment                  temperature can be room temperature or high                  temperature conditions, the treatment time is                  controlled to 1-24 hours, and the specific                  conditions of the high-temperature vacuum drying                  treatment are 60-150&#176; C vacuum drying                  for 6-24 hours.

PT P
PN CN112808266-A
TI Solution type nano gold particle catalyst for carbon dioxide laser, comprises gold atom particles, and carrier, which is mixed liquid of sodium citrate and purified water.
AU LIU J
AE WUHAN SILIWO LASER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2021584010
AB    NOVELTY - A solution type nano gold particle catalyst comprises an active component, which is 0.2-3 %mass 10-15 nm gold atom particles, and a carrier, which is 97-99.8 %mass mixed liquid of sodium citrate and purified water.
   USE - The solution type nano gold particle catalyst is useful for carbon dioxide laser.
   ADVANTAGE - The solution type nano gold particle catalyst: forms thin uniform coating layer on inner wall of carbon dioxide laser discharge tube made of glass or quartz; maintains laser power stably; effectively prolongs the service life of carbon dioxide laser; and can be prepared by a simple method. The coating layer contains nano-gold atom particles, which have excellent catalytic effect, can reduce and regenerate carbon dioxide gas molecules, reduce combination of nitrogen and oxygen to generate nitrous oxide, thus reducing the production of carbon monoxide and nitrous oxide harmful gases, leaves no residue after coating and cleaning, and does not pollute the resonant cavity and lens of the carbon dioxide laser.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the solution type nano gold particle catalyst by reduction method, which involves: (1) adding 1 g gold(III) chloride to a 200 ml graduated cylinder, and diluting with 100 ml purified water to form a gold(III) chloride diluted solution; (2) weighing 0.25 g sodium citrate by using an electronic scale, adding to a 25 ml graduated cylinder, and diluting with 15 ml purified water to form sodium citrate diluted solution; and (3) taking out 7 ml gold(III) chloride diluted solution, adding to a 500 ml beaker, mixing with purified water, placing the beaker in a small electric stove, boiling, then adding the sodium citrate diluted solution, heating for 15 minutes to form a nano-gold particle catalyst solution, stopping heating, and cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precursor of the gold atom is gold(III) chloride. Preferred Method: The preparation method of the catalyst by coating method involves: (1) cleaning an inner wall of laser discharge tube, and drying to remove moisture; (2) blocking one end of the laser discharge tube by using a rubber plug; (3) pouring KH540 (RTM: 3-Aminopropyltrimethoxysilane) into unplugged end of the laser discharge tube, blocking the port with rubber plug, leaving still for 3 hours, pouring out, and cleaning the inner wall of the laser discharge tube with purified water; and (4) taking nano-gold particle catalyst solution, pouring into the unplugged end of the laser discharge tube, blocking the port with rubber plug, leaving still for 4 hours, when the glass or quartz inner tube wall of the laser discharge tube is coated with a thin layer of light purple-red transparent nano-gold film, pouring out all the nano-gold particle catalyst solution, cleaning the laser discharge tube with purified water, and drying in an oven at 150 degrees C. The nano-gold particle catalyst is useful for catalytic oxidation of carbon monoxide and oxygen to carbon dioxide at 30-400 degrees C under the condition that the volume ratio of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, helium, hydrogen and xenon is 10:20:65:1:4.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN CN112037989-A; CN112037989-B
TI Cable flame retardant method involves dispersing heat generated when cable heat-resistant layer is burned through multi-level conduction and decomposing sodium bicarbonate when heated to produce water vapor and carbon dioxide.
AU QIU X
AE JIANGSU TIANYUAN CABLE CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C4074U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves flame heating (S101) the heat-resistant layer of the cable. The flame retardant is emitted (S102) from the heat-resistant layer of the cable. The heat generated is dispersed (S103) when the cable heat-resistant layer is burned through multi-level conduction. The sodium bicarbonate is decomposed (S104) when heated to produce water vapor and carbon dioxide. The water vapor and carbon dioxide are insulated (S105) from the conductor core. The conductor core is shielded (S106). The heat-resistant layer is burnt by the flame to form a breach.
   USE - Flame retardant method for cable (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cable has high-efficiency. The cable is energy-saving and flame-retardant. The flame retardant isolates oxygen to prevent combustion after the heat-resistant layer is burned out of the breach. The radiating fin is directly heated to decompose the sodium bicarbonate into carbon dioxide and water, which simultaneously takes away the heat and isolate the air so as to prevent the lead core from burning as much as possible.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a cable.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the cable flame retardant method. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for flame heating heat-resistant layer of cable (S101)
   Step for emitting flame retardant from heat-resistant layer of cable (S102)
   Step for dispersing heat generated when cable heat-resistant layer is burned through multi-level conduction (S103)
   Step for decomposing sodium bicarbonate when heated to produce water vapor and carbon dioxide (S104)
   Step for insulating water vapor and carbon dioxide from conductor core (S105)
   Step for shielding conductor core (S106)
