PT P
PN DE102021201568-A1; WO2022175030-A1
TI Niobate powder i.e. lithium sodium-potassium            niobate useful for production of piezoelectric            material.
AU SCHNITTER C
   OTTERSTEDT R
   ALBRECHT S
AE TANIOBIS GMBH (TANI-Non-standard)
GA 2022A5357V
AB    NOVELTY - Niobate powder i.e. lithium sodium-potassium                niobate having carbon content of 10-100 ppm/(m2/g)                and Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area is                determined in accordance with and carbon content is                determined by non-dispersive infrared absorption,                for piezoelectric use, is claimed.
   USE - The niobate powder is useful for the                production of piezoelectric material                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The niobate powder is suitable in the field of                synthetic material, inorganic material,                ferroelectric material, polycrystalline ceramic                materials, medical technology, ultrasonic                technology and automotive technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   producing the niobate powder, comprising (a)                providing an aqueous solution of the salts of                lithium, sodium and potassium, where the salt                include oxides, hydroxides, peroxides, superoxides,                nitrates and nitrites of the elements lithium,                sodium and potassium and mixtures, where the                solution does not include carbon dioxide, (b)                providing an aqueous suspension of a second                starting material including oxides and oxide                hydrates of niobium, where the suspension does not                include carbon dioxide, (c) mixing the aqueous                solution and the suspension in the absence of                carbon dioxide to obtain a mixed suspension, (d)                drying the mixed suspension with the exclusion of                carbon dioxide to obtain granules, (e) calcining                the granules in the absence of carbon dioxide, and                (f) conditioning the surface of the calcined                granules in the presence of carbon dioxide;                and
   piezoelectric material, made from niobate                powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The niobate powder is                  Lithium niobate compound of formula                  ((Lix(Na1-yKy)1-x)1+zNb1-uTauO3) (I). The niobate                  powder has Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface area of                  2-8 m2/g, determined using . The niobate powder                  is lead-free or 0.01-0.001 wt.% lead. The niobate                  powder has a stability (expressed as the                  conductivity of a suspension of the niobate                  powder (2 g niobate powder per 100 ml water,                  25&#176; C for 2 minutes) based on its BET                  surface area) of 10-90 (&#956; S/cm)/(m2/g),                  preferably 10-40 (&#956; S/cm)/(m2/g),                  determined in accordance with and the                  conductivity is determined by measuring the                  conductivity of the suspension after 2-minute                  reaction with water (2 g niobate powder per 100                  ml). The conductivity of aqueous suspension of                  niobate powder (2 g niobate powder per 100 ml                  water) does not change by more than 10                  (&#956; S/cm)/(m2/g) increases in 30 minutes.                  The material is a ceramic or a composite                  material. Preferred Method: The conditioning of                  the calcined granules by means of carbon dioxide                  staggered air flow takes place, with the                  proportion of added carbon dioxide in the air                  stream is 1-30 vol.%, preferably 5-20 vol.%,                  based in each case on the total volume of the air                  stream. The calcination is carried out at                  500-1000&#176; C, preferably                  650-800&#176; C for 0.5-2 hours.x=0.02-0.12;y=0.4-0.6;z=-0.05 to 0.05; andu=0-0.25.
   EXAMPLE - Aqueous solution containing 21.62 g lithium                nitrate, 177.02 g sodium nitrate and 210.59 g                potassium nitrate and an aqueous suspension of 1562                g niobium hydroxide containing 26.12 wt.% niobium                were mixed. 20 mol.% niobium hydroxide was replaced                with tantalum hydroxide. The mixtures were each                dried at 95&#176; C in a drying oven under                reduced pressure and under carbon dioxide-dried in                free air atmosphere. The granules were calcined in                a calcination furnace at 700-800&#176; C for                1.5 hours and then air-dried at 250-300&#176;                C. The product was treated at 250-300&#176; C                for 40 minutes in a stream of air containing 5-15                vol.% carbon dioxide to obtain niobate                powder.

PN CN114427392-A
TI Thick oil reservoir mining method, involves injecting third section of plug containing carbon dioxide into injection well to produce product containing crude oil in production well, and optionally executing previous steps.
AU TAO J
   SUN L
   HU C
   WANG H
   YANG Y
   LUN Z
AE SINOPEC EXPLORATION & PRODN RES INST (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2022667251
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining a to-be-mined area. An injection well and a production well are formed in the to-be-mined area. A first section of plug containing steam, nitrogen and carbon dioxide is injected into the injection well. A second section of plug containing nitrogen is injected into the injection well. A third section of plug containing carbon dioxide is injected into the injection well to produce product containing crude oil in the production well. Previous steps are optionally executed. Determination is made to check whether total volume content of the nitrogen and the carbon dioxide is considered as greater than or equal to 85 %. Water vapor and mixed gas containing the nitrogen and the carbon dioxide are alternately injected into the injection well. Viscosity of an underground crude oil reservoir is determined, if temperature of the underground crude oil reservoir is greater than 50m Pa-s.
   USE - Thick oil reservoir mining method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing steam, nitrogen and carbon dioxide as displacing medium, and determining a specific injection mode so as to effectively improve recovery rate of thick oil reservoir.

PN CN113398966-A
TI Preparing porous nitrogen-doped carbon nano fiber            dispersing nickel and molybdenum phosphide            photocatalyst useful for photocatalytic reducing carbon            dioxide to carbon monoxide, comprises e.g. calcining            and carbonizing carbon fiber film.
AU CHEN Z
   GONG S
   NIU Y
   TENG X
   LIU X
   XU M
   XU C
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 2021B0764N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porous nitrogen-doped carbon nano                fiber dispersing nickel and molybdenum phosphide                photocatalyst comprises (i) taking molybdenum                pentachloride, nickel nitrate hexahydrate,                polystyrene and polyacrylonitrile, heating and                dissolving in dimethylformamide to obtain spinning                solution; (ii) preparing spinning solution by                electrostatic spinning to obtain carbon fiber film;                and (iii) placing the carbon fiber film in a                tubular furnace, pre-phosphorizing by sodium                hypophosphite, and then calcining and carbonizing                to obtain the target product.
   USE - The porous nitrogen-doped carbon nano fiber                dispersing nickel and molybdenum phosphide                photocatalyst is useful for photocatalytic reducing                carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst: utilizes in the                photocatalytic process, which can carries out                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by using                sunlight with ~L = less than 420 nm; has wide light                absorption range, improves the ability to reduce                carbon dioxide under visible light, excellent                selectivity for reducing carbon dioxide to carbon                monoxide, has low resistivity, high photo-generated                carrier separation capability, low carrier                recombination rate, has characteristics of good                reducing carbon dioxide cycle stability and; and                can rapidly transfer carriers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                porous nitrogen-doped carbon nano fiber dispersing                nickel and molybdenum photocatalyst, is prepared by                the above preparation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of                  molybdenum pentachloride, nickel nitrate                  hexahydrate, polystyrene, polyacrylonitrile and                  dimethylformamide in the step (i) is 2                  mmol:(0.06-0.12) mmol:0.4 g:0.5 g:5 ml. The mass                  ratio of carbon fiber film to sodium                  hypophosphite in the step (iii) is 1:10.                  Preferred Method: The heating is carried out in                  the step (i) at 60 degrees C. The                  pre-phosphorization is carried out in the step                  (iii) at 350 degrees C in 1 degrees C/minute, the                  calcining and carbonizing are carried out under                  argon atmosphere at 800 degrees C for 2 hours in                  5 degrees C/minute.

GA 202162164F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves e.g. dividing TSR smelting process into oxidation period and reduction period, oxidation period is divided into DEC1 to DEC7 in seven stages, at DEC1-DEC3 stage, top blowing oxygen gun only blowing oxygen, molten pool quickly decarburizing and heating, bottom blowing spray gun blowing mixed gas of oxygen and carbon dioxide, auxiliary decarburization and strengthening molten pool stirring, in DEC4 and DEC5 stage, top blowing oxygen gun respectively blowing mixed gas and oxygen of oxygen and carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide and N2/Ar mixed gas, properly reducing upper part temperature of furnace body, reducing slag line part high temperature melting loss, and improving slag-steel dynamics transmission condition, at same time, blowing oxygen at DEC4 and DEC5 stage of bottom blowing spray gun, carbon dioxide and nitrogen/argon mixed gas, reducing molten pool carbon monooxide partial pressure, promoting molten pool decarburization reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for smelting stainless steel by remoblowing carbon dioxide at top bottom of TSR furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves stirring performance of molten pool and molten pool dynamics condition, and shortens smelting period.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises reducing oxidation burning loss of chromium in molten steel, at DEC6 and DEC7 stage, top blowing oxygen gun stops blowing, bottom blowing spray gun blows mixed gas of oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen/argon, properly reducing oxygen proportion, carrying out deep decarburization, and reducing high temperature melting loss of furnace bottom refractory material, in reduction period, bottom blowing spray gun blowing mixed gas of argon and carbon dioxide, reducing molten pool oxidation, promoting reduction of chromium oxide in slag.

PT P
PN CN112371189-A
TI Heterogeneous catalyst useful for catalytic            reaction of carbon dioxide and epichlorohydrin, is            prepared by adding ZIF series metal-organic framework            to metal salt methanol solution, reacting,            centrifuging, and washing.
AU ZHU Y
   WANG Q
   SHAO W
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202120380T
AB    NOVELTY - A hydroxide-coated ZIF series metal-organic                framework (MOF) heterogeneous catalyst is prepared                by adding ZIF series MOF to 0.12-0.4 mM metal salt                methanol solution, reacting at 50-60 degrees C for                1-48 hours, centrifuging, and washing with                methanol. The usage amount of the metal salt                methanol solution is 0.05-1 ml/mg based on the mass                of the ZIF series MOF. The metal salt is cobalt                nitrate, nickel nitrate or ferrous sulfate. The ZIF                series MOF is ZIF-8, ZIF-7 or ZIF-67.
   USE - The hydroxide-coated ZIF series MOF                heterogeneous catalyst is useful for catalytic                reaction of carbon dioxide and epichlorohydrin to                prepare chloropropylene carbonate (all claimed),                heterogeneous catalytic carbon dioxide addition                reaction, photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction,                and electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydroxide-coated ZIF series MOF                heterogeneous catalyst: has excellent performance                characteristics; controls the growth of different                hydroxides on the surface of ZIF series MOF; and                solves the problem that the surface of ZIF series                MOF cannot accurately control the growth of                hydroxides.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                the hydroxide-coated ZIF series MOF heterogeneous                catalyst for catalytic reaction of carbon dioxide                and epichlorohydrin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ZIF-8 is prepared by:                  (1) preparing 25 mM zinc nitrate solution and 32                  mM polyvinylpyrrolidone solution using a mixed                  solution of methanol and ethanol in a volume                  ratio of 1:1 as a solvent, and mixing the                  obtained zinc nitrate solution and                  polyvinylpyrrolidone solution in a volume ratio                  of 1:(0.5-3) to obtain a mixed liquid (A); (2)                  preparing 100 mM 2-methylimidazole solution and                  100 mM 1-methylimidazole solution using a mixed                  solution of methanol and ethanol, and mixing the                  obtained 2-methylimidazole solution and                  1-methylimidazole solution in a volume ratio of                  1:(0.5-2) to obtain a mixed liquid (B); and (3)                  adding the mixed liquid (B) to the mixed liquid                  (A) in a volume ratio of 1:1, stirring for 5                  minutes, reacting at room temperature for 24                  hours, centrifuging, washing with a mixed                  solution of methanol and ethanol in a volume                  ratio of 1:1 to obtain ZIF-8 crystals, and                  dispersing in methanol. The ZIF-7 is prepared by                  preparing 72 mM zinc nitrate solution and 53.3 mM                  benzimidazole solution using N,N-dimethylamide as                  solvent, evenly mixing the obtained zinc nitrate                  solution and benzimidazole solution in a volume                  ratio of 1:1, reacting in a reactor at 130                  degrees C for 48 hours, centrifuging, washing                  with methanol, and drying the product in an oven                  at 60 degrees C. The ZIF-67 is prepared by: (1)                  preparing 25 mM cobalt nitrate solution and 32 mM                  polyvinylpyrrolidone solution using a mixed                  solution of methanol and ethanol in a volume                  ratio of 1:1 as solvent, and mixing the obtained                  cobalt nitrate solution and polyvinylpyrrolidone                  solution in a volume ratio of 1:1 to obtain a                  mixed liquid (C); (2) preparing 100 mM                  2-methylimidazole solution and 100 mM                  1-methylimidazole solution using a mixed solution                  of methanol and ethanol, and mixing the obtained                  2-methylimidazole solution and 1-methylimidazole                  solution in a volume ratio of 1:1 to obtain a                  mixed liquid (D); and (3) adding the mixed liquid                  (D) to the mixed liquid (C) according to the                  volume ratio of 1:1, stirring for 5 minutes,                  reacting at room temperature for 24 hours, then                  centrifuging, and washing with a mixed solution                  of methanol and ethanol in a volume ratio 1:1 to                  obtain ZIF-67 crystals, and dispersing in                  methanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The application method                  involves activating the heterogeneous catalyst in                  a vacuum oven at 60 degrees C for 12 hours,                  adding epichlorohydrin reactant,                  1-bromo-3,5-difluorobenzene internal standard and                  the activated heterogeneous catalyst to the                  reactor, flushing carbon dioxide to eliminate air                  interference, continuously passing carbon dioxide                  to 1 MPa, reacting at 80 degrees C for 24 hours,                  cooling with ice water, exhausting the remaining                  carbon dioxide, and post-treating the reaction                  liquid to obtain chloropropylene carbonate. The                  usage amount of the epichlorohydrin reactant is                  0.18 mol/g based on the mass of the activated                  heterogeneous catalyst. The molar ratio of the                  epichlorohydrin reactant to the                  1-bromo-3,5-difluorobenzene internal standard is                  9:1.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.)

   YANG Z

   JIANG Q
   HU G
   XIANG H
   LIU Z
   XU X
   GUO H
   SHAO Y
   JIANG M
AE WISDRI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION ENG TECH (WISD-Non-standard)
GA 2022A6791L
AB    NOVELTY - A method for industrial solid waste                electrolytic manganese slag mineralization and                carbon dioxide resource utilization, comprising                (S1) placing a mixture of electrolytic manganese                slag and alkaline waste residue in first container,                adding water to stir and obtaining ammonia gas and                slurry, (S2) introducing the ammonia gas into a                second container filled with water, introducing                industrial tail gas containing carbon dioxide to                the second container, obtaining ammonium carbonate                solution, separating solid and liquid of the slurry                to obtain modified manganese slag, (S3) mixing the                ammonium carbonate solution with electrolytic                manganese slag, preparing calcium carbonate and                ammonium sulfate, filtering the modified manganese                slag to obtain modified manganese slag cake and                alkaline waste water, and (S4) introducing alkaline                waste water into the first container in step (S1),                drying modified manganese slag cake and grinding to                obtain an alkaline sulfate composite                activator.
   USE - The method is used for industrial solid waste                electrolytic manganese slag mineralization and                carbon dioxide resource utilization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures efficient and step-by-step                utilization of electrolytic manganese slag, has                high value-added utilization of electrolytic                manganese slag, and reduces the cost of                mineralization and carbon dioxide treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of gypsum                  is 40-85 wt.% and the content of the ammonia                  nitrogen is 3-15 wt.% in the electrolytic                  manganese slag. The mass ratio of the mixture to                  water is 1: 2-4. The ratio of the nitrogen to                  sulfur of the ammonium carbonate solution and the                  electrolytic manganese slag is not less than 2.0.                  In step (S4), the content of the calcium sulfate                  is 50-85 wt.% and the content of the calcium                  oxide is 3-15 wt.% in the basic sulfate compound                  excitant.Preferred Components: The alkaline waste                  residue is selected from one or more of carbide                  slag, red mud and fly ash. The industrial tail                  gas containing carbon dioxide is selected from                  one or combination of blast furnace gas, coke                  oven gas and lime kiln gas. Preferred Property:                  The electrolytic manganese slag is pre-treated by                  crushing, and the grain diameter is not more than                  5 mm.Preferred Method: In step (S1), in the                  mixture of electrolytic manganese slag and                  alkaline waste residue, the mass ratio of                  electrolytic manganese slag and alkaline waste                  residue is 100: 5-30, the stirring is carried out                  at a speed of100-200 rpm for 1-6 hours. In step                  (S3), the mixing stirring is carried out at a                  speed of 100-200 rpm for 2-8 hours at                  25-60&#8451;. In step (S4), the step of                  introducing the alkaline waste water into the                  first container comprises when the pH of the                  alkaline waste water is greater than 10, directly                  introducing the alkaline waste water into the                  first container, when the pH of the alkaline                  waste water is &#8805; 10, firstly adding                  0.5-5 wt.% of slaked lime into the alkaline waste                  water, stirring and dissolving to make the pH                  &gt; 10, then introducing into the first                  container.

PN CN113249750-A; CN113249750-B
TI Electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide using nitrogen-doped carbon nano-tube with different curvature comprises e.g. pre-treating multi-wall carbon nano-tube, and performing ammonia etching treatment and electrocatalytic reduction.
AU LV K
   ZHU Y
   WAN D
   BAO Y
   LI H
   YANG S
AE CHINA BUILDING MATERIAL TESTING & CERTIF (CHNB-C)
   CHINA BUILDING MATERIALS ACAD (CHNB-C)
GA 2021975288
AB    NOVELTY - Electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide using nitrogen-doped carbon nano-tube with different curvature comprises (1) pre-treating multi-wall carbon nano-tube (CNT), (2) adding pre-treated carbon nano-tube into tube furnace, pre-processing, heating, and performing ammonia etching treatment to obtain nitrogen-doped carbon nano-tube (NCNT), (3) processing 5 mg nitrogen-doped carbon nano-tube, 100 mu l distilled water, 400 mu l ethanol and 75 mu l Nafion (RTM: Polymer electrolyte membrane material) aqueous solution for 30 minutes to obtain nitrogen-doped carbon nano-tube dispersion, taking out 30 mu l nitrogen-doped carbon nano-tube dispersion using pipette, dropping on surface of carbon paper and spreading, (4) using electrode material as working electrode, silver/silver chloride as reference electrode and platinum electrode as counter electrode, where the electrolyte is potassium bicarbonate solution, and performing electrocatalytic reduction reaction on carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide using nitrogen-doped carbon nano-tube with different curvature.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: summarizes the optimal carbon tube size and mechanism of its size limitation; and provides guidance for the selection of catalyst size of carbon nano-tube based materials in the electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the pre-treatment of the carbon nano-tube specifically includes soaking in deionized water, performing ultrasonic treatment for 20-30 minutes, removing impurities on the surface and drying. In the step (4), the electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide uses a double electrolytic cell system with Nafion-520 (RTM: Polymer electrolyte membrane material) as the ion exchange membrane. In the method, during the electrolysis process, continuously passing carbon dioxide through the electrolytic cell at a flow rate of 20 ml/minute, energized and reacted for 1 hour at each reduction potential. The electrolytic catalytic reduction test is carried out at a potential of -0.3 to -1.3V.

PN CN112718014-A; CN112718014-B
TI Treating catalyst carrier in the reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to methanol, involves grinding and mixing the catalyst carrier and sodium borohydrate to obtain a mixture, calcining the mixture under a protective atmosphere.
AU DUAN X
   LU J
   ZOU J
   YE L
   YUAN Y
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 2021472813
AB    NOVELTY - Treating catalyst carrier involves grinding and mixing the catalyst carrier and sodium borohydrate to obtain a mixture, calcining the mixture under a protective atmosphere to obtain a calcined material. The calcined material is washed and freeze-dried in sequence to obtain a treated catalyst carrier.
   USE - Method for treating catalyst carrier used in the reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short production period, improves the catalytic activity of the catalyst, conversion rate of carbon dioxide and selectivity of methanol, in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a catalyst for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to methanol, which omprises a carrier and an active component supported on the carrier, where the carrier is a metal oxide obtained by the treatment method, the metal oxide includes titanium oxide, zirconium oxide or zinc oxide, the active component is metal or its oxide and the metal includes copper, gold, palladium and indium; and
   (2) a method of preparing a catalyst for the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol, which involves mixing the active component precursor and water to obtain an active component precursor solution, impregnating the carrier in the active component precursor solution to obtain a carrier supporting the active component, and freeze-drying active component-loaded carrier and calcining to obtain product.
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the catalyst carrier and sodium borohydrate is 0.01-10:1. The temperature of the calcination is 300-1000 degrees C, and the time of the calcination is 3-12 hours. The roasting material is soaked in water for 1-4 hours, and then washed repeatedly with ethanol and water by centrifugation. The freeze-drying temperature is -50-(-70) degrees C and the freeze-drying time is 12-24 hours. The total mass fraction of the active component is 1-60%. Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises a carrier auxiliary agent supported on the carrier, where the carrier auxiliary agent includes sodium, potassium, boron, lithium, cerium, iron, silver, ruthenium, rhodium, cobalt, nickel, iron and their oxides.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves hydrolyzing the hydrogen sulphide contained in the initial blast furnace gas is rapidly hydrolyzed into carbonyl sulfide and hydrogen sulphide. The hydrogen sulphide is removed and vulcanized. The value of carbon dioxide content is adjusted. The initial blast furnace gas with a specific temperature quenched to hydrolyze carbonyl sulfide into carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulphide. The highly selective non-renewable desulfurization solvent is used to remove hydrogen sulphide, or selectively remove hydrogen sulphide and convert hydrogen sulphide into elemental sulfur. The ethanol amine absorption method is used to adjust the volume fraction of carbonyl sulfide in the blast furnace gas. A catalyst area with a built-in carbonyl sulfide hydrolysis catalyst is set at a height of 600-1000 mm at the top of the air inlet of the quenching device, and the gas can only pass through the catalyst area.

TI Separating a sulfurous material from            multi-component feed stream by injecting            multi-component feed stream including sulfurous            material and fuel gas into contacting column, and            injecting a solvent stream comprising liquid carbon            dioxide.
AU ALLAM R J
   RAFATI M
   RAFATI N
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 201993705E
AB    NOVELTY - A sulfurous material is separated from                multi-component feed stream by injecting a                multi-component feed stream including greater than                or equal to 1 sulfurous material and fuel gas into                a contacting column (110); injecting a solvent                stream comprising liquid carbon dioxide into                contacting column so that solvent stream comprising                the liquid carbon dioxide contacts the                multi-component feed stream; withdrawing from                contacting column a bottom product stream                containing greater than or equal to 1 portion of                sulfurous material from the multi-component feed                stream; and withdrawing from the contacting column                an overhead vapor stream containing greater than or                equal to 1 portion of the fuel gas.
   USE - Separation of sulfurous material from                multi-component feed stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be effective for separating a                carbonaceous stream from contaminating compounds                and uses a separation column system for processing                a contaminated stream at a temperature close to                freezing point of carbon dioxide using liquid                carbon dioxide solvent to produce contaminant-free                overhead vapor and bottoms liquid product enriched                in sulfur-containing contaminants.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the                system for separation of sulfurous material from                multi-component feed stream.
   Solvent source (101)
   Multi-component feed stream source                (102)
   Contacting column (110)
   Overhead vapor treatment unit (121)
   Bottom product separation unit (131)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method comprises                  injecting the multi-component feed stream and                  solvent stream into contacting column in a                  spatially separated arrangement so that the                  solvent stream flows in a downwardly direction                  for contacting multi-component feed stream and                  multi-component feed stream flows in upwardly                  direction for contacting the solvent. The                  contacting column is a distillation column. The                  contacting column is counter-current contacting                  column. Contacting column is operated under                  conditions such that no portion of any carbon                  dioxide in contacting column is solidified during                  passage through the contacting column. The method                  further comprises passing multi-component feed                  stream and/or solvent stream through a heat                  exchanger against greater than or equal to 1                  portion of overhead vapor stream that is                  withdrawn from contacting column so that the                  overhead vapor stream is heated and                  multi-component feed stream and/or solvent stream                  are cooled. The method further comprises                  evaporating greater than or equal to 1 streams of                  liquid refrigerant in the heat exchanger to                  maintain a heat balance. The contacting column is                  configured for heat removal at greater than or                  equal to 1 stages present in the contacting                  column at a position that is higher in contacting                  column than position for the injecting of the                  multi-component feed stream. Contacting column                  comprises a re-boiler. Portion of multi-component                  feed stream is passed through the re-boiler prior                  to injecting into the contacting column. The                  method further comprises passing the overhead                  vapor stream through a separation unit configured                  for removal of greater than or equal to 1 portion                  of any carbon dioxide present in the overhead                  vapor stream. The separation unit is a membrane                  separator configured for separating the overhead                  vapor stream into a product fuel gas stream and                  permeate stream comprising carbon dioxide. The                  method further comprises recycling greater than                  or equal to 1 portion of carbon dioxide from the                  permeate stream to solvent stream prior to                  injecting solvent stream into contacting column.                  The method further comprises passing the bottom                  product stream through greater than or equal to 1                  distillation columns configured for separating                  out greater than or equal to 1 further components                  present in the bottom product stream in addition                  to the sulfurous material. Portion of fuel gas in                  overhead vapor stream is provided into a                  combustor of a power production unit. Preferred                  Components: The multi-component feed stream                  comprises greater than or equal to 2 mol.% carbon                  dioxide prior to injecting multi-component feed                  stream into the contacting column. The sulfurous                  material in multi-component feed stream is                  selected from hydrogen sulfide, carbonyl sulfide,                  thiol-containing compounds, carbon disulfide,                  and/or disulfide bond-containing compounds. The                  streams of liquid refrigerant comprise liquid                  carbon dioxide. The overhead vapor stream                  withdrawn from contacting column comprises less                  than or equal to 2 mol.% sulfurous material. The                  overhead vapor stream withdrawn from contacting                  column comprises less than or equal to 2 ppm                  molar sulfurous material. The product fuel gas                  stream contains less than or equal to 2 ppm molar                  sulfurous material. The product fuel gas stream                  contains less than or equal to 2 mol.% carbon                  dioxide. Preferred Parameters: The                  multi-component feed stream injected into                  contacting column is at a temperature of ? 10                  degrees C to ? 55 degrees C. The solvent stream                  injected into the contacting column is at a                  temperature of ? 10 degrees C to ? 55 degrees C.                  The solvent stream injected into the contacting                  column is at a temperature and pressure such that                  all of the carbon dioxide contained is in a                  liquid state. The solvent stream injected into                  the contacting column is at a pressure of 7-100                  bar. The contacting column is operated at a                  pressure of 7-100 bar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The multi-component                  feed stream is sour natural gas. The bottom                  product stream contains greater than or equal to                  1 portion of hydrogen sulfide and 1-5                  carbon-containing hydrocarbons present in the                  sour natural gas. The overhead vapor stream                  contains a majority of methane present in the                  sour natural gas.

TI Precision cutting method for diamond tool, involves setting turning process parameters of diamond tool for turning and closing carbon dioxide storage tank, after processing.
AU HAO X
   ZHAO W
   SHAN Y
   SUN H
   LI L
   HE N
AE UNIV NANJING AERONAUTICS & ASTRONAUTICS (UNUA-C)
GA 2019114661
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves fixing and clamping the workpiece on a precision numerical control lathe. The diamond cutter is mounted to the feed box of the lathe. A low temperature carbon dioxide device is installed, the positions of the first nozzle and the second nozzle are adjusted, and the low temperature carbon dioxide gas storage tank is opened. The first nozzle is caused to spray low temperature carbon dioxide to the rake face of the diamond tool. The second nozzle sprays low temperature carbon dioxide to the flank of the diamond cutter. The lathe device is turned on, the relative position of the diamond tool and the workpiece is adjusted, and the tool setting is completed. The turning process parameters of the diamond tool are set for turning. The CO2 storage tank is closed, after processing, the spindle, power supply and control software are turned off, and the diamond tool and workpiece are disassembled.
   USE - Precision cutting method for diamond tool.
   ADVANTAGE - The tool life is improved, the sharpness of the cutting edge is maintained, and the processing quality and precision of the workpiece is ensured.

   Bearing ring (2)

PN CN107790632-A
TI Casting of car cover die involves hardening sand, blowing carbon dioxide gas, adding pig iron, scrap steel, ferrochromium, ferromanganese, vanadium iron and ferrosilicon into furnace, melting, filtering and casting insert.
AU CHEN J
AE TAIHU AIJIE MACHINERY CASTING CO LTD (TAIH-Non-standard)
GA 2018217408
AB    NOVELTY - Casting of car cover die involves computer numerical controlled (CNC) milling polystyrene foam model, weighing polystyrene foam model, estimating weight of mold insert, brushing paint, preparing sand box, compacting sand, blowing carbon dioxide gas into sand, hardening sand, blowing carbon dioxide gas until sand is hardened, adding pig iron, scrap steel, ferrochromium, ferromanganese, ferromolybdenum, vanadium iron and ferrosilicon into induction furnace, melting, filtering, removing slag residue, deoxidizing and casting inserts.
   USE - Casting of car cover die.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Casting of car cover die involves CNC milling polystyrene foam model of preset shape and size, weighing polystyrene foam model, estimating weight of mold insert, brushing paint, preparing sand box, compacting sand, blowing carbon dioxide gas into sand for 8 minutes, hardening the sand, blowing carbon dioxide gas for 8 minutes until the sand is hardened, adding pig iron, scrap steel, ferrochromium, ferromanganese, ferromolybdenum, vanadium iron and ferrosilicon into 200 kg induction furnace, melting for 55 minutes, filtering, removing slag residue, deoxidizing with pure aluminum, tapping steel, controlling temperature at 1600 degrees C, casting inserts, pouring into molten metal to fill the original space of the model, cooling the castings, cleaning the box after cooled, cutting after sandblasting, and obtaining near-end shaped mold inserts.

PN CN114669182-A
TI Coupling method of carbon dioxide exhaust gas from            phosphorus chemical industry for reverse flotation of            phosphate rock, involves introducing carbon dioxide gas            into alkali tank and flowing alkali liquid into reverse            flotation return water collection tank, and recovering            obtained calcium carbonate.
AU HUANG J
   ZHANG R
   LUO P
   WANG M
   CAI Z
   HE H
   ZHANG H
   YANG W
AE YUNNAN PHOSPHOR CHEM GROUP CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 202288480T
AB    NOVELTY - Coupling method of carbon dioxide exhaust gas                from phosphorus chemical industry for reverse                flotation of phosphate rock, involves introducing                carbon dioxide exhaust gas into gas treatment                chamber from bottom, spraying top of the gas                treatment chamber with an alkaline solution,                washing the alkaline solution and cooling the                carbon dioxide exhaust gas countercurrently,                entering the carbon dioxide exhaust gas into gas                collection chamber from bottom, spraying top of the                gas collection chamber with tap water, introducing                the carbon dioxide exhaust gas into alkali tank                through exhaust fan and flowing the alkali liquid                into reverse flotation return water collection                tank, and separating backwater in the backwater                collection tank from solid and liquid, returning                upper layer to the phosphate rock reverse flotation                operation for recycling and recovering and selling                bottom solid of calcium carbonate and magnesium                carbonate.
   USE - Coupling method of carbon dioxide exhaust gas                from phosphorus chemical industry for reverse                flotation of phosphate rock.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of reducing                concentration of calcium ion and magnesium ion in                the backwater of phosphate rock reverse flotation                operation, reducing cost of backwater use, reducing                amount of reverse flotation collector, reducing                cost of flotation, reducing direct emission of                carbon into air, and promoting green and                sustainable development of phosphate rock flotation                and phosphorus chemical industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Coupling method of carbon dioxide exhaust gas                from phosphorus chemical industry for reverse                flotation of phosphate rock, involves introducing                carbon dioxide exhaust gas into gas treatment                chamber from bottom, spraying top of the gas                treatment chamber with an alkaline solution with                concentration of 1%, washing the alkaline solution                and cooling the carbon dioxide exhaust gas                countercurrently, entering the carbon dioxide                exhaust gas into gas collection chamber from                bottom, spraying top of the gas collection chamber                with tap water, where the tap water carries out                countercurrent washing and cooling of the carbon                dioxide exhaust gas, and the temperature of the                obtained mixed gas is 50-70&#8451;, introducing                the carbon dioxide exhaust gas into alkali tank                through exhaust fan and flowing the alkali liquid                into reverse flotation return water collection                tank, and separating backwater in the backwater                collection tank from solid and liquid, returning                upper layer to the phosphate rock reverse flotation                operation for recycling and recovering and selling                bottom solid of calcium carbonate and magnesium                carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline solution                  is an aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide,                  sodium carbonate or lime. Preferred Composition:                  The solution in the alkali tank is 10% sodium                  hydroxide or sodium carbonate solution.

PT P
PN US2022219147-A1
TI Lanthanum oxide-promoted copper-based            nanocomposite catalyst for carbon dioxide hydrogenation            to methanol comprises nanoparticulates comprising            copper oxide, zinc oxide, and lanthanum oxide, and            alumina as support.
AU EL-NAAS M
   KUMAR D
   ALI S
AE UNIV QATAR (UYQA-Non-standard)
GA 202292134P
AB    NOVELTY - A lanthanum oxide-promoted copper (Cu)-based                nanocomposite catalyst comprises nanoparticulates                in the size range of 3-20 nm comprising copper                oxide loading of 20-60 wt.%, zinc oxide loading of                40-65 wt.%, and lanthanum oxide loading of 0.5-10                wt.%; and alumina as support.
   USE - A lanthanum oxide-promoted copper (Cu)-based                nanocomposite catalyst for carbon dioxide                hydrogenation to methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The lanthanum oxide-promoted Cu-based                nanocomposite catalyst demonstrates excellent                catalytic performance for carbon dioxide                hydrogenation to methanol. The catalyst is highly                active for carbon dioxide conversion and yields                high amounts of methanol in the temperature range                of 25-350&#8451; and a pressure range of 60-85                bars. Furthermore, the catalyst is highly selective                for methanol, and during the catalytic conversion,                produces no significant amount of the undesirable                byproduct methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for synthesis                of the nanocomposite catalyst which involves a                single-step solution combustion synthesis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Catalyst comprises                  copper loading of 30-60 wt.%, preferably about 30                  wt.%; zinc oxide loading of 50-62 wt.%,                  preferably about 50 wt.%; and lanthanum oxide                  loading of 0.5-10 wt.%, preferably about 1 wt.%.                  Glycine:metal precursor nitrates ratio is                  0.2:1-1.2:1.Preferred Processes: Single-step solution                  combustion synthesis comprises (1) dissolving                  metal precursor nitrates in water and adding                  glycine wherein the nitrates are copper nitrate                  trihydrate, zinc nitrate hexahydrate, aluminum                  nitrate nonahydrate, and lanthanum nitrate                  hexahydrate; (2) stirring the mixture to afford a                  homogeneous mixture and heating the resulting                  solution over a hot plate for combustion that                  upon initiation, proceeds in an autothermal mode                  without any external heating source to afford                  pristine nanocomposites; (3) calcining the                  resultant powder to remove uncombusted precursor                  salts in air in a muffle furnace to afford the                  nanocomposite catalyst; and (4)                  activating/reducing the nanocomposite catalyst by                  passing pure hydrogen stream over the                  nanocomposite catalyst.Preferred Properties: Catalyst has                  Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of                  about 77 mg2/g. Catalyst exhibits a methanol                  (MeOH) yield of about 2.5 gMeOH/gcathour under                  the conditions of a temperature of about                  325&#8451;, a pressure of about 85 bars,                  hydrogen:carbon dioxide ratio of about 3:1-4:1,                  and a gas hourly space velocity of                  7000-55000/hour. Hydrogen:carbon dioxide ratio is                  about 4:1. Gas hourly space velocity is                  27000-55000/hour. Catalyst exhibits a MeOH                  selectivity of about 42 wt.% and carbon dioxide                  conversion of about 30%. Catalyst exhibits                  Cu2+-induced phases of about 18% as measured by                  X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS), where                  percentage of the induced phases is calculated                  based on total area of the integrated peaks.                  Nanocomposite catalyst in step (4) comprises                  nanoparticles ranging in size of 3-20 nm in                  diameter. Nanocomposite catalyst in step (4) is                  characterized by more oxygen vacancies and/or                  surface defects compared to a nanocomposite                  catalyst that does not comprise lanthanum                  oxide.Preferred Conditions: Calcining the                  resultant powder is performed at                  400-800&#8451;. Step (4) is conducted at                  350-500&#8451;.

TI Producing kenaf activation carbon involves carbonizing the kenaf (Hibiscus cannabinus L.) chip while raising the temperature under anaerobic conditions, heating the carbonized kenaf chip, and injecting carbon dioxide gas and water vapor.
AU SON J M
   RHO H M
   SOO K M
   SON H M
AE SON J M (SONJ-Individual)
GA 2021C2209S
AB    NOVELTY - Producing kenaf activationcarbon involves carbonizing the kenaf (Hibiscus cannabinus L.) chip while raising the temperature to 350-450 degrees C under anaerobic conditions. The carbonized kenaf chip is heated under anaerobic conditions to 800-1,000 degrees C, and when this temperature is reached, injected carbon dioxide gas and water vapor while maintaining this temperature condition. The supply of carbon dioxide gas, water vapor, and a heat source is stopped after the activation has elapsed, and cooled below 200 degrees C under anoxic conditions. The carbon dioxide gas and water vapor are each at a rate of 100-1,200 ml/min and 10-100 ml/hr at the same time.
   USE - Method for producing kenaf activation carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce kenaf activation carbon enhances antioxidant activity, has anti-inflammatory, digestive, and antioxidant properties, and improves immunity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The cooled activated carbon is stored at room temperature under sealed conditions. The anaerobic condition is realized by injecting nitrogen gas. The nitrogen gas is injected at a rate of 100-1,200 ml/min. The kenaf chip reaches 350-450 degrees C at a temperature increase rate of 10 degrees C./min.

PN CN111547748-A
TI Preparing battery-grade lithium carbonate by efficiently decarbonizing lithium bicarbonate solution involves subjecting saturated lithium bicarbonate solution to negative pressure reaction, and performing solid-liquid separation.
AU HONG K
AE GANZHOU NONFERROUS METALLURGY INST (GANZ-Non-standard)
GA 202081907F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing battery-grade lithium carbonate by efficiently decarbonizing a lithium bicarbonate solution involves subjecting the saturated lithium bicarbonate solution to negative pressure reaction, and performing solid-liquid separation.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing battery-grade lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the characteristics of gas generated by decarburization of saturated lithium bicarbonate solution; uses the negative pressure to enhance carbon dioxide removal, so that decarburization of lithium bicarbonate can be carried out at relatively low temperature; avoids the energy consumption generated by heating and evaporating water, so that obtained gas is pure carbon dioxide gas, not a mixed gas of water vapor and carbon dioxide; avoids the concentration of impurities in the lithium bicarbonate mother liquor; and can directly return carbon dioxide and the filtrate to the hydrogenation process together, so that the content of the obtained product lithium carbonate meets the battery-grade lithium carbonate standard (YS/T582-2013).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method further involves directly returning the filtrate obtained after solid-liquid separation to the industrial-grade lithium carbonate hydrogenation process as a hydrogenation feed liquid. The saturated lithium bicarbonate solution is prepared by using industrial lithium carbonate as a raw material through carbon dioxide hydrogenation, where the lithium content in the saturated lithium bicarbonate solution is 8.0-8.5 g/l. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the negative pressure reaction is 20-45 degrees C. The pressure of the negative pressure reaction is 0.095-0.085 MPa. The time for the negative pressure reaction is 10-60 minutes.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)

   XI Y

AE ZHENGZHOU RES INST ABRASIVE & GRINDING C (CHNM-C)
GA 2019272530

The carbon dioxide is injected into the high pressure kettle, the pressure and temperature to 72.8 atm and 31.1 degrees C is adjusted to make the carbon dioxide reach a supercritical state and stirred the mixture for 1-3 hours. The supercritical carbon dioxide is converted into a gas under reduced pressure, released, cooled at room temperature and the mixture is taken out.

PN CN109453757-A; CN109453757-B
TI Preparing nano composite catalyst to efficiently convert carbon dioxide into cyclic carbonate comprises e.g. contacting attapulgite powder with modifier, centrifuging, washing and drying reaction product to obtain solid powder.
AU LIU W
   WANG G
   WANG W
   TANG X
   WANG Y
   DOU W
   ZHANG G
AE UNIV LANZHOU (ULAN-C)
GA 201925441X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano composite catalyst to efficiently convert carbon dioxide into cyclic carbonate comprises (i) contacting attapulgite powder with modifier, centrifuging, washing and drying reaction product to obtain solid powder; (ii) contacting solid powder with a metal salt and introducing carbon dioxide (CO2) into reaction system to obtain pre-product; and (iii) sintering pre-product, where metal salt is rare earth salt and/or transition metal salt.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano composite catalyst to efficiently convert carbon dioxide into cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, simple in preparation, has good selectivity, stable structure, high synergistic catalytic efficiency of multi-metal and catalyst is easy to separate, can be recycled and catalyst is not sensitive to air and water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for nanocomposite catalyst, prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal salt is a mixture of rare earth salt and transition metal salt in the ratio 0.01:1000-1000:0.01. In the step (ii) first solvent is at least one of methanol, ethanol, acetonitrile, dimethylformamide, acetone, and water. In the step (ii) modifier is at least one of hydrochloride, sodium hexametaphosphate, polyethylene glycol, polyvinylpyrrolidone, sodium tetrafluoroborate and sodium hexafluorophosphate. In the step (iii) second solvent is at least one of dioxane, methanol, ethanol, acetonitrile, dimethylformamide, acetone and water. Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) crushing and screening natural attapulgite ore to remove impurities to obtain attapulgite powder; (ii) dispersing attapulgite powder into first solvent, adding modifier, stirring reaction, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain solid powder; (iii) dispersing solid powder and metal salt uniformly in second solvent, stirring the reaction and introducing CO2 to form suspension, centrifuging, washing and drying suspension to obtain pre-product; and (iv) sintering pre-product at 25-800 degrees C for 0.5-30 hours to obtain nanocomposite catalyst. In the step (ii) stirring mechanically at a rate of 10-10000 rotations/minute and reaction time is 0.5-100 hours. In the step (ii) drying treatment include treatment temperature of -70 to 200 degrees C. In the step (iii) stirring reaction at a temperature of 0-200 degrees C and reaction time is 0.5-120 hours.

PN KR1944702-B1
TI Manufacture of kimchi packaging container involves mixing masterbatch containing powder having carbon dioxide adsorption function with polymer resin, adding plasticizer and/or methyltrimethoxysilane as crosslinking agent, and extruding.
AU YOO S
   LEE H G
   JEONG S
AE KOREA FOOD RES INST (KOFR-C)
GA 201914026P
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of kimchi packaging container involves preparing a masterbatch containing a functional material powder having a carbon dioxide adsorption function, mixing the prepared masterbatch with a polymer resin, preparing a plasticizer and/or a crosslinking agent, adding 1-2 pts. wt. (with respect to 100 pts. wt. masterbatch-polymer resin mixture) of the plasticizer and/or a crosslinking agent, preferably methyltrimethoxysilane to the obtained masterbatch-polymer resin mixture to prepare a film-forming mixture, and extruding the film-forming mixture to form a film.
   USE - Manufacture of kimchi packaging container (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of kimchi packaging container having improved elongation rate, by adding the crosslinking agent to the raw material, and removal ability of carbon dioxide by adding the functional material powder to the raw material, and suppressed generation of bursting, even when volatile gas, such as carbon dioxide is generated from the contents of the container.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The functional material powder is prepared by mixing 1 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide and 4-6 pts. wt. water and stirring to obtain an aqueous calcium hydroxide solution, mixing 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. powdered activated carbon with respect to 1 pts. wt. of calcium hydroxide, impregnating calcium hydroxide with the powdered activated carbon at 20-25 degrees C under reduced pressure, and drying at 100-105 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: Alternately, manufacture of kimchi packaging container involves preparing masterbatch containing the functional material powder impregnated with calcium hydroxide impregnated with powdered activated carbon, and having carbon dioxide adsorption function, mixing 20-30 wt.% masterbatch and 70-80 wt.% polymer resin comprising a low-density polyethylene, preparing crosslinking agent comprising methyltrimethoxysilane, adding the crosslinking agent to the masterbatch-polymer resin mixture to prepare a film-forming mixture, extruding the film-forming mixture to produce film, and preparing a container by processing the film.

PN CN108395934-A
TI Method for preparing agarwood stock solution, involves pulverizing washed agarwood, adding liquid carbon dioxide and methanol, extracting, transferring agarwood crude oil to gas-liquid separator and separating carbon dioxide and methanol.
AU ZHANG M
AE ZHANG M (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201865266W
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing agarwood stock solution, involves (1) choosing the agarwood and washing, (2) pulverizing the washed agarwood into 20 mesh agarwood powder, (3) placing the 100 g agarwood powder in extraction vessel, adding 88-96% concentration of 20.77 kg liquid carbon dioxide and 4-12% concentration of 0.23 kg methanol, delivering 21 kg mixed liquid to extraction vessel at a flow rate of 3000-3500 g/hour, reacting at pressure of 45-60 MPa and temperature of 4-10 degrees C for 4-6 hours, extracting to obtain agarwood crude oil, (4) transferring agarwood crude oil to gas-liquid separator and separating carbon dioxide and methanol at a temperature of 4-10 degrees C to obtain agarwood stock solution.
   USE - Method for preparing agarwood stock solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of agarwood stock solution with simple process at low cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (3) and step (4) comprises using condensed water at 4-10 degrees C in a state where the extraction and gas-liquid separation are cooled to ensure the temperature condition of the extraction and the temperature conditions of gas-liquid separation. The liquid carbon dioxide is introduced at high pressure state. Preferred Composition: The quality of total amount of liquid carbon dioxide and methanol is 21 kg. Preferred Conditions: The step (3) involves reacting at temperature of 10 degrees C and pressure of 55 MPa at flow rate of 3400 g/hour.

TI Silver nano-crystal useful as carbon dioxide electroreduction catalyst or ethylene epoxidation catalyst, prepared by carrying out reduction treatment of unprotected cubic silver(I) oxide nano-crystal in mixed gas carbon monoxide and argon.
AU HUANG W
   YANG M
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 201821468S
AB    NOVELTY - Silver nano-crystal is claimed. The silver nano-crystal has octahedral topography or cube topography without protective agent.
   USE - The nano-crystal is useful as carbon dioxide electroreduction catalyst or ethylene epoxidation catalyst (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-crystal has excellent catalytic performance and simple preparation method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing silver nano-crystal comprising carrying out reduction treatment of unprotected cubic silver(I) oxide nano-crystal in a mixed gas of carbon monoxide and argon to obtain cubic silver nano-crystal;
   (2) use of silver nano-crystal as carbon dioxide electroreduction catalyst or ethylene epoxidation catalyst comprising taking silver nano-crystal, and performing carbon dioxide electroreduction method;
   (3) ethylene epoxidation catalyst comprising silver nano-crystal and silicon dioxide; and
   (4) epoxidation method of ethylene comprising taking ethylene epoxidation catalyst as catalyst, and then epoxidizing ethylene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises pre-treating unprotected cubic silver(I) oxide nano-crystal in argon gas. The method further comprises pre-treating unprotected octahedral silver(I) oxide nano-crystal in argon gas, and then subjecting to reduction treatment in a mixed gas of carbon monoxide and argon to obtain octahedral silver nano-crystal. The method comprises passing argon gas through an unprotected cubic silver(I) oxide nano-crystal or unprotected octahedral silver(I) oxide nanocrystal at a rate of 10-100 ml/minute, and then heating at 100-200 degrees C at a rate of 0.5-5 degrees C/minute for 0.5-2 hours. The method comprises passing mixed gas of carbon monoxide and argon through pretreated unprotected cubic silver(I) oxide nano-crystal or unprotected octahedral silver(I) oxide nano-crystal at a rate of 10-100 ml/minute, and then heating at 50-200 degrees C at a rate of 0.5-5 degrees C/minute for 0.5-2 hours.

   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst uses europium oxide to promote                interaction between the active component nickel and                carrier gamma -alumina which is conducive to high                dispersion of nickel and improve the adsorption of                carbon dioxide, and has improved catalytic activity                of carbon dioxide methanation compared with the                existing technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass of metal                  nickel is 20 wt.%. The mass of europium oxide is                  5 wt.%. The calcining is done                  at 390-410 degrees C at heating rate of 4-6                  degrees C/minute for 3-5 hours.

TI New perovskite-type carbon dioxide            electro-reduction catalyst of inorganic compound.
AU ZHOU W
   WU X
   SUN Z
   XIE F
   DAI J
   GUO Y
   SHAO Z
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202095435U
AB    NOVELTY - A perovskite-type carbon dioxide                electro-reduction catalyst of inorganic compound                (I), is new.
   USE - As perovskite-type carbon dioxide                electro-reduction catalyst of inorganic compound                (I).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound (I) has no catalytic reduction                activity of carbon dioxide which becomes a highly                efficient catalyst material, high carbon monoxide                Faraday efficiency and has long-term operation                stability, and its performance is significantly                better than traditional silver foil catalysts. The                preparation method of the compound (I) is simple,                easy to be produced and used on a large scale, and                has certain value for reducing the concentration of                carbon dioxide in a present atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A perovskite-type carbon dioxide                electro-reduction catalyst of inorganic compound of                formula: (La0.8Sr0.2)1-xAgxMnO3(I), is new.
   x=0-0.2.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a method for preparing the compound (I),                involving performing sol-gel method, solid phase                reaction method, combustion method, hydrothermal                method or co-precipitation method according to the                stoichiometric ratio; and
   an application of the compound (I) in the                electro-reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sol-gel method                  involves (i) dissolving lanthanum nitrate,                  strontium nitrate, silver nitrate, and manganese                  acetate in deionized water according to the                  stoichiometric ratio and uniformly stirring, (ii)                  adding EDTA and citric acid monohydrate as metal                  ion complexing agents, and aqueous ammonia as a                  pH regulator of the mixed solution, heating and                  stirring the mixed solution to a gel shape, and                  baking at 180-250&#176; C for 3-10 hours to                  obtain a precursor powder, and (iii) calcining                  the precursor powder at 700-900&#176; C,                  preferably 800&#176; C in an oxygen                  atmosphere for 4-6 hours, preferably 5 hours. The                  application method involves adopting a                  three-electrode system, with perovskite oxide as                  a cathode, silver-silver chloride as a reference                  electrode, and platinum sheet as a counter                  electrode, and introducing a 0.1 M potassium                  bicarbonate solution used in the electrolytic                  cell and carbon dioxide gas for reaction.
   Preferred Definitions:
   x=0.05-0.12.
DC J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN KR2020071667-A; KR2239076-B1
TI Producing complex material of graphene and carbon nanofibers from carbon dioxide useful in lithium ion battery electrode material comprises reacting carbon dioxide in presence of magnesium-based carbon dioxide reducing agent.
AU LEE J
   BAIK S Y
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202056129S
AB    NOVELTY - Producing complex material of graphene and carbon nanofibers from carbon dioxide comprises adding and reacting carbon dioxide in the presence of a magnesium-based carbon dioxide reducing agent and a transition metal-based catalyst.
   USE - The complex material is useful in lithium ion battery anode material and cathode material, interlayer of a lithium sulfur battery (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces energy consumption and cost; improves electrochemical activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for complex material of graphene and carbon nanofibers comprising carbon nanofibers coexisting on the surface of graphene sheets or between each sheet, and charging/discharging capacity of 200-2000 mAh/g based on the voltage of 0.1-3.0 V and the current density of 0.05-20 A/g.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flow diagram of the producing complex material of graphene and carbon nanofibers from carbon dioxide (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The magnesium-based carbon dioxide reducing agent is magnesium, magnesium hydride, magnesium chloride, magnesium chloride hydrate, magnesium carbonate, magnesium hydroxide and magnesium boron hydride. The transition metal in transition metal-based catalyst is nickel, cobalt, copper, zinc, titanium, iron or manganese or its salts, hydrates and their oxides. The amount of transition metal-based catalyst is 1-100 mol.%. The thickness of the interlayer of the lithium sulfur battery is less than or equal to 20 mu m. Preferred Method: In the process, reacting by heating at 1-100 atmospheres. The flow rate of carbon dioxide during the heating reaction is 10-200 ml/minute. In the process, heating at 1-10 degrees C/minute to 600-1200 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours. The reactants are hydrochloric acid, perchloric acid, nitric acid and/or sulfuric acid. In the process, washing with distilled water or alcohol to perform neutralization of the carbon material and drying the washed carbon material. In the process, mixing the magnesium-based carbon dioxide reducing agent and the transition metal-based catalyst by physical mixing or dispersing in water, alcohol, and an organic solvent.

TI New ionic liquid loaded silica useful as catalyst            for preparation of cyclic carbonate in cycloaddition            reaction of carbon dioxide.
AU JIANG W
   WANG X
   LIU A
   ZHU R
   DONG Q
AE ANHUI JINHE IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
   UNIV HEFEI (UNHF-C)
GA 2020B7997S
AB    NOVELTY - Ionic liquid loaded silica (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful as catalyst for preparation of                cyclic carbonate in cycloaddition reaction of                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has larger specific surface area of                (108-617 m2/g) and high ionic liquid loading                capacity of (0.71-2.32 mmol/g); and exhibits                excellent catalytic performance, stable recovery                and reuse performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ionic liquid loaded silica of formula (I) is                new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) use of (I) as catalyst for preparation of                cyclic carbonate in cycloaddition reaction of                carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) respectively dissolving                  silicon tetrachloride and N-methylimidazole in                  organic solvent, dripping N-methylimidazole                  organic solution into silicon tetrachloride                  organic solution and carrying out solvent thermal                  reaction to obtain silicon-based ionic liquid,                  and (ii) adding water into the silicon-based                  ionic liquid, and continuously performing solvent                  thermal reaction to obtain (I). Preferred                  Components: The loading amount of ionic liquid is                  0.71-2.32 mmol/g and specific surface area of                  silica is 108-617 m2/g. The molar ratio between                  silicon tetrachloride and N-methylimidazole is                  1:1.5-3. The epoxides participating in carbon                  dioxide cycloaddition reaction are of formula                  (II).R = CH3, CH2CH3, ClCH2, phenyl, PhOCH2,                  CH2=CHCH2OCH2, C4H9, C8H17, C10H21 or                  C12H25.The molar ratio between silica and epoxide                  supported by ionic liquid is 0.005-0.03:1, the                  initial pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.1-3.5                  MPa, and the catalytic reaction temperature is                  70-90 degrees C. Preferred Method: In step (i),                  the organic solvent is toluene, xylene, methyl                  benzoate, diphenyl ether and/or tetrahydrofuran;                  the solvent thermal reaction temperature is                  80-120 degrees C and reaction time is 20-30                  hours. The step (ii) comprises adding amount of                  water to silicon tetrachloride in a molar volume                  ratio of 20 mmol:0.1-10 ml, preferably 20 mmol:3                  ml.

   USE - The ceramic support body is useful conventional water treatment and automobile exhaust gas treatment device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method remove carbon dioxide rapidly generated in large amount due to rapid oxidation at high temperature during heat treatment process, increases oxidation reaction rate, reduces process time and amount of carbon dioxide emitted to atmosphere, lowers possibility of deformation of structure of ceramic support body, and uses carbon dioxide removing agent which selectively reacts with carbon dioxide or material for adsorbing carbon dioxide.

Preferred Method: The heat treatment is carried out at 500-1700 degrees C. The step (c) comprises removing the carbon components, and sintering the laminate. The method further comprises applying pressure on the laminate, preformed before the step (c).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The temperature of the exhaust line of the kiln is less than 100 degrees C. The carbon dioxide removing agent is arranged in the exhaust line of the kiln and uses an independent heating system.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN FR3073429-A1; WO2019096657-A1; CN111372684-A; US2020276572-A1; EP3710162-A1; JP2021502891-W; FR3073429-B1; JP7145947-B2
TI Photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide            comprises e.g. charge containing carbon dioxide and            sacrificial compound is contacted with photocatalyst,            photocatalyst is irradiated with irradiation source            producing wavelength.
AU FECANT A
   BONDUELLE-SKRZYPCZAK A
   ATWI R
   ANTOINE F
   AUDREY B
   RANIN A
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 201942239S
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                carried out in the liquid phase and/or in the gas                phase under irradiation using a photocatalyst                comprising a support based on alumina or silica or                silica-alumina and nanoparticles of sulfide                molybdenum or tungsten sulfide having a band gap                greater than 2.3 eV, comprises: (a) a charge                containing carbon dioxide and at least one                sacrificial compound is contacted with the                photocatalyst; (b) the photocatalyst is irradiated                with at least one irradiation source producing at                least one wavelength less than the band gap width                of the photocatalyst so as to reduce the carbon                dioxide and oxidize the sacrificial compound in the                presence of the photocatalyst activated by the                source irradiation, so as to produce an effluent                containing at least in part at least one                carbon-containing molecules s, different from                carbon dioxide.
   USE - The process is useful for photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process improves the performance compared                to known photocatalysts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process: when carried                  out in the gas phase, the sacrificial compound is                  a gaseous compound comprising water, ammonia,                  hydrogen, methane or an alcohol; when carried out                  in liquid phase, the sacrificial compound is a                  liquid compound comprising water, ammonia, an                  alcohol, an aldehyde or an amine. The                  photocatalyst is prepared according to a process                  comprises: (i) an impregnation step by contacting                  a solution comprising an organic solvent A and at                  least one mononuclear precursor based on Mo or W,                  denoted M1, in their monomeric or dimeric form,                  having at least one M1 is O or M1-OR bond or at                  least one M1 is S or M1-SR bond, where R is CxHy,                  x is greater than 1 and y is (x-1) to (2x+1),                  with a support based on alumina or silica or                  silica-alumina, advantageously previously                  calcined under vacuum or under a stream of inert                  gas to evacuating the water possibly physisorbed                  on the support; (ii) a maturation step; (iii) a                  step of drying the impregnated support, at a                  temperature below 200 degrees C, under an                  anhydrous atmosphere or under vacuum or underflow                  of inert gas; and (iv) a sulfurization step.                  Preferred Components: The diluent fluid is                  present in steps (a) and/or (b). The irradiation                  source is a source of artificial or natural                  irradiation. The irradiation source emits at                  least in a wavelength range greater than 280 nm.                  The porous support does not absorb energy photons                  greater than 4 eV. The photocatalyst comprises                  4-50 wt.% molybdenum sulfide or tungsten sulfide                  photocatalyst. The surface density which                  corresponds to the amount of molybdenum (Mo)                  atoms or tungsten (W) atoms, deposited per unit                  area support, is 0.5 and 12 atoms of Mo or W per                  square nanometer of support. The molybdenum                  precursor comprises e.g. Mo(OCH2CH3)5,                  Mo(OCH2CH3)6. The tungsten precursor comprises                  e.g. W(OCH2CH3)5, W(OCH2CH3)6 and W(=O)(                  OCH2CH3)4.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   VENZ M
   EIDEN S
   BURBACH T
