   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO GERMANY AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 2018A3999H
AB    NOVELTY - Gas diffusion electrode comprises at least one                planar, electrically conductive support and a gas                diffusion layer and applied electrocatalyst                deposited on the support, where the gas diffusion                layer comprises electrocatalyst and/or hydrophobic                polymer. The silver acts as an electrocatalyst, and                electrocatalyst contains silver in the form of                highly porous agglomerated nanoparticles and the                nanoparticles has a surface measured by BET of at                least 2 m2/g.
   USE - The gas diffusion electrode is useful for                reducing carbon dioxide, for the electrolysis of                carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide, in the                chloralkali electrolysis or oxygen                electrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas diffusion electrode ensures carbon                dioxide reduction at high current density and high                selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide to                carbon monoxide, comprising reacting carbon dioxide                cathodically to carbon monoxide at a gas diffusion                electrode and simultaneously producing on the anode                side of chlorine or oxygen.

PT P
PN CN114917742-A
TI Capturing and recycling low-temperature carbon            dioxide using metal oxide and vicinal diol complex as            adsorbent involves dissolving vicinal diol compound in            solvent to obtain vicinal diol compound solution,            adding metal oxide and adjacent glycol solution to            reactor, and introducing gas.
AU LIU J
   WU Y
   ZOU S
   FAN J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2022B0945P
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing and recycling low-temperature carbon                dioxide using metal oxide and vicinal diol complex                as adsorbent involves dissolving vicinal diol                compound (I) in solvent to obtain vicinal diol                compound (I) solution, adding metal oxide and the                vicinal diol compound (I) solution to the reactor,                introducing gas containing carbon dioxide into the                adsorbent suspension at a certain flow rate under                the temperature below 50&#8451; and stirring                conditions to carry out the reaction, separating                solid reaction product by centrifugation after the                reaction is completed, washing with deionized                water, and drying to obtain the solid product of                carbon dioxide capture reaction, namely metal                carbonate.
   USE - Method for capturing and recycling                low-temperature carbon dioxide using metal oxide                and vicinal diol complex as adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes carbon dioxide capture and                regeneration with low energy consumption, and can                sustainably use metal oxide as adsorbents to                capture carbon dioxide as 1C raw materials, which                has great application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Capturing and recycling low-temperature carbon                dioxide using metal oxide and vicinal diol complex                as adsorbent involves dissolving vicinal diol                compound of formula (I) in solvent to obtain                vicinal diol compound of formula (I) solution,                adding metal oxide and the vicinal diol compound                (I) solution to the reactor, introducing gas                containing carbon dioxide into the adsorbent                suspension at a certain flow rate under the                temperature below 50&#8451; and stirring                conditions to carry out the reaction, separating                solid reaction product by centrifugation after the                reaction is completed, washing with deionized                water, drying to obtain the solid product of carbon                dioxide capture reaction, namely metal carbonate,                where the ratio of the amount of the metal oxide to                the amount of the adjacent diol is 0.01-20:1, and                the metal oxide is lead oxide (PbO), lead oxide                (Pb3O4), bismuth oxide (Bi2O3), and zinc oxide                (ZnO), calcining the solid product of carbon                dioxide capture reaction in a muffle furnace to                obtain carbon dioxide gas and metal oxide, and                realizing the regeneration of metal oxide and the                release of carbon dioxide.
   R1, R2, R3, R4=H, hydrocarbon (optionally                substituted), halogen, aldehyde, carboxyl,                methylol, amino, cyano, alkoxy, nitro, furan                (optionally substituted), thiophene, phenyl, and/or                pyridine.

PN WO2022020634-A1; EP4076701-A1
TI Direct air capture device used to capture carbon            dioxide molecules, comprises contactor, polyamine            sorbent associated with contactor, vacuum pump and            resonant cavity comprising gas sealable entrance,            vacuum port and microwave generator.
AU ATWOOD M
AE ATWOOD M (ATWO-Individual)
   ATWOOD M (ATWO-Individual)
GA 202216422A
AB    NOVELTY - Direct air capture (DAC) device comprises: a                contactor; a polyamine sorbent associated with the                contactor, where many carbon dioxide molecules in                the air contacting the polyamine sorbent form a                bond with the polyamine sorbent; a vacuum pump                (1020); and a resonant cavity (1015) comprising a                gas sealable entrance adapted to allow the                contactor to enter the resonant cavity and seal the                resonant cavity, a vacuum port (1025), where the                vacuum pump is in gaseous connection with the                vacuum port to evacuate the resonant cavity and a                microwave generator adapted to be                electromagnetically connected to the resonant                cavity, where the microwave generator is adapted to                select a microwave frequency to optimize breaking                the bond between the polyamine sorbent and the                carbon dioxide molecules releasing the carbon                dioxide molecules into the vacuum port, and the                gaseous carbon dioxide molecules released by the                microwave generator are removed from the resonant                cavity through the vacuum port.
   USE - The device is useful for capturing carbon                dioxide molecules from air with contained release                of carbon dioxide molecules (claimed) and                regenerating the sorbents used to capture the                carbon dioxide which is product used widely in                industry in food and beverage and agriculture                sectors.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: utilizes resonant cavity that is                tuned to provide uniform heating; utilizes vacuum                pump that reduces the pressure in the resonant                cavity to between a lower limit of 0.2 bar and an                upper limit of 0.8 bar; and captures carbon dioxide                in air using solid amine sorbents and a microwave                generator to desorb the carbon dioxide by directly                exciting the amine-carbon bond, thus significantly                reducing the energy cost of releasing the carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) capturing carbon dioxide molecules from                air with contained release of carbon dioxide                molecules; and
   (2) a continuous DAC device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates schematic view of a                holder with associated monolith contactors on which                the sorbent is associated inserted through a gas                sealable entrance into a resonant cavity in which a                microwave or radio frequency source, waveguide and                a tuning element, are used to irradiate the                sorbent.
   Microwave or radio frequency source                (1010)
   Resonant cavity (1015)
   Vacuum pump (1020)
   Vacuum port (1025)
   Monolith contactors (1035)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyamine sorbent                  comprises linear polyethylenimine (PEI), branched                  PEI, aziridine, diethylenetriamine,                  triethylenetetramine, diethylenetriamine                  organosilane, aminopropyl organosilane, linear                  PEI functionalized cellulose acetate silica                  dioxide sorbent, branched PEI functionalized                  cellulose acetate silica dioxide sorbent                  material, linear PEI incorporated into a metal                  organic framework, branched PEI incorporated into                  a metal organic framework, amine incorporated                  into a metal organic framework, polyethylene                  Mobil composition of matter-41, or                  3-trimethoxysilylpropyl diethylenetriamine Santa                  Barbara amorphous-15.

PN KR2303107-B1; CN113522070-A
TI Carbon dioxide dissolution module for injecting carbon dioxide gas into washing water, has fixed block structure that is provided with needle coupling hole at rear end, where carbon dioxide gas is directly injected into water passing through conduit through needle portion to generate treated water.
AU SUK P
   PARK Y
AE DMS CO LTD (DMSD-C)
GA 2021B1118E
AB    NOVELTY - The module (100) has a main structure (110) that is provided with a main pipe portion with a first conduit through which the washing water is transported, and a branch pipe portion that is coupled to the main pipe portion for supplying carbon dioxide gas to the first conduit. A gas injection unit (130) is coupled to the main structure to supply carbon dioxide gas to the first conduit. The gas injection unit is coupled to the branch pipe portion. The housing is provided with a second conduit through which carbon dioxide gas is transported. A first needle coupling hole is formed at the front end of the fixed block structure to support one end of a needle hub. A second needle coupling hole is formed at the rear end of the fixed block structure to support other end of the needle hub. The carbon dioxide gas is directly injected into the washing water passing through the first conduit through a needle portion to generate treated water with controlled specific resistance.
   USE - Carbon dioxide dissolution module for injecting carbon dioxide gas into washing water, used in apparatus (claimed) for processing substrate used for display, semiconductor wafer, LCD and photomask glass.
   ADVANTAGE - The solubility of the carbon dioxide gas in the washing water is significantly increased in a short time by directly injecting micro-sized carbon dioxide gas on the transfer line of the washing water through a needle-type gas injection unit with a micro-channel and by temporarily stopping or delaying the flow of treated water in which carbon dioxide gas is dissolved through the buffer unit. The reduced resistivity value of the treated water supplied to the surface of the substrate is uniform at all times, thus the generation of static electricity between the treated water and substrate is minimized or suppressed, and manufacturing quality of the substrate is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for processing substrate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the apparatus for processing substrate.
   Carbon dioxide dissolution module (100)
   Main structure (110)
   Gas injection unit (130)
   Washing water supply unit (200)
   Gas supply unit (300)

PN CN109097188-A
TI Preparing cooked cumin oil comprises taking cumin, steaming, heating, sonicating, washing, filtering, drying, roasting, naturally cooling, crushing and extracting.
AU LI R
   CHEN S
   WEI Y
AE HANCHENG HONGDA FOOD FLAVOR CO LTD (HANC-Non-standard)
GA 201902434U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cooked cumin oil comprises (i) taking cumin and steaming in water; (ii) adding celery juice into the product, heating, sonicating, washing with water, filtering and drying the residue; (iii) roasting the product, naturally cooling and crushing; (iv) extracting using a supercritical carbon dioxide device for primary extraction at 28-35 degrees C for 8-15 minutes with pressure of 10-15 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-30 l/hour; (v) depressurizing to atmospheric pressure and performing secondary extraction at 35-50 degrees C for 15-20 minutes with pressure of 25-35 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-30 l/hour; (vi) depressurizing to atmospheric pressure and performing third extraction at 55-65 degrees C for 25-35 minutes with pressure of 45-55 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 40-50 l/hour; and (vii) depressurizing to atmospheric pressure and performing fourth extraction at 70-75 degrees C for 5-10 minutes with pressure of 65 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 50 l/hour.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cooked cumin oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is pollution-free and has high extraction efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cooked cumin oil comprises (i) taking 100 pts. wt. cumin and steaming at 100 degrees C for 30-50 minutes in water; (ii) adding 10-20 pts. wt. celery juice into the product, heating at 80-90 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours, sonicating, washing 3-5 times with water, filtering and drying the residue at 100-105 degrees C; (iii) roasting the product at 150-160 degrees C for 8-14 minutes, naturally cooling and crushing; (iv) extracting using a supercritical carbon dioxide device for primary extraction at 28-35 degrees C for 8-15 minutes with pressure of 10-15 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-30 l/hour; (v) depressurizing to atmospheric pressure and performing secondary extraction at 35-50 degrees C for 15-20 minutes with pressure of 25-35 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-30 l/hour; (vi) depressurizing to atmospheric pressure and performing third extraction at 55-65 degrees C for 25-35 minutes with pressure of 45-55 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 40-50 l/hour; and (vii) depressurizing to atmospheric pressure and performing fourth extraction at 70-75 degrees C for 5-10 minutes with pressure of 65 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 50 l/hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method for preparing celery juice comprises washing the celery, removing the leaves, cutting, mixing with 30 times the mass of water, pulping, filtering and taking the filtrate. In the step (ii), sonication is carried out for 20-40 minutes at frequency of 45KHz and input power of 300 W and drying until the moisture content is 3-5%. In the step (iii) passing the product through a 100 meshes sieve.

TI Bismuth vanadate/copper vanadate composite            photocatalyst used in visible light catalytic reduction            of carbon dioxide to generate carbon monoxide, is            formed by growth of copper vanadate nanosheet on            surface of bismuth vanadate microsheet.
AU CHEN L
   DUAN Z
   LI Q
   SONG Y
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202010609T
AB    NOVELTY - Bismuth vanadate/copper vanadate composite                photocatalyst is formed by in-situ growth of a                copper vanadate nanosheet on the surface of a                bismuth vanadate microsheet.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful in photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide, preferably visible                light catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to                generate carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst exhibits synergistic effect                of bismuth vanadate and copper vanadate, has                increased light response range, inhibits                recombination rate of photogenerated                electron-holes, improves activity of photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide and achieves excellent                application effect on catalytic carbon dioxide                reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the photocatalyst involving                dispersing the bismuth vanadate microsheet into a                copper nitrate solution, dripping a sodium                orthovanadate solution, stirring and reacting at                25-95 degrees C, transferring a reaction product to                a high-pressure reaction kettle and carrying out                hydrothermal reaction at 120-180 degrees C to                obtain the photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The bismuth vanadate                  microsheet has a size of 0.5-1.5 mu m and the                  size of the copper vanadate nanosheet is 80-100                  nm. Preferred Property: The copper vanadate                  nanosheet comprises copper vanadate of formula:                  Cu0.4V2O5. Preferred Composition: The                  photocatalyst comprises 6-48 %mass copper                  vanadate nanosheet. The copper nitrate solution                  has a concentration of 0.003-0.024 mol/l. The                  concentration of the sodium orthovanadate                  solution is 0.00075-0.006 mol/l. The copper                  nitrate solution and the sodium orthovanadate                  solution are in a dosage ratio of (3:1)-(1:1)                  metering according to the molar ratio of copper                  nitrate and sodium orthovanadate. Preferred                  Method: The method involves stirring and reacting                  for 5.5-7.5 hours. The method involves carrying                  out the hydrothermal reaction for 8-10                  hours.

PN CN110496604-A; CN110496604-B
TI Preparing cobalt-nickel bimetallic organic carbon            dioxide adsorption material useful for adsorbing            industrial carbon dioxide at room temperature and            normal pressure, comprises e.g. dissolving nickel salt            and cobalt salt in water, and mixing.
AU HE C
   CHEN C
   MA M
   CHENG Y
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2019A40980
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cobalt-nickel bimetallic organic                carbon dioxide adsorption material, comprises                dissolving a nickel salt and a cobalt salt in water                to obtain a solution A, dissolving                2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid in a solution                containing ethanol and N,N-dimethylformamide to                obtain a solution B, uniformly mixing the solution                A and the solution B to obtain a mixed solution,                heating the mixed solution in a microwave                ultraviolet ultrasonic synthesis reactor to 110-140                degrees C for 20-60 minutes, filtering, washing,                drying, and activating under vacuum                condition.
   USE - The material is useful for adsorbing                industrial carbon dioxide at room temperature and                normal pressure (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has a uniform, regular and                dispersed micro-morphology, large specific surface                area, high micropore capacity, and extremely high                carbon dioxide adsorption capacity; and has simple                preparation process with mild adsorption conditions                and large amount of adsorption, and excellent                selectivity and reusability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel salt is                  nickel nitrate, and the cobalt salt is cobalt                  acetate. The mass ratio of nickel nitrate and                  cobalt acetate in solution A is 1:1-6. The volume                  ratio of water, ethanol and N,N-dimethylformamide                  is 1:0.5-3:1-5. The mass ratio of the total mass                  of nickel nitrate and cobalt acetate, and                  2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid is 3-6:1. The                  total mass fraction of nickel nitrate and cobalt                  acetate in the mixed solution is 0.5-3 wt.%.                  Preferred Method: The activation treatment is                  carried out at 200-250 degrees C for 4-6                  hours.

TI Method for controlling air conditioner, involves calculating solid pollutant concentration by solid contaminant detector, and rotating purifying component such that air of air inlet passage is passed through purifying component.
AU MA Y
   CAO H
   CHEN X
   SHAO Y
   CAO L
   PENG L
AE GD MIDEA AIR-CONDITIONING EQUIP CO LTD (MEDE-C)
GA 2019548729
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating concentration of carbon dioxide collected by a carbon dioxide detector, where the carbon dioxide concentration is greater than preset carbon dioxide concentration. A fresh air valve is opened. Solid pollutant concentration is calculated by a solid contaminant detector. A purifying component is rotated such that air of an air inlet passage is passed through the purifying component according to the solid pollutant concentration. A mapping relationship is established between the carbon dioxide concentration and the fresh air valve. A target angle of the purifying component is calculated.
   USE - Method for controlling air conditioner (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving air cleanliness so as to ensure health of a user.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an air conditioner.

AB    NOVELTY - A barn automatic sterilizing method involves clearing a storage space of a barn, injecting a gas mixed with nitrogen and carbon dioxide into the barn, mixing the mixed gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide through the air inlet of the barn, blowing the powder and the insect body on the wall of the storage space of the barn in the spraying process, performing a suffocating insecticide on the storage space of the barn by mixed gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide, and sealing the storage space for 1-24 hours while blocking the entry of gas and static sterilization for any species in the barn.

PN CN109180833-A
TI Extracting polysaccharides from myrtle fruit by supercritical carbon dioxide comprises e.g. selecting myrtle fruit as raw material, carrying out pulverization of myrtle fruit, natural drying, crushing by pulverizer and drying.
AU YIN H
   MENG F
   LIU W
   ZHAO W
   JIANG Y
   QIN Z
   SUN J
AE GUANGXI ZHUANG AUTONOMOUS REGION INST (GUAG-C)
GA 201909330A
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting polysaccharides from myrtle fruit by supercritical carbon dioxide comprises (1) selecting myrtle fruit as raw material, (2) carrying out pulverization of myrtle fruit, natural drying, crushing by pulverizer, passing through 40-80 mesh sieve and drying to constant weight, (3) soaking the peach fruit, adding into beaker and soaking it with an cobalt-solvent, (4) carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, (3) placing infiltrated myrtle fruit in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, and extraction temperature is adjusted to 25-65 degrees C, the cosolvent ratio is 15-40%, and the extraction pressure is 80-150 Bar, total flow rate is 15-30 g/min, extraction time 30-210 minutes, extraction and collecting the extract of myrtle fruit directly at the outlet of the collection kettle.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting polysaccharides from myrtle fruit by supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The purity of the carbon dioxide is 99.99%. The entraining agent comprises 95% ethanol, 80% ethanol, 60% ethanol, and 60% methanol. The process parameter of the supercritical CO 2 extraction process in the step (4) is an extraction temperature at 35 degrees C, 95% ethanol, cosolvent ratio 30%, extraction pressure 100 Bar, total flow rate 25 g/min, extraction time 120 minutes, concentrating the obtained extract, adding 3 times volume of absolute ethanol for alcohol precipitation, centrifugation, discarding and removing supernatant and evaporating precipitate to dryness to obtain a crude polysaccharide.

AU JIAN Y
   LIU X
   WANG X
   GUO R
   HOU Y
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 201868065T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing the relationship between the carbon dioxide concentration of the room and the ventilation volume of the room according to the basic principle of mass balance, and according to the continuous test data of the room carbon dioxide concentration using the measurement method of emitting carbon dioxide as a tracer gas in the human body.    USE - Dynamic measuring method for room ventilation quantity.
   ADVANTAGE - The ventilation of the room is calculated quickly and conveniently in each time interval, without limiting the emission of carbon dioxide in the room, so that the dynamic change of the ventilation volume of the room can be truly reflected based on the measured data of room carbon dioxide concentration.

GA 201820088F

   USE - Functional porous organic polymer catalyst for                photocatalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to                carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The functional porous organic polymer catalyst                achieves high-efficiency visible light catalytic                conversion of carbon dioxide to produce carbon                monoxide. The catalyst has large specific surface                area, and has mild catalytic reaction conditions,                high yield and excellent cycle performance, and                meets the actual production needs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:

PT P
PN US2018179460-A1; US10150926-B2
TI Plant used for removing hydrogen sulfide and            carbon dioxide from feed source, comprises e.g. feed            gas source to provide hydrocarbonaceous feed gas            comprising hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide, and            selective hydrogen sulfide removal unit.
AU MAK J
AE FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
GA 201850617H
AB    NOVELTY - Plant (P1) comprises: (a) a feed gas source                that is configured to provide a hydrocarbonaceous                feed gas (1) comprising hydrogen sulfide and carbon                dioxide at a pressure of at least 300 psig; (b) an                hydrogen sulfide removal unit (51) configured to                selectively remove hydrogen sulfide from the feed                gas to produce an hydrogen sulfide depleted feed                gas (4); (c) a first absorber and a second absorber                fluidly coupled in series and configured to remove                carbon dioxide from the hydrogen sulfide depleted                feed gas using a semi-lean solvent (10) and an                ultra-lean solvent (3) respectively; (d) many                pressure reduction stages configured to receive and                reduce pressure of the rich solvent to generate                work, a carbon dioxide stream, and a cooled flashed                solvent; (e) a first heat exchanger and a second                heat exchanger configured to use the cooled flashed                solvent to cool the hydrogen sulfide depleted feed                gas and the semi-lean solvent; (f) a stripping                unit; and (g) a conduit.
   USE - The plants are useful for removing hydrogen                sulfide and carbon dioxide from feed source                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The plants: provide flexible removal of carbon                dioxide; sequentially and selectively remove                hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide; can be                operated at low temperature and absence (or very                low quantities) of water in the physical solvent,                thus the plants are non-corrosive; do not require                cooling duty; and produce a hydrogen sulfide-free                carbon dioxide stream that can be used for carbon                dioxide sequestration while minimizing energy                consumption of the hydrogen sulfide removal unit by                maximizing the carbon dioxide content to the carbon                dioxide removal unit, thus utilizing carbon dioxide                content for refrigeration, and achieving production                of a low carbon dioxide product stream at lower                energy costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Plant (P1) comprises: (a) a feed gas source                that is configured to provide a hydrocarbonaceous                feed gas (1) comprising hydrogen sulfide and carbon                dioxide at a pressure of at least 300 psig; (b) an                hydrogen sulfide removal unit (51) configured to                selectively remove hydrogen sulfide from the feed                gas to produce an hydrogen sulfide depleted feed                gas (4); (c) a first absorber and a second absorber                fluidly coupled in series and configured to remove                carbon dioxide from the hydrogen sulfide depleted                feed gas using a semi-lean solvent (10) and an                ultra-lean solvent (3) respectively, thus producing                a product gas and a rich solvent; (d) many pressure                reduction stages configured to receive and reduce                pressure of the rich solvent to generate work, a                carbon dioxide stream, and a cooled flashed                solvent; (e) a first heat exchanger and a second                heat exchanger configured to use the cooled flashed                solvent to cool the hydrogen sulfide depleted feed                gas and the semi-lean solvent; (f) a stripping unit                configured to strip the flashed solvent with dried                air to thus produce the ultra-lean solvent; and (g)                a conduit configured to combine a portion of the                ultra-lean solvent with the hydrogen sulfide                depleted feed gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for a plant (P2) comprising: a feed gas                source that is configured to provide a                hydrocarbonaceous feed gas comprising hydrogen                sulfide and carbon dioxide; an hydrogen sulfide                removal unit configured to receive the                hydrocarbonaceous feed gas and selectively remove                hydrogen sulfide from the feed gas to produce an                hydrogen sulfide depleted feed gas; the components                (c)-(f) as above per se; and a conduit configured                to combine a portion of the ultra-lean solvent with                the hydrogen sulfide depleted feed gas between the                hydrogen sulfide removal unit and the first heat                exchanger.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows schematic representation of                the plant.
   Hydrocarbonaceous feed gas (1)
   Ultra-lean solvent (3)
   Hydrogen sulfide depleted feed gas (4)
   Semi-lean solvent (10)
   Hydrogen sulfide removal unit (51)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the plants (P1) and                  (P2): the hydrogen sulfide removal unit is                  configured to remove hydrogen sulfide using a                  solvent or a solid phase adsorbent; the pressure                  reduction stages are configured so that at least                  85% of the carbon dioxide content in the rich                  solvent is removed from the solvent at                  atmospheric pressure; and the first absorber and                  the second absorber are formed in a single vessel                  having a first absorber section and a second                  absorber section. The plant (P2) further                  comprises: a hydraulic turbine configured to                  receive the rich solvent and generate the work; a                  third heat exchanger configured to cool the                  hydrogen sulfide depleted feed gas upstream of                  the first absorber using the product gas; and a                  pressure reduction valve configured to reduce the                  pressure of the cooled flashed solvent and cool                  the cooled flashed solvent. In the plant (P2):                  the second absorber is configured to receive at                  least a portion of the ultra-lean solvent and                  produce the semi-lean solvent due to absorption                  of carbon dioxide in the ultra-lean solvent; and                  the first heat exchanger is configured to cool                  the hydrogen sulfide depleted feed gas upstream                  of the first absorber using the cooled flashed                  solvent, where the second heat exchanger is                  configured to cool the semi-lean solvent from the                  second absorber between the second absorber and                  the first absorber.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is a                  physical solvent comprising propylene carbonate,                  tributyl phosphate, normal methyl pyrrolidone, a                  dimethyl ether of polyethylene glycol, and a                  polyethylene glycol dialkyl ether. The                  hydrocarbonaceous feed gas comprises at least 20                  mol.% carbon dioxide. The dried air has a water                  dew point of less than or equal to -40 degrees F.                  The product gas has a carbon dioxide content of                  500 ppmv to 0.5 mol.% carbon dioxide, and the                  carbon dioxide product is a dry hydrogen                  sulfide-free gas suitable for carbon dioxide                  sequestration.

PT P
PN CN113898430-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide energy storage system utilizing waste heat of internal combustion engine has internal combustion engine unit air outlet connected to exhaust gas-water heat exchanger hot side inlet, and exhaust gas discharged from hot side outlet of exhaust gas-water heat exchanger.
AU GU Z
   ZHANG L
   QIAO Y
   ZHANG X
   WU J
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 202213742Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a heat storage system comprising a hot tank (13) and a cold tank (9). An energy storage system comprises a supercritical carbon dioxide high-pressure storage tank (18) and a supercritical carbon dioxide low-pressure storage tank (14), and an internal combustion engine intake and exhaust system. The outlet of a cold side of the intercooler (2), is connected to the inlet of the hot tank. The outlet of a turbine (4), is connected to the inlet of the hot side of the intercooler. The hot side outlet of the intercooler, is connected to the intake port of an internal combustion engine unit (1). The air outlet of the internal combustion engine unit, is connected to the hot side inlet of an exhaust gas-water heat exchanger (5). The exhaust gas is discharged from the hot side outlet of the exhaust gas-water heat exchanger.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide energy storage system utilizing waste heat of internal combustion engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy storage density of the energy storage system, can be improved. The turbine hysteresis problem of the traditional turbocharger, can be reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a working method of a kind of supercritical carbon dioxide energy storage system utilizing the waste heat of internal combustion engine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a supercritical carbon dioxide energy storage system using an internal combustion engine waste heat. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   internal combustion engine unit (1)
   Intercooler (2)
   Motor (3)
   Turbine (4)
   Waste gas-water heat exchanger (5)
   First-stage supercritical carbon dioxide-water intercooler (6)
   Second-stage supercritical carbon dioxide-water intercooler (7)
   Cold tank (9)
   Radiator (10)
   Supercritical carbon dioxide-water heat exchanger (11)
   Supercritical carbon dioxide-water reheater (12)
   Hot tank (13)
   Supercritical carbon dioxide low-pressure storage tank (14)
   First throttle valve (15)
   First stage compressor (16)
   Second stage compressor (17)
   Supercritical carbon dioxide high-pressure storage tank (18)
   First level turbine (20)
   Second level turbine (21)
   Precooler (22)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); Q78 (Heat exchange in general (F28)); Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P)); Q55 (Positive displacement fluid machines/pumps/compressors (i.e. for driving fluid) (F04B,C))
MC E31-N05C; Q51-J02F; Q51-C; Q55-A; Q78-T99

PT P
PN WO2018176026-A1; US2018273846-A1; CA3056784-A1; CN110461810-A; US10760004-B2; CN110461810-B
TI Pyrolysis of feedstock for producing hydrocarbon            products, involves pyrolyzing feedstock to generate            products comprising tail gas, separating portion of            tail gas and carbon dioxide, converting to formic acid,            and pyrolyzing formic acid.
AU GARCIA-PEREZ M
   WALTER J C
AE TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   UNIV WASHINGTON STATE (UNWS-C)
   UNIV WASHINGTON STATE (UNWS-C)
GA 201875478W
AB    NOVELTY - Pyrolysis (100) of feedstock involves                pyrolyzing (102) a feedstock to generate pyrolysis                reaction products comprising at least some tail gas                from the feedstock, separating (104) a portion of                the tail gas and carbon dioxide from the products                into separated tail gas comprising one or more                components, converting (106) at least some of the                separated tail gas and carbon dioxide to formic                acid, and injecting (108) at least some of the                formic acid into a pyrolysis reactor.
   USE - Pyrolysis of feedstock e.g. bituminous coal,                sub-bituminous coal, lignite, and anthracite (all                claimed), for producing hydrocarbon products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical,                efficiently provides pyrolyzed products with high                yield and high selectivity under supercritical                carbon dioxide atmosphere, and uses catalyst                particles with improved activity that reduces                fouling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                for pyrolyzing coal, which comprises at least one                reaction chamber capable of pyrolyzing coal in                supercritical carbon dioxide atmosphere, a                separation system configured to receive the                supercritical carbon dioxide atmosphere from the                reaction chamber after pyrolysis, condense reaction                products from carbon dioxide, and generate tail gas                and carbon dioxide stream, a dry reforming system                configured to reform the tail gas and carbon                dioxide stream into a stream (S1) containing                syngas, a water-gas shift reactor configured to                convert at least some of the stream (S1) into a                stream (S2) containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen,                a formic acid production unit configured to convert                at least some of the stream (S2) into a stream (S3)                containing formic acid, and an injection system                configured to inject the formic acid into the                reaction chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the                simplified method for pyrolyzing feedstock to                generate donor hydrogen from tail gas at a                high-level.
   Pyrolysis of feedstock (100)
   Pyrolysis of feedstock to generate pyrolysis                reaction products (102)
   Separation of portion of tail gas and carbon                dioxide from reaction products (104)
   Conversion of separated tail gas and carbon                dioxide to formic acid (106)
   Injection of formic acid into pyrolysis                reactor (108)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The system further                  comprises a heat source and a nuclear reactor,                  which provides thermal energy to the reaction                  chamber or to the carbon dioxide before injecting                  the carbon dioxide into the reaction chamber, and                  a valve that controls the flow of carbon dioxide                  from the reaction chamber to the separation                  system including volatile gas separation system.                  The reaction chamber is capable of heating the                  carbon dioxide to a supercritical state.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The particles of                  activated carbon have a mean particle diameter of                  less than 0.045 mm (e.g. passes through a 325 US                  mesh), less than 0.180 mm (e.g. passes through a                  80 US mesh), less than 0.5 mm (e.g. passes                  through a 35 US mesh), or less than 25.4 mm (e.g.                  passes through 1 inch US mesh).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves separating a stream of methane and                  carbon dioxide from the reaction products, and                  converting at least some of the separated methane                  and carbon dioxide to formic acid. The method                  preferably involves pyrolyzing coal in carbon                  dioxide atmosphere in a reactor at a temperature                  and pressure where the carbon dioxide is in a                  supercritical state to generate reaction products                  including at least some methane, removing the                  supercritical carbon dioxide and reaction                  products from the reactor, separating methane and                  carbon dioxide from the removed supercritical                  carbon dioxide and reaction products by lowering                  the temperature and/or pressure, of the carbon                  dioxide, to generate the stream (S1) including at                  least methane and carbon dioxide, dry reforming                  at least a portion of the stream (S1) to generate                  the stream (S2) including at least hydrogen and                  carbon monoxide, converting at least a portion of                  the stream (S2) into the stream (S3) including                  hydrogen and carbon dioxide by water-gas shift                  reaction, converting portion(s) of the stream                  (S3) into formic acid via formic acid generation,                  and injecting at least some of the generated                  formic acid into the reactor. The method further                  involves mixing the formic acid with feedstock at                  a temperature of less than 100 degrees C and a                  pressure of less than 1 MPa to obtain a feedstock                  and formic acid mixture and pyrolyzing the                  mixture, or mixing the feedstock with a ruthenium                  catalyst supported on particles of activated                  carbon to obtain a feedstock and ruthenium                  catalyst mixture and pyrolyzing the mixture, or                  mixing the feedstock with formic acid and a                  ruthenium catalyst supported on particles of                  activated carbon at a temperature of less than                  100 degrees C and a pressure of less than 1 MPa                  to obtain a feedstock/formic acid/ruthenium                  catalyst mixture and pyrolyzing the mixture, to                  generate pyrolysis reaction products. Preferred                  Conditions: The method is carried out using                  supercritical carbon dioxide at a temperature                  (T1) of 150-600 degrees C and a pressure (P1) of                  7-12 MPa.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PN WO2022219087-A1
TI Air treatment module for managing carbon dioxide            scrubbing, comprises rotational filter assembly            comprising filter unit provided with carbon dioxide            capturing media, inlet and outlet, conduit connecting            outlet to inlet, and flow generator.
AU IRANMANESH I
   EDSTROEM A
AE SALLY AB R (SALL-Non-standard)
GA 2022D1889K
AB    NOVELTY - The module (100) captures carbon dioxide from                a main stream of air having a carbon dioxide                concentration of less than 300 parts per million. A                rotational filter assembly (110) comprises a filter                unit that is provided with an upstream side and a                downstream side in a regenerating region (114). A                flow generator is arranged at the downstream side                of the filter unit. Another flow generator in fluid                communication with an inlet (120) generates a flow                via the inlet towards a capturing region (112). The                module receives a control signal to cause another                inlet and an outlet to assume a closed                configuration until a predetermined condition is                fulfilled. A sensor determines the concentration of                carbon dioxide in a collection loop.
   USE - Air treatment module for managing carbon                dioxide scrubbing in commercial, industrial and                residential buildings. Uses include but are not                limited to residential homes, commercial buildings,                industrial buildings, office buildings, and                industrial buildings.
   ADVANTAGE - The module provides energy efficient scrubbing                of the air while collecting carbon dioxide in a                closed loop, allowing non-clean air from a locale,                e.g. an office space, to be scrubbed of carbon                dioxide. The module is configured to only collect                carbon dioxide, thus avoiding collecting a gas with                unspecific composition.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the air treatment module.
   100Air treatment module
   110Rotational filter assembly
   112Capturing region
   114Regenerating region
   120Inlet

PN CN217236627-U
TI Detonating auxiliary device for carbon dioxide            induced cracking tube, has supporting frame and            electric heating wire sleeved outside excitation pipe,            and switchboard provided with switchboard switch and            indicator lamp connected with power supply line.
AU XU C
   LI H
   SHANG Y
   LV B
   LIU Y
   ZHANG H
   LI W
AE HEBEI IRON & STEEL GROUP MINING CO LTD (HBIS-C)
GA 2022B07255
AB    NOVELTY - A detonating auxiliary device of carbon                dioxide cracking tube, belonging to the carbon                dioxide induced cracking tube initiation device,                for heating the carbon dioxide cracking tube before                initiation. The technical solution is as follows:                the supporting frame is a cylindrical frame, the                supporting frame is sleeved outside the excitation                tube, the supporting frame is placed in the carbon                dioxide cracking tube, the electric heating wire is                wound on the outer circumference of the cylindrical                frame of the supporting frame, the three                heat-sensitive switches are connected in series in                the electric heating wire, the electric heating                wire passes through the charging head of the carbon                dioxide cracking tube and is connected with the                power supply line, the power supply line is                connected with the switchboard, the switchboard is                provided with a switchboard switch and an indicator                lamp connected with the power supply line. The                utility model can realize constant temperature                heating of the liquid carbon dioxide in the carbon                dioxide cracking tube before detonating, the liquid                carbon dioxide temperature is kept near the upper                limit of the liquid critical value, which can                reduce the dosage of the excitation tube chemical                material, saving a lot of cracking cost, and can                avoid the dumb gun caused by insufficient heat                supply, It improves the cracking quality of the                whole blasting area.

PN CN114550936-A
TI Pre-eclampsia regulation and analysis system based            on machine learning, has mask placed with carbon            dioxide sensor in carbon dioxide sensor placing groove,            and microprocessor that transmits established            prediction model to early warning analysis unit.
AU WANG L
   WANG Z
   YANG D
   CHENG X
   ZHOU L
AE HENAN PEOPLES HOSPITAL (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 202275448C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a mask placed with a carbon                dioxide sensor in a carbon dioxide sensor placing                groove (1). The glasses with image collecting                device are placed in the carbon dioxide sensor                placing groove. The image collecting device and n                vibration sensors are mounted on the limb, where n                is an integer greater than or equal to 4. The                carbon dioxide sensor is used for monitoring the                carbon dioxide signal in the breath of the user.                The image collecting device is used for collecting                the user pupil image information. The n vibration                sensors are used for collecting the user limb                vibration signals. The n vibration sensors transmit                the collected n vibration signals to the frequency                extracting unit. The frequency extracting unit                calculates the vibration frequency according to the                n pieces of vibration signals. The frequency                extracting unit transmits the vibration frequency                to a microprocessor. The microprocessor transmits                the established prediction model to the early                warning analysis unit.
   USE - Pre-eclampsia regulation and analysis system                based on machine learning.
   ADVANTAGE - The pre-warning analysis unit is provided                according to prediction model for risk judgment, so                that the preeclampsia early regulation analysis                system is enabled to test the user in real time                conveniently.

   Carbon dioxide sensor placing groove                (1)

AE TENUTO KK (TENU-Non-standard)
GA 2021E0450A

PT P
PN CN111363003-A
TI Extracting pearl protein with carbon dioxide supercritical fluid technology comprises adding pearl powder, transforming supercritical fluid, filtering, adding to Sephadex G75 gel filtration column, eluting and freeze-drying.
AU TANG C
   WANG T
   WEI D
   ZHAN G
   RUAN H
AE ZHEJIANG CHANGSHENGNIAO HEALTH TECHNOLOG (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202063641W
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting pearl protein with carbon dioxide supercritical fluid technology comprises (a) adding pearl powder into a supercritical extraction kettle equipped with stirring and heating devices, injecting deionized water, sealing the top cover, turning on the stirrer, delivering carbon dioxide gas, (b) slowly releasing the supercritical body of water and carbon dioxide with active substances in the extraction kettle, entering the separation kettle for separation, reducing the pressure and temperature, transforming supercritical fluid into a carbon dioxide gas layer, a water layer, and an insoluble water active substance precipitation layer, (c) filtering and collecting the water-insoluble precipitation layer in the separator, adding the water-soluble pearl protein solution in the separator to the Sephadex G75 gel filtration column, eluting with phosphate buffer to collect the eluent to obtain soluble pearl protein and (d) mixing and freeze-drying the two active substances.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting pearl protein with carbon dioxide supercritical fluid technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: will not change the original performance of pearl protein and other active substances in pearl powder and cause its denaturation; extracts no residual solvent; has high rate and is environmentally friendly without pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting pearl protein with carbon dioxide supercritical fluid technology comprises (a) adding pearl powder into a supercritical extraction kettle equipped with stirring and heating devices, injecting deionized water, sealing the top cover, turning on the stirrer, delivering carbon dioxide gas, (b) slowly releasing the supercritical body of water and carbon dioxide with active substances in the extraction kettle by adjusting the drain valve, entering the separation kettle for separation, slowly reducing the pressure and temperature of the separation kettle, transforming supercritical fluid into a carbon dioxide gas layer, a water layer, and an insoluble water active substance precipitation layer, releasing carbon dioxide gas, recycling carbon dioxide gas, (c) allowing to stand still, filtering and collecting the water-insoluble precipitation layer in the separator to obtain water-insoluble active protein, adding the water-soluble pearl protein solution in the separator to the Sephadex G75 gel filtration column, eluting with phosphate buffer to collect the eluent to obtain soluble pearl protein and (d) mixing and freeze-drying the two active substances.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size of pearl powder is greater than or equal to 1 mu m and the mass ratio of pearl powder to deionized water is 1:20. Preferred Method: The temperature of the extraction kettle is controlled at 38-42 degrees C, the pressure is 55-65MPa, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is adjusted to 25l/h, and the delivery time is 2 hours. The step (b) comprises adding a cooling device to the separation kettle to control the temperature of the separation kettle at 0 degrees C. The pressure in the separation kettle is controlled at 4-5.5Mpa. The pH of the phosphate buffer is 7.2 and the flow rate is 0.5 ml/min. The pearl protein solution is heated to 15-20 degrees C, filtering and adding the filtrate to a Sephadex G-75 gel filtration column for purification. The recycled carbon dioxide gas and fresh carbon dioxide pass through the heat exchanger, filtering, compressor or pump and heat exchanger and entering the extraction kettle for gas supply.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

PN US2018280998-A1; WO2018187513-A1; IN201947043789-A; KR2019133765-A; CN110740817-A; JP2020512934-W; EP3648898-A1; BR112019020910-A2; US10661287-B2; EP3648898-A4; CN110740817-B; JP6918200-B2; KR2302840-B1; EP3648898-B1
TI Apparatus for producing electrostatically charged and homogeneous carbon dioxide (CO2) composite spray surface, has charged and homogeneous carbon dioxide composite spray containing additive which is projected at substrate surface.
AU JACKSON D P
   JACKSON M A
AE JACKSON D P (JACK-Individual)
   CLEANLOGIX LLC (CLEA-Non-standard)
   JACKSON D P (JACK-Individual)
   JACKSON M A (JACK-Individual)
   CLEANLOGIX LLC (CLEA-Non-standard)
   JACKSON D P (JACK-Individual)
   JACKSON M A (JACK-Individual)
   CLEANLOGIX LLC (CLEA-Non-standard)
   HITACHI HIGH TECH CORP (HTTE-C)
   HITACHI HIGH-TECH CORP (HTTE-C)
   HITACHI HIGH TECH CORP (HTTE-C)
GA 201876761T
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has multiple nozzle electrodes positioned axis symmetrically about an additive injection nozzle. The electrodes comprises an elongated frame with a nozzle tip with a center through hole, and arising from the center through hole are three axisymmetric through ports. CO2 particles are flowed through the adjustable expansion tube assembly to create an electrostatic charge, which is shunted to the three landing guides to electrostatically charge the nozzle electrodes, and mix with air to form air-carbon dioxide aerosol. The charged nozzle electrodes and the air- carbon dioxide aerosol passively charge the air-additive aerosol. The carbon dioxide aerosol combines away from the nozzles to form the charged air-additive- carbon dioxide aerosol. The charged and homogeneous carbon dioxide composite spray containing an additive is projected at the substrate surface.
   USE - Apparatus for producing electrostatically charged and homogeneous carbon dioxide (CO2) composite spray surface containing additive for use on substrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The spray applicator frame can be contained in a three dimensional-printed applicator housing to provide a means for mounting or handling, and manipulating the spray applicator frame during operation, for example providing mounts for robot end-effectors or providing a handle for manual spray operations. The propellant gas flow channels are produced using electrical discharge machining (EDM) and provide a three-point cradle for centering and securing the carbon dioxide particle delivery tube surrounding which flows supersonic velocity propellant gas. The carbon dioxide injection pressure, propellant pressure, and propellant temperature can be decreased as needed to facilitate the formation and maintenance of a uniform pretreatment coating.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a nozzle electrode apparatus for producing an electrostatic field; and
   (2) a method for treating a surface using an apparatus for producing an electrostatically charged and homogeneous carbon dioxide composite spray containing an additive for use on a substrate surface.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the apparatus for producing electrostatically charged and homogeneous carbon dioxide (CO2) composite spray surface.

PT P
PN CN113663741-A
TI Regeneration method of coking catalyst involves contacting coking catalyst with mixed gas of oxygen and carbon dioxide in catalytic cracking unit, reacting to obtain regenerated flue gas, and passing regenerated flue gas to completely replace carbon dioxide in mixture gas.
AU YANG X
   ZHANG R
   ZHANG Y
   WU L
   YAN H
   SUN S
   MENG F
AE SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2021E1010U
AB    NOVELTY - Regeneration method of coking catalyst involves starting catalytic cracking unit, contacting the coking catalyst with a mixed gas containing oxygen and carbon dioxide in cracking unit, so that the coking catalyst is reacted in the presence of the mixed gas and generate regenerated flue gas with a high carbon dioxide content, returning the regenerated flue gas to completely replace the carbon dioxide in the mixed gas during stable operation stage of the catalytic cracking unit, and mixing regenerated flue gas with oxygen and using it as main air to promote the coking regeneration of coking catalyst.
   USE - Regeneration method of coking catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively control the carbon monoxide content in the regenerated flue gas, and prevent the occurrence of tail combustion caused by the high carbon monoxide concentration, thereby improving the safety of regenerator. Since the main air in generator does not contain nitrogen, reaction of nitrogen with oxygen at high temperature to generate nitrogen oxide pollution gas is prevented, and high-purity carbon dioxide is produced without requiring complex capture process, which improves recovery and utilization efficiency of carbon dioxide. Since the heat capacity of carbon dioxide is 60% more than that of nitrogen, the carbon dioxide replaces nitrogen to circulate in the regenerator, which can take away more heat and reduce the heat load of the regenerator heater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves mixing high-purity carbon dioxide and high-purity oxygen in a mixer to obtain mixed gas, then passing the mixed gas into a catalytic cracking regenerator and reacting using main air control system, so that the coke on the coked catalyst in the catalytic cracking regenerator is removed by combustion reaction under the action of the introduced oxygen and purifying the regenerated flue gas discharged from the catalytic cracking unit to produce industrial grade carbon dioxide. The main air control system is used to control the flow rate and molar ratio of the main air components in the catalytic cracking regenerator. The mixed gas is obtained by using air separation device to separate each component gas in the air to separate high-purity oxygen and other gases, introducing high-purity oxygen into combustion furnace, contacting with fuel oil to generate high-purity carbon dioxide, then mixing generated high-purity carbon dioxide and high-purity oxygen in mixer.Preferred Condition: The main air control system controls carbon dioxide content in the regenerated flue gas to be 95 vol.% or more. The temperature in the catalytic cracking regenerator is 600-800degreesC, preferably 650-750degreesC.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of high-purity carbon dioxide to high-purity oxygen in the mixer is 1-10, preferably 3-5.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC J04-E01; J04-E05
IP B01J-038/02; B01J-038/04; B01J-038/12

PN EP3858468-A1
TI Removal of carbon dioxide from vehicle exhaust gas involves pretreating vehicle exhaust to remove nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxide, carbon hydrates and/or particles, and absorbing carbon dioxide by passing portion of exhaust gas.
AU WUENSCH J R
   GEYER F L
   SCHERR G
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 202189545U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pretreating a vehicle exhaust gas to remove nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxide, carbon hydrates and particles. After pretreatment, carbon dioxide is partially absorbed from the exhaust gas by passing a portion of the exhaust gas through an absorption unit (10). The absorption unit has a liquid absorption medium (11) which includes absorption components capable of absorbing carbon dioxide by chemisorption or chemical reaction which is dissolved, dispersed or suspended in a carrier medium. The carrier medium is selected from water, alcohol, aromatic solvent, cyclic carbonate and polar organic solvent.
   USE - Method for removing carbon dioxide from a vehicle exhaust gas. Uses include but are not limited to sports utility vehicles (SUVs), buses, trucks, commercial vehicles, watercraft including boats and ships, aircraft, hybrid vehicles, electric vehicles, plug-in hybrid electric vehicles and hydrogen-powered vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to remove carbon dioxide from vehicle exhaust gas, which reduces the amount of carbon dioxide emitted by vehicles to limit the effects of global warming, and increases the fuel efficiency of the vehicle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an absorption unit used for removing carbon dioxide from a vehicle exhaust gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating an absorption unit used for removing carbon dioxide from a vehicle exhaust gas.
   Absorption unit (10)
   Liquid absorption medium (11)
   Tank (14)
   Pressure regulator (18)
   Valve (30)

PN CN109241625-A; CN109241625-B
TI Oil displacement technology based carbon dioxide            effect evaluation method, involves evaluating carbon            dioxide effect of target block when optimized degree            ratio of mother block is greater than quantization            parameter of mother block.
AU SONG Z
   ZHANG L
   HOU J
   LI Y
   LI M
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 2019130868
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting a mother block                according to oil displacement technology. Judgment                is made to check whether a water content in the                mother block reaches a first water drive recovery                preset value. Judgment is made to check whether                produce gas oil carbon dioxide drive ratio reaches                a second recovery ratio preset value. Relationship                between optimized degree ratio of the mother block                and a quantization parameter of the mother block is                determined. Carbon dioxide effect of a target block                is evaluated according to oil-displacing technology                when the optimized degree ratio of the mother block                is greater than a quantization parameter of the                mother block.
   USE - Oil displacement technology based carbon                dioxide effect evaluation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simulating an actual block                by using the oil-displacing technology under                geological modeling condition, and analyzing and                evaluating carbon dioxide effect of the target                block by using the oil displacement                technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) an oil displacement technology based                carbon dioxide effect evaluation device
   (2) a computer device
   (3) a computer-readable storage medium for                storing a set of instructions for performing an oil                displacement technology based carbon dioxide effect                evaluation method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                an oil displacement technology based carbon dioxide                effect evaluation method. '(Drawing includes                non-English language text)'

TI Catalytic composition used to convert carbon dioxide to flammable gases contains different elements defined by intersection of second to sixth period and first to sixteenth group, with exclusion of technetium, and matrix component.
AU FRECH NABOLD C
   BARONIAN M
   AVETISYAN KAJIKI A
   AVETISYAN K A
   FRECH N C
   ASHOT A
   CHRISTIAN F N
   MIKE B
AE AZAD PHARM INGREDIENTS AG (AZAD-Non-standard)
   AZAD PHARM INGREDIENTS AG (AZAD-Non-standard)

GA 2019050885
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytic composition comprises at least 7 different elements comprising the elements defined by the intersection of the second to the sixth period and the first to the sixteenth group, where technetium is excluded, and a matrix component.
   USE - The catalytic composition is useful for preparing synthesis gas and hydrocarbon compounds from a carbon dioxide-containing gas, preferably for conversion of carbon dioxide to flammable gases (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition provides conversion of carbon dioxide to flammable gases by more than 30%, preferably more than 95% (claimed), at lower temperatures as this decrease energy consumption required for the heating of the reaction mixture and the composition, where the conversion of carbon dioxide utilizes unpurified exhaust gas as carbon dioxide-containing gas, thus no tedious purification steps are necessary.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing synthesis gas and hydrocarbon compounds from a carbon dioxide-containing gas, comprising mixing the carbon dioxide-containing gas with water vapor and subsequently contacting the mixture with the catalytic composition; and
   (2) an apparatus for conducting the method, comprising an inlet pipe carbon dioxide-containing gas, a water inlet pipe, a heating mean for generating water vapor and heating a catalytic composition, a reaction vessel comprising the catalytic composition, and an outlet pipe for discharging the reaction products, where the reaction vessel is flow-connected with the inlet pipes and the outlet pipe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method is carried out: at a temperature below 250 degrees C, preferably a temperature of 130-150 degrees C; and at low pressure, preferably 0.1-2 bar. The heat energy of the carbon dioxide-containing gas is employed to carry out the catalytic reaction. Preferred Components: The matrix: is a porous matrix; and has a surface to weight ratio of at least 40 m2/g. The matrix component comprises natural aluminosilicates, synthetic aluminosilicates, zeolites, vermiculite, activated carbon, obsidian, titanium oxide and/or aluminum oxide. The elements comprise aluminum, carbon, cadmium, cerium, cobalt, chromium, copper, europium, iron, gadolinium, molybdenum, sodium, neodymium, nickel, oxygen or samarium. The carbon dioxide-containing gas is: exhaust gas of a combustion; or exhaust gas of a power plant, smelting plant or cement plant. The carbon dioxide-containing gas is unpurified exhaust gas. The reaction vessel is a single chamber reactor or plug flow reactor.
   EXAMPLE - Typical catalytic composition comprised of: 12 wt.% alumina; 8 wt.% chromium(III) oxide; 7 wt.% copper; 0.5 wt.% europium; 1.5 wt.% gadolinium; 10 wt.% molybdenum oxide (Mo3O7); 5 wt.% nickel; 5 wt.% nickel(II) oxide; 1 wt.% samarium; 25 wt.% obsidian; and 25 wt.% vermiculite.

PT P
PN EP3412356-A1; EP3412356-B1
TI Removal of carbon dioxide from gas stream involves contacting gas stream with preset steam partial-pressure, with surface of membrane permeable to carbon dioxide, and contacting another surface of membrane with solution comprising ammonia.
AU RODE S E
   VILLENEUVE K
   ROIZARD D G
   SAVARY D J L
   COQUEREL J
   CHAUDRON V M
AE SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV LORRAINE (UYLR-C)
GA 201898059Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing carbon dioxide from gas stream involves using a membrane permeable to carbon dioxide, having a surface (s1) and an opposing surface (s2), bringing a gas stream in contact with the surface (s1) of the membrane, and bringing the surface (s2) of the membrane in contact with an aqueous solution comprising ammonia so that portion(s) of carbon dioxide is removed from the gas stream and transferred through the membrane to the aqueous solution. The gas stream has a steam partial-pressure of at least 1200 Pa.
   USE - Removal of carbon dioxide from gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method sensitively improves rapid decrease in carbon dioxide flux through the membrane, efficiently removes carbon dioxide from gas stream, and sensitively delays the time of formation of ammonium crystals on the surface of the membrane on the side of the gas stream. It is possible to delay the pressure increase to feed a membrane contactor at a determined flow rate, in particular in which the membrane contactor presents lumen, such as hollow fiber lumen or lumen between two membrane plates, of small dimension. The method reduces regeneration time between two time slots of carbon dioxide capture operations. It is possible to increase the durability of the membrane before rupture by avoiding high-pressure constraints during operations between two capture cycles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The aqueous solution comprises 0.02-6 mol ammonia expressed as ammonium ion. Preferred Property: The gas stream has steam partial-pressure of at least 15%, preferably at least 98% of the saturated pressure of water at preset temperature (T1).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The membrane comprises a polymer chosen from polyene, polyalkene, polysulfone, polyethersulfone, and perfluoropolymer, preferably chosen from polyethylene, polypropylene, polymethylpentene, PTFE, and polyacrylonitrile. The membrane is a composite membrane made of microporous layer and a dense layer. Preferred Properties: The membrane is shaped as hollow fibers having an external diameter of 100-1000 mu m, preferably 150-500 mu m, and an internal diameter (or lumen diameter) of 50-800 mu m, preferably 100-300 mu m. The membrane has an overall mass transfer coefficient of carbon dioxide at 20 degrees C of 10-6-10-1 m/second, preferably 10-5-10-2 m/second. The dense layer has a thickness of 0.05-20 mu m, preferably 0.1-5 mu m. The membrane has several micropores having mean diameter of 0.05-5 mu m, preferably 0.1-0.5 mu m.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Fixing carbon dioxide by mixing calcium-containing            substance and calcium extractant to obtain gel            containing calcium-containing intermediate, supplying            basic substance and carbon dioxide to precipitate            calcium carbonate, and removing it.
AU INUI M
AE MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND CO LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
GA 202159831N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for fixing carbon dioxide involves                preparing calcium-containing substances containing                calcium, preparing a calcium extractant that                extracts calcium ions from the calcium-containing                substance by reaction with the calcium-containing                substance to produce a calcium-containing                intermediate, mixing the calcium-containing                substance and the calcium extractant to obtain a                gel containing the calcium-containing intermediate,                supplying a basic substance and carbon dioxide to                the gel containing the calcium-containing                intermediate to precipitate poorly soluble calcium                carbonate, and removing the precipitated calcium                carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for fixing carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method immobilizes carbon dioxide in large                quantities and semi-permanently, and is economical                and protects evironment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium extractant                  is at least one of a carboxylic acid or                  carboxylic acid salt having a linear or cyclic                  carbon chain, a protein containing a carboxyl                  group, and pectin. The calcium-containing                  substance is gypsum, wollastonite, or phosphate                  rock. Preferred Method: In the method, the                  calcium carbonate-removed residual substance is                  reused as the calcium extractant. The method                  further involves recovering carbon dioxide in the                  atmosphere, and supplying the carbon dioxide                  recovered from the atmosphere to a gel containing                  the calcium-containing intermediate.

PN CN112387285-A
TI Preparing nickel-based methane carbon dioxide reforming catalyst, comprises e.g. mixing cerium nitrate hexahydrate, nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate, glycine and deionized water, adding silica sol, then stirring and calcining obtained product.
AU WANG L
   ZHANG C
   LIU H
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG OCEAN (UYZO-C)
GA 2021211657
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel-based methane carbon dioxide reforming catalyst, comprises (i) mixing cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate, nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate and glycine, adding deionized water, and stirring uniformly to obtain nitrate solution, (ii) adding silica sol into nitrate solution and stirring uniformly to obtain mother liquor, (iii) heating and evaporating the mother liquor until the sample undergoes combustion reaction, and (iv) calcining the product obtained after combustion to obtain nickel-based methane carbon dioxide reforming catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nickel-based methane carbon dioxide reforming catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The nickel-based methane carbon dioxide reforming catalyst has excellent methane carbon dioxide reforming activity, stability and carbon deposition resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (ii), the silica sol contains 30-35 wt.% silicon dioxide. In step (ii), the mother liquor includes 0.8-3 wt.% nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate, 16-21 wt.% cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate, 4-6 wt.% glycine, 55-70 wt.% deionized water and 9-18 wt.% silica sol. The mother liquor includes 5.3 wt.% glycine and 14.6 wt.% silica sol. The pH of the silica sol in step (ii) is less than 7. Preferred Method: In step (iii): the heating temperature is 220-240 degrees C. In step (iv), the calcination temperature is 550-650 degrees C, and the calcination time is 3-5 hours.
