PN CN112316912-A
TI Material for adsorbing carbon dioxide and volatile organic compound gas, comprises modified diatomaceous earth, mesoporous silica, zeolite, modified graphene oxide, chitosan, bentonite, metal organic framework material.
AU ZENG W
   LI G
   MA N
AE GUANGZHOU TECHWAY BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021151344
AB    NOVELTY - Material comprises 30-50 pts. wt. modified diatomaceous earth, 20-40 pts. wt. mesoporous silica, 15-25 pts. wt. zeolite, 10-20 pts. wt. modified graphene oxide, 6-15 pts. wt. chitosan, 4-10 pts. wt. bentonite, 4-8 pts. wt. metal organic framework material and 1-5 pts. wt. sodium lignosulfonate.
   USE - Material used for adsorbing carbon dioxide and volatile organic compound gas and to the solvent storage bottle cover.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has wide application prospect, absorbs the organic volatile solvent or carbon dioxide, prevents volatilization and leakage of organic solvent and solvent absorption carbon dioxide deterioration, increases the adsorption effect of carbon dioxide, volatile organic compound gas, metal organic frame work material and increases the adsorption effect of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The material comprises 44 pts. wt. modified diatomaceous earth, 32 pts. wt. mesoporous silica, 20 pts. wt. zeolite, 16 pts. wt. modified graphene oxide, 10 pts. wt. chitosan, 7 pts. wt. bentonite, 6 pts. wt. metal organic framework material and 3 pts. wt. sodium lignosulfonate. Preferred Components: The coupling agent is kh550. The metal organic framework material is Mg-MOF-74. Preferred Conditions: The modified diatomaceous earth is prepared by dipping the diatomaceous earth in a hydrochloric acid solution, stirring for the first time at a temperature of 40-50 degrees C, filtering with suction, drying and calcining at 500-600 degrees C for 4-10 hours to obtain pretreated diatomaceous earth, where the weight of the hydrochloric acid solution is 6-10 times that of diatomaceous earth, adding pretreated diatomaceous earth and 2,4-dinitrophenylhydrazine to 5-15 wt.% ferric nitrate solution, stirring for the second time, drying and calcining at 500-600 degrees C for 6-12 hours to obtain modified silicon algae earth, where the ratio of the pretreated diatomaceous earth, 2,4-dinitrophenylhydrazine, and ferric nitrate solution is 1:0.1-0.3:6-10. The concentration of the hydrochloric acid is 4-6 mole/L. The first stirring rate is 200-300 rpm, the stirring time is 30-40 minutes, the second stirring rate is 100-200 rpm, stirring time is 20-40 minutes. The modified graphene oxide is prepared by dispersing 6-10 pts. wt. graphene oxide in 80-100 pts. wt. deionized water to form a graphene suspension, adding 10-14 pts. wt. tetradecyltrimethylammonium bromide, 2-5 pts. wt. glycidyl methacrylate, 2-6 pts. wt. activated carbon, 0.8-1.5 pts. wt. coupling agent, reacting in a water bath for 4-7 hours, pumping, filtering and drying to constant weight to obtain product. The temperature of the water bath is 70-80 degrees C.

PN CN111925286-A
TI Autocatalytically synthesizing formic acid using            solvent comprises adopting use of carbon dioxide as            carbon source and phenylsilane as hydrogen source,            without catalyst subjecting carbon dioxide and            phenylsilane for hydrogenation reaction.
AU YANG S
   WU H
   LI H
   XUE W
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2020B55046
AB    NOVELTY - Autocatalytically synthesizing formic acid                using solvent (I) comprises adopting use of carbon                dioxide as the carbon source and phenylsilane as                the hydrogen source, without a catalyst, subjecting                carbon dioxide and phenylsilane for hydrogenation                reaction in solvent to obtain formic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for autocatalytically                synthesizing formic acid using solvent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is fast; has high selectivity; and                achieves yield of 78-91%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Autocatalytic synthesizing formic acid using                solvent (cabonyl-substituted amine compounds of                formula (CH3)2-N-C(=O)-R) (I)) comprises adopting                use of carbon dioxide as the carbon source and                phenylsilane as the hydrogen source, without a                catalyst, subjecting carbon dioxide and                phenylsilane for hydrogenation reaction in solvent                to obtain formic acid.
   R = not defined.
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of formic                  acid is carried out in a pressurized reactor, the                  reaction system is a closed system, before the                  reaction, the pressure of carbon dioxide is 1-4                  MPa, the reaction temperature is 30-80 degrees C                  and the reaction time is 60-480 minutes. The                  hydrolysis reaction of the silicone formate                  intermediates includes reacting carbon dioxide                  with hydrogen-containing silane to obtain silicon                  formate intermediates, adding hydrochloric acid                  solution, reacting for 30 minutes in an oil bath                  at 30 degrees C to obtain formic acid. Preferred                  Components: The ratio of the amount of the                  phenylsilane substance in mmol to the volume ml                  of solvent is 0.25-1.25:2. The solvent is                  N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, or                  N-methylpyrrolidone or methyl                  5-(dimethylamino)-2-methyl-5-oxoglutarate.

PN DE102020110103-A1; US2020325026-A1; KR2020119515-A; US11142461-B2
TI Recycle of carbon dioxide for producing hydrocarbon compound, involves reacting reaction gas including carbon dioxide, and carbon raw material to produce precursor comprising carbon monoxide and converting precursor to hydrocarbon compound.
AU KOO M S
   SOO K M
AE SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
   SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
GA 2020A0134E
AB    NOVELTY - Recycle of carbon dioxide involves injecting a reaction gas (60) including carbon dioxide, and a carbon raw material (50) into a rotary kiln (100), reacting the reaction gas and carbon raw material in the rotary kiln to produce a hydrocarbon precursor comprising carbon monoxide, and converting the hydrocarbon precursor to a hydrocarbon compound.
   USE - Recycle of carbon dioxide for producing hydrocarbon compound e.g. alcohol compound, ether compound, and saturated hydrocarbon compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recycles carbon dioxide with excellent conversion rate and high efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the rotary kiln.
   Carbon raw material (50)
   Reaction gas (60)
   Rotary kiln (100)
   Front portion (110)
   Rear portion (150)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: During injection of the reaction gas and carbon raw material, the carbon raw material is injected into a front portion (110) in a longitudinal direction of the rotary kiln and the reaction gas is injected into a rear portion (150) in a longitudinal direction of the rotary kiln, or the reaction gas and carbon raw material are injected together into the front portion in longitudinal direction of the rotary kiln. The hydrocarbon precursor is preferably produced by rotating the rotary kiln about an axis in a longitudinal direction at a speed of 0.5-30 rpm while the carbon raw material and reaction gas are moved in the longitudinal direction of the rotary kiln. Recycle of carbon dioxide further involves removing nitrogen and water vapor from the hydrocarbon precursor before converting the hydrocarbon precursor to hydrocarbon compound, and reacting carbon monoxide in the hydrocarbon precursor with hydrogen in presence of a metal catalyst comprising iron oxide to obtain hydrocarbon compound. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of injection rate of the carbon raw material to injection rate of the reaction gas is 2-5. The resident time of the reaction gas in the rotary kiln is 10-60 minutes. The reaction gas and carbon raw material are reacted at 900-1200 degrees C and a pressure of 0.1-10 bar. Preferred Property: The ratio of inner length and inner diameter of the rotary kiln is 5-30. Preferred Components: The carbon raw material comprises coke, carbon black or graphite, and is generated in a thermal unit.

PN CN109569659-A; CN109569659-B
TI New photocatalytic auxiliary agent useful for reducing carbon dioxide, prepared by adding cobalt complex as a precursor and calcining cobalt sulfide and introducing copper by ion exchange.
AU DING Z
   XIONG Z
   ZHANG Z
   DAI W
   SU B
   HUANG L
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2019336161
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic auxiliary agent i.e. copper-cobalt complex are new.
   USE - (I) is useful for reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has more active sites for the adsorption of carbon dioxide; increase the activity of the catalytic auxiliary agent in carbon dioxide reduction; and prolongs the residence time of light in the catalytic auxiliary agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Photocatalytic auxiliary agent i.e. copper-cobalt complex of formula (CuxCo(1-x)S2) (I) are new.
   x = 1-0.05.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) use method of (I) in reducing carbon dioxide comprising combining bipyridyl hydrazine as a photocatalyst with photocatalytic auxiliary agent, then reducing photocatalytic carbon dioxide to form carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises adding cobalt complex (Co-ZIF-67) as a precursor to obtain cobalt complex (CoSy) by a solvothermal method, calcining obtained cobalt sulfide and introducing copper by ion exchange to obtain (I). Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) dissolving Co-ZIF-67 and thioacetamide in ethanol and carrying out solvothermal reaction to obtain CoSy, (ii) calcining obtained CoSy under nitrogen atmosphere to obtain cobalt sulfide, and (iii) dissolving obtained cobalt sulfide and copper nitrate trihydrate in water, ion exchanging by hydrothermal reaction to obtain (I). The method comprises dissolving dimethylimidazole and cobalt nitrate hexahydrate in methanol in a molar ratio 1:1, allowing to stand for 24 hours, centrifuging and drying to obtain (I). In step (i), temperature of the solvothermal reaction is 100-200 degrees C, and reaction time is 4 hours. In step (ii), calcination temperature is 300-550 degrees C, and calcination time is 2 hours. In step (iii), hydrothermal reaction temperature is 30-100 degrees C, and reaction time is 30 minutes. Preferred Components: In step (i), mass ratio between Co-ZIF-67 and thioacetamide is 1:1-1:5. In step (iii), mass ratio between cobalt sulfide and copper nitrate trihydrate is 1:20.

The separation device is connected to the converter in such a way that carbon dioxide (3,11) separated from the raw synthesis gas is returned and used in the thermochemical conversion of the natural gas feed. The processing device is connected to the separation device via a membrane module (Z) comprising the membrane, while the natural gas can be used as a purge gas.

PN CN107964426-A
TI Production of carbon dioxide zero-emission fossil fuel hydrogen by gasification reacting fossil fuel and steam, recycling heat, three-phase separating, dust-removing, desulfurizing, carbon converting, low-temperature washing and absorbing.
AU ZHU W
   MENG L
   WANG Q
   CHAI S
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 201834452K
AB    NOVELTY - Production of carbon dioxide zero-emission fossil fuel hydrogen comprises gasifying fossil fuel and steam to obtain mixed high-temperature fuel gas; feeding recycling heat from mixed high-temperature fuel gas, carrying out three-phase separation and gasification reaction; dust-removing, primary desulfurizing and secondary desulfurizing to obtain primary purified gas; feeding to carbon conversion and low-temperature methanol washing to obtain high purity hydrogen and carbon dioxide gas; and absorbing carbon dioxide by ammonia to obtain triazine alcohol and recycling heat from excess hot gas.
   USE - Method for producing carbon dioxide zero-emission fossil fuel hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces high-purity hydrogen gas which contains almost no impurities and no carbon dioxide discharge and provides heat energy and materials which can be organically used. The method uses low energy consumption, has simple operation and is low cost and suitable for large scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of carbon dioxide zero-emission fossil fuel hydrogen comprises gasifying fossil fuel and steam at 1000-1700 degrees C under 2-10 MPa to obtain mixed high-temperature fuel gas containing hydrogen gas, carbon oxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, water vapor and unreacted gas fossil fuel; feeding mixed high-temperature fuel gas to waste heat recycling device for recycling heat, subjecting recovered high-temperature fuel gas heat to three-phase separation, separating to obtain completely unreacted fossil fuel gas, steam and H2, carbon oxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and water vapor and carrying out gasification reaction; subjecting crude mixed gas to dust-removing, primary desulfurizing and secondary desulfurizing to remove all sulfides to obtain primary purified gas; feeding primarily purified gas for carbon conversion and low-temperature methanol washing to obtain high purity hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas; and absorbing obtained carbon dioxide by ammonia to prepare triazine alcohol product and passing excess hot gas to recycling heat exchanger for recycling heat.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is iron-based high-temperature shift catalyst, copper-based low-temperature shift catalyst or cobalt-molybdenum-based sulfur-tolerant wide temperature shift catalyst. The catalyst is used for preparing triazine alcohol product is ammonium sulfate or ammonium chloride, in amount of 1-5 wt.% of total mass of carbon dioxide and ammonia.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio fossil fuel and water vapor is 1:2-4, preferably 1:3. The molar ratio of water to gas after preliminary purification is 1:1-1:5, preferably 1:1.4. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide to ammonia in triazine product is 0.5:1-1.2:1. Preferred Method: The gasification reaction is carried out by entrained flow gasification method at 1200-1700 degrees C under 2-5 MPa, preferably at 1450-600 degrees C under 4.0 MPa. The dust removal and purification is bag type dust removal or dust removal through venturi tube and fiber demister. The primary desulfurization uses calcium oxide-based wet desulfurization, and secondary desulfurization uses ammonia-based dry desulfurization. The last step comprises converting carbon into gas after preliminary purification and reacting with water in presence of catalyst to obtain hydrogen and carbon dioxide. The carbon conversion is carried out at 150-900 degrees C under 2.5-8 MPa, preferably at 300-600 degrees C under 2.5-6.5 MPa. The preparation of triazine alcohol product is carried out with or without catalyst at 200-300 degrees C under 0-30 MPa.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide stream (13) by:                mixing an adsorbent bed outlet stream with a stream                comprising oxygen to produce a combustor feed                stream; and feeding the feed stream to a combustion                reactor (101), where the contaminants are oxidized                to produce combustor product stream. The outlet                stream of the adsorbents beds has a concentration                of sulfur dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, and carbon                sulfide of less than 20 parts per billion (ppb),                and amine and ammonia concentrations of less than                100 ppb. The contaminants comprise hydrocarbons,                oxygenated hydrocarbon, and/or sulfur                dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide stream for use in the production of                renewable fuels and chemicals, where the carbon                dioxide is used in petroleum refining (all claimed)                and power production, steel manufacture, cement                manufacture and ethanol production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides economical removal of                contaminants from industrial carbon dioxide                sources. It provides synergistic benefit of having                excess high purity oxygen available from the                electrolysis of water using renewable power. The                hydrogen from electrolysis can be combined with                carbon dioxide (e.g. generated from carbon dioxide                in a reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reaction) to                yield fuels and chemicals, while the dioxide can be                used to remove contaminants from the carbon dioxide                feedstock. Serendipitously, using dioxide from                electrolytic can avoid introducing nitrogen into                the system (i.e. if the dioxide were derived from                air) and/or avoids the cost and energy input                required to enrich dioxide from air.

PN CN114459236-A
TI Energy-saving cement kiln flue gas carbon capture            method involves performing one-stage pressure swing            adsorption method to capture carbon dioxide in exhaust            gas of cement kiln, and performing cryogenic            rectification to obtain liquid carbon dioxide.
AU QIN X
   PU Y
   DING Y
   WU Y
   YIN W
   JIN X
   ZHAO M
   CHEN J
   LI K
AE SOUTHWEST CHEM IND RES & DESIGIN INST (CNCC-C)
GA 2022734918
AB    NOVELTY - Energy-saving cement kiln flue gas carbon                capture method involves performing one-stage                pressure swing adsorption (PSA) method to capture                65-75% carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas of cement                kiln, and performing cryogenic rectification to                obtain liquid carbon dioxide. The purity of carbon                dioxide is 97.5% or more. 80-93% Oxygen-enriched                gas produced by a vacuum pressure swing adsorption                (VPSA) device is used as combustion accelerant for                cement rotary kiln fuel to replace air, and the                high-concentration carbon dioxide flue gas                generated by the combustion of a portion of the                cement rotary kiln fuel is circulated into the                combustion chamber to control the furnace                temperature. The carbon dioxide concentration in                the flue gas emitted by the cement kiln is                increased from the 20-30% to 65-75%.
   USE - Energy-saving cement kiln flue gas carbon                capture method. The carbon dioxide captured from                cement kiln flue gas is used in oil field                flooding.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes 80-93% of oxygen-enriched                as combustion improver of cement rotary kiln fuel                to replace the traditional air                combustion-supporting; utilizes a part of                high-concentration carbon dioxide flue gas                generated by combustion of the rotary kiln fuel                that is circulated and returned to the combustion                chamber to control the furnace temperature, thus                improving the concentration of carbon dioxide in                the flue gases discharged from the cement kiln, and                reducing the amount of smoke to 60% (wet basis),                and hence reduces the comprehensive energy                consumption, and provides strong technical support                for the cement industry low-cost carbon capture                technology, and has good technical economic                efficiency. The carbon capture cost per ton of                liquid carbon dioxide is reduced by 30-40% when                compared with two-stage PSA method and MEA                method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of a                boosting pre-processing unit. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: 80-93% Oxygen-enriched                  air produced by the VPSA process is used as the                  combustion accelerant of coal fuel in cement kiln                  to replace air, such that nitrogen in the flue                  gas is reduced, and the concentration of carbon                  dioxide in the flue gas is reduced from 25-30% to                  65-75%.

TI Catalyst composition used for producing ethylene from ethane and carbon dioxide, comprises having substituted metal oxides.
AU GOYAL A
   SAMAD J
AE SOUTHERN RES INST (SOUR-C)
GA 2020854902
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst composition comprises having substituted metal oxides (I).
   USE - The composition is useful for producing ethylene (claimed) from ethane and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: drives an ethane oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) reaction that yields ethylene by using carbon dioxide as a soft oxidant; and is more stable as compared to conventional catalysts used in ODH reactions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst composition comprises having substituted metal oxides of formula (M1M2M3M4Q-X) (I).
   M1 = chromium oxide;
   M2 = iron oxide;
   M3 = cerium oxide;
   M4 = zirconia;
   Q = tin oxide and/or nickel oxide; and
   X = sulfate or phosphate.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing ethylene, comprising (a) reacting ethane with a gas comprising carbon dioxide in the presence of the composition, thus producing ethylene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition comprises: 0.1-30 wt.%, preferably 5-15 wt.% M1; 0.1-30 wt.%, preferably 0.5-5 wt.% M2; 0.1-10 wt.%, preferably 0.5-8 wt.% M3; quantity of M4 is the balance wt.% of the catalyst composition based on the amounts of M1, M2, M3, Q, and X; 0.2-20 wt.%, preferably 0.2-10 wt.% Q, where tin oxide comprises 0.1-10 wt.%, preferably 0.1-5 wt.% and nickel oxide comprises 0.1-10 wt.%, preferably 0.1-5 wt.%, based on the total weight of the catalyst composition; 0.05-10 wt.%, preferably 0.1-5 wt.% X; 0.2-20 wt.% tin oxide; and 0.2-20 wt.% nickel oxide. The composition has: a surface area of 10-200 m2/g, preferably 90-160 m2/g; and a pore size of 3-40 nm, preferably 7-15 nm. Preferred Components: (a) M1 comprises 2.5-15 wt.%, (b) M2 comprises 0.5-10 wt.%, (c) M3 comprises 0.5-8 wt.%, (d) M4 comprises balance wt.% of the composition based on the quantities of Ml, M2, M3, Q, and X, (e) Q comprises 0.1-10 wt.%, where tin oxide comprises 0.1-5 wt.% and nickel oxide comprises 0.1-5 wt.%, and (f) X comprises 0.1-8 wt.%. The chromium oxide comprises chromium(III) oxide, iron oxide comprises ferric oxide, nickel oxide comprises nickel(II) oxide, tin oxide comprises tin(II) oxide, and cerium oxide comprises cerium(IV) oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The gas is a flue gas comprising less than 90 vol.%, preferably less than 30 vol.% carbon dioxide. The gas is coal fired flue gas. The gas comprises at least 90 vol.% carbon dioxide, preferably at least 95 vol.% carbon dioxide. The molar ratio between the carbon dioxide and the ethane is 1:1-30:1, preferably 1.5:1-5:1. The method has an ethylene selectivity of at least 80% and a carbon dioxide conversion of at least 13% at CC to ethane molar ratio of 1.5:1 when the gas is a coal fired flue gas. The method has an ethylene selectivity of at least 85% and a carbon dioxide conversion of at least 10% at a CC to ethane molar ratio of 3:1 when the gas comprises 90 vol.% carbon dioxide. The yield of ethylene is at least 20% after at least 4 hours of performing the method at a carbon dioxide to ethane molar ratio of 1.5:1 when the gas is a coal fired flue gas. The yield of ethylene is at least 15% after at least 2 hours of performing the method at a carbon dioxide to ethane ratio of 3:1 when the gas comprises 90 vol.% carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The second mixture is                  compression molded to obtain iron coke balls. The                  method further involves spraying hot asphalt                  binder of 5-7 wt.% of first mixture and mixing                  uniformly to obtain second mixture.

PN PL421599-A1
TI Method for manufacturing wood-plastic composite product, involves introducing composite into mold and blowing in mold by introducing carbon dioxide gas, followed by granulating, and injecting carbon dioxide.
AU BUDKIEWICZ E
AE PACYGA PACYGA IMPORT-EXPORT KRZYSZTOF (PACY-Non-standard)
GA 201894937A
AB    NOVELTY - A wood-plastic composite product manufacturing method involves introducing a composite into the mold, blowing in the mold by introducing carbon dioxide gas under pressure. The blown composite is subjected to granulation to prepare granulate. The prepared granulate is injected into the mold using a screw-injector into the mold cavity, followed by injecting carbon dioxide under a pressure of 20-25 bar through nozzles installed in the mold filling system, where the composite comprises polyolefins, 10-250 mass% wood dust, 10-35 mass% compatibilizer and 0.3-1.2 mass% antioxidant.
   USE - Method for manufacturing wood-plastic composite product.

The sodium container is provided with a sodium liquid inlet (14) connected with the containing cavity and a sodium liquid outlet. A first heat exchange pipeline is connected with the sodium liquid inlet. A second heat exchange pipeline is used for setting in a heat pipe reactor (1). A third heat exchange pipeline is connected with the sodium liquid outlet. A second heat discharge pipeline is used for connecting a third heat exchange pipeline with a carbon dioxide inflow pipeline (11) of the heat pipe reactor when opening. A heat exchanger (10) is set on a first heat exchange pipeline for cooling and exchanging heat of the sodium liquid in the first heat exchange pipeline.

The heat exchanger is set on the first heat exchange pipeline for cooling and exchanging heat of the liquid sodium in the carbon dioxide flowing pipeline, thus preventing solidification of liquid sodium. The device is provided with a first heat discharging pipeline and a second heat discharge pipeline, so that two heat discharge pipelines can be connected with a carbon dioxide circulation structure of the heat pipe reactor when opening under the accident condition, thus quickly switching carbon dioxide fluid normally operating, and quickly switching the fluid to the waste fluid.

PN US2019177180-A1; JP2019098307-A; CN109896534-A; GB2571601-A; US10730761-B2; JP6949693-B2; GB2571601-B
TI Treating aqueous solution containing urea, ammonia and carbon dioxide, in which aqueous solution contains urea, ammonia and carbon dioxide, that is hydrolyzed, and ammonia and carbon dioxide in aqueous solution are separated and recovered.
AU SASAKI K
AE TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
   TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
   TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
GA 201950837W
AB    NOVELTY - Treating aqueous solution containing urea, ammonia and carbon dioxide, in which aqueous solution contains urea, ammonia and carbon dioxide, that is hydrolyzed, and ammonia and carbon dioxide in the aqueous solution are separated and recovered. The treatment method involves introducing aqueous solution containing urea, ammonia and carbon dioxide into a first stripper and subjecting to stripping, for separating and recovering ammonia and carbon dioxide in the aqueous solution. The aqueous solution after stripping is introduced into a urea hydrolyzer. The aqueous solution is introduced into the urea hydrolyzer, then heated under pressure, for hydrolyzing urea in the aqueous solution, and the aqueous solution after hydrolysis is introduced into a second stripper for stripping and separating ammonia and carbon dioxide in the aqueous solution.
   USE - Method for treating aqueous solution containing urea, ammonia and carbon dioxide for producing granular urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat aqueous solution containing urea, ammonia and carbon dioxide, which allows to improve the treatment equipment of an aqueous solution containing urea, ammonia and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A portion of the aqueous solution is stripped in the first stripper and/or before hydrolyzing in the urea hydrolyzer and is introduced into an exhaust gas treatment equipment, equipped with a urea dust scrubbing equipment and an equipment to suppress emission of ammonia. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for improving treatment equipment of an aqueous solution containing urea, ammonia and carbon dioxide, to lower the equipment load of the treatment equipment, involves adding a pipe to the existing treatment equipment in which the pipe is for introducing a portion of the aqueous solution before stripping in the first stripper, and/or, a portion of the aqueous solution after stripped in the first stripper but before hydrolyzed in the urea hydrolyzer into an exhaust gas treatment equipment equipped with a urea dust scrubbing equipment and an equipment to suppress emission of ammonia; and
   (2) method for producing granular urea, involves introducing a gas containing urea dust and ammonia generated from a urea granulation equipment into an exhaust gas treatment equipment equipped with a urea dust scrubbing equipment and an equipment to suppress emission of ammonia while producing granular urea by the urea granulation equipment, in which an aqueous solution containing urea, ammonia and carbon dioxide is introduced into a first stripper and subjected to stripping, for separating and recovering ammonia and carbon dioxide in the aqueous solution, and the aqueous solution after stripping is introduced into a urea hydrolyzer, in which the aqueous solution introduced into the urea hydrolyzer is heated under pressure, for hydrolyzing urea in the aqueous solution, and the aqueous solution after hydrolysis is introduced into a second stripper, in which the aqueous solution introduced into the second stripper is subjected to stripping, for separating ammonia and carbon dioxide in the aqueous solution, and a portion of the aqueous solution before stripped in the first stripper, and/or, a portion of the aqueous solution is stripped in the first stripper but before hydrolyzed in the urea hydrolyzer is introduced into an exhaust gas treatment equipment equipped with a urea dust scrubbing equipment and an equipment to suppress emission of ammonia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The equipment is used to suppress emission of ammonia is an equipment to suppress emission of ammonia by adding an acid to an aqueous solution circulating in the urea dust scrubbing equipment. The equipment allows to suppress emission of ammonia is an equipment to suppress emission of ammonia by adding an acid to an aqueous solution circulating in the other equipment than the urea dust scrubbing equipment. A portion of the aqueous solution is stripped in the first stripper but before hydrolyzed in the urea hydrolyzer is cooled before introducing into the exhaust gas treatment equipment. Preferred Components: The acid is selected from sulfuric acid, nitric acid, phosphoric acid or organic acids.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   LI Y
AE LANZHOU CHEM & PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CLCP-C)
GA 202206893V

The                reversible ionic liquid is prepared by carrying out                reversible reaction of                2,3,4,6,7,8,9,10-octahydropyrimido(1,2-a)azepine of                formula (I) with alcohol containing compound of                formula (ROH) in presence of carbon dioxide to                obtain                2,3,4,6,7,8,9,10-octahydropyrimido(1,2-a)azepine                compound of formula (II) and liberates carbon                dioxide (ROCO2-).

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reversible ionic liquid is claimed. The                reversible ionic liquid is prepared by carrying out                reversible reaction of                2,3,4,6,7,8,9,10-octahydropyrimido(1,2-a)azepine of                formula (I) with alcohol containing compound of                formula (ROH) in presence of carbon dioxide to                obtain                2,3,4,6,7,8,9,10-octahydropyrimido(1,2-a)azepine                compound of formula (II) and liberates carbon                dioxide (ROCO2-).
   ROH = cyano-containing monoalcohol,                cyano-containing diol, cyano-containing triol,                cyano-containing benzyl alcohol or cyano-containing                polyol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  taking 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene as                  cation part, and generating anions using carbon                  dioxide under the action of                  1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene and cyano                  group-containing ROH, and achieves the                  reversibility of ionic liquids and the capture                  and analysis of carbon dioxide through                  temperature control. The method comprises mixing                  1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene and ROH in a                  molar ratio of 1-4:1, and carrying out an                  absorption reaction with pure carbon dioxide gas                  or mixed carbon dioxide gas to obtain saturated                  ionic liquid, then reacting saturated ionic                  liquid by desorption of carbon dioxide to                  generate 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene and                  ROH. The temperature when the carbon dioxide is                  absorbed to generate the ionic liquid is                  0-40degrees Celsius, and the absorption time is                  0.25-1 hours. The temperature when the ionic                  liquid desorbs carbon dioxide to generate                  1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene and ROH is                  80-120degrees Celsius, and the desorption time is                  0.5-1 hours.Preferred Components: The volume ratio of                  carbon dioxide in the mixed carbon dioxide gas is                  10-30%.

TI Producing hot carbonated water for e.g. warming body, by mixing hot water supplied from water heater with carbon dioxide in mixing unit, where pipe line after adding carbon dioxide to hot water has bending portion at specific angle.
AU OOTA Y
AE VITA INC (VITA-Non-standard)
GA 2019A3722G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing hot carbonated water involves mixing hot water supplied from a water heater with carbon dioxide in a mixing unit to produce high-concentration hot carbonated water, where the pipe line after adding carbon dioxide to hot water has at least one portion that bends at 90 degrees or an acute angle.
   USE - The method is used for generating hot carbonated water, which is useful for promoting blood circulation and warming body.
   ADVANTAGE - The method generates extremely high-concentration artificial carbonated spring, which produces therapeutic effect equivalent to or higher than natural carbonated spring without substantially discharging undissolved carbon dioxide, and increases the dissolved concentration of carbon dioxide in hot water. The method efficiently dissolves carbon dioxide in hot water without using complicated structure, and utilizes small and inexpensive high-concentration carbonate mixing unit which has multiple corners in a small space like the human intestine, has a structure that allows sufficient contact time between carbon dioxide and hot water, and is not only used as a carbonated shower, but also a carbonated dissolving portion of a circulation-type carbonated spring unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bent portion has a cross-sectional area changing portion, where a swirling flow is generated at the bent portion. Preferred Method: The mixing unit takes a time of at least 2 seconds from the addition of carbon dioxide to hot water until the water is discharged from the outlet of the unit.
   ACTIVITY - Vasotropic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

   USE - Used as cosolvent of carbon dioxide thickener.
   ADVANTAGE - The cosolvent: improves polymer thickening agent solubility; and increases the viscosity of the thickener and thickening effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The single hydrocarbon is a carbon 6-10 n-alkane or an iso-alkane. The mixed hydrocarbon is naphtha, mineral spirits, kerosene, and/or diesel. The non-polar low hydrocarbon solvent is n-hexane, n-heptane, n-octane or isooctane. The polar solvent is a low molecular weight ketone, furan, lower alcohol, fatty acid alkyl ester or alkyl alcohol ether. The low molecular weight ketone is acetone or cyclohexanone. The lower alcohols are carbon 1-8 carbon containing alcohols. The furan is tetrahydrofuran. The lower alcohols are methanol, ethanol, propanol, isopropanol, butanol, pentanol, hexanol, heptanol, and/or octanol. The fatty acid alkyl ester is isopropyl myristate, isopropyl laurate, methyl oleate, fatty acid methyl ester derived from vegetable oil or biodiesel. The alkyl alcohol ethers are derivatives of linear diols having 1-4 ethylene oxide and/or propylene oxide units. The mass ratio of the cosolvent to the carbon dioxide thickener is 1-3:1.

AU SHARMA K P
   KUMAR R
   BHATTACHARJEE A
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY BOMBAY (IITB-C)
GA 202287117H
AB    NOVELTY - Hybrid porous liquid comprises polymer grafted hollow silicon dioxide nanorods and bioconjugated carbonic anhydrase.
   USE - The hybrid porous liquid is useful for storing carbon dioxide for a longer period of time, and trap and convert carbon dioxide to calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The hybrid porous liquid enables the sequestration and storage of carbon dioxide, utilizes bioconjugated carbonic anhydrase, which catalytically converts released carbon dioxide to carbonic acid, which reacts with pre-added calcium chloride to form industrially relevant calcium carbonate, and reduces carbon dioxide level by storing and sequestering.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) storing carbon dioxide for a longer period of time, comprising providing hybrid porous liquid comprising hollow silicon dioxide nanorods and the bioconjugated carbonic anhydrase; and
   (2) prepairing hybrid porous liquid, comprising preparing polymer conjugated hollow silicon dioxide nanorods, mixing the polymer conjugated hollow silicon dioxide nanorods and carbonic anhydrase enzyme in buffer by stirring for 12 hours, where the buffer includes phosphate buffer and tris buffer, lyophilizing the above mixture at -55 degrees C for 12 hours and heating to 60 degrees C to obtain hybrid porous liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hollow silicon dioxide nanorods and the bioconjugated carbonic anhydrase are present in a ratio of 1:50. The polymers includes polyethylene glycol and amine-based polymers e.g. polyethylene imine, polypropylene imine, polydiallyl amine ammonium chloride. The porous liquid is mesoporous and having the particle size of 2-50 nm. The hybrid porous liquid is freeze below its glass transition temperature (-58 degrees C). The hybrid porous liquid converts carbon dioxide into industrially applicable products. Preferred Method: The hybrid porous liquid converts carbon dioxide into calcium carbonate at 50 degrees C. The polymer conjugated hollow silicon dioxide nanorods and carbonic anhydrase enzymes are mixed by maintaining the nanorods to enzyme ratio at 1:50.

TI Method for recovering carbon dioxide-enhanced coal gas layer in deep low-permeability coal seam involves drilling vertical well, determining injection wells, excavating horizontal wells, injecting nitrogen and carbon dioxide to recover gas.
AU ZHU Y
   LIU J
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 202154095R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recovering carbon dioxide-enhanced coal gas layer in deep low-permeability coal seam involves drilling a vertical well to the depth above the target coal bed and determining injection wells in the vertical well, monitoring the number and position of gas production wells, excavating horizontal wells in parallel to connect vertical wells, carrying out hydraulic fracturing technology in the horizontal well section to pump water to reduce pressure, monitoring the gas pressure, composition and partial pressure of each gas in the coal seam, injecting nitrogen into injection well and recovering gas in the recovery well until the partial pressure of water vapor in produced gas is almost zero, injecting carbon dioxide into injection well, sealing the hole and resuming recovery after 3-5 days, increasing the injection of carbon dioxide until carbon dioxide can no longer be injected when the partial pressure of gas is almost zero.
   USE - Method for recovering carbon dioxide-enhanced coal gas layer in deep low-permeability coal seam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing phase matching exploitation strategy of nitrogen and carbon dioxide which has improved recovery rate in an effective manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of the adsorbed carbon involves adding beta -amylase into starch aqueous solution in the mass ratio 1 :10-12 to obtain coke, alternately washing the coke using 60-70 wt.% ethanol and distilled water to obtain initial carbon material, mixing the initial carbon material with microcrystalline cellulose, magnesium carbonate under the protection of nitrogen to obtain adsorption carbon. The mass ratio of starch to water is 1 :50-80. The mass ratio of microcrystalline cellulose and magnesium carbonate is 1: 0.3 to 0.5: 0.6-0.8.

PN CN111441748-A; CN111441748-B
TI Method for predicting scaling tendency of carbon dioxide gas flooding oil well, involves preparing water sample from carbon dioxide gas flooding oil well, detecting e.g. ion concentration, and calculating saturation index value.
AU WANG Q
   PAN Z
   LIU J
   MENG X
   ZHANG B
   ZENG N
   YI J
   QIN M
   WU Y
   HU C
   WANG H
   ZHANG H
   YI F
   ZHONG B
   DENG C
   TUO W
   WANG F
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 202071688L
AB    NOVELTY - A water sample is prepared from carbon dioxide gas flooding oil well, an initial pH value of the water sample and an initial ion concentration of each ion in the water sample are detected, and saturation index (SI) value is calculated based on Davis-Stiff saturation index theory. When the saturation index (SI) value is less than 0, set different bicarbonate concentration value, obtain scaling critical concentration value of carbonate corresponding to the scaling tendency of the carbon dioxide gas flooding oil well through an iterative algorithm, and establish relationship between the bicarbonate concentration and carbonate critical concentration. During the production process of carbon dioxide gas flooding oil well, the carbonate concentration and bicarbonate concentration of the water sample is monitored based on the relationship between the bicarbonate concentration and carbonate critical concentration, and the scaling tendency of carbon dioxide gas flooding oil well is determined.
   USE - Method for predicting scaling tendency of carbon dioxide gas flooding oil well.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents and treats calcium carbonate scale in unscaled wellbore, and accurately predicts scaling tendency of the carbon dioxide gas flooding oil well and gas well.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The water sample obtained from the carbon dioxide gas flooding oil well is a water sample obtained at an oil-gas-water separator. The ions including potassium, sodium, barium, calcium, magnesium, strontium, carbonate, bicarbonate, chloride and sulfate, in the water sample are detected.

PN CN111004378-A
TI Synthesizing carbon dioxide-based polymer shielding material in UV shielding, by reacting epoxide, allyl glycidyl ether and Salen catalyst, purifying, adding to SH-cinnamate, adding dichloromethane, reacting, purifying and vacuum-drying.
AU SONG P
   MA F
   GUO R
   MA J
   KOU W
   YANG Z
AE UNIV NORTHWEST NORMAL (UNWN-C)
GA 202032298T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing carbon dioxide-based polymer shielding material, involving (i) taking epoxide, allyl glycidyl ether and Salen catalyst in molar ratio 1:(0.8-1):(500-20000), reacting at a pressure of 2-5 MPa carbon dioxide gas at 30-80 degrees C for 18-48 hours, purifying with dichloromethane and ethanol, and drying in vacuum to obtain a carbon dioxide-based polymer, and (ii) taking carbon dioxide-based polymer and SH-cinnamate in a molar ratio of 1:(0.5-4), putting in Shrek tubes, adding dichloromethane, reacting at 30-60 degrees C for 0.5-6 hours, dissolving the reaction system in dichloromethane, purifying by dialysis, and vacuum-drying to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing carbon dioxide-based polymer shielding material in UV shielding (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The shielding material has excellent light transmittance, stretchability and film-forming property, excellent reversibility, is reusable. The method is mild, uses of the greenhouse gas carbon dioxide, proposes solutions to reduce environmental pressure, and uses cheap and easily available various raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a use of the carbon dioxide-based polymer shielding material in UV shielding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the epoxide comprises propylene oxide, cyclohexene or styrene oxide. Preferred Conditions: The shielding material is reversible in UV shielding.

TI Method for preparing gas-liquid mixture of carbon dioxide and water, involves mixing carbon dioxide in secondary mixing chamber composed of tangential circle and liquid, and outputting mixed liquid as final product from liquid outlet.
AU LOU Z
   MA Y
   ZHANG L
   ZHANG R
   CEN Y
AE FANWU TECHNOLOGY SHANGHAI CO LTD (FANW-Non-standard)
GA 201940019R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves supplying tap water to the water supply pipe through the water inlet. The carbon dioxide gas dispersed in the disperser in one mixing chamber is once mixed with the pressurized tap water, where primary mixing is a hedging mixing of water and gas. The carbon dioxide in the secondary mixing chamber composed of the tangential circle and the liquid are mixed once after mixing, where secondary mixing is a hedging mixing of the carbon dioxide gas with the liquid after one mixing. The mixed liquid after the second mixing is outputted as a final product of the gas-liquid mixture from the liquid outlet.
   USE - Method for preparing gas-liquid mixture of carbon dioxide and water.
   ADVANTAGE - The sufficient mixing of gas and liquid to prepare a higher concentration of carbonated spring is achieved, thus achieving high efficiency and good therapeutic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for preparing gas-liquid mixture of carbon dioxide and water.

PN US2018099248-A1; US10293304-B2
TI Process for recovery of carbon dioxide from flue gases, involves charging co-current absorption column with aqueous solution of salt and amine, and cycling retentate back to column input when permeates are heated to strip out carbon dioxide.
AU STAUFFER J E
AE STAUFFER J E (STAU-Individual)
GA 201827704U
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves charging a gravity fed co-current absorption column (10) with an aqueous solution of salt and amine to absorb carbon dioxide (12) from flue gases applied co-currently to the input of the column and fed by gravity through the column. The column is followed by the osmotic filters from which the retentate is cycled back to the column input while the permeates are heated to 50 degrees C to 120 degrees C to strip out the carbon dioxide, in which the column operates at a temperature between about 15 degrees C and 50 degrees C.
   USE - Process for recovery of carbon dioxide from flue gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide can be efficiently removed from the gas stream and the ease with which the solvent can be filtered and regenerated. The co-current flow system can minimize the loss of ammonia.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for recovering carbon dioxide from flue gases.
   Co-current absorption column (10)
   Vent air (11)
   Carbon dioxide (12)
   Osmotic filters (18, 20, 22)
   Osmotic filter membranes (40a-40c)

   NOVOZYMES AS (NOVO-C)
GA 201807405M
AB    NOVELTY - Polypeptide comprises 80% sequence identity to                the mature polypeptide containing certain                nucleotide sequences of (SEQ ID NO: 2), not given                in the specification, or a fragment having carbonic                anhydrase activity.
   USE - Polypeptide for absorption of carbon dioxide                by media, and separating carbon dioxide and                compositions (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polypeptide has reduced carbon dioxide                content in syngas production, and heat-stable                carbonic anhydrase.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a method for polynucleotide comprises                encoding polypeptide; and
   (2) constructing nucleic acid or expression                vector involves polynucleotide, which is operably                linked to one or more control sequences that direct                the production of the polypeptide in an expression                host; and
   (3) recombinant host cell comprising                polynucleotide, which is operably linked to one or                more control sequences that direct the production                of the polypeptide; and
   (4) producing a polypeptide having carbonic                anhydrase activity involves cultivating the host                cell, and subjecting under certain conditions                conducive for production of the polypeptide;                and
   (5) whole broth formulation or cell culture                composition comprises polypeptide; and
   (6) performing extraction of carbon dioxide                from a carbon dioxide-containing medium involves                contacting the carbon dioxide-containing medium                with the polypeptide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The polypeptide having                  at least 85%, at least 90%, at least 91 %, at                  least 92%, at least 93%, at least 94%, at least                  95%, at least 96%, at least 97%, at least 98%, at                  least 99% or 100% sequence identity to the mature                  polypeptide containing certain nucleotide                  sequences of (SEQ ID NO: 2), not given in the                  specification. The mature polypeptide containing                  1-226 amino acids sequences of (SEQ ID NO: 2),                  not given in the specification.                  (ftp://ftp.wipo.int/pub/published_pct_sequences/publication).                  The recovering of polypeptide is carried out by                  producing a polypeptide. The carbon                  dioxide-containing medium is a                  bicarbonate-containing liquid and the carbon                  dioxide extraction is the conversion of                  bicarbonate to carbon dioxide. Preferred                  Components: The composition further comprises                  polypeptide, where composition is a liquid, a                  solid or a sition, and which further comprises an                  excipient. The polypeptide is immobilized with a                  matrix suitable for immobilization, preferably a                  matrix selected from the group consisting of                  polymers, gels, capsules, beads, fabrics, fibers,                  hollow fibers, membranes, particulates, porous                  surfaces, rods or tubes. The carbon                  dioxide-containing medium is a gas or a                  multiphase mixture such as a flue gas, a raw                  natural gas, a syngas, a biogas, a respiration                  gas, an atmospheric gas, or a gas or multiphase                  mixture emitted from combustion. The carbon                  dioxide-containing medium further comprises a                  carbonate-based compound or an amine-based                  compound such as sodium carbonate, potassium                  carbonate, an amino acid, a modified amino acid,                  Tris, or methyl diethanolamine (MDEA). The                  aqueous carbon dioxide absorption solution                  comprises polypeptide, and a carbonate-based                  compound or an amine-based compound.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2018004070-A1; KR2018001860-A
TI Apparatus for manufacturing molten iron, has carbon dioxide removal device that is connected to reduction and blast furnaces, removes carbon dioxide from exhaust gas of reduction furnace, and blows carbon dioxide into blast furnace.
AU SON S H
   KIM W H
   JANG D
   JANG D S
AE POSCO (POSC-C)
GA 201801172N
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a reduction furnace (22) that is connected to a melt gasifier furnace (10), and manufactures the reduced iron from an iron ore by using a reducing gas to be discharged from melt gasifier furnace. The reduction furnace provides the reduced iron to the melt gasifier furnace. The iron ore and coke are charged into a blast furnace (50), so as to manufacture the molten iron. A carbon dioxide removal device (60) is connected to the reduction and blast furnaces, removes the carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas of reduction furnace, and blows the carbon dioxide into blast furnace.
   USE - Apparatus for manufacturing molten iron.
   ADVANTAGE - The exhaust gas generated from the molten iron manufacturing process is efficiently utilized, thus reducing the carbon dioxide generation rate from the coal usage amount. The energy efficiency is increased within the blast furnace.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for manufacturing molten iron.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the apparatus for manufacturing molten iron. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Melt gasifier furnace (10)
   Reduction furnace (22)
   Winnowers (30,55)
   Blast furnace (50)
   Carbon dioxide removal device (60)

PN DE102021101532-A1; WO2022106347-A1; EP4031623-A1
TI Production of elastomeric material e.g. activated            masterbatch, involves inserting basic mixture            containing carbon black, heating, inserting basic            mixture containing silicate, removing basic mixture,            rolling, stripping, inserting ready-made mixture            containing polyglycol, and cooling.
AU PEINE A
   FRASCA D
   SCHULZE M
AE KOEPP GMBH & CO KG W (KOEP-Non-standard)
   KOEPP GMBH & CO KG W (KOEP-Non-standard)
GA 202272719P
AB    NOVELTY - Production of elastomeric material involves                preheating an internal mixer with a closed mixing                chamber to a preheating temperature, inserting the                mixture components of a basic mixture comprising                carbon dioxide-PECUs, carbon black, polyglycol and                amide wax, mixing and heating the mixture                components of the masterbatch, inserting the                mixture components of the basic mixture comprising                silicate and substituted amides, mixing and cooling                the base mixture, removing the basic mixture cooled                to the cooling temperature, rolling the taken base                mixture and cooling with coolable, stripping the                base mix skins formed on the coolable rollers,                cooling the base mix skins, preheating the internal                mixer or another internal mixer with a closed                mixing chamber, inserting the mixture components of                a ready-made mixture comprising e.g. base compound                skins, polyglycol, wetting agents, amide wax and                silane adhesion promoter with functionalized                surface as filler and process oil, and cooling the                finished mixture.
   USE - Production of elastomeric material e.g.                activated masterbatch with improved processability                (claimed) used in foamed and non-foamed                applications for producing elastomer moldings,                elastomer mats and elastomer conveyor belts.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces elastomeric material with                improved processability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of elastomeric material with                improved processability using a carbon                dioxide-containing, non-crosslinked                non-thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer                (CO2-PECUs) as the raw polymer involves preheating                an internal mixer with a closed mixing chamber to a                preheating temperature in a temperature window of                50-100 degrees C, inserting the following mixture                components of a basic mixture comprising 100 phr                carbon dioxide-PECUs as raw polymer, 20-60 phr                carbon black as coloring agent, 1-10 phr polyglycol                as processing aid, 1-10 phr amide wax as processing                aid and chain extenders from the group of diamines                with two matching amino groups as crosslinking                agents into the mixing chamber, which is preheated                to the preheating temperature, mixing and heating                the mixture components of the masterbatch to a                temperature above the melting temperature of the                carbon dioxide-PECUs, inserting the following                additional mixture components of the basic mixture                comprising 1-10 phr silicate as filler and 0.5-5                phr substituted amides as processing aids into the                mixture components that is preheated to a                temperature above the melting temperature of the                carbon dioxide-PECUs, performing continue mixing                and cooling the base mixture to a cooling                temperature in a temperature window of 100-120                degrees C, removing the basic mixture cooled to the                cooling temperature, rolling the taken base mixture                and further cooling the base mixture with coolable                rollers of a rolling mill to a temperature of 60-80                degrees C, stripping the base mix skins formed on                the coolable rollers during cooling, subsequently                cooling the base mix skins to a temperature of less                than 40 degrees C, preheating the internal mixer or                another internal mixer with a closed mixing chamber                to a preheating temperature within a temperature                window of 50-100 degrees C, inserting the following                mixture components of a ready-made mixture                comprising 124-200 phr base compound skins, 1-10                phr polyglycol as processing aid, 1-7 phr wetting                agents, namely calcium salts of fatty acids and/or                amide esters as processing aids, 1-7 phr amide wax                as processing aid, 1-5 phr silane adhesion promoter                as crosslinking agent, 0.5-5 phr chain extenders                from the group of diamines with two matching amino                groups as crosslinking agent, 1-10 phr hydrolysis                stabilizer as anti-aging agent, 60-150 phr silicate                as filler, 5-25 phr silicate with functionalized                surface as filler and 10-60 phr process oil as a                plasticizer into the mixing chamber, which is                preheated to the preheating temperature, mixing and                heating the mixture components of the non-activated                ready-mix to a temperature within a temperature                window of 90-110 degrees C, activating the                non-activated ready-mix by mixing in at least one                0.5-10 phr peroxidic crosslinking agent and/or                sulfur-based vulcanization system, and cooling the                finished mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The activation of the                  non-activated ready-mix and the subsequent                  cooling of the ready-mix involves (a) inserting                  at least one peroxidic crosslinking agent and/or                  the sulfur-based vulcanization systems as further                  mixture components without interrupting the                  mixing process into the mixture components heated                  to a temperature in the temperature window of                  90-110 degrees C in the mixing chamber of the                  internal mixer, (b) mixing up to a maximum                  temperature of 115 degrees C in order to disperse                  the other ingredients of the mixture, (c)                  removing the ready-mixed ready-mix from the                  mixing chamber without a cooling phase, (d)                  rolling the removed ready mix and cooling the                  ready mix with coolable rolls of a rolling mill                  to a temperature of 60-80 degrees C, (e)                  stripping the ready-mix skins formed on the                  coolable rollers during cooling, (f) cooling the                  ready-mix skins to a temperature of less than 40                  degrees C, (g) preheating the internal mixer or                  another internal mixer with a closed mixing                  chamber to a preheating temperature within a                  temperature window of 50-100 degrees C, (h)                  inserting the non-activated ready-mix and the at                  least one peroxidic crosslinking agent and/or the                  sulfur-based vulcanization systems as further                  mixture components into the mixing chamber                  preheated to the preheating temperature, (i)                  mixing up to a maximum temperature of 115 degrees                  C in order to disperse the other ingredients of                  the mixture, (j) removing the ready-mixed                  ready-mix from the mixing chamber without a                  cooling phase, (k) rolling the removed ready mix                  and cooling the ready mix with coolable rolls of                  a rolling mill to a temperature of 60-80 degrees                  C, (l) stripping the ready-mix skins formed on                  the coolable rollers during cooling, (m) cooling                  the ready-mix skins to a temperature of less than                  40 degrees C, (n) preheating the rolls of a                  rolling mill to a temperature of more than 60                  degrees C, (o) feeding the non-activated ready                  mix and the at least one peroxidic crosslinking                  agent and/or the sulfur-based vulcanization                  systems as further mix components onto the rolls                  of the rolling mill, which is preheated to the                  preheating temperature, (p) mixing the other                  components of the mixture placed on the preheated                  rollers up to a maximum temperature of 80 degrees                  C in order to distribute the other components of                  the mixture, (q) stripping the ready-mix skins                  forming on the rollers, and (r) cooling the                  ready-mix skins to a temperature less than 40                  degrees C. The additional components of the                  ready-mixed mixture comprising 1-33 phr foaming                  agent, 0.5-5 phr urea and 0.5-5 phr zinc oxide                  are added to the mixing chamber, which is                  preheated to the preheated temperature. The                  cooled ready-mix skins are processed into a foam                  in a foaming process. Preferred Components: The                  chain extender is a diamine with two matching                  amino groups and a ring structure. The chain                  extender is                  4,4'-methylenebis(3-chloro-2,6-diethylaniline).                  The process parameters for the production of the                  elastomer material are not changed in several                  discontinuously produced batches. The basic                  mixture skins are comminuted and the comminuted                  basic mixture skins are used as a mixing                  component of the finished mixture in the                  preheated mixing chamber.

PT P
PN CN114369080-A; CN216910238-U
TI Synthesizing ethylene carbonate or propylene            carbonate, comprises mixing ethylene oxide or propylene            oxide with carbon dioxide, reacting under catalyst,            subjecting crude product to removal of light            components, heavy components and purifying.
AU ZHANG M
   WANG P
   GUO W
   MAO J
AE CARY ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CARY-Non-standard)
GA 2022598729
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing ethylene carbonate or propylene                carbonate, comprises mixing ethylene oxide or                propylene oxide with carbon dioxide, reacting under                the catalysis of a catalyst to generate ethylene                carbonate or propylene carbonate crude product,                subjecting the crude product of ethylene carbonate                or propylene carbonate successively to removal of                light components, removing heavy components and                purifying, and obtaining electronic grade products                of ethylene carbonate or propylene carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing ethylene                carbonate or propylene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not decompose metal ions, does                not react chemically with raw materials in the                reaction system e.g. carbon dioxide or ethylene                oxide/propylene oxide, and is efficient,                environmentally friendly, safe and reliable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                device for synthesizing ethylene carbonate or                propylene carbonate, comprising a material mixer                (1), a shell and tube reactor (2), a flash tank                (3), a light-removing tower (5), a weight-removing                tower (6) connected in sequence, and refining tower                (7). The material mixer is provided with feed port                1-1, feed port 1-2 and discharge port 1. The feed                port I-I is connected with a device capable of                providing carbon dioxide.; The feed port I2 is                connected to a device capable of supplying ethylene                oxide (EO) or propylene oxide (PO). Discharge port                1 is connected with shell and tube reactor. The                shell-and-tube reactor is provided with feed port                II and discharge port II. The feed port II is                connected with the discharge port I of the material                mixer, and the discharge port II is connected with                the flash tank. The shell and tube reactor, the                whole is a vertical tubular structure, the cross                section of the tubular structure is hexagonal, and                the shell and tube reactor is evenly arranged with                vertically distributed shell tubes of flash tank,                tower top is provided with CO Discharge port, tower                wall one side middle and lower part is provided                with feed port III, the other side middle part is                provided with discharge port III. The carbon                dioxide discharge port is connected with the                compressor, and the outlet of the compressor is                connected with the feed port 1-1 of the material                mixer. The feed port III is connected with the                discharge port II of the tubular reactor; the                discharge port III is connected with the light                stripping tower. The described light-removing tower                is provided with a feed port IV in the middle, a                light component outlet at the top, and a discharge                port IV at the bottom. Feed port IV is connected                with the discharge port III of the flash tank, and                the light component outlet is connected with the                tail gas absorption system, and the discharge port                IV is connected with the weight-removing tower. The                weight-removing tower is provided with a feed port                V in the middle, and a discharge port V at the top.                The bottom is provided with a heavy component                outlet. The feed port V is connected with the                discharge port IV of the light-removing tower. The                heavy component outlet is connected with the heavy                component recovery system, and the discharge port V                is connected with the refining tower. The refining                tower is provided with a feed port VI in the                middle, a tail gas discharge port is provided at                the top, and a discharge port VI is provided at the                bottom. The feed port VI is connected with the                discharge port V of the weight-removing tower, and                the tail gas discharge port is connected with the                tail gas absorption system, Outlet VI is connected                to a device for recovering ethylene carbonate (EC)                or propylene carbonate (PC).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an integral structure                schematic diagram of the device for synthesizing                ethylene carbonate or propylene carbonate.
   2Tube reactor
   3Flash tank
   4Compressor
   5Light removing tower
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically                  comprises (1) feeding carbon dioxide into the                  material mixer (1) from the feeding port 1-1,                  feeding ethylene oxide or propylene oxide to the                  material mixer from the feeding port 1-2, and                  mixing, and feeding the material port II into the                  tubular reactor (2), and reacting under the                  catalytic action of the catalyst in it,                  discharging the product from the discharge port                  II and feeding to the flash tank (3) from the                  feed port III, (2) feeding the product into flash                  tank by feed port III and flashes, and top                  obtains carbon dioxide, bottom obtains ethylene                  carbonate or propylene carbonate reactant,                  discharging carbon dioxide from the carbon                  dioxide discharge port, compressing by the                  compressor (4), and returning to the material                  mixer for recycling, discharging ethylene                  carbonate or propylene carbonate reactant from                  outlet III and enters into the light-removing                  tower from inlet IV, (3) feeding Ethylene                  carbonate or propylene carbonate reactant into                  light-removing tower to carry out light-removing                  reaction from feed port IV and through liquid                  distributor uniform distribution, and the tower                  top obtains light components, and the tower                  bottom obtains ethylene carbonate or propylene                  carbonate crude product, discharging the light                  components into the tail gas absorption system                  from the light component outlet, and the crude                  ethylene carbonate or propylene carbonate from                  the discharge port IV at the bottom of the tower                  and feeding to the weight-removing tower from the                  feed port V, (4) feeding ethylene carbonate or                  propylene carbonate crude product into the                  weight-removing tower to carry out                  weight-removing reaction by feeding port V and                  after being evenly distributed through the liquid                  distributor, and the tower top obtains ethylene                  carbonate or propylene carbonate primary product,                  the tower bottom obtains the heavy component,                  discharging the heavy components from the heavy                  component outlet to the heavy component recovery                  system, and ethylene carbonate or propylene                  carbonate primary product from the discharge port                  V at the top of the tower and enters the refining                  tower from the feed port VI, and (5) feeding the                  primary product of ethylene carbonate or                  propylene carbonate to the refining tower for                  refining after uniform distribution through the                  feed port VI and the liquid distributor at the                  bottom of the tower, ethylene carbonate or                  propylene carbonate electronic grade products are                  obtained, discharging the tail gas obtained at                  the top of the tower into the tail gas recovery                  system through the tail gas discharge port, and                  discharging ethylene carbonate or propylene                  carbonate electronic grade products from outlet                  VI for recovery. The catalyst is prepared by (A)                  mixing polymerized monomer, cross-linking agent,                  siloxane, benzoyl peroxide and No. 80                  microcrystalline wax evenly, adding 1% polyvinyl                  alcohol solution, and performing polymerization                  reaction at 40-105&#8451; and normal pressure                  for 4-5 hours to obtain macroporous white balls,                  controlling the cross-linking degree of                  macroporous white balls to 5-10%, extracting and                  removing No.80 microcrystalline wax, and drying,                  (B) performing chloromethylation reaction with                  1-100 pts. wt. zinc chloride and 1-400 pts. wt.                  chloromethyl ether from the macroporous white                  spheres at 30-55&#8451; and normal pressure,                  and the reacting for 15-20 hours to generate                  benzyl balls, controlling the chlorine content in                  benzyl balls to be greater than 17%, (C) adding                  1-100 pts. wt. dimethylformamide and 1-50 pts.                  wt. aminating agent to the benzyl spheres, and                  the pH of the regulation system is 12-14, and                  heating to 40-60&#8451;, performing amination                  reaction under normal pressure for 12-15hours,                  and washing the solid product with deionized                  water until neutral to obtain amine balls, and                  (D) mixing 1-100 pts. wt. N-ethylimidazole, 1-30                  pts. wt. polyepichlorohydrin, and 1-100 pts. wt.                  absolute ethanol, and heating to                  30-90&#8451;, reacting under normal pressure                  for 10-15 hours, adding the amine balls into the                  obtained system after the reaction, and                  supplementing the pressure to 1MPa with nitrogen,                  heating to 110-160&#8451; and the reaction                  was carried out at this temperature for 1-30                  hours, cooling to 70&#8451;, washing with                  absolute ethanol until no white color is obtained                  to obtain a resin catalyst for synthesizing                  ethylene carbonate or propylene carbonate. In                  step (1), in the shell-and-tube reactor, the                  reaction temperature is room                  temperature-200&#8451;, and the pressure is                  normal pressure-10MPa; In step (2), the flash                  evaporation conditions of the flash tank are:                  room temperature-100&#8451;, normal                  pressure-0.5MPa, and the reflux ratio is 0.1-10.                  In step (3), in the delighting reaction, the                  reaction conditions are: the temperature is room                  temperature 200&#8451;, and the pressure is                  normal pressure-10MPa; In step (4), deweighting                  reaction, reaction conditions are: temperature is                  room temperature-200&#8451;, and pressure is                  normal pressure 10MPa; In step (5), the refining                  conditions are: the temperature is room                  temperature 200&#8451;, and the pressure is                  normal pressure-10MPa.Preferred Components: In step (1), the molar                  ratio of described ethylene oxide or propylene                  oxide and carbon dioxide is 1:1-10; and after the                  ethylene oxide (EO) or propylene oxide (PO) is                  mixed with carbon dioxide, the volume space                  velocity of the mixture entering the shell and                  tube reactor is 1000-10000/h.
