PT P
PN CN111960451-A
TI Producing soda ash, alumina and blended cement by using red mud comprises e.g. diluting untreated red mud with water, washing, passing carbon dioxide gas, mixing, grinding, evaporating and concentrating.
AU TAO Y
AE TAO Y (TAOY-Individual)
GA 2020B8033A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing soda ash, alumina and blended cement by using red mud comprises e.g. diluting untreated red mud with water in a mixing dilution unit to obtain thin red mud, then adding into a a-level washing unit for washing, then adding the dilute red mud into a carbonization unit A, passing the gas whose reaction component i.e. carbon dioxide, adding the reacted thin red mud into a b-level washing unit for washing, then adding the dilute red mud into a filter unit, mixing and grinding the low-sodium red mud with lime to obtain blended cement, during the process collecting the red mud washed water in the b-level washing unit and adding into a a-level washing unit, collecting the red mud washed water in a a-level washing unit, adding the collected washing water into the m-level evaporation concentration unit, evaporating and concentrating, then adding into the carbonization unit B, and adding the carbonization unit B with a gas whose reaction component i.e. carbon dioxide for reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for producing soda ash, alumina and blended cement by using red mud.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing soda ash, alumina and blended cement by using red mud comprises diluting untreated red mud with water in a mixing dilution unit (1.1.1) to obtain thin red mud, then adding into a a-level washing unit (1.1.2) for washing, where a is greater than or equal to 3, then adding the dilute red mud into a carbonization unit A (1.1.3), passing the gas whose reaction component i.e. carbon dioxide into a carbonization unit A (1.1.3) for reaction, adding the reacted thin red mud into a b-level washing unit (1.1.4) for washing, where b must meet a+b is greater than or equal to 6, and then adding the dilute red mud into a filter unit (1.1.5), obtaining low-sodium red mud after filtration, and mixing and grinding the low-sodium red mud with lime to obtain blended cement, during the process collecting the red mud washed water in the b-level washing unit (1.1.4) and adding into a a-level washing unit (1.1.2) to wash the red mud, during the process collecting the red mud washed water in a a-level washing unit (1.1.2), sending out in two ways, one way to the mixing and dilution unit (1.1.1), sending the other into the m-level evaporation concentration unit (1.2.1), where m satisfies m greater than or equal to 4, adding the collected washing water from the a-level washing unit (1.1.2) into the m-level evaporation concentration unit (1.2.1), evaporating and concentrating, and then adding into the carbonization unit B (1.2.2), adding the carbonization unit B (1.2.2) with a gas whose reaction component i.e. carbon dioxide for reaction, making the concentrate appear to precipitate, adding the material from the carbonization unit B (1.2.2) into the filter washing unit (1.3.1), filtering and washing to obtain solid phase, filtrate, and washing liquid, passing the solid phase through the drying unit B (1.3.2) and the calcination unit (1.3.3) in sequence to obtain alumina, and adding the filtrate into the first stage evaporation concentration in the n-stage evaporation concentration unit (1.2.3), where n satisfies m+n greater than or equal to 9, carrying out nth stage of evaporation and concentration, where the concentration of soda ash is greater than 25%, and then continues adding into the evaporation drying unit (1.2.4), and further evaporating and drying to obtain dry soda ash.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the a-level washing unit (1.1.2) and b-level washing unit (1.1.4), the washing water that has washed the thin red mud in each level of washing is sending to the upper level of washing, the washing water used as the upper level washing to wash the thin red mud, where the washing water that has washed the thin red mud in the first level washing of the b-level washing unit is sending to the a-level washing of the a-level washing unit. The m-level evaporation concentration unit and n-level evaporation concentration unit use steam as a heat source. The steam condensate recovery unit is also provided, and the core device is a steam condensate tank, the steam enters from the first stage of the m-stage evaporation concentration unit (1.2.1), heating m-level evaporation and concentration unit (1.2.1), and the n-stage evaporation concentration unit (1.2.3) collects the steam condensate generated during the process and directs the condensed water to the steam condensate tank. The liquid phase separated by the filtration unit (1.1.5) is sending to the b-level washing unit (1.1.4), which is used as the b-level washing water to wash the thin red mud. The steam condensate tank leads out a pipeline to lead the steam condensate to the b-level washing unit (1.1.4) as the washing water for the b-level washing. In the washing process of the filtering washing unit (1.3.1), the washing liquid produced after washing is directed to the m-th stage evaporation concentration in the m-stage evaporation concentration unit (1.2.1), and then enters the carbonization unit B (1.2. 2). The red mud from the filtration unit (1.1.5) first enters the granulation unit (1.1.6) for granulation, and then enters the preliminary drying unit (1.1.7), dry the free water in the red mud, and then enter the high-temperature drying unit (1.1.8), the crystal water in the red mud is baked off, and then the dried red mud is sending to the red mud crushing unit (1.1.9) to be crushed to obtain fine red mud, then send the limestone to the mixed grinding unit (2.1.3); send the limestone to the limestone calcination unit (2.1.1) for calcination to obtain lime, subsequently, the lime is sending to the lime crushing unit (2.1.2) for crushing, then send the crushed lime to the mixing and grinding unit (2.1.3), and in the mixing and grinding unit (2.1.3), the fine red mud and lime are fully mixed and ground, and finally mixed cement is obtained. The heat source of the preliminary drying unit (1.1.7) is the steam from the n-stage evaporation concentration unit (1.2.3), and the steam passes through the preliminary drying unit (1.1.7), and a heat exchange with the red mud from the granulation unit (1.1.6), it is sending out as waste steam. The limestone calcination unit (2.1.1) burns limestone into lime on the one hand, and produces high-temperature exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide on the other hand, part of the high temperature exhaust gas goes to the high temperature drying unit (1.1.8), as a heat source to bake off the crystal water in the red mud, another part of the high temperature exhaust gas goes to the waste heat boiler unit (2.1.4), process water is passed into the waste heat boiler unit to heat the process water into steam, and merges with the high-temperature exhaust gas from the high-temperature drying unit (1.1.8) as a gas whose reaction component is carbon dioxide, decarbonization unit A (1.1.3), and carbonization unit B (1.2.2) after the high temperature exhaust gas exits the waste heat boiler unit (2.1.4).

TI Device for treating wastewater, includes lime storage tank, carbon dioxide storage tank and pH detection box, each connected to sewage neutralization tank, pH detection sensor, controller, display module, and stirring motor.
AU JIANG H
AE JIANGSU JINPAI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION ENG CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202079570T
AB    NOVELTY - A wastewater treatment device includes a sewage neutralization tank (2) connected to top middle position of substrate (1) and including stirring motor (7), a lime water storage tank (17) connected to top left side portion of substrate and connected to sewage neutralization tank through water conveying pipe (23), a carbon dioxide (CO2) storage tank (3) connected to top right side of substrate and connected to sewage neutralization tank through gas conveying pipe (4), a pH detection box (8) connected to top left upper corner of substrate, a cover plate (11) provided on top of pH detection box, a pH detection sensor (10) inserted into middle portion of cover plate, a controller (19) connected to top left lower corner of substrate, and a display module (18) embedded in upper front side portion of controller. The middle front side portion of pH detection box is connected to upper portion of sewage neutralization through connecting pipe (9).
   USE - The device is used for treating wastewater (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device intelligently monitors sewage before neutralization reaction by setting the pH detection sensor. If detected sewage is acidic and alkaline, the controller automatically selects to introduce certain lime water or CO2 to perform neutralization reaction. This avoids direct addition of lime water and CO2 into the neutralization tank like in neutralization reaction in the prior art which causes resource waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A wastewater treatment device comprises a substrate (1), a sewage neutralization tank (2) fixedly connected on top middle position of substrate vertically upward, an overhauling top cover (6) fixed on upper middle position of sewage neutralization tank, a stirring motor (7) fixedly connected to top middle position of overhauling top cover vertically downward, a lime water storage tank (17) fixedly connected in top left side portion of substrate, a water conveying pipe (23) having head end fixedly connected on upper middle position of lime water storage tank and tail end fixedly connected to upper left side portion of sewage neutralization tank, a lime water extraction pump fixedly connected on top of lime water storage tank and to water conveying pipe, a lime water valve (14) fixedly connected on middle portion of water conveying pipe, a carbon dioxide (CO2) storage tank (3) fixedly connected to top right side of substrate, a gas conveying pipe (4) having head end fixedly connected to top middle position of CO2 storage tank and tail end fixedly connected to top right side of sewage neutralization tank, a CO2 exhaust valve (5) fixedly connected to middle section of gas conveying pipe, a pH detection box (8) vertically and fixedly connected to top left upper corner of substrate, a cover plate (11) fixedly provided on top of pH detection box, a pH detection sensor (10) fixed and sealed vertically downward and inserted into middle portion of cover plate, a controller (19) fixedly connected to top left lower corner of substrate vertically upward, and a display module (18) fixedly embedded in upper front side portion of controller. The lower end face of transmission shaft of stirring motor is fixedly connected to upper end of pull rod, and the lower end face of pull rod is fixedly connected to stirring impeller. The middle front side portion of pH detection box is fixedly connected to upper portion of sewage neutralization through connecting pipe (9). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for wastewater treatment method using the device.

PN CN110282843-A
TI Combustion of manure in extraction of methane, involves injecting water into manure, purifying extracted methane gas by high-temperature puffing, collecting non-pure biogas, extracting methane and carbon dioxide, and pre-purifying methane.
AU YANG F
AE YANG F (YANG-Individual)
GA 2019856734
AB    NOVELTY - Combustion of manure involves injecting water into manure, purifying an extracted methane gas by high-temperature puffing, collecting a non-pure biogas for extracting methane in natural biogas, pre-purifying and collecting carbon dioxide, producing a gaseous substance comprising more than 70% methane medium and 95% ethane resin, adding bacteria as anaerobic biological substances capable of producing methane in the manure, uniformly compressing and cooling the collected gases, simultaneously removing impurity, such as hydrogen sulfide and the water vapor from the biogas using a separation device, adding biogas fermentation microorganisms mainly ferment raw materials from the process of heating and thermal expansion, and absorbing carbon and nitrogen, decreasing efficiency of anaerobic digestion, generally controlling the fermentation bacteria to control the concentration of harmful substances in biogas fermentation, and extracting the gas comprising more than 70% methane.
   USE - Combustion of manure for extracting methane used as compressed natural gas for motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables environmentally-friendly combustion of manure with reduced consumption of energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Combustion of manure involves injecting the same volume of water into manure, purifying an extracted methane gas under the action of high-temperature puffing, collecting a non-pure biogas as a multi-gas mixed gas for extracting methane in natural biogas, pre-purifying and collecting carbon dioxide, producing a gaseous substance comprising more than 70% methane medium and 95% ethane resin, adding bacteria as anaerobic biological substances capable of producing methane in the manure, and adding small amount of oxygen to affect its growth and reproduction, generating large amount of biogas and combustible methane gas, collecting the manure, filtering and cooling the concentrated gas by gas filtration and refrigeration compression through high-temperature expansion to achieve solid gas separation and gas collection treatment, uniformly compressing and cooling the collected gases using a cooling equipment which is adapted to a high temperature of 0-80 degrees C, preferably 50 degrees C, a low temperature of 20 degrees C and a medium temperature of 30 degrees C, combusting at optimum ignition temperature of 15 degrees C, 35 degrees C and 53 degrees C and at optimum pH of 7-7.5, simultaneously removing impurity, such as hydrogen sulfide and the water vapor from the biogas using a separation device, mutually adsorbing and fusing the gas generated in the separation device to obtain a gas comprising combustible gases with high combustion efficiency and no pollution, and compressed natural gas material, adding biogas fermentation microorganisms mainly ferment raw materials from the process of heating and thermal expansion, and absorbing carbon (energy for biogas microorganisms) and nitrogen, and elemental resins, such as hydrogen, sulfur and phosphorus, and biogas fermentation resins, in which ratio of carbon and nitrogen is 20:1-30:1, decreasing efficiency of anaerobic digestion, generally controlling the fermentation bacteria which needs nitrogen, phosphorus and other nutrients, such that the ratio of carbon and nitrogen is 20:1-25:1 to control the concentration of harmful substances in biogas fermentation, extracting the gas comprising more than 70% methane, collecting the gas by blowing method in the purification storage space, adsorbing small amount of oxidizing gas, collecting the combustion-supporting substance of the non-pure combustion gas, extracting the natural methane gas from the collected gas, concentrating and pressurizing, storing in a separation chamber, heating the separation chamber to normal temperature, such that the top of the separation chamber becomes pure methane, and the bottom of the separation chamber becomes pure carbon dioxide, and a thin intermediate zone is formed between the top and the bottom, for generating a mixture of flowing gas resin as a gas of methane and carbon dioxide, converting the complex high-molecular organic substances in the manure into low-molecular organic substances, such as acetic acid, propionic acid, butyric acid, etc., to provide and maintain the living conditions required for various microorganisms in the biogas fermentation, and to decompose various flammable resins by fermentation.

AU ZHENG W
   WANG J
   GUO X
   LI Y
   WANG W
   MA C
   SONG P

   ZHAO Z
   DAI C
AE CHINA SHIPBUILDING IND CORP 719TH RES (CSHI-C)

AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a methanol synthesis gas involves (i) providing a first feed comprising hydrocarbon and steam, (ii) providing a second feed comprising hydrocarbon, steam and carbon dioxide, where the molecular carbon dioxide/hydrocarbon ratio is 0.1-0.6, preferably 0.25-0.4, (iii) reforming the first feed in a two-step steam reforming process to a first synthesis gas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide and having a module M of 2.05 plus minus 0.2, where M is (hydrogen - carbon dioxide)/(carbon monoxide + carbon dioxide), (iv) steam reforming the second feed in parallel with the two-step reforming process in step (iii) to a second synthesis gas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide and (v) employing the first and second synthesis gas in their combination as feed for methanol synthesis.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a methanol synthesis gas and increasing production capacity of existing methanol plants based on two step reforming in the production of methanol synthesis gas or other methanol plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines two-step reforming and steam reforming of a carbon dioxide containing feed in parallel and has reduced energy consumption and a reduced carbon dioxide foot-print.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for revamping methanol synthesis gas plant involving adding a steam reformer in parallel to an existing two-step reforming section, connecting the steam reforming section to a carbon dioxide source and connecting an outlet line of the parallel steam reforming section to an outlet line of the existing two-step reforming section or connecting the outlet line of the parallel steam reforming section to an existing methanol reactor in the methanol synthesis plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves desulfurizing the first feed upstream to the two step steam reforming process. The method further involves desulfurizing the second feed upstream to the tubular steam reforming, where the second synthesis gas has the module M of 1.6-2.6, preferably 1.85-2.35 and the methanol synthesis gas has the module M of 1.9-2.2. The second feed comprises carbon dioxide obtained from flue and/or off-gas and/or imported to plant. The steam reforming in step (iv) is performed in a standard tubular reformer, a tubular reformer followed by an adiabatic reformer, where carbon dioxide is added before and/or after the tubular reformer, a bayonet type reformer, a convective reformer or a tubular reformer and carbon dioxide is added into the reformer tubes after the feed has initially come into contact with the reforming catalyst. The method further involves converting the methanol synthesis gas to a methanol product.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108821290-A; CN108821290-B
TI Carbon dioxide production device comprises a gas mixture recovery device, a cooling and dehumidifying device, a compression device, a drying device, a non-condensing gas treatment device, a liquefaction device.
AU XU X
   LI D
AE SHANDONG JINGBO ZHONGCHENG CLEANING ENER (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201891926H
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide production device comprises a gas mixture recovery device, a cooling and dehumidifying device, a compression device, a drying device, a non-condensing gas treatment device, a liquefaction device, a gas-liquid separation device, a purification device, and a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank. The gas mixing recovery device is provided with a decarburization gas inlet and a high purity non-condensable gas inlet. The non-condensable gas treatment device is provided with a high-purity non-condensable gas outlet, a high-purity non-condensable gas inlet, a low-purity non-condensable gas inlet, and a low-purity non-condensable gas outlet. The high-purity non-condensable gas outlet is connected to the recovered high-purity non-condensable gas inlet. The high purity non-condensable gas inlets are respectively connected to a purification device and a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank. The low purity non-condensable gas inlet is connected to the gas-liquid separation device.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide production device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbon dioxide, comprising (i) sequentially decarburizing, dehumidifying, compressing, drying, non-condensing, liquefying, and gas-liquid separation, obtaining liquid carbon dioxide to be purified and low purity non-condensable gas and performing the low-purity non-condensable gas recovery in the above non-condensable gas treatment process and (ii) purifying the liquid carbon dioxide to be purified obtained in step (i) to obtain liquid carbon dioxide and high purity non-condensable gas, where the high-purity non-condensable gas recovery is carried out by the non-condensable gas treatment process in the step a), and the high-purity non-condensable gas after the heat exchange is mixed with the decarburization gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the carbon dioxide production device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The gas-liquid separation device is provided with a liquid outlet and a gas outlet. The gas outlet is in communication with the low-purity non-condensable gas inlet. The condensable gas processing device is provided with a voltage-stabilizing control system and a heat exchange system. The low-purity non-condensable gas outlet connected with the torch system. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the compression pressure is 2.5-3 MPa. The process of the non-condensable gas treatment in the step (i) specifically comprises carrying out heat exchange of the low-purity non-condensable gas and the high-purity non-condensable gas after the pressure reduction and the decarburization gas after drying, and the high purity after the heat exchange Non-condensing gas is mixed with decarburizing gas, where the reduced pressure is 50-100 KPa and cooling from -35 to -40 degrees C. The temperature of the high-purity non-condensable gas after the heat exchange is 0 to 5 degrees C and the volume content of the carbon dioxide is 95-98%. In the step (i), the temperature of the liquefaction is from -15 to -20 degrees C. The ratio of the intake of the high-purity non-condensable gas and the decarburized gas after the heat exchange is (7-10):100.

TI Methanol water high-pressure hydrogen production system, has water-cooled heat exchanger which is arranged on carbon dioxide mixed residual gas output pipe, which is provided for water-cooling and cooling carbon dioxide mixed residual gas.

GA 202015716M
AB    NOVELTY - The system has reformer (1) whose inlet is connected with methanol water vapor pipe, which delivers methanol water vapor into reformer. The outlet of reformer and inlet of hydrogen separation device (2) are connected to a first mixed gas conveying pipe, which are provided for conveying mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide produced in reformer to the hydrogen separation device for hydrogen separation. The carbon dioxide mixed residual gas output pipe is connected to carbon dioxide separator (6). The carbon dioxide separator is connected with a carbon dioxide output pipe and hydrogen mixed residual gas output pipe. A water-cooled heat exchanger (5) is arranged on carbon dioxide mixed residual gas output pipe, which is provided for water-cooling and cooling carbon dioxide mixed residual gas that is transported. The water-cooled heat exchanger is connected to water-cooled tower. The operating temperature of water-cooled heat exchanger is 18 to 30.8 degrees C.
   USE - Methanol water high-pressure hydrogen production system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system produces hydrogen in a high pressure environment with high hydrogen production efficiency. The gas in the system is circulated and purified. The theoretical yield reach 100% and the hydrogen yield is greater than or equal to 95%.

TI Mushroom grower, has circulation fan interposed in ventilation openings for air circulation within multiple vinyl houses, and ultrasonic humidifier and vent installed in upper part of inner side of building.
AU HONG Y C
   HONGYOUNGKI
   KONG M
   HAN J H
   LEE J
AE LSIST CO LTD (LSIS-Non-standard)
GA 201970354E
AB    NOVELTY - The grower (100) has a building (10) formed with a partitioned inner space (S). A side of a greenhouse (20) is connected with an internal space of the building. Multiple internal carbon dioxide exhaust ports (21) are formed in a lower portion of a side wall of the greenhouse for discharging carbon dioxide heavier than air. Multiple external carbon dioxide exhaust ports (11) are formed in a lower portion of another side wall of the building. An end of a carbon dioxide exhaust pipe (30) is connected to the internal carbon dioxide exhaust ports. A circulation fan (41) is interposed in ventilation openings (40) for air circulation within multiple vinyl houses. An ultrasonic humidifier (70) and a vent (60) are installed in an upper part of an inner side of the building.
   USE - Mushroom grower.
   ADVANTAGE - The ultrasonic humidifier and the vent are installed in the upper part of the inner side of the building so as to discharge rich carbon dioxide from the grower to an outer side without using an expensive carbon dioxide separator, thus growing mushrooms at low cost. The grower prevents generation of moisture particles to a mushroom surface in a large form during humidity control, so that wind speeds can be brought close to natural conditions to optimize the mushroom growth environment and contamination and malformations can be reduced, thus improving quality of the mushrooms.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a mushroom grower.

PN DE102017123807-A1; WO2019072814-A1; EP3694621-A1
TI Recovering phosphate from raw material by            extraction with carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. allowing            carbon dioxide in suspension to act on raw material,            separating undissolved solids from suspension, and            precipitating phosphates.
AU OPITZ E
   KLEIN S
   GOTTSCHALL A
   SCHNEE R
   MUESSIG M
   KOTZBAUER U
   POGANSKI M
AE CHEM FAB BUDENHEIM OETKER R A (BUDE-C)
   REMONDIS AQUA GMBH & CO KG (REMO-Non-standard)
GA 201936039F
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering phosphate from a raw material by                extraction with carbon dioxide, comprises (a)                initiating carbon dioxide in aqueous and/or                alcoholic suspension of the raw material, (b)                allowing the carbon dioxide in the suspension to                act on the raw material under elevated pressure,                (c) separating undissolved solids from the                suspension, where a liquid is obtained, and (d)                precipitating phosphates from the liquid obtained                in step (c) and separating phosphates from the                liquid. The steps (a) and (b) are carried out in a                continuously operated reactor until the pH of the                suspension is less than or equal to 6.9.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering phosphate                from a raw material by extraction with carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows improved phosphate recovery                from raw material by extraction with carbon                dioxide, and achieves higher yields.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                plant for producing phosphate from an aqueous,                alcoholic or aqueous or alcoholic suspension of a                raw material by extraction with carbon dioxide,                comprising an extraction reactor for the extraction                of the phosphate from the suspension of the raw                material with carbon dioxide, a device for                introducing carbon dioxide into the extraction                reactor, a device for adjusting an elevated                pressure in the extraction reactor, a means for                separating solids from the extracted suspension and                a precipitation reactor for precipitating the                extracted phosphates. The extraction reactor is a                reactor suitable for continuous operation, where at                least one sensor is arranged at the distal end of                the reactor or at a distal end of a reactor                section, with which the pH of the suspension in the                reactor can be determined during operation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The suspension, when its                  pH at the distal end of the reactor is more than                  6.9, is at least partially recycled to the                  proximal section of the reactor to at least                  partially re-pass through the reactor. The                  suspension, when its pH at the distal end of a                  first section of the reactor is more than 6.9, is                  passed on to a subsequent second section of the                  reactor to pass through the second section of the                  reactor. In step (a), gaseous and/or liquid                  carbon dioxide is introduced in the molar ratio                  of 90:10-10:90 to each other. The step (b) is                  performed under a pressure of 2-100 bar,                  preferably 5-16 bar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The pH sensor is                  arranged at the distal end of the reactor and a                  recirculation loop through which at least a                  portion of the suspension from the distal end of                  the reactor can be returned to the proximal end                  of the reactor. The reactor has in the                  longitudinal direction at least two sections                  merging into one another, at whose respective                  distal end a respective pH sensor is arranged.                  The additional feed lines for carbon dioxide are                  provided at the proximal end of the second,                  third, fourth, fifth or n-th reactor section. The                  means for introducing carbon dioxide into the                  extraction reactor is provided for the                  introduction of gaseous carbon dioxide and/or for                  the introduction of liquid carbon dioxide. The                  ratio of the length of the reactor interior to                  the diameter of the interior of the reactor is                  50:1-10000:1. The reactor is in the form of a                  tube with 5-30 turns of 90-180 degrees . The                  plant comprises (a) a flash tank downstream of                  the reactor space, (b) a sieve, a filter and/or a                  centrifuge for the removal of the solids still                  undissolved after the carbon dioxide extraction                  from the suspension and/or (c) a settling funnel,                  a sieve, a filter, a centrifuge or a hydrocyclone                  in the precipitation reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN AT523779-A1
TI Production of hydrogen and oxygen involves passing biogas, biogenic, methane and carbon dioxide to tank, mixing, compressing, cooling, separating carbon dioxide, passing obtained gas mixture comprising methane into reactor, and separating methane into hydrogen in gas phase and carbon.
AU GRUBER-SCHMIDT J
AE GS-GRUBER-SCHMIDT (GSGR-Non-standard)
GA 2021D84316
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrogen (49) and oxygen (42) from biogas (1), biogenic gas (94), methane (11,95) and carbon dioxide (2), involves (ii) passing biogas comprising a gas mixture comprising 40-70% methane and 30-60% carbon dioxide, biogenic gas comprising a gas mixture comprising 10-70% methane and 30-90% carbon dioxide, methane having a methane concentration of 80-100% and a carbon dioxide concentration of 0-20%, and carbon dioxide having a carbon dioxide concentration of 100% in a vapor phase to tank (3), mixing, compressing, cooling, separating carbon dioxide in liquid phase from the gas mixture, passing the gas mixture comprising 100% methane into heat exchanger (45) and heating methane at 100-400 degrees C, injecting heated methane in gas phase into reactor (55), and separating methane in the form of gas bubbles into hydrogen in gas phase and carbon in amorphous phase.
   USE - Production of hydrogen and oxygen from biogas, biogenic gas, methane and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, economical and environmentally-friendly production of hydrogen and oxygen from biogas, biogenic gas, methane and carbon dioxide with zero emission and external heat supply, and reduced consumption of energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of hydrogen and oxygen from biogas, biogenic gas, methane (11,95) and carbon dioxide, involves (i) using a tank (3), hydraulic piston compressors (4,7), a heat exchanger (10), a tank (13), heat exchangers (45,60), a reactor (55), a cyclone separator (52), a gas scrubber (48), reactors (26,30,32) and heat exchangers (38,41), (ii) passing biogas comprising a gas mixture comprising 40-70% methane and 30-60% carbon dioxide, and having a pressure of 1-10 bar and a temperature of 0-35 degrees C, biogenic gas comprising a gas mixture comprising 10-70% methane and 30-90% carbon dioxide, and having a pressure of 1-10 bar and a temperature of 0-35 degrees C, methane having a methane concentration of 80-100%, a carbon dioxide concentration of 0-20%, a pressure of 1-10 bar and a temperature of 0-35 degrees C, and carbon dioxide having a carbon dioxide concentration of 100% in a vapor phase, a pressure of 1-10 bar and a temperature of 0-35 degrees C to the tank (3) having a volume of 1-10 m3, (iii) mixing the biogas, biogenic gas, methane and carbon dioxide in the tank (3) having a pressure of 1-10 bar and a temperature of 0-35 degrees C, (iv) compressing the obtained gas mixture with compressor (4) at a pressure of 12-15 bar, and compressing with compressor (7) at a pressure of 50-100 bar, cooling the gas mixture to 0-25 degrees C in the heat exchanger (10), (v) separating carbon dioxide in liquid phase from the gas mixture and storing the carbon dioxide in the tank (13) having a volume of 1-10 m3, a pressure of 50-100 bar and a temperature of 0-35 degrees C, (vi) passing the gas mixture comprising 100% methane having temperature of 0-25 degrees C and pressure of 50-100 bar from the heat exchanger (10) into heat exchanger (45) and heating methane at 100-400 degrees C, passing into heat exchanger (60) and heating methane at 400-800 degrees C, (vii) injecting heated methane in gas phase into the reactor (55) comprising a metal lead (54) which is present in liquid phase due to inductive heating, and having a volume of 1-10 m3 via a nozzle assembly (58), and sealing the reactor (55) with inductive windings (31,56) in order to heat the reactor (55) at 600-1200 degrees C and at 50-100 bar, (viii) separating methane in the form of gas bubbles with a diameter of 0.1-10 mm in the reactor (55) into hydrogen in gas phase and carbon in amorphous phase, passing mixture of hydrogen and carbon into the cyclone separator which separates 70-95% carbon, (ix) cooling hydrogen to 100-200 degrees C and cooling hydrogen with water to 5-50 degrees C, removing 1-10 ppm carbon from the gas scrubber, (x) evaporating liquid carbon dioxide (12) in the reactor (26) with a bed of metal oxide pellets encased using iron oxide, with a layer thickness of 1-100 mu m, and a ceramic base component comprising alumina at 200-400 degrees C, cooling to 200-400 degrees C, converting gaseous carbon dioxide into 75-98% carbon monoxide with the help of iron oxide pellets in the reactor (30), and converting iron(II) oxide into iron(III) oxide through gaseous carbon dioxide, heating the reactor (26) at 800-1400 degrees C by inductive heating the bed in which iron(III) oxide which releases oxygen (42) and which is reduced to 95-100% iron(II) oxide, (xi) evaporating liquid carbon dioxide in the reactor (32) with a bed of metal oxide pellets encased using iron oxide, with a layer thickness of 1-100 mu m, and a ceramic base component comprising alumina at 200-400 degrees C, cooling to 200-400 degrees C, converting gaseous carbon dioxide into 75-98% carbon monoxide with the help of iron oxide pellets in the reactor (26), and converting iron(II) oxide into iron(III) oxide through gaseous carbon dioxide, heating the reactor (26) at 800-1400 degrees C by inductive heating the bed in which iron(III) oxide which releases oxygen and which is reduced to 95-100% iron(II) oxide, and (xii) cooling gaseous carbon monoxide having pressure of 50-100 bar from the reactors (26,32) to 25-50 degrees C via heat exchanger (38), and cooling gaseous carbon monoxide having pressure of 50-100 bar to 25-50 degrees C via heat exchanger (41). The reactor (26) comprises a cylindrical shell, and has a minimum volume of 1-10 m3, a pressure of 50-100 bar and a temperature of 100-1400 degrees C. The reactor (30) comprises a cylindrical shell, and has a minimum volume of 1-10 m3, a pressure of 50-100 bar and a temperature of 100-1400 degrees C.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system for production of hydrogen and oxygen from biogas, biogenic gas, methane and carbon dioxide.
   Biogas (1)
   Carbon dioxide (2)
   Tanks (3,13)
   Hydraulic piston compressors (4,7)
   Heat exchangers (10,38,41,45,60)
   Methane (11,95)
   Liquid carbon dioxide (12)
   Reactors (26,30,32,55)
   Inductive windings (31,56)
   Oxygen (42)
   Gas scrubber (48)
   Hydrogen (49)
   Cyclone separator (52)
   Metal lead (54)
   Nozzle assembly (58)
   Biogenic gas (94)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The reactor (r1) comprises a cylindrical shell, and has a minimum volume of 1-10 m3, a pressure of 50-100 bar and a temperature of 100-1400 degrees C. The reactor (r2) comprises a cylindrical shell, and has a minimum volume of 1-10 m3, a pressure of 50-100 bar and a temperature of 100-1400 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further involves using a tank (t1), reactors (r1,r2), heat exchangers (h1,h2) and gas scrubber, passing carbon monoxide into tank (t1) having a helium concentration of 10-50%, a volume of 1-10 m3, a pressure of 50-100 bar and a temperature of 0-35 degrees C, heating gas mixture of carbon monoxide and helium in the reactor (r1) at 200-400 degrees C, cooling the gas mixture in the reactor (r2) with a bed of metal oxide pellets encased using manganese oxide, with a layer thickness of 1-100 mu m, and a ceramic base component comprising alumina at 200-400 degrees C, converting carbon monoxide into 75-98% carbon with the help of the metal oxide pellets and converting manganese(II) oxide into manganese(III) oxide, heating the reactor (r2) at 100-500 degrees C by inductive heating the bed in which manganese(III) oxide which releases oxygen and which is reduced to 95-100% manganese(II) oxide, cooling gaseous mixture of helium having pressure of 50-100 bar and carbon from the reactors (74,80) to 25-50 degrees C via the heat exchanger (h1), cooling helium and removing 1-10 ppm carbon from the gas scrubber, and cooling oxygen (93) having pressure of 50-100 bar from the reactors (74,80) to 25-50 degrees C via the heat exchanger (h2).

PN BR102013012165-A2
TI Converting carbon dioxide for obtaining synthesis gas through reverse shift reaction by contacting gas feed mixture containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen with a catalyst.
AU APPEL L G
   BARROS GASPAR A
   DA COSTA ZONETTI P
   SOUSA-AGUIAR E F
AE INST NACIONAL TECNOLOGIA (NATE-Non-standard)
   PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
GA 2020B3107K
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide is converted for obtaining synthesis gas through reverse shift reaction by contacting gas feed mixture containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen with a catalyst.
   USE - Conversion of carbon dioxide for obtaining synthesis gas through reverse shift reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains synthesis gas such as very active, stable and selective carbon monoxide catalytic system with low methane production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the use of mixed oxides with low nickel (Ni) concentration which involves avoiding the formation of methane in reverse shift reactions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is based on nickel oxide. The nickel oxide-based catalyst is supported in mixed oxides. The mixed oxides are selected from cerium lanthanum (CeLa), cerium zirconium (CeZr), cerium manganese (CeMn), CeGb(sic), magnesium aluminum (MgAl), and/or perovskites. The catalyst consists of oxides of cerium (Ce) and magnesium (Mg). The catalyst comprises greater than or equal to 1 transition metal as a promoter chosen from nickel (Ni), rhenium (Re), osmium (Os), iridium (Ir), and tungsten (W). Mass of catalyst added to reaction is 10-300 mg, preferably 20-50 mg. The catalyst/diluent ratio is 1. Feed mixture contains hydrogen and carbon dioxide in a ratio of 0.1/1-4/1, preferably 1/1-3/1. Amount of nickel layer in the reaction is 0.1-2.5%, preferably 0.2-1%. The support of mixed oxides is present in the reaction in a concentration of 0.1-15 wt.%, preferably 1-10 wt.% nickel. Preferred Parameters: The reaction is carried out at 400-900 degrees C, preferably 600-800 degrees C at a pressure of 1-50 atm, preferably 0.5-2 atm.

PN US2020283920-A1
TI Post-combustion carbon dioxide capture and re-use            of that captured liquid carbon dioxide (CO2) to syngas            and oxygen out of CO2-lean CO2-stream, and other            portion of the CO2-stream discharged to further            processing for CO2 condensation.
AU BAIRAMIJAMAL F
AE BAIRAMIJAMAL F (BAIR-Individual)
GA 202087943A
AB    NOVELTY - Post-combustion carbon dioxide capture and                re-use of that captured liquid carbon dioxide (CO2)                to syngas and oxygen out of a CO2-lean CO2-stream                (i.e. in this case the flue gas of a fossil fueled                power plant) (P1), where the CO2-stream of present                process invention is further characterized by the                enhancement in CO2 concentration through the                recirculation of a portion of the CO2-rich                CO2-stream back to the combustion section and/or                the HRSG section of the plant at one side, while                the other portion of that the CO2-Stream is                discharged to further processing for                CO2condensation from that CO2-rich CO2-Stream in                the CO2-CC section, whereas the circulated                CO2-Stream and respectively it's discharge portion                upstream of the CO2-CC section reaches a                CO2concentration of 60-95 vol.% with the remaining                portion of that the COO2-stream consisting utmost                of oxygen.
   USE - The process is useful for Post-combustion                carbon dioxide capture and re-use of that captured                liquid carbon dioxide to syngas and oxygen out of a                carbon dioxide-lean carbon dioxide-stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: very high concentrated CO2-rich                CO2-stream at low gas flow rate, both factors lead                to number of extraordinary benefits , both in                processing for low operation costs as well in                investment of the plants; provides improved                carbon-rich fossil power plants from petcoke in                first priority, the coal in every grade, then crude                oil, following with bio-mass, as well as the                waste-to-energy, waste-to-hydrocarbons plants and                natural gas in combined heat and power combined                heat and power generation plants, yet more                importantly for gas turbine single or combined                cycle plants; and provides economically beneficial                to be rerouted back to core line of processing in                CO2-CC cooling and condensation (e.g.                regasified-CO2stream).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing (P2) CO2-enriched CO2-Stream at high                CO2concentration with its constituent (i.e. utmost                oxygen-obtained from the flue gas of fossil fuels,                is harnessed for DC back-up power generation and                syngas generation, where the concentrated                CO2-stream is routed in one part to a gasification                process for generation of additional process heat,                which in turn is either integrated in the CO2-HR                and CO2-PG of the first Thermodynamic cycle with                the supercritical CO2turbine(s) and/or is carried                out for generation of superheated steam with steam                turbine(s), whereas either working media is                eventually utilized for generation of primary AC                current in order to back up the high-pressure low                temperature electrochemical syngas generator(s)                (HPLTE-SG) electrolysis with additional DC power                supply post the alternating current (AC)/direct                current (DC) converter of the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The CO2-stream is                  recirculated and further distinguished by the                  addition of pure oxygen from the HPLTE-SG section                  of this process to the extent of 25-40% oxygen by                  volume in the recirculated CO2-Stream (also                  referred to flue gas oxy-fueling), in order to                  replace the nitrogen-rich intake air for the                  combustion entirely, while keeping a residual                  oxygen in the flue gas post the combustion and                  heat recovery steam generator of 5-25 vol.%. The                  CO2-stream itself can originate from a CO2-water                  electrolyte fed to a low pressure-, intermediary                  pressure-, specifically that CO2-Stream can                  originate from a high pressure low temperature                  electrochemical electrolysis, referred to as                  HPLTE-SG, where it's electrolyte has been                  consisting of liquid CO2and water- and this                  electrolyte is dissociated in the HPLTE-SG to                  that the CO2-Stream as the cathodic syngas in the                  composition carbon monoxide/hydrogen (CO/2H2)                  first, by then that the CO2-stream of CO/2H2,                  either totally or in part further processed to                  water shift converter(s), whence the syngas in                  composition CO/3H2is obtained, where this syngas                  in composition of CO/3H2with the concomitant                  CO2post the water shift converter(s) presents                  this the pre-combustion CO2-stream, with the                  peculiar features: (i) the pre-combustion of this                  the CO2-stream stems, specifically from HPLTE-SG,                  post the water shift converter(s) (ii) this                  CO2-Stream is processed through a                  CO2absorption/desorption process (i.e. use of                  abrorbens as Selexol, Rectisol, Benfield or any                  other) for separation of CO2from the                  CO2/3H2CO2-stream prior to any combustion, (iii)                  whence a high concentrated CO2-rich CO2-Stream                  obtained from the desorption above, and (iv) the                  obtained CO2-rich CO2-Stream is purified by                  intermittently operating scavenger adsorber                  column, where the traces of absorbens (e.g.                  Selexol, and Rectisol) and water are removed to                  ppb concentration, thus (v) that CO2-rich                  CO2-Stream can be routed to the parent patent's                  CO2-CC section for cooling and separation of                  carbon dioxide via condensation will be performed                  preferably in supercritical pressure of 1070 psi,                  (vi) whereas the carbon monoxide purge gas                  downstream of the CO2-CC section is recompressed                  and rerouted back to the main gas stream of                  HPLTE-SG, upstream to the water shift converters.                  The carbon monoxide traces trace in the                  recirculated flue gas will be oxidized by use of                  catalytic reaction with the concomitant oxygen                  post flue gas oxy-fueling and conditioning of the                  recirculated flue gas by preheating, where                  preferably hydrogen-oxygen torch(es) is employed                  for the reheating. The post-combustion carbon                  dioxide capture and re-use of that captured                  carbon to syngas and oxygen out of a CO2-lean                  CO2-stream is distinguished to perform                  zero-CO2-emission fossil energy, that implies the                  capture and recovery of any low flow rate or lean                  CO2-stream by use of a CO2absorber that absorbs                  the residual carbon dioxide with water,                  particularly from the purge gas of CO2-CC                  section, i.e. the oxygen-rich purge gas and                  recovers that the absorbed CO2by desorption                  process, which takes place preferably in situ in                  the scrubber of the parent patent. The                  oxygen-rich purge gas from the CO2-CC section                  post the CO2absorber will be dehydrated and                  undergoes partial expansion that delivers liquid                  oxygen (LOX) and gaseous oxygen (GOX) out of that                  oxygen-rich gas with the none-condensing gases.                  The advanced combustion is integrated in a new                  fossil fuel fired power plant i.e. coal, crude                  oil, biomass, waste carbonaceous material and                  natural gas is implemented in a dual-combined                  cycle, where the heat recovery is consisting of                  combination of classic Rankin cycle with steam as                  working fluid with the first Bairamijamal cycle                  with the carbon dioxide as working fluid that                  leads to a gross thermal efficiency in the margin                  of 65-75%. The advanced combustion is implemented                  in a new gas turbine triple-Combined cycle                  implemented, where the (ix) classic Brayton                  cycle, (iix) with the HERSG of the classic Rankin                  cycle and (iiix) the first Bairamijamal cycle are                  combined together that leads to a gross thermal                  efficiency in the margin of 80-85%. The other                  CO2-sidestreams are as well routed to that the                  part of the flue gas to the gasification for                  additional process heat generation in order to                  reduce the extent of processing work and                  equipment with those side-streams, whereas the                  CO2-Sidestreams are specifically, (iy) the                  re-gasified pure CO2that has been utilized as                  condensing media for the condensation of the                  CO2-stream in the CO2-CC section by partial                  pressure relief of the liquid carbon dioxide into                  lower pressure level of ca. 45 bar, which                  typically consists of 5% of the circulated flue                  gas CO2to the CO2-CC section, and (ii) the                  oxygen-rich CO2containing vent gas downstream of                  the CO2-CC condenser(s) with over 90 vol.% oxygen                  and to lesser portion of CO2constituent at ca. 75                  bar pressure, which typically constitutes nearly                  10% of the origin CO2-enriched CO2-stream of the                  advanced combustion to the CO2-CC section. The                  process (P2) the step of distinguished in                  generation of additional process heat, whereas                  the gasification of that the CO2-Sidestreams with                  portion of that CO2-enriched CO2-Stream is                  carried out with the anodic oxygen obtained from                  the HPLTE-SG and the natural gas preferably to a                  two-stage autothermal catalytic gasification                  (also referred to as bi-reforming). The process                  (P2) is further characterized that the process                  heat generation in the gasification of that the                  CO02-streams with oxygen and natural gas is                  integrated in the overall heat recovery COO2-HR                  and CO2-PG with the supercritical CO2-turbine(s),                  syngas turbine(s), oxygen turbine(s) and the                  steam turbine(s) for DC back-up power, yet then                  as such, it comprises also the superheating of                  saturated steam obtained in the adjacent                  downstream production plant, where the conversion                  of the syngas takes place to produce the final                  products, i.e. methanol, transportation gasoline,                  aviation fuel, ethanol, ammonia, and other final                  products. The portion of the primary CO2-enriched                  oxygen-containing CO2-stream (i.e. the circulated                  primary flue gas from the advanced combustion,                  aka termed the flue gas oxy-fueling; i.e. figure                  which is given in the specification 14, the                  stream 1) with the other CO2-sidestreams                  (according to figure which is given in the                  specification 14, i.e. the streams 3.2, 4 and 6)                  are fed singularly or jointly to that the                  gasification, comprises 1-45% of the primary                  carbon mol stream as carbon dioxide over the                  circulated flue gas (which is the stream 1),                  preferably that the CO2mol stream to the                  gasification is carried out of 15-25% of the                  primary mol stream of carbon obtained post                  combustion as carbon dioxide obtained from the                  primary carbon mol stream over the circulated                  flue gas. The process (P2) characterized that the                  extent of the secondary natural gas injection                  into the second stage of the catalytic                  autothermal gasification is specified in ratio to                  the generated water steam in the first stage of                  the gasification in a way, that mol ratio of the                  water steam of the first stage to the secondary                  natural gas into the second stage stands in the                  mol ratio of (1.75-3.0) water to 1 natural gas,                  preferably 2.5 mol water to 1 mol natural gas.                  The low temperature oxygen-rich CO2-sidestream                  from the purge gas downstream of the CO2-CC                  condenser(s) at ca. 75 bar is integrated in the                  CO2-HR and CO2-PG by preheating from waste heat                  and process heat, then routed to a turbine with                  the attached generator to generate additional AC                  power, before it is added to that portion of the                  CO2-stream to the gasification.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN WO2022191095-A1
TI System used for neutralizing carbon dioxide gas            discharged from power generation system, consists of            electrolysis unit comprising electrolyte, cathode            electrode, anode electrode and diaphragm, carbon            dioxide treatment unit, acidic solution neutralization            unit and discharge unit.
AU KAZUHIRO S
   YUGO M
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 2022B6715K
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide gas neutralization treatment                system consists of an electrolysis unit, a carbon                dioxide treatment unit, an acidic solution                neutralization unit and a discharge unit. The                electrolysis unit comprises an electrolyte, a                cathode electrode, an anode electrode and a                diaphragm. The electrolyte is an aqueous solution                containing sodium chloride. In the electrolysis                unit, the electrolyte is electrolyzed, basic                aqueous solution (a1) is generated on the cathode                side of the electrolysis unit and an acidic aqueous                solution (a2) is generated on the anode side of the                electrolysis unit. In the carbon dioxide treatment                unit, the aqueous solution (a1) is produced on the                cathode side and carbon dioxide gas undergo a                neutralization and dissolution reaction to produce                an aqueous solution (a3) containing anions of                carbonate. In the acidic liquid neutralization                unit, the aqueous solution (a2) generated on the                anode side is neutralized with a basic substance to                generate an aqueous solution (a4).
   USE - System used for neutralizing carbon dioxide                gas (claimed) discharged from power generation                system for electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has suppressed the side reaction,                reduced the electrolytic voltage reduced carbon                dioxide on a large scale and economically, and                suppressed global warming caused by carbon dioxide                and promotes the realization of a low-carbon                society.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide gas neutralization treatment                system consists of an electrolysis unit, a carbon                dioxide treatment unit, an acidic solution                neutralization unit and a discharge unit. The                electrolysis unit comprises an electrolyte, a                cathode electrode, an anode electrode and a                diaphragm. The electrolyte is an aqueous solution                containing sodium chloride. In the electrolysis                unit, the electrolyte is electrolyzed, a basic                aqueous solution (a1) is generated on the cathode                side of the electrolysis unit and an acidic aqueous                solution (a2) is generated on the anode side of the                electrolysis unit. In the carbon dioxide treatment                unit, the aqueous solution (a1) is produced on the                cathode side and carbon dioxide gas undergo a                neutralization and dissolution reaction to produce                an aqueous solution (a3) containing anions of                carbonate. In the acidic liquid neutralization                unit, the aqueous solution (a2) generated on the                anode side is neutralized with a basic substance to                generate an aqueous solution (a4). The discharge                unit consists of a discharge unit (s1) and a                discharge unit (s2). The discharge unit (s1)                discharges the aqueous solution (a1) or the aqueous                solution (a3) to the external environment. The                discharge unit (s2) discharges the aqueous solution                (a2) or the aqueous solution (a4) to the external                environment. The energy unit consumption for carbon                dioxide absorption is at least more than the energy                unit consumption for carbon dioxide emissions of                the power generation equipment used in the                electrolysis device. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for neutralization of carbon dioxide gas,                which involves performing electrolysis process, a                carbon dioxide treatment process, an acidic liquid                neutralization process, and a discharge process.                The electrolysis step is performed by using the                electrolytic device. In the electrolysis step, the                electrolyte is electrolyzed, a basic aqueous                solution (a1) is generated on the cathode side of                the electrolysis unit and an acidic aqueous                solution (a2) is generated on the anode side of the                electrolysis unit. In the carbon dioxide treatment                step, the aqueous solution (a1) is produced on the                cathode side and carbon dioxide gas undergo a                neutralization and dissolution reaction to produce                an aqueous solution (a3) containing anions of                carbonate. In the acidic liquid neutralization                step, the aqueous solution (a2) generated on the                anode side is neutralized with a basic substance to                generate an aqueous solution (a4). The discharge                process is performed by using a discharge                sub-process (p1) and a discharge sub-process (p2).                The discharge sub-step (p1) discharges the aqueous                solution (a1) or the aqueous solution (a2) to the                external environment. The discharge sub-step (p2)                discharges the aqueous solution (a2) or the aqueous                solution (a4) to the external environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Properties: The energy consumption                  rate for carbon dioxide absorption is 0.5                  kg-carbon dioxide/kWh or more. In the                  electrolysis device, the operation rate of                  electrolysis is 20% or more. Preferred                  Components: In the acidic liquid neutralization                  unit, the basic substance that neutralizes the                  aqueous solution (a2) comprising basic minerals                  or basic wastes. The carbon dioxide treatment                  unit comprises a storage compartment (c1) for                  transferring the aqueous solution (a1) from the                  cathode side to the storage compartment (c1). The                  acidic liquid neutralization device comprises a                  storage compartment (c2), where the aqueous                  solution (a2) generated on the anode side is                  transferred to the second storage compartment. In                  the storage compartment (c2), the aqueous                  solution (a2) is neutralized with the basic                  substance, and the neutralized aqueous solution                  is discharged to the external environment. The                  device further consists of an oxygen reduction                  device, where a gas-containing oxygen is supplied                  to perform oxygen reduction using the oxygen                  reduction device on the cathode side of the                  electrolytic device, a sensor comprising a sensor                  (o1) that monitors the pH or a sensor (o2) that                  monitors the composition of the lysate to                  neutralize the aqueous solution (a2). The aqueous                  solution on the cathode side is brought into                  contact with carbon dioxide supplied from the                  carbon dioxide treatment device to generate                  hydrogen carbonate ions. The ion exchange                  membrane is arranged on the surface of the anode                  electrode or in the vicinity of the anode                  electrode. The high-purity oxygen is generated on                  the anode side to the cathode side using the                  oxygen reduction device, and passed to a thermal                  power generation unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The basic minerals or                  basic wastes contain iron(II)-ion,                  vanadium(IV)-ion, and manganese(II)-ion. TI System for extracting essential oils from plant matter comprises fluid system consisting of extraction vessel which holds plant matter from which essential oil is extracted and separation vessel of essential oil and carbon dioxide.
AU BAKER K C
AE BAKER K C (BAKE-Individual)
GA 2018742007
AB    NOVELTY - A system for extracting essential oils from plant matter, comprises fluid system consisting of extraction vessel (30) which holds plant matter from which essential oil is extracted, separation vessel (34) which separates essential oil from carbon dioxide, compressor (22) which uses super critical carbon dioxide to push the plant matter from extraction vessel to separation vessel, needling valve (32) which is attached to fluid system between extraction vessel and separation vessel and cools carbon dioxide by a drop in pressure before entering the separation vessel, heat exchanger (12) which is combined with chiller (10) and is upstream of extraction vessel and downstream of compressor and cools carbon dioxide released from compressor, and control system which is connected to compressor and programmed with instructions for monitoring flow rate of carbon dioxide through fluid system.
   USE - A system for extracting essential oils from plant matter.
   ADVANTAGE - Extraction yield is increased by providing stabilization period of 30 minutes to provide adequate contact between dry material and solvent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system for extracting essential oils from plant matter, comprises fluid system consisting of extraction vessel (30) which holds plant matter from which essential oil is extracted, separation vessel (34) which is connected to extraction vessel downstream in fluid system from to extraction vessel and separates essential oil from carbon dioxide, compressor (22) which is connected to extraction vessel and is upstream of extraction vessel and downstream of separation vessel where compressor uses super critical carbon dioxide to push the plant matter from extraction vessel to separation vessel, needling valve (32) which is attached to fluid system between extraction vessel and separation vessel and cools carbon dioxide by a drop in pressure before entering the separation vessel, heat exchanger (12) which is combined with chiller (10) and is upstream of extraction vessel and downstream of compressor and cools carbon dioxide released from compressor, and control system which is connected to compressor and programmed with instructions for monitoring flow rate of carbon dioxide through fluid system by adjusting the needling valve over time while operating a timer by iteratively performing the following steps until timer reaches a predetermined value, determining the flow rate of carbon dioxide through fluid system, and adjusting fluid system to ensure flow rate is constant over time where adjusting the needling valve further involves creating pressure differential by dropping a temperature of carbon dioxide forming solid carbon dioxide and creating low pressure at the suction port of compressor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for dry plant extraction using supercritical fluid.

Preferred Processes: Monitoring the flow rate of carbon dioxide through fluid system further involves measuring the flow rate, pressure and temperature of carbon dioxide, calculating a target flow rate, target pressure and target temperature of the carbon dioxide. Adjusting the fluid system further involves increasing an output pressure of the compressor.

   WANG T

AB    NOVELTY - Method for directly preparing methanol by                carbon dioxide and hydrogen, comprises adopting                carbon dioxide hydrogenation synthesis tower, the                method comprises e.g. mixing the pressurized                hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material gas with the                pressurized compressed circulating gas to obtain                the raw material mixed gas, preheating the raw                material mixed gas, dividing into two parts,                respectively the first inlet tower gas and the                second inlet tower gas are divided into two parts,                entering the first reaction bed layer to react                after preheating in the tower, preheating the                second tower gas in the tower, preheating the first                inlet tower gas, cooling three-bed outlet gas,                separating the gas and liquid, the separated liquid                enters the next methanol flash evaporation process,                compressing the separated gas as the compressed                recycle gas and mixed with the pressurized                hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material gas, to obtain                the raw material mixed gas, then participating in                the reaction again.
   USE - Method for directly preparing methanol by                carbon dioxide and hydrogen, which is useful in                chemical industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low investment and high carbon                dioxide one-way conversion rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for directly preparing methanol by                carbon dioxide and hydrogen, comprises adopting                carbon dioxide hydrogenation synthesis tower, where                the carbon dioxide hydrogenation synthesis tower is                provided with three reaction bed layers and two                shell-and-tube heat exchangers, the method                comprises (1) mixing the pressurized                hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material gas with the                pressurized compressed circulating gas to obtain                the raw material mixed gas, preheating the raw                material mixed gas, dividing into two parts,                respectively the first inlet tower gas and the                second inlet tower gas are divided into two parts                (2) entering the first reaction bed layer to react                after preheating in the tower, preheating the                second tower gas in the tower, entering into the                first reaction bed layer to react, (3) preheating                the first inlet tower gas by one bed outlet gas as                heat source, the one bed outlet gas after heat                exchange as the second bed inlet gas enters the                second reaction bed layer reaction, (4) preheating                the second inlet gas by the second bed outlet gas                as heat source, the second bed outlet gas after                heat exchange as three-bed inlet gas enters the                third reaction bed layer reaction, (5) preheating                raw material mixed gas by the three-bed outlet gas                as heat source, cooling three-bed outlet gas after                heat exchange, separating the gas and liquid, the                separated liquid enters the next methanol flash                evaporation process, compressing the separated gas                as the compressed recycle gas and mixed with the                pressurized hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material                gas, to obtain the raw material mixed gas, then                participating in the reaction again.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the process, the                  hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material gas after                  being pressurized, counting hydrogen and carbon                  dioxide after being folded, and the molar ratio                  is 3: 1. The pressure of the pressurized                  hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material gas is                  5.0-10.0 MPa, the pressure of the pressurized                  compressed circulating gas is 5.0-10.0 MPa. In                  the step (1), a strand is separated from the raw                  material mixed gas as the cold shock gas and                  directly enters the first reaction bed layer top                  part the synthesis tower and used for regulating                  the inlet gas temperature of the first reaction                  bed layer. The operation pressure in the carbon                  dioxide hydrogenation synthesis tower is 5-10.0                  MPa, and the operation temperature is                  210-290&#8451;. The inlet gas of each bed                  layer is pre-heated to 210-250&#8451;, and                  then enters into each bed layer to react. In the                  step (5), the separated gas is pumped out to form                  a purge gas and the rest gas is used as the                  compressed recycle gas after being compressed. In                  the process, the separated liquid enters the                  methanol flash evaporation tank, and obtaining                  the crude methanol and flash steam after flash                  evaporation.Preferred Components: The volume ratio of                  the pressurized compressed circulating gas to the                  pressurized hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material                  gas is 3- 5: 1.

PN CN114681741-A
TI Carbon dioxide absorbing tank for use in            anesthesia machine, comprises cover body provided with            filter screen and fixing plate for supporting filter            screen, where fixing plate is provided with air inlet            hole and set of air holes.
AU XU H
AE SHANGHAI DRAEGER MEDICAL DEVICES CO LTD (DRAG-C)
GA 202289866F
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide absorption tank comprises                cover body and tank body. The cover body is                detachably connected to tank body, and the tank is                provided with a chamber for accommodating carbon                dioxide adsorption. An air inlet pipe and an air                outlet are provided on the cover body. Both sides                of the cover are provided with sliding grooves on                both sides. A filter screen and a fixing plate for                supporting the filter screen are provided in the                cover. The filter screen is located between the                cover and the fixing plate. A first air intake hole                for the air to pass through and multiple                micro-holes communicating with the air outlet. The                micro-pores are capable of allowing the filtered                gas to pass and to block and filter the carbon                dioxide absorbent. The fixing plate is provided for                connecting the air intake pipe and the multiple air                holes. The multiple breathable holes communicate                with the chamber.
   USE - The carbon dioxide absorbing tank is used in                anesthesia machine i.e. laparoscopic anesthesia                machine, for performing chemical and physical                treatment with a carbon dioxide absorbent in a                respiratory loop of the anesthesia machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The micro-pores allow the filtered gas to pass                through and block the filter of the carbon dioxide                absorbent, thus preventing the carbon-di-oxide                absorbent dust from entering the breathing loop                along with the air flow. The filter screen is                supported by the fixing plate, so that the filter                screen can be quickly removed to clean. The fixing                plate can be poured in the tank body, so as to                prevent the absorbent such as carbon dioxide, from                entering into the air inlet pipe. The tank body is                provided with the cover body and tank body                detachably connected, thus allowing the tank to be                replaced, and hence realizing the repeated use of                the tank. The cover body is slidably connected to                the anesthesia machine, thus realizing the quick                dismounting and mounting of the absorbing                tank.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                anesthesia machine.

AU LI D
AE LI D (LIDD-Individual)
GA 2019391451
AB    NOVELTY - Drying ammonium bicarbonate, comprises using a dryer for drying wet ammonium bicarbonate, using a separator for separating the finished product from the exhaust gas after drying the wet ammonium bicarbonate, arranging a reactor before the dryer, firstly conveying wet ammonium bicarbonate into reactor, passing ammonia and carbon dioxide gas into the reactor, to obtain excess ammonium carbamate by reacting two moles of ammonia with one mole of carbon-dioxide, heating to increase the temperature of the material, passing carbon dioxide-rich circulating cooling gas for cooling, as the material moves toward the tail and is in full contact with the cooling gas and carbamate in wet materials, carrying out relatively slow hydrolysis of ammonium producing ammonium bicarbonate by reacting one mole of ammonium carbamate with two moles of water and one mole of carbon-dioxide to obtain two moles of ammonium bicarbonate and passing reactive heat heating cycle cooling gas.
   USE - The method is useful for drying ammonium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not require external heat, and reduces production costs and exhaust emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Drying ammonium bicarbonate, comprises using a dryer for drying wet ammonium bicarbonate, using a separator for separating the finished product from the exhaust gas after drying the wet ammonium bicarbonate, arranging a reactor before the dryer, firstly conveying wet ammonium bicarbonate into reactor, passing ammonia and carbon dioxide gas into the reactor, to obtain excess ammonium carbamate by reacting two moles of ammonia with one mole of carbon-dioxide, heating to increase the temperature of the material, controlling at 50 degrees C, passing carbon dioxide-rich circulating cooling gas for cooling temperature close to 58 degrees C, as the material moves toward the tail and is in full contact with the cooling gas and carbamate in wet materials, carrying out relatively slow hydrolysis of ammonium producing ammonium bicarbonate by reacting one mole of ammonium carbamate with two moles of water and one mole of carbon-dioxide to obtain two moles of ammonium bicarbonate, passing reactive heat heating cycle cooling gas, heating gas, passing a part of the moisture in the wet material to circulating cooling gas to reach near saturation state, discharging from the tail of the dryer, cooling down, after the water mist is separated, conveying into dryer by a circulation fan to continue the reciprocating cycle.
