   .. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a gas separation membrane comprises polymer membrane;
   (2) a method for producing carbon dioxide, which involves bringing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide into contact with one end of the polymer membrane, selectively transferring carbon dioxide in the mixed gas to the other end side and separating carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide;
   (3) a method for separating carbon dioxide, which involves introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide into the primary side communicating with the inside of the hollow fiber membrane of the hollow fiber membrane module and introducing carbon dioxide in the mixed gas into the hollow fiber membrane, and permeating the carbon dioxide to the next side;

The average weight molecular weight of the water-soluble polymer is 2000-1000000. The water-soluble polymer and the amine compound are dissolved using one solvent selected from the group consisting of water, methanol, ethanol, propanol, acetone, dimethylacetamide, and tetrahydrofuran. The aqueous solution is brought into contact with the inner membrane side of the hollow fiber membrane of the hollow fiber membrane module, and then the hollow fiber membrane module is dried. The hollow fiber membrane is a UF membrane. The polymer membrane is formed in the inner-membrane or film side of a hollow fiber membrane. The membrane separation is expected as a next generation carbon dioxide separation and recovery technology. The mixed gas containing a carbon dioxideis introduce into the primaryside which leads in the hollow fibermembrane of the hollow fiber membrane module. The separation or isolation method of the carbon dioxide characterized by including the process which makes the secondary side which leads to the outer periphery space of a hollow fiber membrane permeate or transmit the carbon dioxide in mixed gas.

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model model claims a calcining device for reducing carbon content in nickel cobalt oxide, wherein it comprises a carbon dioxide capturing device and a calcining device main body, the carbon dioxide capturing device is set in the calcining device main body or the air inlet of the calcining device. The alkaline molecular sieve will react with the carbon dioxide in the air, greatly reducing the content of carbon dioxide in the air passing through the filter, so as to reduce the contact and carbon dioxide in the process of heating and calcining. the filter can be recycled by changing the molecular sieve in the filter, the replaced molecular sieve is modified by dilute sulphuric acid leaching and re-diluted sodium hydroxide solution, it can be used repeatedly.

GA 202269554U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing gasoline fraction hydrocarbons by hydrogenating carbon dioxide involves subjecting raw material gas contains hydrogen and carbon dioxide to reactor provided with composite catalyst to obtain final product, where composite catalyst comprises first component and second component; first component comprises active component and auxiliary agent, where auxiliary agent is loaded on active component, which is iron oxide (Fe3O4) nanometer micro-sphere; particle diameter of iron oxide nanometer microsphere is 100-1000 nanometer (nm), and second component includes acidic molecular sieve.
   USE - Method for preparing gasoline fraction hydrocarbon by carbon dioxide hydrogenation used in catalytic synthesis field for rich carbide fuels such as coal, petroleum and natural gas, promotes human civilization, economic and social development.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to provide high reaction activity, high selectivity of carbon dioxide and methane in reaction process of existing technology in carbon dioxide hydrogenation preparation of gasoline fraction hydrocarbon, where synthesized hydrocarbon product has low octane number, poor stability of catalyst, and has good industrial application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of first component to second component is 1:10-10:1, preferably 1:3-3:1. The silicon-aluminum ratio of acidic molecular sieve is 5-570. The molar ratio of ferric salt to alkaline precipitant is 1:2-8; solid-to-liquid ratio of iron salt to solvent is 1 g:10-50 mL. The reaction conditions are: molar ratio of hydrogen (H2) to carbon dioxide (CO2) is 1-8:1, reaction temperature is 240-400degrees Celsius, reaction pressure is 0.1-10 Megapascal (MPa), and space velocity is 500-30000 mL/(hour.g)cat), preferably molar ratio is 2-4: 1, reaction temperature is 280-350degrees Celsius, reaction pressure is 1-4 MPa, and space velocity is 2000-10000 mL/(hour.g)cat). The reduction conditions are: reducing in situ for 2-24 hours at 300-500degrees Celsius in reducing atmosphere, preferably reducing atmosphere is selected from H2 and carbon monoxide (CO).Preferred Components: The auxiliary agent is selected from oxides of alkali metal or alkaline earth metal or cobalt (Co), copper (Cu), zinc (Zn), zirconium (Zr), silicon (Si), aluminum (Al) and cerium (Ce), where content of auxiliary agent is 0.1-30% of total mass of first component, and content of auxiliary agent is calculated by mass of metal elements in auxiliary agent, preferably content of auxiliary agent is 0.1-10% of total mass of first component. The alkali metal is selected from at least one of lithium (Li), sodium (Na), potassium (K), rubidium (Rb), cesium (Cs). The alkaline earth metal is selected from at least one of magnesium (Mg), calcium (Ca), barium (Ba) and strontium (Sr). The acidic molecular sieve is selected from at least one of high-silica pentasil zeolite socony mobil-5 (HZSM-5), HZSM-23, HSAPO-11, HY, HBeta, HMOR, HMCM-22 molecular sieves. The composite catalyst further comprises reduction step before use. The iron salt is selected from ferric salts; ferric salt is selected from at least one of ferric chloride, ferric sulfate, ferric acetate and ferric nitrate.Preferred Composition: The mixed solution comprises 1-6 wt.% surfactant.Preferred Method: The method for preparing Fe3O4 nano-microsphere by solvothermal method involves reacting mixed solution contains ferric salt, alkaline precipitator, solvent and surfactant for 2-24 hours at 150-220degrees Celsius in closed container to obtain Fe3O4 nano microspheres; soaking Fe3O4 nano microspheres in solution contains auxiliary agent salt, drying and roasting to obtain Fe3O4 nano-microspheres contains auxiliary agent, where auxiliary salt is at least one of chloride, nitrate and acetate of alkali metal or alkaline earth metal or Co, Cu, Zn, Zr, Si, Al and Ce.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is selected from ethylene glycol; alkaline precipitator is selected from at least one of sodium acetate, sodium oxalate and sodium citrate; surfactant is at least one selected from polyethylene glycol, polyethyleneimine and polyvinylpyrrolidone.

PN CN113087065-A
TI Removing lead ion ability by using carbon dioxide to improve adsorbent involves placing adsorbent in adsorption reactor, introducing carbon dioxide gas into reaction system, converting precipitation into basic lead carbonate removal.
AU YU Y
   SHANG H
   LI Y
   FENG Y
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 202180270G
AB    NOVELTY - Removing lead ion ability by using carbon dioxide to improve the adsorbent involves placing adsorbent in the adsorption reactor or the stirring reactor. The carbon dioxide gas is introduced into the reaction system. The precipitation is converted into basic lead carbonate removal, where the adsorbent has the ability to produce a precipitate by ion exchange and the pH of the water body is adjusted.
   USE - Method for removing lead ion ability by using carbon dioxide to improve the adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses carbon dioxide to promote adsorption, shortens the processing time, increases the particle diameter, so as to facilitate separation, improve the processing effect, expand the application range, according to the performance of the adsorbent, the solution is only limited to alkaline heavy metal wastewater is expanded to neutral and acidic wastewater treatment, at the same time, introducing carbon dioxide, which is good for improving adsorption rate, so that the process of depositing and forming larger particles is more rapid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The adsorbent is modified carbon. The adsorption reactor is a fixed bed reactor or a fluidized bed reactor. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide gas and lead-containing wastewater are mixed in the gas-liquid mixing device, then the adsorbent-loaded adsorption reactor or stirring reactor is introduced for heavy metal adsorption, the water is filtered into the gas-liquid mixing to obtain the product, then carbon dioxide gas is introduced into the gas-liquid mixing device for precipitation conversion, and then absorbed by the adsorption reactor or membrane separation reactor for solid-liquid separation, then the water is discharged. The lead-containing wastewater is filled into the stirring reactor filled with adsorbent, and then the carbon dioxide gas is pumped to stir and adsorb, and the water can be filtered out.

PN CN111298762-A; CN111298762-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide modified fly ash-based adsorbent used for treating arsenic ion-containing solutions, by mixing and heating fly ash and water, introducing carbon dioxide to modification reaction, and drying.
AU ZHANG L
   MIAO X
   FU X
   WANG Y
   GAN M
   LI X
AE INST ROCK & SOIL MECHANICS CHINESE ACAD (CRSM-C)
GA 202058189R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a carbon dioxide modified fly ash-based adsorbent involves (a) mixing and heating fly ash and water, (b) introducing carbon dioxide to carry out the modification reaction in a closed environment, and (c) drying to obtain carbon dioxide-modified fly ash-based adsorbent.
   USE - The method is used for preparing a carbon dioxide modified fly ash-based adsorbent which is used for treating arsenic ion-containing solutions (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the porosity, specific surface area and connectivity of fly ash, enhances the adsorption of free arsenic ions in the solution by fly ash, forms calcium carbonate when carbonic acid reacts with calcium oxide, has excellent an adsorption effect on free arsenic ions in solution, and effectively enhances the adsorption capacity of fly ash to free arsenic ions in solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a use of carbon dioxide modified fly ash-based adsorbent for treating arsenic ion-containing solutions obtained by the above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fly ash is fly ash discharged from coal-fired power plant. The carbon dioxide is carbon dioxide emitted from coal-fired power plant. Preferred Composition: The fly ash comprises greater than or equal to 10 %mass calcium oxide. Preferred Properties: The particle size of the fly ash is not greater than 0.1 mm. The pH value of arsenic ion-containing solution is 6-7, the concentration of arsenic ions is not greater than 3 mg/l, and the temperature is 20-30 degrees C. Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature in step (b) is 50-60 degrees C, the time is 2-4 hours, and the pressure is 1-2 MPa. The reaction temperature in step (b) is room temperature, the time is 1-2 hours, and the pressure is normal pressure.

TI Preparing high-purity valeric acid trimester comprises e.g. providing Valeriana jatamansi, removing impurities, drying, pulverizing, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and performing separation.
AU LI J
   PU S
AE SHURELI BIOPHARMA CO LTD (SHUR-Non-standard)
GA 201953896M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity valeric acid trimester comprises (A) providing Valeriana jatamansi, removing impurities, drying and pulverizing to obtain material a, (B) placing material a in the extraction vessel of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, maintaining extraction temperature at 50-55 degrees C, extraction pressure at 30-35 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate at 30-50 Kg/h, extracting to obtain material b, transfering material b to primary separation tank and maintaining supercritical state of carbon dioxide, (C) subjecting material b to primary separation in supercritical state of carbon dioxide at 40-45oC at 10-15 MPa to obtain target high-purity valeric acid and supercritical carbon dioxide in which volatile oil is dissolved, and (D) subjecting supercritical carbon dioxide in which the volatile oil is dissolved to secondary separation at 20-25 degrees C at 2-5 MPa to obtain a volatile oil.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity valeric acid trimester.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical; and realizes purity up to 91%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (A), removing dirt and stone impurities in Valeriana jatamansi, drying naturally or drying at constant temperature, and pulverizing using high-speed pulverizer.

TI Synthesizing methanol, comprises washing out carbon dioxide from gas stream with hydrogen and carbon dioxide, feeding gas stream to a reactor arrangement for catalytic and feeding carbon oxides into gas mixture with water and methanol.
AU WAGNER U
   BALTHASAR W
   MUELLER D
AE WAGNER U (WAGN-Individual)
   BALTHASAR W (BALT-Individual)
   MUELLER D (MUEL-Individual)
GA 201813367J
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing methanol, comprises at least partially washing out the carbon dioxide from a gas stream with hydrogen and carbon dioxide, by a washing medium, feeding the gas stream to a reactor arrangement for the catalytic and at least partially converting hydrogen after washing and feeding carbon oxides into a gas mixture with water and methanol. The washing medium comprises an aqueous solution of ammonia for washing out the carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method synthesizes methanol in environmental friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for plant for the synthesis of methanol with a scrubber for the washing out of carbon dioxide by a scrubbing medium from a gas stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide, comprising a reactor arrangement, where the gas stream is supplied after the scrubber for the catalytic and at least partial conversion of hydrogen and carbon oxides into a gas mixture with water and methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: A separation arrangement of the reactor arrangement releases an unreacted residual gas with hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide for separating a crude methanol stream with water and methanol from the gas mixture. A recycle gas stream with at least part of the residual gas is fed to the reactor arrangement for conversion. The gas stream at least partially comprises the recycle gas stream such that the carbon dioxide of the residual gas is at least partially washed out by the washing medium. The reactor arrangement has a number of process-technically serially arranged reactor stages, respectively for the catalytic and at least partial reaction of hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the gas mixture with water and methanol, preferably the separation arrangement is downstream of the last reactor stage of the reactor arrangement. The deposition arrangement is arranged between two reactor stages of the reactor arrangement, where a first residual gas stream of the residual gas is fed downstream of the separation arrangement for reaction and a second residual gas stream of the residual gas is fed to the reaction stage upstream of the separation arrangement, where the gas stream contains the first residual gas stream or the second residual gas stream. The gas stream is supplied downstream of a synthesis gas compressor for compressing the gas stream to wash out, where the synthesis gas compressor is directly upstream of the reactor arrangement in terms of process technology. The hydrocarbons having at least two carbon atoms of the energy carrier stream are split into methane, hydrogen and carbon dioxide in a pre-reformer of the reformer arrangement, where the splitting of the hydrocarbons in the pre-reformer is carried out in an adiabatic reaction at a temperature of 400-500 degrees C, and at a pressure of 25-30 bar. The washing medium comprises an aqueous solution of ammonia and carbon dioxide, preferably the molar ratio of ammonia to carbon dioxide in the solution is 1-4, preferably the molar ratio of ammonia to carbon dioxide in the solution is 1.5-3. The gas stream is at least partially fed to an absorption stage for receiving the carbon dioxide in the washing medium, preferably the washing medium in a washing arrangement containing the absorption stage and a regeneration stage for the release of carbon dioxide from the washing medium is driven in a circuit. The carbon dioxide is released from the washing medium in the regeneration stage at a substantially constant volume, preferably at constant pressure of the washing medium. The washing arrangement has a heating device for heating the washing medium such that the washing medium has a higher temperature in the regeneration stage than in the absorption stage, such that the carbon dioxide in the regeneration stage is discharged at a higher partial pressure than the carbon dioxide of the gas stream in the absorption stage. The carbon dioxide released in the regeneration stage is injected for storage in an underground deposit.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 202275597P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves delivering fuel gas to an anode of the solid oxide fuel cell. The fuel gas and the oxygen-containing gas is generated electricity, and a process gas is discharged. The electrochemical is reacted to the fuel gas and the oxygen-containing gas is generated the electricity, at the same time process gas is discharged. The process gas is compressed, and then the process gas is burnt in the presence of carbon dioxide circulating fluid to obtain combustion gas stream containing carbon dioxide. The combustion air flow containing the carbon dioxide is expanded to generate electricity to obtain mixed pressure reducing air flow. The carbon dioxide is separated from the mixed pressure to reduce air flow to form a carbon dioxide circulation fluid, and carbon dioxide circulate fluid is conveyed to process gas combustion operation.
   USE - Power generation method using integrated gasification fuel cell (IGFC) system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables combining the solid oxide fuel cell and the Alame cycle, so that the Alaam cycle can fully utilize the temperature of the unreacted fuel gas and process gas in the process gas to generate electricity, thus improving the power generation efficiency of the fuel.

   USE - Anti-carbon deposition type methane carbon                dioxide reforming catalyst for use in methane                carbon dioxide reforming reaction process                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon deposition on the surface of the                catalyst is quickly eliminated in the reforming                reaction process of methane and carbon dioxide, so                that the carbon deposition resistance and the                stability of the catalyst are obviously improved,                and the effect of the catalyst for catalyzing the                reforming reaction of methane and carbon dioxide is                better than that of the traditional catalyst for                the reforming reaction of methane and carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing anti-carbon deposition type methane                carbon dioxide reforming catalyst, which involves:                i. taking complexing agent to prepare an aqueous                solution, and sequentially adding aqueous solution                of the soluble salt of the selected element A and                the aqueous solution of the soluble nickel salt,                and mixing well; ii. evaporating the obtained mixed                solution fully to obtain gel, and after drying                treatment, performing high temperature roasting to                obtain perovskite oxide; iii. adding the perovskite                oxide into the auxiliary and mixing well, and                adding cerium salt aqueous solution, and mixing                well; and iv. dehydrating obtained mixed solution                to obtain a gel, drying and calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The complexing agent                  includes citric acid and/or                  ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid. The auxiliary                  agent is citric acid, ethylenediaminetetraacetic                  acid, acrylamide and/or glycine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soluble salts of                  the selected element A include soluble lanthanum                  salts, soluble alkali metal salts and/or soluble                  alkaline earth metal salts. The soluble lanthanum                  salt is lanthanum(iii) chloride, lanthanum(iii)                  nitrate and/or lanthanum sulfate. The alkali                  metal salt is sodium nitrate, sodium chloride,                  sodium sulfite, potassium nitrate, potassium                  chloride and/or potassium sulfite. The alkaline                  earth metal salt is magnesium nitrate, barium                  nitrate, calcium nitrate, and/or strontium                  nitrate. The soluble nickel salt is nickel                  nitrate and/or nickel(ii) acetate. The cerium                  salt is cerium nitrate, cerium acetate and/or                  cerium carbonate. Preferred Composition: The                  molar ratio of the total amount of metal ions in                  the soluble nickel salt to the total amount of                  metal ions in the soluble salt of the selected                  element A is 1:1. The molar ratio of the total                  amount of metal ions in the soluble salt of the                  selected element A and the soluble nickel salt to                  the complexing agent is 1:0.5-1:3. The molar                  ratio of the total amount of the soluble salt of                  the selected element A, the soluble nickel salt                  and the metal ion in the cerium salt to the                  auxiliary agent is 1:1-1:4. In the catalyst, the                  mass content of the cerium dioxide is 10-30                  wt.%.Preferred Method: The method for preparing                  anti-carbon deposition type methane carbon                  dioxide reforming catalyst involves evaporation                  treatment at 50-80 &#176;C, the temperature                  of the drying treatment step is 80-200                  &#176;C, the temperature of the                  high-temperature roasting step is 300-1200                  &#176;C. The step of removing the moisture to                  obtain the gel involves sol-gel method: the                  obtained mixture is evaporated water at 60-90                  &#176;C to obtain a gel or solvothermal                  method: after the obtained mixture is subjected                  to hydrothermal reaction at 80-150 &#176;C,                  the water is evaporated to dryness to obtain a                  gel. The temperature of the drying step is 80-120                  &#176;C, the temperature of the roasting step                  is 300-1000 &#176;C.

PN US2022098504-A1; WO2022232896-A1
TI Producing synthesis gas involves extracting and            collecting gases containing carbon dioxide from a            source, cleaning gases containing carbon dioxide of            impurities if required in downstream process            operations, pressuring gases and feeding into heated            pressurized catalytic reactor.
AU KELLY K S
   MELNICHUK L J
AE KELLY K S (KELL-Individual)
   MELNICHUK L J (MELN-Individual)
   KELLY K S (KELL-Individual)
GA 2022460290
AB    NOVELTY - Producing synthesis gas involves extracting                and collecting gases containing at least carbon                dioxide from a source, cleaning the gases                containing at least carbon dioxide of impurities if                required in downstream process operations, and                pressuring the gases containing at least carbon                dioxide and feeding into a heated pressurized                catalytic reactor. The gas stream containing at                least gaseous hydrocarbons of impurities is cleaned                if required in downstream process operations,                pressurized and fed into the heated pressurized                catalytic reactor. A calculated volume of steam is                added to the reactor to promote the reactions to                produce a synthesis gas containing at least carbon                monoxide and hydrogen in a ratio which favors the                production of an end product. The formed synthesis                gas containing at least the desired ratio of carbon                monoxide to hydrogen is fed into to a reactor or                series of reactors to produce an end product                comprising at least carbon from the input carbon                dioxide.
   USE - Method for producing synthesis gas comprising                at least carbon from carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method helps in reducing the carbon                footprint of the entire process, and reducing the                carbon intensity of all products. The method is                scalable, economical, efficient, clean, internal                use of energy significantly reduces or eliminates                the need for external energy, rendering the process                environment friendly and enhancing the carbon index                of the products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                process for the creation of synthesis gas                comprising substituted fossil carbon using carbon                dioxide as a feedstock.
   Carbon dioxide (1)
   torage (3)
   Reformer (4)
   contaminants (6)
   external heat source (9)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The gases containing                  at least carbon dioxide are produced by anaerobic                  digestion or fermentation or are captured from a                  synthesis process. The gases containing at least                  gaseous hydrocarbons are provided directly from a                  gas well or from a pipeline. The gases containing                  at least gaseous hydrocarbons is synthetic                  natural gas produced from biomass or coal. The                  steam is fed into the reactor at a temperature                  range of 210-225degrees Celsius and preferably                  215degrees Celsius, and at a pressure of 1.5-2.0                  MPa and preferably at 1.9 MPa. The first reactor                  produces methanol or a mixture of methanol and                  dimethyl ether comprising at least carbon from                  carbon dioxide and the second reactor produces                  gasoline-range hydrocarbons comprising at least                  carbon from carbon dioxide. The first reactor                  produces a mixture of methanol and dimethyl ether                  comprising at least carbon from carbon dioxide                  which is separated into methanol and dimethyl                  ether. The methanol is converted in a second                  reactor to olefins, and the dimethyl ether is                  converted in a third reactor into hydrocarbons                  and aromatics and the olefins, and hydrocarbons                  and aromatics are blended into jet fuel                  comprising at least carbon from carbon                  dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. TI Separating gaseous carbon dioxide from gas mixture            involves cyclic adsorption/desorption using unit            containing adsorber structure with sorbent material,            where unit is openable to flow-through of gas mixture            and for contacting it with material.
AU REPOND N
   MEGERLE B
AE CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
GA 2021D9370W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves contacting a gas mixture                with a sorbent material to allow gaseous carbon                dioxide to adsorb on the material under ambient                atmospheric pressure conditions and ambient                atmospheric temperature conditions in an adsorption                step. The sorbent is isolated with adsorbed carbon                dioxide in a unit (1,2) from the flow through of                gas mixture while maintaining the temperature in                the sorbent. A stream of saturated steam is                injected essentially at ambient atmospheric                conditions. The material is brought to ambient                atmospheric condition conditions. The speed of                steam flow through the unit is in the range of                0.5-10 times.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide from                ambient atmospheric air using cyclic                adsorption-desorption process. Can also be used in                direct air capture (DAC).
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively captures carbon dioxide                from ambient atmospheric air using a flow of steam                at atmospheric pressure conditions. The sorbent has                a process cyclical carbon dioxide capacity in the                range of 0.3-3 mmol/g and/or a water uptake of less                than 70%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for separating gaseous carbon                dioxide from a gas mixture; and
   (2) a device for carrying out a method for                separating gaseous carbon dioxide from a gas                mixture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                method for capturing carbon dioxide from ambient                atmospheric air using cyclic adsorption-desorption                process.
   Unit (1,2)
   Bypass conduit (3)
   Second absorber structure (6)
   Stream source (8)
   Condenser (9)

TI Apparatus for mixing carbon dioxide with ultrapure water, has control unit that compares first purity measured by first circulation line purity measurement unit with second purity, to determine whether first and second purity are different.
AU HANTAEHEE
AE JOOYOUNG CS CO LTD (JOOY-Non-standard)
GA 2021C89013
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a first circulation line purity measuring unit (26) for measuring the purity of the mixed water. The flow control valves (43,44) are provided on the carbon dioxide supply lines (121,122a,122b) to control the supply amount of the carbon dioxide. The supply amount of the carbon dioxide supplied through the flow control valve is adjusted so that the purity of the mixed water measured by the first circulation line purity measurement unit becomes a set purity. A second circulation line purity measurement unit (31) for measuring the purity of the remaining mixed water supplied to the processing apparatus is provided on the circulation line connected to the rear end of a processing apparatus inlet (4a). The control unit compares the first purity measured by the first circulation line purity measurement unit with the second purity measured by the second circulation line purity measurement unit to determine whether the first purity and the second purity are different.
   USE - Apparatus for mixing carbon dioxide with ultrapure water used for cleaning in semiconductor manufacturing process.
   ADVANTAGE - The device maintains purity of ultrapure water mixed with carbon dioxide at desired purity, and minimizes defect rate.

   Processing apparatus inlet (4a)
   Circulation line purity measurement units (26,31)

   Carbon dioxide supply lines (121,122a,122b)

PT P
PN US2021301633-A1; WO2021201891-A1
TI Method for perforating a downhole formation for            producing hydrocarbon fluids from subterranean            formations in an oil and gas industry, involves            detonating carbon dioxide filled units to perforate            surfaces selected from group consisting of wellbore            casing, cement, and down hole formation.
AU ALRUWAILI K M
   ALNOAIMI K R
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2021B1991E
AB    NOVELTY - The downhole formation method involves                attaching a carbon dioxide perforating device (20)                to a wireline, where the device comprises carbon                dioxide filled perforation units. The carbon                dioxide perforating device is arranged at a depth                within a wellbore. The carbon dioxide filled                perforating units are detonated to perforate                surfaces selected from a group consisting of a well                bore casing, cement, and a downhole formation. A                position of the carbon dioxide perforating device                is stabilized within the well bore, and the carbon                dioxide filled perforating units are radially                positioned within the well bore. The devices are                detonated simultaneously, and are configured to                discharge the units at different pressures.
   USE - Method for perforating a downhole formation                for producing hydrocarbon fluids from subterranean                formations in an oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables detonating the carbon                dioxide filled perforating units to perforate the                surfaces selected from the group consisting of the                wellbore casing, cement, and the downhole                formation, thus allowing multiple zones of a well                to be stimulated at once, and hence increasing the                production of hydrocarbons in a cost effective                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for:
   (a) a system for perforating a downhole                formation for producing hydrocarbon fluids from                subterranean formations in an oil and gas                industry.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a crocc-sectional view of a                carbon dioxide perforating device.
   Filled perforating device (20)
   Electric charge (22)
   Heat source (24)
   Discharge head (27)
   Discharge outlet (28)
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

TI Use of aluminum porphyrin oligomer as catalyst in preparation of unsaturated carbon dioxide-based polyols.
AU CAO H
   GONG R
   ZHOU Z
   WANG X
   WANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202199506M
AB    NOVELTY - Use of aluminum porphyrin oligomer (I) as catalyst in preparation of unsaturated carbon dioxide-based polyols, is claimed.
   USE - (I) is useful as catalyst in preparation of unsaturated carbon dioxide-based polyols (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has excellent catalytic activity, polyol selectivity and proton tolerance; breaks through the limitation of itaconic acid acidity and low-temperature polymerization; produces efficiently and controllably synthesize unsaturated carbon dioxide-based polyols with active double bonds; provides a convenient and feasible control platform for the synthesis of multiple functional carbon dioxide-based polyurethanes; and ensures the use method is simple and easy to promote.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of aluminum porphyrin oligomer of formula (I) as catalyst in preparation of unsaturated carbon dioxide-based polyols, is claimed.
   X = halo, -NO3, CH3C(=O)O-, CCl3C(=O)O-, CF3C(=O)O-, ClO4-, BF4-, BPh4-, -CN, -N3, p-methylbenzoate, p-toluenesulfonate, O-nitrophenol oxygen anion, p-nitrophenol oxygen anion, m-nitrophenol oxygen anion, 2,4-dinitrophenol oxygen anion, 3,5-dinitrophenol oxygen anion, 2,4,6-trinitrophenol oxygen anion, 3,5-dichlorophenol oxygen anion, 3,5-difluorophenol oxygen anion, 3,5-bistrifluoromethyl phenol oxygen anion or pentafluorophenyl phenol oxygen anion;
   R1 = 1-10C aliphatic groups or 6-30C aryl groups (both optionally substituted), H, halo, amino, nitro or cyano;
   n = 4-20;
   x, y = 2-25; and
   R = H, 1-10C alkyl, phenyl, chloroalkyl, cyclohexyl or cyclopentyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing unsaturated carbon dioxide-based polyols, comprising performing telomerization preferably epoxides with carbon dioxide in presence of (I) as catalyst and itaconic acid as an initiator to obtain final product; #unsaturated carbon dioxide-based polyols of formula (II).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: (II) is ultra-low molecular weight unsaturated carbon dioxide-based polyol. (II) contains carbonate segments of 10-70%. The molecular weight of (I) is 1000-5000 g/mol. The epoxide is composed of ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, 1-butylene oxide, 2-epoxybutane, epoxycyclohexane, epoxycyclopentane, epichlorohydrin, glycidyl methacrylate, methyl glycidyl ether, phenyl glycidyl ether, styrene alkylene oxide, 4-vinyl-1,2-epoxycyclohexane and/or vinyl propylene oxide. The molar ratio of catalyst to initiator is 50000:10-50. The molar ratio of epoxide to initiator is 1:10-50. (I) is aluminum porphyrin oligomer of formulae (Ia)-(Ib). Preferred Method: In the use method, the pressure of the carbon dioxide is 0.1-10 MPa; the telomerization reaction is performed at 25-80 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours; and the content of the by-product cyclic carbonate of the telomerization reaction is less than 1%.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R = methyl.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining the equipment                performance, where the five key parameters are                provided. The liquid carbon dioxide plunger pump                outlet pressure, the liquid carbon dioxide plunger                pump inlet pressure, the liquid carbon dioxide                plunger pump outlet flow, the liquid carbon dioxide                plunger pump return pressure and a high-pressure                nitrogen cylinders number are provided. The                horizontal principal stress in a borehole is                calculated according to a formula, where the                circumferential stress is caused by a hole wall.                The another circumferential stress caused by the                liquid carbon dioxide injection pressure in a coal                seam is calculated at the distance from a borehole                center according to the formula. The radius of the                borehole is provided. The temperature inside a                high-pressure nitrogen bottle is provided.
   USE - Method for designing the downhole                high-pressure liquid carbon dioxide fracturing and                anti-reflection coal and a rock complete                equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - The horizontal principal stress in a borehole                is calculated according to a formula, where the                circumferential stress is caused by a hole wall,                and hence ensures increasing the performance while                designing the downhole high-pressure liquid carbon                dioxide fracturing and anti-reflection coal and                reduces the pressure in the fracturing construction                caused by a jet hole.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a method for                designing the downhole high-pressure liquid carbon                dioxide fracturing and anti-reflection coal and a                rock complete equipment. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

TI Control method of air conditioner in passenger rail vehicle, involves generating control command according to command parameter data and controlling operation of vehicle air conditioner according to control command.
AU ZHANG Q
   YU Y
AE TCT CO LTD (TCTT-C)
GA 202030390D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting (S102) the temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide concentration in a vehicle compartment using an infrared temperature sensor, a humidity sensor and a carbon dioxide sensor. A command parameter data including temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide concentration of the vehicle compartment collected by the infrared temperature sensor, humidity sensor and carbon dioxide sensor is acquired (S104). A control command including a temperature control command and a wind control command is generated (S106) according to the command parameter data. The operation of the vehicle air conditioner is controlled according to the control command.
   USE - Control method of air conditioner in passenger rail vehicle such as train and subway vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The temperature and/or ventilation of vehicle air conditioner are intelligently controlled. The influence of factors such as temperature, carbon dioxide concentration and humidity on the mid-height position of the concentrated passenger compartment on passenger comfort are considered and the passenger comfort experience is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a control system of air conditioner in rail vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for controlling air conditioner of rail vehicle. (Drawing includes non- English language text)
   Step for collecting temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide concentration in vehicle compartment using infrared temperature sensor, humidity sensor and carbon dioxide sensor (S102)
   Step for acquiring command parameter data including temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide concentration of vehicle compartment collected by infrared temperature sensor, humidity sensor and carbon dioxide sensor (S104)
   Step for generating control command according to command parameter data and controlling operation of vehicle air conditioner according to control command (S106)

PN GB2576762-A; WO2020044048-A1; GB2576762-B; IN202117014012-A; EP3844107-A1; SG11202101999-A1; US2021253438-A1; US11434142-B2
TI Production of sodium bicarbonate e.g. raising            agent, involves contacting carbon dioxide with aqueous            sodium carbonate solution to form slurry, and            separating solid component from liquid component to            form sodium bicarbonate and liquor.
AU FOXLEY C
   IRAVANIAN L
   HOUGHTON P
AE TATA CHEM EURO LTD (TTTA-C)
   TATA CHEM EURO LTD (TTTA-C)
GA 202019004B
AB    NOVELTY - Production of sodium bicarbonate involves (a)                treating a carbonaceous feedstock (2) to form a                product stream comprising up to 10                (volume)/(volume)% carbon dioxide, (b) capturing                portion(s) of the carbon dioxide from the product                stream to form a carbon dioxide stream, (c) feeding                the carbon dioxide stream to a reaction vessel                (34), (d) feeding an aqueous sodium carbonate                solution (26) to the reaction vessel, (e)                contacting portion(s) of the carbon dioxide stream                with portion(s) of the aqueous sodium carbonate                solution to form a slurry comprising solid sodium                bicarbonate, and (f) separating the solid component                of the slurry from the liquid component of the                slurry to provide sodium bicarbonate and an aqueous                liquor (52).
   USE - Production of sodium bicarbonate used for                raising agent in food application and                haemo-dialysis in medical application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is energy efficient and performed                in single plant or using integrally connected                vessels or plants, and provides sodium bicarbonate                having excellent purity with low content of carbon                dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                illustrating production of sodium                bicarbonate.
   Carbonaceous feedstock (2)
   Carbonaceous material source (4)
   Water stream (8)
   Steam stream (10)
   Condensate streams (12,16,20)
   Portions of steam stream (14,18,22)
   Aqueous sodium carbonate solution (26)
   Liquid carbon dioxide stream (30)
   Vaporized carbon dioxide stream (32)
   Reaction vessel (34)
   Sodium carbonate feed (36)
   Slurry stream (50)
   Liquor (52)
   Slurry cake (54)
   Liquor stream (56)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  the carbon dioxide in the product stream is 1-10                  (volume)/(volume)%, or 2-9 (volume)/(volume)%, or                  3-8 (volume)/(volume)%, or 4-7                  (volume)/(volume)%, or 5-6 (volume)/(volume)%.                  The step (b) captures at least 99.9                  (volume)/(volume)% carbon dioxide, at most 20 ppm                  moisture content, at most 2.5 ppm ammonia, at                  most 30 ppm oxygen, at most 2.5 ppm nitric oxide,                  at most 2.5 ppm nitrogen dioxide, at most 10 ppm                  non-volatile residue (particulate), at most 5 ppm                  non-volatile organic residue (oil and grease), at                  most 50 ppm volatile hydrocarbon (methane), at                  most 0.2 ppm acetaldehyde, at most 0.02 ppm                  aromatic hydrocarbon, at most 10 ppm carbon                  monoxide, at most 10 ppm methanol, at most 0.1                  ppm carbonyl sulfide, at most 0.1 ppm hydrogen                  sulfide, and at most 1 ppm sulfur dioxide. In                  step (c), the concentration of the carbon dioxide                  is 20-90 (volume)/(volume)%, preferably 40-45                  (volume)/(volume)%. Preferred Process: The step                  (a) involves combusting the feedstock in a                  heating and power plant, and producing heat in                  which portion(s) of the heat is used to vaporize                  a water stream (8) to form a steam stream, in                  which the portion(s) of the steam stream is                  condensed to provide a condensate stream (12).                  The production method optionally involves                  supplying water from the stream (12) to stream                  (8) and the aqueous sodium carbonate solution,                  heating the liquor to convert portion(s) of the                  aqueous sodium bicarbonate to aqueous sodium                  carbonate, carbon dioxide, and water or cooling                  the liquor to precipitate sodium bicarbonate,                  collecting the portion(s) of the carbon dioxide                  by heating the liquor to form carbon dioxide                  off-gas which is cooled using a water stream (S2)                  to produce cooled carbon dioxide stream and a                  heated water stream, and further involves using                  portion(s) of the heated water stream to form the                  stream (8). The step (b) involves contacting the                  product stream with a solvent, absorbing carbon                  dioxide from the product stream into the solvent                  to form a carbon dioxide-enriched solvent, and                  desorbing carbon dioxide from the carbon                  dioxide-enriched solvent to form carbon dioxide                  stream. The desorbing process involves heating                  the solvent and subjecting the carbon                  dioxide-enriched solvent to reduced pressure. The                  carbon dioxide is formed prior to feed into the                  vessel by diluting the captured carbon dioxide                  with a diluent gas optionally comprising nitrogen                  or air. The carbon dioxide-off gas is cooled                  using a water stream (S2) to produce cooled                  carbon dioxide stream and a heated water stream.                  Preferred Components: The carbonaceous feedstock                  is a hydrocarbon feedstock, preferably natural                  gas. The portion(s) of the steam stream generates                  electricity which is used to power the apparatus                  for process steps, and used to heat the solvent,                  and optionally provision of the heat to the                  solvent by the steam stream condenses portion(s)                  of the steam stream to form condensate stream                  (16) and condensate stream (20). The stream (16)                  provides water for stream (8) and the aqueous                  sodium carbonate solution. The captured carbon                  dioxide is liquefied. The liquor comprises                  aqueous sodium bicarbonate and aqueous sodium                  carbonate. The stream (20) provides water for                  stream (8) and the aqueous sodium carbonate                  solution. The portion(s) of residual carbon                  dioxide which does not react with the aqueous                  sodium bicarbonate solution in the vessel is                  formed into a carbon dioxide waste stream. The                  carbon dioxide from the cooled carbon dioxide                  stream and waste stream is recycled by feeding                  into the vessel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent comprises                  alkanolamine chosen from N-methyldiethanolamine,                  2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol,                  2-diethylamino-ethanol, diisopropanolamine,                  methylaminopropylamine, 3-aminopropanol,                  2,2-dimethyl-1,3-propanediamine,                  3-amino-1-cyclohexylaminopropane, diglycolamine,                  1-amino-2-propanol, 2-methyl-methanolamine,                  monoethanolamine, and diglycolamine.
DC B06 (Inorganics - including fluorides for toothpastes etc.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Carbon dioxide fluidity control device comprises            high pressure stirring unit, reciprocating plunger            pump, booster pump, high pressure stirring tank,            plunger pump and atomization spray probe.
AU ZHANG C
   LI Z
   LI S
   WANG Y
   LV G
   WU S

   WANG M
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM (UYPE-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2019628727
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide fluidity control device                comprises sample tank (1), high pressure stirring                unit (7), reciprocating plunger pump and booster                pump (6). The high pressure stirring unit comprises                high pressure stirring tanks. The atomizing spray                probe and piston are disposed in each of the high                pressure stirring tanks. The discharge port of the                sample tank is connected to the atomization spray                probe (8) via a plunger pump (4), and the                reciprocating plunger pump is connected to the                pisto and pushing the piston to reciprocate. The                booster pump is in communication with the high                pressure stirring tank for supplying supercritical                carbon dioxide to the high pressure stirred tank.                The discharge port of the high pressure stirred                tank is connected to the oil well group.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide fluidity control                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: enhance stability of the foam, and                improves timeliness of its control of carbon                dioxide flow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                carbon dioxide fluidity controlling comprises (1)                adding surfactant and nanoparticle to the sample                tank to obtain surfactant and a nanoparticle mixed                solution, (2) injecting carbon dioxide into                high-pressure stirring tank through the booster                pump and heating, (3) atomizing mixed solution                prepared through the plunger pump and spraying                through the atomizing spray probe and then spraying                into the high-pressure stirring tank, and stirring                (4) injecting supercritical carbon                dioxide-nanosilica microemulsion into the                subsequent oil well group.

   Plunger pump (4)
   Booster pump (6)

PT P
PN US2018292362-A1; WO2018189654-A1; US10996204-B2
TI Method for efficiently transferring carbon dioxide based separation procedure from reference chromatographic system, involves calculating average column pressure for carbon dioxide based separation in field chromatographic system.
AU MICHIENZI J D
   SHREVE J A
   HILL J F
   TARAFDER A
   FOGWILL M O
   FADGEN K
AE WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
GA 201878239V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves identifying an average system pressure. The tubing is modified between a pump outlet and a column inlet. An average system pressure, an average column pressure, and an elution time for the analyte in the carbon dioxide based separation in the modified reference chromatographic system is identified. An elution time differential and a correction coefficient are calculated for analyte in the carbon dioxide based separation. An average system pressure and an elution time are identified for analyte in the carbon dioxide based separation in a field chromatographic system. An average column pressure for the carbon dioxide is calculated based separation in the field chromatographic system as the product of an average system pressure and the correction factor. The correction factor comprises a function of the elution time, the elution time differential, and the fitting coefficient for the analyte in the carbon dioxide based separation.
   USE - Method for efficiently transferring carbon dioxide based separation procedure from reference chromatographic system to field chromatographic system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method relates to efficiently transferring carbon dioxide based separations between systems. The average column pressure of the target system is greater than the average column pressure in the reference chromatography method, the back pressure regulator in the target system is adjusted to a lower pressure and/or the pump pressure setting in the target system is reduced. An efficiently transferred separation reproduces the chromatographic integrity of the separation obtained on the first chromatographic system on the second chromatographic system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a reference chromatographic system.
   Pump (1060)
   Detector (1070)
   Back pressure regulator (1080)
   Column (1100)

PT P
PN RU2651918-C1
TI Method and plant for mechanical and thermal energy generation.
AU KOSOI A S
   POPEL O S
   SINKEVICH M V
AE AS RUSSIA HIGH TEMP JOINT INST (ARUS-Non-standard)
GA 201841894F
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to heat engineering. Method for mechanical and thermal energy generation is carried out in the plant by directing hot gases from the combustion chamber to the inlet of the steam-gas turbine, after which the gases exhausted in the steam-gas turbine enter the heat and water recycling unit, where they are cooled to the temperature required to separate water from the exhaust gases by condensing it. Condensed water is then withdrawn from the heat and water recycling unit, and the exhaust gases from the heat and water recycling unit containing carbon dioxide as the main constituent are sent to the inlet to the carbon dioxide compressor. Compressed exhaust gas is supplied to the heat and carbon dioxide recycling unit, where it is cooled to the temperature required for the condensation of carbon dioxide. Further, the condensed carbon dioxide is removed from the heat and carbon dioxide recycling unit. Some of the drained water from the heat and water recycling unit is fed to the inlet of a water pump-regulator, which pumps it into the combustion chamber. Some of the carbon dioxide condensed in the heat and carbon dioxide recycling unit is supplied to the inlet of a carbon dioxide pump-regulator, which pumps it into the combustion chamber. Carbon fuel and oxygen are fed into the combustion chamber by a fuel pump-regulator and an oxygen pump-regulator.
   USE - Energy.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention provides increased efficiency of the plant by increasing the average heat input temperature in the thermodynamic cycle, increased heat recovery and obtaining more expansion work, due to the use of condensed CO2 in the power plant.
17 cl, 1 dwg

PN CN113262802-A
TI Preparation of iridium-copper/titania nanosheet catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves dissolving iridium-copper alloy nanoparticles prepared by ethylene glycol reduction, in ethanol, immersing in solution of titania nanosheet and ethanol, evaporating, drying, and grinding.
AU WAN H
   TANG K
   ZOU W
   DONG L
   WU C
   ZHANG L
   XU Y
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
GA 2021992087
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of iridium-copper/titania nanosheet catalyst, involves preparing iridium-copper alloy nanoparticles by ethylene glycol reduction, dissolving the nanoparticles in ethanol to obtain an alloy ethanol solution, immersing the alloy ethanol solution in a solution of titania nanosheet and ethanol, evaporating and drying, and grinding.
   USE - Preparation of iridium-copper/titania nanosheet catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide into methane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides catalyst having excellent activity and methane selectivity in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: Drying is carried out for 5-7 hours at 65-75 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of iridium and copper in the iridium-copper alloy is 1:1-8:1. Preferred Method: The alloy nanoparticles are obtained by adding ethylene glycol to a reaction container, heating at 120-130 degrees C under microwave conditions for 25-35 minutes, adding sodium hydroxide, iridium chloride, and copper chloride, maintaining for 25-35 minutes, cooling quickly, adding acetone to the reaction liquid, centrifuging for 10-20 minutes at 8000-12000 rpm, removing the supernatant, dispersing the residual solid in ethanol, adding b-hexane, centrifuging for 10-20 minutes at 8000-12000 rpm, removing the supernatant, and completely dissolving the solid in ethanol. The volume ratio of reaction liquid and acetone is 1:6.

   ADVANTAGE - The carrier enhances carrier's adsorption of                carbon dioxide, increases conversion rate and                reduces optimal activity temperature. The particles                alleviate problem of easy agglomeration at high                temperature. The catalyst has controllable                morphology, high efficiency and stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalyst is prepared                  by preparing solution containing nickel and                  solvent e.g. mixed solution of oleic acid and                  oleylamine, mixing solution under the protection                  of inert gas at 110-150 degrees C, adding borane                  tributylamine, reacting for 0.5-2 hours,                  centrifuging to obtain nickel nanoparticles,                  preparing solution containing cobalt and solvent                  e.g. mixed solution of ethanol and water,                  reacting at 80-220 degrees C for 2-30 hours,                  washing, drying to obtain precursor of tubular                  cobalt tetroxide, calcining to obtain tubular                  cobalt tetroxide, mixing solution containing                  cerium with cobalt tetroxide at 50-200 degrees C                  for 2-12 hours, washing, drying to obtain cerium                  (IV) oxide-cobalt tetroxide and impregnating                  nickel particles and cerium (IV) oxide-cobalt                  tetroxide. Preferred Components: The nickel                  source of nano nickel particles is                  nickel-containing compound. The nickel-containing                  compound is nickel nitrate, nickel acetate and/or                  nickel acetylacetonate. The cobalt source of                  tubular cobalt tetroxide is cobalt-containing                  compound. The cobalt-containing compound is                  cobalt nitrate and/or cobalt acetate. The cerium                  (IV) oxide modified cerium source for carrier is                  cerium-containing compound. The cerium-containing                  compound is cerium nitrate and/or cerium                  acetate.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Manufacture of 5,5'-methylenedisalicylic acid used            as modifying monomer for resins and stabilizer,            involves reacting bisphenol-F dialkali metal salt with            carbon dioxide in reaction medium.
AU KUNO T
   YANAGAWASE K
AE UENO PHARM CO LTD (UENO-C)
   UENO FINE CHEM IND LTD (UENO-Non-standard)
GA 2020325553
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of 5,5'-methylenedisalicylic acid                (II) involves reacting bisphenol-F dialkali metal                salt (I) with carbon dioxide in a reaction                medium.
   USE - Manufacture of 5,5'-methylenedisalicylic acid                used as modifying monomer for resins and                stabilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of                5,5'-methylenedisalicylic acid (I) having high                purity, high yield, high reactivity and high                reaction selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of 5,5'-methylenedisalicylic acid                of formula (II) involves reacting a bisphenol-F                dialkali metal salt (I) with carbon dioxide in a                reaction medium.
   M = Na, K, Li, Ru or Cs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reaction is carried                  out in the presence of an organic acid and/or an                  organic acid salt. The method further involves                  recrystallizing in the presence of organic                  solvent. Preferred Components: The organic acid                  salt comprises sodium acetate. The reaction                  medium comprises light oil, kerosene, gasoline,                  lubricating oil, white oil, alkylbenzene,                  alkylnaphthalene, hydrogenated triphenyl,                  diphenyl ether, alkylphenyl ether, alkyldiphenyl                  ether and isooctyl alcohol. The organic solvent                  comprises methanol. Preferred Conditions: The                  reaction is carried out at a temperature of                  100-250 degrees C, and pressure of 10 MPa or                  less.

AU ZHAO M
   WANG F
   HASAN M M Z
   MEMON M Z H
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
   ENVIRONMENTAL INNOVATIONS SUZHOU RES INS (ENVI-Non-standard)
   UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 201934177Q
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing sodium zirconate comprises (i) mixing the crown material waste with solid sodium carbonate; and (ii) calcining the product obtained in step (i) at 750-850 degrees C for 5-8 hours.
   USE - The sodium zirconate is useful for carbon dioxide adsorbent and pyrolysis hydrogen production process for biomass i.e. methylcellulose, spirulina or willow sample, or biomass waste i.e. sludge (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and easy to operate; has excellent carbon dioxide adsorption capacity; and recycles the crown material waste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crown material waste is yttria-stabilized zirconia (YSZ). The mass ratio of zirconium dioxide contained in the YSZ solid and sodium carbonate solid is 1:1-1.2, preferably 1:1. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the calcining is carried out at 850 degrees C for 6 hours. In the step (i), the yttria-stabilized zirconia is grinded in a ball mill at 500-550 revolutions/minute, preferably 500 revolutions/minute for 1.5-2 hours, preferably 2 hours before or after mixing with the sodium carbonate solid.

The monitoring result is                evaluated. An evaluation result of the target area                is obtained, where the evaluation result comprises                buried capacity data, potential leakage risk data,                injection pressure data, injection volume data and                influence degree data of carbon dioxide to stratum                and fault.

TI Method for production of carbonated beverage in            disposable packaging, involves mixing water portion            with carbon dioxide prior to being mixed with beverage            concentrate where movement with carbon dioxide takes            place at preset overpressure.

   EMPL G
   FISCHER D

   KRUGER M
   KRIGER M
   KLUG M
AE FREEZIO AG (FREE-Non-standard)
   FREEZIO AG (FREE-Non-standard)
   FREEZIO AG (FREE-Non-standard)
GA 202151127B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing a beverage                concentrate portion (2) with a water portion (3).                The water portion is mixed with carbon dioxide                prior to being mixed with the beverage concentrate.                The movement with carbon dioxide takes place at an                overpressure of 9-11 bars. The water is made                available at preset temperature before mixing with                carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide is dissolved in                the water by mixing in a static mixer (5). The flow                velocity in the static mixer is set in specific                range. A compensator (6) is provided downstream of                the static mixer. The ratio of the amount of water                to the amount of carbon dioxide is regulated.
   USE - Method for portion-wise production of                carbonated beverage in disposable or reusable                packaging e.g. plastic or metal cartridge.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is added to the water and                mixed with one another in a static mixer in such a                way that the added carbon dioxide dissolves as far                as possible in the water. The sealing elements are                provided on the setting unit and the insert to                prevent the water flowing in the direction of the                outlet from the compensator at undesired                locations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for producing beverage in portions.

   Beverage concentrate portion (2)
   Water portion (3)
   Static mixer (5)
   Compensator (6)

A densitometer (D) is provided on exhaust line to detect the carbon dioxide concentration in the exhaust gas. GA 202059608E

A carbon dioxide injection pipe is provided on the inner top side of the incubator body, and a gas filter is provided on the bottom end of the carbon dioxide injection pipe. The carbon dioxide enters the carbon dioxide injection pipe, is filtered by the gas filter, and enters the inside of the incubator body. The rotation direction of the two exhaust fans on one culture plate is non-stop, the rotation direction of the exhaust fans located on the same vertical line is the same. The carbon dioxide form a cycle inside the incubator body. The bottom of the incubator body is moved to contact a humidifying disc when the carbon dioxide forms a cycle. The carbon dioxide carries water continuously circulating inside the incubator body.

An end of an exhaust duct (18) is connected to an outlet of the carbon dioxide removal device. PT P
PN WO2018185705-A1; EP3606642-A1; US2020114301-A1; US11285426-B2
TI Capturing chemical species from fluid composition e.g. gas stream, involves contacting pyrazine-containing metal-organic framework with fluid composition comprising water, carbon dioxide and other chemical species.
AU CADIAU A
   ADIL K
   BELMABKHOUT Y
   BHATT P M
   EDDAOUDI M
AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 201878158S
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing chemical species from a fluid composition involves providing a pyrazine-containing metal-organic framework, contacting the metal-organic framework with a fluid composition comprising water, carbon dioxide and other chemical species and capturing water and carbon dioxide simultaneously from the fluid composition.
   USE - Capturing chemical species from fluid composition such as natural gas and gas streams from industries related to energy, environment and medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is efficient, economical, and feasible method of removing carbon dioxide and water. The capture of water and carbon dioxide via the metal-organic framework occurs at distinct adsorption sites, preventing and/or minimizing any competitive interactions between the two and providing an efficient, effective, and selective adsorption. The metal-organic frameworks exhibits a greater affinity for water or carbon dioxide sufficient to achieve an effective separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Capturing chemical species from a fluid composition involves providing a metal-organic framework, contacting the metal-organic framework with a fluid composition comprising water, carbon dioxide and other chemical species and capturing water and carbon dioxide simultaneously from the fluid composition. The metal-organic framework is of formula: MaMbF5(O/H2O)(pyrazine)2.x(solv), where Ma is nickel cation, Mb is niobium ion, aluminum ion, ferric cation, iron(V) ion, vanadium(III) ion or vanadium(V) ion, x is 0-10 and solv is water, carbon dioxide, dimethylformamide, ethanol, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone and/or methanol.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of method of capturing chemical species from a fluid composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal (Mb) further comprises lanthanum cation, rhodium(III) ion, titanium(III) ion, chromium cation, gallium cation, indium cation, scandium cation or yttrium cation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The other chemical species comprises nitrogen, hydrogen, methane, n-butane, isobutane, 1-propanol and isopropanol and further comprises linear and/or branched hydrocarbon. Carbon dioxide is preferentially captured via interactions with fluorine atom(s) of pillars and hydrogen atom(s) of pyrazine. Preferred Properties: The metal-organic framework exhibits a high uptake of water and/or carbon dioxide at low partial pressures. The metal-organic framework is saturated with water or carbon dioxide at a partial pressure of 0.5 P/P0.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN108210466-A; CN108210466-B
TI Preparing trans-resveratrol-poloxamer 188 solid            dispersion by starting supercritical crystallization            granulation system, compressing carbon dioxide by high            pressure pump, preheating and opening carbon dioxide            outlet valve.
AU BAO H
AE JIANGSU FOOD & PHARM SCI COLLEGE (JSFP-C)
GA 201853553A
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing                trans-resveratrol-poloxamer 188 solid dispersion                involves (a) starting a supercritical                crystallization granulation system, adjusting the                temperature of the crystallization kettle to 38-42                degrees C, compressing carbon dioxide by a high                pressure pump, preheating introducing carbon                dioxide into the crystallization kettle, (b)                opening carbon dioxide outlet valve at bottom of                the crystallization kettle and adjusting the fine                adjustment valve to 2.8-3.2 ml/minute, and (c)                dissolving trans-resveratrol and poloxamer 188 in a                mixed solvent of ethanol and pyridine, pumping the                solution into a crystallizer by a high pressure                infusion pump, introducing carbon dioxide into a                separation kettle through a fine adjustment valve,                discharging carbon dioxide through a rotor flow                meter, continuously feeding carbon dioxide into                carbon monoxide, closing the carbon dioxide inlet                valve and opening the crystallization kettle to                collect trans-resveratrol-poloxamer 188 solid                dispersion.
   USE - Method is useful for preparing                trans-resveratrol-poloxamer 188 solid                dispersion.
   ADVANTAGE - The trans-resveratrol-poloxamer 188 solid                dispersion has high stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - In the step (a), the preheating step is                carried out at a pressure of 11-13 MPa. In the step                (b), the step of adjusting the fine adjustment                valve is carried out to 2.8-3.2 ml/minute, where                the temperature and pressure in the                to-be-crystallized kettle are stable. In the step                (c), the mass ratio of ethanol and pyridine is                1:4-6. In the step (c), the step of pumping is                carried out at a volume flow rate of 1.1-1.3                ml/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In the step (a), the                  temperature of the crystallization kettle is                  adjusted to be 40 degrees C and the pressure of                  the crystallization kettle is adjusted to be 12                  MPa. The solution was pumped into the                  crystallizer at a volume flow rate of 1.2                  ml/minute. Preferred Composition: The                  concentration of trans-resveratrol is 15                  mg/ml.
