PT P
PN CN109942009-A; CN109942009-B
TI Preparing battery grade lithium carbonate comprises e.g. dissolving industrial lithium carbonate, mixing with water, hydrogenating lithium carbonate, heating, subjecting filter residue further to countercurrent pulp washing and drying.
AU KANG S
   YANG Z
   FAN X
   LI D
   SONG Y
AE BEIJING CHEM ENG & METALLURGY RES INST (CNNU-C)
GA 201958450B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing battery grade lithium carbonate comprises e.g. (i) dissolving industrial lithium carbonate, mixing with water and filtrate and stirring; (ii) hydrogenating lithium carbonate, adding the lithium carbonate slurry, passing high-purity carbon dioxide from bottom of the tower gas, collecting carbon dioxide gas, adding collected slurry into the top of the previous column to be added, circulating sequentially, filtering and returning residue to prepare a slurry of lithium carbonate; (iii) adding filtrate obtained in the with a sulfate complexing agent, stirring, heating, filtering and recovering part of lithium; and (iv) washing lithium carbonate obtained with a sodium hydroxide solution, filtering, returning filtrate, subjecting filter residue further to countercurrent pulp washing and washing with deionized water, carrying out primary washing with water to prepare a sodium hydroxide solution, using fresh washing water for the second washing and drying secondary washing residue.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing battery grade lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases contact area and chemical reaction power of lithium carbonate slurry with carbon dioxide gas, hydrogenation rate of lithium carbonate and returning number of times the lithium mother liquor; controls rate of carbon dioxide release from lithium bicarbonate; reduces formation of polycrystalline; destroys polycrystalline structure; reduces impurity content; and obtains high-quality battery-grade lithium carbonate which reaches standard of the industry battery-grade lithium carbonate products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing battery grade lithium carbonate comprises (i) dissolving industrial lithium carbonate to -100 meshes, mixing with water and the filtrate returned in steps (iii) and (iv) in specific ratio, and stirring; (ii) hydrogenating lithium carbonate, adding the lithium carbonate slurry obtained in the step (i) from the top of the last packed column, passing high-purity carbon dioxide from bottom of the tower gas which escapes from the top of the previous tower, so that the two are in countercurrent contact in the packed tower, collecting carbon dioxide gas escaping from the slurry at the bottom of the column and the top of the column separately, adding collected slurry into the top of the previous column to be added, until the first tower, where bottom effluent as the product liquid, returning carbon dioxide gas escaping from the last tower into the first tower, introducing first carbon dioxide gas from the bottom of the first tower, where it is in countercurrent contact with the last stage lithium carbonate slurry in the tower, passing carbon dioxide gas escaping from the top of the tower into the second tower, circulating sequentially, filtering liquid collected at the bottom of the first tower, collecting filtrate and returning residue into the step (i) to prepare a slurry of lithium carbonate; (iii) adding filtrate obtained in the step (ii) with a sulfate complexing agent, stirring, mixing uniformly, heating to drive off the carbon dioxide, stirring while heating until no bubbles escape, heating and stirring for 10 minutes, filtering obtained slurry to obtain lithium carbonate and a filtrate, returning filtrate partially to the step (i) for pulping, and recovering part of the lithium; and (iv) washing lithium carbonate obtained in the step (iii) firstly with a sodium hydroxide solution having a pH of 12-12.5, filtering, returning the filtrate, subjecting filter residue further to countercurrent pulp washing and washing with deionized water for not less than 2 times, carrying out primary washing with water to prepare a sodium hydroxide solution, using fresh washing water for the second washing, using obtained washing water as the next washing water, and drying secondary washing residue as a product to analyze the impurity content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of lithium carbonate to water and the filtrate in step (i) obtained by the steps (iii) and (iv) is 1:20-30. The using amount of lithium solution in step (iii) is 0.05-0.5 g/l. The solid content of the washing liquid in step (iv) is 0.5-2 at greater than or equal to 70 degrees C, and not less than 10 minutes. The volume-to-mass ratio (l/kg) of deionized water and lithium carbonate in step (iv) is 0.5-2 and washing at greater than or equal to 70 degrees C for not less than 10 minutes. Preferred Method: The stirring in step (i) is carried out at room temperature to form uniform slurry. The filler filling height in step (ii) is not less than 1000 mm, where single tower residence time of the slurry is not less than 5 minutes and the molar ratio of the carbon dioxide gas and lithium carbonate in the slurry is greater than or equal to 1.1. The carbon dioxide temperature in step (iii) is greater than or equal to 70 degrees C and the heating is carried out at not less than 30 minutes. Preferred Components: The filler in step (ii) comprises triangular spiral filler, theta ring filler, a calendered pore filler, and a step ring filler. The sulfate complexing agent in step (iii) comprises polyoxyethylene sorbitan fatty acid ester, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, cetyltrimethylammonium chloride, cetyltrimethylammonium hydroxide, dodecyltrimethylammonium bromide, dodecyltrimethylammonium chloride, dodecyltrimethylammonium hydroxide, sodium hexadecyl sulfonate, sodium dodecyl sulfate or triethanolamine.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC E10-A09A2; E10-A09B5; E10-A22G; E10-B03B2; E33-G; A10-E07C; A12-E06; X16-B01F1

PN CN113930342-A
TI Culture system for culturing photosynthetic organisms and or fixing carbon dioxide, comprises polymerization-induced luminescence molecule.
AU LIU H
   TANG B
AE UNIV HONG KONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UHKS-C)
GA 202217273T
AB    NOVELTY - Culture system comprises a polymerization-induced luminescence molecule.
   USE - Culture system for culturing photosynthetic organisms and or fixing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The culture system provides application prospect for carbon dioxide fixation of photosynthetic organisms biological resource development and so on
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the method for promoting the growth of photosynthetic organisms, which involves culturing the photosynthetic organisms in the presence of aggregation-induced luminescence molecules.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - The aggregation-induced light emitting molecule is capable of converting the short wavelength light of 300-450 nanometer into long wavelength light of 450-700nm of the photosynthetic organism such as wavelength of 450-600nm from 450-550nm or from 500-600nm. The aggregation-inducing luminescent molecule is biocompatible with the photosynthetic organism. The aggregation-induced light emitting molecule is any one or two of the following. The aggregation-induced luminescence molecule in the culture medium is in the form of nano- aggregate, the average size of the nano- aggregate is 80-400nm. The culture medium the concentration of the aggregation-induced luminescence molecule is 1-20 M. The nano- aggregate is dispersed around the photosynthetic organism.

GA 2018683204
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-based catalyst comprises heteroatom and intrinsic carbon defects.
   USE - The carbon-based catalyst is useful for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to synthesis gas (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for preparing carbon-base catalyst, which involves calcining metal organic framework-5 crystal; and
   (2) use of carbon-based catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to synthesis gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heteroatom comprises nitrogen atom and boron atom. Preferred Conditions: The calcining step is carried out at temperature of 1000-1100 degrees C for 2-4 hours. Preferred Method: The application method involves coating the carbon-based catalyst on the glassy carbon electrode as working electrode to perform constant-voltage electrolysis of electrolyte in a sealed three-electrode system, where the volume ratio of carbon monoxide and hydrogen gas in the synthesis gas is 0.05-6:1.

PT P
PN WO2020048809-A1; CN112638849-A; EP3847146-A1; US2021363007-A1; US11292717-B2; RU2021108998-A
TI Manufacture of methanol used for producing e.g.            acetic acid, involves feeding syngas to methanol            synthesis unit, distilling low-boiling stream,            obtaining carbon dioxide enriched stream, and feeding            enriched stream to syngas generating unit.
AU VICARI M
   GEIGER T
   KATZ T
   VIKARI M
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 202020698X
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of methanol involves generating a                syngas from a carbon-containing feedstock in a                syngas generating unit (A), feeding syngas to a                methanol synthesis unit (B) in the presence of a                methanol synthesis catalyst to give a raw methanol                stream comprising methanol, water, syngas, dimethyl                ether and methane-containing reaction mixture,                feeding raw methanol stream into a relaxation unit                and depressurized to obtain low-boiling stream                containing carbon dioxide and dimethyl ether,                distilling the low-boiling stream containing carbon                dioxide and dimethyl ether to obtain bottom stream                enriched with methanol and water, distilling                water-containing high boiler stream to obtain                methanol, feeding expansion gas, gaseous stream and                low boiling stream to a combustion unit, supplying                oxygen containing gas to obtain carbon                dioxide-containing flue gas, feeding flue gas to a                carbon dioxide recovery unit to obtain carbon                dioxide enriched stream, and passing enriched                stream to the unit (A) and/or (B).
   USE - Manufacture of methanol used as solvent for                synthesis of formaldehyde, acetic acid, methyl                tert-butyl ether (MTBE), dimethyl terephthalate,                methyl methacrylate and methyl amines.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and                enables simple manufacture of methanol having                excellent productivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of methanol involves generating a                carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and                hydrogen-containing syngas from a carbon-containing                feedstock in a syngas generating unit (A), feeding                syngas to a methanol synthesis unit at 150-300                degrees C under a pressure of 5-10 MPa in the                presence of a methanol synthesis catalyst to give a                raw methanol stream comprising methanol, water,                syngas, dimethyl ether and methane-containing                reaction mixture, removing raw methanol stream from                the methanol synthesis unit (B), feeding raw                methanol stream in to a relaxation unit (C)                depressurized to a pressure of 0.1-2 MPa to obtain                low-boiling stream containing carbon dioxide and                dimethyl ether, distilling the low-boiling stream                containing carbon dioxide and dimethyl ether in a                distillation unit to obtain bottom stream enriched                with methanol and water, separating                water-containing high boiler stream using                distillation device to obtain methanol, feeding                expansion gas, gaseous stream and low boiling                stream to a combustion unit, supplying oxygen                containing gas having an oxygen content of 30-100                vol.% to obtain carbon dioxide-containing flue gas,                feeding flue gas to a carbon dioxide recovery unit                to obtain carbon dioxide enriched stream, and                passing carbon dioxide enriched stream to a syngas                generating unit and/or methanol synthesis                unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonaceous                  feedstock comprises natural gas, biogas, coal,                  wood, plastics, petroleum, bionaphtha or                  hydrocarbonaceous streams from petroleum or                  natural gas processing, from chemical production                  processes, from renewable raw materials or from                  plastics recycling. The syngas is generated by                  steam reforming, by autothermal reforming, by a                  combination of steam reforming and autothermal                  reforming or by partial oxidation. The syngas                  comprises methane. The methanol synthesis                  catalyst comprises copper and zinc-containing                  heterogeneous catalyst. The carbon dioxide from                  the carbon dioxide-containing flue gas is                  absorbed in an absorber in a basic solvent to                  form the exhaust gas stream, and the carbon                  dioxide-enriched stream is released from the                  solvent loaded with carbon dioxide in a desorber                  and the solvent depleted of carbon dioxide is                  returned to the absorber. The base solvent                  comprises aqueous solution of an organic amine.                  The organic amine comprises monoethanolamine,                  piperazine, 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol,                  triethylenediamine, N-methyldiethanolamine or                  tert-butylaminoethoxyethanol. The carbon dioxide                  enriched stream containing oxygen is                  catalytically hydrogenated before it is returned                  to the syngas generation unit and/or methanol                  synthesis unit. Preferred Composition: The oxygen                  containing gas comprises an oxygen content of                  90-100 vol.%. Preferred Apparatus: The methanol                  synthesis unit (B) comprises a compressor for                  compressing the synthesis gas, a reactor for                  converting the synthesis gas, a condenser for off                  contains condensation of the raw methanol stream                  and a line for returning uncondensed gas to the                  reactor. The combustion unit further comprises a                  combustion chamber and a condenser, water is                  condensed from the combustion gas obtained in the                  combustion chamber in the condenser and as a                  stream discharges from the combustion unit, and                  the remaining gaseous stream forms the carbon                  dioxide-containing flue gas. Before supplying                  electricity to the combustion unit hydrogen is                  separated off in a hydrogen recovery unit by                  pressure swing adsorption. In addition to the                  hydrogen supplied through the synthesis gas, the                  hydrogen synthesis unit is fed with further                  hydrogen.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

   QIN J

   PAN B
AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
GA 202259327J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of cobalt ferrite cocatalyst, which involves: i. dissolving compound solid as cobalt source and the compound solid as iron source in a solvent to prepare a solution, stirring resulting solution for 10 minutes; ii. adding sodium borohydride solution dropwise to the solution while stirring, continue stirring for 10 minutes, and then centrifuging with a centrifuge, washing obtained precipitate with ethanol until the ion concentration is less than 10 parts per million, drying at 40-120 degreesC for 12-24 hours, and grinding to obtain cobalt iron(ii) oxide (CoFeOX) precursor; iii. calcining the precursor in an inert atmosphere muffle furnace at 200-800 degreesC, for 2-4 hours to obtain CoFeOX powder.
   USE - Cobalt ferrite cocatalyst preparation method used as a cocatalyst in the photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction system (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Preparation method of cobalt ferrite cocatalyst has high activity exposure ratio, etc., effectively promote the performance of photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction, preparation method is simple and feasible, the reaction system conditions are mild, and it has broad application prospects in the use of solar energy to achieve carbon dioxide fixation and conversion.

Consumption of the cobalt ferrite cocatalyst is 1-30% of the mass of the photosensitizer. Preferred Method: Photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction system is a batch photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction system under normal pressure, and the bipyridine ruthenium complex (Ru(bpy)3)Cl2.6H2O is used as a visible light photosensitizer, and three ethanolamine is used as an electron sacrificial agent, and acetonitrile or a mixed solution of acetonitrile and water is used as a solvent. The reaction temperature of the photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction system is 10-80 degreesC, and the reaction time is 15-180 minutes.

PN CN111468162-A
TI Carbon dioxide reduction catalyst composition comprises porous graphene aerogel, cobalt nitrate, zinc nitrate, organic ligand, and polyvinylpyrrolidone.
AU MEI H
AE MEI H (MEIH-Individual)
GA 202074592L
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide reduction catalyst composition comprises porous graphene aerogel, cobalt nitrate, zinc nitrate, organic ligand, and polyvinylpyrrolidone in a mass ratio is 30-80:100:65-70:500-550:20-30.
   USE - Used as a carbon dioxide reduction catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has good catalytic activity, good conductivity, low boiling point evaporation at high temperature, and high number of vacancies and pore structures in carbon matrix.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of the carbon dioxide reduction catalyst comprising (i) adding graphene oxide and urea in distilled water solvent, ultrasonically dispersing in constant-temperature ultrasonic dispersion device, adding polyvinylpyrrolidone as cross-linking agent, continuously and ultrasonically processing, pouring solution to in reaction kettle, reacting at 170-190 degrees C for 10-15 hours, freeze drying to remove solvent, washing, and drying to obtain functional graphene aerogel, (ii) adding functional graphene aerogel to N,N-dimethylformamide solvent, uniformly and ultrasonically dispersing to add cobalt nitrate and zinc nitrate, allowing to stand for 12-24 hours, uniformly stirring to add organic ligand 4-amino-3,5-di-3-pyridyl-4H-1,2,4-triazole and surfactant polyvinylpyrrolidone, pouring solution in reaction kettle, reacting at 170-200 degrees C for 24-36 hours, centrifuging, washing, and drying to obtain cobalt-zinc-metal-organic frameworks supported graphene, and (iii) heating cobalt-zinc-metal-organic frameworks supported graphene in atmosphere furnace, thermally insulating, and calcining to obtain carbon dioxide reduction catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the step (iii), the heating rate of atmosphere furnace is 5-10 degrees C/minute; the calcining is carried out at 850-950 degrees C for 2-4 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the step (i) the constant-temperature ultrasonic dispersion device includes an ultrasonic probe. A water bath groove is set on the constant-temperature ultrasonic dispersion device. Constant temperature heaters are fixedly connected to both sides of the constant-temperature ultrasonic dispersion device. The surface of the constant temperature heater is set with a horizontal spring rod. The horizontal spring rod is fixedly connected with a clamp plate. The bottom of the constant-temperature ultrasonic dispersion device is movably connected with a base. The base is fixedly connected with a vertical spring rod. An object stage is fixedly connected above the vertical spring rod. A reaction bottle is set above the object stage.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic ligand is 4-amino-3,5-di-3-pyridyl-4H-1,2,4-triazole. In the step (i), the mass ratio of graphene oxide, urea, and polyvinylpyrrolidone is 100:5-15:3-6.

AB    NOVELTY - Eye drop having stable pH comprises eye drop liquid medicine and eye drop container. The container includes an inner package and a barrier bag. The eye drop liquid medicine is contained in the inner package, a barrier bag is disposed outside the inner package. The cavity between the inner package and the barrier bag is filled with carbon dioxide gas. The carbon dioxide permeation amount of the barrier bag is lower than the carbon dioxide permeation amount of the inner package. The eye drop liquid medicine comprises 0.5-2 g/l dextran, 1-5 g/l polymer compound, 0.5-3 g/l buffer salt, 5-10 g/l inorganic salt, specific amount of pH adjuster and an injection liquid. The buffer salt is capable of generating carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Uses as eye drop having stable pH.
   ADVANTAGE - The eye drop has improved stability during storage. The method: has simple operation; is economical; has high efficiency; and is convenient in quality control and more suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the eye drop, comprising (a) dissolving the active ingredient and the auxiliary material in water for injection to obtain an eye drop liquid medicine, (b) packing the eye drops prepared in the step (a) in an inner package and placing into a barrier bag, and (c) filling the cavity between the inner packaging and the barrier bag with carbon dioxide gas, and sealing the bag.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The buffer salt is sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate and/or potassium carbonate. The concentration of carbon dioxide between the inner package and the barrier bag is 3-20%, preferably 6-15% and more preferably 8-12%. The barrier bag has a carbon dioxide permeation amount of lees than 0.5 cm3/(m2.24h.0.1 MPa). The inorganic salt is sodium chloride, magnesium chloride, calcium chloride, zinc chloride and/or potassium chloride, preferably, the inorganic salt comprises sodium chloride, magnesium chloride, calcium chloride, zinc chloride and potassium chloride, more preferably, the inorganic salt comprises sodium chloride, magnesium chloride, calcium chloride, zinc chloride and potassium chloride. The polymer compound is polyvinyl alcohol, hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, polyvinyl pyrrolidone, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose and/or hydroxyethyl cellulose, preferably, the polymer compound is hydroxypropyl methylcellulose. The inner package is polypropylene soft bag, polypropylene eye drop bottle, polypropylene ampoule, polyethylene eye drop bottle, polyethylene ampoule or cycloolefin eye drop bottle. The barrier bag is aluminum-plastic composite bag. The aluminum-plastic composite bag is polyester/aluminum foil/polyethylene composite bag, polyester/aluminum foil/nylon/polyethylene composite bag, polypropylene/aluminum foil/polyethylene composite bag, polypropylene/aluminum foil/nylon/polyethylene composite bag, nylon/aluminum foil/polyethylene composite bag, nylon/aluminum foil/nylon/polyethylene composite bag, polyester/aluminized/polyester/polyethylene composite bag or aluminized/polyester/polyethylene composite bag. The eye drops comprises 1 g/l dextran, 3 g/l polymer compound, 1.2 g/l buffer salt, 7.9 g/l inorganic salt, and an specific amount of pH adjuster, preferably, each 1 l of eye drops comprises 1 g dextran, 3 g hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, 1.2 g sodium bicarbonate, 6.5 g sodium chloride, 1.2 g potassium chloride, 0.1 g magnesium chloride, 0.1 g calcium chloride, adjusted to pH of 5.8-8 with pH adjuster, preferably adjusted to pH of 6-8. The pH adjuster is hydrochloric acid, citric acid, sulfuric acid, boric acid, triethanolamine, diethanolamine and/or methylglucamine. Preferred Method: The method further optionally comprises adjusting the eye drop medicine prepared in the step (a) to pH of 5.8-8, preferably pH of 6-8 using a pH adjuster, and/or filtering the eye drop medicine prepared in the step (a), where the filtering method adopts a 0.45 mu m and/or 0.22 mu m filter pressure filtration method, and/or, sterilizing the eye drop medicine prepared in the step (a), where the sterilization method can be sterilized by moist heat sterilization and/or filtration, and/or, the active ingredient and the auxiliary material of the step (a) may be separately dissolved, mixed or dissolved one by one, and the dissolution method may be carried out by heating, stirring and shearing.

PN KR2019071371-A; KR2046494-B1
TI Refining of reducing gas involves charging reducing iron to gasifying furnace, gasifying, mixing ammonia gas with reducing gas, cooling, removing carbon dioxide, supplying purified gas to reducing furnace, liquefying and pyrolyzing.
AU CHO M K
   JO M K
AE POSCO (POSC-C)
GA 201956714X
AB    NOVELTY - Refining of reducing gas involves charging reducing iron to a melting and gasifying furnace, gasifying, mixing ammonia gas with the reducing gas to provide a mixed gas, cooling the mixed gas to 50 degrees C or less to produce ammonium carbonate salt, removing carbon dioxide contained in the mixed gas to purify the reducing gas, supplying the purified reducing gas to a reducing furnace, liquefying the ammonium carbonate salt, pyrolyzing the liquefied salt into carbon dioxide and ammonia, and washing to produce aqueous ammonia, which is heated to produce ammonia gas used for mixing with reducing gas.
   USE - Refining of reducing gas.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide contained in the reducing gas can be selectively removed by generating ammonium carbonate salt. The loss of reducing power of the flue gas can be minimized, and the flue gas can be re-introduced into the reduction furnace.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for reducing gas refining apparatus, which consists of a gas mixer for supplying reducing gas from the reducing furnace and mixing the ammonia gas with the ammonia gas to provide mixed gas, a cooler for cooling the mixed gas to generate ammonium carbonate salt, a decomposer for liquefying ammonium carbonate salt and pyrolyzing to produce carbon dioxide and ammonia, and washing ammonia to produce aqueous ammonia, and ammonia heater for heating aqueous ammonia to generate the ammonia gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The ammonium carbonate salt is liquefied by adding steam to the ammonium carbonate salt. The reducing gas is supplied with water, and molar ratio of the carbon dioxide, ammonia gas and water is same. Preferred Apparatus: The reducing furnace is a fluidized bed-type reducing furnace or a packed bed-type reducing furnace.

The cellulose is added into B                product based carbon dioxide reversible ionic                liquid compound in ratio of 0.1-2:1 of halogenated                compound at carbon dioxide atmosphere of 0.1-10                meagapascal pressure, and reacted at 10-100 degrees                C for 0.1-48 hours to obtain C product. The water                is added into product C or 1-3C of low grade fatty                alcohol, filtered to obtain solid D product. The D                product is added with water or 1-3C lower fatty                alcohol, washed, purified, and dried to obtain                cellulose carbonate material.
   USE - Method for synthesizing cellulose carbonate                material in lithium ion battery solid electrolyte,                bio-medical fixing splint, surgical suture line,                thermoplastic 3 D printing material, polylactic                acid and toughening composite materials, food                packaging films, solar battery substrate and the                flexible base material field of electronic                (claimed).

       electricity, which mainly contains carbon dioxide,                the methane-containing fractionation gas stream,                the flash gas stream containing carbon monoxide and PN WO2018152238-A1; US2020018544-A1
TI Method for capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas,            involves removing stream enriched in dense carbon            dioxide fluid from separator, and removing stream            enriched in one other component from separator.
AU JACOBY W
   ESPANANI R
AE UNIV MISSOURI (UMOR-C)
   JACOBY W (JACO-Individual)
   ESPANANI R (ESPA-Individual)
   UNIV MISSOURI (UMOR-C)
GA 201865812T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves compressing a vapor that                is provided with carbon dioxide and one other                component to form a compressed mixture comprising a                dense carbon dioxide fluid and one other component.                The compressed mixture comprising dense carbon                dioxide fluid and other components is fed to a                high-pressure density-driven separator. A stream                enriched in dense carbon dioxide fluid and stream                enriched in one other component are formed. The                stream enriched in dense carbon dioxide fluid is                removed from separator. The stream enriched in one                other component is removed from the                separator.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide from flue                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The moderate temperature heat transfers fluids                including process streams, ambient fluids, and/or                fluids from other operations. The compression and                expansion efficiencies are improved. The heat                transfer fluids increase carbon dioxide separation                volumetric efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method involves providing thermodynamic                state of vapor for a substantially complete                separation of carbon dioxide from one other                component. The fluids in vessel of high-pressure                density-driven separator are cooled. The                high-pressure density-driven separator comprises a                heat exchanger with a tube for flow of heat                transfer fluid. The flow of the heat transfer fluid                is such that carbon dioxide is preferentially                cooled relative to other component(s) of vapor. A                portion of dense carbon dioxide fluid removed from                high-pressure density-driven separator as a heat                transfer fluid to a tube the heat exchanger. The                dense carbon dioxide fluid that is recirculated                undergoes a change of state through heat transfer                to the separator and any surface internals. A                portion of dense carbon dioxide fluid removed from                high-pressure density-driven separator is expanded                or heated, such that the dense carbon dioxide fluid                undergoes a change in state. The dense carbon                dioxide fluid undergoes a change in state in an                engine or device external to the high-pressure                density-driven separator. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for an apparatus for separating vapor                comprises several compressors with an inlet for                introducing a vapor to the compressor and an outlet                for removing a compressed fluid. A high-pressure                density-driven separator is provided with vessel,                feed inlet, first outlet, and a second outlet. The                feed inlet of high-pressure density-driven                separator is in fluid communication with outlet of                compressor. Several expanders are in fluid                communication with first outlet and/or second                outlet of high-pressure density-driven separator.                The vessel of high-pressure density-driven                separator is provided with surface internals which                are configured to induce cyclonic fluid motion. The                high-pressure density-driven separator is further                provided with a spiralized conical packing. The                feed inlet is provided with a nozzle to induce                cyclonic fluid motion. The nozzle has an                orientation that is tangential or approximately                normal to the direction of fluid flow in the vessel                of high-pressure density- driven separator. The                high-pressure density-driven separator is further                provided with a heat exchanger with a tube for flow                of heat transfer fluid. The vessel of high-pressure                density- driven separator comprises a heat                exchanger in a tube-in-tube configuration and a                tube-in-shell configuration. The high-pressure                density-driven separator further comprises a second                inlet. A portion of apparatus is positioned                underground. An underground well is in fluid                communication with first outlet and/or second                outlet of high-pressure density-driven separator,                and is provided with a self-sealing cap which is                configured to be open at low pressure and sealed at                high pressure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                process for removal of carbon dioxide from flue                gas.

PT P
PN CN113375357-A; CN216481672-U
TI Carbon dioxide (CO2) phase change liquefaction cycle refrigeration system for air conditioning refrigerator, has evaporators respectively connected to liquid CO2 storage tank to alternately receive liquid CO2 refrigerant provided by liquid CO2 storage tank and vaporize in evaporator.
AU YANG J
AE SHANGHAI FULUDI FLUID TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A8171N
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a pressure gas source (1) used to provide high-pressure gas for the system. Two liquid CO2 storage tanks are arranged in parallel, and respectively connected to the pressure gas source, so as to alternately pass in the high pressure gas to control the pressure in the liquid CO2 storage tank. The evaporators (62) are respectively connected to the liquid CO2 storage tank to alternately receive the liquid CO2 refrigerant provided by the liquid CO2 storage tank and vaporize in the evaporator. The latent heat of vaporization absorbed by the gasification of the liquid CO2 refrigerant and the sensible heat absorbed by the temperature rise of the vaporized low-temperature vapor are exchanged by the sensible heat released by the cooling of the refrigerant flowing through the outer surface of the evaporator tube wall, so that the liquid CO2 in the evaporator is converted into gaseous CO2 and flows out.
   USE - Carbon dioxide (CO2) phase change liquefaction cycle refrigeration system for air conditioning refrigerator.
   ADVANTAGE - The sensible heat released by the cooling of the refrigerant is absorbed, and the temperature drops after the refrigerant releases the sensible heat to realize the refrigeration process. The liquid CO2 can be alternately introduced to cool the gaseous carbon dioxide in the recovery tank function and recycle after liquefaction, which meets the continuous refrigeration requirements of the air-conditioning heat exchanger and improves the heat exchange efficiency and the recovery efficiency of carbon dioxide. An injection mechanism is installed on the top of the storage tank in order to strengthen the cooling and liquefaction rate of the gaseous carbon dioxide in the recovery tank, which uses the latent heat of gasification of throttle expansion and the low-temperature gas carbon dioxide after gasification to quickly cool the gaseous carbon dioxide in the recovery tank and liquefy, thus greatly improving the efficiency of gas-liquid transfer. The use of high-pressure gas as the power source realizes the direct liquefaction of the vaporized gaseous refrigerant under higher pressure, while the high-pressure gas absorbs the sensible heat when the gaseous carbon dioxide is liquefied, without high-pressure refrigerant compressor is required. The pressure in each storage tank can be quickly adjusted, and the gas-liquid separation principle is used to realize the transportation and circulation of the liquid cooling medium with gaseous substances, which can greatly reduce energy consumption. The back pressure control of high-pressure air is used to keep the circulation system and storage tank running at a pressure higher than the pressure that produces dry ice. The air conditioner refrigerator adopting the liquefaction cycle system can realize a variety of temperature zone controlled refrigeration according to the size of the refrigeration demand, which is suitable for different application scenarios and has the characteristics of flexible use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide phase change liquefaction cycle refrigeration method for the refrigeration system of air conditioning refrigerator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a CO2 phase change liquefaction cycle refrigeration system for air conditioning refrigerator. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Pressure gas source (1)
   First drainage electric valve (25)
   Second liquid discharging electric valve (41)
   Pressure reducing valve or flow meter (58)
   Evaporator (62)
DC Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25)); X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)

TI Adsorbing carbon dioxide from mixed gas involves            using organic molecular composite is supported on            porous or non-porous support material.
AU SAKWA-NOVAK M A

   PING E W

   GEORGIA TECH RES CORP (GTEK-C)
GA 2022461349
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorbing carbon dioxide from mixed gas                involves using an organic molecular composite,                which is triphenyl compound supported on a porous                or non-porous support material.
   USE - Method for adsorbing carbon dioxide from mixed                gas
   ADVANTAGE - The method has cost-effective, and improve the                performance and expand the library of amine                sorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Adsorbing carbon dioxide from mixed gas                involves using an organic molecular composite                denoted triphenyl compound (Ph-3-PD) supported on a                porous or non-porous support material.

PN WO2020227812-A1; CA3139777-A1; KR2022019700-A; EP3968753-A1; JP2022533342-W; US2022248612-A1; BR112021022639-A2
TI Reducing pathogens e.g. Escherichia coli            (bacteria) on plant leaves, by determining optimal            frequency of plant species with carbon dioxide-infused            water, and foliar spraying plant of plant species with            carbon dioxide-infused water.
AU ARCHIBALD J
AE CO2 GRO INC (COTW-Non-standard)
   CO2 GRO INC (COTW-Non-standard)
   CO2 GRO INC (COTW-Non-standard)
GA 2020B4919K
AB    NOVELTY - Method of reducing pathogens on plant leaves                involves determining the optimal frequency of                foliar spraying a plant species with carbon                dioxide-infused water, and foliar spraying a plant                of that plant species with carbon dioxide-infused                water at the determined optimal frequency.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing pathogens                chosen from Escherichia coli (bacteria), Leveillula                taurica (powdery mildew), Fusarium oxysporum                (fungi), and Acyrthosiphon pisum (aphid) on plant                leaves or controlling pathogens found on plant                leaves, preferably unicellular pathogens.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the pathogens without                significantly harming the plant, and reduces the                spoilage, and growth time, and improves the plant                yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The step of determining                  the optimal frequency of foliar spraying with                  carbon dioxide-infused water involves determining                  the optimal frequency as the highest frequency                  which does not harm a sample plant of the plant                  species. The step of determining the optimal                  frequency of foliar spraying with carbon                  dioxide-infused water involves selecting a low                  frequency of foliar spraying, foliar spraying a                  sample plant of the plant species with carbon                  dioxide-infused water at the selected frequency,                  if no yellowing of leaves of the sample plant is                  observed, increasing the frequency of foliar                  spraying, repeating the preceding two steps until                  yellowing of the leaves is observed, and                  determining the optimal frequency to be the final                  frequency of foliar spraying used. The step of                  determining the optimal frequency of foliar                  spraying with carbon dioxide-infused water                  involves selecting a low frequency of foliar                  spraying, foliar spraying a sample plant of the                  plant species with carbon dioxide-infused water                  at the selected frequency, if no or acceptable                  yellowing of leaves of the plant is observed,                  increasing the frequency of foliar spraying,                  repeating the preceding two steps until                  unacceptable yellowing of the leaves is observed,                  and determining the optimal frequency to be the                  final frequency of foliar spraying used. The step                  of determining the optimal frequency of foliar                  spraying with carbon dioxide-infused water                  involves measuring a baseline level of                  chlorophyll A within a sample plant of the plant                  species, selecting a low frequency of foliar                  spraying, foliar spraying sample plants of the                  plant species with carbon dioxide-infused water                  at the selected frequency, measuring a current                  level of chlorophyll A within the sample plant,                  if the measured current level of chlorophyll A                  within the sample plant is the same as the                  baseline level, increasing the frequency of                  foliar spraying, repeating the preceding three                  steps until the measured current level of                  chlorophyll A of the sample plant is below the                  baseline level, and determining the optimal                  frequency to be the final frequency of foliar                  spraying used.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Fungicide; Insecticide. No                biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The water is applied by foliar spraying                (claimed). No dosage details given.
DC C05 (Biological control - excluding veterinary medicine, but including use of microorganisms, predators and natural products.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); P11 (Soil working, planting (A01B, C).); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
